topics.title: 9000 3D Extraoral Imaging System Integration
topics.body: 9000 3D Extraoral Imaging System Integration 9000 3D Extraoral Imaging System Integration You can access the 9000 3D system from within the SoftDent charting module, giving you the ability to capture 3D volumes, which are displayed in the document manager. Using the 9000 3D System in the Charting Module You can access the 9000 3D system and work with 3D volumes in the charting module. The following features are available: 3D Button and X-ray Menu Option The 3D button on the main charting toolbar enables you to acquire 3D volumes using the 9000 3D system. Click the 3D button to launch the 3D Acquisition window for the 9000 3D system. For more information, see Using the Main Charting Toolbar . Selecting the Capture 3D Volume option from the X-ray menu on the main charting menu also launches the 3D Acquisition window for the 9000 3D system. For more information, see Using the X-ray Menu . These options are not enabled if you do not have a 9000 unit. V iewing 3D Volumes on the Clinical Images Tab A 3D volume displays in the Clinical Images tab thumbnail viewer in the same manner as other clinical images. Keep the following in mind: To display 3D volumes, select the Other option in the filter dialog. While a 3D volume is being acquired, a temporary 3D placard is displayed, along with the acquisition date in MM/DD/YY format. When the 3D volume has been fully acquired, the placard for a finalized 3D volume is added to the thumbnail viewer. 3D volumes cannot have tooth or region associations. When a 3D volume is selected in the thumbnail viewer, the highlight color is green. A 3D button has been added to the main charting toolbar to enable you to acquire 3D volumes using the Kodak 9000. Clicking the 3D button launches the 3D Acquisition window for the Kodak 9000 3D system. For more information, see Using the Main Charting Toolbar . A new menu option, Capture Kodak 3D Volume , has been added to the X-ray menu on the main charting menu. Selecting this option launches the 3D Acquisition window for the Kodak 9000 3D system. For more information, see Using the X-ray Menu . These options are not enabled if you do not have a Kodak 9000 unit. Right-Click Menu for 3D Volumes in Thumbnail Viewers When you right-click on a 3D volume in a thumbnail viewer�found in the chart, document manager, patient list, scheduler, or InOffice�the following menu items are available: View�Select this option to display the 3D volume in the dental imaging software's 3D volume-specific application. Properties�Select this option to display the properties window for the 3D volume selected. Delete�Select this option to delete the volume. Select All Images�Select this option to select all images in the thumbnail viewer. Unselect All Images�Select this option to deselect all images. All other options are disabled for 3D volumes. Viewing 3D Volume Details on the Clinical History Tab The Clinical History tab is updated when a 3D volume is added. Two items are added to the clinical history: Image�The acquisition date, file path, and image name are displayed. Note�The acquisition date and the description of the note are displayed. To view a 3D volume, right-click on the 3D volume and select View . Scan for 3D Temporary Volumes option in Video Capture/Display Properties Window Select the Scan for 3D Temporary Volumes option in the Images section of the Video Capture/Display Properties window to have SoftDent monitor the patient�s image storage drive for new temporary 3D volume folders

topics.title: Accessing ePostings
topics.body: Accessing ePostings eServices > Using the ePostings Service Accessing ePostings You can view ePostings details from several areas of the software: Main Menu: Access ePostings by selecting Insurance > Insurance Payments > ePostings Service . The following ePostings menu options are available: ePostings Payment Viewer: Displays the ePostings Payment Viewer window , containing records waiting to be processed. Completed ePostings Payments: Displays the Completed ePostings Payment window , containing records that have already been processed. ePostings Adjustments: Displays the ePostings Adjustments window , containing a list of ePostings adjustment codes. At Startup : To display the ePostings Payment Viewer window at startup, select the ePostings Payment Viewer option in the Startup Preferences window. To access this window, select System > Startup Preferences . See Setting Startup Preferences for more information. Practice Central: ePostings payments can be displayed in two places in Practice Central: ePostings Notifications: A listing of ePostings payments is displayed under an ePostings Notifications header, provided the following conditions are met: ePostings is enabled. The Show ePostings Notifications option is selected in the Rights by User or Users by Rights security window. The ePostings Notifications option is selected in the Practice Central Preferences window. Office Expert Summary: An ePostings Notifications entry detailing the status of ePostings payments is displayed under the Office Expert Summary section, provided the following conditions are met: ePostings is enabled. The ePostings Notifications option is selected in the Active Office Expert Entries section of the Office Expert Setup window. To view ePostings payment information in Practice Central, do one of the following: In the ePostings Notifications section, click an item in the list. In the Office Expert Summary section, click the ePostings Notification entry. The ePostings Payment window is displayed. See Using Practice Central for more information. Office Expert: To add the ePostings summary to the Office Expert, select the ePostings Notifications option in the Active Office Expert Entries section of the Office Expert Setup window. When you select the ePostings Notifications item in the Office Expert list, the status of ePostings payments is displayed at the bottom of the window. When you click the Take Action button with the ePostings Notifications option selected, the ePostings Payment Viewer window is displayed. See Using the Office Expert for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Accessing ePrescriptions Reports eServices > Using the ePrescriptions Module Accessing ePrescriptions Reports After you have enabled the ePrescriptions service, you can retrieve the following report information: ePrescriptions Authorizations ePrescriptions Reports Today's ePrescriptions Status Summary Select System > Modules > ePrescriptions and then the menu option for the information you want to retrieve. The appropriate ePrescriptions window is displayed with the current options and information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accessing The Exchange
topics.body: Accessing The Exchange Finding Answers Accessing The Exchange You can use The Exchange, Carestream Dental's online user community, to ask questions, find answers to common questions, make suggestions, and interact with other SoftDent users, support representatives, and trainers. To access The Exchange: Click The Exchange , or type https://community.carestreamdental.com/t5/SoftDent-Discussion/bd-p/softdentdiscussion into the Internet browser address field. The SoftDent Discussion page is displayed. Do one of the following: If you have a user name and password, click Sign In . See Signing In to The Exchange for more information. To register, click Sign In, and then click the New User? Register here link. See Registering for the Exchange for more information. You must have an active Internet connection to access The Exchange. Related Topics Registering for The Exchange Signing In to The Exchange Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Account Balance Summary
topics.body: Account Balance Summary eServices > Using eServices Reports Account Balance Summary The eServices Account Balance Summary report shows the previous balance, the submission date, claim quantity, price per claim, total dollar amount, and the existing balance with the eServices clearinghouse as of the submission date. Also, any payments made to the account to increase the level of funds available for claims submission are shown. Any specific messages for your account from the eServices clearinghouse or any general message to be conveyed to all electronic claims submitters is displayed at the bottom of the report. To receive this report, you must have loaded, validated, and transmitted your claims to the eServices clearinghouse. You will receive this report upon the next transmission. The report lists the last 10 transactions and does not show a charge for that day's claims. You might want to retain your reports for reference. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accounting Reports
topics.body: Accounting Reports Handling Reports Accounting Reports You can access the following reports from the Reports > Accounting menu: Report Name Description Frequency Daysheet This report provides an overview of the day's finances. All transactions posted, receivables totals, and the number of new and existing patients seen are included in the report. For more information, see Running the Daysheet Report . Daily Account Aging Report This report generates a list of all accounts with aged balances that exceed a specified amount. Use this report to identify overdue accounts. For more information, see Running the Account Aging Report . Weekly or Monthly Provider Daily Summary This report generates a list of charges, production adjustments, payments, balances, and collection to production ratios for the selected doctors. For more information, see Running the Provider Daily Summary Report . Daily Statements You can run monthly, batched, or electronic statements. You can also view a list of batched statements and rerun the current day's regular or electronic statements. For more information see Handling Billing Statements and Generating Automated Statements . As Needed Transactions For A Period This report details all transactions posted during a specified date range and summarizes transactions by provider. Use this report to find out how many adjustments were posted during a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Transactions for a Period Report . As Needed Registers You can run daily, monthly, yearly, period, cumulative, and summary registers. Use a register to summarize receivables and production information. For more information, see Running Register Reports . As Needed Budget Plan Reports You can run the Account, Account Missed Payments, and Patient Budget Plan reports. Use these reports to generate account, overdue, and patient budget plan information. You can also run the Payment Plan report to generate a summary of the outstanding payment plan balance, the number of payments due, and the average monthly income generated from payment plan payments. For more information, see Running Budget Plan Reports . Monthly Accountant's Earnings This report provides a detailed list of transaction codes posted. This report is used to calculate your net collections, net productions, net charges, tax, and the expected income for a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Accountant's Earnings Report . Monthly Deposit Slip This report details the total check and cash amounts posted for your practice or a specific doctor within a specific date range. For more information, see Running the Deposit Slip Report . Weekly Insurance Payment Distribution This report provides a list of all patients covered by insurance payments posted for a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Insurance Payment Distribution Report . Monthly Credit Card Settlement To reconcile credit card transactions, you must generate a Credit Card Settlement report at the end of each day. For more information, see Printing Settlement Reports . Reprint Credit Card Settlement This report enables you to reprint the Credit Card Settlement report. For more information, see Reprinting Settlement Reports . Credit Card Expiration Date This report generates a list of credit cards that expire between a specified date range. The report results include the patient name and identification number, the credit card name and number, and the date on which the card expires. For more information, see Generating the Expiration Date Report . Recurring Credit Card Details Selecting this option displays the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details window, where you can view and sort the payments from your practice�s credit card payment plans. For more information, see Using the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details Window . Time Card This report generates a list of time card entries for each employee. For more information, see Generating the Time Card Report . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accounting Reports
topics.body: Accounting Reports What's New in Version 14.0 > Reports Accounting Reports Accounting reports have received enhancements in the following areas: Register Reports Export to Excel The following register reports can now be exported to Excel: Daily Register, Monthly Register, Yearly Register, Register for a Period, Cumulative Production Report, and the Register Summary Report. In addition, the Cumulative Production Report and the Register Summary Report enable you to specify how you want the information exported to Excel: Print Raw Data �Exports the report data to a tab in Excel where each piece of data in the report is in a separate column, enabling you to further manipulate the data using Excel. The report information is displayed on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data �Exports the report data to a tab in Excel where the report data is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer. The report information is displayed on the Formatted tab. You can also select both options. See Running Register Reports for more information. Provider Daily Summary Report Exports to Excel The Provider Daily Summary Report can now be exported to Excel. In addition, you can specify how you want the information exported to Excel: Print Raw Data �Exports the report data to a tab in Excel where each piece of data in the report is in a separate column, enabling you to further manipulate the data using Excel. The report information is displayed on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data �Exports the report data to a tab in Excel where the report data is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer. The report information is displayed on the Formatted tab. You can also select both options. See Running the Provider Daily Summary Report for more information. Printing Sections of the Daysheet Report A Print Options section has been added to the Daysheet Report, enabling you to print a specific part of the daysheet. You can print only the patient transactions, only the Provider Summary, only the Deposit Slip, or a combination of these options. Select all of the options to print the entire daysheet. See Running the Daysheet Report for more information. Account Aging Report Enhancements Several enhancements have been made to the Account Aging report: Overdue Ranges Added to the Account Aging Report The Account Aging report now enables you to select from a range of overdue accounts, instead of entering a single number and receiving information on all accounts overdue by more than that number. For example, instead of typing 30 and receiving information about all accounts overdue by more than 30 days (which also includes accounts overdue by 60 and 90 days), you can now select a specific range for the report. Ranges for overdue accounts include: 0-30 days, 31-60 days, 61-90 days, and > 90 days. See Running the Account Aging Report for more information. Accounts Receivable Percentages Added to the Account Aging Report The Practice Aging Summary and Outstanding Insurance Breakdown sections of the report now contain a column showing the Accounts Receivable as a percentage for each of the aging categories�current, 30 days, 60 days, 90 days, credits, amounts not on budget plans, and amounts transferred to budget plans. See Running the Account Aging Report for more information. Budget Plan Report Enhancements Day to Post Field Added to the Patient Budget Plan Report A Day To Post field has been added to the Patient Budget Plan report. Information in this f

topics.title: Activating and Deactivating the KIOSK Check-In Module
topics.body: Activating and Deactivating the KIOSK Check-In Module Using the KIOSK Check-In Module Activating and Deactivating the KIOSK Check-In Module Before you can activate the KIOSK check-in module to communicate with your other computers, you must install the KIOSK module and share the KIOSK computer's hard drive. For more information on sharing and mapping drives and folders, see the documentation and online help provided with your Microsoft Windows operating system. After the KIOSK computer's hard drive has been shared, follow these steps to activate the KIOSK: On the SoftDent server, select System > Change System Settings > KIOSK Configuration Options > Activate/Deactivate KIOSK workstations . The KIOSK Workstations window is displayed. Click Add . The Browse for Folder window is displayed. Browse to the workstation you want to activate, and select the folder containing the SDKIOSK.EXE file. Click OK . The KIOSK workstation is displayed in the list of active workstations. Click Close . To run the KIOSK module on the dedicated workstation, select Start > All Programs > SoftDent KIOSK > SoftDent KIOSK . To deactivate an active KIOSK workstation: On the SoftDent server, select System > Change System Settings > KIOSK Configuration Options > Activate/Deactivate KIOSK workstations . The KIOSK Workstations window is displayed. Select the KIOSK computer from the list, and click Remove . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding HIPAA Acknowledgement Contacts
topics.body: Adding HIPAA Acknowledgement Contacts System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features > Handling HIPAA Contacts Adding HIPAA Acknowledgement Contacts You can attach HIPAA related contact information to a patient record. This feature enables you to track when you informed your patients about your HIPAA compliance efforts. To add a HIPAA acknowledgement contact: Select an option: In a patient record : Select HIPAA from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Daily Appointments window : Select the appointment, right-click, and select HIPAA Acknowledgement . In the Book At A Glance window : Select the appointment, right-click, and select HIPAA Acknowledgement . In the InOffice window : Select the appointment and select HIPAA Acknowledgement from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Contacts for window is displayed. Type the appropriate information in the Re field. Type response information and any notes in the appropriate fields. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Activating New Patients and Accounts
topics.body: Activating New Patients and Accounts Scheduling Appointments > Scheduling Appointments for New Patients Activating New Patients and Accounts When you schedule a new patient , e ntries are made in the Account List and Patients List windows, but no permanent entries are made in your patient and account database unless you click Additional Account Info. The new entries are displayed in fuchsia and most features in the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu are disabled. When the patient arrives for the appointment , t he patient and account must be activated. Select one of the options to activate the patient and account records: Select the new patient in the InOffice window : Click Activate on the toolbar. The information for the account is displayed in the Account window. Complete the account information, and click OK . In the Daily Appointments window : Select an appointment and select Activate from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. When a patient record is displayed in the Patient window : Click Edit on the toolbar, and type the information in the window. Click OK . A message that asks you to activate the patient is displayed. Click Yes . When you activate a new patient, the associated new account is automatically activated. When an account record is displayed in the Account window : Click Edit on the toolbar and type the information in the window. Click OK . A message that asks you to activate the account is displayed. Click Yes . When you activate a new account, you must individually activate each patient associated with the account. If you add a new patient to an existing account, you must activate the patient. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Activating New Accounts or Patients
topics.body: Activating New Accounts or Patients Setting Up Patients > Adding Patient Records > Adding New Patients/Accounts from the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance Windows Activating New Accounts or Patients When you add a new account or patient in the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance windows, the account information is stored in the database and can be viewed and edited from the Account List, Account, Patient, and Patient List windows. The title bar in these windows indicates that the account or patient is new. As long as the patient or account is considered new, most features on the Options menu are not available. To ensure that new patients who do not keep scheduled appointments do not adversely affect your bookkeeping, do not activate the new patient or account until the patient arrives for the appointment. New patients added to existing accounts must be activated individually. To activate a new account or patient: In the Daily Appointments window, select the scheduled time unit for the new patient and select Activate from the Options menu of the Express bar or the Options menu. If the patient is associated with an existing account, the New Patient window is displayed. If the patient is associated with a new account, the New Account window is displayed. Type the information in the fields and click OK . A message that asks you to activate the new patient or account is displayed. Click Yes . When you activate a new patient, the account is automatically activated. If you activate a new account, however, you still must individually activate each patient in the account. If you add a new patient to an existing account, you must activate that patient. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Activating the New Patient�s Account
topics.body: Activating the New Patient�s Account Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions > Adding Unscheduled Patients to the InOffice Window Activating the New Patient�s Account After you have added the unscheduled new patient to the appointment book, you must activate the patient�s account. To activate the new patient�s account: Select the new patient appointment and select Activate from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The New Account window is displayed. Type the new account information in the appropriate fields and click OK . A message that asks you to confirm the new patient account activation is displayed. Click Yes . The patient is activated. Click Close . The Patient window is displayed. Enter the appropriate information and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accepting Treatment Plan Groups
topics.body: Accepting Treatment Plan Groups Charting Treatment Plans > Creating Treatment Plan Groups Accepting Treatment Plan Groups To change the treatment plan state to indicate that the patient has accepted the proposed treatment plan procedure: On the Treatment Planning tab in the Charting for window, select the procedure, and select Click to change the treatment plan state on the Treatment Plan toolbar . The Change Treatment Plan State window is displayed. Do one of the following: To change the state of a group in the primary treatment plan: Select the state from the drop-down list in the Primary Treatment Plan section, and type the group number. To change the state of a group in the secondary treatment plan: Select the state from the drop-down list in the Secondary Treatment Plan section, and type the group number. To change the state of a group in the tertiary treatment plan: Select the state from the drop-down list in the Tertiary Treatment Plan section, and type the group number. If the patient does not agree to all procedures in the group, delete the unaccepted procedures or reassign them to another group number. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accessing the Contact Expert from the Office Expert
topics.body: Accessing the Contact Expert from the Office Expert Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Accessing the Contact Expert Accessing the Contact Expert from the Office Expert After running an office expert item , y ou can take action and view the output in the contact expert. The contact list and contact-specific information are displayed in the Contact Expert window for easy reference during communication with a contact. Example : After running the Outstanding Claims By Company (Elec) report in the office expert, click Take Action . A list of insurance companies and their identification numbers are displayed in the left pane of the Contact Expert window. You can call an insurance company, view a list of previous company contacts, view claim information, add notes, and log the contact. To access the Contact Expert window from the Office Expert window: Select an item from the list. Click Take Action . The Contact Expert window is displayed. Need more information? topics.title: Accessing the Software
topics.body: Accessing the Software Getting Started > Starting the Software Accessing the Software To access the software: Select an option: Double-click the SoftDent icon ( ) located on your desktop. Click Start and select All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Software . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . If you are the first person to use the software after installation, type the default user name, DOCTOR, in the Name field, and type the default password, DOCTOR12, in the Password field. The default name and password give you access to all application features. OR If a fingerprint scanner is attached to the computer and you have a fingerprint in the database, scan your fingerprint. topics.title: Running the Account Aging Report
topics.body: Running the Account Aging Report Handling Reports > Accounting Reports Running the Account Aging Report The Account Aging report lists all accounts with aged balances exceeding a specified amount. It also shows the date and amount of the last payment, the total balance, the insurance amount, and the current, 30-, 60-, and 90-day aging amounts. Use this report to identify overdue accounts. Additional Account Aging report info: By default, this report excludes accounts with user codes C, I, or L; inactive accounts; accounts with $0.00 balances; and accounts with credit balances. If you do not select the Show Details Of Individual Accounts checkbox, this part of the report prints balance mismatch information only. If you run this report for a specific provider, the summary does not include budget plan amounts. If you have future transactions posted in your system, the account will show as having a balance mismatch. True balance mismatches display a status box in the Account Transactions window. This report does not include = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('Dangl.htm');return false;">dangling transactions. You should not compare the receivables total from the Account Aging report with the receivables number on the daysheet. The defaults for the Account Aging report exclude a number of accounts, while the daysheet does not exclude any accounts. To reconcile the Account Aging report with other accounting reports, you must not exclude any user codes, and you must include accounts with credit balances when you set up the report. The Outstanding Insurance Breakdown section of the report shows the dollar total and aging of insurance balances. The dollar total equals the insurance balance for accounts included in the report. This is not the same calculation as for outstanding claims, and this total will not match any other report. The Accounts Receivable is also shown as a percentage for each of the aging categories�current, 30-, 60-, 90-, credits, amounts not on budget plans, and amounts transferred to budget plans. The Outstanding Insurance Breakdown section includes a total of the outstanding income for the practice. This is calculated by adding the total outstanding insurance and the total amount due of each account and then subtracting any prepayments that have been made. T he Accounts Receivable section includes a total of all the debit account balances (without credit balances factored in). You can see that your debit balances match your total outstanding income. Both of these amounts are displayed in blue. The Accounts Receivable section is calculated as follows: NET PRODUCTIONS = Productions (100.00+) + Charges (61.00-95.89, 97.00-97.89, 99.00-99.89) +/-Production Adjustments (40.00-60.89) NET COLLECTIONS = Cash (1.00-1.89) + Checks (2.00-10.89) + Credit Cards (11.00-20.89) +/-Collection Adjustments (21.00-39.98, 96.00-96.89, 98.00-98.89) NET PRODUCTIONS - NET COLLECTIONS = ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE Your receivables total is the amount awaiting or requiring payment (the difference between the services performed and the amount collected or adjusted off for these services). The Total (Net 30) field displays the total of all productions, charges, and adjustments to productions you expect to collect within the next 30 days (excluding budget plan amounts, listed in the On Budget Plans field). The Accounts Receivable is also shown as a percentage for each of the aging categories�current, 30-, 60-, 90-, credits, amounts not on budget plans, and amounts transferred to budget plans. Sample Report To run the Account Aging report: Select Reports > Accounting > Account Aging . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output type and click OK . The Account Aging Report Setup window is displayed. By default, the report is set up to be run for all doctors. To run the report for a specific doctor, select the By Doctor option, and use the drop-down list to select the doctor. Select the type of accounts you want to include in the report. Select the Show Details of Individual Accounts option to list individual accounts on the report, and select an option to include addresses or print the accounts in oldest balance order. Type the user codes for the accounts you want to include or exclude. Click OK . For an explanation of the fields in the report, click here . For Practice Acct ID and Account Name �Account ID number and name of guarantor one. Balance �Total account balance when run for the practice. Ins Amt �The account's insurance balance. Amt Due �The amount due on the account. Prepay �Any amounts prepaid against expected insurance. Current �Portion of the balance that is current. 30 Days, 60 Days, 90 Days �Portion of the balance that is outstanding 30, 60, or 90 days. Date LP �Date of last payment (codes 1.00-20.89). The LAST PAYMENT field in the Account Information window is updated for personal payments (codes 1.00, 3.00-20.89). Amount LP �Amount of last payment (codes 1.00-20.89). Bdgt Pln/CC Plan �Total of account budget plan/credit card payment plan balance. For Individual Provider Acct ID and Account Name �Account ID number and name of guarantor one. Balance �Portion of the account balance due the individual provider. Ins Amt �The account's insurance balance. Amt Due �The amount due on the account. Prepay �Any amounts prepaid against expected insurance. Current �Portion of balance current with the individual provider. 30 Days, 60 Days, 90 Days �Portion of the balance outstanding 30, 60, or 90 days with the individual provider. Date LP �Date of last personal payment posted for the individual provider (codes 1.00-20.89). The LAST PAYMENT field in the Account Information window is updated for personal payments (codes 1.00, 3.00- 20.89). Amount LP �Amount of last payment (codes 1.00-20.89). Bdgt Pln/CC Plan �Total of account budget plan/credit card payment plan balance. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Account Aging
topics.body: Understanding Account Aging Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Understanding Account Aging The current, 30-, 60-, and 90-day aging amounts for patients and accounts a re automatically calculated by the software. These aging amounts are used in printing billing charges, finance charges, and messages to overdue accounts on statements. Account aging is based on all transactions in the account. Patient aging is based on transactions assigned to the patient. The patient/account balance consists of any charges that remain after all credits have been applied. Payments are applied and aging is calculated in the following order: Insurance checks received with an assigned claim number : Used to pay off the deferred insurance portion of the charges on the claim. Any check overpayments are applied to the patient portion of the claim charges. The oldest charges on the claim are paid first. If the insurance check is less than the deferred amount, the difference is added to the account�s amount due. The account�s amount due begins to age on the claim�s completion date. All credits : Applied toward the oldest account balance. When posting personal checks : The deferred insurance portion of outstanding claim charges is always held current and always paid off last. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Account Budget Plan List Window Options
topics.body: Using Account Budget Plan List Window Options Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans Using Account Budget Plan List Window Options The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: Coupons : Prints payment coupons for a fixed loan or patient budget plan. This command is enabled only when a fixed or patient budget plan is displayed in the window. Truth in Lending : Prints the federally mandated loan agreement specification for a fixed loan budget plan. Interest Disclosure : Prints the federally mandated interest disclosure form for a revolving budget plan. Compression : Compresses any budget plan transactions after the balance has been paid. This information is stored with other compressed account information. You can view this information from the Account Transactions History window. Print Transactions : Displays the Transactions List Report Setup window. Use this command to print the transactions posted to the budget plan with a specific date range. Print Credit Card Receipt : This command is enabled only if you are Using ePayments . View Credit Card Number : This command is enabled only if you are Using ePayments to handle credit card payments. Terms : Use this command to edit the loan terms for a revolving account budget plan. You can use this command only when you initially set up a fixed loan budget plan. A message that asks you to adjust the existing balance and create a new budget plan that includes the outstanding balance is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Account Balances for a Revolving Credit Budget Plan
topics.body: Setting Up Account Balances for a Revolving Credit Budget Plan Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans Setting Up Account Balances for a Revolving Credit Budget Plan Using a revolving credit plan enables you to define: Credit limit Interest rate What fraction of the balance is due each month Before using the revolving credit budget plan, you must: Check with the state�s Dental/Consumer Credit Board to verify that this credit plan is in accordance with state law Verify the state�s requirements for the monthly interest charge revolving credit budget plans After you have the information from your state, you must enable the revolving credit feature. Related Topics Enabling the Revolving Credit Plan Feature Transferring an Account Balance to a Revolving Credit Budget Plan Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Assigning a Discount to an Account
topics.body: Assigning a Discount to an Account Posting Payments > Handling Discounts Assigning a Discount to an Account When you assign a discount to an account and post a discountable procedure to any patient in the account, the discount is calculated. You can assign any discount to an account. To assign a discount to an account: In the Account List window, select the account, and select Discount from the Options section of the Express bar or Options bar. The Select Discount window is displayed. Select the discount, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Accountant's Earnings Report
topics.body: Running the Accountant's Earnings Report Handling Reports > Accounting Reports Running the Accountant's Earnings Report The Accountant's Earnings report shows a breakdown of transaction codes posted. Using this information, SoftDent calculates your net collections, net productions, net charges, tax, and the expected income for the specified time period. To run the Accountant's Earnings report: Select Reports > Accounting > Accountant's Earnings . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Accountant's Earnings Report window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows in the Starting and Ending fields to select the report dates from a calendar. In the Provider section, click the drop-down arrow to select the provider number from a list, or leave the first provider set to 999 to report on all providers. Click OK . Additional Details: The first section of the report lists all transactions posted for the specified period, categorized by code number. It includes the codes, descriptions, number of times posted, and dollar total. The second section of the report lists net collection and net production totals for the specified period, along with a tax summary. The tax summary only prints if you have tax enabled in the system settings, and it differs depending on the tax method your practice uses. This report includes every transaction in the TRANS.DAT file, including = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('Dangl.htm');return false;">dangling transactions. Report Elements NET COLLECTIONS = Total Collections + Increase Collections - Decrease Collections Total Collections = Total dollar amount posted to collections (codes 1.00-20.89). Adjust (Increase Collections) = Total dollar amount posted to adjustment codes 21.00-29.89, 96.00-96.89. Adjust (Decrease Collections) = Total dollar amount posted to adjustment codes 30.00-39.89, 98.00-98.89. NET PRODUCTION/CHARGES = Total Production + Total Charges + Increase Production/Charges - Decrease Production/ Charges Total Production = Total dollar amount posted to production codes 100.00+. Total Charges = Total dollar amount posted to charge codes 61.00-95.89, 97.00-97.89, 99.00-99.89. Adjust (Increase Prod/Charges) = Total dollar amount posted to adjustment codes 40.00-49.89. Adjust (Decrease Prod/Charges) = Total dollar amount posted to adjustment codes 50.00-60.89. TAX SUMMARY - Tax Method 1 (based on collections) Tax on Collections = Tax on collections posted (codes 1.00-29.89, 96.00-96.89). Tax Credited back from adjustments = Tax credited on adjustments posted (codes 30.00-39.89, 98.00-98.89). Tax Liability for this period = Tax on Collections - Tax Credited back from adjustments. TAX SUMMARY - Tax Method 2 (based on productions) Tax Charged on Services = Tax charged on codes 40.00-49.89, 60.00-99999.89. Tax Credited back from adjustments = Tax credited on adjustments posted (codes 50.00-59.89). Tax Liability = Tax Charged - Tax Credited back from adjustments. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Account Guarantors
topics.body: Understanding Account Guarantors Setting Up Accounts Understanding Account Guarantors Each account must have at least one person responsible for payment. Guarantor 1 is the default responsible party for the account : Information for the responsible party must be entered on the Guarantor 1 tab of the Account window. This person does not have to be insured or be a patient in your practice. His or her sole function is to be responsible for account payment. Up to four account guarantors can be designated for each account. The billing statement is normally sent to Guarantor 1. The second, third, and fourth guarantors : Used when alternative insurance is available. The following list provides some guidelines when assigning a Guarantor 1 to an account: One patient on account : This individual is most often the Guarantor 1. If the patient is a minor or incapacitated, the Guarantor 1 can be the legal guardian. One insured patient on multi-patient account : The insured individual is typically Guarantor 1 for the account. Two to four insured patients on an account : The individual who wants to receive the bills is typically Guarantor 1. The remaining parties can be assigned Guarantor 2 through Guarantor 4. You can also indicate primary or secondary insurance on the Insurance tab of the Patient window. No Insured Patients On An Account : The individual who wants to receive the bills is typically Guarantor 1. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Activating the Voice Activation Module
topics.body: Activating the Voice Activation Module Periodontal Charting > Using the Voice Activation Module Activating the Voice Activation Module To activate the voice activation module: Obtain an activation code from your SoftDent Support or Sales representative. Select Help > About SoftDent Software . Select Enter Activation Code . Type the activation code in the displayed field and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Account List by ID Report
topics.body: Running the Account List by ID Report Setting Up Accounts > Running Account Reports Running the Account List by ID Report The Account List by ID Report generates an account list sorted by account ID. Information on this list includes account guarantor one's home phone number, current balance, and whether the account guarantor has insurance. Sample Report To run the Account List by ID report: Select Reports > Account > ID Order . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The List Accounts By ID window is displayed. In the Account IDs section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending account IDs you want in the report. Select Include Inactive Accts to include inactive accounts in the report. In the Account User Codes section, type the user codes you want to include or exclude from the report. If you selected the Merge Letter Expert output option, the Letter Expert Template ID field is active. Click the drop-down arrow to select a template from the list. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Account List and Account Window Options
topics.body: Using Account List and Account Window Options Setting Up Accounts Using Account List and Account Window Options The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: Patients : Edits account patient records. Transactions : Views the transaction history. Documents : Views existing or captures new documents. Payments : Views a list of account payments. CareCredit Integration : Access the CareCredit integration to perform account lookups, complete online applications, and use a payment calculator. eReminders Settings: Displays the permitted reminder delivery methods for the responsible party. This command is enabled only if you have enrolled in the eReminders service. Payoff Amount Due : Posts a check payment equal to the amount due. Billing Statement : Prints a single billing statement or adds the billing statement to the list of batched statements that can be printed at your convenience. Letters and Labels > Letter Expert : Merges account information to a letter template. Letters and Labels > Account Information : Prints the Account Details report and Transactions List report for the selected account record. After the account details are printed, you can print a list of transactions for the selected account. Letters and Labels > Overdue Letter : Prints an overdue payment letter for the selected account. Letters and Labels > Labels : Prints a label or laser envelope for the selected account. History : Creates a history file using your word processing software. A history file enables you to enter more extensive notes about the selected account. WalkOut : Prints a walkout statement listing all procedures performed today for all patients attached to the selected account. Completed Claims : Views account claims. Insurance Rollover : Resets used insurance benefits to zero. Contacts : Adds, edits, or deletes account contact information that your office has for the selected account; adds, edits, or deletes HIPAA contacts. Auto Dialer (0) : Dials an account phone number. If you do not have a modem, the account phone numbers are displayed for your reference. TeamTalk : Adds, edits, and deletes TeamTalk notes. Account Budget Plan (Ctrl + G) : Views account budget plan information. Credit Card Payment Plan: Displays the credit card payment plan window for the selected account. Transfer to BP (Z) : Transfers any or all of the account balance to a fixed loan or revolving credit account budget plan. Discount : Applies a discount. Validate Balance : Validates balance and fixes any balance mismatches. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Account List by Zip Code Report
topics.body: Running the Account List by Zip Code Report Setting Up Accounts > Running Account Reports Running the Account List by Zip Code Report The Account List by Zip Code report lists accounts sorted alphabetically by the account guarantor's zip code. Information on this list includes account guarantor one's home phone number, current balance, and whether the guarantor has insurance. Sample Report To run the Account List by Zip Code report: Select Reports > Account > Zip Code Order . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The List Accounts By Zip Code window is displayed. In the Account Zip Codes section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending zip codes you want in the report. Select Include Inactive Accts to include inactive accounts in the report. In the Account User Codes section, type the user codes you want to include or exclude from the report. If you selected the Merge Letter Expert output option, the Letter Expert Template ID field is active. Click the drop-down arrow to select a template from the list. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Account Budget Plans
topics.body: Handling Account Budget Plans Budget Planning Handling Account Budget Plans Using an account budget plan, you can transfer any part of the account balance (minus any insurable amounts) to a fixed revolving loan budget plan. Charges posted to the Patient Transactions or Account Transactions window can be adjusted and transferred to the Account Budget Plan window. This amount is kept as a separate balance. Each account can have only one budget plan : This plan can be applied to one or all patients in the account. When an account budget plan is set up, however, you cannot transfer any additional portions of the patient or account balance to the budget plan. In addition to receiving a monthly statement, accounts with budget plans receive a separate budget plan statement : If you do not want accounts with fixed loan budget plans to receive this separate statement, enter a tilde (~) symbol in the User Codes field in the common area of the Account window. Send budget plan statements in addition to regular monthly statements to update the account's budget plan status. Budget plan interest charges : Interest charges are added to the account when statements are run. Statements should be run at least once a month, since this enables the software to handle overpayments. Any extra payments posted to an account reduce the: Balance Following month's interest charge Related Topics Using Account Budget Plan List Window Options Selecting Budget Plan Coupon Formats Transferring Account Balances to a Fixed Loan Budget Plan Setting Up Account Balances for a Revolving Credit Budget Plan Posting Account Budget Plan Payments Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Account Budget Plans Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

To view a list of patient transactions : In the Patient List window, select a patient and click Transactions . The account or patient Transactions List window is displayed. topics.title: Understanding Account and Patient Transactions
topics.body: Understanding Account and Patient Transactions Handling Transactions and Codes Understanding Account and Patient Transactions To keep your billing accurate and current: Post transactions as they are performed. With few exceptions, post all transactions to the patient�s Transactions window. Post only billing and finance charges to the account�s Transactions window. Need more information? topics.title: Understanding the Account and Patient Transactions List Windows
topics.body: Understanding the Account and Patient Transactions List Windows Handling Transactions and Codes Understanding the Account and Patient Transactions List Windows The patient or account transaction details are displayed in the middle section of the account or patient Transactions List windows. The columns of the account or patient Transactions List windows are: Date : Date the transaction was posted. ID : Patient identification number to which the procedure was posted. Code : ADA, CPT, or transaction code identification number. T# : Affected tooth number. SU/QD : Affected surface or quadrant number. D$ : Provider who is credited for the collections. DR : Provider who is credited for the production. Description : Posted procedure description. Charge : Total charges of the posted procedure. Debits to the account are displayed in blue. Credit : Any credit or payment posted to the selected account or patient record. Credits to the account are displayed in red. Balance : Total balance due for the selected patient or account. Related Topics U sing Patient Transactions List Window Options Using Account Transactions List Window Options Accessing Account and Patient Transactions Need more information? topics.title: Account Reports
topics.body: Account Reports What's New in Version 14.0 > Reports Account Reports Account reports have received enhancements in the following areas: Account Reports Export to Excel The following account reports can now be exported to Excel: Alphabetical Account List Accounts By ID Accounts By ZIP Insured Accounts Uninsured Accounts In addition, you can specify how you want the information exported to Excel: Print Raw Data �Exports the report data to a tab in Excel where each piece of data in the report is in a separate column, enabling you to further manipulate the data using Excel. The report information is displayed on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data �Exports the report data to a tab in Excel where the report data is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer. The report information is displayed on the Formatted tab. You can also select both options. See Running Account Reports for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Accounts
topics.body: Setting Up Accounts Setting Up Accounts The following information is maintained for each account: Billing Insurance Every patient must be assigned to an account, and an account must exist before adding a patient. Transactions are: Posted to individual patients Billed to an account More than one patient can belong to an account, and the account balance is the sum of the individual patient transactions for the account. Related Topics Understanding Account Guarantors Understanding User Codes Using Account List and Account Window Options Adding New Accounts Adding New Accounts from the Scheduler Windows Editing Accounts Deleting or Deactivating Accounts Running Account Reports Need more information? topics.title: Using Account Transactions List Window Options
topics.body: Using Account Transactions List Window Options Handling Transactions and Codes > Understanding the Account and Patient Transactions List Windows Using Account Transactions List Window Options The account Transactions List window options are: Outstanding Claims: Displays a list of open claims for the transaction. Claim Status : Displays the claim status for the transaction. CareCredit Integration: Access the CareCredit integration to perform account lookups, complete online applications, use a payment calculator, accept payments, enter adjustments, reprint a receipt, or view the payment plan. View Claim for Transaction : Edits, reprints, and resubmits primary and secondary claim information. Payments: Displays a list of account payments. Documents : Displays documents attached to the claim. Completed Claims : Displays a list of completed claims for the transaction. Print Transactions : Prints a list of account transactions posted within a specific date range. Walkout Statement : Prints a walkout statement. Credit Card Receipt : Displays or prints a credit card receipt. To use this feature, you must purchase ePayments. Print Signature Form : Processes credit card payments and prints the Signature on File form. To use this feature, you must purchase ePayments. View Credit Card Number : Displays credit card information. To use this feature, you must purchase ePayments. X-fer to Account Budget Plan : Transfers any part of the account balance to a fixed or revolving loan budget plan. Budget Plan : Displays the account budget plan. Compression : Compresses transactions to increase file space. Only use this option if the selected patient has more than 400 transactions. All transactions posted within a selected date range are replaced with a compression debit or credit balance entry. Transaction History : Displays compressed patient transactions. Zoom: Displays the transaction details. Clinical Notes : Adds a clinical note or displays an existing clinical note for the account. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Account User Codes
topics.body: Using Account User Codes Setting Up Accounts > Understanding User Codes Using Account User Codes Use account user codes to group accounts together according to specific criteria. There are nine defined account user codes Example : Type I to indicate the account as inactive. You can run reports including or excluding inactive accounts. The account user codes are: C : The account has been sent to a collection agency, no billing statement is printed for this account, and it is not included in the Account Aging report. I : The account is inactive, no billing statement is printed for the account, and it is not be included in the Account Aging report. L : The account is involved in a legal case, no billing statement is printed for the account, and it is not included in the Account Aging report. M : An additional message (entered in the Notes field in the common area of the Account window) prints in addition to any dunning messages on laser billing statements. This information replaces running messages on preprinted statements. N : The account is not included when the current billing statement is generated. X : The account's insurance estimation is disabled. Y : The account billing statement does not include dunning messages. Note : This applies to automated/electronic statements only and does not affect paper billing statements. Z : The account is not charged finance or billing charges. F3: The account will accrue finance charges after it is 30 days past due. F6: The account will accrue finance charges after it is 60 days past due. F9: The account will accrue finance charges after it is 90 days past due. ~ (Tilde) : Prevents an account with a fixed loan budget plan from receiving a budget plan statement. T o view a printable job aid that lists the default account and finance charge codes, and gives you a place to record user-defined codes for your practice, see Account User Codes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Specific ADA Codes for Use with ePostings
topics.body: Specific ADA Codes for Use with ePostings eServices > Using the ePostings Service Specific ADA Codes for Use with ePostings Several ADA codes are designated specifically for use with ePostings. These codes include: ePostings Reversal�31.92 ePostings Payment by Check�2.92 ePostings Payment by EFT�2.93 ePostings Write-off�50.92 These codes are displayed on the Default ADA tab of the Practice Information window. To access this window, select File > Practice and click the Default ADA tab. See Entering Practice Information for more details. These codes are automatically added to the database when you load the latest version of SoftDent. If you were using one of these codes for another reason, that code is not overwritten, and you must manually update the Practice Information window with the code. Your eServices representative will assist you in verifying that these codes are set up properly. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using ADA/CPT/Transaction Code Window Options
topics.body: Using ADA/CPT/Transaction Code Window Options Handling Transactions and Codes > Understanding Transaction and Procedure Code Ranges Using ADA/CPT/Transaction Code Window Options The following options are displayed in the Options menu for the ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window: Select Fee Schedule: To display a list of codes containing a specific fee schedule, select Select Fee Schedule . Change Color : If your practice uses color scheduling, select Change Color to change the color group for the code. The Select a Scheduling Color window is displayed. Reset Color : If your practice uses color scheduling, select Reset Color to reset the color for the selected code to the Daybook color. Generic Notes: Select Generic Notes to display the Generic Clinical Notes window, which contains a list of all generic and default clinical notes associated with a particular code, sorted by provider ID. For more information, see Setting Up Color and Goal Scheduling . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding New Patients from the Account Window
topics.body: Adding New Patients from the Account Window Setting Up Patients > Adding Patient Records Adding New Patients from the Account Window If you are adding a new patient and new account, you must first set up the account. After you create the account, you can associate it with the new patient. For more information, see Adding New Accounts . To add patients from an account window: Select an option: After you type the information in the Account window for the new account record : Select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. All patients assigned to the account are displayed in the Patient List window. Click OK . The Patient List window is displayed. From an existing account record : Select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. All patients assigned to the account are displayed in the Patient List window. Click Add . The Patient window is displayed. Follow steps number 3 through 29 from Adding New Patients from the Patient Windows . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Saving Presentations as ADA Procedure Presentations
topics.body: Saving Presentations as ADA Procedure Presentations Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint Saving Presentations as ADA Procedure Presentations To save a displayed presentation as an ADA procedure presentation: While viewing the presentation, select SoftDent Case Presentation Files > Save Current Presentation > ADA Procedure Presentation . The Save As ADA Procedure Presentation window is displayed. Type a presentation name in the Enter a filename for the ADA procedure presentation field and click OK . A message that informs you that the presentation is saved and asks you whether to link the presentation with an ADA code is displayed. Select an option: To link the presentation with an ADA code : Click Yes . The Specify the ADA Codes that you would like to associate with the presentation window is displayed. Select the appropriate codes and click Save Links . A confirmation message is displayed. Click OK . To save the presentation without linking it to an ADA code : Click No . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Linking ADA Codes to Presentation Files
topics.body: Linking ADA Codes to Presentation Files Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module Linking ADA Codes to Presentation Files A default set of presentations is included when the software is installed. You can link ADA codes to presentation files in SoftDent or in PowerPoint. To link a code with a presentation file in PowerPoint, see Saving Presentations as ADA Procedure Presentations . To link an existing presentation with a procedure code in SoftDent: Select an option: Select List > ADA Codes . From any window, press Ctrl + C. Select ADA Code List Window from the Power bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. The ADA/Transaction Codes List window is displayed. Select the code to associate with a case presentation and click Edit . The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window is displayed. Click the Presentation Options tab. The tab is the active window. Select the Presentation File drop-down list. The list of procedure presentations are displayed in the Select a Presentation window. Select the presentation from the list and click OK . The Presentation Options tab is displayed. Click OK and Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Using ADA/Transaction Code List Window Options Handling Transactions and Codes > Understanding Transaction and Procedure Code Ranges Using ADA/Transaction Code List Window Options The following options are displayed in the Options menu for the ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window: Select Fee Schedule: To display a list of codes containing a specific fee schedule, select Select Fee Schedule . Change Color : If your practice uses color scheduling, select Change Color to change the color group for the code. The Select a Scheduling Color window is displayed. Reset Color : If your practice uses color scheduling, select Reset Color to reset the color for the selected code to the Daybook color. Generic Notes: Select Generic Notes to display the Generic Clinical Notes window, which contains a list of all generic and default clinical notes associated with a particular code, sorted by provider ID. topics.title: Adding Bluebook Entries
topics.body: Adding Bluebook Entries Setting Up Insurance > Using the Insurance Bluebook Adding Bluebook Entries To add a new bluebook entry for an insurance plan: Select List > Bluebook . The Bluebook entries for Plan window is displayed. Select a plan from the Plan drop-down list. In the Insurance Plan List window : Select an insurance plan and select Bluebook from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Insurance Plan window : Select Bluebook from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Bluebook entries for window is displayed. Click Add . The Bluebook entries for window is displayed in ADD mode. The plan number, plan name, and office identification number are displayed at the top of the window. Select a code and press Tab. The code's fee schedule information or insurance plan's coverage information is displayed. To modify the information that is displayed in any of the fields, see Editing Bluebook Entries . Type any notes in the Notes field. If an appointment is made for this procedure and for a patient covered by this plan, the note is displayed in the Assistant. To set age, waiting period, or tooth limitation, click the Limitations tab. The Limitations tab is displayed. Select an option: To set an age before and after which the procedure is not performed : Select Age Limit , select the age restriction, and type the age in the Patient must be _ years old to receive this service field. To set a waiting period before which the procedure should not be performed : Select Wait Period and type the month in the field. To restrict the procedure to a specific tooth or teeth : Select Tooth Limitation and click Select Teeth . The Teeth Covered by Insurance window is displayed. Select the teeth and click OK . Click OK . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + B to access the Bluebook entries for Plan window. In the Insurance Plan List or Insurance Plan windows, select Bluebook to display the Bluebook entries for window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Clinical Notes at the End of the Day
topics.body: Adding Clinical Notes at the End of the Day Working with Clinical Notes Adding Clinical Notes at the End of the Day If you prefer not to enter clinical notes at the time of the procedure , y ou can enter them later using the End of the Day clinical notes feature. To configure the software to enable you to add clinical notes to all charts at the end of the day, select Add Clinical Notes at End Of Day in the Chart Options Properties window. See Setting Charting Options and Properties . To post an end of day clinical note regardless of how you have configured the software, select List > Post End of Day Notes . To add clinical notes at the end of the day: Chart and post a procedure. Any time after posting the procedure, select an option: While on the Restorative, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tab in the chart : Click Review end of day notes from the Common toolbar . While on any tab in the chart : Select Clinical Notes > Post End Of Day Notes . OR S elect List > Post End of Day Notes . The Work Performed window is displayed. All actual and treatment plan procedures for the current date and providers that do not have notes are displayed in the list. To display procedures for a previous date, click the drop-down arrow in the Work Date field and select the date. Select an option: To display a list of current and treatment plan work that does not have a note associated with it : Select Current and Treatment Plan Work Without Notes Assigned . To display a list of current and treatment plan work that has notes associated with it : Select Current and Treatment Plan Work that has had Notes Assigned . To display current and treatment plan work for a specific provider : Select the provider from the Provider drop-down list. Select the procedure and click Add/Edit Note . The Patient Clinical Notes window is displayed. Type a brief description of the note's contents in the Re field. The text entered in this field is displayed in all clinical notes list windows. Complete the following information: Select the procedure code to which the note refers from the ADA Code drop-down list. You can select only a production or explosion code from the list. Select the condition from the drop-down list. Type the tooth and surface information in the fields, or click the drop-down arrows to select the information. Select the type of watch from the drop-down list. Select the provider from the list. The provider does not affect the chart. Select Reviewed by Dr. to indicate the doctor has seen this note. Type text in the Note field. If you have the proper security permissions, you can edit this note after it is saved until the end of the day. After that time period, you can only amend the note. To use this note as a default when posting the procedure, check Use this text as the default for this ADA Code . Click OK . The current date is added at the end of the note. If no generic note is associated with the procedure code for the provider, a message is displayed. Click Yes to save the note as the generic note for the procedure. Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Clinical Notes Per Visit
topics.body: Adding Clinical Notes Per Visit Working with Clinical Notes Adding Clinical Notes Per Visit You can enter all of the clinical notes associated with a visit at one time�when you close the patient chart�instead of entering a note after you post each procedure. To configure the software to enable you to add per-visit clinical notes to a chart, select Add Clinical Notes Per Visit and Add Clinical Notes While Posting in the Chart Options Properties window. See Setting Charting Options and Properties . To add all of the clinical notes for a visit after posting the procedure: Post all procedures as you typically would and close the chart. The Add Clinical Notes For Visit window is displayed. More: Each procedure checkbox is selected by default, unless the ADA code is not required to have a clinical note. (An ADA code is not required to have a note if you have selected the Never add a note option in the ADA/Transaction Codes window.) If the code is set to Automatically add the default note or Manually add a note , and a default note exists for either all providers or the production provider for that code, the note is displayed in the Default Note column. If no default note exists, the column displays a NO NOTE DEFINED - Doctor must add manually message. Select the procedures to which you want to add clinical notes for this visit. Click OK . The Patient Clinical Notes window is displayed. Select an option: Add a clinical note by highlighting the << Default Note not found. Please add a note here. >> text and typing the note. Add to an existing note by typing in the area below the Tth-Surf/Quad line. Use the scroll bar to view all of the procedures for the appointment. Click OK . Related Topics Adding Clinical Notes Per Visit at the End of the Day Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Clinical Notes Per Visit at the End of the Day
topics.body: Adding Clinical Notes Per Visit at the End of the Day Working with Clinical Notes Adding Clinical Notes Per Visit at the End of the Day You can enter clinical notes that are grouped by visit at the end of the day, instead of adding the notes after posting the procedures. To configure the software to enable you to add per-visit clinical notes to all charts at the end of the day, select Add Clinical Notes Per Visit and Add Clinical Notes at End Of Day in the Chart Properties window. See Setting Charting Options and Properties . To add clinical notes by visit at the end of the day: Chart and post a procedure. Any time after posting the procedure, select an option: While on the Restorative, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tab in the chart: Click Review end of day notes from the Common toolbar . W hile on any tab in the chart : Select Clinical Notes > Post End Of Day Notes . OR S elect List > Clinical Notes > Post End of Day Notes . The Add Clinical Notes for Visits window is displayed. Select an option: To display a list of visits that do not have notes associated with them : Select Current Visits Without Notes Assigned . To display a list of visits that have notes associated with them : Select Current Visits that have had Notes Assigned . Select a patient and click Add/Edit Note . The Patient Clinical Notes window is displayed. Select an option: Add a clinical note by highlighting the << Default Note not found. Please add a note here. >> text and typing the note. Edit an existing note by typing in the area below the Tth-Surf/Quad line. You must have the appropriate security permissions to edit a clinical note. See Amending Patient Clinical Notes and Editing Patient Clinical Notes for more information. To view a patient's chart, select a patient and click View Chart . The patient's chart is displayed. You must close the chart before you can return to the Add Clinical Notes for Visits window. Click OK . Related Topics Adding Clinical Notes Per Visit Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Contact Notes
topics.body: Adding Contact Notes Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert Adding Contact Notes To speed the contact process, you can define up to 10 contact notes. You can select these predefined notes from the contact expert instead of typing each note individually. To add a note to a contact: In the Contact Expert window, select a record from the list in the left pane. Select a predefined note from the drop-down list in the Notes section. For more information, see Setting Up Contact Notes . Type a note in the Notes field. Click Log Contact and Remove . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes
topics.body: Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes Handling Transactions and Codes Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes The fields displayed in this window are code-type specific. Scheduling, insurance submission, and goal tracking fields are active for procedure codes, while the description and effect on balances are displayed for payment and adjustment codes. If you use the charting module, select the correct tooth and surface for each procedure code. You must set the Tax Method field in the General System Settings window ( System > Change System Settings > General System ) before adding a taxable charge. To add an ADA, CPT, or transaction code: Select List > ADA Codes . The ADA/Transaction Codes List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The ADA/Transaction Code window is displayed. Type the new code number in the Code field. The code type is displayed beneath the field. See Understanding Transaction and Procedure Code Ranges for a complete list of codes. To schedule or post transactions using an office code, type an easy-to-remember group of letters and numbers in the Office Code field. Example : When scheduling a patient for a single root canal, type CT1 instead of 3310. You cannot enter identical office codes. TIP: Think of the office codes as a type of shorthand for your office, and make your codes easy to remember. For example: REF for refund, NSF for bounced check, CC for courtesy credit, and so on. Type a description, alternate description, and alternate insurance information. The office description is printed on insurance forms, statements, and reports and is displayed when you schedule appointments and post transactions. The alternate description is printed on walkouts, statements, and treatment plans. When the Alt. Ins Display field is enabled and a medical carrier requires customized coding on claims, type the code. Select the place of service from the Place of Service field. Use this field to assign HCFA codes and descriptions to medical procedures. Your selection is used as the default setting in the Transactions window. Select an option: If you are entering an ADA or CPT code : Select a tooth or surface and quad from the Tooth/Surface/Quad drop-down list. If the new procedure is associated with opening or closing a lab case : Select Open Lab Case or Close Lab Case from the Lab Prep Code drop-down list. To indicate whether the code requires a clinical note: Select from the following: Manually add a note �You are prompted to add a clinical note when you post the code. If you enter clinical notes at the end of the day, the code is displayed in the Work Performed window. Automatically add the default note �You are not prompted to add a note after posting, and the code is not displayed in the Work Performed window. Instead, the default note (which you have previously assigned) is added automatically. If a default note has not been assigned, the software defaults to the process for manually adding a note. Never add a note �You are not prompted to add a note after posting, and the code is not displayed in the Work Performed window. If you are adding an orthodontic code : Select Ortho Code . Each time you post this procedure, you are prompted to create an ortho insurance case. If you are adding a hygiene code (or an explosion code that contains a hygiene code) : Select Hygiene Code . You cannot assign a hygienist to this procedure unless this option is selected. If you are adding a prosthesis-, crown-, or inlay-related code :

topics.title: Adding Scheduling Color Group Definitions
topics.body: Adding Scheduling Color Group Definitions Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Color Scheduling Adding Scheduling Color Group Definitions You can create up to 15 color group definitions based on procedure codes or production amounts. To add a color group: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Last year�s production goal, last year�s production amount, last year�s scheduled production amount, and the current day�s production goal are displayed for each day of the month that the practice is open. This year's production will be one day forward from last year's production, and two days forward in a leap year. Select Set Up Color Groups from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Scheduling Color Definitions window is displayed. The first four color groups are already set up, and you cannot edit the group descriptions or delete these groups. You can change the colors of these reserved groups (except for the Daybook).
