By default, the Daybook color is navy, lunch hours are green, holidays are burgundy, and the open schedule is white. Click Add . A new line is displayed. Type the color group description in the Description field. For example, to create color groups to help you schedule similar procedures together, type Hygiene, Oral surgery, or Crown & Bridge in the field. This title is used to label slots in the Daybook after it is painted. Press Tab and select an option: To color schedule by procedure : Select the ADA or CPT codes from the Min Code and Max Code drop-down lists and press Tab. A message asking you whether to associate a minimum and maximum dollar amount with the code range is displayed. You can only color schedule ADA or CPT codes. ADA codes are installed with the software, but you must set up the CPT codes in the ADA/Transaction Codes window. Any procedural code within the selected range should be scheduled in a time slot identified by this color. To color schedule by procedure and production amount : Select the ADA or CPT codes from the Min Code and Max Code drop-down lists and press Tab. A message asking you whether to assign a minimum and maximum dollar amount with the code range is displayed. To color schedule by production amount : Press Tab twice and do not select any codes from the Min Code and Max Code drop-down lists. Skip to number 7. Select an option: To color schedule by procedure : Click No . Press Tab twice. Skip to number 8. To color schedule by procedure and production amount : Click Yes . Select an option: To color schedule by procedure and production amount : Type the dollar amounts in the Min$ and Max$ fields, and press Tab. Procedures in the specified procedural range and dollar range should be scheduled with this color. For example, a color group could consist of procedure codes 1000�1999 and with charges between $50�$100. To color schedule by production amount : Type the dollar amounts in the Min$ and Max$ fields, and press Tab. Procedures in the specified dollar range should be scheduled with this color. For example, color group 1 contains all procedures with charges $1.00�99.99, and color group 2 contains all procedures with charges $100.00�299.99. Double-click the Color column. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . The Scheduling Color Definitions window is displayed. Click OK . Click Close . A message asking you to confirm the changes to the color table is displayed. Click Yes . Need more informatio

topics.title: Adding an Insurance Company
topics.body: Adding an Insurance Company Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Company Information Adding an Insurance Company To add an insurance company: Select List > Ins. Co . The Insurance Co List window is displayed. Press Enter. Click Add . The Insurance Co window is displayed. Type the insurance company name, address, and phone numbers. You can enter two-line company addresses; however, only the Rev. 90 ADA and laser forms can accommodate two-line addresses. If you subscribe to the Trojan service, select a Trojan carrier identification number from the Trojan ID drop-down list. If you are enrolled with the NEA FastAttach service, select a payer from the Payer drop-down list. If you submit claims electronically through eClaims, select the payer type, insurance payer identification number, and Provider/Practice identification numbers from the drop-down lists. If you have subscribed to ePostings and this insurance company makes payments using ePostings, select ePostings Capable . If you have subscribed to the Real Time Claim Submission service, and this insurance company supports Real Time claims, select Real Time Claims Capable . Type any notes in the Notes field. To associate the new insurance company with a website, type a URL in the Web Link field. To use Web Link, you must use Windows 7 software with Internet Explorer software, version 8 or higher. To associate the new insurance company with a default insurance plan, select the plan from the Default Plan drop-down list. Select the version of diagnosis codes the insurance company accepts (the default is ICD-9). Click Set Up Plans to Use Diagnosis Codes to select the Use Diagnosis Codes option (on the Claim Filing tab of the Insurance Plan window) for all of the plans administered by this insurance company. Click OK and click Close . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + I to access the Insurance Company List window. If the insurance company serves several plans and uses multiple claim mailing addresses, create the company headquarter office and entries for each mailing address. ICD-10 Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Contacts
topics.body: Adding Contacts Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts Adding Contacts You can add contacts t o patient, insurance plan, account, referring doctor, provider, employer, insurance company, school, laboratory, claim, and pharmacy records. To add a contact to a record: Select an option: To add a contact to a patient record : In the Patient List window, select the patient. To add a contact to an insurance plan record : In the Insurance Plan List window, select the plan. To add a contact to an account record : In the Account List window, select the account. To add a contact to a referring doctor record : In the REFERRING DR List window, select the doctor. To add a contact to a provider record : In the Provider List window, select the provider. To add a contact to an employer record : In the Employer List window, select the employer. To add a contact to an insurance company record : In the Insurance Company List window, select the company. To add a contact to a school record : In the School List window, select the school. To add a contact to a laboratory record : In the Labs List window, select the laboratory. To add a contact to a pharmacy record : In the Pharmacy List window, select the pharmacy. To add a contact to a claim record: In the Claim List window, select the claim. To add a contact to a completed claim record: In the Completed Claim List window, select the claim. Select Contacts > By Date or Contacts > By Type from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Contacts for List window is displayed. Click Add . The Contacts for window is displayed. Type the contact description in the Re field. The information entered in the field is displayed in the Description column of the Contacts for List window. Select an option: If you make contact : Select Made from the Status drop-down list and select the method of contact from the By drop-down list. If you do not make contact : Select Need to Contact from the Status drop-down list and select the date by which contact is required from the By drop-down list. If applicable, select the date you expect a response from the Expected by drop-down list. If applicable, select the date the response is received from the Received On drop-down list. Type a contact note or select a default note from the Notes section. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding CPT Codes
topics.body: Adding CPT Codes Medical Cross-Coding Adding CPT Codes Medical procedures are indicated by CPT codes and must be added to the system manually. You can also apply the new CPT code format to standard ADA codes using the software. This format employs the old ADA code number, a prefix of the letter D, and enough zeroes to make five digits on claims. Example : The standard ADA code 110 becomes D0110 on the claim, but the familiar ADA code is displayed. You can customize individual insurance plans to print the CPT format on a specific claim or on claims for a specific insurance company, or you can customize all claims to use the format. For more information, see Handling Insurance Plan Information . CPT codes are used to indicate medical procedures and are numbered 10000.00 through 99999.99. To add a CPT code: Select an option: Select List > ADA Codes . Click ADA Code List Window on the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. In any window, press Ctrl + C. The ADA/Transaction Codes List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The ADA/Transaction Codes window is displayed. Type the code number in the Code field and click OK . Type an office code in the Office Code field. Type a code and alternate description in the fields. The text entered in the Description field is printed on insurance forms, statements, and reports. To print the alternate description on walkouts, statements, and treatment plans : Select System > Change System Settings > General System . The General System Settings window is displayed. Select Printer Preferences from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The System Printing Preferences window is displayed. Select Print second description on walkouts, statements, and treatment plans . When the Alt. Ins Display field is enabled and a medical carrier requires customized coding on claims, type the code. To print alternate insurance information on a claim, see Handling Insurance Plan Information . Select the place of service, type of service, code classification, and default diagnosis codes from the drop-down lists. Select the place of service from the appropriate field. The Place of Service field enables you to assign HCFA codes and descriptions to medical procedures. For more information, see Understanding Account and Patient Transactions . Verify default diagnosis codes before you enter them. If you have Medicaid or Medicare plans that require a special code before the CPT code, type it in the HCPCS Code Prefix field. You must select Use HCPCS Codes in the Insurance Plan Claim Filing window to print this code on a claim. For more information, see Adding Insurance Plans . Select the tooth, surface, and quad from the Tooth/Surface/Quad drop-down list. You must specify the tooth and surface requirement for each procedure code to use the charting module. Select an option: If the new procedure is usually associated with opening or closing a lab case : Select either Open Lab Case or Close Lab Case from the Lab Prep Code drop-down list. If you select Open Lab Case, for example, you are prompted to open a new lab case when you post this procedure for a patient. If you are adding a hygiene code (or an explosion code that contains a hygiene code) : Select Hygiene Code . You cannot assign a hygienist to this procedure unless this checkbox is selected. Select Taxable . You must first set the Tax Method field in the General System Settings window. Select an option: To add a call-back notification

topics.title: Adding Credit Card Payments
topics.body: Adding Credit Card Payments eServices > Using the ePayments Service Adding Credit Card Payments To add credit card payments: From the SoftDent main menu, select List > Account . The Accounts List window is displayed. Select the account and select Payment from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Account Payments window is displayed. Select Personal . The Payments - Personal Payment window is displayed. Select the payment code from the Code drop-down list. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Click Post . The Carestream ePayments Charge Authorization window is displayed. Swipe the card, or manually enter the information and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Documents to the Review Queue
topics.body: Adding Documents to the Review Queue Using the Document Manager > Reviewing Documents Adding Documents to the Review Queue To add a document to the Review Queue: Select a record in the list window, and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar. The Documents for window is displayed. Select the document to be reviewed and select Review . The Document Review window is displayed. To add a reviewer, select the name in the Users Available To Review list and click Add . To remove a reviewer, select the name in the Users Assigned To Review list and click Remove . To add a document note, type the text in the Current Note field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a Clinical Condition
topics.body: Adding a Clinical Condition Restorative Charting > Using Clinical Conditions Adding a Clinical Condition To add a user-defined clinical condition: Select List > Clinical Conditions . The Clinical Conditions List window is displayed. Click Add . The Clinical Conditions window is displayed. Type the condition name and description. If a tooth, surface, or quadrant is required, select it from the Tooth/Surface/Quad drop-down list. Click OK . You cannot delete clinical conditions. Mark unused conditions inactive. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Address Commands
topics.body: Address Commands Address Commands The commands are case sensitive and must be entered on the Palm organizer or in the Microsoft Outlook software in all capital letters. Address Commands Command Function SD Address Command (from the Address Applications on the Palm Organizer or the Microsoft Outlook Software Contact Category) PC SoftDent Software Output To remind you to make an appointment for a contact \SDA [LAST NAME] (Enter this command in the last name field for a contact.) [ NOTE ] Tip: To help with scheduling, describe the appointment in the note field on the Palm organizer Places a reminder memo in the memo list that specifies an appointment should be made for the contact. The memo displays in blue. To remind you to call a contact \SDC [LAST NAME] (Enter this command in the last name field for a contact.) [ NOTE ] Tip: To help with contact processing, describe the nature of the call in the note field on the Palm organizer. Places a memo in the memo list that specifies a call should be made to the specified contact. The memo displays in blue. To update the patient or referring doctor�s phone and address information \SDU [LAST NAME] (Enter this command in the patient or referring doctor last name field after making changes to the record.) [ ADDRESS OR TELEPHONE NUMBER UPDATE ON LIST ENTRY ] (Enter telephone number or address changes for the patient or referring doctor record.) Important: Phone information must be entered on the Palm organizer in the following manner: (555) 555-1212 x123. The area code must be included within the parentheses, the main phone number within the dash, and the extension after the "x" in order for the PC SoftDent software to read the information correctly. Updates the following fields on the referring doctor or patient record: � street address � zip code � state � city � phone numbers To create a refill record for the existing Rx record \SDR [RX RECORD] (Enter this command in the last name field for a patient in the Recent Rx category.) Places a refill in the patient�s prescription information for an existing prescription. Lists the patient�s preferred pharmacy. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a HIPAA Contact
topics.body: Adding a HIPAA Contact System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features > Handling HIPAA Contacts Adding a HIPAA Contact You can track HIPAA consents, authorizations, disclosures, and complaints by adding these contacts to a patient or account record. To add a HIPAA contact: Select an option: In a patient record : Select Contacts > By Date from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In an account record : Select Contacts > By Date from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Contacts for List window is displayed. Click Add . The Contacts for window is displayed. Select the HIPAA category from the drop-down list. Type the information in the Re field. Select the contact status and the means of communication in the appropriate drop-down lists. Type the response information and any notes in the appropriate fields. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding and Editing Time Card Entries
topics.body: Adding and Editing Time Card Entries Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card > Handling Time Card Entries Adding and Editing Time Card Entries To add or edit a Time Card entry: Select List > Employees . The Employees List window is displayed. Select a record and select Time Card . The Time Card List window is displayed. To add a new entry, click Add . The Time Card window is displayed. To edit an entry, double-click the entry. The entry is displayed in the Time Card window. Click the drop-down list in the Type field to display the Time Card Types window, and select a time card type by double-clicking a type in the list. Click the drop-down list in the Start Date and End Date fields to select the dates from a calendar. Type the clock-in and clock-out times. Click OK . The Time Card List window is displayed. Click Close . The Employee List window is displayed. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Additional Enhancements
topics.body: Additional Enhancements What's New in Version 14.0 Additional Enhancements SoftDent, version 14.0, contains the following additional enhancements: SoftDent Charting Module Training Workbook Available from the Start Menu A workbook containing lessons and exercises specifically for the charting module is now available from the Start menu. To access the SoftDent Charting Module Training Workbook, select Start > All Programs > SoftDent > Online Documentation > Charting Workbook . Help Demos Several help demos have been added to this version of the online help. Help demos are short, animated demonstrations which answer the most frequently asked questions our trainers and support team receive. New demos will be added in future releases, as we identify your most-asked questions. See Viewing Help Demos for more information. Referral Reason Section Changed in the Referral Intro Note Quick Letter The Referral Reason section in the Referring Doctor Intro Letter Setup window ( List > Patient > Letters and Labels > Quick Letters > Referral Intro Note ) has been changed from three separate fields to one multi-line field where you can type continuous text. The text in this field automatically wraps as you type and displays in the letter as a paragraph instead of as separate lines of text. In past versions of the software, pressing Enter in the Referral Reason section generated the letter. Now, pressing Enter starts a new line in the letter. There is no limit to the number of separate lines you can create. See Understanding Patient List and Patient Window Options for more information. Lab Technician Added to the Specialty Drop-Down List in the Provider Window A Lab Technician option has been added to the Specialty drop-down list in the Provider window ( List > Provider > double-click a provider ). This option is also available in any window in the software that contains the Specialty drop-down list. The Alphabetical Referring Dr List and the Referring Dr List By ID reports now include the Lab Technician specialty as well. Selecting a Guarantor in CareCredit When you access CareCredit to submit an application and the patient has multiple guarantors, the Guarantor Selection window is now displayed. Select the guarantor and click OK to continue the application process for that guarantor. See Using CareCredit for more information. Number of Broken Appointments Condition Now Available in TeamTalk A new condition�Number of Broken Appointments�has been added to the conditions list in the TeamTalk Setup Wizard window for the Patient database type ( TeamTalk > TeamTalk Setup > Patient > Extra Settings ). When you select Number of Broken Appointments, a window is displayed that enables you to specify the number of broken appointments that must occur before a TeamTalk note is displayed. See Using TeamTalk and Patient Database TeamTalk Conditions for more information. Broken Appointment Field Added to the Recall Tab of the Patient Window A Broken field has been added to the Recall tab of the Patient window ( List > Patient > Double-click patient name > Recall ). Each time a patient's appointment is canceled within the number of days specified in the Scheduler Settings window, the number in the Broken field is increased by one. Tracking Late Appointments in the Tracked Appointments Window W hen you check a patient in after his appointment time and the number of minutes past the appointment has exceeded the allowed amount you have previously set in the Patient Visit

topics.title: Adding an ePostings Adjustment Code
topics.body: Adding an ePostings Adjustment Code eServices > Using the ePostings Service > Using the ePostings Adjustments Window Adding an ePostings Adjustment Code To add an ePostings adjustment code: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > ePostings > ePostings Adjustments . The ePostings Adjustments list window is displayed. Click in the ePostings Adjustments window to activate it. Click Add . The ePostings Adjustments window is displayed. In the Group Code field, click the drop-down arrow and select a code from the drop-down list . CO�Contractual Obligations CR�Corrections and Reversals OA�Other Adjustments PI�Payor Initiated Reductions PR�Patient Responsibility In the Reason Code field, click the drop-down arrow and select a reason code from the list. In the Action field, select the action to be applied. Select Update Bluebook if this code should update the bluebook. Click OK . Click Close . Tip To print the ePostings Adjustment Actions report, click Print , select an output option, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a Topic as a Favorite
topics.body: Adding a Topic as a Favorite Finding Answers > Using Online Help Adding a Topic as a Favorite To create a list of favorites in the online help: In the online help, locate a topic. Click the Favorites tab. The Favorites pane is displayed. Click Add . The title of the topic is displayed in the list as a favorite. Software updates can overwrite favorites. Related Topics Using Online Help Accessing Online Help Searching the Online Help Printing an Online Help Topic Viewing Help Demos Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Address Information
topics.body: Address Information Trojan > Handling Trojan Traditional Plan > Employer Plans Address Information The following fields contain name and address information: Trojan ID : Displays the Employer Plan ID number assigned by Trojan. Update disks use this number to locate the file and update plan information. You cannot edit Trojan plans or their ID numbers. Carrier : Lists the insurance company ID name or number associated with this plan. Name : Displays the employer/company name. Address : Displays the corporate address. Phone : Lists the corporate telephone number. Eligibility Phone : Displays the number to call to verify that your patient is covered by this plan. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a New Report to a Report Group
topics.body: Adding a New Report to a Report Group Handling Reports > Generating Reports Using Report Manager > Setting Up a Report Manager Report Adding a New Report to a Report Group To add a new report to a report group: Select Reports > Report Manager > Advanced Options . The Report Group window is displayed. Select a report group and click Edit Reports . The Report Group Members window is displayed. Click Add . The Previously Set Up Reports window is displayed. Click anywhere on the list of reports and then click Add . The Reports drop-down menu is displayed. Select a report. The Output Options window is displayed. Verify that Printer is selected, and click OK . The report setup window is displayed. Type the information and click OK . The Report Output window is displayed. Type the number of copies you want to print and click Next . The Edit Description window is displayed. Change the report description, if necessary. Click OK . A message is displayed, asking if you want to add this report to the group. Click Yes . Add another report to the group, or close all open windows by clicking Close or by clicking Close All in the General Operations section of the Express Bar. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a Patient Portrait
topics.body: Adding a Patient Portrait Adding Patients > Patient Portraits Adding a Patient Portrait At the patient's first visit, after the account has been activated, you can add a patient portrait. To add a patient portrait: Do one of the following: In the Patient Information window: Click Portrait > Create Portrait from the Options section of the Express Bar. The Patient Portrait window is displayed. In the patient chart: Click the avatar on the upper-left corner of the patient banner, or click the Capture Patient Portrait button on the Common toolbar. The Patient Portrait window is displayed. Click the Import Images from a Digital Camera button. The Digital Camera Images window is displayed. Click Browse , navigate to the folder containing your digital images, and click OK . Select the portrait and click Import Images . Click the X in the upper-right corner of the window to close the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding an Existing Report to a Report Group
topics.body: Adding an Existing Report to a Report Group Handling Reports > Generating Reports Using Report Manager > Setting Up a Report Manager Report Adding an Existing Report to a Report Group To add an existing report to a report group: Select Reports > Report Manager > Advanced Options . The Report Group window is displayed. Select a report group and click Edit Reports . The Report Group Members window is displayed. Click Add . The Previously Set Up Reports window is displayed. Select a report name and click Select . The Report Group Members window is displayed. Add another report to the list, or close all open windows by clicking Close or by clicking Close All in the General Operations section of the Express Bar. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding and Removing SUS Accounts
topics.body: Adding and Removing SUS Accounts Getting Started > Using the Software Update Service (SUS) Adding and Removing SUS Accounts Adding Accounts To add an account to the SUS: Right-click the triangle logo and select Configure Update Service . The Software Update Service � Configure the Software Update Service window is displayed. Click Add Account . The Software Update Service � Carestream Single-Sign-On window is displayed. Type the email address and password, and click Sign In . Click OK . Removing Accounts To remove an account from the SUS: Right-click the triangle logo and select Configure Update Service . The Software Update Service � Configure the Software Update Service window is displayed. Select an account from the list and click Remove Account . Click OK . If you remove all accounts from the list, online updates are disabled. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Clinical Documents
topics.body: Adding Clinical Documents Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Adding Clinical Documents If you need to scan or import clinical images but do not have CS Imaging software, you can scan or import the images using the Capture New Clinical Document button. Images are added to the Secondary Capture Images category in the patient document tree. To add a new clinical document: Select an option: In the Patient List window, select the patient to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the patient chart, click the Documents tab. The Documents for window is displayed. Click Capture New Clinical Image . The Scan or Import Image window is displayed. Do one of the following: To scan a clinical image: Click Scan a Clinical Image . The scanner software window is displayed. Scan the image. After the image is scanned, it is added to the Secondary Capture Images category in the document tree. To import a clinical image: Click Import a Clinical Image . The Import Images window is displayed. Select the image and click Open . After the image is imported, it is added to the Secondary Capture Images category in the document tree. To select a scanner: Click Select Scanner . The scanner software window is displayed. Select the scanner driver and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: ePayments Secure--Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Manually
topics.body: ePayments Secure--Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Manually eServices > ePayments Secure ePayments Secure--Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Manually To enter a payment when the card is not present: In the patient Transactions List window, click Add . The Transaction window is displayed. In the Code field, select the credit card payment code from the drop-down list and press Tab . The Payment window is displayed. In the Amount field, type the amount you want to charge to the credit card, and click OK . The ePayments Secure�Take Payment window is displayed with the amount of the charge and the Device Selected field set to the default device (System Tray App) for this computer. Select Enter Card Information Manually , and then click Submit . The Process Credit Card window is displayed. Enter the requested information and click the Pay $XXX button, which displays the amount of the charge. The ePayments Secure service processes the transmission and returns a "Payment Successfully Completed" notification and an authorization number. Click Finished . The ePayments Secure�Select a Print Device window is displayed. Select the device (System Tray App) if necessary, and click Print . The Payment Receipt window is displayed. Note: Print Receipt: Yes must be selected on the Practice tab of the ePayments Setup window. For more information, see ePayments Secure--Printing Receipts . Select a printer from the drop-down list, and click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: ePayments Secure--Adding Payments to Patient Transactions with the Card Reader
topics.body: ePayments Secure--Adding Payments to Patient Transactions with the Card Reader eServices > ePayments Secure ePayments Secure--Adding Payments to Patient Transactions with the Card Reader To enter a payment with the card reader: In the patient Transactions List window, click Add . The Transaction window is displayed. In the Code field, select the credit card payment code from the drop-down list and press Tab . The Payment window is displayed. In the Amount field, type the amount you want to charge to the credit card, and click OK . The ePayments Secure�Take Payment window is displayed with the amount of the charge and the Device Selected field set to the default device (System Tray App) for this computer. Select Swipe/Insert/Tap Card , engage the card, and then click Submit . OR If the patient�s credit card number has been stored from a previous transaction, select the option to use that card, and then click Submit . The ePayments Secure service processes the transmission and returns a "Payment Successfully Completed" notification and an authorization number. Click Finished . The ePayments Secure�Select a Print Device window is displayed. Select the device (System Tray App) if necessary, and click Print . The Payment Receipt window is displayed. Note: Print Receipt: Yes must be selected on the Practice tab of the ePayments Setup window. For more information, see ePayments Secure--Printing Receipts . Select a printer from the drop-down list, and click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a Discount
topics.body: Adding a Discount Posting Payments > Handling Discounts Adding a Discount To add a discount: Select System > Discount Schedule . The Discount Schedule List window is displayed. Click Add . The Discount Schedule window is displayed. Type a discount name, and select the transaction code number to which the discount will be applied. Select the discount type from the Type drop-down list. In the Value field, type the percentage or dollar amount. Select an option from the Post drop-down list: To create one credit discount transaction per visit : Select Per Visit . To apply a discount transaction for each posted discountable procedure : Select Per Line Item . Do not use this posting method when creating a fixed discount type. Select an option from the Rounding drop-down list: To apply no rounding to the discounted amount : Select None . To round the discounted amount up to the nearest amount : Select Up and select the amount in the Nearest drop-down list. To round the discounted amount down to the nearest amount : Select Down and select the amount in the Nearest drop-down list. To round the discounted amount up or down to the nearest amount : Select Closest and select the amount in the Nearest drop-down list. You can select $1.00, $.50, and $.25 from the Nearest drop-down list. To restrict the discount to a set of procedure codes, select Enable , and select the starting and ending codes from the drop-down lists. Type any discount notes in the Notes field. The notes are not displayed elsewhere in the software. To set this discount as your current special, select set as current . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a New Lab
topics.body: Adding a New Lab Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Labs Adding a New Lab You must enter information for each lab before you can track a lab case. To add a new lab: Select an option: Select List > Labs . Click Lab List Window on the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. In any window, press Ctrl + L. The Labs List window is displayed in FIND mode. Click anywhere in the list area or press Enter or Esc to switch to SCAN mode. Click Add . The Labs window is displayed in ADD mode. Labs and pharmacies are stored in the same database; therefore, the first lab you enter may not have an identification number of 1. Type the name and address of the lab in the fields. If a website is associated with the lab, select it from the list. Windows 95/98 with Internet Explorer 4.0 must be installed to use this field. Type any phone numbers and notes associated with the lab. Click the Contact Info tab. Type the lab contact information in the fields. Type the normal pick-up or drop-off times for the lab in the Pick-Up/Drop-Off Times section. Type the number of days that are usually required by this lab to return a case in the Default Days for Lab Case Return field. The default value is 14. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a Multi-Page Document
topics.body: Adding a Multi-Page Document Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Adding a Multi-Page Document Using a multi-page scanner, you can scan multiple pages into SoftDent without having to scan one page at a time or respond to multiple prompts. After you feed in all of the pages to your multi-page scanner, SoftDent creates a document containing an image of each scanned page. To add a new document: Select an option: To add an account document : In the Account List window, select the account to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. If Statement Archiving is selected in the billing statement system settings, each time the Billing Statement report is generated a copy of the report is saved to each account included in the results and displayed in the Account Archived Document section of the tree in the Documents for window. To add a patient document : In the Patient List window, select the patient to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. OR In the patient chart, click the Documents tab. To add a referring doctor document : In the REFERRING DR List window, select the patient to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to an insurance company record : In the Insurance Co List window, select the company to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to an insurance company plan record : In the Insurance Plan List window, select the plan to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to an insurance claim record : In the Claim List window, select the claim to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to a completed claim record : In the Completed Claim List window, select the claim to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a lab case document : In the Labs List window, select the lab to which the case is to be added and select Multi-Case Document from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Multi Lab Case Capture window is displayed. In this window, you can attach a document to several lab cases. Click Add Lab Case , or select the command from the Options section of the Express bar. The Lab Case List window is displayed. Double-click the lab cases. A message asks you to confirm the lab case addition. Click Yes . The Lab Case List window is displayed. Select Capture Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar. The Documents for window is displayed. Select Capture New Document from the Options section of the Express bar. The Documents for add window is displayed. Type the document�s name in the Document Description field. To use the name as the default for all new documents of the selected type, select Make Default . For more information, see Setting Capture Preferences . The Category field defaults to a category based on where the capture process was started. Example: If you are capturing a new document from the Patient List window, the Category field defaults to

topics.title: Adding a Referring Doctor to a Patient Record
topics.body: Adding a Referring Doctor to a Patient Record Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals Adding a Referring Doctor to a Patient Record To credit an outside doctor with referring a new patient to your practice: From the Patient window of the patient that has been referred, select the doctor from the Doctor drop-down list in the Referred In By section of the Info tab. Type the reason the patient was referred in the For field. Select the date the patient is expected to return to the referring doctor from the Due Back drop-down list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding or Removing eForms
topics.body: Adding or Removing eForms eServices > Using the eForms Module Adding or Removing eForms You can add or remove eForms for a specific appointment. Forms added or deleted here are exceptions to the forms a patient should receive, as specified in the default eForms template settings. Exceptions to the normal qualified eForms are highlighted with either red or green. Red indicates eForms that have been deleted from the list of qualified eForms. Green indicates eForms that have been added to the list of qualified eForms. Light blue indicates a template is selected. To add or remove eForms for a patient: In the Daily Appointments or Book Quick Glance window, right-click on an appointment slot and select eForms > Add/Remove eForms . The Appointment eForms Templates window is displayed. For additional information, see the eForms online help. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering an Address Command on the Palm Organizer
topics.body: Entering an Address Command on the Palm Organizer Using PracticeToGo > Sending Data Back to SoftDent > SoftDent Commands and the Palm Organizer Entering an Address Command on the Palm Organizer Address commands enable you to transfer information from the Palm organizer to the PC SoftDent software in the form of reminder memos, patient or referring doctor record updates, and prescription refill records. To view a list of available Address commands, click = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('AddComPTG.htm');return false;">here . To transfer information to the PC SoftDent software using the Address commands: On the Palm Home screen, tap the SoftDent icon, and then tap Recent Rx , Call Backs , Referring Doctors , or Patients on the SoftDent-Palm application menu. The Address Book is displayed. In the Address List on the Palm organizer, tap the entry you want so that a cursor displays on the desired line. Tap Edit at the bottom of the screen to enter the Address Edit mode. Tap the Last Name field to place the cursor there. Tap the 123 dot in the graffiti writing area underneath the screen. A Number Keyboard is displayed. The cursor is at the beginning of the Last Name field. Begin by entering the \ character (unless you have selected something else as your command character in the Command Tag field in the PracticeToGo User Preferences window in the PC SoftDent software). Next, tap ABC at the bottom of the Keyboard window to open the Letter Keyboard. Enter the rest of the SD Address Command in the Last Name field. Example: Enter \SDU to update a contact record. The command is case sensitive, so it must be entered in all capital letters. The command must also be entered without spacing between the characters. For online tips about ways to reach the onscreen Keyboard, tap the tips icon (the lowercase i) in the upper-right corner of the Keyboard window. When you are finished, tap Done to exit the keyboard window and return to Address Edit mode. To update a contact record (\SDU), you must also enter the new address or telephone information in the contact�s entry. Enter telephone information on the Palm organizer like this: (111) 555-1212 x321. Be aware that you must place the area code within the parentheses, the main phone number must include a dash, and the extension number must come after the x in order for the PC SoftDent software to read the information correctly. Tap Note at the bottom of the screen if you want to view the notes for the entry. To edit the note or place additional information in it, tap the ABC or 123 dot in the graffiti writing area at the bottom of the screen to open either the Letter or Number keyboard, as needed. Place the cursor at the beginning of the text field and enter the desired information. To ensure that the note will not get cut off in the PC SoftDent software, limit the text to two lines. When you are finished, tap Done to exit the keyboard and return to the note field. Tap Done to exit the note field and return to Address Edit mode. Tap Done to return to Address List mode. At the next synchronization, information about the entries you marked with SoftDent commands will be transferred to the PC SoftDent software in the form of memos, referring doctor or patient record updates, and prescription refill records. Memos added to the PC SoftDent software from the Palm organizer display in the Memos list window in blue. Once the informa

topics.title: Adding Prescription Drugs
topics.body: Adding Prescription Drugs Pharmacies and Prescriptions > Handling Prescriptions Adding Prescription Drugs Before using the software to prescribe a drug, you must enter the drug information into the drug database. To add a drug: Select List > Prescription Drugs . The Drug List window is displayed in FIND mode. Click Add . The Drug window is displayed. Type the drug name, prescription, and refill information in the fields. Select an option: To prescribe this drug : Select Prescriptible by this office . If this checkbox is not selected, the drug is used only in medical histories. To trigger a medical alert when prescribing this drug : Select Trigger Medical History/Medical Alert . To print your DEA number on the prescription : Select Print DEA Number . To include additional notes : Type the note in the Additional Note Text field. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Additional Automated Statements Options
topics.body: Additional Automated Statements Options eServices > Using the Automated Statements Service > Using Automated Statements Additional Automated Statements Options You can: Preview Automated Statements Print a Pending Statements report Delete pending Automated Statements Regenerate Automated Statements that have been transmitted Related Topics Previewing and Printing Statements Printing Pending Statements Reports Deleting Pending Statements Regenerating Automated Statements Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Employers
topics.body: Adding Employers Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Employer Information Adding Employers To add a new employer: Select List > Employer . The Employer List window is displayed. Employers are stored in the same database as schools; the first employer might have an identification number greater than 1, and employers subsequently entered might not have sequential identification numbers. Press Enter and click Add . The Employer window is displayed. The employer identification number is displayed at the top of the window. Type the company name, address, and phone numbers. To add contact information to the record, click Contact Info or press Ctrl + Tab to display the Contact Info tab. Type the name, phone numbers, and e-mail addresses for the employer contact person and click OK . Click Close . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + E to access the Employer List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Additional eServices Options
topics.body: Using Additional eServices Options eServices Using Additional eServices Options eServices provide the following additional options: Receive Only Clean Up Old Files Summary Report Update New Plans Update Benefits to Patient Utility Electronically Capable Payer Report Related Topics Using Receive Only Using Clean Up Old Files Using the Summary Report Using Update New Plans Using Update Benefits to Patient Utility Using the Electronically Capable Payer Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Explosion Codes
topics.body: Adding Explosion Codes Handling Transactions and Codes Adding Explosion Codes You can assign as many as six procedure codes to a single code number. This single code is an explosion code. Codes 100000.00 through 100999.89 are reserved for explosion codes. Example : An exam consists of procedure codes for a prophy, two bitewings, and an exam. To save entry time, create a single explosion code that encompasses all three procedures. When posting the explosion code, all three procedures are displayed as separate transactions. You cannot assign a hygienist to the appointment unless Hygiene Code is selected. To create an explosion code: Select List > ADA Codes . The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window is displayed. Type the new code number in the Code field. The code type is displayed beneath the field. See Understanding Transaction and Procedure Code Ranges for a complete list of codes. To schedule or post transactions using an office code, type an easy-to-remember group of letters and numbers in the Office Code field. Type a description, alternate description, and alternate insurance information. If the explosion code includes procedures a hygienist must perform, select Hygiene Code . Click the Scheduler/Fees tab. When adding an explosion code, only the Time Units, Use Scheduler Template, and Code fields are enabled. To use the scheduler template, select Use Scheduler Template . Providers are automatically assigned to the code, and a warning is displayed if there are scheduling conflicts. Type the number of time units required for the coded procedure in the Time Units field, and type the number of real time minutes it takes to complete the coded procedure in the RTU minutes field. Select the codes of the explosion code from the Code drop-down lists. When you post an explosion code, these codes are posted separately. If you set up color scheduling by procedure, each code description is displayed in the assigned scheduling color. Click OK . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + C to access the ADA/CPT/Transaction Code List window. The real time unit is the actual time it takes to perform a procedure in minutes. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding an Unscheduled Existing Patient
topics.body: Adding an Unscheduled Existing Patient Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions > Adding Unscheduled Patients to the InOffice Window Adding an Unscheduled Existing Patient To add an appointment for an existing patient in the InOffice window: In the InOffice window, select InOffice Add from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Patient List window is displayed. Type the patient�s name in the Find field and press Enter. Double-click the patient record, or press Enter. The Daybook for the current day is displayed in the Daily Appointments window. Double-click the appointment time slot. A message asking you to confirm your selection is displayed. To confirm the appointment, click Yes . The Appointment Card window is displayed. Type the appointment information in the appropriate fields and click OK . If there are Tickler entries or open lab cases associated with the patient, the appropriate reminder messages are displayed. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Diagnosis Codes
topics.body: Adding Diagnosis Codes Medical Cross-Coding Adding Diagnosis Codes Diagnosis information is indicated by ICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes and must be added to the system manually. To add an ICD-9 or ICD-10 diagnosis code: Select an option: Select List > Diagnosis . In any window, press Ctrl + G. The Diagnosis Code List window is displayed. Press Enter . Click Add . The Diagnosis Code window is displayed. Type a code number and description in the appropriate fields, and click OK . A diagnosis code can be any alphanumeric characters. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Insurance Plans
topics.body: Adding Insurance Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Adding Insurance Plans Enter insurance company information before associating insurance plans. When making an appointment or posting transactions, the patient insurance effective date and the plan�s limitations are used to dictate whether the patient is covered for a procedure. Before associating an insurance plan with an employer, enter the employer information. For more information, see Handling Employer Information . You cannot change the plan type after saving the record. You can add percentage, flat fee, preferred provider, DMO/Capitation, Medicare, and Medicaid plans. To add an insurance plan: Select List > Ins. Plan . The Insurance Plan List window is displayed. Press Enter. The name of the insurance company is displayed in the title bar. Click Add . The Insurance Plan window is displayed. The identification number for the new insurance plan is displayed next to the Plan ID field. If you subscribe to Trojan, select a Trojan carrier identification number from the Trojan ID drop-down list. Type the insurance plan name. Select the insurance company that sends payments from the Insurance Co drop-down list, select the insurance company office to send claims to from the Mail To drop-down list, and if the insurance plan is associated with only one employer, select it from the Default Employer drop-down list. Type any phone numbers and notes. Press Alt + Enter to move the cursor to the Information tab, and type the plan's group number in the Group Number field. Select an option from the Plan Type drop-down list: To enter a dental plan : Select Dental . To enter a medical plan : Select Medical . The Maximum Coverage, Family Deductible, Orthodontics Maximum, and Individual Deductible fields are disabled for medical insurance plans. Select the estimation type from the Estimation Type drop-down list: If the insurance plan uses a flat fee rate : Select Flat Fee . If the insurance plan pays a percentage of procedural costs : Select Percentage . If the insurance plan pays a percentage of the procedural costs based upon your allowed amounts : Select % PPO . If the insurance plan pays a flat fee for procedural costs : Select Flat Fee PPO . If the insurance plan negotiates a reimbursement amount for procedural costs : Select DMO/Capitation . If the insurance plan pays a flat fee for procedural costs based upon the Medicare plan�s allowed amounts : Select Flat Medicare . If the insurance plan pays a percentage of the procedural costs based upon the Medicare plan�s allowed amounts : Select % Medicare . If the insurance plan negotiates a reimbursement amount for procedural amounts for patients on medical assistance or welfare : Select Medicaid . Select the month within which the plan�s deductible and year-to-date benefits are reset from the Anniversary Month drop-down calendar. If the plan has a lifetime deductible, select None/Other . Select an option: After the maximum coverage amount is reached, the estimated insurance amount is 0. Treatment plan procedures affect this amount after they are completed and posted. If the insurance plan has a yearly maximum : Select Maximum Coverage and select the amount from the calculator. If the insurance plan has an orthodontics maximum : Select the amount from the Orthodontics Maximum calculator. If the insurance plan has a family deductible : Select the amount from the Family Deductible calculator. The family deductible amount mus

topics.title: Adding Lab Cases
topics.body: Adding Lab Cases Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Lab Cases Adding Lab Cases You can enter lab case information from the Patient List, Patient, Transactions, Electronic Router, or Labs windows, as well as the patient chart. When you add or edit a lab case, the information is updated in both the patient and lab records. You can also add a lab case to a patient record from the Appointment Card window when you post a procedural code that is set to prompt you to add a lab case. For more information, see Using Lab Case Prompting . To add a lab case: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Clinical Management from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A submenu is displayed. Select View Lab Cases . In the Patient window : Select Clinical Management from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A submenu is displayed. Select View Lab Cases . In the patient chart: Click the Labs tab, or click Display the Patient's Lab Cases on the Common toolbar. In the Labs List window : Select the lab and select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Labs window : Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the patient Transactions window : Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Electronic Router window : Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Lab case window is displayed. Click Add . The Lab case window is displayed in ADD mode. Select the patient, doctor, and lab from the drop-down lists and make the changes to the Opened and Expect Back fields. Type the information in the Tooth, Shade, and Description fields. Click OK . The lab case is saved. A message prompts you to create a reminder memo for the day the lab case is due back. Click Yes to generate a memo. The Lab case window is displayed. Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Medical Conditions
topics.body: Adding Medical Conditions Handling Patient Medical History > Understanding Medical Conditions Adding Medical Conditions After you create a medical condition, you cannot edit or delete it. To change the wording of a condition, you must add and display a new one with the correct text. If you decide not to display a condition, the information is saved in a patient's medical history. Each new condition is displayed on the Conditions tab of the Medical History window automatically if there are fewer than 50 conditions. New conditions are displayed in the Order of Appearance field. If the Conditions tab is full (maximum is 50 conditions), this number is zero and you must remove a condition to display a new one. To add a medical condition: Select System > Customize Medical History . The Medical Conditions List window is displayed. Press Enter, and click Add . The Medical Conditions window is displayed. Type the condition name in the Condition Text field. A sample of the condition text is displayed in the Sample section. To trigger a medial alert when the condition is associated with a patient record, select Should this condition set the patient�s Med Alert Flag? . If this checkbox is selected, the condition text is displayed in red in the Sample section. Click OK . The Medical Conditions window is displayed. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Medical Histories
topics.body: Adding Medical Histories Handling Patient Medical History Adding Medical Histories Only users with the proper security settings can enter a medical history. For more information, see Setting Medical History Security . When you enter or edit information in the Medical History window, the associated patient record is locked. You can view the Medical History window without locking the patient record. To add medical history information for a patient: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click a patient in the list. In the Power bar : Select Patient List Window . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click a patient in the list. In the Account List or Account window : Select Patients from the Options menu in the Express bar. A Patient List window containing all of the patients associated with the account is displayed. Select a patient. In the InOffice window : Select Patient from the Options menu in the Express bar. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click a patient in the list. In any window : Press Ctrl + O. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click a patient in the list. The patient is displayed in the Patient window. Click OK and select Clinical Management > Patient Medical History from the Options menu in the Express bar. OR In the patient chart, hover over the Medical Conditions, Allergies, or Medicines section of the patient banner, and click the link. The Medical History window is displayed. If medical history information exists for the patient, this information is displayed. Click Update . The current day�s date is displayed in the Medical History Recorded On field. Type the physician�s name and phone number in the appropriate fields. If the patient is currently taking prescription or non-prescription medication, click Add Drug and select the drug from the list. The medication must be entered in the Drug window before you can add it to a medical history. For more information, see Adding Prescription Drugs . Select an option: To indicate the patient is allergic to a substance : Select Yes next to the substance text in the Allergies section. To indicate the patient is not allergic to a substance : Select No next to the substance text in the Allergies section. To add additional information about a patient allergy : Type the information in the Other field. To include heart rate, blood pressure, height, weight, or smoking-related patient information to the medical history : Type the information in the Miscellaneous section. To include birth control, pregnancy, or nursing information to the medical history : Select a checkbox in the For women only section. The For women only section is enabled only if Female or Unknown is selected from the Sex drop-down list on the Info tab of the Patient window. Press Ctrl + Tab to make the Conditions tab the active window. Medical condition information is contained on this tab. Medical conditions that trigger a medical alert are displayed in red text. Select an option: To indicate the patient suffers from a medical condition : Select Yes next to the condition text. To indicate the patient does not suffer from a medical condition : Select No next to the condition text. To manipulate the condition information that is displayed on the tab, see Understanding Medical Conditions . Press Ctrl + Tab to make the Other tab the active window. Additional patient medical condition information is contained on this tab. To indica

topics.title: Accessing the Auto Dialer While Viewing Memos
topics.body: Accessing the Auto Dialer While Viewing Memos Contacts and Memos > Using Memos Accessing the Auto Dialer While Viewing Memos To access the auto dialer from the Memos List or Memos windows : Click Auto Dialer or select it from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Your computer's modem is used to call the patient. If you do not have a modem, the Phone Numbers window is displayed for reference only. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Contact Memos
topics.body: Adding Contact Memos Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Handling Contact Expert Tasks Adding Contact Memos Use contact memos t o add reminders about pending contacts with patients and insurance companies. Memos are also a convenient means of interoffice communication and personal reminders. When you create a memo, you specify when the reminder first appears and how often it is displayed. To add a contact memo: In the Contact Expert window, select a record from the list in the left pane. Select an option: To create a memo for the current contact : Select Create Memo from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To create a memo for a previous contact : Select a contact from the Previous Contacts section of the page and select Create Memo from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Memos window is displayed. To change the date on which the memo first is displayed, click the Appear On drop-down list, select a date, and click OK . Select how frequently you want the memo to appear from the Frequency drop-down list. Type the sender�s name in the From field. Type the memo recipient�s name in the To field. Type the message text in the Message field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Memos to a Contact
topics.body: Adding Memos to a Contact Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts Adding Memos to a Contact To add a memo to a contact associated with a record: Select an option: To add a memo to a contact for a patient record : In the Patient List window, select the patient. To print a contact for an insurance plan record : In the Insurance Plan List window, select the plan. To add a memo to a contact for a referring doctor record : In the REFERRING DR List window, select the doctor. To add a memo to a contact for a provider record : In the Provider List window, select the provider. To add a memo to a contact for an employer record : In the Employer List window, select the employer. To add a memo to a contact for an insurance company record : In the Insurance Company List window, select the company. To add a memo to a contact for a school record : In the School List window, select the school. To add a memo to a contact for a laboratory record : In the Labs List window, select the laboratory. To add a memo to a contact for a pharmacy record : In the Pharmacy List window, select the pharmacy. To add a memo to a contact for an account record : In the Account List window, select the account. To add a memo to a contact for a claim record: In the Claim List window, select the claim. To add a memo to a contact for a completed claim record: In the Completed Claim List window, select the claim. Select Contacts > By Date or Contacts > By Type from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Contacts for List window is displayed. Select the contact and click Create Memo , or select Create Memo from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. A message asks you whether to add a memo to the selected contact. Click Yes . The Memos window is displayed. Select the date on which to display the memo from the Appear On drop-down list. Select how frequently the memo is to display from the Frequency drop-down list. Type a message in the Message field. If the contact involves a patient or account, this information is displayed in the Patient Information and Account Information sections. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding New Documents
topics.body: Adding New Documents Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Adding New Documents To add a new document: Select an option: To add an account document : In the Account List window, select the account to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. If Statement Archiving is selected in the billing statement system settings, each time the Billing Statement report is generated a copy of the report is saved to each account included in the results and is displayed in the Account Archived Document section of the tree in the Documents for window. To add a patient document : In the Patient List window, select the patient to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. OR In the patient chart, click the Documents tab. To add a referring doctor document : In the REFERRING DR List window, select the patient to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to an insurance company record : In the Insurance Co List window, select the company to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to an insurance company plan record : In the Insurance Plan List window, select the plan to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to an insurance claim record : In the Claim List window, select the claim to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to a completed claim record : In the Completed Claim List window, select the claim to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a lab case document : In the Labs List window, select the lab to which the case is to be added and select Multi-Case Document from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Multi Lab Case Capture window is displayed. In this window, you can attach a document to several lab cases. Click Add Lab Case , or select the command from the Options section of the Express bar. The Lab Case List window is displayed. Double-click the lab cases. A message asks you to confirm the lab case addition. Click Yes . The Lab Case List window is displayed. Select Capture Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar. The Documents for window is displayed. To append a page to an existing document, see Appending (Adding) Pages to Documents . Select Capture New Document from the Options section of the Express bar. The Documents for add window is displayed. Type the document�s name in the Document Description field. To use the name as the default for all new documents of the selected type, select Make Default . For more information, see Setting Capture Preferences . The Category field defaults to a category based on where the capture process was started. Example: If you are capturing a new document from the Patient List window, the Category field defaults to the Misc Patient Documents category. To select a different category, double-click it in the Category drop-down field. Move the slider to the appropriate compression quality. You can also type the quality

topics.title: Adding New Office Expert Items
topics.body: Adding New Office Expert Items Using Experts > Using the Office Expert > Setting Up Office Expert Items Adding New Office Expert Items You can add items to the office expert list. If the security system is enabled, you must have the appropriate permission to perform this task. If the proper permissions are not set and you want to add an office expert item, see your software administrator. To add a new item to the office expert: From the Experts menu, select Office Expert . The Office Expert window is displayed. Select Setup Experts from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Office Expert Setup window is displayed. The left pane contains a list of items that can be added to the office expert item. The right pane contains the list of active office expert items. Select the item you want to add from the Available Office Expert Items list, and click Add Selected Item . A prompt is displayed. For a complete list of prompts for each item, see Using the Office Expert . The Statements To Print, Yesterday�s Login Failures, Yesterday�s Added Rx Count, and ePostings Notifications items do not have corresponding prompts. Skip to step number 5. Type the appropriate criteria, and click OK . The Office Expert Setup window is displayed. Select from the following: To remove an entry from the Active Office Expert Entries section: Select the entry, and click Remove Selected Item . A prompt is displayed, asking if you are sure you want to remove the item. Click Yes. To edit the settings for a specific entry: Select the entry, and click Edit Prompts . The prompt window is displayed. To specify how frequently the selected entry will run: Select the entry, and click Set Frequency . The Office Expert Item Frequency window is displayed. See Setting Office Expert Item Frequency for more information. To set the priority of the selected entry: Select the entry, and click Set Priority . The Office Expert Priority window is displayed. See Setting Office Expert Item Priorities for more information. To specify the view permissions for the selected entry: Select the entry, and click Show Users . The Office Expert User Administration window is displayed. See Setting Office Expert Item View Permissions for more information. Click OK . Items do not display in the office expert list until they have been run. To run an office expert item see Running Office Expert Items . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Clinical Notes While Posting
topics.body: Adding Clinical Notes While Posting Working with Clinical Notes Adding Clinical Notes While Posting When posting procedures, you can specify how you want to be prompted to add clinical notes. Select from the following: To display a message that asks you whether to add a clinical note to the chart after posting each procedure: Select Add Clinical Notes While Posting in the Chart Options Properties window. To display a single window where you can add all of the clinical notes for a visit after posting the procedures: Select Add Clinical Notes Per Visit and Add Clinical Notes While Posting . The Clinical Notes Per Visit window is displayed when you close the chart. To display a single window at the end of the day where you can add all of the clinical notes for a visit after posting the procedures: Select Add Clinical Notes Per Visit and Add Clinical Notes At End of Day . The Clinical Notes Per Visit At End of Day window is displayed when you select Clinical Notes > Post End of Day Notes . See Setting Charting Options and Properties . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding New Accounts
topics.body: Adding New Accounts Setting Up Accounts Adding New Accounts To add a new account record: Select List > Account . The Account List window is displayed in FIND mode. Press Enter. The account name for the selected account is displayed in the title bar. To add a new account from the Patient List window, click Add . A message is displayed, asking if you are adding the new patient to the account. Click No . Click Add . The Account window is displayed and the Guarantor 1 tab is active. Type the name and address of the account guarantor in the Name and Address section. Select the employment or school status of the guarantor in the Employment/School section. If the patient is employed, select the guarantor's employer. If the guarantor is a student, select the guarantor�s school. Select the medical or dental insurance plan in the Insurance section. Select an option: If the guarantor has Blue Cross/Blue Shield insurance : Type the policy number in the BCBS # field. If the guarantor has Medicaid : Type the policy number in the Medicaid # field. Type the guarantor�s employee number in the Employee # field if it is different from the Social Security number. To use the employee number instead of the guarantor�s Social Security number on claim forms, select Use Guarantor Employee # in the Insurance Plan window. Type or select the guarantor�s home, work, and cell telephone numbers, Social Security number, date of birth, sex, and marital status in the Personal section. Select the guarantor's preferred contact method from the Preferred Contact drop-down list. The preferred contact is highlighted in yellow. The preferred contact is displayed in bold italics on the patient appointment in the scheduler, in the scheduling Assistant window (accessed from the Zoom icon), in the Confirm Appt for window, and on account-specific reports. Press Alt + Enter to move to the common area of the window, and if applicable, enter finance charge or account user codes in the User Codes field. See Running Account Reports . Select an account doctor from the Dr drop-down list. The Account, Insurance, and Budget Plan fields cannot be edited. This information is automatically updated as payments are made. Enter a minimum payment amount toward a specified account balance in the Min Payment field. Select an insurance calculation method from the ICM drop-down list: Select ICM 0 : If all charges must be paid at the time of service. Select ICM 1 : If the account is not billed until all insurance payments are made. Select ICM 2 : If the estimated patient portion (balance minus estimated insurance) must be paid at the time of service. Type any notes in the Notes field. To print a note on billing statements, type the note in the Notes to appear on Billing Stmts: field. Select electronic statement options in the Send Automated Statements section: By Mail: Select this option to have the eServices clearinghouse send a printed statement to the guarantor. Note: You must be enrolled in automated statements to use this feature. Online: Select this option to have the eServices clearinghouse send an online statement to the guarantor. Note: You must be enrolled in automated statements to use this feature. To add information for a second, third, or fourth guarantor for the account, click Guarantor 2 . To view the next patient identification number, the account creation date, the date the account was last updated, and any category codes set for the account, click Other Info . The patient identificat

topics.title: Adding New Patients/Accounts from the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance Windows
topics.body: Adding New Patients/Accounts from the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance Windows Setting Up Patients > Adding Patient Records Adding New Patients/Accounts from the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance Windows You can add a new patient from the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance scheduler windows when a new patient calls to schedule an appointment. When the patient arrives for the appointment, enter the remainder of the patient information and activate the new patient and account records. To add a new patient from the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance scheduler windows: Select an option: In the Daily Appointments window : Select a time slot, and select New Patient Appt from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Book At A Glance window : Select a time slot, and select New Patient Appt from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The New Patient Appt window is displayed. Type the patient�s name and date of birth in the appropriate fields. Select a provider from the Doctor drop-down list. When adding a patient or account from this window, the software does not check for the correct provider type. Type the patient�s cell, home, and work phone numbers. If the new patient was referred to your office by an existing patient or another doctor, select the information from the drop-down lists in the Referral Info section. Select an option: To schedule the patient for an initial exam : Select Initial Exam . The default initial exam procedural code information is displayed in the Code, Description, T#, Su/Qd, Fee, and Slots fields. To change the default codes for initial exams, click Change Defaults . The New Patient Appt Defaults window is displayed. Select a procedural code and click OK . The New Patient Appt window is displayed. To schedule the patient for an emergency exam : Select Emergency Exam . The default emergency exam procedural code information is displayed in the Code, Description, T#, Su/Qd, Fee, and Slots fields. To change the default codes for emergency exams, click Change Defaults . The New Patient Appt Defaults window is displayed. Select a procedural code and click OK . The New Patient Appt window is displayed. To schedule the patient for a different exam : Select Other and select a procedural code from the Code drop-down list. The procedural code information is displayed in the Code, Description, T#, Su/Qd, Fee, and Slots fields. Type any notes in the Appt Notes field. These notes are displayed in the Appointment Card window. Type any medical alerts in the Medical Alert field. The patient is displayed in red text, and a red cross is displayed in the Alerts column in the Patient List window. Medical alert information is printed on the Daily Operatory schedule, routing slips, and the Patient Information report. To enter additional medical information or to edit the medical alert information, select Clinical Management > Patient Medical History from the Options menu section of the Express bar. For more information, see Adding Medical Histories and Updating Medical Histories .
