If you are adding the new patient to an existing account, select an account from the Add patient to an existing account drop-down list. To add or view additional account information, click Additional Account Info . For more information, see Setting Up Accounts . Click OK . The new appointment is displayed in the appropriate window. In the Daily Appointments window, a New icon is displayed next to

topics.title: Adding a New Pharmacy
topics.body: Adding a New Pharmacy Pharmacies and Prescriptions > Handling Pharmacies Adding a New Pharmacy To add a new pharmacy record: Select an option: Select List > Pharmacy . Click Pharmacy List on the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. In any window, press Ctrl + H. The Pharmacy List window is displayed. Click anywhere in the list area or press Enter or Esc to switch to SCAN mode. Click Add on the toolbar. The Pharmacy window is displayed. Type the pharmacy name and address in the appropriate fields. To include a web link in the pharmacy record, select the Web Link drop-down list to display the Web_sites window. To add contact information, click Contact Info and type the contact�s name, phone number, fax number, beeper number, and e-mail address. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding New Accounts from the Scheduler Windows
topics.body: Adding New Accounts from the Scheduler Windows Setting Up Accounts Adding New Accounts from the Scheduler Windows You can add a new account from the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance scheduler windows when a new patient calls to schedule an appointment. When the patient arrives for the appointment, enter the remainder of the patient information and activate the new patient and account records. To add a new patient or account from the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows: Select an option: In the Daily Appointments window : Select a time slot, and select New Patient Appt . In the Book At A Glance window : Select a time slot, and select Appoint For New Patient/Account . The New Patient Appt window is displayed. Type the patient�s name and date of birth. After you enter the patient�s date of birth, the patient age is displayed in the Age field. Select the patient�s doctor from the Doctor drop-down list. Type the patient�s cell, home, and work telephone numbers in the appropriate fields. If the new patient was referred to your office by an existing patient or another doctor, select the information from the drop-down lists in the Referral Info section. Select an option: To schedule the patient for an initial exam : Select Initial Exam . The default initial exam procedural code information is displayed in the Code, Description, T#, Su/Qd, Fee, and Slots fields. To schedule the patient for an emergency exam : Select Emergency Exam . The default emergency exam procedural code information is displayed in the Code, Description, T#, Su/Qd, Fee, and Slots fields. To schedule the patient for a different exam : Select Other and select a procedural code from the Code drop-down list. The procedural code information is displayed in the Code, Description, T#, Su/Qd, Fee, and Slots fields. Type any notes in the Appt Notes field. These notes are displayed in the Appointment Card window. Type any medical alerts in the Medical Alert field. The patient name is displayed in red text and a red cross is displayed in the Alerts column in the Patient List window. Medical alert information is printed on the Daily Operatory schedule, routing slips, and the Patient Information report. Select an option: To add the new patient to an existing account : Select the account from the Add patient to an existing account drop-down list. To add additional account information to the new account : Click Additional Account Info and enter the new account record. After you add the new account, a message is displayed, asking you to activate the new account. Click Yes . The New Patient Appt window is displayed. Click OK . In the Daily Appointments window, a New icon is displayed next to the appointment. New patients are displayed in the Patient List and Account List windows in fuchsia. Tip Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. When adding a patient or account from this window, the correct provider type information is not automatically checked. To change default emergency codes, click Change Defaults . Select the procedural code and click OK . To enter additional medical information or edit medical alerts, see Updating Medical Histories . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Pages to Documents
topics.body: Adding Pages to Documents Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Adding Pages to Documents To add a page to an existing patient, account, lab case, referring doctor, or claim document: In the Documents for window, select the document from the Documents section of the tree, and select Append Page(s) from the Options section of the Express bar. The Documents for capture window is displayed. Select an option: To select a TWAIN compliant device : Click Select a TWAIN source . The Select a Source window is displayed. Select the source, and click Select . A scanner is an example of a TWAIN compliant device. To acquire a page using a TWAIN device : Click Acquire an image from the TWAIN source. The source software is launched. Acquire the image. To import a page that is already on the network : Click Import An Image . The Import Images window is displayed. Select the image, and click Open . Note: If you are importing a document that is not an image file, click the drop-down arrow in the Files of Type field and select All Files . Click Save Capture . A message asks you to confirm the addition. Click Yes . The Enter Name Information window is displayed. Type the page label text in the Page Label field, and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding and Removing Power Bar Buttons
topics.body: Adding and Removing Power Bar Buttons Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Power Bar Adding and Removing Power Bar Buttons To add a Power bar button: Right-click on the Power bar and select Customize Toolbar from the submenu. The Customize Toolbar window is displayed. Select an option: To add a button to the Power bar : Select it from the Available toolbar buttons section and click Add . The button is displayed in the Current toolbar buttons section. To remove a button from the Power bar : Select it from the Current toolbar buttons section and click Remove . The button is displayed in the Available toolbar buttons section. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Daily Appointment Window Profiles
topics.body: Adding Daily Appointment Window Profiles Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows > Setting Daily Appointment Window Properties and Using Profiles Adding Daily Appointments Window Profiles You can create a profile to enable you to view the entire day or create a profile with large, easy-to-read fonts. You can also enable all users to access the profile or restrict it to your user login. To add Daily Appointments window profiles: In the Daily Appointments window, right-click an empty time slot, and select Properties ; or select Properties > Appointment from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Properties... window is displayed. Click Add . The Add Profile window is displayed. Type the profile name in the Name field. Select a source profile from the Source Profile drop-down list. The source profile is a profile that contains the colors, fonts, and background images that you want to apply to the new profile. Select an option: To enable all users to access the new profile : Select Add profile for ALL Users . This checkbox is selected by default. To restrict access to the new profile to your login ID : Deselect Add profile for ALL Users . You cannot modify the access setting for a profile after it is saved. Click OK . Make any additional modifications to the profile and click OK . The new profile settings are displayed in the Scheduler window. See Setting Daily Appointments Window Properties . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a Provider
topics.body: Adding a Provider Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Provider Information Adding a Provider The provider type is used to determine payment allocation and scheduling. If you do not select a collections assignee and the new provider is a doctor, the doctor receives payment for any work performed. If the new provider is a hygienist, the patient�s default doctor receives payment. To properly use color and goal scheduling, enter provider vacation days and office hours. When Alternating Scheduling is enabled, the two schedules are rotated from week to week. To add a new provider record: Select List > Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The Provider window is displayed. The assigned provider identification number is displayed at the top of the window. Type the provider�s last name, first name, middle name, title, and degree information. Select the provider type and click OK . Type the provider�s address and personal information. On the Information tab, select the doctor to receive collections from the Assign Collections to drop-down list. Example : To assign all collections produced by a doctor to the doctor who owns the practice, select the practice owner from the list. Select the doctor identification number to print on insurance forms as the billing (collecting) provider from the Dr on Insurance forms drop-down list. If you do not assign a provider, the patient�s doctor is printed on the forms. Select the provider�s speciality from the Specialty drop-down list. This is used when submitting electronic claims. Select the provider�s preferred assistant from the Assistant drop-down list. When scheduling the provider for appointments requiring an assistant, the preferred assistant is automatically scheduled. To set a default scheduling book, select the book from the Auto Sched Book drop-down list. If no book is selected, the user�s default Daybook is displayed when scheduling appointments. To set an auto-scheduling book, select the book that contains the rooms and hours in which the provider wants to be scheduled from the Auto Sched Book drop-down list. If the provider prefers to use a routing slip that is different from the office default form, type the form number in the Routing Slip field. To find the form number, select System > Form Selection and select the Routing Slips drop-down list. Use the Modify Routing Slip Codes utility to create custom forms. To use another business address instead of the practice address on all correspondence, type the business address in the fields. To use this address as the default for this doctor, in the System Printing Preferences window, select Account Doctor from the drop-down list. For more information, see Selecting a Printer . Type any notes in the Notes field. Select the vacation date range to schedule for the provider from the Vacation/Days Off section. Click ID�s or press Ctrl + Tab to display the ID�s tab. Select an option: To print the practice�s federal tax identification number on claim forms : Leave the Federal Tax ID field blank. To change the practice�s federal tax identification number, see Entering Practice Information . To print the provider�s federal tax identification number on claim forms : Type the number in the Federal Tax ID field. To print the provider�s Social Security number on claim forms automatically : Type the number in the Social Security field. Type the provider�s license, DEA regulation, anesthesia license, associat

topics.title: Adding Patients to an Account
topics.body: Adding Patients to an Account Setting Up Accounts > Adding New Accounts Adding Patients to an Account To add patients to an account: Select an option: From a new account record : Click OK . The Patient List window is displayed. From an existing account record : Click Patients . Click Add . The Patient window is displayed. If the guarantor 1 for the account is not the same as the patient, make the appropriate changes. Select an option: To enter personal information, medical alerts, and referral information : Type the information on the Info tab. To enter dental and medical insurance information : Click the Insur. tab and type the information. To enter patient recall information and patient scheduling preferences : Click the Recall tab and type the information. To enter an important date and claim date information : Click the Dates tab and type the information. To enter patient diagnosis codes and category codes : Click the Codes tab and type the information. To enter notes, employer and school information, and emergency numbers and contacts for the patient : Click the Notes tab and type the information. Click OK and click Close . Select an option: To add more patients to the account : Click Add and repeat steps 1 through 4. To return to the Account List window : Click Close . Tip Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. If you assign medical or dental insurance to guarantor 1 in the account record, enter the patient�s relationship to the guarantor and the patient�s insurance information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Unscheduled Patients to the InOffice Window
topics.body: Adding Unscheduled Patients to the InOffice Window Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Adding Unscheduled Patients to the InOffice Window You can make appointments for new and existing patients in the InOffice window in cases of: Emergency appointments Walk-in appointments Related Topics Adding an Unscheduled Existing Patient Adding Unscheduled New Patients Activating the New Patient�s Account Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Patient Clinical Notes
topics.body: Adding Patient Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes > Handling Patient Clinical Notes Adding Patient Clinical Notes To add a patient clinical note while charting: Select a procedure and tooth, and do one of the following: Click the Notes tab. Select Clinical Notes > View Clinical Notes. Click Add a descriptive note on the main toolbar. The Patient Clinical Notes List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The Patient Clinical Notes window is displayed. Type a brief description of the note's contents in the Re field. The text entered in this field is displayed in all clinical notes list windows. Complete the following information: Select the procedure code to which the note refers from the ADA Code drop-down list. You can select only a production or explosion code from the list. Select the condition from the drop-down list. Type the tooth and surface information in the fields, or click the drop-down arrows to select the information. Select the type of watch from the drop-down list. Select the provider from the list. The provider does not affect the chart. Type text in the Note field. If you have the proper security permissions, you can edit this note after it is saved until the end of the day. After that time period, you can only amend the note. To use this note as a default when posting the procedure, check Use this text as the default for this ADA Code . Click OK . The current date is added at the end of the note. If no generic note is associated with the procedure code for the provider, a message is displayed, asking if you want to make a generic clinical note for this doctor or for all doctors. If you selected Use this text as the default for this ADA Code , a message is displayed, asking if you want to create the default note for this doctor or for all doctors. Select an option and click Yes . Click Close twice. You can also add, amend, edit, and delete clinical notes in the Patient and Patient List windows. For more information, see Understanding Patient List and Patient Window Options . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Patient Records
topics.body: Adding Patient Records Setting Up Patients Adding Patients You can: Add a patient to new or existing accounts. Add up to 99 patients to each account. Access patient data from most windows in the application. You can add new patient records from the following windows: Account Account List Daily Appointments Book At A Glance Patient List Patient Related Topics Adding New Patients from the Patient Windows Adding New Patients from the Account Window Adding New Patients/Accounts from the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance Windows Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a Referring Patient to a Patient Record
topics.body: Adding a Referring Patient to a Patient Record Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Patient Referrals Adding a Referring Patient to a Patient Record To credit a current patient with referring a new patient to your practice: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the referred patient. The Patient window is displayed in EDIT mode. Select the referring patient from the Patient drop-down list displayed in the Referred In By section of the Info tab. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding New Patients from the Patient Windows
topics.body: Adding New Patients from the Patient Windows Setting Up Patients > Adding Patient Records Adding New Patients from the Patient Windows To add a new patient record from the patient windows: Select an option: To add a new patient to a new account in the Patient List or Patient windows : Click Add . A message is displayed, asking to add the new patient to the selected patient account. Click No . The Account window is displayed. Type the information, and click OK . A blank Patient List window is displayed. Click Add . To add a new patient to an existing account from the Patient List or Patient windows of a member of the account : Click Add . A message is displayed, asking to add the new patient to the account of the selected patient. Click Yes . The Patient window is displayed. If you are adding a patient to an existing account, the guarantor 1�s last name, address, and home telephone number are displayed. Any account balances are displayed in the Balances section. Type the patient�s name, address, nickname, and telephone numbers in the appropriate fields. Select an option: To add an additional patient record identifier : Type it in the Chart # field. The identification number must be unique for each patient. To add a patient user code to the record : Type it in the User Codes field. When adding more than one code, do not add a space or comma between codes. For a list of patient user codes, see Understanding Patient User Codes . Codes in the Acct Codes field are user codes associated with the account and are entered in the User Codes field in the account record. For more information, see Using Account User Codes . Select the patient�s default doctor and hygienist from the drop-down lists. Type the fee schedule for the patient. When you post a procedure to the patient, the fee schedule determines the cost of service. Fee schedule 0 is the default. NO INSURANCE is displayed beneath the Fee Sched field until you specify a relationship between the patient and the account guarantor on the Insurance tab. Select the patient's preferred contact method from the Preferred Contact drop-down list. The preferred contact is highlighted in yellow. The preferred contact is displayed in bold italics on the patient appointment in the scheduler, in the scheduling Assistant window (accessed from the Zoom icon), in the Confirm Appt for window, and on patient-specific reports. Press Alt + Enter to save the information. Select the patient�s sex and marital status from the drop-down lists, and type the patient�s Social Security number and date of birth in the appropriate fields. Select an option: To enter a patient medical alert : Deselect Has No Alert and type the medical alert information in the Medical Alerts field. The patient is displayed in red text, and a red cross is displayed in the Alerts column in the Patient List window. To enter additional medical information or to edit the medical alert information, select Clinical Management > Patient Medical History from the Options menu section of the Express bar. For more information, see Adding Medical Histories and Updating Medical Histories . To indicate that the patient was referred to your practice by another doctor or patient : Enter the information in the Referred In By section. Press Ctrl + Tab to make the Insur. tab the active window. If the patient is insured, select the primary and secondary guarantors from the drop-down lists and select an option: If the patient requires a dental identification number

topics.title: Adding a Record
topics.body: Adding a Record Getting Started > Using List Windows > Using Toolbar Options in a List Window Adding a Record To add a record in a list window: Click Add. Type the information and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a Referring Doctor
topics.body: Adding a Referring Doctor Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals > Handling Referring Doctor Records Adding a Referring Doctor To add a referring doctor: Select an option: Select List > Referring Dr . In any window, press Ctrl + F. Click List Referring Providers on the Power Bar. The REFERRING DR List window is displayed. Click Add . The REFERRING DR window is displayed. The identification number for the new referring doctor is displayed in the ID field. Type the doctor's degree in the Degree field. Type the first name, last name, middle name, and title of the doctor in the appropriate fields. Type the address, phone number, provider identification, license number, Blue Cross/Blue Shield number, Medicare identification number, Medicaid identification number, Social Security number, tax identification number, and national provider ID for the doctor in the appropriate fields. Select the specialty, sex, and referral category using the drop-down lists and click OK . The list of available categories is based on the categories you created for use in the Referral Source Analysis report. For more information, see Creating Referral Categories . Click Additional Info . The Additional Info tab of the REFERRING DR window is displayed. If the referring doctor has a second office in a different location, type the address and phone number information in the Additional Address Information section. To include the doctor�s referred patient list in the referred doctor patient list of the Palm organizer, select Include Patients referred with PracticeToGo entry . Click Contact Info . The Contact Info tab of the REFERRING DR window is displayed. Type the fax number, alternate phone number, and e-mail address for the referring doctor�s office in the fields. Type the phone numbers and e-mail address for the contact person within the referring doctor�s office in the fields and click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Reminder Memos
topics.body: Adding Reminder Memos Contacts and Memos > Using Memos Adding Reminder Memos To add a reminder memo: Select List > Memos . The Memos List window is displayed. Click Add . The Memo window is displayed. Select the date on which to display the memo from the Appear On drop-down list. Select how frequently the memo is to display from the Frequency drop-down list. Type the name of the person who is sending the memo in the From field, and type the name of the person who is receiving the memo in the To field. Type the memo subject in the Re field. This information is displayed in the Topic column in the Memos List window. Type a message in the Message field. If the memo involves a patient or account, select the record from the Patient Information and Account Information sections. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Schools
topics.body: Adding Schools Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling School Information Adding Schools Schools are stored in the same database as employers; the first school might have an identification number greater than 1, and schools subsequently entered might not have sequential identification numbers. To add a school: Select List > School . The School List window is displayed. Press Enter . Click Add . The School window is displayed. The assigned school identification number is displayed at the top of the window. Type the school name, address, and telephone numbers. If the school has a web address that is already entered into the software, select it from the Web Link drop-down list. To add contact information, click Contact Info or press Ctrl + Tab to display the Contact Info tab. Click OK and Close . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + T to access the School List window. To add a web link to the list, see Adding Websites . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Stamps
topics.body: Adding Stamps Handling Images While Charting > Using Annotation Mode Adding Stamps Use the following stamps to rubber stamp images: Approved Copy Important Sent Urgent To add an annotation stamp: On any tab in the Chart for window, select the image to annotate on the Clinical Images tab in the Clinical Detail Container, right-click, and select Annotate . Select Annotation Stamp Tools . Select buttons on the Image Annotate toolbar . To specify the stamp line color, click the color box in the Attributes section. The Color window is displayed. Select the color and click OK . Select the stamp and use the pointer to select the stamp insertion point. To hide text and note annotations, deselect Show Annotations . Select Save Annotations . Tip On the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select Annotation Mode and drag-and-drop the image from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Allowance Tables
topics.body: Adding Allowance Tables Setting Up Insurance > Using Allowance Tables Adding Allowance Tables To add an allowance table: Select List > Allowance Table . The Allowance Table List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The Allowance Table window is displayed. Type the allowance table name. Select an option: To use fee0 amounts : Select Use Fee0 Amounts . To exclude codes with no amounts, select Exclude Codes with Zero Amounts . To copy codes from an existing insurance company allowance table : Select Copy From Existing Table and select the table. To create an empty table : Select Make Empty Table . Click OK and click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Appointment Information to the Tickler File
topics.body: Adding Appointment Information to the Tickler File Scheduling Appointments > Using the Tickler Adding Appointment Information to the Tickler File To add appointment information to the tickler file: Select List > Tickler File . The Appt Ticklers List window is displayed. Click Add Tickler Entry on the toolbar, or select the command from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient for whom to add appointment information. The Tickler Entry window is displayed. Select the first procedure to be performed from the Code drop-down list. Press Tab to view the patient description. The Assistant window is displayed : If a bluebook entry is associated with the procedure in the patient's insurance plan. If color scheduling is enabled, the Description field is highlighted in the color assigned to this type of procedure. If necessary, type the tooth number and surface or quadrant information in the appropriate fields. Select any other procedure codes to schedule for the appointment. The total appointment fees and any applicable sales tax are displayed in the Payment Summary section. If the patient does not have any previously scheduled appointments : The insurance estimation amounts are displayed in the Pat Pays and Exp Ins fields. If the patient does not have insurance coverage : No Ins is displayed. The amounts displayed in these fields are gathered from the Used field in the Guarantors/Insurance tab of the account record. If a patient has two or more appointments on different days, the insurance amount is estimated only for the first appointment. After a transaction is posted, the insurance amount is estimated for the second appointment. If a patient has two appointments scheduled for the same day, the insurance amount for both appointments is estimated. Type the number of time slots required for the procedures in the Slots field. The default value is the number of time units specified on the Scheduler/Fees tab in the ADA/Transaction Codes window. The maximum number of time units available for the appointment is displayed to the right of the field. You cannot schedule the patient for more than the available number of time units. Type the letter associated with the provider types required to perform the procedures in the Assignment field. If a scheduler template is associated with a procedure, the field is populated automatically. Select the doctor, assistant, and hygienist to perform the procedures in the Doctor, Assistant, and Hygienist drop-down lists. The default values for the Doctor and Hygienist drop-down lists are the providers in the patient record. If an assistant is associated with a doctor in the provider record, the assistant is the default value in the Assistant field. The operatory information is displayed for reference only and cannot be changed in this window. If the patient is willing to reschedule the appointment to fill a cancelation, select Short Call . To associate the appointment with a lab case, select a case from the Lab Case drop-down list. Type any appointment notes in the Appt Notes field. When notes are associated with an appointment, a small red mark is displayed in the upper-right corner of the appointment. You can print these notes in the Daily Operatory Schedule report. Manual Entry is displayed in the Tickler Reason field in the Treatment Entry window. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Annotation Shape Tools
topics.body: Adding Annotation Shape Tools Handling Images While Charting > Using Annotation Mode Adding Annotation Shape Tools You can add and delete the following annotations: Rectangles Solid rectangles Ellipses Solid ellipses Lines Freehand lines Pointers Redaction To add an annotation shape: On any tab in the Chart for window, select the image to annotate on the Clinical Images tab in the Clinical Detail Container, right-click, and select Annotate . To specify the shape line color, click the color box in the Attributes section. The Color window is displayed. Select the color and click OK . To specify the shape line size, select it from the Line Size list. In the Shapes section, select the shape and use the pointer to select the insertion point. Use redaction to create a black box over parts of the image. Draw the shape. To hide text and note annotations, deselect Show Annotations . Select Save Annotations . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Clinical Notes for Treatment Plans
topics.body: Adding Clinical Notes for Treatment Plans Working with Clinical Notes Adding Clinical Notes for Treatment Plans Treatment plan notes: Can be amended. Can be edited if you have the proper security permissions and you are editing the note on the date on which it was created. Are displayed in red text for primary treatment plans, magenta for secondary plans, and aqua for tertiary plans. For more information, see Handling Patient Clinical Notes . You can designate any treatment plan note as a: Generic note Default note Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Annotation Text and Notes
topics.body: Adding Annotation Text and Notes Handling Images While Charting > Using Annotation Mode Adding Annotation Text and Notes To add text or a note to an image: On any tab in the Chart for window, select the image to annotate on the Clinical Images tab in the Clinical Detail Container, right-click, and select Annotate . Select Annotation Text Tools . Select buttons on the Image Annotate toolbar . To specify the text or note text color, click the color box in the Attributes section. The Color window is displayed. Select the color and click OK . Select an option: To add text : Select Text and use the pointer to select the insertion point. Type the text in the box. To add a note : Select Note and use the pointer to select the insertion point. Type the note in the box. To hide text and note annotations, deselect Show Annotations . Select Save Annotations . Tip On the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select Annotation Mode and drag-and-drop the image from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Unscheduled New Patients
topics.body: Adding Unscheduled New Patients Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions > Adding Unscheduled Patients to the InOffice Window Adding Unscheduled New Patients To add an appointment for a new patient in the InOffice window: In the InOffice window, select Daily Appointment Book from the Options menu or Daily Appt. Book from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Daily Appointments window is displayed. Select the time slot and select Appoint For New Patient/Account from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The New Patient Appt window is displayed in ADD mode. The current date, time slot, and operatory selected in the Daily Appointments window is displayed in the appropriate fields. Type the patient name and birthday in the appropriate fields. Select a provider from the Doctor drop-down list. Type the patient phone numbers in the appropriate fields. Select an option: To associate the new patient with a patient referrer : Select the patient from the Refer Pat drop-down list. To associate the new patient with a doctor referrer : Select the doctor from the Refer Dr drop-down list. To schedule the patient for an initial exam : Select Initial Exam . To schedule the patient for an emergency exam : Select Emergency Exam . To schedule the patient for a miscellaneous exam : Select Other and select a code from the Code drop-down list. To change the default initial or emergency exam codes, click Change Defaults , select the appropriate codes in the New Patient Appt Defaults window, and click OK . Type any notes in the Notes field. To add the new patient to an existing account, select the account from the Add patient to an existing account drop-down list. To update the account record, click Additional Account Info . Click OK . Click Close . The InOffice window is displayed. Click Fill InOffice . The Fill InOffice window is displayed. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Users
topics.body: Adding Users System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Adding Users You can add an unlimited number of users to the software. User ID 1 is solely responsible for assigning which functions each user can perform. To add a new user: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The Rights By User window is displayed. Type a unique user name and the user�s full name in the appropriate fields. A unique user is assigned and cannot be changed. Click View/Change Password . Type a password between 8 and 15 alphanumeric characters (no special characters) in the fields and click OK . The user can change the password after accessing the software. If the strong passwords feature is enabled in the HIPAA Options window, the password must meet the criteria. For more information, see Setting HIPAA Security and Privacy Options . Select the default daybook to display when the user accesses the software from the Default Book drop-down list. Click the drop-down arrow in the Date of Hire field to select the employee hiring date from a calendar. Click the drop-down arrow in the Status field to select the employee's overtime status. If this employee should not use the default pay frequency, click the drop-down arrow in the Pay Frequency field, and select the pay frequency for this specific employee. See Setting Time Card Preferences for more information about the default pay frequency. Type the user's Carestream single-sign-on (SSO) account email address in the field. If the user doesn't have an SSO account, type the user's work email address in the field. Select Enable Mobility if your practice uses SoftDent Cloud and this employee will use the Mobility feature. This option is available only to SoftDent Cloud users. Select an option: To restrict the user�s access : Select the appropriate category from the left side of the window and select the checkbox next to each task the user can perform in the right side of the window. To give the user rights to perform all the tasks : Click Select All . To copy the task permissions from a previously created template : Click Select from Template . The User Template List window is displayed. Double-click the template. The Rights By User window is displayed. For a complete list of tasks by category, see Tasks with Limited Access . Click OK . The Rights By User List window is displayed. The new user is displayed in the list. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Websites
topics.body: Adding Websites Using E-mail and WebLink > Accessing the Internet Adding Websites To add websites to the Web Sites List window: Select Internet > Other Web Sites . The Web Sites List window is displayed. Click Add . The Web Sites window is displayed in ADD mode. To add a website, select from the following options: Type a URL (website address) in the Site Name field. Click Browse to launch Internet Explorer : Navigate to the site you want. Minimize the window, and in SoftDent, click Current Site in the Web Sites window. A message that asks you whether to use the currently selected website is displayed. Click Yes . Type information about the website in the Notes field. Click OK . The website is saved. Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: After the Integration
topics.body: After the Integration Using PracticeToGo > Installing PracticeToGo After the Integration After the integration, your SoftDent user name and password are stored on the Palm organizer, and no login is required to perform a synchronization, unless: You change your user name and password in the PC SoftDent software OR You select the option in the PC SoftDent software to force a login each time. This means: If you DO NOT force a login for each synchronization, anyone with access to your Palm organizer can set the Palm organizer in the cradle and perform a synchronization. It is up to you to set up security on the Palm organizer so that no one can view private information. If you DO force a login, anyone with access to your Palm organizer can still perform a synchronization, but only according to their own Palm privileges, unless that person knows your login and password. Therefore, it is a good idea to keep your login secret, or create a separate, secret login that has high-level security privileges. It is recommended that you DO force a login. See PracticeToGo System Settings for instructions on how to force a login. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the All Fee Schedules Report
topics.body: Running the All Fee Schedules Report Handling Transactions and Codes > Transaction Code Reports Running the All Fee Schedules Report The All Fee Schedules report generates a list of transaction codes and fees for all fee schedules. The report includes transaction codes, descriptions, and fees for all fee schedules. Sample Report To run the All Fee Schedules report: Select Reports > ADA/Trans/Diag Codes > All Fee Schedules . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Fee Schedule Summary Report window is displayed. In the Subtitle section, type a subtitle for the report, if desired. In the Codes section, type the code ranges to include in the report. To specify a single code, type the code in the left field and type 0 in the right field. Select an option: To include inactive codes in the report: Select Include Inactive Codes . To exclude codes with no fee: Select Exclude codes with a fee of $0 . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Allowance Tables
topics.body: Using Allowance Tables Setting Up Insurance Using Allowance Tables Use an allowance table to update all: Insurance plan entries Bluebook entries Related Topics Adding Allowance Tables Editing Allowance Tables Associating an Allowance Table with an Insurance Plan Updating Bluebook Allowance Table Fees Deleting Allowance Tables Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Account List Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Account List Report Setting Up Accounts > Running Account Reports Running the Alphabetical Account List Report The Alphabetical Account List report lists accounts in your system sorted by account guarantor one's last name. Information on this list includes the guarantor's home phone number, current balance, and whether the guarantor has insurance. Sample Report To run the Alphabetical Account List report: Select Reports > Account > Alphabetical . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Account List Report window is displayed. In the Account Names section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending names you want in the report. Select Include Inactive Accounts to include inactive accounts in the report. In the Account User Codes section, type the user codes you want to include or exclude from the report. If you selected the Merge Letter Expert output option, the Letter Expert Template ID field is active. Click the drop-down arrow to select a template from the list. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Labs Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Labs Report Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Labs > Running Lab Reports Running the Alphabetical Labs Report The Alphabetical Labs report generates an alphabetical list of labs, displaying the lab ID, name, mailing address, telephone numbers, contact person, and pickup times. Sample Report To run the Alphabetical Labs report: Select Reports > Rx/Pharmacies/Labs > Alphabetical Labs . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Lab Report Setup window is displayed. In the Lab Names section, click the drop-down arrows to select the start and end lab names from a list. To include inactive labs in the report, select Include Inactive Labs . To use color/shading in the report, select Include Color/Shading . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Drugs Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Drugs Report Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Running Pharmacy and Prescription Reports Running the Alphabetical Drugs Report The Alphabetical Drugs report generates an alphabetical list of prescription drugs, displaying the drug ID, name, prescription name, and quantity. Sample Report To run the Alphabetical Drugs report: Select Reports > RX/Pharmacies/Labs > Alphabetical Drugs . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Prescription Report Setup window is displayed. In the Prescription Names section, click the drop-down arrows in the First and Last fields to select prescription names from a list. To include inactive drugs in the report, click the Include Inactive Drugs checkbox. To use color/shading in the report, select the Include Color/Shading checkbox. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Insurance Plan List Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Insurance Plan List Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Plan Reports Running the Alphabetical Insurance Plan List Report The Alphabetical Insurance Plan List report generates an alphabetical list of insurance plans. Sample Report To generate an alphabetical list of insurance plans: Select Reports > Insurance > Alphabetical Plan . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Insurance Plan Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending plan names and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Insurance Companies Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Insurance Companies Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Company Reports Running the Alphabetical Insurance Companies Report The Alphabetical Insurance Companies report generates an alphabetical list of insurance companies. Sample Report To generate an alphabetical list of insurance companies: Select Reports > Insurance > Alphabetical Company . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Insurance Company Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending company names and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Other Facilities Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Other Facilities Report Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using Other Facilities Running the Alphabetical Other Facilities Report Other facilities are locations other than your office that can be used on medical procedure claim forms. The Alphabetical Other Facilities List report generates a list of other facilities in the database. The list is sorted alphabetically. Sample Report For each facility, the report includes: Identification number Name Address Phone numbers Contact person To run the Alphabetical Other Facilities List report: Select Reports > Insurance > Alphabetical Other Facility . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Other Facilities By Name Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending facility names to include in the report from the drop-down lists. To include other facilities that are inactive, select Include Inactive Other Facilities . To use color shading in the report, select Include Color Shading . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Pharmacies Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Pharmacies Report Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Running Pharmacy and Prescription Reports Running the Alphabetical Pharmacies Report The Alphabetical Pharmacies report generates an alphabetical list of pharmacies and includes pharmacy ID, name, mailing address, telephone numbers, and contact person. Sample Report To run the Alphabetical Pharmacies report: Select Reports > RX/Pharmacies/Labs > Alphabetical Pharmacies . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Pharmacy List Report Setup window is displayed. In the Pharmacy Names section, click the drop-down arrows in the Start and End fields to select pharmacy names from a list. To include inactive pharmacies in the report, click the Include Inactive Pharmacies checkbox. To use color/shading in the report, select the Include Color/Shading checkbox. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Patient List Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Patient List Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Alphabetical Patient List Report The Alphabetical Patient List report generates an alphabetical list of patients. Sample Report To generate an alphabetical list of patients: Select Reports > Patient > Alphabetical . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patient List Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Related Topics Generating a List of Inactive Patients Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Provider Labels Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Provider Labels Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Alphabetical Provider Labels Report The Alphabetical Provider Labels report generates labels for providers. The labels are sorted alphabetically. The name and address of each provider are printed on the labels. Sample Label To run the Alphabetical Provider Labels report: Select Reports > Provider/Referring Dr > Provider > Labels > Alphabetical . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Provider Label Report window is displayed. Select the start and end provider last names. To print the provider�s home address on the labels, select Use Provider�s Home Address . Type the number of lines to print per label in the Lines Per Label field. Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Provider Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . Tip If the Use Provider's Home Address option is not selected, the office address is printed on the labels. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Referring Doctor List Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Referring Doctor List Report Patient and Doctor Referrals > Running Referral Reports Running the Alphabetical Referring Doctor List Report The Alphabetical Referring Doctor List report generates an alphabetical list of incoming and outgoing referring doctors. Sample Report To run the Alphabetical Referring Doctor List report: Select Reports > Provider/Referring Dr > Referring Dr > Alphabetical . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Referring Doctor Report Setup window is displayed. In the Referring Doctors section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending referring doctor names to include in the report. To include inactive referring doctors, click the Include Inactive Referring Doctors checkbox. Select the appropriate options from the Options section. To use color shading in the report, click the Include Color Shading checkbox. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Referring Doctor Labels Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Referring Doctor Labels Report Patient and Doctor Referrals > Running Referral Reports Running the Alphabetical Referring Doctor Labels Report The Alphabetical referring Doctor Labels report generates alphabetically sorted labels for referring doctors. Sample Label To run the Alphabetical Referring Doctor Labels report: Select Reports > Provider/Referring Dr > Referring Doctor > Labels > Alphabetical . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Referring Doctor Labels Report Setup window is displayed. In the Referring Doctors section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending doctor names from a list. To include inactive referring doctors, click the Include Inactive Referring Doctors checkbox. Select the appropriate option from the Options section. Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Referring Doctor Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Provider List Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Provider List Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Alphabetical Provider List Report The Alphabetical Provider List report generates a list of providers in alphabetical order. Sample Report To run the Alphabetical Provider List report: Select Reports > Provider/Referring Dr > Provider > Alphabetical . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Provider List Report Setup window is displayed. Select the start and end provider names. To include inactive providers in the report, select Include Inactive Providers . To print the provider�s home address in the report, select Use Provider�s Home Address . To use color shading in the report, select Include Color Shading . Click OK . Tip If the Use Provider's Home Address option is not selected, the office address is printed in the results. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Alphabetical Schools List Report
topics.body: Running the Alphabetical Schools List Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Alphabetical Schools List Report The Alphabetical Schools List report generates a list of schools in the database. The list is sorted alphabetically. Sample Report For each school, the report includes: Identification number Name Address Contact person To run the Alphabetical Schools List report: Select Reports > Insurance > Alphabetical School . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Schools By Name Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending school names to include in the report from the drop-down lists. To use color shading in the printed report, select Include Color Shading . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Alternating Recalls
topics.body: Setting Alternating Recalls Recalling Patients > Setting Up Recalls Setting Alternating Recalls You can recall a patient for two different procedures on an alternating basis. Example : You can set alternating recalls for exam-and-prophy and exam-and-bitewings. The patient receives a recall notice at the specified interval, but is recalled alternately for one procedure, then the other. To set alternating normal Recall information: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click a patient. The patient record is displayed. Type the patient date of birth in the DOB field and click the Recall tab. The default Normal Recall information is displayed in the Last Exam section. If you change the name of your Normal Recall system, the name of the Last Exam section changes. To select the first recall procedure, select the Code drop-down list. The ADA/Transaction Codes window is displayed. Double-click a code. The code is displayed in the Code drop-down list in the patient record. To set the number of time slots the Normal Recall procedure requires in the scheduler, type the number in the TU field. To select the alternate recall procedure, select the Alt Code drop-down list. The ADA/Transaction Codes window is displayed. Double-click a code. The code is displayed in the Alt Code drop-down list in the patient record. To set the number of time slots the alternate Recall procedure requires in the scheduler, type the number in the Alt TU field. If you update the notices sent count when running the Normal Recall report, the value in the Notices field is updated every time a recall notice is sent to the patient. If this option is not selected when running the Recall report, the value in the Notices field is always 0. Click OK . Click Close twice . In the Alt Code field, enter the alternative procedure code, and the number of required time units for that procedure in the Alt TU field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Amending Patient Clinical Notes
topics.body: Amending Patient Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes > Handling Patient Clinical Notes Amending Patient Clinical Notes If you have the proper security permissions, you can edit a patient clinical note on the day it is created; however, after that time has passed, you can only amend a patient clinical note with additional text. Each amendment is automatically dated, and the original note and all amendments can be viewed in the Clinical Notes History window. To amend a clinical note: While a patient�s chart is displayed in the Chart for window, do one of the following: Click the Notes tab. Select Clinical Notes > View Clinical Notes. Click Add a descriptive note in the main Charting toolbar. The Patient Clinical Notes List window is displayed. Select the note to amend and click Amend Note Text . The note is displayed in the Patient Clinical Notes window. Type the note text in the field. To view a clinical note with all amendments and revisions, click the Notes History tab. Select Reviewed by Dr. to indicate the doctor has seen this note. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Annotating Images
topics.body: Annotating Images Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Images Annotating Images To annotate an image: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tabs in the Chart for window, click the Clinical Images tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the image, right-click, and select Annotate . For more information, see Using Annotation Mode . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Annotation Mode
topics.body: Using Annotation Mode Handling Images While Charting Using Annotation Mode When the Clinical Imaging tab is displayed in annotation mode, you can add and edit annotation text and shapes for clinical images. You can annotate images from the following: Trophy software CS Imaging software LeadTools software The Image Capture toolbar is displayed when the tab is in capture mode. To view the tab in annotation mode, select Annotation Mode on the Clinical Imaging toolbar . Related Topics Viewing Images in Annotation Mode Adding Annotation Text and Notes Editing Annotation Text and Notes Adding Annotation Shape Tools Adding Stamps Deleting Annotations Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Document Manager - Adding Annotation Text and Notes
topics.body: Using the Document Manager - Adding Annotation Text and Notes Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents > Annotating Images Adding Annotation Text and Notes To add text or a note to an image: In the Documents for window, select the image, and click Annotate . The Annotate Image window is displayed. Click Annotation Text Tools . To specify the text or note text color, click the color box in the Attributes section. The Color window is displayed. Select the color and click OK . Select an option: To add text : Select Text and use the pointer to select the text insertion point. Type the text in the box. To add a note : Select Note and use the pointer to select the note insertion point. Type the note in the box. To hide text and note annotations, deselect Show Annotations . Click Save Annotations . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Automatically Posting Appointment Procedures
topics.body: Automatically Posting Appointment Procedures Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Automatically Posting Appointment Procedures To post appointment procedures automatically: In the InOffice window, select the patient appointment and click Check-Out at the top of the window. The patient�s appointment information and the scheduled procedures are displayed in the Appointment Card window. To make changes to the procedures on the appointment card, click Edit , make the changes, and click OK . To auto-post all procedures on the appointment card, click Post . If no patient copayment is expected : The Transactions window is displayed. If an insurance copayment is expected : The transaction code associated with a check payment is displayed in the Transactions - Check Payment On window. Click Close . If Do not process is selected from the Insurance/Walkout/Appointment questions after transaction screen drop-down list in the General System Settings window : The InOffice window is displayed. If Automatically process is selected from the Insurance/Walkout/Appointment questions after transaction screen drop-down list in the General System Settings window : The Transactions Submission window is displayed. The current day is displayed in the Start Date and End Date fields. Click OK . The insurance claim information for the posted procedures is displayed in the Claim window. Review the claim information and click OK . The patient Appointments window is displayed. If Ask to process is selected from the Insurance/Walkout/Appointment questions after transaction screen drop-down list in the General System Settings window : A dialog box is displayed. Select one of the following options: Submit Insurance : Select and click OK to display the Transaction Submission window. Specify a date range for claims to be submitted. If the patient does not have insurance, this checkbox is disabled. For more information, see Submitting Dental And Medical Claims . Schedule Appointment : Select and click OK to display the Daily Appointments window. Schedule a follow-up patient appointment. For more information, see Scheduling Follow-Up Appointments . Print Walkout Statement : Select and click OK to display the Output Options window. Select the option from which to print the statement. Reconcile Treatment Plan Items : This option is enabled only when the patient has a treatment plan. Select and click OK to reconcile the posted transaction with transactions that are included in the treatment plan. You can delete and add treatment plan procedures. After you have completed all processing, the InOffice window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Appointments in the Book At A Glance Window
topics.body: Handling Appointments in the Book At A Glance Window Using the Scheduler > Understanding Book At A Glance Window Options Handling Appointments in the Book At A Glance Window Scheduling appointments in the Book At A Glance window is the same as in the Daybook. Color scheduling colors are not displayed in the window. To see appointment details: Right-click an appointment. Select Zoom on the floating menu that is displayed, or double-click the appointment to bring up the appointment card as in the Daybook. Related Topics Using Book At A Glance Appointment Submenu Commands Using Book At A Glance Time Slot Submenu Commands Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Appointment Conflicts
topics.body: Handling Appointment Conflicts Scheduling Appointments Handling Appointment Conflicts If you are scheduling an appointment in the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows, a warning message is displayed if one of the following scheduling conflicts occurs: The appointment conflicts with the provider's schedule. The appointment exceeds the pre-defined number of Medicaid/capitation/PPO patient appointments. The appointment exceeds available time slots. The appointment does not meet the specified color requirements for the time slots. This message is displayed only when color scheduling is enabled. These messages: Do not prevent you from scheduling the appointment Only inform you of the circumstance. To override the warning and schedule the patient, click Yes in the message window. Related Topics Scheduling Multiple Procedures Scheduling Order for Multiple Procedures Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Appointment Reminder Labels
topics.body: Printing Appointment Reminder Labels Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports Printing Appointment Reminder Labels You can print appointment reminder labels for patients scheduled during a specified period. The labels include the patient's name and mailing address. You can also define the number of lines to print per label. Sample Label To print appointment reminder labels: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Labels/Postcards/Dir > Appt Remind Labels . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Patient Reminder Labels Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, do the following: Click the drop-down arrows to select the Start and Stop dates from a calendar. To print labels for appointments made a specified number of days before the current date, type the number in the field, or leave as 0. In the Patient User Codes section, type the user codes you want to include or exclude from printing. In the Appointments section, deselect an option to limit the types of appointments for which labels are printed. In the Lines Per Label section, type the number of lines to print on each label. In the Dentist IDs section, click the drop-down arrows in the Start and Stop fields to select a range of doctors, or leave 999 to include all doctors. In the Options section, select from the following: Deselect the Exclude if no Address option if you want to print a label for patients with no address. Select the Include Inactive Patients option to print labels for inactive patients. In the Code Ranges section, click the drop-down arrows to select up to five code ranges for which labels are printed. To specify a single code, type the code in the left column and leave the right column 0. Click OK . I f you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK. OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Appointment Reminder Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Book At A Glance Appointment Submenu Commands
topics.body: Using Book At A Glance Appointment Submenu Commands Using the Scheduler > Understanding Book At A Glance Window Options > Handling Appointments in the Book At A Glance Window Using Book At A Glance Appointment Submenu Commands The commands available from the appointments submenu in the Book At A Glance window are: Appt Book : Select this command to display the Daily Appointments window. Month At A Glance : Select this command to display the Month At A Glance window. History Appointments : Select this command to display the History Appointments Date Range window. In this window, you can select a specific date range of appointments to display. AutoSched New Appt : Click an available time slot and select this command to auto schedule an appointment for a new patient. The Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters for window is displayed. In this window, you can enter auto scheduling restrictions for the new patient. View Appt : Select this command to display the Appointment Card window. In this window, you can view, add, edit, and delete procedures scheduled for the appointment. Edit Appt : Select this command to display the Appointment Card window in EDIT mode. In this window, you can view, add, edit, and delete procedures scheduled for the appointment. Confirm Appt : Select this command to display the Confirm Appt for window. In this window, you can confirm patient information. After you confirm the appointment, you are prompted to log a contact for the call. Auto Sched Appt : Select this command to display the Auto Scheduler Date Range window. In this window, you can set a date range within which to auto schedule a patient appointment. See Setting the Auto Scheduling Book . Cancel Appt : Select this command to display the Cancel/Broken Appointments Wizard window. The appointment information is temporarily saved, and you have the option to reschedule the appointment or save the information to the Tickler file. Shorten: Select this command to shorten the highlighted appointment by one time unit. Lengthen: Select this command to lengthen the highlighted appointment by one time unit. Zoom : Select this command to display the Assistant window. In this window, you can view additional patient and appointment information. You can also access this information from the Zoom appointment icon. Check In : Select this command to check a patient in. eForms > Review eForms: Click an appointment and select this command to display a list of eForms completed by the patient. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Send eForms Now: Click an appointment and select this command to send eForms to the patient now. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Add/Remove eForms: Click an appointment and select this command to display a window where you can add or remove eForms templates for the appointment. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Patient Settings: Click an appointment and select this command to display a window where you can edit a patient's eForms settings. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. HIPAA Acknowledgement : Select this command to display the Contacts for window. In this window, you can add HIPAA specific contact information for the patient. Change Location: Select this command to move a patient from the waiting room to the operatory or from the operatory to the waiting room. Check Out : Select this command to check a patient out and display the Appointment Card window.

topics.title: Viewing Appointments and Financials
topics.body: Viewing Appointments and Financials Using PracticeToGo > Viewing SoftDent Data in Microsoft Outlook Viewing Appointments and Financials In Microsoft Outlook, your SoftDent appointments and financial information transfer to the Microsoft Outlook Calendar. TIP: To easily view appointments and financials in the Microsoft Outlook software, select to "view by category." In the Calendar, select View > Arrange By > Current View > By Category. To view appointments and financials in Microsoft Outlook: Open the Calendar. If you have elected to "view by category," the information in the calendar is sectioned into categories. The SoftDent categories are called: SD Appointments and SD Finances. The category is displayed in the list, if an entry or entries for that category have transferred during the data sync. To view the record list in a category, click the plus sign. The list of records that transferred during the data sync is displayed. To view an individual record in a category, select the record entry, and double-click the entry to open it. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Appointments and Financials
topics.body: Viewing Appointments and Financials Using PracticeToGo > Viewing SoftDent Data on the Palm Organizer Viewing Appointments and Financials To view appointment or financial information: Select an option: On the Palm Home screen, tap the Date Book icon. Tap the SoftDent icon and select Appointments or Financials. The Palm Date Book is displayed. Office appointment information is in the Palm Date Book, along with any other scheduling information you have entered. If you have scheduling information for more than one day, you can navigate through the Date Book in the usual manner. To view practice financials, scroll to the top of the Date Book screen. The Palm organizer shows receivables (production and collections) for a past or current day, and goals and scheduled production for a future day or days, according to your User Preferences settings in the PC SoftDent software. You will see the titles SD PRODUCTION and SD COLLECTIONS, or the title SD GOALS. To view the financial information, tap the note icon to the right of the title. When you synchronize, financial information�including all receivables from past days and scheduled production and goals for future days (as specified in the User Preferences window)�is transferred to the Palm organizer. However, production and collections for today will only display if you have run a Daysheet report, and these totals will only be accurate for the day if you have run a Final Daysheet report. If you select a day in the future in the Date Book and you have set goals in the PC SoftDent software, you will see the title SD GOALS. To see the financial projections, tap the note icon to the right of the title. Daily goal information passed to the Palm organizer is based on goals and scheduled production for a future date. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Appointment Icons
topics.body: Understanding Appointment Icons Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows > Handling Appointments in the Daily Appointments Window Understanding Appointment Icons The icons are displayed at the bottom of patient appointments in the Daily Appointments window. Click the icon for the description. The Color Mismatch icon is displayed if the procedures scheduled for the appointment do not conform to color scheduling. Double-click this icon to display the Appointment Card window. In this window, you can select procedures that conform to color scheduling. For more information, see Setting Up Color Scheduling. The Color Match icon is displayed if the appointment conforms to color scheduling. Double-click this icon to display the Appointment Card window. In this window, you can select procedures to be performed during the selected appointment. For more information, see Setting Up Color Scheduling. Click the Zoom icon to display additional appointment and patient information in the Assistant window. The View Medical Alert icon is displayed if the patient has a medical alert. Click the icon to display the patient�s medical alert information in the Assistant window. The Dial Patient for Shortcall icon is displayed if the Short Call checkbox is selected in the Appointment Card window. Click the icon to display the auto dialer in the Patient Phone Numbers window. The View Patient Portrait icon is displayed if there is a patient portrait. Click this icon to view the portrait associated with the patient. You can also access the patient�s portrait using the Patient�s Portrait command located in the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. You can also access this command from the appointments shortcut menu in the Daily Appointments window. The yellow telephone icon is displayed when an appointment does not occupy enough slots to display all of a patient's telephone numbers. Roll over the icon to view the phone numbers. Click the icon to display the Auto Dialer in the Patient Phone Numbers window. The New icon is displayed when the appointment is scheduled for a new patient. After the patient is activated, this icon is no longer displayed. For more information, see Activating New Accounts or Patients. The Lab Case is outstanding icon is displayed when there is an outstanding lab case associated with the patient. Click this icon to display the Lab Case window. For more information, see Scheduling Appointments with Lab Cases. The Lab Case has been returned icon is displayed after a lab case associated with the patient has been returned. Click this command to display the Lab Case window. For more information, see Scheduling Appointments with Lab Cases. The Hidden Slot Note Exists! icon is displayed when a note is associated with the time slot. Click this icon to display the note in the Assistant window. The User Code icon is displayed when patients are assigned user codes. Roll your cursor over this icon to display the associated user code.
