The Forms Need to be Completed/Signed icon is displayed if the patient has eForms that are not complete. Click this icon to open the eForms Review window. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. The Forms Need to be Reviewed icon is displayed if the patient has submitted eForms that need to be reviewed by the office. Click this icon to open the eForms Review window. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. I f a note is associated with an appointme

topics.title: Viewing Appointment Information for the Practice
topics.body: Viewing Appointment Information for the Practice Using the Scheduler > Understanding Book At A Glance Window Options Viewing Appointment Information for the Practice To view information for the entire practice in the Book At A Glance window: Select Select Practice from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Appointment List Report
topics.body: Running the Appointment List Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports Running the Appointment List Report The Appointment List report lists all appointments scheduled within a specified date range. This report includes the patient's appointment time, ID, name, chart number, phone numbers, age, appointment provider ID, account balance, and the appointment reason. It also displays the date the appointment was made, the fee, the patient's portion, the estimated insurance payment, and any medical alerts. Sample Report To run the Appointment List report: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Appointment List . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Appointment List Report Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the Start and Stop dates from a calendar. In the Patient User Codes section, type the user codes you want to include or exclude from the report. Select Include Inactive Patients to include inactive patients in the report. In the Lab Cases Info section, deselect a checkbox to include only lab or non-lab cases. In the Doctors section, click the drop-down arrow to select the doctors from a list, or leave 999 to report on all doctors. In the Hygienists section, click the drop-down arrow to select the hygienists from a list, or leave 999 to report on all hygienists. In the Code Ranges section, click the drop-down arrows to select up to five code ranges on which to report. To specify a single code, type the code in the left column and leave the right column 0. If you selected the Merge Letter Expert output option, the Letter Expert Template ID field is active. Click the drop-down arrow to select a template from the list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Appointment Properties
topics.body: Setting Appointment Properties Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows > Handling Appointments in the Daily Appointments Window Setting Appointment Properties You can set the color in which an appointment is displayed when it is: Selected. Confirmed. A confirmed and selected appointment. Being dragged to a new slot, appointment text color, appointment background color, and the display preferences for the patient information. To restore the system defaults, click Restore Defaults in the Appointment Properties window. To set properties that dictate how appointment information is displayed in the Daily Appointments window: Select from the following options: Select Scheduler > Daily Appointments . In any window : Click Alt + F2. In the Patient Appointments window : Select Daily Appt Book from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the InOffice window : Select Daily Appt Book from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Select Go to Daily Appointments in the Power Bar. The Daily Appointments window is displayed. Select an appointment, right-click, and select Properties ; or select Properties > Appointment from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Appointment Properties window is displayed. Select an appointment template from the Appointment Template drop-down list. If you select the Front Office template : The patient name, patient home and work phone numbers, and scheduled procedural codes are displayed. If you select the Front Office - HIPAA template : The patient initials and scheduled procedural codes are displayed. The patient home and work phone numbers are not displayed. If you select the Back Office template : The patient name, scheduled procedural codes, and the patient home and work phone numbers are displayed. If you select the Back Office - HIPAA template : The patient initials and scheduled procedural codes are displayed. The patient home and work phone numbers are not displayed. Select one of the HIPAA templates to turn on HIPAA privacy in the Daily Appointments window. For more information, see Setting Daily Appointments Window Privacy . To change the text color for patient names, patient home phone numbers, patient work numbers, provider identification numbers, and office codes, click the square next to the appropriate item. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . When selecting a text color, ensure adequate contrast with the other scheduler window colors. Select a format to display patient names in the Daily Appointments window from the Name Format section. Select a format to display patient phone numbers in the Daily Appointments window from the Phone Format section. Select an option: To display the tooth number and surface information associated with the scheduled procedure in the appointment time slot : Select Show Tooth and Surface . To display the patient age in the appointment time slot : Select Show Patient Age . To display the patient age if the patient is younger than a specific age in the appointment time slot : Select Show Patient Age , select Show if Age less than , and type the age in the field. To display a background color in appointment time slots, select a background from the Background Image drop-down list. To change the background color for appointment time slots, click the square next to the appropriate item in the Background Color Options section. The Color window is displayed.

topics.title: Running the Appointments Tracking List Report
topics.body: Running the Appointments Tracking List Report Running the Appointments Tracking List Report The Appointments Tracking List report contains appointment information for a specific patient. The report includes the following details: appointment date; appointment type; treatment units; appointment fee; doctor ID and name; notes you have entered concerning specific appointments; and separate fee totals for missed, broken, and late appointments. To run the Appointments Tracking List report: Select an option: In the Patient List window, select a patient and click Other Management > Missed/Broken/Late Appointments . In the Patient window, click Other Management > Missed/Broken/Late Appointments . The Tracked Appointments window is displayed. Click Print . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Appointments Tracking Report window is displayed. Click OK . Related Topics Tracking Missed, Broken, and Late Appointments Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Appointment Reminder Postcards
topics.body: Printing Appointment Reminder Postcards Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports Printing Appointment Reminder Postcards You can print appointment reminder postcards to remind your patients of the date and time of their next scheduled appointment. Sample Postcard To print appointment reminder postcards: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Labels/Postcards/Dir > Appt Remind Postcards . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual postcards on a dot-matrix printer, select Printer . To print postcards on a laser printer, select Laser Postcard File . Click OK . The Confirmation List Report Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, do the following: Click the drop-down arrows to select the Start and Stop dates from a calendar. To print postcards for appointments made a specified number of days before the current date, type the number in the field, or leave as 0. In the Patient User Codes section, type the user codes you want to include or exclude from printing. In the Appointments section, deselect an option to limit the types of appointments for which postcards are printed. In the Dentist IDs section, click the drop-down arrows in the Start and Stop fields to select a range of doctors, or leave 999 to include all doctors. In the Options section, select from the following: Deselect the Exclude if no Address option if you want to print postcards for patients with no address. Select the Include Inactive Patients option to print postcards for inactive patients. In the Code Ranges section, click the drop-down arrows to select up to five code ranges for which to print postcards. To specify a single code, type the code in the left column and leave the right column 0. Click OK . I f you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK. OR If you selected the Laser Postcard File option, s elect Reports > Print Laser Postcards . The Laser Postcards window is displayed, and the number of postcards to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Appt Remind Postcards , and select Print Selected from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the postcards should be cleared. Verify that the postcards have printed correctly, and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Appointment Reports
topics.body: Appointment Reports Scheduling Appointments Appointment Reports The following reports are available under Recall/Appt on the Report menu: Daily Op Schedule : Lists all patients due in the office, their scheduled providers, and notes/alerts for all operatories. If specified, tooth and surface/quadrant information also is displayed. Operatory notes print on a separate page. Three formats are available. Laser Daily Op Schedule : Designed for laser printers. Four operatories print on a page. An envelope symbol means there is a note on the notes page. A flag symbol means there is a medical alert on the notes page. A bell symbol means there is both a note and a medical alert. A telephone symbol means the appointment is confirmed. The patient�s name is italicized and bold if it is a new patient. Daily Op Schedule : Suitable for any printer. Three operatories print on a page. An "N" followed by a number means there is a note with that number on the notes page. An "A" followed by a number means there is a medical alert with that number on the notes page. An asterisk (*) following the patient�s name means the appointment is confirmed. An asterisk before the patient�s name means it is a new patient. Six Room Operatory Schedule : Similar to the Daily Op Schedule, except the columns are compressed so six operatories fit on a page. The patient name is displayed in all capital letters if the appointment is confirmed. An asterisk before the patient�s name means it is a new patient. Recalls : Lets you set up different recall notices for different groups of patients. See Recalling Patients for more information. User-Defined Recalls : Enables you to generate user-defined recall notices. Missed Appointments : Lists and removes missed appointments from the scheduler. Do not run this report for a future date. Tickler File : Lists patients in the Tickler file. Confirmation List : Lists the confirmation status of patient appointments for a specified date. Appointment List : Lists all appointments for a specified date range. End of Day Call Backs : Lists any appointments tagged for a follow-up call at the end of the day. These appointments have the Call Back field marked in the Transactions Codes information screen or the Transaction window. Short Call List : Lists scheduled patients who are willing to move their appointment if there is an opening earlier in the schedule and/or patients with Tickler entries who are willing to schedule an appointment on short notice. Labels/Postcards/Dir : Prints appointment and Tickler postcards and labels. You can also print recall and confirmation phone directories. Routing Slips By Date : Prints routing slips for a specific date for one or all providers. Related Topics P ractice Management Reports Audit Trail Reports H andling the Daily Operatory Schedule Report Running the Missed Appointments Report Running the Tickler File Report Running the Confirmation List Report Running the Appointment List Report Running the End of Day Call Backs Report Running the Short Call List Report Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports Running the Routing Slips By Date Report Running Daily Operatory Schedule Reports Need more information? topics.title: Using the Appointment Submenu Commands
topics.body: Using the Appointment Submenu Commands Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows > Handling Appointments in the Daily Appointments Window Using the Appointment Submenu Commands The commands available from the appointments submenu in the Daily Appointments window are: Book Quick Glance : Select this command to display the Book At A Glance window. Month At A Glance : Select this command to display the Month At A Glance window. History Appointments : Select this command to display the History Appointments Date Range window. In this window, you can select a specific date range of appointments to display. AutoSched New Appt : Click an available time slot and select this command to auto schedule an appointment for a new patient. The Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters for window is displayed. In this window, you can enter auto scheduling restrictions for the new patient. View Appointment : Select this command to display the Appointment Card window. In this window, you can view, add, edit, and delete procedures scheduled for the appointment. Edit Appointment : Select this command to display the Appointment Card window in EDIT mode. In this window, you can view, add, edit, and delete procedures scheduled for the appointment. Confirm Appointment : Select this command to display the Confirm Appt for window. In this window, you can confirm patient information. After you confirm the appointment, you are prompted to log a contact for the call. Auto Schedule Appointment : Select this command to display the Auto Scheduler Date Range window. In this window, you can set a date range within which to auto schedule a patient appointment. See Setting the Auto Scheduling Book . Cancel Appointment : Select this command to display the Cancel/Broken Appointments Wizard window. The appointment information is temporarily saved, and you have the option to reschedule the appointment or save the information to the Tickler file. Print Routing Slip: Select this command to print the patient's routing slip. See Manually Printing Routing Slips . Shorten : Click an appointment and select this command to shorten the appointment by one time unit. Lengthen : Click an appointment and select this command to lengthen the appointment by one time unit. Zoom : Select this command to display the Assistant window. In this window, you can view additional patient and appointment information. You can also access this information from the Zoom appointment icon. Check In : Select this command to check a patient in. The background of the appointment is changed to blue to indicate that the patient is checked in. eForms > Review eForms: Click an appointment and select this command to display a list of eForms completed by the patient. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Send eForms Now: Click an appointment and select this command to send eForms to the patient now. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Resend eForms: Click an appointment and select this command to resend eForms to the patient. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Add/Remove eForms: Click an appointment and select this command to display a window where you can add or remove eForms templates for the patient. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Patient Settings: Click an appointment and select this command to display a window where you can edit a patient's eForms settings. This option is enabled on

topics.title: Setting Daily Appointments Window Properties
topics.body: Setting Daily Appointments Window Properties Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows > Setting Daily Appointment Window Properties and Using Profiles Setting Daily Appointments Window Properties To customize the look of open time slots in the Daily Appointments window: In the Daily Appointments window, right-click an empty time slot, and select Properties ; or select Properties > Appt Book from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Properties... window is displayed. The access status for the selected profile is displayed beneath the profile name. Select an option: To add a background image to each open time slot for the selected profile : Select it from the Background Images drop-down list. To change the fonts used for text that is displayed in each open time slot for the selected profile : Click Change Font . The Font window is displayed. Select the fonts from the Font, Font Style, and Size lists. To apply a specific character set to the selected font, select it from the Script drop-down list. Click OK . If you are using color scheduling and want to display a descriptive label in time slots that have been assigned a color : Select Show Color Wording . For example, if you have assigned yellow to indicate slots that should be scheduled with hygiene procedures, the word Hygiene is displayed in an open slot that is painted with this color. To change the background color for open time slots for the selected profile : Click the square next to Background Color. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the line color for open time slots for the selected profile : Click the square next to Line Color. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the line color that delineates each hour for the selected profile : Click the square next to Hourly Color. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To restore the default text colors : Click Restore Defaults . When selecting colors, ensure adequate contrast with the other scheduler window colors. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Archive and Delete Utility
topics.body: Running the Archive and Delete Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running the Transaction History Maintenance Utilities Running the Archive and Delete Utility Run this utility to transfer compressed history transactions to an archive file and clear the compressed transactions from the main database. Running the utility frees hard disk space. To view archived transactions, use the audit trail utilities on the System menu or run the Print Archived Transaction History utility . Save a complete backup in addition to your regular backup before running this utility. To run this utility: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Transaction Hist Maintenance > Archive & Delete . Enter the drive and directory in which you want to store the TRANS.HST file. Click Apply . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Assigning a Discount to a Patient
topics.body: Assigning a Discount to a Patient Posting Payments > Handling Discounts Assigning a Discount to a Patient When you assign a discount to a patient record and post a discountable procedure for the patient, the discount is calculated. You can assign any discount to a patient. To assign a discount to a patient, from the Info tab of the Patient window: Select the discount from the Discount drop-down list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Associating Images with Teeth
topics.body: Associating Images with Teeth Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Images Associating Images with Teeth To associate an image with a specific tooth: On the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select Organization Mode from the Clinical Imaging toolbar. Drag the image from the Clinical Images tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container section, and drop it on the tooth on the Clinical Imaging tab. A yellow dot is displayed on the tooth to indicate that an image is associated with it. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Associating with Insurance Plans
topics.body: Associating with Insurance Plans Setting Up Insurance > Using Allowance Tables Associating an Allowance Table with an Insurance Plan After you create an allowance table, you can attach it to an insurance plan. All bluebook entries created for the insurance plan are updated when the allowance table is updated. After an insurance plan is associated with an allowance table, you can no longer update the entries information from the Bluebook entries for window. These entries must be updated from the allowance table. To associate an allowance table with an insurance plan: In the Insurance Plan window, click Coverage . The tab is the active window. Select the allowance table from the Allowance Table drop-down list. The table is associated and bluebook entries are created. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Assistant in Transactions Windows
topics.body: Using the Assistant in Transactions Windows Handling Transactions and Codes > Posting Patient Procedures Using the Assistant in Transactions Windows The Assistant window is displayed in a popup window that contains information about the patient�s transaction. Related Topics Displaying the Assistant in the Transactions Window Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Attaching and Detaching Documents
topics.body: Attaching and Detaching Documents Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Attaching and Detaching Documents You can attach or detach a document to additional records. You can perform this function only for documents that are displayed beneath the Documents section of the tree in the Documents for window. To attach a document to or to detach a document from another record: In the Documents for window, select the document from the Documents section of the tree, and select Attach/Detach to other Rec(s) from the Options section of the Express bar. The Document(s) scan window is displayed. Select an option: To detach the document from a record : Deselect the checkbox next to the record. To attach the document to a record : Select the record type from the Record Type drop-down list, click Select Record , and select the record. Click OK . A message asks you to confirm the action. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Audit Trail Reports
topics.body: Audit Trail Reports Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports Audit Trail Reports The following reports are found under Reports > Audit Trail > Scheduler Audit Trail : Scheduler Audit Trail by Date: Generates a list of every appointment creation, modification, or deletion within a specified date range. The reported is sorted by date. Scheduler Audit Trail by User: Generates a list of every appointment creation, modification, or deletion within a specified date range. The reported is sorted by user. Scheduler Audit Trail by Patient Name: Generates a list of every appointment creation, modification, or deletion within a specified date range. The reported is sorted by patient name. Related Topics Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Date Report Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By User Report Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Patient Name Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Audit Trails
topics.body: Using Audit Trails System Security and HIPAA Compliance Using Audit Trails Using the audit trails features you can track the following: Transactions Scheduling Prescriptions Patient Information Charts Security settings PracticeToGo Reports Archived Transactions Time Cards The following are tracked in the audit trail: Name of the user who performs a security-protected task Date and time the task is performed You can generate the following Audit Trail reports: Transaction Audit Trail : Contains a record of every deletion or modification, including back-dating, to transactions, patients, and account records. Scheduler Audit Trail : Tracks the dates and times appointments were made, shortened, lengthened, canceled, or entered into the tickler file. Prescription Audit Trail : Contains a record of every prescription issued by a provider. Patient Information Audit Trail: Contains a record of all changes made to patient demographic data (excluding changes to clinical data). Charting Audit Trail : Lists every patient's deleted chart or clinical note. HIPAA Security Audit Trail : Documents failed logins to the software due to password error and successful overrides of features that require passwords. PracticeToGo Audit Trail : Tracks Palm users and the work performed. Reports Audit Trail : Supplies you with a description of all reports run by your office. Time Card Audit Trail: Displays a list of changes made to employees' time cards. Related Topics Viewing Audit Trails Running Transaction Audit Trail Reports Running Scheduler Audit Trail Reports Running Prescription Audit Trail Reports Running Patient Information Audit Trail Reports Running Charting Audit Trail Reports Running HIPAA Security Audit Trail Reports Running PracticeToGo Audit Trail Reports Running Reports Audit Trail Reports Running Time Card Audit Trail Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Reports Audit Trail By Date Report
topics.body: Running the Reports Audit Trail By Date Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Reports Audit Trail Reports Running the Reports Audit Trail By Date Report The Reports Audit Trail by Date report tracks the reports run from SoftDent. The report includes the date and time a report was printed, the user who printed the report, and the output option used for the report. Sample Report To generate a list of reports by the date on which they were generated: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Reports Audit Trail > By Date . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Reports Audit Trail By Date window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Reports Audit Trail By User Report
topics.body: Running the Reports Audit Trail By User Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Reports Audit Trail Reports Running the Reports Audit Trail By User Report The Reports Audit Trail by User report tracks the reports run from SoftDent. The report includes the date and time a report was printed, the user who printed the report, and the output option used for the report. Sample Report To generate a list of reports by the user who generated each report: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Reports Audit Trail > By User . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Reports Audit Trail By User window is displayed. Type the starting and ending users in the appropriate fields. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Charting Audit Trail By User Report
topics.body: Running the Charting Audit Trail By User Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Charting Audit Trail Reports Running the Charting Audit Trail By User Report The Charting Audit Trail by User report generates a list of every patient chart or clinical note deleted by a specific user. Sample Report To generate a list of chart or clinical note deletions by the user who deleted the information: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Charting Audit Trail > By User . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Charting Audit Trail By User window is displayed. Type the starting and ending user name in the appropriate fields. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Auto Advance for Perio Charting
topics.body: Using Auto Advance for Perio Charting Periodontal Charting Using Auto Advance for Perio Charting When using Auto Advance, the default charting path is used. To use Auto Advance: In the Chart for window, select Perio > Options . The Perio Properties window is displayed. Select Auto Advance in the Data Display section of the window. When charting by arch : The default path begins with the distal facial surface of #1 and continues on the facial to the distal facial surface of #16, then moves to the distal lingual of #16 and continues on the lingual to the distal lingual of #1, then moves to the distal lingual of #17 and continues on the lingual to the distal lingual of #32. When charting by quadrant : The default path begins at the facial surface of tooth #1 and continues on the facial to the mesial facial of tooth #8, then goes to the mesial lingual of tooth #8, and continues on the lingual to tooth #1, then goes to the distal facial of tooth #16 and continues on the facial to the mesial facial of tooth #9, then moves to the mesial lingual of tooth #9 and continues on the lingual to the distal lingual of tooth #16. Then it drops down to the distal facial of tooth #17 and continues on the facial to the mesial facial of tooth #24, then to the mesial lingual of tooth #24 and continues on the lingual to the distal lingual of tooth #17, then moves to the distal facial of tooth #32 and continues on the facial to the mesial facial of tooth #25 and continues on the lingual to the distal lingual of tooth #32. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accessing the Auto Dialer
topics.body: Accessing the Auto Dialer Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Handling Contact Expert Tasks Accessing the Auto Dialer Use the contact expert to organize and store your telephone contacts. You can call a contact and make the appropriate notes from the Contact Expert window. To use the auto dialer, a modem must be installed on your computer. If no modem is installed, the contact�s telephone number is displayed for your reference. To access the auto dialer: From the Contact Expert window, select a record from the list in the left pane. Click Dial . The SmartDialer window is displayed. Click Dial . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Pre-Authorized Coverage Amounts
topics.body: Updating Pre-Authorized Coverage Amounts Treatment Planning > Submitting Treatment Plans for Pre-Authorization Updating Pre-Authorized Coverage Amounts To update a pre-authorized coverage amount, do one of the following: Post the estimated insurance payment in the Treatment Plan window, using codes 2.00-2.89 : The estimated payment for the entire claim is displayed. Using this method, the Explanation Of Benefits (EOB) is your only record of estimated payments for each procedure on the claim. Edit the insurance payment amount in the Transaction Codes window to update the Ins Amt field : Change the Type field from Primary to Estimate to note that the pre-authorization for the procedure has been received from the insurance company, and the insurance payment estimate has been updated to match the EOB information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Authenticating an Existing SSO Account and Configuring the SUS
topics.body: Authenticating an Existing SSO Account and Configuring the SUS Getting Started > Using the Software Update Service (SUS) Authenticating an Existing SSO Account and Configuring the SUS To get the full benefit of the SUS, your SSO account must be authenticated and associated with your account number/CustomerID. If you created an SSO account for a service that does not require an account number or if you did not know your account ID at the time you created your SSO account, your SSO account is not authenticated. You will receive only critical updates to your software until you authenticate your SSO account. To authenticate an existing SSO account and configure the SUS: Right-click the triangle logo and select Configure Update Service . The Software Update Service � Configure the Software Update Service window is displayed. Note the [Authenticate] notation in the Account No. section. If you have multiple accounts, select an account from the list. Click Authenticate Account . The Software Update Service � Authenticate Account window is displayed. Type your account number/CustomerID and your zip code, and click Authenticate . The Software Update Service � Account Details Accepted window is displayed. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using AutoCaps
topics.body: Using AutoCaps Getting Started > Entering Data Using AutoCaps The AutoCaps feature automatically capitalizes specific text and is enabled in the following fields: Names : The first letter typed, any letter following a space, and any letter that directly follows an end-of-sentence punctuation mark, hyphen, or colon are automatically capitalized in the Last Name, Company Name, Plan Name, or other name fields. Notes and Medical Alerts : The first letter typed and any letter that directly follows an end-of-sentence punctuation mark or colon are automatically capitalized in the Notes or Medical Alerts fields. Addresses : The first letter typed, any letter following a space, and any letter that directly follows an end-of-sentence punctuation mark, hyphen, or colon are automatically capitalized in Address fields. Find Bar : The first letter typed in the Find field and the first letter after a comma in the field are automatically capitalized. Related Topics Data Entry Tips Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Automatically Running Office Expert Items
topics.body: Automatically Running Office Expert Items Using Experts > Using the Office Expert > Running Office Expert Items Automatically Running Office Expert Items To run office expert items automatically: From the Experts menu, select Office Expert . The Office Expert window is displayed. Select When To Run? from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Office Expert Run Options window is displayed. Select an option: To run items every day at a specified time : Select Every day at a specified time and type the time you want the items to run in the field. To run items every day when you log in : Select Every day when the user logs in . To run items only when a user requests : Select Only when manually asked to by a user . If you select this option, items are not automatically generated. Click OK . Related Topics Setting Office Expert Item Frequency Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Assigning Payer ID Numbers Automatically
topics.body: Assigning Payer ID Numbers Automatically eServices > Using the eClaims Service > Setting Up Insurance Companies Assigning Payer ID Numbers Automatically To assign payer ID numbers automatically: Select an option: In the eServices window : Click Insurance Setup . From the main menu : Select Setup > Insurance Co/Plans . The Payer Setup window is displayed, listing all insurance companies in the database that accept claims electronically. Click Assign ID . The Insurance Company List window is displayed. Click Auto IDs . The Payer ID Automatic Assignment window is displayed. To assign paper electronic claims, select Assign all unmatching companies to be paper electronic claims . If you assign paper electronic claims, contact an eServices support representative for a free review of your setup. A technician can assist you with correcting potential errors. Click Yes . The payer ID numbers are displayed in the Payer column of the Insurance Company List window. Click Exit to save the settings. A message informs you that payer identification numbers have been automatically assigned. Click Yes . The Payer Setup window is displayed. Click Exit . Every 30 days, you are prompted to update insurance plans automatically. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Automated Statements Service
topics.body: Using the Automated Statements Service eServices Using the Automated Statements Service The Automated Statements Service enables you to send paper statements to patients via a clearinghouse, or you can send patients statements online. Automated Statements Electronic billing statements are transmitted by eServices and sent to a clearinghouse, where they are printed and mailed to your patients. You automatically receive a report, which lists the patients who will receive a statement. For more information, see Using Automated Statements . Online Statement Service With the Online Statement Service, an e-mail is sent to your patients with a link to a secure server, where they can view their statements online. If your office uses ePayments, your patients can also make a credit card or ACH payment online. For more information, see Using the Online Statement Service . Related Topics Using Automated Statements Using the Online Statement Service Running the Set Up Account Automated Statements Service Options Utility Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Auto Scheduling Appointments from Patient or Account Records
topics.body: Auto Scheduling Appointments from Patient or Account Records Scheduling Appointments > Auto Scheduling Patient Appointments Auto Scheduling Appointments from Patient or Account Records To auto schedule an appointment for an existing patient from a Patient or Account window: Select an option: To auto schedule an appointment for an existing patient from the Patient List window : Select the patient for whom to schedule the appointment and select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar. The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Auto Schedule from the Options section of the Express bar. To auto schedule an appointment for an existing patient from the Patient window : Select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar. The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Auto Schedule from the Options section of the Express bar. To auto schedule an appointment for an existing patient from the Account List window : Select the account with which the patient is associated and select Patient from the Options section of the Express bar. The Patients List window is displayed. Select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar. The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Auto Schedule from the Options section of the Express bar. To auto schedule an appointment for an existing patient from the Account window : Select Patient from the Options section of the Express bar. The Patients List window is displayed. Select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar. The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Auto Schedule from the Options section of the Express bar. The Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters window is displayed. Select a date range, type a time range, select the procedures for which the appointment is to be scheduled, and type the number of time slots and the slot usage schedule in the appropriate fields. The Time Slots and Slot Usage Schedule fields are populated automatically if the procedure is associated with a scheduler template. If a bluebook note is associated with the procedure in the patient's insurance plan, the Assistant window is displayed. Click the Preferred Days tab or press Ctrl + Tab to make the tab the active window. The default value for the Dr field is the dentist specified in the Doctor field of the patient record. If the procedure includes a hygiene code : The default value for the HYG field is the hygienist specified in the Hygienist field of the patient record. The default selections in the Days to Search section are the preferred days specified in the patient record. In the Search Rooms in Book section, click the drop-down arrow to select a book. If the procedures to be scheduled do not include a hygiene code: The default book is the book in the Auto Sched Book field in the dentist�s Provider window. If the dentist does not have a preferred auto scheduling book: The default book for the practice is displayed. If the procedures to be scheduled include a hygiene code: The default book is the book in the Auto Sched Book field in the hygienist's Provider window. If the hygienist does not have a preferred auto scheduling book: The default book for the practice is displayed. OR Click Specify Rooms . The Specify Rooms window is displayed. Do the following: Select the rooms you want to search to create a temporary schedule book. A temporary schedule book is automatically deleted after the appointment is scheduled. To save the room selections for

topics.title: Setting the Auto-Scheduling Book
topics.body: Setting the Auto-Scheduling Book Setting Up the Scheduler Setting the Auto-Scheduling Book An auto scheduler book contains only rooms and hours in which a provider wants to be scheduled for appointments. This book is set as the provider�s auto-scheduling book in the provider record. The rooms and hours in the selected book are used to auto schedule appointments for the provider. Any book can be selected as the auto-scheduling book for a provider. To select the auto-scheduling book for a provider: Select List > Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. Select a provider and click Edit . The Provider window is displayed. Select a book from the Auto Sched Book drop-down list. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Auto Scheduling Patient Appointments
topics.body: Auto Scheduling Patient Appointments Scheduling Appointments Auto Scheduling Patient Appointments Using the auto scheduler enables you to locate the most convenient time slot to schedule a patient appointment. You can schedule a patient appointment from available time slots that meet the: Patient�s preferred times Other appointments Closed days Provider vacation days Blocked times Lunch hour criteria If the patient accepts the time slot, you can schedule it with a single click. topics.title: Backing Up Data
topics.body: Backing Up Data Backing Up Data Data retained in your system depends on your system's hard drive. To decrease the possibility of a hard drive failure affecting your practice�s information, you should back up your data each day your system is used. When you perform a backup, you copy data from the computer�s hard drive to a separate medium. If data on your hard drive is lost or corrupted, you can typically perform a restore procedure, which copies the data from your most recent backup media onto the hard drive to restore it. This section includes general recommendations for creating a backup routine. It is recommended that you work with your hardware vendor to create and maintain a routine that is specific to your practice. If you have multiple sets of data residing in multiple locations, be sure to back up each set. Related Topics Using a Backup Checklist Establishing a Backup Routine Testing Your Backup System Files to Back Up Types of Backup Media Storing Backup Media Maintaining Backup Devices Replacing Backup Media Restoring Data Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Batched Billing Statements
topics.body: Printing Batched Billing Statements Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Printing Batched Billing Statements Batched statements can include: Billing statement messages Billing charges for overdue accounts Finance charges for overdue accounts An itemized list of account transactions Tip To send a statement to the batch, select an account in the Account List window, select Billing Statement , and click No when prompted to print the statement. To print batched billing statements: Select Reports > Accounting > Statements > Paper > Run Batch . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Single Billing Statement Setup window is displayed. Select from the following options: To include a message in the billing statement : Type the message in the Message field. To apply a different billing charge to the billing statements : Type the information in the appropriate fields. To apply a different finance charge to the billing statements : Type the information in the appropriate fields. To itemize transactions for a specific number of days : Type the number of days in the Itemize transactions for the last _ days field. The default value is 45. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing the Batched Statements Count
topics.body: Viewing the Batched Statements Count Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Viewing the Batched Statements Count You can view a list of statements that are batched. To view a list of batched statements: Select Reports > Accounting > Statements > Batch Count . The Statements In Batch window is displayed. Select from the following options: To display this window each time you access the software : Select Show this window when starting SoftDent . To close this window : Click Close . To clear the batched list without generating the statements : Click Clear Batch . A message that asks you to confirm whether to clear the batch is displayed. Click Yes . To run the batched statements : Click Run Batch . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Patient Labels by Birthday
topics.body: Printing Patient Labels by Birthday Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports > Printing Patient Labels Printing Patient Labels by Birthday To print patient labels by the patient�s birthday: Select Reports > Patient > Labels > Birthday Order . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Patient Labels Birthday Label Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient names and starting and ending patient birth dates from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include labels for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude labels for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. To restrict the patient labels to a specific age group : Type the starting and ending ages in the Age section. Type the number of lines per label in the Lines Per Label field. Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Patient Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the printer and click OK . A message is displayed. Verify that the labels have printed correctly and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Account Budget Plans
topics.body: Handling Account Budget Plans Planning Budgets Handling Account Budget Plans Using an account budget plan, you can transfer any part of the account balance (minus any insurable amounts) to a fixed revolving loan budget plan. Charges posted to the Patient Transactions or Account Transactions window can be adjusted and transferred to the Account Budget Plan window. This amount is kept as a separate balance. Each account can have only one budget plan : This plan can be applied to one or all patients in the account. When an account budget plan is set up, however, you cannot transfer any additional portions of the patient or account balance to the budget plan. In addition to receiving a monthly statement, accounts with budget plans receive a separate budget plan statement : If you do not want accounts with fixed loan budget plans to receive this separate statement, enter a tilde (~) symbol in the User Codes field in the common area of the Account window. Send budget plan statements in addition to regular monthly statements to update the account's budget plan status. Budget plan interest charges : Interest charges are added to the account when statements are run. Statements should be run at least once a month, since this enables the software to handle overpayments. Any extra payments posted to an account reduce the: Balance Following month's interest charge Related Topics Using Account Budget Plan List Window Options Selecting Budget Plan Coupon Formats Transferring Account Balances to a Fixed Loan Budget Plan Setting Up Account Balances for a Revolving Credit Budget Plan Posting Account Budget Plan Payments Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Account Budget Plan Reports
topics.body: Running Account Budget Plan Reports Budget Planning > Running Budget Plan Reports Running Account Budget Plan Reports You can run the following reports: Transaction List Account Budget Plan Missed Budget Plan Payment Related Topics Running the Transaction List Report Running the Account Budget Plan Report Running the Account Budget Plan Missed Payment Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Account Budget Plan Missed Payment Report
topics.body: Running the Account Budget Plan Missed Payment Report Budget Planning > Running Budget Plan Reports > Running Account Budget Plan Reports Running the Account Budget Plan Missed Payment Report The Account Budget Plan Missed Payment report g enerates a list of accounts with overdue budget plan payments. The report includes the account number; the name, address, and identification number of the account guarantor; the budget plan balance; the date of the last payment; and the regular payment amount. Sample Report To run the Account Budget Plan Missed Payment report: Select Reports > Accounting > Budget Plan Reports > Account Missed Payment . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Account Budget Plan Missed Payment Report window is displayed. Select an option: To restrict the report to a specific account user code : Type it in the Include field. To exclude a specific account user code : Type it in the Exclude field. To restrict the report to a specific set of accounts : Select the starting and ending account numbers from the drop-down lists. To restrict the report to accounts that do not have a posted payment in a specific number of days : Type the number of days in the Only include Accounts with last budget plan payments older than _ days. field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Book At A Glance Time Slot Submenu Commands
topics.body: Using Book At A Glance Time Slot Submenu Commands Using the Scheduler > Understanding Book At A Glance Window Options > Handling Appointments in the Book At A Glance Window Using Book At A Glance Time Slot Submenu Commands The commands available from the blank time slot submenu in the Book At A Glance window are: Appt Book : Select this command to display the Daily Appointments window. Month At A Glance : Select this command to display the Month At A Glance window. History Appointments : Select this command to display the History Appointments Date Range window. In this window, you can select a specific date range of appointments to display. Make Appt : Select this command to display the Patient List window. In this window, you can select a patient for scheduling. After the appointment is made, a yellow background is displayed in the time slot. New Patient Appt : Select this command to make an appointment for a new patient. The New Patient Appt window is displayed. In this window, you can enter new patient account information. After the appointment is made, a yellow background is displayed in the time slot. See Setting the Auto Scheduling Book . AutoSched New Patient Appt : Select this command to auto schedule an appointment for a new patient. The Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters for window is displayed. In this window, you can enter auto scheduling restrictions for the new patient. After the appointment is made, a yellow background is displayed in the time slot. Select Patient : Select this command to display the Patient List window. In this window, you can select a patient for whom to make an appointment. After the appointment is made, a yellow background is displayed in the time slot. Tickler > Scan Tickler : Select this command to display the Tickler Entry window for the patient who has been in the Tickler list the longest and whose procedures require the same amount of time available in the schedule. Tickler > Scan Tickler Short Calls : Select this command to display the Tickler Entry window for patients whose entries are marked "short call" and whose procedures require the same amount of time that is available in the schedule. Select Book : Select this command to display the Scheduler Books window. See Displaying Different Books . Select Room #: Click a time slot or appointment and select this command to display the Scheduler Rooms window. In this window, you can select a room to display in the Display Appointments window. See Viewing Additional Rooms . Block Slot : Select this command to display the Blocked Slots Properties window. In this window, you can block the time slot as unavailable for scheduling. See Using Blocked Time Slots . Add Note : Select this command to display the Assistant window. In this window, you can associate a note with a time slot. Only one note can be associated with a time slot. Slot notes are not displayed in the Book At A Glance window. C olumn Properties > Save Width as Default: Select this command to save the width of a column as the default. C olumn Properties > Make All Rooms Default Width: Select this command to make the width of all columns the default width. C olumn Properties > Make All Rooms This Width: Select this command to make the width of all columns the width of the selected column. C olumn Properties > Save Column Order for Daily Op Schedule: Select this command to print the current column order on the Daily Operatory report. Properties : Select this command to display the Properties...

topics.title: Batching Billing Statements from the Patient Account
topics.body: Batching Billing Statements from the Patient Account Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements > Printing and Batching Statements from the Account Window Batching Billing Statements from the Patient Account To batch a billing statement from the account record: Select an option: Select List > Account . Click Account List Window on the Power bar. In any window, press Ctrl + A. The Account List window is displayed in FIND mode. Click anywhere in the list area or press Enter to switch to SCAN mode. Type the account name in the Find field and press Enter. Select Billing Statement from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message is displayed that asks you to print the statement for the account. To batch the billing statement to print later, click No . A message informs you that a billing statement for the account is added to the batch file. To print batched billing statements, see Printing Batched Billing Statements . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Book At A Glance Window Properties
topics.body: Setting Book At A Glance Window Properties Using the Scheduler > Understanding Book At A Glance Window Options Setting Book At A Glance Window Properties Using this feature, you can customize the date format, background color and texture, appointment and slot notes border colors, and the fonts and font sizes used in the Book At A Glance window. To customize the Book At A Glance window properties: From the Daily Appointments or Month At A Glance window, select Book At A Glance from the appointment or time slot submenus; or select Book Quick Glance from the drop-down list at the top of the window. The Book At A Glance window is displayed. Select an option: Click an appointment : Right-click, and select Properties . Click an open time slot : Right-click, and select Properties . Select Properties > Book At A Glance from the Options section of the Express bar or from the Options menu. The Properties... window is displayed. Select an option: To customize how the date is displayed in the title bar : Select a format from the Date Format section. To add a background image to the window : Select an image from the Background Image drop-down list. To change the font in which the hour markers and column headings are displayed : Click Change Font . The Font is displayed. Select the fonts from the Font, Font Style, and Size lists. To apply a specific character set to the selected font, select it from the Script drop-down list. Click OK . To change the background color for blank time slots : Click the square next to Background Color. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the appointment border color : Click the square next to Appointment Border Color. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the slot note color : Click the square next to Slot Note Color. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To set the selected properties for all users : Select Set for ALL USERS . To reset the default settings for all users : Click Restore ALL USERS . To restore the default settings for your user login : Click Restore Defaults . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Book At A Glance Window Options
topics.body: Understanding Book At A Glance Window Options Using the Scheduler Understanding Book At A Glance Window Options In the Book At A Glance window, you can perform several functions. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu when using Enhanced Workspace are: Make Appt : Click an open time slot and select this command to make a patient appointment. The Patient List window is displayed. In this window, select the patient for whom you want to schedule the appointment. See Scheduling Appointments . New Patient Appt : Click an available time slot and select this command to make an appointment for a new patient. The New Patient Appt window is displayed. In this window, you can enter new patient account information. See Setting the Auto Scheduling Book . AutoSched New Appt : Click an available time slot and select this command to auto schedule an appointment for a new patient. The Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters for window is displayed. In this window, you can enter auto scheduling restrictions for the new patient. View Appt : Click an appointment and select this command to display the Appointment Card window in SCAN mode. In this window, you can view, add, edit, and delete procedures scheduled for the appointment. Edit Appt : Click an appointment and select this command to display the Appointment Card window in EDIT mode. In this window, you can view, add, edit, and delete procedures scheduled for the appointment. Confirm Appt : Click an unconfirmed appointment and select this command to display the Confirm Appt window. In this window, you can confirm patient information. After you confirm the appointment, you are prompted to log a contact for the call. AutoSched Appt: Select this command to display the Auto Scheduler Date Range window. In this window, you can set a date range within which to auto schedule a patient appointment. See Setting the Auto Scheduling Book . Cancel Appt : Select this command to display the Cancel/Broken Appointments Wizard window. The appointment information is temporarily saved, and you have the option to reschedule the appointment or save the information to the Tickler file. Resched Appt : This command is enabled only after an appointment is canceled. Select a date and time slot to reschedule the appointment and select this command. AutoSched Canceled : This command is enabled only after an appointment is canceled. Select this command to display the Auto Scheduler Date Range window. In this window, you can set a date range within which to auto schedule the patient appointment. Resched Overlap : This command is enabled only when two appointments overlap. Select a date and time slot to reschedule the appointment and select this command. AutoSched Overlap : This command is enabled only when two appointments overlap. Click an appointment and select this command to display the Auto Scheduler Date Range window. In this window, you can set a date range within which to auto schedule the patient appointment. Shorten : Click an appointment and select this command to shorten the highlighted appointment by one time unit. Lengthen : Click an appointment and select this command to lengthen the highlighted appointment by one time unit. Check In : Click an appointment and select this command to check a patient in. The background of the appointment is changed to blue to indicate that the patient is checked in. HIPAA Acknowledgment : Click an appointment and select this command to display t

topics.title: Viewing Backup Information
topics.body: Viewing Backup Information Getting Started Viewing Backup Information To view a list of folders and files to include when backing up your data: Select Help > BackUp Information . The Backup Information window is displayed. To print the list of files and folders, click Print . To close the window, click OK .
