topics.title: Updating the Bluebook After an Insurance Payment
topics.body: Updating the Bluebook After an Insurance Payment Setting Up Insurance > Using the Insurance Bluebook Updating the Bluebook After an Insurance Payment Use the bluebook to track the amount each insurance plan pays for charges submitted by your practice. When you post an insurance payment, you can automatically update the bluebook file so future estimates of insurance benefits are accurate. To set up the bluebook to update the entries for each insurance plan after insurance payments are posted: Select System > Change System Settings > Insurance . The Insurance Settings window is displayed. Select an option: To display a message asking you to update the bluebook after posting an insurance check payment : Select Update bluebook after insurance payments . To disable the prompt to update the bluebook entry after posting an insurance payment : Deselect Update bluebook after insurance payments . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Building Case Module Presentations
topics.body: Building Case Module Presentations Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module Building Case Module Presentations A presentation is usually customized to a patient�s proposed treatment plan and focuses on all aspects of the treatment. You can build, view, and show a customized presentation to the patient. Create a presentation by combining blueprint slides with slides associated with specific procedure codes. You can build a presentation from SoftDent or from the charting module. A presentation can be accessed as a patient document from the document manager. To build a presentation from the charting module, see Building Case Presentation Module Presentations . To build a presentation: In the Patient List window, select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Select Case Presentation Module from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The SoftDent Case Presentation Module - Select the Transactions to Include in your presentation window is displayed. Select the procedures to add to the case presentation. To select consecutive items, press Shift. To select non-consecutive items, press Ctrl. Click Click to build a SoftDent Case Presentation for the selected procedures . The Build a New Case Presentation window is displayed. Type the name of the presentation in the Specify a label for the new presentation field. Select the case blueprints to use in the presentation. Select an option: To display the fee for each item in the presentation : Select Display the fee for each treatment plan item listed . To exclude the fee from the presentation : Deselect Display the fee for each treatment plan item listed . Click Build . After the presentation is built, the SoftDent Case Presentation Module - Your Presentation is Ready! window is displayed. Select an option: To view the presentation in the slide show viewer : Select I want to show the presentation . When you select this option, the Afterwards, prompt me to specify which treatment items the patient accepted checkbox is enabled. To display the prompt after viewing the presentation, select the checkbox. After you view the presentation, select File > Exit . To edit the presentation in the slide show application : Select I want to edit the presentation . Make the changes, select File > Save, and select File > Exit . To e-mail the presentation : Select I want to e-mail the presentation to the patient or a referring Doctor . The SoftDent E-mail window is displayed. Enter the appropriate information, and click OK (Send) . The e-mail is sent. To do nothing with the presentation : Select Nothing. I will view the presentation later . To start the presentation with the first ADA presentation slide instead of introductory information : Select Set up this presentation to start at the first included ADA procedure during slide-shows, skipping any introductory material from the case blueprint . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Building Case Presentation Module Presentations
topics.body: Building Case Presentation Module Presentations Charting Treatment Plans > Handling Treatment Plan Items Building Case Presentation Module Presentations You can build a case presentation to show the advantages of accepting the proposed treatment plan. To create a case presentation module presentation: On the Treatment Planning tab, select the treatment plan items, and click Click to build a PowerPoint presentation for the selected procedures on the Treatment Plan toolbar . The Build a New Case Presentation window is displayed. Type the name of the presentation in the Specify a label for the new presentation field. Select the group of slides to use in the presentation, and click Build . After the presentation is built, the SoftDent Case Presentation Module - Your Presentation is Ready! window is displayed. Select an option: To view the presentation in the slide show viewer : Select I want to show the presentation . After you view the presentation, select File > Exit . To edit the presentation in the slide show application : Select I want to edit the presentation . Make the changes, select File > Save , and select File > Exit . To e-mail the presentation : Select I want to e-mail the presentation to the patient or a referring Doctor . The SoftDent E-mail window is displayed. Enter the appropriate information, and click OK (Send) . The e-mail is sent. To do nothing with the presentation : Select Nothing. I will view the presentation later . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Blocking Provider Vacation Appointment Slots
topics.body: Blocking Provider Vacation Appointment Slots Blocking Provider Vacation Appointment Slots Providers can block appointment slots to prevent users from scheduling appointments during dates the provider is on vacation. All appointment slots are blocked during business hours for the vacation date range, and Provider on Vacation is displayed in the scheduler. To block appointment slots for a provider�s vacation: Select List > Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. Select the provider and click Edit . The Provider window is displayed. Select the start and end dates in the Vacation/Days Off section and click the appropriate Block Slots button. The Block Provider Vacation Dates window is displayed. The start and end vacation days are displayed in the Vacation Dates section, and the rooms to which the provider is assigned are selected. Slots are not blocked on holidays or days the office is closed. Select the rooms in which to block slots. Tip If scheduled appointments are within the vacation date range, a message is displayed, informing you to cancel or reschedule the appointments. To set the blocked time slot background color, select the colored box. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To set the blocked time slot text color, select the colored box. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . The Block Provider Vacation Dates window is displayed. Click OK . The Provider window is displayed. Click OK . To unblock provider vacation slots, right-click on the slot or slots and select Unblock Slot or Unblock Range . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Blocking Time Slots
topics.body: Blocking Time Slots Setting Up the Scheduler > Using Blocked Time Slots Blocking Time Slots To block a scheduler time slot: In the Daily Appointments (Daybook) or Book At A Glance windows, select the first time slot to block, and select an option: Press B. Select Block/Unblock Slot from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Select Block/Unblock Slot from the short-cut menu. The Blocked Slot Properties window is displayed. Select an option: To enter a new message for the blocked slot : Type the message in the Message section. The message is added to the Pre-Defined Messages drop-down list. To use a pre-defined message : Select it from the Pre-Defined Messages section. To change the text color : Select the color box. The Color window is displayed. Select a text color and click OK . To change the background color : Select the color box. The Color window is displayed. Select a background color and click OK . Click OK . To extend the block to cover a range of slots, select it and drag the square in the lower-right corner to the end of the set of time slots to block. If you attempt to schedule a patient appointment in the blocked slot, a message asking you whether to override the blocked slot is displayed. If you click Yes , the slot is unblocked and the appointment is scheduled. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Blocked Time Slots
topics.body: Using Blocked Time Slots Setting Up the Scheduler Using Blocked Time Slots Use blocked time slots to prevent users from scheduling appointments for a specific time period. Users cannot schedule patient appointments during a blocked time slot unless the feature is overridden. Example : To prevent office employees from scheduling patient appointments during the office lunch hour or during an office meeting, block the appropriate time slots for the day. For blocked time slots, you can set the default: Color Text The following reports do not include blocked time slots: Auto scheduling Time usage You can also view blocked slots in the History Appointments window if your office does not use the Remove Blocked Slots from Scheduler option. See Setting Scheduler Defaults for more information. Related Topics Setting the Default Blocked Slot Color Blocking Time Slots Unblocking Slots Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Case Blueprints
topics.body: Creating Case Blueprints Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint Creating Case Blueprints You can insert the following patient information placeholders into a case blueprint or ADA procedure presentation: Portrait Name Address Phone number Chart Images Financial ADA code When the patient presentation is assembled, the placeholder is replaced with the appropriate patient data. To create a case blueprint: In PowerPoint, select SoftDent Case Presentation Files > New Case Blueprint . The New SoftDent Case Blueprint window is displayed. Type the case presentation file name in the Enter a filename for the new case blueprint field and click OK . Slide 1 on the new case blueprint is displayed. To insert a new slide, select Insert > New Slide . The new slide is displayed. You can insert placeholders for : Full or quadrant views of the current restorative chart Treatment plan primary restorative chart Treatment plan secondary restorative chart Presentation manager images Full or quadrant views of the perio chart Perio numeric history chart Most recent extraoral images Most recent miscellaneous images Most recent third-party images Most recent intraoral images Primary and secondary treatment plans Both treatment plans Summary financial information To insert a placeholder into the slide, select the placeholder from the Insert > SoftDent Placeholders menu. Move the placeholder to the appropriate location. After you create the blueprint, click Save and close the PowerPoint software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Bluebook Allowance Table Fees
topics.body: Updating Bluebook Allowance Table Fees Setting Up Insurance > Using Allowance Tables Updating Bluebook Allowance Table Fees After an insurance plan is associated with an allowance table, you can no longer update the entries information from the Bluebook entries for window. These entries must be updated from the allowance table. When an allowance table fee is added or updated, bluebook entries are automatically updated. To view the updated entry, select a bluebook entry from the Bluebook entries for List window. The entry information is displayed. The updated allowance table amount is displayed in the Allowed Amount field. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding the Bluebook Entries for List Window
topics.body: Understanding the Bluebook Entries for List Window Setting Up Insurance > Using the Insurance Bluebook Understanding the Bluebook Entries for List Window The plan estimation type-specific columns displayed in the Bluebook entries for List window are: Tag : Updates when you change estimation information in the Insurance plan window, change your fees, or update a bluebook amount. Code : Procedure code. Description : Procedure code description. Ins Pymt : Procedure code insurance payment amount. Allowed : Maximum fee an insurance plan accepts for a procedure or the usual and customary rate (UCR). This column is displayed for percentage, PPO, PPO percentage, Medicare percentage, and Medicare flat fee plan estimation types. % : Percentage entered on the Coverage tab of the Insurance plan window. This column is displayed for percentage, PPO percentage, and Medicare percentage plan estimation types. Plan Fee : Fee schedule or insurance payment. The insurance payment plus the copayment is displayed for plans requiring a per-procedure copayment. This column is displayed for DMO/Capitation and Medicaid plan estimation types. Copay : The copayment entered on the Coverage tab of the Insurance plan window is displayed in this column. This column is displayed for DMO/Capitation, Medicaid, PPO, and Medicare flat fee plans. Note: When a PPO or Medicare flat fee plan is selected, the value in this column is the difference between the insurance payment and the allowed amount. The write-off is automatically calculated as the difference between the allowed amount and your fee. Fee : Insurance payment plus the copay. Date : Bluebook entry date. Ded : If the copayment is applied to the deductible, Y is displayed in this column. If the copayment is not applied to the deductible, N is displayed in this column. Notes : If notes are associated with the bluebook entry, Y is displayed in this column. If no notes are associated with the bluebook entry, N is displayed in this column. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Bluebook Report
topics.body: Running the Bluebook Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports Running the Bluebook Report You can run the Bluebook report to generate bluebook data for insurance plans. In addition to the reports provided with the software, you can run user-selected reports based on criteria that you define. To generate the Bluebook report: Select Reports > Insurance > Bluebook . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Bluebook Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending plans. Select an option: To include information associated with insurance plans that have bluebook entries : Select Include Only Plans with Bluebook Entries . To include information associated with all insurance plans : Deselect Include Only Plans with Bluebook Entries . To include age limitations, tooth limitations, and waiting period information in the report: Select Include Limitation Information on Bluebook . Click OK . Additional Details: The Percentage By Class section displays coverage information as entered on the Coverage tab of the Insurance Plan window. Bluebook entries are displayed after this information. The coverages and bluebook entries will differ according to the plan's estimation type. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting an Insurance Calculation Method
topics.body: Selecting an Insurance Calculation Method Billing and Receivables > Setting Insurance Defaults in Billing and Receivables Selecting an Insurance Calculation Method The ICM d ictates the following for each account: Billing method Account aging How estimated insurance payments are calculated Each new account added uses the selected default method. The default ICM can be overridden for individual accounts. To select an ICM: Select System > Change System Settings > Insurance . The Insurance Settings window is displayed. Select an option: If your practice bills an account the full fee at the time of service and does not accept assignment of the benefits : Select ICM0 from the Default Insurance Calculation Method for new accounts drop-down list. When you select this ICM, the amount due ages immediately from the service date, and the software calculates the estimated insurance payment. If your practice bills an account after billing and receiving payment from the insurance company : Select ICM1 from the Default Insurance Calculation Method for new accounts drop-down list. When you select this ICM, all patient charges are held current until the insurer has paid the appropriate portion. When using ICM1, the amount due for the account is always $0 for any insurable transactions. After the final insurance payment has been posted and the claim has a status of complete, the account is billed for any remaining charges. The unpaid portion is the amount due and ages from the final insurance payment date. If ICM1 is assigned to an account with insurance, the Insurance Calculation wizard is displayed. The wizard reminds you that to bill the patient portion immediately, the account must be set to ICM2. If your practice bills the estimated portion (the fee less any estimated insurance payment) at the time of service : Select ICM2 from the Default Insurance Calculation Method for new accounts drop-down list. The estimated insurance amount does not age until you post the final insurance payment and the claim has a status of complete. The estimated patient portion ages from the service date. Select an option: To display a message that asks you to print or batch insurance claims : Select Ask Print or Batch from the Process claims after submitting insurance drop-down list. To send the claim to the batch file automatically after it is submitted : Select Send to Batch from the Process claims after submitting insurance drop-down list. To print the claim automatically after it is submitted : Select Send to Batch from the Process claims after submitting insurance drop-down list. Type the number of copies of each form to print in the Default number of copies for each insurance form: field. To enable insurance estimation, select Enable Insurance estimation . If this option is selected, the insurance payment is estimated when you schedule or post a procedure. The payment amount is determined by the insurance bluebook data, percentages, and copays entered on the Coverage tab of the Insurance Plan record. This feature is enabled by default. Deselect this option when all your accounts are set up as ICM0 or if your office does not inform patients of the estimated insurance payment amount. To disable the insurance estimation for a specific patient, type X in the User Codes field in the Patient window. Select Update bluebook after insurance payments to display a message that prompts you to update the bluebook after posting an insurance payment. Using the bluebook enables you

topics.title: Using the Calculus Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Calculus Toolbar Periodontal Charting > Using Perio Tab Toolbars Using the Calculus Toolbar The Calculus toolbar commands enable you to chart periodontal calculus. Click the icon for the description of the Calculus toolbar buttons. Click this button and select a tooth to note no calculus . Click this button and select a tooth to note low calculus . Click this button and select a tooth to note a medium amount of calculus . Click this button and select a tooth to note high amounts of calculus . Need more information? topics.title: Making a Support Call
topics.body: Making a Support Call Finding Answers Making a Support Call If you cannot find an answer to your question in the online help or the Carestream Dental Institute, you can contact a technical support representative for assistance . To facilitate your call, have the following information ready: Your customer ID number The version number of your software The number of computers in your practice including the server The operating systems that are running on the affected computers When the issue started Whether the office has recently installed any updates The customer ID number is also known as the client ID number, the support ID number, or the account number. Related Topics Locating Your Customer ID Number Using Online Help Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Cancelling and Resubmitting an Insurance Claim
topics.body: Cancelling and Resubmitting an Insurance Claim Submitting and Tracking Claims > Resubmitting and Deleting Claims Cancelling and Resubmitting an Insurance Claim To cancel and resubmit an existing insurance claim: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient and click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Outstanding Ins . The Outstanding Insurance window is displayed. Select the claim and click View Claim . The Claim window is displayed. Select Cancel Claim . A Cancelling claim. Are you sure? prompt is displayed. Click Yes . A Delete claim? prompt is displayed. Click Yes , and close th e Outstanding Insurance window. From the Express Bar of the Transactions window, click Submit Ins to resubmit the claim. A Submit transactions between: Start date and End date message is displayed. Click OK . The claim now has a new claim number. Click OK . A Do you want to submit electronically? prompt is displayed. Click OK. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Capitation Collections Summary Report
topics.body: Running the Capitation Collections Summary Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Running the Capitation Collections Summary Report The Capitation Collections Summary report summarizes the collections received for any capitation plan over a designated time period. You can only generate this report for plans marked as DMO/capitation in the Insurance Plan record. To run the Capitation Collections Summary report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Capitation Collections Summary . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Capitation Collections Summary Report Setup window is displayed. In the Date Range section, click the drop-down arrows to select the dates from a calendar. In the Doctors section, use the drop-down arrows to select specific providers, or leave 999 to report on all providers. In the IDs section, use the drop-down arrows to select an insurance company or insurance plan. Click OK . Additional Capitation Collections Summary Report Information: This report will not run unless you specify a valid ID in the Company ID or Plan ID fields. This report itemizes your capitation receivables into capitation payments, patient payments (including per-visit charges), and supplemental payments paid to your practice for handling the plan. The report contains the following information: Capitation Payments�Payments posted to the plans� Transaction window within the specified date range. Patient Payments�Patient payments posted within the specified date range (codes 1.00-20.99, excluding 2.00-2.89 and 11.93, 12.93, 13.93, and 14.93). Supplemental Ins. Payments�Insurance payments posted within the specified date range (codes 2.00-2.89; includes codes 11.93, 12.93, 13.93, and 14.93). Total Payments�Total Payments = Capitation Payments + Patient Payments + Supplemental Ins. Payments. Patients Seen�Total patients seen whose transaction dates are within the specified date range and who have the specified insurance company or plan. Average Collection Per Pat.�Average Collection Per Patient = Total Payments divided by Patients Seen. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Capitation Insurance Company Encounter Report
topics.body: Running the Capitation Insurance Company Encounter Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Running the Capitation Insurance Company Encounter Report The Capitation Insurance Company Encounter report lists all patients with transactions posted for a specific time period for a selected capitation plan. It also shows how many patients were seen in this period, the total number of procedures performed, and the total amount charged to these patients. The report is sorted by insurance company. To run the Capitation Insurance Company Encounter report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Capitation Ins. Co. Encounter . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Contractual Ins. Co. Production by ADA Codes Report window is displayed. In the Date Range section, click the drop-down arrows to select the dates from a calendar. In the Ins. Co. ID section, click the drop-down arrows to select the insurance company. In the Doctors section, use the drop-down arrows to select specific providers, or leave 999 to report on all providers. Click OK . Additional Capitation Insurance Company Encounter Report Information: This report will not run unless you specify a valid Ins. Co. ID. Information on this report includes the patient�s ID and name, Social Security number, birth date, and the employee�s name and Social Security number. The code, its description, service date, fee, and co-payment amount are also displayed. RVU�Number of time units specified for the transaction code on the Scheduler/Fees tab of the ADA/Transaction Code window. Fee�Fee charged for service (based on the plan fee in the bluebook). Co-pay�Patient�s portion of the fee charged (based on the plan fee in the bluebook). Total Patients Seen�Total number of patients included in the report. Total procedures performed�Total number of production transactions included in the report. Total Amount charged to patients�Total FEE amount charged for service. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Running the Capitation Plan Encounter Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Running the Capitation Plan Encounter Report The Capitation Plan Encounter report lists all patients with transactions posted for a specific time period for a selected capitation plan. It also shows how many patients were seen in this period, the total number of procedures performed, and the total amount charged to these patients. To run the Capitation Plan Encounter report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Capitation Plan Encounter . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Contractual Plan Production by ADA Codes Report window is displayed. In the Date Range section, click the drop-down arrows to select the dates from a calendar. In the Plan ID section, click the drop-down arrows to select the insurance plan. In the Doctors section, use the drop-down arrows to select specific providers, or leave 999 to report on all providers. Click OK . Additional Capitation Plan Encounter Report Information: This report will not run unless you specify a valid plan ID. Information on this report includes the patient�s ID and name, Social Security number, birth date, and the employee�s name and Social Security number. The code, its description, service date, fee, and co-payment amount are also displayed. RVU�Number of time units specified for the transaction code on the Scheduler/Fees tab of the ADA/Transaction Code record. Fee�Fee charged for service (based on the plan fee in the bluebook). Co-pay�Patient�s portion of the fee charged (based on the plan fee in the bluebook). Total Patients Seen�Total number of patients included in the report. Total procedures performed�Total number of production transactions included in the report. Total Amount charged to patients�Total Fee amount charged for service. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Capture Mode
topics.body: Using Capture Mode Handling Images While Charting Using Capture Mode When the Clinical Imaging tab is displayed in capture mode, you can start and pause live video recording. With images, you can: View Save Discard Set capture size Set color depth Import You can also: Modify camera hardware settings Acquire images from a TWAIN source Select a TWAIN source The Image Capture toolbar is displayed when the tab is in capture mode. To view the tab in capture mode, select Capture Mode from the Clinical Imaging toolbar . Related Topics Capturing Live Video Images Importing Images Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Capturing Live Video Images
topics.body: Capturing Live Video Images Handling Images While Charting > Using Capture Mode Capturing Live Video Images To capture images from a live video source: On the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select Live Video . Select Capture an Image from Live Video . With certain cards, you can also use a foot pedal to capture images. Select buttons from the Image Capture toolbar . Select an option: To discard the image : Select Discard Image . To discard the image and resume the video : Select Live Video . To save the image : Select Save Image . The image is saved with an STV/IO image extension. Tip Before capturing live video, set up video preferences and adjust the camera focus and contrast. For more information, see Setting Video Capture Options . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accessing CareCredit
topics.body: Accessing CareCredit eServices > Using CareCredit Accessing CareCredit You can access the CareCredit integration and its features from several areas in the software. Depending on the specific window you are in, these features can include: Lookup by SSN and Zip: Search by Social Security Number or zip code to verify the patient has a CareCredit account when the patient does not have his CareCredit card. Lookup by Account No.: Search by CareCredit account number to verify the patient has a CareCredit account when the patient does not have his CareCredit card. Online Application: Access the CareCredit web site, where the patient can complete an application and receive instant approval. Payment Calculator: Access the CareCredit web site, where you can use a payment calculator to present various payment plans to the patient. Enter a Payment: Accept a CareCredit payment in the same way that you would a credit card. Enter a Refund: Make a refund to a CareCredit account in the same way that you would a credit card account. To access CareCredit from the software, select an option: In the Patient List window : Select a patient, and select CareCredit Integration from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Patient window : Select CareCredit Integration from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Account List window : Select a patient, and select CareCredit Integration from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Account window: Select CareCredit Integration from the Options section of the Express bar. In the InOffice window : Select CareCredit Integration from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Daily Appointments window : Select CareCredit Integration from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Appointment Card window: Select CareCredit Integration from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Book Quick Glance window: Select CareCredit Integration from the Options section of the Express bar. In the patient Transactions window : Select CareCredit Integration from the Options section of the Express bar, or click CareCredit Payment on the Payment Toolbar. In the account Transactions window : Select CareCredit Integration from the Options section of the Express bar, or click CareCredit Payment on the Payment Toolbar. On the Power Bar: Click . The CareCredit Integration button and option are disabled if the CareCredit software is not enabled. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using CareCredit
topics.body: Using CareCredit eServices Using CareCredit The CareCredit program: Enables a dental practice to offer financing options for healthcare expenses to its patients Establishes a revolving credit line that can be used for future treatment without the need to reapply Requires the practice to pay a processing fee for CareCredit transactions A key benefit of CareCredit : A practice receives a direct electronic transfer of the treatment fee into its bank account within two business days, with no recourse if the patient delays payment or defaults. For more information, see the CareCredit documentation and website. When this feature is activated: CareCredit is launched from the software. The required patient and account information is sent to CareCredit. CareCredit uses the information to process the request. Contact CareCredit for assistance with using the CareCredit program. Note: A CareCredit icon displayed in color in the upper-right of the Patient window indicates that the patient is a CareCredit customer; an icon displayed in B&W indicates that the patient is not a CareCredit customer. Even if your office is currently offering CareCredit, you must perform several configuration steps to use the new integration. See Setting Up CareCredit for more information. Related Topics Setting Up CareCredit Accessing CareCredit Using CareCredit Lookup Windows Using the CareCredit Online Application Using the CareCredit Payment Calculator Posting a CareCredit Payment Posting a CareCredit Refund Viewing CareCredit Payment Plans Reprinting CareCredit Receipts Running CareCredit Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Using a Card Swiper
topics.body: Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Using a Card Swiper eServices > Using the ePayments Service Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Using a Card Swiper Before you add a credit card payment, you must install the card swiper. See Installing the Card Swiper . To add a credit card payment using the card swiper: In the patient Transactions List window, click Add . The Transaction window is displayed. Select the credit card payment code from the Code drop-down list and press Tab. The Payment window is displayed. Swipe the credit card. In the CVD Number field, type the credit card verification number. If you do not enter a CVD number, a message requesting the number is displayed. To ignore this message, click OK . Click Submit . After you receive an authorization number, add notes, if necessary. To print a receipt, click OK at the prompt. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Lab Case Prompting
topics.body: Using Lab Case Prompting Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Lab Cases Using Lab Case Prompting Lab case prompting e nables you to associate specified procedural codes with opening or closing a lab case. Whenever one of the specified procedures is posted, you are prompted to open a new lab case or close the existing case for the selected patient. The default list of procedural codes that prompt you to open a lab case when posted are: Code Code Code Code Code Code Code 1510 1515 1525 2510 2520 2530 2540 2610 2620 2630 2640 2740 2750 2751 2790 2791 2810 2954 2962 5110 5120 5130 5140 5211 5212 5213 5214 5510 5520 5610 5650 5750 5751 5760 5810 5811 5820 5821 5860 5861 5862 5982 6055 6210 6211 6545 6750 6790 6950 6970 6972 Related Topics Setting Up Lab Case Prompting Need more information? topics.title: Saving Presentations as Case Blueprints
topics.body: Saving Presentations as Case Blueprints Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint Saving Presentations as Case Blueprints Only presentations that contain an ADA code slides placeholder can be saved as a case blueprint. To save the currently displayed presentation as a case blueprint: While viewing the presentation, select SoftDent Case Presentation Files > Save Current Presentation > Case Blueprint . The Save SoftDent Case Blueprint window is displayed. Type a presentation name in the Enter a filename for the new case blueprint field and click OK . Need more information? topics.title: Viewing Lab Case Status by Lab
topics.body: Viewing Lab Case Status by Lab Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Lab Cases > Viewing a Lab Case Status Viewing Lab Case Status by Lab To view a case status from the Labs List window: Select the appropriate lab. Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Lab case List window is displayed. Each case associated with the selected lab is displayed in the list. Lab cases that are at the lab : Displayed in green text. Lab cases that have been returned to your office : Displayed in blue text. Lab cases that are complete : Displayed in red text. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Case Presentations
topics.body: Handling Case Presentations Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module Handling Case Presentations When you build a treatment plan case presentation, the presentation: Is attached to the patient�s record Can be accessed from the document manager You can do the following to case presentations: Edit Show Print View properties E-mail Related Topics Updating Presentation Dates Editing Presentations Showing Presentations Printing Presentations E-mailing Presentations Deleting Presentations Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Case Presentation Module
topics.body: Using the Case Presentation Module Treatment Planning Using the Case Presentation Module Using the case presentation module, you can: Integrate Microsoft PowerPoint to build a custom presentation about proposed treatment plans for a patient Create a case blueprint or presentation, link it to procedure codes, and use it to create customized presentations Incorporate charting information, x-rays, images, and insurance or cost information into case presentations Case blueprints : Presentations that contain placeholders that denote where to import patient and treatment plan specific information. Use them as outlines for creating treatment plan case presentations. Example : You can create a blueprint with slides that contain placeholders for patient chart, procedure code, and proposed treatment cost information. Linking presentations to the ADA procedure codes enables you to include information automatically while building presentations. A default set of presentations are linked with procedure codes when the software is installed. You can customize the case presentation module to meet the needs of your practice. Example : You can create a bridge work presentation that includes slides specific to multiple crown and bridge procedures and link the presentation to the appropriate procedure codes. After you create the presentation, you can: View or print them on any computer that has the appropriate version of PowerPoint software installed Attach presentations to the PowerPoint Viewer program and e-mail them to users who do not have PowerPoint software Related Topics Linking ADA Codes to Presentation Files Building Case Module Presentations Handling Case Presentations Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint Software Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Copying Colors and Goals
topics.body: Copying Colors and Goals Setting Up the Scheduler Copying Colors and Goals You can copy colors and goals for the practice or a specific provider from one day or week to another: Day. Week. Month. Related Topics Copying Colors Copying Goals Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Canceling Appointments
topics.body: Canceling Appointments Scheduling Appointments Canceling Appointments When you cancel an appointment, the appointment information is saved temporarily to enable you to: Reschedule the appointment immediately. Place it in the Tickler file to reschedule later. To cancel an appointment, select an option: From the Daily Appointments window : Select an appointment and click Cancel . From the Book At A Glance window : Select an appointment and click Cancel . From the Patient List window : Select a patient and select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select an appointment and click Cancel . From the Patient window : Select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select an appointment and click Cancel . After canceling an appointment, you can: Reschedule the appointment. Place it in the Tickler file to reschedule later. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Canceling a Claim
topics.body: Canceling a Claim Submitting and Tracking Claims > Resubmitting and Deleting Claims Canceling a Claim On certain occasions, you might want to cancel, rather than delete, a submitted claim . Example : If you entered the wrong insurance company information. Canceling a claim changes the status of all submitted transactions associated with the claim from Pr to Not Sent. This enables you to resubmit the claim after making any corrections. To cancel a claim: Select List > Patient or press Ctrl + O. The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient and click Transactions on the toolbar. The patient�s Transactions window is displayed. Select any transaction associated with the claim to cancel and click Claim Stat on the toolbar. The status of each transaction is displayed in the Claim column. Select View Claim for Transaction from the section Options of the Express bar or Options menu. The Claim window is displayed. Select Cancel Claim from the section Options of the Express bar or Options menu. A cancellation confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . A delete confirmation message is displayed. Select an option: To delete the claim : Click Yes . To resubmit the claim after making changes : Click No . The status of all transactions that were included in the claim changes to Not Sent. To resubmit, click Submit Ins . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling Credit Card Processing by Provider
topics.body: Enabling Credit Card Processing by Provider eServices > Using the ePayments Service Enabling Credit Card Processing by Provider If there are multiple providers in your practice using separate merchant accounts for credit card processing, you must enable processing for each provider. To enable credit card processing by provider: From the SoftDent main menu, select File > Practice . The Practice Information window is displayed. Select Enable Credit Card Processing from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The System Settings - Enable CC Processing window is displayed. Select Enabled for Providers , and then click OK . The Practice Information window is displayed. Select List > Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. Double-click a provider to display the provider�s settings. In the Credit Card Processing section, select Participating Provider and click OK . The card swiper must be set up and detected from each provider�s window when integrated credit card processing is enabled by provider. Insert the CD containing the merchant profile (INI file) in the CD drive, or navigate to the folder where the file is stored. Click OK . Select Credit Card Options to display the Carestream ePayments Setup window, where you can load or reload your VisaNet ini file, view or edit workstation settings, view or edit merchant information, and view or edit settings shared by the practice. Tip Repeat these steps for each provider. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Issuing Credit Card Refunds
topics.body: Issuing Credit Card Refunds eServices > Using the ePayments Service Issuing Credit Card Refunds To issue a credit card refund: In the patient Transactions List window, select Payments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Payments window is displayed. Select Refunds from the Select Operation section. The Payments - Refund Credits window is displayed. The default credit refund code is displayed in the Code drop-down list. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Click Post . The Carestream ePayments Refund Authorization window is displayed. Swipe the card, or manually enter the information. Do one of the following: For a VISA card only : Click Submit . Note : This change, required by VISA, is effective as of November 2019. For all other card types, click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Contacts Made Report
topics.body: Running the Contacts Made Report Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts > Running Contact Reports Running the Contacts Made Report The Contacts Made report generates a list of all contacts with whom you have made contact. You can run this report for: Patients Accounts Insurance plans Insurance companies Employers Referring doctors Providers Pharmacies Claims The report includes the following information for each contact listed in the report: Identification number Name Address Phone numbers Date on which the contact was made Name of the user who made the contact Contact method Sample Report To run the Contacts Made report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Contact Reports > Contacts Made . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option, and click OK . The Contacts By Date Range Report window is displayed. Select the type of contact from the drop-down list. Select the from and to dates from the drop-down lists. Type an account identification number only if you are generating results for account records. Type a patient last name range only if you are generating results for patient records. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering Code Scheduling Information
topics.body: Entering Code Scheduling Information Handling Transactions and Codes > Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes Entering Code Scheduling Information Before attempting to enter ADA, CPT, or transaction code scheduling information, enter general information on the Information tab. For more information, see Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes . If you are adding a payment or adjustment code, you cannot change information displayed on the Scheduler/Fees tab. You can create up to 27 different fee schedules and set up different charges for the same code. This feature is useful for multiple-provider practices that use different fees for each doctor or unique requirements, such as senior citizen discounts. Fee schedule 0 can be used for bluebook entries. To enter code scheduling information for a new code: From the ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window, click the Scheduler/Fees tab or press Ctrl + Tab . To change the color group definition assignment, click OK and select Change Color . Type the number of time units required to perform the procedure in the Time Units field. Type the number of time units required when scheduling the procedure with other procedures in the Multi Time Units field, and type the number of real time minutes it takes to complete the procedure in the RTU minutes field. To use the scheduler template, select Use Scheduler Template . If you use this feature, providers are automatically assigned to the code, and a warning is displayed if there are scheduling conflicts. Type provider codes for the personnel required for each time unit of an appointment in the Scheduler Template field. Example : If a procedure requires the assistant for the first time unit, the hygienist for the next two units, and the dentist for the last time unit, type AHHD in the Scheduler Template field. Type the doctor�s fee in the 0 field. Fields 0 through 26 specify charge amounts for up to 27 different fee schedules. To save the new procedure code information, click OK . Tip The code number is displayed in the Code field. After you save the code, the code description is displayed. If you are color scheduling, the code�s assigned color is displayed in the Color field. Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The real time unit is the actual time it takes to perform a procedure in minutes. For example, 20 RTU�s equal 20 minutes. To include a hygienist in the scheduler template, select Hygiene Code on the Information tab. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Conditions Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Conditions Toolbar Restorative Charting > Using Restorative Tab Toolbars Using the Conditions Toolbar The Conditions toolbar commands e nable you to chart tooth conditions. All conditions function as toggle switches: charting the same condition to the same tooth a second time removes the condition. To add a button to the toolbar, see Customizing Toolbars . Click the icon for the description of the Conditions toolbar buttons. Click this button and select a tooth to chart a periapical condition . Click this button and select a surface to chart cracks . Click this button and select an area to chart diastema . Click this button and select a tooth to chart decay . Click this button and select a tooth to chart missing teeth . Click this button to chart tooth conditions. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accessing the Contact Expert from a Report
topics.body: Accessing the Contact Expert from a Report Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Accessing the Contact Expert Accessing the Contact Expert from a Report After running the report , y ou can send the report output to the contact expert. The contact list and contact specific information are displayed in the Contact Expert window for easy reference during communication with a contact. To access the contact expert from a report: Select the report from the Reports menu. The Output Options window is displayed. Select Contact Expert . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing a Document Category
topics.body: Changing a Document Category Using the Document Manager > Organizing Documents in the Document Tree Changing a Document Category To change the category assigned to a document: Select an option: Select the document in the document tree and select Change Category from the Options menu on the Express bar. Select the document in the document tree and right-click to display the context menu. Select Change Category . The Change Category window is displayed. Double-click a new category in the New Category drop-down list. Click OK . Related Topics Customizing Document Categories Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing Criteria or Output Settings for Reports in the Report Manager
topics.body: Changing Criteria or Output Settings for Reports in the Report Manager Handling Reports > Generating Reports Using Report Manager > Modifying Reports and Report Groups Changing Criteria or Output Settings for Reports in the Report Manager To modify the selection criteria or output settings for a scheduled report in a group: Select Reports > Report Manager > Advanced Options . The Report Group window is displayed. Select a report group and click Edit Reports . The Report Group Members window is displayed. Select a report and click Prompts . The Output Options window is displayed. Verify that Printer is selected and click OK . The report setup window is displayed. Make the changes to the report criteria and click OK . If the Report Output window is displayed, type the number of copies you want to print and click Next . The software prompts you to update the criteria for all groups that contain this report. Click Yes . If you need to change the criteria for a report in only one group, create a new report and give it a different name. Otherwise, the criteria changes for every group that contains this report. Modify another report, or close all open windows by clicking Close or by clicking Close All in the General Operations section of the Express Bar. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing the Insurance Form Used by an Insurance Plan
topics.body: Changing the Insurance Form Used by an Insurance Plan Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Changing the Insurance Form Used by an Insurance Plan To change the insurance form used by an insurance plan: Click List > Ins. Plan . The Insurance Plan list window is displayed. Find the insurance plan you need from the list, and double-click the plan name. The Insurance Plan Information window is displayed. Click the Claim Filing tab, note the number that is displayed in the Print Claim Form # field, and close the Insurance Plan Information window. Click Insurance > Select Insurance Forms . The Insurance Forms Selection window is displayed. Click the drop-down arrow next to the form number that corresponds with the "Print Claim Form #" determined in Step 3. Select the new insurance form you want to use, and click OK . Close the Insurance Plan list window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing the Name of a Report Group in the Report Manager
topics.body: Changing the Name of a Report Group in the Report Manager Handling Reports > Generating Reports Using Report Manager > Modifying Reports and Report Groups Changing the Name of a Report Group in the Report Manager To change the name of a report group: Select Reports > Report Manager > Advanced Options . The Report Group window is displayed. Select the report group you want to change and click Edit . A window is displayed that enables you to edit the report description, as well as the frequency and next run date. Make the changes and click OK . Close all open windows by clicking Close or by clicking Close All in the General Operations section of the Express Bar. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing the Name of a Report in the Report Manager
topics.body: Changing the Name of a Report in the Report Manager Handling Reports > Generating Reports Using Report Manager > Modifying Reports and Report Groups Changing the Name of a Report in the Report Manager To change the name of a previously set up report: Select Reports > Report Manager > Advanced Options . The Report Group window is displayed. Select a report group and click Edit Reports . The Report Group Members window is displayed. Click Add . The Previously Set Up Reports window is displayed. Select the report you want to change and click Edit Description . A window is displayed that enables you to edit the report description. Type a new report name in the Enter Description field and click OK . The new name is reflected in the Previously Set Up Reports window and in all report groups that contain the report. Close all open windows by clicking Close or by clicking Close All in the General Operations section of the Express Bar. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Impacted Teeth
topics.body: Charting Impacted Teeth Restorative Charting > Using Clinical Conditions Charting Impacted Teeth To chart impacted teeth: In the charting module, on the Conditions toolbar, click Other Conditions . The Clinical Conditions window is displayed. Select Impacted Horizontally or Impacted Vertically . Click Chart Selected . Chart the impacted teeth. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Mixed Dentition
topics.body: Charting Mixed Dentition Restorative Charting > Handling Mixed Dentition Charting Mixed Dentition You can chart permanent teeth on a juvenile patient, as well as chart primary teeth on an adult patient. Primary teeth are automatically displayed in the charting module for patients younger than the age specified in the Patient Adult Age field in the Practice Information window ( File > Practice ). Charting Permanent Teeth on a Juvenile Patient To chart permanent teeth on a juvenile patient: Open the restorative chart for a child younger than your practice's "patient adult age." The chart displays all primary dentition. To chart a tooth that has been shed, click Chart missing teeth on the Conditions toolbar. Click on the tooth itself, not the gray box that displays the tooth number. (The tooth changes from a primary tooth with red roots to a permanent tooth with black roots.) Always apply conditions to the tooth , never to the number above or below the tooth in the gray box. After removing a primary tooth, the permanent tooth displays as "Unerupted." To indicate that the child has a permanent tooth, click Other Conditions on the Conditions toolbar, and select Unerupted from the Clinical Conditions List window. Click on the permanent tooth. (The Unerupted label is removed.) Perform steps 2 through 5 for any number of teeth. Click Save All . Charting Retained Primary Teeth on an Adult Patient To chart retained primary teeth on an adult patient: Open the patient's restorative chart. The chart displays all adult dentition. Click Chart missing teeth on the Conditions toolbar. Click the permanent tooth (for example, Tooth 6) you want to display as a primary tooth. (The tooth graphic is removed, and a condition of "Missing" is recorded on the Clinical History tab.) Always apply conditions to the tooth , never to the number above or below the tooth in the gray box. On the Clinical History tab, locate and right-click on the "Charted Missing" condition associated with the primary tooth (Tooth C, the counterpart to Tooth 6 in this example). Select Delete . A prompt asking if you are sure you want to delete the selected item(s) is displayed. Click Yes . (The tooth changes from a permanent tooth with black roots to a primary tooth with red roots.) Perform steps 2 through 6 for any number of teeth. Click Save All . OR An alternate method t o chart retained primary teeth on an adult patient: Open the patient's restorative chart. The chart displays all adult dentition. Click Chart missing teeth on the Conditions toolbar. Click the permanent tooth you want to display as a primary tooth. (The tooth graphic is removed, and a condition of "Missing" is recorded on the Clinical History tab.) Always apply conditions to the tooth , never to the number above or below the tooth in the gray box. Click the gray tooth number box for the permanent tooth you clicked in Step 3. (The tooth number changes from the permanent tooth number [a numeral] to the primary tooth number [a letter]). Click the blank spot on the arch that was created in Step 3. (The primary tooth number changes to the permanent tooth number.) Click the gray tooth number box again. (The primary tooth and tooth number are displayed.) Perform steps 2 through 6 for any number of teeth. Click Save All . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Supernumerary Teeth
topics.body: Charting Supernumerary Teeth Restorative Charting > Using Clinical Conditions Charting Supernumerary Teeth To chart supernumerary teeth: In the charting module, on the Conditions toolbar, click Other Conditions . The Clinical Conditions window is displayed. Select ID 8 - Supernumerary . Click Chart Selected . Chart the supernumerary teeth. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Tips and Tricks
topics.body: Charting Tips and Tricks Charting Tips and Tricks Use these charting tips to increase your productivity when charting patient procedures: Power Bar Tip: If you are customizing the Charting Module especially the Charting toolbars, be consistent from one treatment room to the next. There�s nothing more frustrating than having your staff searching for something on the computer screen, because it�s in a different place on this machine. Clinical Note Codes Tip: Make up �dummy� codes that you can use to attach default clinical notes. For example, you might have a code that you use for local anesthesia associated with a default clinical note describing the anesthetic and its usual administration. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Charting Audit Trail By Doctor Report
topics.body: Running the Charting Audit Trail By Doctor Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Charting Audit Trail Reports Running the Charting Audit Trail By Doctor Report The Charting Audit Trail by Doctor report generates a list of every patient chart or clinical note deleted by the production doctor. Sample Report To generate a list of chart or clinical note deletions by the production doctor associated with the patient record: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Charting Audit Trail > By Doctor . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Charting Audit Trail By Doctor window is displayed. Select the starting and ending doctor identification numbers from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Checking Patient Benefits
topics.body: Checking Patient Benefits Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Checking Patient Benefits Frequently, a patient will ask what his remaining insurance benefit is. This information is found on the Patient Guarantors window. You can access this window from several areas in the software. To check insurance benefits from the PhoneCenter: Click View PhoneCenter on the Power bar. The Phone Center window is displayed. Type the patient's name in the Find field. The Phone Center window is displayed with the patient record selected. If several patients are on the account, select the name of the patient, and click Insurance Info . The Patient Guarantor window is displayed. Verify the insurance information and click OK . To check insurance benefits from the Patient List window: Click Patient List Window on the Power bar. The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient name. Click Guarantors . The Patient Guarantor window is displayed. Verify the insurance information and click OK . To check insurance benefits from the InOffice: Click InOffice Window on the Power bar. The InOffice window is displayed. Select the patient name and click Patient . The Patient Information window is displayed. Click Guarantors . The Patient Guarantors window is displayed. Verify the insurance information and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Checking Prescription Status
topics.body: Checking Prescription Status eServices > Using the ePrescriptions Module Checking Prescription Status Using the ePrescriptions module, you can check the status of orders, reports, and prescriptions. To check the status of prescriptions: Select System > Modules > ePrescriptions > Prescription Status . The ePrescribing status window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Checking the Amount of Insurance Benefits a Patient Has Used
topics.body: Checking the Amount of Insurance Benefits a Patient Has Used Checking the Amount of Insurance Benefits a Patient Has Used To check the amount of insurance benefits a patient has used: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient for whom you want to verify remaining benefits. From the Options section of the Express bar, click Guarantors. The Guarantors window is displayed. OR Click View Phone Center . The Phone Center window is displayed. Select the patient for whom you want to verify remaining benefits. Click Insurance Info . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Change ADA/ CPT/Transaction Code Utility
topics.body: Running the Change ADA/ CPT/Transaction Code Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Change ADA/CPT/Transaction Code Utility Run this utility to replace an ADA or CPT code in all transaction records. The utility does not place the code in scheduled appointments, tickler entries, or claims. Run the Remap ADA Codes utility to replace the code in appointments and tickler entries. To change the ADA or CPT code in transaction records: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Change ADA/CPT/Transaction Code . The Change ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window is displayed. Type the old code and the code that replaces it in the fields. Click OK . A message informs you that the process is complete. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing the Length of Appointments
topics.body: Changing the Length of Appointments Scheduling Appointments > Editing Appointments Changing the Length of Appointments To change the length of an appointment, select an option: From the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows : Select the appointment to shorten or lengthen. Drag the square located at the lower-right side of the appointment to the appropriate length. If you drag the square preceding the appointment starting slot, the appointment is not shortened, but instead is extended upwards. From the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows : Select the appointment to shorten or lengthen, and select Shorten or Lengthen from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. From the Appointment Card window : Type the number of time slots in the Slots field and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing the Color Group Definition of Individual Procedures
topics.body: Changing the Color Group Definition of Individual Procedures Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Color Scheduling Changing the Color Group Definition of Individual Procedures You can change the color of an individual procedure contained within the minimum and maximum code range for a color group definition. For more information, see Handling Transactions and Codes . To change the color of a procedure contained within a color group definition: Select an option: Select ADA Codes from the List menu. From any window, press Ctrl + C. Select ADA Code List Window from the Power bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. The ADA/Transaction Codes List window is displayed. Press Enter. The color associated with the color group definition for each code is displayed in the Color field. Select a code from the list and click Edit . The ADA/Transaction Codes window is displayed. Click OK . Select Change Color from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Select a Scheduling Color window is displayed. Select the new color group definition from the list and click Select and Close . Click Close twice. Related Topics Resetting the Color Group Definition for an Individual Procedure to the Daybook Color Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing the Display Order of Conditions
topics.body: Changing the Display Order of Conditions Handling Patient Medical History > Understanding Medical Conditions Changing the Display Order of Conditions To dictate the display order of conditions: Select System > Customize Medical History . The Medical Conditions List window is displayed. Press Enter, and click Change Order . The Select and Sort Conditions To Be Displayed window is displayed. Conditions that are not currently displayed in the Medical History window are displayed in the Hidden Conditions section, and conditions that are currently displayed in the Medical History window are displayed in the Displayed Conditions section. Select an option: To display conditions in alphabetical order : Click Sort Alphabetically . To change the location of a condition : Select the condition and click Move Up or Move Down . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Change Doctor Utility
topics.body: Running the Change Doctor Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Change Doctor Utility Run this utility to assign a doctor to accounts or patients that do not have a doctor or to associate a new doctor to patient and account records. You can also run this utility to assign or replace hygienists in patient records. To change an assigned doctor or hygienist: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Change Dr . The Set/Replace Patient/Account Dr ID window is displayed. Select the appropriate option from the Pick an option to run this utility drop-down list. The DR ID to be replaced field is only enabled if you select a Replace option. If you select any of the Set All options, the selected provider is populated in the Set New Account Dr To field in the Practice window, and the selected doctor will be the default for all new accounts. Type the identification number associated with the replacing doctor in the New Doctor ID field. Type the identification number associated with the doctor to be replaced in the DR ID to be replaced field. Click OK . A message asks you to confirm the identification number replacement. Click Yes . A message informs you the process is complete. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing a Fee Schedule
topics.body: Changing a Fee Schedule Handling Transactions and Codes > Using Fee Schedules Changing a Fee Schedule You can raise or lower the fees associated with a particular fee schedule. To change a fee schedule: Select System > Fees Maintenance > Change Fee Schedule . The Change Fee Schedule window is displayed. In the Fee Schedule # to change field, type the number of the fee schedule you want to change. Select the Increase or Decrease option button. Select the percentage by which to increase or decrease the fee schedule. If you don't want to use the available percentages, click the Other option button and type the percentage you want. Click OK . A window is displayed, asking you to confirm the change. Click Yes . The changes might take several minutes, during which time status messages are displayed. The Update Fee Schedule window is displayed. Select an option: To update the fee for existing appointments: Select Update Appointment Fee. T o update the fee for existing treatment plans: Select Update Treatment Plan Fee. To update only those treatment plans of a particular age, type the number of days or a date in the appropriate field. Click OK . If you click Cancel , no updates are made, but the fee schedule is changed. Related Topics Using Fee Schedules Copying a Fee Schedule Updating a Fee Schedule Updating Bluebook Fees Copying a Bluebook Between Plans Copying an Allowed Amount from Plan to Plan Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing Treatment Groups
topics.body: Changing Treatment Groups Charting Treatment Plans > Creating Treatment Plan Groups Changing Treatment Groups To move a treatment plan item to another treatment group: On the Treatment Planning tab, select the treatment plan item and select Click to change the treatment plan group on the Treatment Plan toolbar . The Modify Group Number window is displayed. Select the group number, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing the History Daybook Date Range
topics.body: Changing the History Daybook Date Range Using the Scheduler > Using the History Appointments Window Changing the History Daybook Date Range To change the date range of appointments that are displayed in the History Appointments window: While the History Daybook window is displayed, click Change Date Range , or select Change Date Range from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The History Appointments Date Range window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates for which to view posted appointments from the drop-down lists and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Change All Account ICMs Utility
topics.body: Running the Change All Account ICMs Utility Utilities > Running Customize Menu Utilities Running the Change All Account ICMs Utility Run this utility to change the insurance calculation method (ICM) for all accounts. However, the ICM for accounts with a budget plan balance cannot be changed. The billed dates are set to the current date for accounts that are converted to the new ICM. To change the ICM for all accounts: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Customize > Change All Account ICMs . The Change All Account ICMs window is displayed. Type the new ICM in the Enter new ICM for system and all accounts field. Click Apply . A message asks you to confirm the selections. Click Yes . A message informs you the process is complete and specifies the number of modified records. Click OK . Click Close . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing the Power Bar Location
topics.body: Changing the Power Bar Location Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Power Bar Changing the Power Bar Location To move the Power bar: Select it. Drag it to the appropriate location. Release the mouse. The next time you access the software, the Power bar is displayed in the location. If you drag the Power bar to the extreme left or right of the window, the buttons are displayed vertically. You cannot place the Power bar at the bottom of the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing Search Result Sort Order
topics.body: Changing Search Result Sort Order Getting Started > Searching for Records > Using Find to Search for a Record Changing Search Result Sort Order To change the search method and sort order for search results, select the option from the Sort By section of the list window. The list is automatically re-sorted in the new order. Changing the sort order does not change the mode. In any list window, you can search by identification number rather than by name without changing the sort order. Type the number in the Find field and press: Tab Enter Esc The search results are sorted by name, and the matching record is highlighted. When searching by identification number or other numeric values, the search does not begin until you press: Tab Enter Esc If the list is sorted by name and you search for a name containing an alpha-numeric, the search automatically begins when you type the first non-numeric character. See Record Search Methods for a list of search methods for each record type. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing the Order of ADA Procedure Presentations Within a Patient Presentation
topics.body: Changing the Order of ADA Procedure Presentations Within a Patient Presentation Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint Changing the Order of ADA Procedure Presentations Within a Patient Presentation The presentation must contain more than one ADA procedure presentation to change the order. To change the order in which ADA procedure presentations are viewed in a patient presentation: While viewing the patient presentation, select Tools > Change ADA Code Order . The Specify the ADA Code Presentation Order window is displayed. Select the code to move and click Move Up or Move Down until it is in the appropriate order. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing User Passwords
topics.body: Changing User Passwords System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Changing User Passwords When changing your password , r emember that passwords can be any combination of letters and numbers (no special characters) and must be 8- to 15-characters in length. To change your password: Select System > Change User Password . The Change Password window is displayed. If the strong passwords feature is enabled in the HIPAA Options window, a message at the top of the window informs you of the password constraints. See Setting HIPAA Security and Privacy Options . Type your current password in the Existing Password field. Type your new password in both the New Password and Confirm New Password fields. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing Payment Allocation While Posting Transactions
topics.body: Changing Payment Allocation While Posting Transactions Billing and Receivables > Understanding Payment Allocation Changing Payment Allocation While Posting Transactions When you post transactions in the patient Transactions window, the doctor information that is displayed in the Collection Dr field indicates the provider who is to receive payment for the procedure. The doctor identification number is the doctor selected for payment allocation in the provider record. You can assign collections to a different provider for the transaction. To change the payment allocation while posting a patient transaction: In the patient Transactions window, click Add . The Transactions window is displayed. Select the transaction code from the Code drop-down list. To change the doctor who is to collect payment for the transaction, select the appropriate doctor from the Collection Dr drop-down list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing Treatment Plan Groups
topics.body: Changing Treatment Plan Groups Charting Treatment Plans > Handling Treatment Plan Items Changing Treatment Plan Groups To switch a treatment plan item from one treatment plan to another, select an option: To switch an item to the primary treatment plan on the Treatment Planning tab : Select the treatment plan item, and select Switch selected items to TP1, TP2, or TP3 > Primary - TP1 on the Treatment Plan toolbar . To switch an item to the secondary treatment plan on the Treatment Planning tab : Select the treatment plan item, and select Switch selected items to TP1, TP2, or TP3 > Secondary - TP2 on the Treatment Plan toolbar . To switch an item to the tertiary treatment plan on the Treatment Planning tab: Select the treatment plan item, and select Switch selected items to TP1, TP2, or TP3 > Tertiary - TP3 on the Treatment Plan toolbar . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing Treatment Plan Acceptance Status
