topics.body: Changing Treatment Plan Acceptance Status Treatment Planning Changing Treatment Plan Acceptance Status To change the state of a treatment plan: In the Patient List window, select the patient and select Treatment Plan . The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Select Change State . The Change Treatment Plan State window is displayed. Do one of the following: To change the state of the primary treatment plan, select the correct state from the State drop-down list in the Primary Treatment Plan section, and type the group number. To change the state of the secondary treatment plan, select the correct state from the State drop-down list in the Secondary Treatment Plan section, and type the group number. To change the state of the tertiary treatment plan, select the correct state from the State drop-down list in the Tertiary Treatment Plan section, and type the group number. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Checking In a Patient
topics.body: Checking In a Patient Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Checking In a Patient When you fill the InOffice window , t he text in most columns is displayed in black. Fifteen minutes before each patient appointment time, the time in the Appt column is displayed in red. To log a patient�s arrival: Select the appropriate entry. Click Check In located in the InOffice window. The current system time is displayed in the Arrive column. If you are checking in a new patient, see Activating New Accounts or Patients . If the HIPAA Privacy Acknowledgement Notification is enabled, the appropriate message is displayed. For more information, see Setting HIPAA Security and Privacy Options . If you are using the KIOSK Check-In Module, select Monitor KIOSK Check-In to display a list of patients who check in using the KIOSK module. See Monitoring Checked-In Patients . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Checking Out Patients
topics.body: Checking Out Patients Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Checking Out Patients The check out process enables you to: Post patient transactions Submit insurance Print a patient walkout statement Schedule the next appointment You can set up the process to display a window that prompts you to select check out options or to complete check out options automatically. Related Topics Setting Patient Check Out Options Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing the Order of Rooms in the Scheduler Help Demo
topics.body: Changing the Order of Rooms in the Scheduler Help Demo Loading multimedia control... Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Features
topics.body: Charting Features What's New in Version 14.0 Charting Features The Charting module has received enhancements in the following areas in version 14.0 of the software: Clinical Notes: Treatment Plans: Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting ADA Procedures
topics.body: Charting ADA Procedures Restorative Charting Charting ADA Procedures All ADA procedures, i ncluding non-chartable transactions such as a prophy, can be posted from the charting module. You can chart procedures for both primary and permanent teeth and roots. To chart ADA procedures: Select the mode to indicate whether the procedure to be charted is current work, work performed outside your practice, or treatment plan work. Select the procedure from the Procedure Codes toolbar . To chart a procedure that is not displayed in the toolbar, select it from the ADA drop-down list located at the top of the toolbar. You can select only one procedure at a time, but many procedures can be applied to multiple teeth. Select the tooth. If the procedure requires a surface or quadrant, you can select a single tooth. If the ADA code is not set up correctly in the practice management software, the procedure cannot be charted. For example, if the tooth or surface requirements are not defined in the ADA/Transactions Code window, the procedure is not graphically represented in the chart. Click Save All . If you post a partial denture procedure, the tooth numbers are displayed in the Notes field of the Transactions window. If you neglect to enter the tooth numbers correctly, the Need Tooth Numbers (separate teeth with commas) window is displayed. Type the tooth numbers, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Charting Audit Trail By Patient Name Report
topics.body: Running the Charting Audit Trail By Patient Name Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Charting Audit Trail Reports Running the Charting Audit Trail By Patient Name Report The Charting Audit Trail by Patient Name report generates a list of every patient chart or clinical note deleted. The report is sorted by patient name. Sample Report To generate a list of chart or clinical note deletions by patient name: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Charting Audit Trail > By Patient Name . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Charting Audit Trail By Patient Name window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Charting Audit Trail By Date Report
topics.body: Running the Charting Audit Trail By Date Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Charting Audit Trail Reports Running the Charting Audit Trail By Date Report The Charting Audit Trail by Date report generates a list of every patient chart or clinical note deleted within a specified date range. Sample Report To generate a list of chart or clinical note deletions by the date on which they were deleted: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Charting Audit Trail > By Date . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Charting Audit Trail By Date window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Blood and Suppuration
topics.body: Charting Blood and Suppuration Periodontal Charting Charting Blood and Suppuration To chart blood or suppuration: In the Chart for window, click the Perio tab. Select the display mode on the Perio Views toolbar . Select an option: To chart blood : Select the tooth and select Blood on the Keypad toolbar . Blood is indicated by a red drop on the pocket. To chart suppuration : Select the tooth and select Suppuration on the Keypad toolbar . Suppuration is indicated by a yellow drop on the pocket. When using Auto Advance, you can chart blood or suppuration before entering the next pocket depth by right-clicking the mouse. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Clinical Conditions
topics.body: Charting Clinical Conditions Restorative Charting > Using Clinical Conditions Charting Clinical Conditions Clinical conditions are not posted as transactions. Tooth conditions charted to the current chart are displayed in the treatment plan mode. All conditions function as toggle switches: Charting the same condition to the same tooth a second time removes the condition. No tooth conditions are posted to the practice management software as transactions. You can chart conditions for both primary and permanent teeth and roots. If you are charting for a patient who has mixed dentition, ensure that you select the correct tooth and surface. To chart a condition: Select the mode to indicate whether the condition is to be charted as current work or treatment plan work. Select the condition from the Conditions toolbar . You can select more than one condition at a time to chart to multiple teeth. To chart conditions not represented by a button, select Other Conditions from the Conditions toolbar. The Clinical Conditions List window is displayed. In the Select column, click the checkbox for the condition you want and click Chart Selected . Select the teeth to which the condition applies. All selected conditions are charted to all selected teeth. Click Save All . unerupted tooth Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Working with Clinical Notes
topics.body: Working with Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes SoftDent contains three types of clinical notes: Patient clinical notes: Notes that specify an ADA code, tooth number, surface, provider, or condition which are attached to patient records. Generic clinical notes: Notes that are associated with a specific provider or procedure which can be attached to any patient's chart. Default clinical notes: Generic notes for the most common procedures, which are automatically attached to a patient�s chart when the procedure is posted. You can record clinical notes from the following tabs in the Chart for window: Overview Restorative Perio Exams Clinical Imaging Treatment Planning Notes You can create: Clinical notes to attach to patient records Generic notes to reuse Default notes that are automatically attached to a patient�s clinical notes when the associated procedure is performed Notes that apply to all providers You can also create primary, secondary, and tertiary treatment plan notes, which function in the same way as patient clinical notes. You can add notes: While you are charting When you are posting transactions At the end of the day You can view patient clinical notes in the following windows: Patient Patient List For more information, see Understanding Patient List and Patient Window Options . Related Topics Handling Patient Clinical Notes Handling Generic Clinical Notes Handling Default Clinical Notes Reviewing Generic and Default Clinical Notes Adding Clinical Notes While Posting Adding Clinical Notes at the End of the Day Adding Clinical Notes Per Visit Adding Clinical Notes Per Visit at the End of the Day Adding Clinical Notes for Treatment Plans Copy ing Generic and Default Clinical Notes Copying Generic and Default Clinical Notes from One Provider to Another Printing Clinical Notes Deleting Clinical Notes Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Exams
topics.body: Charting Exams Charting Exams Using the charting module, you can chart: Periodontal tissue Occlusal Soft tissue Temporomandibular joint (TMJ) Periodontal screening and recording (PSR) exams You can save up to six dated exam histories for each type of exam, so you can track treatment progress over time for each patient. You can also: View the patient exam summary Print exam data with the chart Delete an exam Related Topics Setting the Default Exam Sequence Using Exams Tab Toolbars Understanding Exams Tab Exams Viewing and Comparing Perio Exams Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Furcation Grades
topics.body: Charting Furcation Grades Periodontal Charting Charting Furcation Grades To chart furcation grades: In the Chart for window, click the Perio tab. Select the display mode on the Perio Views toolbar . Select the tooth, and select Furcation Grades on the Probe toolbar . The Furcation Grades Tooth window is displayed. You must chart furcation grades on teeth with multiple roots. Click the pocket, and select the class. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting FGM and MGJ
topics.body: Charting FGM and MGJ Periodontal Charting Charting FGM and MGJ You must chart FGM t o calculate attachment loss automatically when the chart is displayed in perio numeric chart mode. You can chart negative FGM values. When charting FGM or MGJ, the pocket depth values entered do not change. To chart FGM and MGJ: In the Chart for window, click the Perio tab. Select the display mode on the Perio Views toolbar . Select an option: To chart FGM : Select Free Gingival Margin on the Probe toolbar , and select the first tooth in the appropriate quadrant or arch. By default, FGM is charted in maroon. After you chart FGM, subsequently charted perio depths precede the FGM data. To chart MGJ : Select Muco-Gingival Margin on the Probe toolbar , and select the first tooth in the appropriate quadrant or arch. By default, MGJ is charted in blue. To set colors, see Setting Perio Chart Options and Properties . If Auto Advance is enabled, the next pocket or next tooth is selected after you enter a value. Click Save All . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Chart Mode Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Chart Mode Toolbar Restorative Charting > Using Restorative Tab Toolbars Using the Chart Mode Toolbar The Chart Mode toolbar commands enable you to view the chart in the selected mode. Click the icon for the description of the Chart Mode toolbar buttons. Displays the chart in Inside Work mode. Displays the chart in Outside Work mode. Displays the initial chart . Displays the current chart . Charts treatment plan procedures. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Chart Menu
topics.body: Using the Chart Menu Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the Chart Menu The Chart menu e nables you to set charting preferences, view tooth details, select a new provider, view patient documents and images, and access patient education software. You can also chart decay, periapical conditions, cracks, missing teeth, diastema, all primary teeth as missing, and all permanent teeth as unerupted. The commands available from the Chart menu are: Options : Select this command to set restorative chart options. See Setting Charting Options and Properties . Decay : Select this command to chart patient decay. See Using the Conditions Toolbar and Charting ADA Procedures . Periapical Condition : Select this command to chart periapical conditions. See Using the Conditions Toolbar and Charting Restorative Conditions. Chart Cracks : Select this command to chart cracked teeth. See Using the Conditions Toolbar and Charting Restorative Conditions. Chart Missing : Select this command to chart missing teeth. See Using the Conditions Toolbar and Charting Restorative Conditions. Chart Diastema : Select this command to chart diastema. See Using the Conditions Toolbar and Charting Restorative Conditions. Chart All Primary Teeth as Missing : Select this command to indicate that the patient has lost all primary teeth. See Viewing Permanent Teeth . Chart All Permanent Teeth as Unerupted : Select this command to indicate that none of the patient's permanent teeth have erupted. See Viewing Primary Teeth . Tooth Details : Select this command to view the details of the selected tooth. See Using the Procedure Codes Toolbar . Provider List : Select this command to select a provider to assign to the chart or procedures. See Selecting Providers . Patient Documents and Images : Select this command to display the Documents window. In this window, you can view, e-mail, and amend documents and images associated with a patient. Patient Education : Select this command to access your third party patient education software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Mobility, Plaque, and Calculus
topics.body: Charting Mobility, Plaque, and Calculus Periodontal Charting Charting Mobility, Plaque, and Calculus The degree of tooth mobility, plaque, and calculus do not display graphically, but are noted beneath the tooth. To chart mobility, plaque, and calculus: In the Chart for window, click the Perio tab. Select the display mode on the Perio Views toolbar . Select an option: To chart plaque : Select the tooth and select a button on the Plaque toolbar . The plaque degree is displayed beneath the tooth. To chart mobility : Select the tooth and select a button on the Mobility toolbar . The mobility degree is displayed beneath the tooth. To chart calculus : Select the tooth and select a button on the Calculus toolbar . The calculus degree is displayed beneath the tooth. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accessing the Charting Module
topics.body: Accessing the Charting Module Using the SoftDent Charting Module Accessing the Charting Module To access the charting module, select an option: In the Patient List window : Select a patient and click Chart ; or select Clinical Management > Chart or Chart from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Click Chart ; or select Clinical Management > Chart or Chart from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Daily Appointments window : Select a patient appointment and select Patient�s Chart from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Book At A Glance window : Select a patient appointment and select Patient�s Chart from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Charting Options and Properties
topics.body: Setting Charting Options and Properties Restorative Charting Setting Charting Options and Properties Charting options are workstation-specific. To set restorative charting options and properties: Select Chart > Options . The Chart Properties window is displayed. To set charting defaults, select an option: To use only a pointing device to chart information : Select Use pointing device . You can also use the mouse to chart information when this option is selected. To display a message that asks each user to remove tooth watches while posting transactions : Select Ask to remove watches while posting . To display the Watch Summary window when you access a chart that contains a watched tooth : Select Enable Watch Summary . To load all uncharted history transactions each time you access a patient�s chart : Select Chart History Transactions . If this checkbox is not selected, only new procedures and uncharted procedures listed in the patient Transactions window are displayed. To select an anatomical display title : Select it from the Anatomical Display Title drop-down list. To set mobility display defaults, select an option: To never display mobility information : Select Do Not Show . To display the mobility values for all teeth : Select Show All Values . To display charted mobility values only : Select Show Charted . To set clinical notes defaults, select an option: To display a single window where you can add all of the clinical notes for a visit after posting the procedures: Select Add Clinical Notes Per Visit and Add Clinical Notes While Posting . The Add Clinical Notes For Visit window is displayed when you close the patient chart. To add a clinical note to a chart after posting each transaction : Select Add Clinical Notes While Posting . To add clinical notes to all charts at the end of the day : Select Add Clinical Notes at End Of Day . T o display a single window at the end of the day where you can add all of the clinical notes for a visit: Select Add Clinical Notes Per Visit and Add Clinical Notes At End of Day . The Work Performed Per Visit window is displayed when you select Clinical Notes > Post End of Day Notes . To add clinical notes to a chart manually : Select Add Clinical Notes Manually . To set the default charting tab, select an option: To display the Overview tab when accessing charts: Select Overview . To display the Perio tab when accessing charts : Select Perio . To display the Restorative tab when accessing charts : Select Restorative . To set the default treatment plan mode on the Treatment Planning tab, select an option: To display current work in treatment plan primary mode : Select Show current Work in TP 1 . To display current work in treatment plan secondary mode : Select Show current Work in TP 2 . To display current work in treatment plan tertiary mode: Select Show current Work in TP3 . To populate default clinical note text in the notes field of a clinical note that is added when you add a procedure to the treatment plan, select Use Default Text in TP Notes . If this option is not selected, the treatment plan clinical notes field remains blank. To change the default colors used when the Restorative tab is the active window, click the colored button next to Outside Work. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . The new color for work performed outside your practice is displayed next to the item. Repeat for each item you want to change. To help indicate the mode in which you are charting, the

topics.title: Charting Pocket Depth
topics.body: Charting Pocket Depth Periodontal Charting Charting Pocket Depth To chart pocket depth: In the Chart for window, click the Perio tab. Select the display mode on the Perio Views toolbar , and select Pocket Depth on the Probe toolbar . Select the first tooth in the appropriate quadrant or arch, and click the numerical key that corresponds with the correct depth on the Keypad toolbar . You can also type the number. If Auto Advance is enabled, the next pocket or next tooth is selected after you enter a value. Click Save All . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Treatment Plan Items
topics.body: Charting Treatment Plan Items Charting Treatment Plans > Handling Treatment Plan Items Charting Treatment Plan Items When the Restorative tab is displayed in a treatment plan mode, you can: Chart primary, secondary, or tertiary treatment plan procedures Display Inside and outside work Display conditions To chart treatment plan items: On the Restorative tab in the Charting for window, select an option: To chart primary treatment plan items : Select Treatment Plan Primary from the Mode drop-down list or select Treatment Plan 1 from the Chart Treatment Plan button on the Chart Mode toolbar . To chart secondary treatment plan items : Select Treatment Plan Secondary from the Mode drop-down list, or select Treatment Plan 2 from the Chart Treatment Plan button on the Chart Mode toolbar . To chart tertiary treatment plan items: Select Treatment Plan Tertiary from the Mode drop-down list, or select Treatment Plan 3 from the Chart Treatment Plan button on the Chart Mode toolbar . Select the procedure on the Procedure Codes toolbar . To chart a procedure that is not displayed in the toolbar, select it from the ADA drop-down list located at the top of the toolbar. You can select only one procedure at a time, but many procedures can be applied to multiple teeth. Select the tooth. If the procedure requires a surface or quadrant, you can select a single tooth only. If the ADA code is not set up correctly in the practice management software, the procedure cannot be charted. For example, if the tooth or surface requirements are not defined, you cannot chart the procedure. Click Save All . If Add Clinical Notes While Posting is selected in the Chart Options window, the Add Clinical Notes window is displayed. Add the notes and click Yes . To view the treatment plan items, click the Treatment Planning tab. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Checking Patient Eligibility
topics.body: Checking Patient Eligibility eServices > Using the eVerifications Service > Using the eVerifications Insurance Eligibility Service Checking Patient Eligibility To check patient eligibility: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient, and click Eligibility , or select Eligibility from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Click Eligibility , or select Eligibility from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. To print this information, click Print . The Print window is displayed. Select the printer, and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Claim and Statement Transmission Reports
topics.body: Claim and Statement Transmission Reports eServices > Using eServices Reports Claim and Statement Transmission Reports Transmission Summary The Transmission Summary report lists�by provider�all claims successfully transmitted, detailing the last name, first name, patient ID, and claim number. The total for each provider is listed, as well as the total for the entire transmission for all providers. To receive this report, you must have loaded, validated, and transmitted your claim file to the eServices clearinghouse. Transmission Log The Transmission Log report lists all the claims transmitted to the eServices clearinghouse for the previous transmission a day or two ago. This report should match the Transmission Summary from a day or two ago when the last transmission occurred. The report lists the patient's name, patient ID, claim number, and total charge of each claim. To receive this report, you must have loaded, validated, and transmitted your claims to the eServices clearinghouse. You will receive this report upon the next transmission. It lists the claims sent during the previous session. Statement Transmission Summary This report lists all statements successfully transmitted to the eServices clearinghouse. The patient name, SoftDent account number, amount due, and the minimum payment on the statement are included in the report. To receive this report, you must have created statements in SoftDent first and selected the Electronic option from the statement preview window and printed the listing of statement names. Once the file is created and placed in eServices, you transmit the statement file to the eServices clearinghouse. Statement Audit Listing This report is a confirmation returned by the clearinghouse that statements sent on the listed date have been printed and mailed. The office�s eServices account number is printed on the report, along with the patient name and amount due of the statement. To receive this report, you must have created statements in SoftDent first and selected the Electronic option from the statement preview window and printed the listing of statement names. Once the file is created and placed in eServices, you transmit the statement file to the eServices clearinghouse. This report is delayed by one to two business days, depending on the time of day transmitted. It lists the statements sent during the previous session. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Claim Error and Rejection Reports
topics.body: Claim Error and Rejection Reports eServices > Using eServices Reports Claim Error and Rejection Reports United Concordia Claim Rejections This report indicates a rejection of a claim by United Concordia. The patient�s last name and claim total are listed, along with the data that was found in error at United Concordia. To receive this report, you must have loaded, validated, and transmitted your claims to the eServices clearinghouse. This report is returned by the insurance company within one to three business days. It lists the claims sent during the previous session. Transmission Log Errors This report indicates that a problem was encountered processing a recently received file. This could be due to an interrupted transmission or corrupt data. Contact eServices whenever this error occurs. To receive this report, you must have loaded, validated, and transmitted your claims to the eServices clearinghouse. You will receive this report upon the next transmission. It lists the claims sent during the previous session. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Claim Status Reports
topics.body: Claim Status Reports eServices > Using eServices Reports Claim Status Reports eServices Claim Status Report This report details the claims sent on a particular day while also including any claim errors encountered. Claim errors are noted with an error number in the Message # column along with a description at the bottom of the report. Only claims marked with a REJ and an error number are not forwarded to the insurance company by the clearinghouse. Contact eServices if any help is needed in handling a rejection or error. To receive this report, you must have loaded, validated, and transmitted your claims to the eServices clearinghouse. You will receive this report upon the next transmission. This report lists the claims sent during the previous session. Accumulative Claim Status Report This report is typically returned within three business days and lists any claim payment messages returned by insurance companies, as well as claims in which the insurance company acknowledges receipt. To receive this report, you must have loaded, validated, and transmitted your claims to the eServices clearinghouse. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Claim Validation Error Codes
topics.body: Claim Validation Error Codes eServices Claim Validation Error Codes There are several claim validation error codes you might receive. The error number, a description, and the steps necessary to correct the error are included. The codes are listed numerically and are split into two groups: Claim Validation Error Codes 0999 - 2021 Claim Validation Error Codes 2022 - 21050 Related Topics Claim Validation Error Codes 0999 - 2021 Claim Validation Error Codes 2022 - 21050 Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Claim Validation Error Codes 0999 - 2021
topics.body: Claim Validation Error Codes 0999 - 2021 eServices > Claim Validation Error Codes Claim Validation Error Codes 0999 - 2021 Following is a list of error codes you might receive. Click the error number to view a description of the error and the steps necessary to correct it. [ Error 0999 ] Description: Internal error. Character field size greater than 1. To correct: This error should not occur. If it does, contact your eServices representative. [ Error 1000 ] Description: This error appears when a field required for the claim is not completed. To correct: Look at the validation report for the name of the field that is empty. Return to SoftDent and enter the missing information for the specified field(s). Example: Group Number, SSN, Specialty Code, etc. Rerun the validation phase to verify that the error is corrected If Error #1000 occurs, but the error message looks similar to the following, the above steps do not apply. Error #1000, E6-12, Rendering Provider Network ID Required field is empty. This error indicates the treating provider for the claim specified is missing some information on his/her provider window in SoftDent. Typically, this error will indicate which field on the provider window is empty and needs to be filled in. Once this field is filled in, the error should be corrected. Return to the eServices program and reload and validate your claims to verify they have passed validation. If Error #1000 occurs, but the error message looks similar to the following, the above steps do not apply. Error #1000, DA0-18 Insured Id No Required field is empty. This error indicates an invalid Blue Shield card ID. Refer to Subscriber's ID card for proper ID. To correct: The Subscriber ID should be entered in the BCBS# field (on the Account Information window) without any spaces or dashes. Example: Subscriber ID 123-45-6789 should be entered as 123456789. [ Error 1001 ] Description: Field contains invalid characters which have been removed. To correct: There may have been a dash "-" or a space " " in the required data field. No action is required. [ Error 1002 ] Description: This error indicates that the specified field(s) contains invalid characters, which are displayed as part of the message. Invalid characters include the following: spaces, hyphens (dashes), percent signs, etc. You must make any changes before submitting the claim. In the future, be aware of the invalid characters, and remove them from your claim, patient, or provider information. To correct: Look at the validation report for the names of fields containing the invalid characters. Return to SoftDent and remove any spaces, hyphens, or unusual characters from the specified field(s). Rerun the validation phase to verify that the error is corrected. [ Error 1003 ] Description: Number is out of range, must be between __ and __. To correct: The number used must fall within the specified range. Correct and resubmit. [ Error 1004 ] Description: This is an internal error that appears when the specified number you entered in a field is not in the allowed range. To correct: This error should not occur. If it does, contact eServices. [ Error 1005 ] Description: This error indicates that you have incorrectly specified the patient�s relationship to the insured on the Patient Information window or this field has been left blank. To correct: Look on the validation report for the name of the patient missing this relationship criteria. Return to SoftDent and open this person's Patient window

topics.title: Claim Validation Error Codes 2022 - 21050
topics.body: Claim Validation Error Codes 2022 - 21050 eServices > Claim Validation Error Codes Claim Validation Error Codes 2022 - 21050 Following is a list of error codes you might receive. Click the error number to view a description of the error and the steps necessary to correct it. [ Error 2022 ] Description: Procedure code(s) indicated may require x-rays. To correct: Return to SoftDent, and select Insurance > Review Claim . Type the claim number requiring x-rays, and click OK . The Insurance Claim Entry window is displayed. Click Edit , and click the Status tab. Deselect the ECS checkbox (in the bottom middle of the window), and print the claim to be sent with x-rays. If you send this claim electronically and the insurance company still requires x-rays or additional information, the insurance company will send a request for information on one of your electronically received reports. [ Error 2025 ] Description: The insurance company only allows the specified number of transactions per electronic claim. To correct: Distribute the transactions over more than one claim. [ Error 2026 ] Description: The claim has multiple transactions but different places of service. To correct: Look on the validation report for the name of the patient with the incorrect place of service. Return to SoftDent and open this person's Patient window, and click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Find the transactions that are in the claim. Verify that the place of service matches for each transaction. [ Error 2027 ] Description: Claim is missing other facility information. This error occurs on a claim that has a place of service or facility listed other than In-Office. This occurs with medical procedure codes that cannot be sent electronically with SoftDent. To correct: Select the claim in the Claim Submission List window and click Delete Claim (one of the Power Buttons on the right). This will place the claim back in the SoftDent print batch for printing at a later time. [ Warning 2028 ] Description: This facility's NPI value is empty. Some claims may require that the NPI value be filled in. To correct: WARNING: The NPI field on the Practice window is empty. This is only used as a type 2 (facility) NPI. Enter NPI if applicable. [ Warning 2029 ] Description: The Referring Dr�s NPI value is empty. Some claims may require the NPI for submission and could be rejected. To correct: WARNING: The NPI field on the referring provider�s screen is empty. Find the correct referring provider within the list and enter the NPI if needed. [ Error 2030 ] Description: This claim's attachment has not been sent to NEA. Please transmit claim when done sending attachments. To correct: The attachment for this claim has either not been sent or the NEA data was not correctly received. Resend the attachment and verify that the NEA tracking information populates in SoftDent. [ Warning 2031 ] Description: The Referring Dr�s First Name is blank. Some claims may require this and could be rejected. To correct: WARNING: The referring provider used on this claim has a blank First Name field. Find the correct referring provider within the list and enter a first name if needed. [ Warning 2032 ] Description: The Referring Dr�s Last Name is blank. Some claims may require this and could be rejected. To correct: WARNING: The referring provider used on this claim has a blank Last Name field. Find the correct referring provider within the list and enter a last name if needed. [ Error 2050 ] De

topics.title: Using the Claim Adjustment Window
topics.body: Using the Claim Adjustment Window eServices > Using ePostings > Understanding the ePostings Payment Detail Window Using the Claim Adjustment Window The Claim Adjustment window enables you to distribute claim adjustments among two or more Dr$ IDs. To distribute claim adjustments among two or more Dr$ IDs: Do one of the following: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > ePostings > ePostings Payment Viewer. Click in the ePostings Notifications section in Practice Central. Select the ePostings Notifications entry in the Office Expert Summary in Practice Central. The ePostings Payment Viewer window is displayed. Double-click a payment. The ePostings Payment Detail window is displayed. Select a claim to populate the fields at the bottom of the window with specific information about the procedures involved with the claim. In the Status field, select from the following: Verify Distrib: Accept Distribution�After verifying all adjustment reasons, select this option to confirm that the SoftDent estimation did not match the actual ePostings distribution. If the Update Bluebook checkbox is selected and enabled, the bluebook is updated. Note: The Bluebook window is not displayed. Edit Distribution�Select this option to display the ePostings Claim Detail window and make changes to the totals in the Pat Resp. and Writeoff columns. For more information, see Understanding the ePostings Claim Detail window. Manual Distrib: Edit Distribution�Select this option to display the ePostings Claim Detail window and make changes to the totals in the Pat Resp. and Writeoff columns. For more information, see Understanding the ePostings Claim Detail window. Accept Distribution�Select this option to indicate that the reasons provided are acceptable and that the distributions should take place based on the original reason codes. If the Update Bluebook checkbox is selected and enabled, the bluebook is updated. Note: The Bluebook window is not displayed. After clicking Accept Distribution or Edit Distribution and OK , the Claim Adjustment window is displayed. In the Amount to Post column, type the amount that should be posted for each Dr$ ID and for each transaction code. You must distribute the full adjustment amount. Partial distributions are not saved. OR Click Distribute by % to distribute the adjustment amount as a percentage of the total amount the doctor is receiving for the procedure. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Clinical Detail Tab Container
topics.body: Using the Clinical Detail Tab Container Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the Clinical Detail Tab Container The Clinical Detail Tab Container enables you to access the following associated with a patient: Clinical images Clinical history This tab is displayed at the bottom of the Chart for window no matter which tab is the active window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Overview Tab
topics.body: Overview Tab Overview Tab The Overview tab gives you key information about the patient's clinical history in one window. You can see the patient's current condition, the treatment scheduled for the current visit, and the treatment proposed and/or scheduled for future visits. The Overview tab contains five sections: Clinical History: Displays procedures performed, clinical notes, and thumbnails of the last images taken. Use the sliders to scroll through additional items in the list. Restorative Tab Chart: Displays a view-only version of the patient's current restorative tooth chart. Click the View Current , View Initial , or View Tx Plan buttons to change the chart view. Perio Tab Chart: Displays a view-only version of the patient's current perio chart. Treatment Plan: Displays the following: -- Drop-down list of treatment plans -- Drop-down list of treatment plan groups -- Number of procedures included in the selected treatment plan or treatment plan group -- Number of treatment plan procedures scheduled -- Treatment plan date -- Scrollable list of treatment plan procedures Next Appointment: Displays the time of the next appointment, a list of the next appointment's scheduled procedures, and the Add New button. -- Click on the next appointment to open the appointment card for the appointment. -- Click Add New to open the patient appointment list to schedule another appointment. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Clinical Notes
topics.body: Clinical Notes What's New in Version 14.0 > Charting Features Clinical Notes Clinical notes have received enhancements in the following areas: Accessing Generic Clinical Notes from Outside the Charting Module You can now access clinical notes from several areas outside of the charting module. List Menu A Clinical Notes option has been added to the List menu on the Main Menu bar, enabling you to display the Generic Clinical Notes window without opening a patient's chart. Select List > Clinical Notes > Generic Clinical Notes to display the Generic Clinical Notes window. See List Menu Commands and Handling Generic Clinical Notes for more information. ADA/Transaction Codes Window A Generic Notes option has been added to the Options menu of the ADA List and ADA windows. Clicking this option displays the Generic Clinical Notes window, which contains a list of all generic and default clinical notes associated with a particular code, sorted by provider ID. Posting End of Day Notes from Outside the Chart A Clinical Notes option has been added to the List menu on the Main Menu bar, enabling you to display the Work Performed window without opening a patient's chart. Select List > Clinical Notes > Post End of Day Notes to display the Work Performed window. See List Menu Commands for more information. Post End of Day Notes Option Is Now Always Available In past versions of the software, you did not have the option of adding clinical notes at the end of the day and adding clinical notes while posting. (The Post End of Day Notes menu option was disabled if Add Clinical Notes While Posting was selected in the Chart Properties window.) Now, the Post End Of Day Notes menu option is always enabled, regardless of the setting in the Chart Properties window. New Procedure Date and Date Created Fields on Info Tab of the Patient Clinical Notes Window The Info tab of the Patient Clinical Notes window now contains a Procedure Date field next to the date and time the note was created. This enables you to post a clinical note on a date that is different from the date on which the procedure was performed. When you add a clinical note in the Patient Clinical Notes window, the Procedure Date field defaults to today's date, and the Date Created area displays the date and time the note was created. When you add an end-of-day clinical note, the Procedure Date field defaults to the date you selected in the Work Date field, and the Date Created area displays the date and time the note was created. See Adding Clinical Notes at the End of the Day for more information. Ability to Edit a Clinical Note Until the End of the Day You can now edit a saved clinical note, provided you are editing the note on the day it was created and you have the proper security permissions. When you select a clinical note created today, an Edit Note Text button is displayed. When you click the button, the current note text from the Notes History tab is displayed in the note field on the Info tab, and the date stamp is removed. You can edit or add to the note, and the edited text replaces the current note text. When you click OK , the date stamp is added to the edited note text. See Editing Patient Clinical Notes for more information. Clinical Security Changes Several changes have been made to the Clinical security permissions: The Modify and Delete Clinical Notes security permission has been replaced by the Amend Clinical Notes (anytime) and the Delete Clinical Notes security permissions. Users wh

topics.title: Setting Clinical Condition Security and Overrides
topics.body: Setting Clinical Condition Security and Overrides Restorative Charting > Using Clinical Conditions Setting Clinical Condition Security and Overrides When system security is enabled, access to the Clinical Condition database is restricted. Enable the setting to allow a user to access the database to add or edit conditions. You can also set a login and password override to enable an employee to access the database. Related Topics Setting Clinical Condition Security Setting Clinical Condition Overrides Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Clinical Condition Security
topics.body: Setting Clinical Condition Security Restorative Charting > Using Clinical Conditions > Setting Clinical Condition Security and Overrides Setting Clinical Condition Security To enable users to add, edit, and delete conditions: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Double-click the employee. The Rights By User window is displayed. Select Clinical . A list of Clinical security settings is displayed. Select Add Clinical Conditions and click OK . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Click Close . By default, only the super user can display the new Practice Central sections. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Clinical Imaging Tab Toolbars
topics.body: Using Clinical Imaging Tab Toolbars Handling Images While Charting Using Clinical Imaging Tab Toolbars The following toolbars can be displayed in the Toolbars section of the Clinical Imaging tab: Common Clinical Imaging Layout To use the Common toolbar, see Using the Common Toolbar . Related Topics Using the Clinical Imaging Toolbar Using the Image Layout Toolbar Using the Image Capture Toolbar Using the Image Annotate Toolbar Using the Image Editor Toolbar Need more information? topics.title: Clocking In Without Clocking Out
topics.body: Clocking In Without Clocking Out Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card > Clocking In and Out Clocking In Without Clocking Out If you failed to clock out for a previous work period, the software prompts you to enter a memo the next time you clock in. To clock in without clocking out for a previous work period: Select Clock In from the General Operations section of the Express bar. Type your user name and password, and click OK . OR If a fingerprint scanner is attached to the computer and you have a fingerprint in the database, scan your fingerprint. A message is displayed, stating that there is no clock out time and asking if you still want to clock in. Click Yes . A record is added to the time clock database, and a message is displayed, asking if you want to add a memo for the time card that is not clocked out. Click Yes . The Memos window is displayed. The fields are pre-populated, and a message is displayed, stating that there is no clock out time for the employee. Example There is no Clock Out time for John Sample on 01/16/2015 (clocked in at 2:55 pm). Type the time card administrator's name in the To: field, and change any other fields as necessary. Click OK . Related Topics Clocking In and Out Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: How the Claim Balance Transfer Works
topics.body: How the Claim Balance Transfer Works Submitting and Tracking Claims > Transferring Claim Balances to the Patient How the Claim Balance Transfer Works The date entered in the First Sent date drop-down list on the Status tab of the Claim window is used to determine whether the insurance balance is transferred to the patient. If this date is prior to the date in the Transfer Old Insurance Claims to Patients window, the balance is transferred. If the patient is covered by only one insurance plan, claims prior to the specified date are marked complete and deleted. If the patient has both primary and secondary coverage: Any primary claims prior to the specified date are marked complete. The patient becomes responsible for the estimated primary portion of the claim. Secondary claims are sent to the insurance claim batch file so they can be submitted to the secondary insurance. The estimated secondary portion of the claim remains part of the insurance balance and is not transferred back to the patient. Before an outstanding claim balance is transferred : Its status in the patient�s Transaction window is Pr, indicating that primary insurance payment is pending. After you transfer the outstanding balance : The claim status changes to Tr, to show that the insurance balance has been transferred to the patient. For any balances covered by a secondary plan that were submitted : The status becomes Sc to indicate that secondary insurance payment is pending. The Billed date is replaced with the date the transfer was performed. In addition: Code 103000.50 is automatically posted to the patient�s Transactions window for every claim balance transferred. The claim number is displayed in the Notes field. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Checking Claim Status Information
topics.body: Checking Claim Status Information Submitting and Tracking Claims > Submitting Dental Claims Checking Claim Status Information You can check the status of a claim when you submit it or after you submit it. To check the status from the Claims window, click the Status tab or press Ctrl + Tab. The following information is displayed: Account guarantor Insurance plan Claim status Form number eVerifications insurance eligibility capability If the current open claim is a primary claim, the Dr Signature on File option uses the primary plan's Dr Signature on File setting. If the current open claim is a secondary claim, the Dr Signature on File option uses the secondary plan's Dr Signature on File setting. In either case, you can manually change the setting before submitting the claim. Claims have the following statuses: Open Primary : Primary insurance is not complete. Open Secondary : Secondary insurance is not complete. Complete : Claim has been paid. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Claim List and Claim Window Options
topics.body: Using Claim List and Claim Window Options Submitting and Tracking Claims Using Claim List and Claim Window Options The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu in the Claim List or Claim windows are: Print Copy: Prints a copy of the claim. Review Transactions: Displays all transactions associated with the selected claim. eClaim Status: Displays the claim status of the electronic claim. This option is only available if you use eClaims. Submit Primary : Generates an insurance form to submit to the primary insurance carrier. You can print the claim or submit it to the batch file to print later. This updates the First Sent date on the Status tab of the Claim record. If you use the Electronic Claim Service (ECS), you can submit the claim electronically. If you use the Real Time Claim Submission service, and the carrier is ECS capable, you can submit the claim for immediate payor response. Submit Secondary : Generates an insurance form to submit to the secondary insurance carrier. You can print the claim or submit it to the batch file to print later. This updates the First Sent date on the Status tab of the Claim record. If you use the Electronic Claim Service (ECS), you can submit the claim electronically. If you use the Real Time Claim Submission service, and the carrier is ECS capable, you can submit the claim for immediate payor response. Cancel Claim : Cancels, rather than deletes, a submitted claim. Canceling a claim changes the claim status of all submitted transactions associated with the claim from Pr to Not Sent. You can resubmit the claim after making corrections. Missing Teeth : Displays a window from which you specify missing permanent or primary teeth for the patient on the claim. Select the option for each missing tooth and click OK . If you use the charting module, any missing teeth marked on this window are also noted on the patient's chart. Auto Dialer : Displays the Phone Numbers window for the patient. If you have a modem set up to work with the software, you can highlight the number to call and click Dial on the toolbar. The modem dials the number for you. If you do not have a modem, the Phone Numbers window is displayed for your reference only. Contacts: Displays a list of contacts for the selected claim sorted by date or by type. Documents : Opens the Documents For Claim tree view, in which you can select scanned or captured documents and images associated with this claim to view. View Insurance Company: Displays insurance company information for the selected claim. Create NEA Attachment : Opens the Send Attachments to NEA window, which enables you to select images and type in narratives associated with the claim. Send NEA Attachments : Starts the NEA software and transmits any existing files to the NEA repository. You must have an open connection to the Internet to do this. 1 Check for NEA Updates: Displays updated files sent by the NEA repository. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Claim Record
topics.body: Using the Claim Record Submitting and Tracking Claims Using the Claim Record The claim record contains information that is printed on insurance claim forms. Primary insurance claim forms are typically filed during the checkout process. For more information, see Setting Insurance Defaults and Checking Out Patients . To view a claim: Select List > Claim or press Ctrl + Y. The Claim List window is displayed. Double-click the claim. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Company Report
topics.body: Running the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Company Report Submitting and Tracking Claims > Running Claim Reports Running the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Company Report The Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Company report lists all outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical insurance claims with a benefit owed to the patient. The report is sorted by insurance company. Sample Report To run the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Company report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Co. The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Outstanding Insurance Claims by Company window is displayed. In the Insurance Companies section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending insurance companies for the report. Click ePostings Capable Companies Only to report on only those insurance companies that participate in ePostings. In the Provider section, click the drop-down arrow to select a provider from the list, or leave 999 to report on all providers. In the Options section, select the options to include in the report, and type a number to indicate the age of the claims. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Patient Report
topics.body: Running the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Patient Report Submitting and Tracking Claims > Running Claim Reports Running the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Patient Report The Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Patient report lists all outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical insurance claims with a benefit owed to the patient. The report is sorted by patient name. Sample Report To run the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Patient report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Patient. The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Outstanding Insurance Claims by Patient Report Setup window is displayed. In the Patient Names section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending names for the report. In the Provider section, click the drop-down arrow to select a provider from the list, or leave 999 to report on all providers. In the Options section, select the options to include in the report, and type a number to indicate the age of the claims. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Clinical Images Tab - Editing Images
topics.body: Clinical Images Tab - Editing Images Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Images Editing Images To edit an image: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tabs in the Chart for window, click the Clinical Images tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the image, right-click, select Edit , and make the changes. For more information, see Using Edit Mode . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container
topics.body: Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container A chronological list of the following items associated with the patient are displayed on the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container: Treatment plans Posted transactions Posted conditions Saved exams Prescriptions Medical histories Lab cases Case presentation module presentations Clinical notes Capture image descriptions Outgoing referrals Missed appointments Contacts Related Topics Handling Posted Transactions Deleting Pending Transactions from a Patient Chart Handling Treatment Plan Items Handling Prescriptions Handling Medical Histories Handling Lab Cases Handling Case Presentation Module Presentations Handling Clinical Notes Handling Conditions Handling Exams Need more information? topics.title: Clearing Images from the Clinical Imaging Tab
topics.body: Clearing Images from the Clinical Imaging Tab Handling Images While Charting > Using Layout Mode > Viewing Images Clearing Images from the Clinical Imaging Tab To clear an image from the tab when layout mode is selected, select Clear Layout View on the Image Layout toolbar . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container
topics.body: Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container A chronological list of all CS Imaging software, third-party x-ray, intraoral, extraoral, imported, and digital camera images for a patient is displayed on the Clinical Images tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. These images include RVG, panoramic, cephalometric, 3D, STV, secondary capture, and digital camera images acquired using Trophy software, versions 4.2 and 5.0, and dental imaging software, versions 6.0 and 6.8. If a portrait is associated with the patient, it is displayed before any other image on the tab. Third party images are x-ray images captured from a non-Carestream digital x-ray device. The following image types are displayed on the Clinical Images tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container: RVG 6000 8000 digital panoramic 8000C digital panoramic and cephalometric 9000 3D 1000 intraoral video FMS STV/IO (intraoral camera) Digital camera (DX) Portrait Secondary capture Other Related Topics Handling Images Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Clinical Imaging Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Clinical Imaging Toolbar Handling Images While Charting > Using Clinical Imaging Tab Toolbars Using the Clinical Imaging Toolbar Use the Clinical Imaging toolbar commands t o display the tab in layout, capture, annotate, edit, or organization modes. Click the icon for the description of the Clinical Imaging toolbar buttons. Displays the tab in Layout mode . Displays the tab in Capture mode . Displays the tab in Annotate mode . Displays the tab in Edit mode . Displays the tab in Organization mode . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Clinical Note Options
topics.body: Using Clinical Note Options Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Clinical Notes Using Clinical Note Options To use the clinical note options: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, or Treatment Planning tab in the Chart for window, click the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the clinical note, right-click, and select an option: To display the clinical note : Select View . To add a new clinical note : Select Add . The Clinical_note window is displayed. Type the clinical note, and click OK . To edit a clinical note : Select Amend . The details of the note are displayed in the Clinical_note window. Make the changes, and click OK . To delete a clinical note : Select Delete . A message asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . To view the transaction to which the note is attached : Select View Attached Transaction . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Clinical Notes Menu
topics.body: Using the Clinical Notes Menu Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the Clinical Notes Menu The Clinical Notes menu e nables you to add and amend patient clinical notes, add and amend generic notes, and post end of day notes. You can also review a list for each provider by date or patient, and review a list of generic and default notes by ADA code. The commands available from the Clinical Notes menu are: View Clinical Notes : Select this command to view a list of clinical notes associated with a patient. See Viewing and Reviewing Patient Clinical Notes . Add Note : Select this command to add a patient clinical note. See Adding Patient Clinical Notes . Generic Notes : Select this command to add a generic note for the procedure and provider. See Handling Generic Clinical Notes . Post End of Day Notes : Select this command to add clinical notes for all procedures posted on the current day that do not have notes assigned. See Adding Clinical Notes at the End of the Day . Review > Patient Notes by Date : Select this command to review a list of clinical notes associated with a patient by date. See Viewing and Reviewing Patient Clinical Notes . Review > Patient Notes by Tooth : Select this command to review a list of clinical notes associated with a patient by tooth. See Viewing and Reviewing Patient Clinical Notes . Review > Provider Notes by Date : Select this command to review a list of generic and default clinical notes associated with a provider by the date they were entered. See Reviewing Generic and Default Clinical Notes . Review > Provider Notes by Patient : Select this command to review a list of generic and default clinical notes associated with a provider by patient identification number. See Reviewing Generic and Default Clinical Notes . Notes By ADA Code : Select this command to review a list of generic and default clinical notes associated with an ADA code. See Reviewing Generic and Default Clinical Notes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Clean Up Old Files
topics.body: Using Clean Up Old Files eServices > Using Additional eServices Options Using Clean Up Old Files Use the Clean Up Old Files option t o conserve hard drive space by removing outdated reports. To delete report files that are over 60 days old: In the eServices window, select File > Clean Up Old Files . A message asks you to confirm the deletion of files that are over 60 days old. Click Yes . The old files are deleted, and the eServices window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Clinical History Tab - Handling Prescriptions
topics.body: Clinical History Tab - Handling Prescriptions Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container Handling Prescriptions The following information is displayed for each prescription: Prescribed date Drug description Provider Rx is displayed in the Type column of each prescription. Related Topics Viewing Prescriptions Using Prescription Options Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Client Area
topics.body: Using the Client Area Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Understanding the Enhanced Workspace Using the Client Area The client area of the Enhanced Workspace contains the active window. Different buttons and controls are displayed on the toolbar, depending on the window that is displayed or the operation that is performed. When a List window is displayed in the client area, the Find bar is displayed beneath the Toolbar . You can type criteria and search for a specific record or use the Find bar to specify the sort order of records. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Clocking In and Out
topics.body: Clocking In and Out Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card Clocking In and Out Clocking In To clock in: Select Clock In from the General Operations section of the Express bar. The SoftDent Clock In window is displayed. Type your user name and password, and click Clock In . OR If a fingerprint scanner is attached to the computer and you have a fingerprint in the database, scan your fingerprint. A window is displayed for verification. Review the information and click Clock In . Clocking Out To clock out: Select Clock Out from the General Operations section of the Express bar. The SoftDent Clock Out window is displayed. Type your user name and password, and click Clock Out. OR If a fingerprint scanner is attached to the computer and you have a fingerprint in the database, scan your fingerprint. A window is displayed for verification. Review the information and click Clock Out . Clocking Out for Lunch and Clocking In after Lunch If your practice uses the Track Lunch option (located in the Tracking Options section of the Time Card Preferences window), an Out for Lunch button and a Return From Lunch button are available on the SoftDent Clock Out and SoftDent Clock In windows, respectively. To clock out for lunch: Select Clock Out from the General Operations section of the Express bar. The SoftDent Clock Out window is displayed. Type your user name and password, and click Out for Lunch . To clock in after lunch: Select Clock In from the General Operations section of the Express bar. The SoftDent Clock In window is displayed. Type your user name and password, and click Return From Lunch . Related Topics Clocking In Without Clocking Out Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing Logins
topics.body: Changing Logins System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Changing Logins You can change logins without exiting the software. To login when another user is accessing the software on a workstation: Select System > Change Login or press Ctrl+F7 . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user login and password in the fields. Click OK . OR If a fingerprint scanner is attached to the computer and you have a fingerprint in the database, scan your fingerprint. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Clear Scheduler Block Utility
topics.body: Running the Clear Scheduler Block Utility Utilities > Running Customize Menu Utilities Running the Clear Scheduler Block Utility Run t his utility to remove blocked-out colors and times in scheduling windows. Use this utility when you have mistakenly copied blocked-out time slots for a future date. To clear scheduler blocked color or slot notes: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Customize > Clear Scheduler Block . The Clear Scheduler Blocks Utility window is displayed. Select the date range from which to remove the blocked out time. Deselect the Clear Blocked Slots or Clear Slot Notes options, if necessary. Click OK . After the process is complete, restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Color and Goal Scheduling
topics.body: Setting Up Color and Goal Scheduling Setting Up the Scheduler Setting Up Color and Goal Scheduling The following can be used separately or jointly to increase the efficiency and profitability of your work schedule: Color scheduling : Color scheduling enables you to color-code the Daily Appointments (Daybook) window by procedure or by production amount. Using color scheduling as a visual aid for your front desk staff helps your office expedite appointment handling. Goal scheduling : Goal scheduling helps you calculate production goals and track scheduled and actual production amounts in relation to your goals. Color scheduling is not the same as assigning colors to rooms. Room colors are for visual reference only and do not affect color or goal scheduling. Before setting up color or goal scheduling, decide how your office will schedule appointments by: Procedure. Production amount. Each approach requires a slightly different setup. Use color scheduling by procedure : To control when and where specific procedures are scheduled. Use color scheduling by production amount : To attain production goals by controlling the production dollars being scheduled. Use color scheduling by procedure and production amount : To control when and where specific procedures are scheduled while simultaneously controlling the production dollars being scheduled. Use goal scheduling : To track your scheduled or posted production amounts in relation to your production goals. For more information, see Goal Scheduling . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Clearing Items from the Office Expert
topics.body: Clearing Items from the Office Expert Using Experts > Using the Office Expert Clearing Items from the Office Expert To clear an item from the Office Expert window: Select the item Click the Clear Entry button. Selecting this button removes the item from the list, but does not delete it. To remove the item from the list of active office expert items, see Removing Office Expert Items . Need more information? topics.title: Clearing Patients from the InOffice Window
topics.body: Clearing Patients from the InOffice Window Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Clearing Patients from the InOffice Window You can remove a patient from the InOffice window if a patient: Cancels Does not show up for an appointment Has been checked out and you want to remove the information from the window To clear a patient from the InOffice window: Select the appointment. Select Remove from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Appointments are removed only from the InOffice window. They are still displayed in the Daily Appointments window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Clearing Recall Notices
topics.body: Clearing Recall Notices Recalling Patients Clearing Recall Notices To clear all recall notices: Select System > Change System Settings > Recall > Clear Recall Notices Sent . The Clear Recall Notices window is displayed. Select an option: To clear recall notices for your Normal Recall system : Select Last Exam . To clear recall notices for your User-Defined recall system : Select Ortho . If you have changed the name of your Normal Recall or User-Defined Recall system, select the new name of the report. For example, if the name of your User-Defined Recall system is Ortho, select Reports > Recall/Appt > Ortho . Select an option: To clear recall notices for all patients : Select All Patients . To clear recall notices for patients who have received more than a specified number of recall notices : Select Patients with more than _ notices and type the notice number in the field. To clear recall notices for patients who have received a specific number of recall notices : Select Patients with exactly _ notices and type the notice number in the field. Click OK . If you are using the Motivated Recall system, the Notices field on the Recall tab of the patient record is reset to zero after the patient is checked out. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding the Classic Workspace
topics.body: Understanding the Classic Workspace Getting Started > Using the Workspace Understanding the Classic Workspace If your monitor resolution is less than 1024 x 768, or if you prefer to have several software windows stacked on top of each other, use Classic Workspace. Classic Workspace: Windows: Several windows are open at the same time. Each window is stacked on top of the previously opened window; however, only the top window is active. Main Menu bar: Displayed beneath the Title bar and enables you to select commands that open various features of the software. For more information, see Using the Main Menu Bar . Common functions : Displayed beneath the toolbar. Power bar : Can be positioned to display beneath the Main Menu bar or anywhere within the workspace and enables access to various software features. For more information, see Using the Power Bar . Active windows : The name of the active window is displayed beneath the toolbar. The title of the active window is displayed at the top of the window. Drag and drop icons are displayed at the bottom of the window and enable you to access frequently used features and operations. Status bar: The user name of the person currently logged in, the system date and time, and the software operating mode are displayed at the bottom of the workspace in the Status bar. The Reminder Notes for Today text is displayed in the Status bar when there are reminder memos for the current date. Icons in the status bar indicate the presence of batched electronic statements, letters, reports, claims, statements, and memos for today. If the icons are not visible, floating over the empty space displays a message stating "No Batched Letters," "No Overdue Reports," and so on. Clicking on an icon displays the window associated with completing the particular task. The following icons can display: E: Electronic Statements�Click this icon to display the Electronic Services window ( Insurance > Electronic Services ). L: Batched Letters�Click this icon to display the Letter Expert Merge window ( Experts > Letter Expert > Merge All Batched Letters ). R: Overdue Reports�Click this icon to run overdue scheduled reports ( Reports > Report Manager > Run Scheduled Reports ). C: Batched Claims�Click this icon to display the Insurance Batch Run Setup window ( Insurance > Print Batched Claims ). S: Batched Statements�Click this icon to display the Statements in Batch window ( Reports > Accounting > Statements > Batch Count ). M: Reminder Notes for Today�Click this icon to display the Today's Memos window ( List > Today's Memos ). The number of windows that can be opened at one time using Classic Workspace is limited by your computer�s resources. To see the windows that are currently open: Click Window on the Menu bar. At the bottom of this menu is a list of all open windows. The active window is selected. You can change active windows by selecting another window from the drop-down list. To switch from the Classic Workspace to the Enhanced Workspace, see Setting Workspace Preferences . Related Topics Using Drag-and-Drop Icons Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Collection Summary Report
topics.body: Running the Collection Summary Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Collection Summary Report The Collection Summary report generates a list of collection amounts generated for the selected provider during a specific date range. To run the Collection Summary report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Collection Reports > Summary . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Collection Summary Report Setup window is displayed. Select a collection date range from the drop-down lists. In the Doctors section, click the drop-down arrow to select the ID numbers for the providers you want to include in the report, or leave 999 in the first field to include information for all providers. To include the details for each transaction in the report, select Include Detail Transactions . Click OK . Additional Collection Summary Report Information: The Collection Summary report contains the following fields: Date�Date of payment. Dr�Provider credited with payments. Cash�Dollar amount for cash payments posted (codes 1.00-1.99). Pers. Chk�Dollar amount for personal check payments posted (codes 3.00-10.99). Ins. Chk�Dollar amount for insurance check payments posted (codes 2.00-2.99). Credit Card�Dollar amount for credit card payments posted (codes 11.00-20.99). Subtotals�Totals for cash, personal payments, insurance payments, and credit card payments. Totals�Total of subtotals. Sample Report Related Topics End of Year Reports Related Job Aid: End of Year Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Compressing Contacts
topics.body: Compressing Contacts Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts Compressing Contacts After logging contacts for several years, you can: Free up disk space Clear contact notes that are no longer needed Compress the contact information by running the Contact Compression utility Need more information? topics.title: Setting Compression Defaults
topics.body: Setting Compression Defaults Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Setting Compression Defaults To save space on the hard drive, image files are compressed. The greater the compression, the lower the quality of the image. To set the default compression level: Select System > Change System Settings > Video Capture Options . The Video Options Properties window is displayed. Drag the slider to the appropriate compression level for documents and dental images in the Compression section. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Confirming Appointments
topics.body: Confirming Appointments Scheduling Appointments Confirming Appointments To confirm a patient appointment: Select an option: In the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance window : Select the appointment to confirm and select Confirm Appt from the Options section of the Explorer bar or the Options menu. I n the Family Appointments window : Select the appointment to confirm and click Confirm . The Confirm Appt for window is displayed. A list of the patient�s telephone numbers is displayed in the window.
