If the patient has a preferred method of contact set in the Patient Information window, that method is highlighted in bold italics . If your system is configured for auto-dialing, select the phone number to call, and click Dial . If your system is not configured for auto-dialing, the numbers are for your reference. Select APPOINTMENT CONFIRMED . Type a confirmation note in the Note field. Click Close . If Ask to log contact after confirming appointment checkbox is selected in the Scheduler Settings window, a message that asks you whether to log a contact is displayed. For more information, see Adding Contacts . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring eForms E-Mail Settings
topics.body: Configuring eForms E-Mail Settings eServices > Using the eForms Module Configuring eForms E-Mail Settings Use the eForms E-mail Settings window to create the e-mail alert patients receive when they have an eForm that must be verified or completed. You can also use this window to enter an e-mail address for your office where you can receive a summary e-mail when eForms are sent to the Carestream website. To configure e-mail settings for eForms: Select System > Modules > eForms > E-mail Settings . The eForms E-mail Settings window is displayed. For additional information, see the eForms online help. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring Patient eForms Settings
topics.body: Configuring Patient eForms Settings eServices > Using the eForms Module Configuring Patient eForms Settings Use the Patient eForms Settings window to enable or disable eForms delivery for a patient or responsible party. To configure eForms settings for a patient: Select an option: In the Patient List or Patient window: Select e Forms > Patient Settings from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Daily Appointments or Book Quick Glance window: Right-click on an appointment slot and select e Forms > Patient Settings from the drop-down menu. The Patient eForms Settings window is displayed. For additional information, see the eForms online help. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring the eForms Module
topics.body: Configuring the eForms Module eServices > Using the eForms Module Configuring the eForms Module To configure the eForms module: Select System > Modules > eForms > Configure eForms . The eForms Configuration window is displayed. For additional information, see the eForms online help. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring KIOSK Check-In Module Settings
topics.body: Configuring KIOSK Check-In Module Settings Using the KIOSK Check-In Module Configuring KIOSK Check-In Module Settings You can customize the appearance of the KIOSK Welcome window, the messages that are displayed, and the way the patient interacts with the KIOSK. Customize the following items for your KIOSK: Welcome Window: Greet patients with your logo or custom image and a welcome message. KIOSK Messages: Create custom messages, telling patients what they should do after a successful or unsuccessful check-in. Daily Messages: Create date-specific messages that can change from day to day, such as upcoming holidays, and so on. Related Topics Customizing the Welcome Window Defining KIOSK Messages Defining Daily Messages Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Confirmation List Report
topics.body: Running the Confirmation List Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports Running the Confirmation List Report The Confirmation List report lists the confirmation status of patient appointments for a specified date. This report includes the patient's appointment time, ID, name, chart number, phone numbers, e-mail address, age, appointment provider and hygienist IDs, account balance, and the appointment reason. It also displays the date the appointment was made, the fee, the patient's portion, the estimated insurance payment, and any medical alerts. You can run the report by date and time or by patient name. Additional Confirmation List Report Information: The Dr ID from the appointment card is always displayed in the Dr/Hyg column in the first row of the appointment information. The hygienist ID from the appointment card is displayed in the Dr/Hyg column in the second row of the appointment information. Keep the following in mind: Any non-zero hygienist ID is displayed, even if there are no H's in the template. If the hygienist ID from the appointment card is zero and there are H's in the template, two asterisks are displayed instead of zero. If the hygienist ID from the appointment card is zero and there are no H's in the template, nothing is displayed. This scenario includes non-hygiene appointments as well as hygiene appointments where the office does not use templates. Sample Report -- by Date and Time Sample Report -- by Patient Name To run the Confirmation List report: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Confirmation List > By Date and Time or By Patient Name . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Confirmation List Report Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, do the following: Click the drop-down arrows to select the Start and Stop dates from a calendar. To have the report display appointments made a specified number of days before the current date, type the number in the field, or leave as 0. In the Patient User Codes section, type the user codes you want to include or exclude from the report. In the Appointments section, deselect a checkbox to limit the types of appointments included in the report. If you selected the Merge Letter Expert output option, the Letter Expert Template ID field is active. Click the drop-down arrow to select a template from the list. In the Dentist IDs section, click the drop-down arrows in the Start and Stop fields to select a range of doctors, or leave 999 to report on all doctors. In the Options section, deselect a checkbox if you want to include patients with no address or if you do not want to include inactive patients. In the Code Ranges section, click the drop-down arrows to select up to five code ranges on which to report. To specify a single code, type the code in the left column and leave the right column 0. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring System Phone Preferences
topics.body: Configuring System Phone Preferences General System Settings Configuring System Phone Preferences The System Phone Preferences window enables you to specify how the software uses phone numbers. To configure system phone preferences: Select System > Change System Settings > General System . The General System Settings window is displayed. Click Phone Preferences . The System Phone Preferences window is displayed. Select from the following options: Use 10-digit Phone Numbers: Select this option to always include area codes with phone numbers. Default new Patient and Account area codes to Practice area code: Select this option to have the software enter the area code of the practice in phone number fields for new patients and accounts. Click OK . Related Topics General System Settings Configuring System Printing Preferences Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Contacts and Memos
topics.body: Contacts and Memos Contacts and Memos You can: Set up contacts and memos Manage contacts and memos Related Topics Using Contacts Using Memos Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Counting Forms Not Printed
topics.body: Counting Forms Not Printed Submitting and Tracking Claims > Printing Claims Counting Forms Not Printed To see how many insurance forms are pending to be printed: Select Insurance > Count of Forms not Printed . The Batch File Form Summary window is displayed. For each of the 10 forms specified for use in your office, a number is displayed in the Count column to show how many of these forms are pending. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Contractual Plan Analysis Report
topics.body: Running the Contractual Plan Analysis Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Running the Contractual Plan Analysis Report This report lists all productions posted using a DMO/capitation fee schedule and compares their charges to the total of the same production for fee schedule 0. To run the Contractual Plan Analysis report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Contractual Plan Analysis . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Contractual Plan Analysis Setup window is displayed. In the Date Range section, click the drop-down arrows to select the dates from a calendar. In the Doctors section, use the drop-down arrows to select specific providers, or leave 999 to report on all providers. In the IDs section, use the drop-down arrows to select a specific insurance company or plan, or leave as 0 for all companies and plans. To include transaction details, click the Include Transaction Details checkbox. If you do not want to include plan details in the report, deselect Include Plan Details . Click OK . Additional Contractual Plan Analysis Report Information: The Contractual Plan Analysis report contains the following sections: Production Production�Total of insurable codes (100.00+) posted by using the contractual fee. Charges�Total of all charges posted for the specified period (codes 61.00-89.99). Adj to Prod�Total adjustments to production posted for the specified period (codes 40.00-59.99). Excludes reserved budget plan codes 48.00 and 48.90. Prod total if charged at Fee Schedule 0�Total dollar amount of transactions if they had been posted using fee schedule 0. Per Visit Charges�Total per visit charges posted during the specified period (code 40.90). Insurable Production�Total of all transactions posted that are insurable. Uninsured Production�Total of all transactions posted that are not insurable. Collections Personal Payments�Total payments posted (excluding insurance payments) for the specified period (codes 1.00-20.99). Insurance Payments�Total insurance payments posted for the specified period (codes 2.00-2.89; includes codes 11.93, 12.93, 13.93, and 14.93). Total Income Generated�Total Income Generated = Personal Payments + Insurance Payments Plan Profitability Index All Production Posted/All Production at Fee 0�Production (insured and uninsured) charged at contractual fee divided by Production total if charged at fee schedule 0. This percentage shows how much money you are collecting on the dollar for the plan. Example: If the percentage is 96%, you are collecting 96 cents on the dollar for this particular plan. If it is over 100%, it means your contractual plans are more profitable than plans using the fee schedule 0 amount. Insurance Payments/Insured Transactions at Fee 0�This figure is used to determine if the plan is profitable, based on the percentage collected from the plan compared to the insurable production if charged using fee schedule 0. This percentage tells you how much money you are collecting on the dollar for insured procedures on this plan. All Payments/All Production at Fee 0�This figure is used to determine if the plan is profitable, based on the percentage collected from the plan and patients compared to the insured and uninsured production using fee schedule 0. The figure is calculated using Total Income Generated divided by Production Total if charged at Fee Schedule 0. Sample Report N

topics.title: Running the Contractual Plan Production Report
topics.body: Running the Contractual Plan Production Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Running the Contractual Plan Production Report The Contractual Plan Production report lists all procedures posted using a DMO fee schedule. The quantity performed and the monies generated by the production are included in the report. To run the Contractual Plan Production report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Contractual Plan Production . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Contractual Plan Production by ADA Codes Report window is displayed. In the Date Range section, click the drop-down arrows to select the dates from a calendar. In the Plan ID section, click the drop-down arrow to select an insurance plan. In the Doctors section, use the drop-down arrows to select specific providers, or leave 999 to report on all providers. Click OK . Additional Contractual Plan Production Information: This report displays the code number, description, the dollar amount if charged at fee schedule 0, the contractual fee (including co-payment), the number of times the code was posted, and the practice dollar total at the contractual fee for that code. This report will not run unless you specify a valid plan ID. If fee schedule 0 or the plan�s fee in the bluebook is updated, the dollar amount charged at fee schedule 0 reflects the updated fee. The report contains the following information: Code, Description�Transaction code, including extension, and its description. Fee 0�Current fee for transaction code at fee schedule 0. Cap Fee�Current plan fee for transaction code for insurance plan�s bluebook. Quantity�Total number of transactions posted. Amnt $�Actual dollar amount of insurable transactions posted. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Contractual Plan Production by Company Report
topics.body: Running the Contractual Plan Production by Company Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Running the Contractual Plan Production by Company Report The Contractual Plan Production by Company Report lists all procedures posted using a DMO fee schedule. It displays the quantity performed and the amount of money generated by their production. To run the Contractual Plan Production by Company report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Contractual Plan Production By Company . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Contractual Ins. Co. Production by ADA Codes Report window is displayed. In the Date Range section, click the drop-down arrows to select the dates from a calendar. In the Ins. Co. ID section, click the drop-down arrow to select an insurance company. In the Doctors section, use the drop-down arrows to select specific providers, or leave 999 to report on all providers. Click OK . Additional Contractual Plan Production by Company Report Information: This report displays the code number, description, the dollar amount if charged at fee schedule 0, the contractual fee (including co-payment), the number of times the code was posted, and the practice dollar total at the contractual fee for that code. This report will not run unless you specify a valid Ins. Co. ID. If fee schedule 0 or the plan�s fee in the bluebook is updated, the dollar amount charged at fee schedule 0 reflects the updated fee. The report contains the following information: Code, Description�Transaction code, including extension, and its description. Fee 0�Current fee for transaction code at fee schedule 0. Cap Fee�Current plan fee for transaction code for insurance plan�s bluebook. Quantity�Total number of transactions posted. Amnt $�Actual dollar amount of insurable transactions posted. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Converting Documents to Clinical Images or Portraits
topics.body: Converting Documents to Clinical Images or Portraits Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Converting Documents to Clinical Images or Portraits You can create an intraoral, STV, 3rd party x-ray, or portrait image copy of a patient document. Converted clinical images are displayed beneath the Clinical Images section of the tree. To convert a document to a clinical image: In the Documents for patient window, select the document from the Documents section of the tree, right-click the document in the preview pane, and select Convert to Carestream Imaging format . The Select Clinical Image Type window is displayed. Select the image type from the drop-down list, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Converting Images to the CS Imaging Software Format
topics.body: Converting Images to the CS Imaging Software Format Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Images Converting Images to the CS Imaging Software Format To convert a non-CS-Imaging software image to the CS Imaging software format: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tabs in the Chart for window, click the Clinical Images tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the image, right-click, and select Convert to Clinical Image . The Select Clinical Image Type window is displayed. Select the image type from the drop-down list. Click OK . The original image is still available. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Collection Adjustments
topics.body: Posting Collection Adjustments Posting Payments > Posting Account Payments > Posting Adjustments Posting Collection Adjustments Post a collection adjustment when the collection amount is changed. Post collection adjustments, credit balances, returned checks and fees, and collection debit adjustments from the Payments � Collections Adjustments window. Example : You post a $300 payment to Patrick Patient�s account instead of to Patricia Patient�s account. To correct the mistake, post a collection debit adjustment (codes 30.00 to 39.89) of $300 to Patrick�s account and post a payment code (1.00 to 20.89) to Patricia�s account. To post a collection adjustment to an account or patient record: Select an option: In the Account List window : Select the account and select Payments . In the Account window : Select Payments . In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Transactions . The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments . In the Patient window : Select Transactions . The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Select Collection Adj. in the Select Operation section. The Payments � Collections Adjustments window is displayed. Select a payment adjustment code (codes 21.00 � 39.89) from the Code drop-down list. The total for transactions displayed in the window is displayed in the Amount field. Type the adjustment amount in the Amount column for each transaction. To add a note to a transaction, select the Notes column and type the information. Click Post . Click Close . Tip When posting a returned check, the balance and provider collections are increased. Select codes 61.00-91.89 to post a returned check charge. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Color Mode
topics.body: Using Color Mode Using the Scheduler > Understanding Book At A Glance Window Options Using Color Mode When the Book At A Glance window is displayed in color mode, the following is displayed in the title bar: Name of the book Day Date Slot that is selected Displayed book (practice or provider) When you use color scheduling: Open time slots in each column are displayed in the scheduling colors just as in the Daily Appointments window. Appointments are indicated by a yellow background and a border color that you select. All provider assigned time slots are cross-hatched. Blocked slots are displayed in the same color as in the Daily Appointments window. Slot notes are displayed with a border color that you select. To view information in the Book At A Glance window in color mode, click View Colors at the top of the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Color Scheduling
topics.body: Setting Up Color Scheduling Setting Up the Scheduler Setting Up Color Scheduling Using Color Scheduling: You can "paint" your Daily Appointments (Daybook) window with colors that represent a specific range of procedure codes or production amount targets Is an excellent visual tool that enables you to efficiently schedule appointments Does not damage or delete existing appointments, or prevent you from scheduling any procedure A warning is displayed if you attempt to schedule an appointment outside your scheduler defaults and settings. Color scheduling can be used only with the following codes: ADA CPT You cannot color-code payment codes, adjustment codes, explosion codes, and so on. You can, however, assign a color to an individual code that is part of an explosion code. Color groups defined by code and production amount take precedence over groups defined just by code or just by production amount. For example, if yellow is assigned to a combination group consisting of procedures in the code range 100�1999 and in the dollar range $1�$100 and blue is assigned to all procedures in the dollar range $50�$150, procedures that fall in both groups are color-coded yellow. Related Topics Adding Scheduling Color Group Definitions Editing Color Group Definitions Deleting a Color Group Definition Changing the Color Group Definition of Individual Procedures Resetting the Color Group Definition for an Individual Procedure to the Daybook Color Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Common Commands
topics.body: Using Common Commands Getting Started Using Common Commands Common command buttons are displayed in most windows. The Common command buttons are: Cancel : Cancels the current operation. Any changes made are not saved, and any options selected are not enabled. Close : Exits the active window. Enter : Saves information while in Add or Edit mode and displays the highlighted record in a list window. OK : In a tabbed window, saves any changes or new information. In a message, proceeds with the operation or accepts the selected options. In windows that do not have an edit command, closes the windows. Esc : Cancels the operation and closes the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Common Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Common Toolbar Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using Toolbars Using the Common Toolbar The Common toolbar contains buttons that enable you to access commonly used functions from the Restorative, Exams, Clinical Images, and Treatment Planning tabs. For information on adding a button to the common toolbar, see Customizing Toolbars . Click the icon for the description of the Common toolbar buttons. Click this button to display the Patient Appointment Card window In this window, you can edit and post procedures to be performed on the current date. If the patient does not have an appointment for the current date, a message is displayed. Click this button to display the Patient Education Setup window. In this window, you can run a patient education program from the charting module. The first time you run the program, you must enter the drive, directory, and path in which the program is located. Click this button to access the Vipersoft software program. Click this button to launch your x-ray capture program. Click this button to display the Patient Appointments window. In this window, you can schedule a patient appointment. Click this button to display the Daily Appointments window. Click this button to add notes for procedures posted on the current day. This command is enabled if the Add Clinical Notes at End of Day option is selected in the Chart Properties window. See Adding Clinical Notes at the End of the Day . Click this button to display the SoftDent E-Mail window. See E-mailing Charts . Click this button to display the Documents List window. Click this button to display the patient's Medical History window. In this window, you can edit and add information to the patient's medical history. Click this button to display the Prescriptions for List window. In this window, you can view, refill, and add prescriptions for a patient. Click this button to display the Patient window. The personal, medical history, insurance, account balance, and patient balance information is displayed in this window. Click this button to display the Lab Case List window. A list of all lab cases for the patient and the status of each is displayed in this window. Click this button to display the Patient Portrait window. In this window, you can capture and import a portrait to associate with a patient's chart. Click this button to display the Electronic Router window. In this window, you can schedule a follow-up appointment. Click this button to display the Letter Merge window. In this window, you can merge patient information with a letter. Related Topics Customizing Toolbars Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Clinical Condition Overrides
topics.body: Setting Clinical Condition Overrides Restorative Charting > Using Clinical Conditions > Setting Clinical Condition Security and Overrides Setting Clinical Condition Overrides To set a login and password override to enable an employee to access the database: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Security Overrides > Clinical Settings . The System Settings - Override Settings window is displayed. Click the Clinical 2 tab. Select Add Clinical Conditions and do one of the following: To enable a user to access the information after entering a password: select Enter Password from the drop-down list and type a password. To enable a user to access the information after entering another user�s name and password: select User Login . Click OK and click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accessing the Contact Expert
topics.body: Accessing the Contact Expert Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert Accessing the Contact Expert You can access the contact expert from the following windows: Office Expert Report Output Pending Contacts Lists Related Topics Accessing the Contact Expert from the Office Expert Accessing the Contact Expert from a Report Accessing the Contact Expert from a Contact List Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing the Confirmation Phone Directory
topics.body: Printing the Confirmation Phone Directory Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports Printing the Confirmation Phone Directory You can print a phone directory of patients who need their appointments confirmed. The directory lists the patient's name, telephone numbers, the next appointment date, and user codes. Sample Report To generate a confirmation phone directory: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Labels/Postcards/Dir > Confirmation Phone Dir . The Select Dates window is displayed. Click the drop-down arrows to select the Start and Stop dates from a calendar. Click OK . A message that informs you of the number of patients added to the directory is displayed. Click OK . A message that asks you to print or delete the patient directory is displayed. Select Print and click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring CareCredit Providers from the System > CareCredit Option
topics.body: Configuring CareCredit Providers from the System > CareCredit Option eServices > Using CareCredit Configuring CareCredit Providers from the System > CareCredit Option You can configure CareCredit providers from the System > CareCredit menu option, using the Provider -- Configure the Provider for CareCredit Service list window. This provider window is specific to CareCredit and indicates which providers are CareCredit providers, as well as their CareCredit merchant numbers and practice codes. To configure CareCredit providers: Select System > CareCredit > Configure Providers . The Provider -- Configure the Provider for CareCredit Service list window is displayed. Double-click on a provider name. The Provider -- Configure the Provider for CareCredit Service window is displayed. Type the CareCredit merchant number and practice code in the fields. If you are a new CareCredit client or if you do not know the merchant number, it can be found on the enrollment materials or your monthly statements. Contact CareCredit Provider Services to obtain the practice code. Click Add Service . A success message is displayed, and "Service Added" is displayed on the window. Click OK . Related Topics Setting Up CareCredit CareCredit Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring CareCredit Providers in the Provider List Window
topics.body: Configuring CareCredit Providers in the Provider List Window eServices > Using CareCredit Configuring CareCredit Providers in the Provider List Window You can configure CareCredit providers in the Provider List window. To configure CareCredit providers: Select List > Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. Select a provider from the list, and click Add CareCredit Service . The Provider -- Configure the Provider for CareCredit Service window is displayed. Type the CareCredit merchant number and practice code in the fields. If you are a new CareCredit client or if you do not know the merchant number, it can be found on the enrollment materials or your monthly statements. Contact CareCredit Provider Services to obtain the practice code. Click Add Service . A success message is displayed, and "Service Added" is displayed on the window. Click OK . Related Topics Setting Up CareCredit CareCredit Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring Patient eReminders Settings
topics.body: Configuring Patient eReminders Settings eServices > Using the eReminders Service Configuring Patient eReminders Settings To configure patient eReminders settings, follow these steps: Select an option: In the Patient List window: Select a patient and click eReminders Settings in the Options section of the Express Bar. In the Patient window: Select eReminders Settings in the Options section of the Express Bar. In the Patient Appointments window: Select eReminders Settings in the Options section of the Express Bar. In the Daybook: Select a patient and select eReminders Settings in the Options section of the Express Bar, or right-click on the appointment and select eReminders Settings from the drop-down menu. In the Book Quick Glance view: Select an appointment and select eReminders Settings in the Options section of the Express Bar, or right-click on the appointment and select eReminders Settings from the drop-down menu. The eReminders Patient Settings window is displayed. Select an option: If the patient has a responsible party: The Use Responsible Party option is selected by default. Click Responsible Party Settings to display the eReminders Responsible Party Settings window, and select the permitted reminder delivery methods for the responsible party. Click OK . If there is no responsible party: Select the permitted reminder delivery methods for the patient, and click OK . Reminder settings made in this window override the settings your office uses. Example: If your practice is using a frequency template that specifies the second reminder is to be delivered via phone and the patient has disallowed phone reminders, the patient will not receive a second reminder but will receive all other reminders of the allowed delivery types. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring Responsible Party eReminders Settings
topics.body: Configuring Responsible Party eReminders Settings eServices > Using the eReminders Service Configuring Responsible Party eReminders Settings To configure responsible party eReminders settings, follow these steps: Select an option: In the Account List window: Select an account and click eReminders Settings in the Options section of the Express Bar. In the Account window: Select eReminders Settings in the Options section of the Express Bar. In any patient window: Click eReminders Settings in the Options section of the Express Bar. If the patient has a responsible party, the Use Responsible Party option is selected by default. Click Responsible Party Settings . The eReminders Responsible Party Settings window is displayed. Select the permitted reminder delivery methods for the responsible party, and click OK . Reminder settings made in this window override the settings your office uses. Example: If your practice is using a frequency template that specifies the second reminder is to be delivered via phone and the responsible party has disallowed phone reminders, the responsible party will not receive a second reminder but will receive all other reminders of the allowed delivery types. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring Settings for the ePrescriptions Module
topics.body: Configuring Settings for the ePrescriptions Module eServices > Using the ePrescriptions Module Configuring Settings for the ePrescriptions Module Before you can fully access ePrescriptions features, you must configure your settings from the Modules menu. The ePrescriptions menu options are only enabled when the ePrescriptions module has been licensed and enabled. User Login To set up the user login: Select System > Modules > ePrescriptions > Configure eRx Login . The ePrescribing Settings window is displayed. On the User Account tab, type the user ID and password given to you by eServices, and click OK . Drug Utilization Review (DUR) Preferences To set up DUR preferences: Select System > Modules > ePrescriptions > DUR Preferences . The ePrescribing DUR Preferences window is displayed. Use the features on this window to set the drug review warning preferences for the appropriate facility or doctors in the list. Pharmacy Preferences To set up pharmacy preferences: Select System > Modules > ePrescriptions > Pharmacy Preferences . The ePrescribing Pharmacy Preference List window is displayed. Use the features on this window to add pharmacies to the list for your facility. Prescription Preferences To set up the prescription preferences: Select System > Modules > ePrescriptions > Prescription Preferences . The ePrescribing Rx Preference List Management window is displayed. Use the features on this window to add prescription drugs and physician comments for your facility. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring the SUS
topics.body: Configuring the SUS Getting Started > Using the Software Update Service (SUS) Configuring the SUS The SUS enables you to receive notifications when an update is available. You will need to configure the service only once, and it can be done on any machine, including the server. Tip: Have your account ID/Customer ID number handy, as you will be asked for this information during the configuration process. If you don't know your account ID/CustomerID number, before you start the configuration process, launch SoftDent (on another machine, if necessary). From the Help menu, click About SoftDent . Click Show License Details . Locate the CustomerID number. To configure the SUS: Do one of the following: Select Start > All Programs > Carestream > Software Update Service > Configure Software Update Service . Right-click the triangle logo and select Configure Update Service . After installing the latest version of SoftDent, double-click the SoftDent icon on your desktop. ( Note: If you have already set up the update service, the SoftDent Login window is displayed.) The Software Update Service � Start Using the Software Update Service (SUS) window is displayed. If you have a Carestream single-sign-on (SSO) account, type your email address and password for your single-sign-on account, and click OK . The Software Update Service � Account Details Accepted window is displayed. Tip: Not sure if you have a single-sign-on account? � You might have created an SSO account for the Customer Portal. � A Carestream Dental Institute representative might have created an SSO account for you, in which case you would use your email address and the password mwonline or carestream (unless you changed your password). � Your email address is always the login for your SSO account. If your single-sign-on-account email address has not been verified, you will receive an email and be instructed to click a link to verify the email address and activate your account. If you do not know your password and decide to reset it, the password will also be changed for other services accessed with that SSO. Click OK . OR If you do not have a single-sign-on account : Select I need to create a Single-Sign-On account and click OK . The Software Update Service � Create Carestream Single-Sign-On Account message is displayed. Read the message and click OK . The Single-Sign-On web page is displayed. In the Register section of the web page, type your email address and a password, and re-enter the information to confirm it. Click Register . The Account Information window is displayed. Select your region from the drop-down list, and type your account ID (CustomerID) and zip code and click OK . A message is displayed, stating that an email has been sent to you, and you must click a link in the email to activate your account. Tip: To find your account ID/CustomerID number, launch SoftDent. From the Help menu, click About SoftDent . Click Show License Details . Locate the CustomerID number. After you have clicked the link on the email verification page, close your web browser and return to the Software Update Service � Start Using the Software Update Service (SUS) window. Type your email address and password, and click OK . The Software Update Service � Account Details Accepted window is displayed. Click OK . Related Topics Authenticating an Existing SSO Account and Configuring the SUS Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accessing the Contact Expert from a Contact List
topics.body: Accessing the Contact Expert from a Contact List Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Accessing the Contact Expert Accessing the Contact Expert from a Contact List You can access the contact expert from existing contact lists. These lists are generated by reports. To access the contact expert from a contact list: Select Contact Expert from the Experts menu. The Pending Contact Lists window is displayed. Select an option: Double-click a list. Select a list and select Load this List from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Select a list and type T. The Contact Expert window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Practice Management Reports
topics.body: Practice Management Reports What's New in Version 14.0 > Reports Practice Management Reports Practice Management reports have received enhancements in the following areas: Claim Contact Type Added to Contact Reports A Claim contact type has been added to the Expected Response, Contacts Made, and Contacts Pending report setup windows. If you select Claim in the Type of Contact drop-down list, contacts entered for open and completed claims are displayed on each report. The report includes the following information: The ID column on the report displays the claim ID. The Name & Address column displays the name on the claim and an identification of the claim type ( Claim , for open claims, and Completed Claim ), the plan name, the contact for the plan, and contact information. The Phone column displays the plan's phone number. The Work or Other column displays the plan's eligibility phone number. The Cell or FAX column displays the plan's fax number. See Running Contact Reports for more information. Write-Off Column Added to the Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown Report A Write Off column has been added to the Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown report for Percentage PPO, Flat Fee PPO, Flat Fee Medicare, and Percentage Medicare plans. In previous versions, the write off total was included in the Insurance column. See Running the Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown Report for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Converting CS Imaging Software Images to JPG Format
topics.body: Converting CS Imaging Software Images to JPG Format Using E-mail and WebLink > Sending an E-mail > E-mailing CS Imaging Software Images Converting CS Imaging Software Images to JPG Format Before sending an e-mail, you can attach a CS Imaging software image and convert it to the JPG format. Recipients do not require the CS Imaging viewer to display the attached JPG image. Tip You cannot attach the CS Imaging software image viewer to a .jpg image. To convert an attached image to the JPG format: In the patient record, select Documents . The Documents for window is displayed. Patient documents, images, and charts are displayed in the window. Expand the clinical images, select the image to e-mail, and select E-Mail . The SoftDent E-Mail window is displayed. The image is displayed in the Attached Items section of the window, and the patient name and an annotation is displayed in the Message section of the window. Tip To carbon copy or blind carbon copy a recipient, type an e-mail in the CC or BCC field. To send the e-mail to different recipient, select the addressee from the Recipient drop-down list. The recipient�s name and e-mail address are displayed. Tip To attach the Microsoft PowerPoint software viewer, select Attach PowerPoint Viewer. Type the e-mail subject and message. Select Convert Images to JPG Format . To embed annotations on the converted image, select Embed Annotations on JPG Image . Click OK (Send) . Recipients cannot remove embedded annotations. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Completed Claim Documents
topics.body: Viewing Completed Claim Documents Using the Document Manager > Viewing Documents Viewing Completed Claim Documents To view documents associated with a completed insurance claim, select an option: In the Completed Claim List window : Select the claim and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Completed Claim window : Select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Documents for window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Detail Window
topics.body: Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Detail Window eServices > Using the ePostings Service Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Detail Window The Completed ePostings Payment Detail window enables you to view the completed and matched claims belonging to the ePostings payment you selected in the Completed ePostings Payment window. The left half of the list at the bottom of the window contains information about a specific SoftDent transaction. The right half of the list contains information about an ePostings line item. Since these claims have already been matched and the payment has been posted, all distribution discrepancies are resolved. To view the detail of completed ePostings payments: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > ePostings > Completed ePostings Payments . The Completed ePostings Payment window is displayed. Double-click a payment. The Completed ePostings Payment Detail window is displayed. Use the Sort By options at the upper right of the window to sort claims by date of service, claim ID, or patient. Select a claim, and use the following options from the Express bar to view additional information about the claims: View Associated Claim: Select this option to open the Claim window and view the claim information. View Original ePostings Data: Select this option to display the ePostings payment data for the selected claim as it was received from the clearinghouse. View/Add Notes: Select this option to display the ePostings - View/Add Notes window, where you can view existing notes or add notes associated with the selected ePostings payment. Review: Select this option to display the Document Review window for the selected ePostings payment. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Window
topics.body: Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Window eServices > Using the ePostings Service Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Window The Completed ePostings Payment window displays the posted ePostings payment information. To view completed ePostings payments: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > ePostings > Completed ePostings Payments . The Completed ePostings Payment window is displayed. Use the Sort By options at the upper right of the window to sort the completed payments by posted date, check/routing number, payment type, date received, and insurance company. The search parameters used in the Find field vary, depending on the option you select. Select a completed payment from the list. Information about the payment is displayed directly below the list. The following options are available in the Options section of the Express bar: View Details: Select this option to open the Completed ePostings Payment Detail window, displaying a list of all claims associated with the completed ePostings payment you have selected. View Original ePostings Data: Select this option to display the ePostings payment data as it was received from the clearinghouse. View/Add Notes: Select this option to display the ePostings - View/Add Notes window, where you can view existing notes or add notes associated with the selected ePostings payment. Review: Select this option to display the Document Review window for the selected ePostings payment. Double-click a completed payment to open the Completed ePostings Payment Detail window. See Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Detail Window for more information. Click Close . Related Topics Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Detail Window Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Capital Letter Format Utility
topics.body: Running the Capital Letter Format Utility Utilities > Running Customize Menu Utilities Running the Capital Letter Format Utility Run t his utility : to dictate which entries are capitalized. You can opt to capitalize the first letter or capitalize the whole word. To set the capitalization options: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Customize > Capital Letter Format . The Capital Letters Format Utility window is displayed. Select the option and click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Completing Treatment Plan Work
topics.body: Completing Treatment Plan Work Charting Treatment Plans > Handling Treatment Plan Items Completing Treatment Plan Work If a patient accepts the treatment plan and the work is performed, you can post the treatment plan procedures as actual work from the charting module. To post completed treatment plan work, select an option: To complete primary treatment plan items on the Restorative tab in the Charting for window : Select Mode > Complete Primary Treatment Plan Items . The Treatment Plan Items window is displayed. Select the items, and click OK . A message asks you to post all items for the treatment group. Click the appropriate button, and click OK . To complete secondary treatment plan items on the Restorative tab in the Charting for window : Select Mode > Complete Secondary Treatment Plan Items . A message asks you to post all items for the treatment group. Click Yes . To complete tertiary treatment plan items on the Restorative tab in the Charting for window: Select Mode > Complete Tertiary Treatment Plan Items . A message asks you to post all items for the treatment group. Click Yes . To complete primary treatment plan items on the Treatment Planning tab in the Charting for window : Select Complete primary treatment plan items on the Treatment Plan toolbar . A message asks you to post all items for the treatment group. Click Yes . To complete secondary treatment plan items on the Treatment Planning tab in the Charting for window : Select Complete secondary treatment plan items on the Treatment Plan toolbar . A message asks you to post all items for the treatment group. Click Yes . To complete tertiary treatment plan items on the Treatment Planning tab in the Charting for window: Select Complete tertiary treatment plan items on the Treatment Plan toolbar . A message asks you to post all items for the treatment group. Click Yes . To complete a specific treatment plan item on the Treatment Planning tab in the Charting for window : Select the item, and select Complete selected treatment plan items on the Treatment Plan toolbar . A message asks you to post the item. Click Yes . To complete a specific treatment plan item in the Clinical Detail Tab Container : Select the treatment plan item, right-click, and select Mark As Completed . A message asks you to post the item. Click Yes . If Add Clinical Notes While Posting is selected in the Chart Options window, the Add Clinical Notes window is displayed. Add the notes, and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding the CPT Code Format
topics.body: Understanding the CPT Code Format Handling Transactions and Codes > Understanding Transaction and Procedure Code Ranges Understanding the CPT Code Format The new CPT code format uses: Old ADA code number Prefix consisting of the letter D Enough zeroes to make five digits on claims Example : Standard ADA code 110 becomes D0110 on the claim. Within the software, the prefix is ignored and the ADA code is displayed. You can customize individual insurance plans to print the CPT format on a specific claim or on claims for a specific insurance company, or customize all claims to use this format on forms. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Capture Preferences
topics.body: Setting Capture Preferences Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Setting Capture Preferences You can set the capture preferences for claim, patient, lab case, portrait, referring doctor, account, and clinical image documents. To set the capture preferences for a document type: In the Documents for window, select Capture Preferences from the Options section of the Express bar. The Preferences for window is displayed. Select the document type from the Type drop-down list. Type the default page name for all documents of the same type in the Default description for the new documents field. Type the default tool tip label for all documents of the same type in the Default Description for new Images field. To save each document automatically, select AutoSave each acquisition . To enter information for each document automatically when e-mailing from the document manager, select Initialize message text with page annotations when E-Mailing Documents . To display the Enter Name Information window each time you save a document, select Prompt for document name when adding new documents . If this checkbox is selected, you can type a document name for each document added from the document manager. To display the Enter Name Information window each time you save a document, select Prompt for page label when scanning a new page . If this checkbox is selected, you can type a page label for each document added from the document manager. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Copying an Allowed Amount from Plan to Plan
topics.body: Copying an Allowed Amount from Plan to Plan Handling Transactions and Codes > Using Fee Schedules Copying an Allowed Amount from Plan to Plan To copy the allowed amount column from one plan to another: Select System > Fees Maintenance > Copy Allowed Amount from Plan to Plan . The Copy Allowed Amount from Plan to Plan window is displayed. In the From Plan field, click the drop-down arrow to display the Insurance Plan window, and double-click the plan from which you want to copy the allowed amounts. In the To section, select All Plans belonging to this insurance company or select One Plan and click the drop-down arrow to select the plan from the list. Deselect the Add Non-existing Bluebook Entries option if you do not want new bluebook entries to be created based on the copied bluebook. Click OK . Related Topics Using Fee Schedules Co pying a Fee Schedule Changing a Fee Schedule Updating a Fee Schedule Updating Bluebook Fees Copying a Bluebook Between Plans Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Copying a Bluebook Between Plans
topics.body: Copying a Bluebook Between Plans Handling Transactions and Codes > Using Fee Schedules Copying a Bluebook Between Plans To copy a bluebook between plans: Select System > Fees Maintenance > Copy Bluebook Between Plans . The Copy Bluebook Between Plans window is displayed. In the From Plan field, click the drop-down arrow to display the Insurance Plan window, and double-click the plan from which you want to copy the bluebook. In the To Plan field, click the drop-down arrow to display the Insurance Plan window, and double-click the plan to which you want to copy the bluebook. In the Bluebook Entries section, deselect codes you do not want to copy, if necessary. Select Add New Bluebook Entries Only to add only new entries, if necessary. Click OK . Related Topics Using Fee Schedules Copying a Fee Schedule Changing a Fee Schedule Updating a Fee Schedule Updating Bluebook Fees Copying an Allowed Amount from Plan to Plan Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Copying Colors
topics.body: Copying Colors Setting Up the Scheduler > Copying Colors and Goals Copying Colors After you have painted or marked the Daybook, you can copy the color scheduling group definitions for the practice or a specific provider from the selected day or week to a specific day, week, or month. To copy the painted or marked Daybook color scheduling group definitions for the entire practice or a specific provider: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select an option: To copy the painted or marked Daybook time slots assigned to the practice : Select Select Practice from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. W hen this option is selected, only time slots and operatories (rooms) that contain color scheduling definitions associated with the office are copied. In the Daybook, 0 is displayed in painted or marked time slots that are associated with the practice. To copy the painted or marked Daybook time slots assigned to a specific provider : Select Select Provider from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Provider List window is displayed. Double-click a provider. When this option is selected, only time slots and operatories (rooms) containing color scheduling definitions associated with the provider are copied. In the Daybook, the provider identification number is displayed in painted or marked time slots that are associated with the provider. Select the date containing the painted or marked schedule, and select Copy Colors from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Copy Colors for window is displayed. Select an option: To copy the schedule for all operatories : Select All Ops . To copy the schedule for one or more operatories : Select Op Range and type the numbers for the operatories you want to copy in the From and To fields. To copy one operatory at a time, type the same number in both fields. Select an option: To copy the schedule for the selected calendar date : Select This Day from the From section. To copy the schedule for the entire week : Select This Week from the From section. Select an option in the To section. To copy any slot notes annotated on the selected date, select Copy Slot Notes . To copy any blocked slots applied to the selected date, select Copy Blocked Slots . Click OK . A message asking you to confirm the color copy is displayed. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Copying Colors�End of Year
topics.body: Copying Colors�End of Year Copying Colors�End of Year You can schedule ahead a maximum of 18 months from the current date. At some point, it will be necessary to copy scheduling colors into the future to continue your color scheduling. To copy the painted or marked Daybook color scheduling group definitions for the entire practice or a specific provider: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select an option: To copy the painted or marked Daybook time slots assigned to the practice : Select Select Practice from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. W hen this option is selected, only time slots and operatories (rooms) that contain color scheduling definitions associated with the office are copied. In the Daybook, 0 is displayed in painted or marked time slots that are associated with the practice. To copy the painted or marked Daybook time slots assigned to a specific provider : Select Select Provider from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Provider List window is displayed. Double-click a provider. When this option is selected, only time slots and operatories (rooms) containing color scheduling definitions associated with the provider are copied. In the Daybook, the provider identification number is displayed in painted or marked time slots that are associated with the provider. Select a date, without appointment s, having the color scheduling parameters you want to copy, and select Copy Colors from the Options section of the Express bar. The Copy Colors for window is displayed. Select an option: To copy the schedule for all operatories : Select All Ops . To copy the schedule for one or more operatories : Select Op Range and type the numbers for the operatories you want to copy in the From and To fields. To copy one operatory at a time, type the same number in both fields. Select an option: To copy the schedule for the selected calendar date : Select This Day from the From section. To copy the schedule for the entire week : Select This Week from the From section. Select an option in the To section. Options displayed in this section are specific to the option selected in the From section. Deselect Copy Slot Notes . If you do not deselect this option, slot notes from the previous year will be copied to the same dates for the new time period. Deselect Copy Blocked Slots . If you do not deselect this option, the blocked slots from the previous year will be copied to the same dates for the new time period. Make sure the blocked slots are set up correctly if you leave this option selected. Click OK . A message asking you to confirm the color copy is displayed. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Copying a Fee Schedule
topics.body: Copying a Fee Schedule Handling Transactions and Codes > Using Fee Schedules Copying a Fee Schedule Instead of entering amounts for all the fee schedules, you can copy one fee schedule to another and then modify it by a percentage. Fee schedules display in the ADA/Transaction Codes list window for all procedure codes. Always use fee schedule 0 as your base charge. To copy a fee schedule: Select System > Fees Maintenance > Copy Fee Schedule . The Copy Fee Schedule window is displayed. In the Copy From Fee Schedule # field, type the number of the fee schedule you want to copy. This field defaults to 0 for the cash payment schedule since most new fee schedules are based on a percentage of the fee schedule 0 amount. In the Copy to Fee Schedule # field, type the number of the fee schedule to which you want to copy the information. Click OK . Related Topics Using Fee Schedules Changing a Fee Schedule Updating a Fee Schedule Updating Bluebook Fees Copying a Bluebook Between Plans Copying an Allowed Amount from Plan to Plan Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Copying Goals
topics.body: Copying Goals Setting Up the Scheduler > Copying Colors and Goals Copying Goals After you set a monthly or daily production goal for your office or a specific provider, you can copy the goals from the selected day or week to a specific day, week, or month. To copy the monthly or daily production goal for your office or a specific provider: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select an option: To copy goals set for the practice : Select Select Practice from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. T o copy goals set for a specific provider : Select Select Provider from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Provider List window is displayed. Double-click a provider. Select the date that contains the goals to copy, and select Copy Goals from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Copy Goals for window is displayed. Click Options and select Copy Goals . The Copy Goals dialog box is displayed. Select an option: To copy the goals for the selected calendar date : Select This Day from the From section. To copy the goals for the entire week : Select This Week from the From section. Select an option in the To section. The options displayed in this section are specific to those selected in the From section. Click OK . A message asking you to confirm is displayed. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Copying Generic and Default Clinical Notes
topics.body: Copying Generic and Default Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes Copying Generic and Default Clinical Notes You can copy a generic or default clinical note and add it to a specific provider's list of notes. To copy a generic or default clinical note: Select an option: Select List > Clinical Notes > Generic Clinical Notes . Select List > ADA Codes > Generic Notes . In the patient chart, from the Overview, Restorative, Perio, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tabs, select Clinical Notes > Generic Notes . The Generic Clinical Notes window is displayed. Select a note in the list and click Copy Note . The Copy Note to Selected Providers window is displayed. Do one of the following: Click or use the arrow keys to select a single provider and click OK . Press the Shift key and click provider names in the list to select multiple adjacent providers and click OK . Press the Ctrl key and click provider names in the list to select multiple non-adjacent providers and click OK . The new note, containing all of the same information that is on the currently selected note, is added to the list of notes for each provider you specified. Related Topics Copying Generic and Default Clinical Notes from One Provider to Another Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Copying Generic and Default Clinical Notes from One Provider to Another
topics.body: Copying Generic and Default Clinical Notes from One Provider to Another Working with Clinical Notes Copying Generic and Default Clinical Notes from One Provider to Another You can copy generic and default clinical notes from one provider to another. To copy clinical notes from one provider to another: Select List > Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. Select the provider who needs notes, and select Add Default Clinical Notes from the Options menu. The Add Notes from Selected Provider window is displayed. Select a provider and click OK . The default and generic clinical notes from the provider you selected in the Add Notes from Selected Provider window are added to the provider you selected in the Provider List window. Related Topics Copying Generic and Default Clinical Notes Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Installing the Card Swiper
topics.body: Installing the Card Swiper eServices > Using the ePayments Service Installing the Card Swiper Before installing the card swiper , e nsure that your merchant account is active and that you have received the merchant profile (INI file). To install the card swiper: Plug the card swiper into the computer used to process credit cards. If you are installing a USB card swiper, the New Hardware Wizard is displayed. Follow the instructions. Launch SoftDent. Select System > Special Edit Field Enable . Select File > Practice . The Practice Information window is displayed. Insert the CD containing the merchant profile (INI file) in the CD drive. Select Credit Card Options from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Credit Card Settings window is displayed. Select Enable Credit Card Processing from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The System Settings - Enable CC Processing window is displayed. Select one of the following: For practices submitting with one merchant number : Click Enabled For Practice . For practices in which each provider submits with an individual merchant number : See Enabling Credit Card Processing by Provider . Click OK . The Open Settings File window is displayed. Browse to the CD drive, and double-click the VisaNet.ini file. Select your time zone and click OK . Verify that the fields on the VisaNet Site Info 1 and VisaNet Merchant Info 2 tabs are populated with information. Close the Credit Card Options window. The Practice Information window is displayed. Click the Default ADA tab. The default values for the payment and refund codes are displayed on the tab. The default settings are recommended, but can be edited. Transactions using the ADA code specified for refunds are printed on the credit card deposit slip. All codes used for integrated credit card processing must be edited and enabled for VisaNet. For more information about editing ADA codes, see Editing Codes . Select Credit Card Option s. The Credit Card Settings window is displayed. Click the VisaNet Merchant Info tab, and click Edit . Click Detect Device . To process credit card transactions over the Internet, select Enable Online Credit Card Transactions . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating a Patient List for Individual Referring Doctors
topics.body: Creating a Patient List for Individual Referring Doctors Using PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo User Preferences Creating a Patient List for Individual Referring Doctors You can create a referring doctor patient list based on individual referring doctors. To create a referring doctor patient list based on individual referring doctors: Select List > Referring Dr . The REFERRING DR list window is displayed. Double-click the doctor you want. The REFERRING DR record is displayed. Click the Additional Info tab. The REFERRING DR Additional Info tab is displayed. Select the Include patients referred with PracticeToGo entry checkbox. Click OK . The next time you perform a sync, this doctor�s referred patient list will be included in the data transfer. In order to create a referring doctor patient list based on individual referring doctors, make sure the Create Referred Patient List for Ref Drs checkbox is not selected in the PracticeToGo User Preferences window. If selected, the User Preferences setting overrides the individual setting in the Referring Dr record and will create a referred patient list for all referring doctors. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating a Treatment Location
topics.body: Creating a Treatment Location Creating a Treatment Location If you use a P.O. box for your practice address, some payers may not accept a P.O. box as an address of where work was done. In this case, you need to create a treatment location address, and you will need to indicate which address to put on a claim. You can set up both a practice (billing) address and a treatment address, if your office uses one, using the Treatment Location Preferences window. You can access this window from several areas of the software, including: Practice Information window ( File > Practice ) Insurance Settings window ( System > Change System Settings > Insurance ) Insurance Wizard ( Wizards > Insurance Wizard ) To create a treatment location: Select an option: In the Practice Information window: Click the Treatment Location button, or select Treatment Location from the Options menu. In the Insurance Settings window: Click the Treatment Location button, or select Treatment Location from the Options menu. In the Insurance Wizard: Select the Modify Treatment Location Address and Claim Address Preferences checkbox. The Treatment Location Preferences window is displayed. In the Treatment Location section, type the address where work is performed. In the Claim Address Preferences section, select the locations to use for your billing address and your treatment address, using the drop-down arrows. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Attachments During Checkout
topics.body: Creating Attachments During Checkout Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services > Creating Attachments to Send to NEA Creating Attachments During Checkout To create attachments during the checkout process: In the Transactions window, add a transaction requiring an attachment and click Close. The Checkout Dialog box is displayed. Click Submit Insurance and OK (Process) . The Transaction Submission window is displayed. Select the transaction date range and click OK . The Claim window is displayed. Verify the claim information and click OK . The Submit window is displayed. Click OK . The Send Attachments to NEA window is displayed. Select the document or image to attach to the claim and click Add . If the image you want to attach is not in the list at the top of the window, click Launch NEA and create the image you need. The NEA Image Information window is displayed. Select an image from the Type drop-down list. If you select X-Ray, select the orientation and x-ray date from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Repeat steps 6 and 7 for each additional image. Type narrative information in the Narrative section of the window. If you are creating a previously submitted claim, type the reference number in the Payer Reference # field. If you have not yet filed the claim and you are attaching images to be sent at the same time as the claim, you do not need to enter a reference number. Select one of the following: To send attachments immediately, click Launch NEA . A message asking to add an attachment history for the claim is displayed. Click Yes or No . The FastAttach software is displayed. To batch the attachment, click Send Later . A message asking to add an attachment history for the claim is displayed. Click Yes or No . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Attachments in the Claim Window
topics.body: Creating Attachments in the Claim Window Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services > Creating Attachments to Send to NEA Creating Attachments in the Claim Window To create an NEA attachment in the Claim window: Select List > Claim . The Claim List window is displayed. Double-click the claim. The Claim window is displayed. Select Create NEA Attachment from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Send Attachments to NEA window is displayed. Select the document or image to attach to the claim and click Add . If the image you want to attach is not in the list at the top of the window, click Launch NEA and create the image you need. The NEA Image Information window is displayed. Select an image type from the Type drop-down list. If you select X-Ray, select the orientation and x-ray date from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Repeat steps 4 and 5 for each additional image. Type narrative information in the Narrative section of the window. If you are creating a previously submitted claim, type the reference number in the Payer Reference # field. If you have not yet filed the claim and you are attaching images to be sent at the same time as the claim, you do not need to enter a reference number. Select one of the following: To send attachments immediately, click Launch NEA . A message asking to add an attachment history for the claim is displayed. Click Yes or No . The FastAttach software is displayed. To batch the attachment, click Send Later . A message asking to add an attachment history for the claim is displayed. Click Yes or No . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Credit Card Payment Plans
topics.body: Creating Credit Card Payment Plans Budget Planning Creating Credit Card Payment Plans When using ePayments, you can set up recurring credit card payment plans and automatically submit monthly credit card payments independent of fixed loan account and patient budget plans. Credit card payment plans do not affect production. After setting up a credit card payment plan, a transaction for each credit card payment is created and displayed on the CC Records tab of the window. The following credit card payment types are displayed on the tab: Failed authorization Pending authorization Authorized Settled Additionally, you can edit or void payments on this tab. Credit card payment plan transactions display on settlement reports with an asterisk next to the credit card type. Related Topics Setting Up Credit Card Payment Plans Reviewing and Editing Payments in a Credit Card Payment Plan Printing a Payment Plan Consent Form Using the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details Window Authorizing Credit Card Payment Plan Transactions Updating Credit Card Payment Plan Credit Card Information Disabling Credit Card Payment Plans Voiding Credit Card Payment Plan Transactions Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating User-Selected Reports
topics.body: Creating User-Selected Reports Handling Reports > Generating User-Selected Reports Creating User-Selected Reports To create a user-selected report: Select Reports > Patient > User-Selected or Reports > Account > User-Selected , depending on whether you want to create a patient or an account report. (A patient report is used in this example.) The User-Selected list window is displayed. Click Add . The User-Selected report title window is displayed. In the Report Title field, type a title for the new report and click OK . The Selection Criteria window is displayed. Items marked with an X display on the report. When generating user-selected reports, the following items are marked with an X by default: User-Selected Patient Reports User-Selected Account Reports Patient ID Account ID Last Name First Guarantor's Last Name Social Security Number NA Home Phone Number Home Phone Number Cell Phone G #1 Cell Phone G #2 Cell Phone E-Mail G #1 E-Mail G #2 E-Mail G #3 E-Mail G #4 E-Mail Patient Balance Account Balance User Codes User Codes Primary Insurance Plans NA Double-click an item to select it, or select an item and click Edit Values . For example, double-click Age . The User-Selected Values window is displayed. Limit the scope of your report by selecting operators in the Op fields and typing values in the corresponding Value fields. Example: To limit the report to all patients between the ages of 10 and 21, do the following: In the upper Op field, select GE (greater than or equal to) from the drop-down list. Type 10 in the Value 1 field. In the lower Op field, select LE (less than or equal to) from the drop-down list. Type 21 in the Value 2 field. Select the Print checkbox if you want the criteria (in this case, Age) to display on the report. If you want the criteria to help define the report but not to display on the printed copy, leave the checkbox blank. If you mark the checkbox, an x is displayed next to the item in the Selection criteria window. Click OK . The Selection Criteria window is re-displayed. Repeat this selection process as necessary, until you have selected all of your criteria on the Info tab. Click the Trans/Category tab. The Selection Criteria Window�Trans/Category tab is displayed. The Trans/Category tab enables you to further define your report by including category codes and either including or excluding transaction codes, transaction dates, transaction types, billing dates, billing providers, and service providers. Example: To exclude a particular provider from the report, do the following: Double-click Dr in the Transactions Excluded area. The User-Selected Values window is displayed. In the upper Op field, select NE (not equal) from the drop-down list. In the Value 1 field, type the provider number or select it from the drop-down list. When you have finished entering the report criteria on the Info and Trans/Category tabs, click OK . A message prompts you to save the new report setup. Click Yes . The software searches the database for patients who match the specified criteria and displays a status window, indicating the number of patients searched and the number of matches found. Click OK . A message is displayed, prompting you to print the report. Click Yes to print the report or select another output option, or click No to create another report or to print the report at a later time. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Generic Clinical Notes from Patient Clinical Notes
topics.body: Creating Generic Clinical Notes from Patient Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes > Handling Generic Clinical Notes Creating Generic Clinical Notes from Patient Clinical Notes This method of creating a generic note enables you to add notes as you work. As you enter clinical notes, you are building a library of reusable generic notes. To make a patient note generic: Select the procedure and tooth, and do one of the following: Click the Notes tab. Select Clinical Notes > View Clinical Notes. C lick Add a descriptive note on the main Charting toolbar. The Patient Clinical Notes List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The Clinical Note window is displayed. To enter the details of the notes, see Adding Patient Clinical Notes . Click OK . The current date is added at the end of the note. If no generic note is associated with the procedure code for the provider, a message is displayed, asking if you want to make a generic clinical note for this doctor or for all doctors. Select an option and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Create Records From All Transactions Utility
