topics.body: Running the Create Records From All Transactions Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running Receivables Totals Utilities Running the Create Records From All Transactions Utility Run this utility to recalculate your receivables records using every transaction entered in your system, including those which have been compressed. Recreating the receivables file may affect your ability to run accounting reports that compare information because you can no longer view your compressed transactions. To recalculate the receivables records for all transactions: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > RCVTOT > Create Records From All Transactions . A message asks you whether to run the utility. Click Yes . A message informs you when the process is complete. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Referral Categories
topics.body: Creating Referral Categories Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals > Handling Referring Doctor Records Creating Referral Categories Before you can run the Referral Source Analysis report, you must create the categories by which you will sort your referral sources. Once you have created the categories, you can assign a category to each of your referring doctors. See Adding a Referring Doctor for more information on assigning categories. To create referral categories: Select System > Referral Categories (1). The REFERRAL CATEGORIES list window is displayed. Click in a field to activate the window, and click Add . The REFERRAL CATEGORIES window is displayed. Type the category name and click OK . Click Add , and type the name of the next category. When you have added all of your categories, click Close . The REFERRAL CATEGORIES list window is displayed. For information about running the Referral Source Analysis report, see Running Referral Reports . Related Topics Editing Referral Categories Running Referral Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Customizing Time Card Types
topics.body: Customizing Time Card Types Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card > Setting Up Time Card Customizing Time Card Types The Time Card Types window enables you to customize the types that you assign to an employee's time. Several time card types are included in the database by default, and you can create any type you need, such as Training (Paid), Seminar (Paid), and so on. The types you create display in the Time Card report as well. If your practice does not use a specific time card type on the default list, make the type inactive by selecting the type and clicking Delete . A message window is displayed. Click Mark Inactive . To customize time card types: Select System > Change System Settings > Time Card > Customize Time Card Types . The Time Card Types window is displayed. Select an option: To add a type: Click Add . The Time Card Types window is displayed. Type a description for the time card type, and deselect the Time Is Paid checkbox if this type is not paid. Click OK . Click Close . To edit a type: Select a type from the list and click Edit . The Time Card Types window is displayed. Make your changes and click OK . Click Close . To make a type inactive: Select a type from the list and click Delete . A message window is displayed. Click Mark Inactive . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Customizing the Welcome Window
topics.body: Customizing the Welcome Window Using the KIOSK Check-In Module > Configuring KIOSK Check-In Module Settings Customizing the Welcome Window You can customize the Welcome window displayed on your KIOSK workstation with a message to welcome or instruct your patients. You can also add color and an image or logo to suit your practice. To customize your KIOSK Welcome window: Select System > Change System Settings > KIOSK Configuration Options > Configure KIOSK workstations. The KIOSK Check-In Module Settings window is displayed with the Welcome Page tab selected. Click Load Image . The Open window is displayed. Navigate to the folder containing your logo or image. Select the logo or image and click Open to automatically re-size the logo to fit the space. Use the text box and formatting options to enter the default text for the Welcome window. Under Welcome Page Style Options, select the type of layout you want for your logo and text. Click Background Color to select the color for the KIOSK windows. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Contact Compression Utility
topics.body: Running the Contact Compression Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Contact Compression Utility Run this utility to free disk space by compressing and clearing contact notes that are no longer needed. To compress contact notes: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Contact Compression . The Contact Compression window is displayed. Type the number of days before which notes are compressed in the Compress contacts older than field. Notes created before the date are compressed. To save the compressed notes to a location other than the contact.txt file, type the file name in the appropriate field. Click OK . A message asks you whether to start the compression process. Click Yes . A message informs you that the compression is complete. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Contact Expert
topics.body: Using the Contact Expert Using Experts Using the Contact Expert Use the contact expert to automate communications to and from your practice, and generate and manage lists of contacts, such as a list of patients whose claims must be resubmitted to insurance carriers. A contact is automatically logged each time you: Add a patient to a merge file Generate a patient quick letter You can also manually log contacts. Example : To remind yourself of a fax you received or to track HIPAA Privacy Acknowledgement Notifications. Logging contacts creates a history of your interaction with the following: Patients Account guarantors Providers Insurance companies Insurance plans Referring doctors Employers Pharmacies Contacts remain in the system until you delete them. You can access the contact expert directly from an office expert item. After running the item, you can send the output to the contact expert. The contact list and contact-specific information are then displayed in the Contact Expert window. The following reports can be generated as contact experts: Account Aging report Account reports Appointment List Budget Plan reports (Account and Patient) Confirmation List Employer reports (Alphabetical and ID) End of Day Callback report Insurance Company reports (Alphabetical and ID) Insurance Plan reports (Alphabetical and ID) Schools (Alphabetical and ID) Other Facilities (Alphabetical and ID) Lab Case report Missed Appointments report Missed Budget Plan Payment report No Response Contacts List Outstanding Referrals report Patient reports Provider reports (Alphabetical and ID) Recall report Provider reports by ID and name Referring Dr. reports by ID and name Short Call List Tickler File report Treatment Plan Details report User-Defined Recall report Office Expert Items Related Topics Using the Contact Expert Window Accessing the Contact Expert Handling Contact Expert Tasks Adding Contact Notes Merging Contact Letters Deleting Contact Lists Creating TeamTalk Notes for Contacts Scheduling Appointments from the Contact Expert Handling Contact Expert Options Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Contact Expert Window
topics.body: Using the Contact Expert Window Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert Using the Contact Expert Window The Contact Expert window c ontains all the information you need to manage contact lists. You can record details of any attempted or successful contacts. In the Contact Expert window, you can view all of the information necessary when organizing and storing your telephone contacts, and ensure that a record exists for all contacts you make. The information available in each field and list in the Contact Expert window: Expert Field : The office expert item name is displayed in this field. Report Field : The report name is displayed in this field. Count Field : The number of records contained in the report is displayed in this field. ID/Name List : The identification number and name for each record contained in the report are displayed in this drop-down list. Note: Records containing deferred contacts are displayed in grey. If the selected record is listed on other Office Expert reports, that information is displayed at the bottom of the left pane. Auto Dialer List : A list of numbers associated with a record is displayed in this drop-down list. If applicable, the name and age of the record is displayed to the right of the drop-down list. Log Contact Tab: Previous Contacts Lists : The date and time the previous contact was entered, the subject of the contact, and the contact status are displayed in this drop-down list. Note Field - Previous Contact : Any contact notes associated with the previous contact are displayed in this field. Name Field - Current Contact : The name of the current contact is displayed in this field. Status Field - Current Contact : The status of the current contact is displayed in this field. By Field - Current Contact : The contact method for the current contact is displayed in this field. By Whom Field - Current Contact : The user ID of the person who entered the current contact is displayed in this field. Subject Field - Current Contact : The subject of the current contact is displayed in this field. Expected By Field - Current Contact : The expected by date for the current contact is displayed in this field. Received On Field - Current Contact : The received on date for the current contact is displayed in this field. Notes - Current Contact : Select a note from the drop-down list to display it in the Notes field. Defer Contact List : If you check the Defer Contact checkbox , select a date from the Defer Contact drop-down list. The contact is deferred until that date. D epending on the report used to generate contact expert information, additional tabs containing report-specific information are displayed in the Contact Expert window. The following buttons are available in the Contact Expert window: Dial : Displays the SmartDialer window. View Previous Contact : Displays the Contacts for window. Contact And Remove : Logs the contact information and removes a record from the left pane of the Contact Expert window. Commands associated with the contact expert are displayed in the Options section of the Express bar or can be accessed from the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Contact Notes
topics.body: Setting Up Contact Notes Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts Setting Up Contact Notes You can define up to ten default contact notes that are frequently used by your office when recording contact information. When adding or editing a contact, you can select from the predefined notes. To define default contact notes: Select System > Change System Settings > Contact Notes . The Contact Notes window is displayed. Select an option: To add a note : Type it in a blank field. To edit a note : Select the text and type the appropriate information. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Contacts Pending Report
topics.body: Running the Contacts Pending Report Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts > Running Contact Reports Running the Contacts Pending Report The Contacts Pending report g enerates a list of all contacts that you need to make. You can run this report for: Patients Accounts Insurance plans Insurance companies Employers Referring doctors Providers Pharmacies Claims The report includes the following information for each contact listed in the report: Identification number Name Address Phone numbers Date on which the contact was made Date by which the contact should be made Sample Report To run the Contacts Pending report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Contact Reports > Contacts Pending . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option, and click OK . The Contacts By Date Range Report window is displayed. Select the type of contact from the drop-down list. Select the from and to dates from the drop-down lists. Type an account identification number only if you are generating results for account records. Type a patient last name range only if you are generating results for patient records. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running a Cumulative Register Report
topics.body: Running a Cumulative Register Report Handling Reports > Accounting Reports > Running Register Reports Running a Cumulative Register Report To run a cumulative register report: Select Reports > Accounting > Registers > Cumulative . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Cumulative Production Report Setup window is displayed. In the Report Date section, click the drop-down arrow to select the date from a calendar. In the Providers section, click the drop-down arrow to select the provider number from a list, or leave the first provider set to 999 to report on all providers. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected in the Excel Options section: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Click OK . Additional Details: This report includes every transaction, including = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('Dangl.htm');return false;">dangling transactions. Report Elements: Provider Name/Type�Name and type of provider. Total: M-T-D is the number of patients seen by each of the specified providers during the previous month. TODAY is the number of patients seen by each of the specified providers for today only. Current M-T-D is the number of patients seen by each of the specified providers from the beginning of the month until today. Theoretical Time�Amount of time (sum of the ADA Fee RTUs) each provider spent with patients. Actual Time�Amount of time the provider spent performing patient care. Total Production�Total dollar amount posted to productions (codes 100.00+) for the specified period. Production per Hour�For individual providers: Total Production � Actual Time (excluding lunch hours, vacation hours, and holidays). Sample Report Related Topics Running Daily, Monthly, Yearly, or Period Register Reports Running a Register Summary Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Applying the Current Discount to a Patient or Account
topics.body: Applying the Current Discount to a Patient or Account Posting Payments > Handling Discounts Applying the Current Discount to a Patient or Account When a current discount is set up, the Discount and Discounted fields are displayed at the bottom of the patient and account Transactions windows. The discount amount and the discounted amount due if the discount is applied are displayed in these fields. To apply the current discount to a transaction: In the patient or account Transactions List windows, select View > Payment Bar . The Payment Bar is displayed in the window. Select Current Discount from the Payment Bar. The discount is applied, and the discount transaction is displayed in the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting the Current Discount
topics.body: Setting the Current Discount Posting Payments > Handling Discounts Setting the Current Discount Use the current discount for a one-time discount offers. Example : You can apply a current special discount to new patients. You can set up one current discount at a time. To set the current discount, select an option: In the Discount Schedule List window : Select the discount and click Current . In the Discount Schedule window : Click Current . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Current Chart
topics.body: Using the Current Chart Restorative Charting > Understanding Restorative Tab Modes Using the Current Chart When the current chart is selected , t he most up-to-date charting information is displayed. You can chart ADA procedures and tooth conditions in the current chart. When you open the chart, any transactions that can be graphically displayed on the patient chart are displayed. For example, an extraction is chartable; prophy is not. Treatment plan items are not displayed in the current chart. To view the current chart: Select Current Chart from the Mode drop-down list. Select the appropriate button from the Chart Mode toolbar, or press F5. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Customizing Document Categories
topics.body: Customizing Document Categories Using the Document Manager > Organizing Documents in the Document Tree Customizing Document Categories You can group items in the document manager by category. Several categories are predefined, and you can add categories of your own. Documents that are automatically archived (letters, billing statements, HIPAA acknowledgements, and so on) are automatically assigned a category. You can assign any category to documents that you scan or import. Categories are displayed in two colors: Blue text: Categories that are pre-defined by the software and distributed as part of the installation. These items have IDs in the range 1 - 1000. Black text: Categories that are added by the user. These items have IDs numbered 1001 and greater. To customize document categories: Select System > Customize Document Categories . The Customize Document Categories window is displayed. Select an option: To add a category: Click Add . The Customize Document Categories window is displayed. Type a description in the Description field and click OK . To edit a category: Select a category from the list and click Edit . The Customize Document Categories window is displayed. Change the category name and click OK . To change the display order of a category in the document tree: Select a category from the list and click Change Order . The Change Order of Appearance in Document Manager window is displayed. Select an option: Click the Move Up or Move Down button until the category is in the correct position in the list. Click OK . Select the Sort Alphabetically button to list the categories in alphabetical order. Click OK . Click Close . Related Topics Changing a Category Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Customizing Toolbars
topics.body: Customizing Toolbars Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using Toolbars Customizing Toolbars Using the charting module, you can dictate whether to display a button and the order in which it is displayed on the toolbar. To customize a toolbar: In the Charting for window, select a toolbar, right-click, and select Customize Toolbar . The Customize Toolbar window is displayed. Select an option: To add a button to the toolbar display : Select it from the Available toolbar buttons section of the window and click Add . To remove a button from the toolbar display : Select it from the Current toolbar buttons section of the window and click Remove . To change the order in which the buttons are displayed in the toolbar : Select the button and click Move Up to move the button up one space or Move Down to move the button down one space until you have the correct order. Click Close . Tip When customizing toolbars, be consistent from one workstation to the next so that staff members don't need to search for a particular button. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Placing Canceled Appointments in the Tickler File
topics.body: Placing Canceled Appointments in the Tickler File Scheduling Appointments > Using the Tickler Placing Canceled Appointments in the Tickler File To place a canceled appointment in the Tickler file: After canceling an appointment in the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows, select an option: To place the appointment in the Tickler file : Select Tickler > Place in Tickler from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. You can also click and drag the appointment to the Show Tickler List icon. To place the appointment in the Short Call Tickler list : Select Tickler > Place In Short Call Tickler from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. You can also click and drag the appointment to the Show Short Call Tickler List icon. If this is the first time you are entering an appointment in the Tickler, the Tickler Wizard window is displayed. Click OK . A message that informs you the appointment is added to the Tickler file is displayed. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Daily Reports: End of Day
topics.body: Daily Reports: End of Day Handling Reports > Frequently Used Reports Daily Reports: End of Day The following table lists the recommended reports that you should run at the end of each day. Name and Path Description Daysheet Reports > Daysheet � Overview of daily finances (for example, posted transactions, production, collections, receivables, and number of new and existing patients seen). � Generating the final Daysheet ensures the accuracy of the receivables totals and accounting reports. End of Day Call Backs Reports > End of Day Call Backs � Appointments marked for a follow-up call at the end of the day. � List can be sent to the Contact Expert. Daily Register Reports > Daily Register � Daily summary of the number of patients seen, number of new patients, production, and collection amounts. Unsubmitted Claims Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Unsubmitted Claims � Insurable transactions not submitted to an insurance company for payment. � Includes unsubmitted charges for patients by dates of service. Daily Operatory Schedule Reports > Daily Op Schedule � Patient appointments by room and provider. � Report setup defaults to tomorrow's date. � Prints patient name, procedure, confirmation icon, medical alerts, and appointment notes. � Option to print in HIPAA privacy mode. To view a printable job aid that lists the reports you should run at the end of the day, see Daily Reports: End of Day . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Daily Reports: Morning
topics.body: Daily Reports: Morning Handling Reports > Frequently Used Reports Daily Reports: Morning The following table lists the recommended morning reports that you should run each morning. Name and Path Description Confirmation List Reports > Confirmation List � Confirmation status of patient appointments for a specified date. � List can be sent to the Contact Expert. Short Call List Reports > Recall/Appt > Short Call List � Scheduled patients willing to move their existing appointment to an earlier opening. � Patients in the tickler file willing to accept an appointment on short notice. � List can be sent to the Contact Expert. Missed Appointments Reports > Missed Appointments � Patients who missed their appointment yesterday. � Moves missed appointments from the Daily Appointments window to the tickler file. � Moves posted appointments to the history file. � List can be sent to the Contact Expert. To view a printable job aid that lists the reports you should run each morning, see Daily Reports: Morning . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Dangling Transactions
topics.body: Dangling Transactions Dangling Transactions Transaction information is saved in the TRANS.DAT file. Report accuracy can be affected if this file contains dangling transactions � transactions that are no longer associated with an account or patient. If your receivables totals do not match, or if transactions appear on a report without an associated patient, the TRANS.DAT file might contain dangling transactions. Reports that use information from the TRANS.DAT file � for example, the Transactions for a Period report, register reports, production reports, and collection reports could contain dangling transactions. If you suspect your reports contain dangling transactions, contact Carestream support for assistance in resolving this problem. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Account Database Events
topics.body: Account Database Events Using TeamTalk > Understanding TeamTalk Events Account Database Events The account record events that can be set up to display a TeamTalk note are: Adding a Patient : Displays a TeamTalk note when a patient is added to an account. Calling Account : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are calling the guarantor associated with an account. Editing Account : Displays a TeamTalk note when an account is being edited. Posting Account Payment : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are posting a payment to an account. Printing Billing Statement : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are printing a billing statement for an account. Printing Walkout Statement : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are printing a walkout statement for an account. Transferring $ to Budget Plan : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are transferring an amount to an account budget plan. Viewing Budget Plan : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing an account budget plan. Viewing Contact List : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing a contact list associated with an account. Viewing Transactions : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing the transactions associated with an account. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Data Entry Tips
topics.body: Data Entry Tips Getting Started > Entering Data Data Entry Tips The following list of data entry tips can help you properly enter data: Dates : Use the mm/dd/yyyy format for date fields. A warning is displayed if you enter an invalid date. Type T in any date field to insert the current system date automatically. Notes : After you type enough data to fill the field, an additional row is automatically added to contain your data. Use the arrow keys to scroll up or down and view the entire text entry. The beginning of your note is displayed at the top of the field every time the window is opened. The following multi-line Notes fields are found in the software: Patient personal notes, Plan notes, Prescription drug instructions/additional text, Lab notes, Lab descriptions, Pharmacy notes, and Memo notes. Phone and Fax Numbers : If you press Tab to select a Phone or Fax field, the cursor is displayed at the beginning of the seven-digit number. To enter a 1 and the area code, press Backspace until the cursor is displayed at the beginning of the field, or use the mouse to click the space to the left of the parenthesis. A phone extension number can contain up to four characters. Times : To enter an on-the-hour time, such as 8:00 a.m., type 8a and press Tab. If an office is not open at 8:00 p.m., type 8 and the time defaults to 8:00 a.m. Codes : Certain codes are only recognized when they are entered in an unbroken sequence. For example, the user codes F6, M, and X should be entered into the User Codes field in the Account window as F6MX. Likewise, type provider assignment codes in the Scheduler Template field without commas or spaces (for example, HHBB) in the Scheduler/Fees tab of the ADA/Transaction Codes window. States : Ensure that you enter the correct two characters for each state. Zip Codes : Zip code fields contain nine spaces with a hyphen after the first five. Enter the nine-digit code if available. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Appointments in the Daily Appointments Window
topics.body: Handling Appointments in the Daily Appointments Window Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows Handling Appointments in the Daily Appointments Window The following information can be displayed for each appointment: Patient name Preferred method of contact Patient age Provider and assistant identification numbers Procedure or office codes Tooth numbers When more procedures than can be displayed in the appointment time slot are scheduled, a small plus sign is displayed after the last code. For more information, see Setting Scheduler Defaults . Related Topics Using the Appointment Submenu Commands Setting Appointment Properties Understanding Appointment Icons Lengthening and Shortening Appointments Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Daily Appointments Window Privacy
topics.body: Setting Daily Appointments Window Privacy System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features > Using the HIPAA Privacy Feature Setting Daily Appointments Window Privacy You can toggle HIPAA Privacy in the Daily Appointments window. When HIPAA Privacy is enabled, patient initials are displayed instead of full names, and no phone numbers are displayed. Additionally, you can customize how patient names and phone numbers are displayed when a HIPAA appointment template is selected. To customize a HIPAA template or enable HIPAA Privacy in the Daily Appointments window: Select an option: To enable or disable HIPAA Privacy in the Daily Appointments window : Skip to step 7. To customize a HIPAA template in the Daily Appointments window : Select an existing appointment, right-click, and select Properties . The Appointment Properties window is displayed. Select a template from the Appointment Template drop-down list. If you select the Front Office - HIPAA template : The patient initials are displayed on the first line of the appointment time slot. The scheduled procedural codes are displayed beneath the patient name. The patient home and work phone numbers are not displayed. If you select the Back Office - HIPAA template : The patient initials are displayed on the first line of the appointment time slot. The scheduled procedural codes are displayed beneath the patient name, and the patient home and work phone numbers are not displayed. Select the format in which to display patient names from the Name Format section. Select the format in which to display patient phone numbers from the Phone Format section. Select an option: To apply the property settings to all users : Select Set for ALL USERS . To restore the default property settings to all users : Select Restore ALL USERS . To restore the default property settings for the logged in user : Select Restore Defaults . Click OK twice. The Daily Appointments window is displayed. Select an option: To enable the HIPAA Privacy feature in the Daily Appointments window only : Click the Privacy On icon in the Scheduler bar. If the global HIPAA Privacy feature is disabled, this feature is disabled when you close the window. To disable the HIPAA Privacy feature in the Daily Appointments window only : Click the Privacy Off icon in the Scheduler bar. If the global HIPAA Privacy feature is enabled, this feature is enabled when you close the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows
topics.body: Using the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows Using the Scheduler Using the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows The list of shortcut commands available to help you navigate through the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance windows are: : Press this key to select the next time slot in an operatory. : Press this key to select the previous time slot in an operatory. + : Press this key to display the next day. Press this key to display the previous day. : Press this key to select the operatory to the right of the selected operatory. : Press this key to select the operatory to the left of the selected operatory. Ctrl + : Press these keys to jump several rooms to the right. Ctrl + : Press these keys to jump several rooms to the left. Ctrl + H : Press these keys to display the current day in the Daily Appointments window. Page Up : Press this key to select the first time slot in an operatory. Page Down : Press this key to select the last time slot in an operatory. Related Topics Using the Scheduler Icon Bar U sing the Provider Detail Bar Viewing Different Days Displaying Different Books Viewing Additional Rooms Customizing the Hour and Minute Markers Using Slot Notes Understanding Daybook Options Handling Appointments in the Daily Appointments Window Setting Daily Appointment Window Properties and Using Profiles Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Daily Appointment Window Properties and Using Profiles
topics.body: Setting Daily Appointment Window Properties and Using Profiles Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows Setting Daily Appointment Window Properties and Using Profiles You can customize the background color and texture, and the color of the lines that delineate slots and hours in the Default Daybook profile. In addition, you can create other book profiles and enable others to access the books to change the appearance of time slots in the Daily Appointments window. Related Topics Using Time Slot Submenu Commands Setting Daily Appointments Window Properties Adding Daily Appointment Window Profiles Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Daily Goals
topics.body: Setting Daily Goals Setting Up the Scheduler > Goal Scheduling Setting Daily Goals To set a daily production goal for your office or a specific provider: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select an option: To set a daily production goal for your practice : Select Select Practice from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To set a daily production goal for a specific provider : Select Select Provider from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Select a day and select Set Day�s Goal from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Type the daily goal amount and press Enter. After you have saved a daily production goal, you can copy the goal to other days. For more information, see Copying Goals . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up the Daily Operatory Schedule Report
topics.body: Setting Up the Daily Operatory Schedule Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Handling the Daily Operatory Schedule Report Setting Up the Daily Operatory Schedule Report You can define how the report is printed. You can dictate how appointment information is printed in the report. To set Daily Operatory Schedule report properties: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Daily Op Schedule > Set Daily Op Properties . The Daily Operatory Schedule Properties window is displayed. Select a report template from the Report Template drop-down list. Each template defines how the report is printed. Type the number of rooms to print per page in the Rooms per page field. The default value for this field is four. Select an option: To always print the specified number of rooms in the report : Select Always next to the Rooms per page field. For example, if you specify four rooms but only have three, then four columns are printed and the extra column is blank. To never print more than the specified number of rooms on one page : Select Max next to the Rooms per page field. For example, if you specify four rooms but have six rooms, the fifth and sixth rooms are always printed on a separate page. Type the number of hours to print per page in the Hours per page field. The default value for this field is ten. Select an option: To always print the specified number of hours in the report : Select Always next to the Hours per page field. For example, if you specify twelve hours but your office is only open ten, the extra time slots are blank. To never print more than the specified number of hours on one page : Select Max next to the Hours per page field. For example, if you specify ten hours but are open twelve, the extra hours are always printed on a separate page. Select a page layout. To print the report in color, select Print In Color . Select the book to print from the Print Rooms in Book drop-down list. Select an option: To print the assigned goal color for each time slot and room : Select Show Color Columns . To print a time column in the report : Select Show Time Columns . To print appointment notes : Select Print Notes On Appointment . To print appointment confirmation notes : Select Print Confirmation Notes On Appointment . To print all appointment notes, confirmation notes, and medical alerts on a separate page : Select Print All Notes, Medical Alerts and User Codes on Separate Page(s) . Type the number of copies of the schedule and notes to print in the Copies of Schedule and Copies of Notes fields. Click the Appt tab. The Appt tab is the active window. Select an appointment template from the Appointment Template drop-down list. Select an option: To customize a text color : Select the box next to the appropriate item in the Text Colors section. The Colors window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To customize a background color : Select the box next to the appropriate item in the Background Color Options section. The Colors window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . Select a format in which to print patient names from the Name Options section. To print patient phone numbers in the report, select Show Phone Numbers , and to print area codes, select Include Area Code . To print the tooth and surface numbers in the report, select Show Tooth & Surface . To include the patient�s age in the report, select Show Patient Age . To print the patient�s age only if the patient is younger than a specific age, select Show if Age less than

topics.title: Viewing Daily Production Goals
topics.body: Viewing Daily Production Goals Setting Up the Scheduler > Tracking Goals Viewing Daily Production Goals You can view the daily production and goal information for the entire practice or for each provider. To view daily production and goal information: Select: To view the daily production and goal information for your practice : Select List > View Daily RCVTOT Totals > Goals/Prod > Practice Goals . The Rcvtot List window is displayed. The date of activity, practice identification number, practice name, actual daily goal, scheduled goal amount, percentage of daily achieved goal, and the production amount for the practice are displayed in the window. To view the daily production and goal information for a provider : Select List > View Daily RCVTOT Totals > Goals/Prod > Provider Goals . The Select Provider Goals window is displayed. Select a provider from the list and click OK . The Rcvtot List window is displayed. The date of activity, provider identification number, provider name, actual daily goal, scheduled goal amount, percentage of daily achieved goal, and the production amount for the provider are displayed in the window. Estimated write-off amounts for PPO procedures are not included in production amounts. Example : If a procedure�s fee is $54, the allowed amount is $44, the estimated insurance payment is $42, the copay is $2, and the write-off is $10; the production amount is $44, the estimated copay plus the estimated insurance payment. To compare the production and scheduling goals to the actual productions and collections for a specific date, double-click the goal or actual production in the list. The Rcvtot window is displayed. The information that is displayed in this window cannot be modified. After you have viewed the information, click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting a Daily Production Goal
topics.body: Setting a Daily Production Goal Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Provider Information > Setting Production Goals Setting a Daily Production Goal To set a daily production goal for a specific provider: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select Select Practice . The practice name is displayed preceding the calendar. Select the day and select Set Day�s Goal . The selected day is enabled, and the cursor is displayed in the day. Type the daily goal amount and press Enter . After you have saved a daily production goal, you can copy the goal to other days. For more information, see Copying Goals . Tip Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Daily, Monthly, Yearly, or Period Register Reports
topics.body: Running Daily, Monthly, Yearly, or Period Register Reports Handling Reports > Accounting Reports > Running Register Reports Running Daily, Monthly, Yearly, or Period Register Reports To run daily, monthly, yearly, or period register reports: Select Reports > Accounting > Registers > report type . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Register Setup window is displayed. In the Providers section, click the drop-down arrow to select the provider number from a list, or leave the first provider set to 999 to report on all providers. Select the Include Receivables Totals checkbox if you want the report to print the receivables figure for the entire system to date. Your Receivables Total file (RCVTOT.DAT) must be current for these figures to be accurate. Click OK . Additional Details: This report includes every transaction, including = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('Dangl.htm');return false;">dangling transactions. Report Elements: Patients Seen�Count of patients seen during the specified date range. New Patients Seen�Count of patients seen whose First Visit dates are within the specified date range. Productions�Total dollar amount posted to productions (codes 100.00+). Charges�Total dollar amount posted to charges (codes 61.00-95.89, 97.00-97.89, 99.00-99.89). Adjustments To Production�Total dollar amount posted to production adjustments (codes 40.00-60.89). Amount due to PPO write-offs�Total dollar amount posted to PPO write-offs (code 50.90). Tax Liability�Tax on production codes (if tax feature is enabled). Net Productions�Net Production = Productions + Charges +/- Adjustments to Production - Tax. Collections�Total collections = Cash + Check + Credit Cards + EFT. Cash�Total dollar amount posted to cash payments (codes 1.00-1.89), including plan transactions. Check�Total dollar amount posted to check payments (codes 2.00-10.89), including plan transactions. Credit Cards�Total dollar amount posted to credit card payments (codes 11.00-20.89), including plan transactions. EFT�Total dollar amount posted to electronic fund transfer payments (codes 2.90 and 2.93), including plan transactions. Adjustments to Collections�Total dollar amount posted to collections adjustments (codes 21.00-39.89, 96.00-96.89, 98.00-98.89), including plan transactions. Net Collections�Net Collections = Collections +/- Adjustments to Collections. Regular Collections�Subtotal of Net Collections posted to accounts and patients. Average Production per Patient�Average Production per Patient = Net Productions divided by Patients Seen. Average Production per Hour For practice = Net Productions � All Providers' Current Hours (includes current hours for all providers who had production for the day and excludes holidays, even when they have been cleared by running the Missed Appointments Report). Example: A provider works eight hours a day, five days a week, and you run the report for one week. If the provider only had production on three of those five days, the total hours would be 24. For individual providers = Net Productions � Provider Hours (includes current hours for all providers who had production for the day, excluding lunch hours, vacation hours, and holidays). Previous Receivables Total Daily previous day's entry in the RCVTOT.DAT file. Monthly previous month's ending entry in the RCVTOT.DAT file. Yearly previous year's ending entry in the RCVTO

topics.title: Running the Daysheet Report
topics.body: Running the Daysheet Report Handling Reports > Accounting Reports Running the Daysheet Report The daysheet gives an overview of the day's finances, including all transactions posted, receivables totals, and the number of new and existing patients seen. The report summarizes productions, charges, adjustments, collections, and receivables for any or all providers. Finally, the report lists all checks for deposit and credit card charges, as well as Electronic Fund Transfer (EFT) payments. The deposit slip lists check and cash payments posted for the day. You can use this information when completing your bank deposit slip. The credit card deposit slip lists all personal and insurance credit card payments posted for the day. The EFT deposit slip lists all EFT payments posted for the day. The CareCredit deposit slip shows all CareCredit payments posted for the day. The deposit slip for credit card, EFT, or CareCredit payments does not print if payments of that type were not posted for the day. Additional Daysheet Info: The daysheet includes every transaction, including any = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('Dangl.htm');return false;">dangling transactions that have not been fixed. If you edit a past transaction, you must rerun a final daysheet for that day and all days since to ensure the accuracy of your receivables total and accounting reports. If you need to edit a transaction that is more than a few days old, it is better to make an adjustment. For information on adjustments, see Posting Adjustments . If you run the final daysheet for the day and then find that you have forgotten to include a payment, you should add the payment as you typically would and then re-run the final daysheet. You should not compare the receivables total from the Account Aging report with the receivables number on the daysheet. The defaults for the Account Aging report exclude a number of accounts, while the daysheet does not exclude any accounts. Sample Report To run the Daysheet report: Select Reports > Accounting > Daysheet . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output type and click OK . The Daysheet Report window is displayed. Select the date for the daysheet you want to run, and check the Is this the FINAL daysheet for this date? checkbox if this is the last daysheet for the day. You must run a final daysheet at the end of every workday to ensure the accuracy of your receivables total and accounting reports. Type the number of the format in which you want to print the first section of the daysheet. Select from these options: 1. List Each Transaction Separately �Lists every transaction posted during the specified date. 2. Summarize Patient Transactions �Lists the patient only once and groups the dollar amounts by production, charges, and so on. 3. Only List Totals �Lists only the totals for the specified date, grouped by production, charges, and so on. To prevent a section of the daysheet from printing, deselect the option you do not want to print. Select the provider(s) you want to include in the report, and select an option button by which to sort the report. Click OK . For an explanation of the items contained in the daysheet report, click here . ID and Name �Patient or account ID and name. Transactions posted to the account will have the account's first name. D$ �Collecting provider. Dr �Service provider. Code and Description �Posted transaction code and ADA description. Prod �Dollar amount of production codes posted (codes 100.00+). Charges �Dollar amount of charges posted (codes 61.00-95.89, 97.00-97.89, 99.00-99.89). Prod Adj �Dollar amount of production adjustments posted (codes 40.00-60.00). Cash �Dollar amount of cash payments (codes 1.00-1.89). Check �Dollar amount of check payments (codes 2.00-10.89). Credit �Dollar amount of credit card payments (codes 11.00-20.89). Pay Adj �Dollar amount of collection adjustments posted (codes 21.00-39.89, 96.00-96.89, 98.00-98.89). Total Tax on Productions and Charges �Dollar amount of tax charged (if the tax feature is enabled). Today's Receivables �Total accounts receivables for the day, including payments posted to insurance plans. Insurance Plan Payments �Dollar amount of payments posted to insurance plans. Expected Insurance �Dollar amount expected from insurance based on today's productions, including expected write-off for PPO plans. Expected Write-Offs �Portion of Expected Insurance to be written off. The expected write-off is stored with the transaction. Transferred to Budget Plans (code 60) �Total of any account balances transferred to a budget plan. These accounts were credited the transferred amount using code 60. (This field does not appear if you choose format 3.) Charged to Budget Plans (code 48) �Total of any amounts charged to accounts with budget plans using code 48. (This field does not appear on format 3.) The amount transferred to budget plans should equal this amount. If an account budget plan was not set up correctly, BUDGET PLAN MISMATCH appears below the transaction. Patients �Total number of patients who have production posted for the daysheet date. When the daysheet is run for individual providers, the patient is counted for the collecting provider and the service provider. When the daysheet is run for the practice, the patient is counted once, regardless of the number of production codes posted or providers seen. New Patients �Number of patients with production posted for the daysheet date, and whose First Visit date also matches the daysheet date. Electronic Funds Transfers �Number of transfers posted and the total dollar amount posted for reserved codes 2.90 and 2.93. Previous Receivables Total �Previous day's entry in the Receivables Total (RCVTOT.DAT) file. Listed only if the Is this the FINAL daysheet for this date? option is selected in the Daysheet Report window. Today's Receivables �Total accounts receivable for the day. Listed only if the Is this the FINAL daysheet for this date? option is selected in the Daysheet Report window. New Receivables Total �Previous Receivables Totals +/- New Receivables Total. Listed only if the Is this the FINAL daysheet for this date? option is selected in the Daysheet Report window. Provider Summary �Lists the following information: ID �Doctor who collected/produced today's charges. Provider �Name of provider. Production$ �Total dollar amount posted for provider Dr$ who collects on production codes 100.00+. Production �Total dollar amount posted for service provider Dr on production codes 100.00+. Charges �Total dollar amount posted for Dr$ charge codes 61.00-95.89, 97.00-97.89, 99.00-99.89. Prod Adj$ �Total dollar amount posted for Dr$ production adjustment codes 40.00-60.00. Prod Adj �Total dollar amount posted for Dr production adjustment codes 40.00-60.00. Tax �Total tax for today's transactions using Dr$ on transaction. Collections �Total dollar amount posted for Dr$ collection codes 1.00-20.89. Coll. Adj. �Total dollar amount posted for Dr$ collection adjustment codes 21.00-39.89. Receivables �Change in receivables for the day. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!
