topics.title: Deferring Contacts
topics.body: Deferring Contacts Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Handling Contact Expert Tasks > Accessing the Auto Dialer Deferring Contacts Use the contact expert to defer a contact. Example : You speak with a contact for an overdue account balance and are informed that a payment has been mailed. To defer a contact: In the Contact Expert window, select a record from the list in the left pane. Select Defer Contact . The drop-down list defaults to one calendar week from the current date. To change the deferment date, click the drop-down list, select the appropriate month and day, and click OK . Type any notes in the Notes field. Click Log and Remove Contact . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Defining Daily Messages
topics.body: Defining Daily Messages Using the KIOSK Check-In Module > Configuring KIOSK Check-In Module Settings Defining Daily Messages You can define date-specific messages for the Welcome window that you can change from day to day to reflect information about your practice, such as upcoming holiday closings. You can save daily messages for reuse, or define new ones as needed. To define a daily message: Select System > Change System Settings > KIOSK Configuration Options > Configure KIOSK workstations. The KIOSK Check-In Module Settings window is displayed with the Welcome Page tab selected. Click the Message Of The Day tab. Use the text box and formatting tools to type a message to display. Click Save as New . You are prompted to type a name for the message, such as July 4th, Closing Early . Type a name for the message and click OK . The message is saved to the Predefined Messages list. Select the Show Message of the Day on Welcome screen option to use this as the current message. To edit and reuse an existing message, select the message from the list, enter the new text for the message, and click Save Message . To delete a message from the list, select the message and click Delete Message . If the Show Message of the Day on Welcome screen option is not selected, then only the Welcome message is displayed on the Welcome window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Defining a Discount
topics.body: Defining a Discount Posting Payments > Handling Discounts Defining a Discount Before defining a discount, ensure that the: Transaction to which the discount will be applied is added to the database Code is posted for the discount credit transaction Code description is printed on the patient�s statement New credit code is created for each discount to generate reports that track its effectiveness Code must be in the 50.00�60.89 range Codes 50.90, 50.91, and 60.00 are reserved by the software and should not be edited. You can redefine codes 50.00 and 58.90, but code 58.90 is predefined for transferring a balance to a patient budget plan. If you edit this code, ensure that the code is not entered in the Credit Code field in the Patient Budget Plan section of the General System Settings window. Related Topics Understanding Discount Types Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting the Default Exam Sequence
topics.body: Setting the Default Exam Sequence Charting Exams Setting the Default Exam Sequence To specify the default exam sequence: Select an option: Select Chart > Options . The Chart Options Properties window is displayed. Click Exams Order . From the soft tissue, perio tissue, occlusal, TMJ, or PSR exams, select Click here to change exam order . The Patient Exams Order window is displayed. Select an option: To remove an exam from the list : Deselect the appropriate checkbox. To move an exam up in the list : Select it and click Move Up until it is in the appropriate location. To move an exam down in the list : Select it and click Move Down until it is in the appropriate location. After you have completed the order, click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Defining KIOSK Messages
topics.body: Defining KIOSK Messages Using the KIOSK Check-In Module > Configuring KIOSK Check-In Module Settings Defining KIOSK Messages You can define the messages that display when your patients attempt to check in on the KIOSK computer. To define your KIOSK messages: Select System > Change System Settings > KIOSK Configuration Options > Configure KIOSK workstations. The KIOSK Check-In Module Settings window is displayed with the Welcome Page tab selected. Click the Result Messages/Options tab. Use the text boxes and formatting tools to enter the messages you want to display for a failed and a successful check-in attempt. In the Available KIOSK Input Methods section, select all of the methods by which patients will access the KIOSK computer. The instructions to continue are displayed as follows: Keyboard: "Please press any key to continue." Mouse: "Please click the mouse to continue." Touch Screen: "Please touch the screen to continue." In the Return to Welcome Screen after this many seconds idle field, type the number of seconds the KIOSK can remain idle before the Welcome window is displayed. This value in this field defaults to 30 seconds and must be at least 10 seconds. In the KIOSK Shutdown Password field, type the password to shut down the KIOSK station and return control to the operating system. The default password is "LETMEOUT." Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Default Clinical Notes from Patient Clinical Notes
topics.body: Creating Default Clinical Notes from Patient Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes > Handling Default Clinical Notes Creating Default Clinical Notes from Patient Clinical Notes To create a default clinical note: Select a procedure and tooth, and do one of the following: Click the Notes tab. Select Clinical Notes > View Clinical Notes. C lick Add a descriptive note on the main toolbar. The Patient Clinical Notes List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The Clinical Note window is displayed. To enter the details of the notes, see Adding Patient Clinical Notes . To use this note as a default when posting the procedure, check Use this text as the default for this ADA Code . Click OK . The current date is added at the end of the note. If no generic note is associated with the procedure code for the provider, a message is displayed, asking if you want to make a default clinical note for this doctor or for all doctors. Select an option and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting or Deactivating Accounts
topics.body: Deleting or Deactivating Accounts Setting Up Accounts Deleting or Deactivating Accounts You can recover deleted data only from backup tapes or files. When you delete an account, an audit trail entry is made. The name of the user who deleted the data is recorded. To delete an account: Select List > Account . The Account List Window is displayed. Select the account and click Delete . The Delete Account or Patient Wizard window is displayed. Select Delete Account/Patient . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . A message asks you to print an Account Detail report. To print the report, click Yes . To mark an account record inactive: Select List > Account . The Account List window is displayed. Double-click the record. The record is displayed in the Account window. Select Inactive . Click OK . A message is displayed. To mark all patients assigned to the account inactive, click Yes . To maintain current patient statuses, click No . Click Close . Tip To ensure the accuracy of end-of-year reports, deactivate accounts. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting an ePostings Adjustment Code
topics.body: Deleting an ePostings Adjustment Code eServices > Using the ePostings Service > Using the ePostings Adjustments Window Deleting an ePostings Adjustment Code To delete an ePostings adjustment code: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > ePostings > ePostings Adjustments . The ePostings Adjustments list window is displayed. Select the code you want to delete. Click Delete . A message is displayed, asking you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Annotations
topics.body: Deleting Annotations Handling Images While Charting > Using Annotation Mode Deleting Annotations To delete annotation text or shapes: On the Clinical Imaging tab of the Chart for window, select Annotation Mode . Select and drag the image from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. You can also select an image in the Clinical Detail Container, right-click, and select Annotate from the menu. Or select buttons on the Image Annotate toolbar . Select the annotation and select Delete Selected Annotation(s) . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Books
topics.body: Deleting Books Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Books Deleting Books You can delete books that are no longer useful. Every office has a default office book that contains all the rooms in the office and the hours that the office is open. This book is displayed in blue in the Scheduler Books List window and cannot be deleted. If you delete a book that is used as the auto-scheduling book for a provider, the default office book is used with the auto scheduler. To delete a book: Select Scheduler > Setup Books . The Scheduler Books List window is displayed. Select a book and click Delete . Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Claims By Age
topics.body: Deleting Claims By Age Submitting and Tracking Claims > Resubmitting and Deleting Claims Deleting Claims by Age When you use this function, you are not actually deleting the claims . You are moving them from the pending claims batch to the Completed Claims file, marking them paid in the patient transactions list, and transferring the balance to the patient. A new "transfer balance" transaction is not created. The amount is moved from the Insurance column to the Patient Balance column. This function does not remove unsubmitted claims from the pending claims batch even if they are within the date range specified. To delete claims by age: Select Insurance > Resubmit or Delete by Age . The Resubmit/Delete Old Insurance Claims window is displayed. To delete incomplete claims, select Delete Incomplete Claims . To retain ortho claims, select Exclude Ortho Claims . Select Paper , ECS only , or All . Select First Sent Date or Last Sent Date from the Process All Claims With drop-down list. To set the date range, select an option: Older than __ days : Enter the number of days (Older than __ days) or a cutoff date (Prior to __). In date range : Enter the beginning and ending dates. Click OK . A message is displayed, asking you to confirm the deletion process. Click Yes . A message is displayed, listing the number of records that have been processed. Click OK . A message is displayed, asking if you want to print the log file. Click Yes . The Action Options window is displayed. Select Print and click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Clinical Notes
topics.body: Deleting Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes Deleting Clinical Notes To delete clinical notes, you must have the necessary security rights. When you delete a clinical note, an entry is added to the charting audit trail file that records the date, time, and name of the user who made the deletion. To delete clinical notes: In any clinical notes window, select the note and click Delete . A message asks you whether to delete the note. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Color Group Definitions
topics.body: Deleting Color Group Definitions Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Color Scheduling Deleting Color Group Definitions To delete a color group definition: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select Set Up Color Groups from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Scheduling Color Definitions window is displayed. Select a group definition and click Delete . A message asking you to confirm the deletion is displayed. Click Yes . The ADA file is updated, and the appointment scheduler is reset when you delete a color group definition. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Codes
topics.body: Deleting Codes Handling Transactions and Codes > Editing Codes Deleting Codes To delete a code: Select List > ADA Codes . The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code List window is displayed. Select the code and click Delete . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + C to access the ADA/CPT/Transaction Code List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Conditions
topics.body: Deleting Conditions Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Conditions Deleting Conditions To delete a condition on the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, or Treatment Planning tab in the Chart for window, click the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the condition, right-click, and select Delete . A message asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Contacts
topics.body: Deleting Contacts Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts Deleting Contacts To delete a contact: Select an option: To delete a contact for a patient record : In the Patient List window, select the patient. To delete a contact for an insurance plan record : In the Insurance Plan List window, select the plan. To delete a contact for a referring doctor record : In the REFERRING DR List window,select the doctor. To delete a contact for a provider record : In the Provider List window, select the provider. To delete a contact for an employer record : In the Employer List window, select the employer. To delete a contact for an insurance company record : In the Insurance Company List window, select the company. To delete a contact for a school record : In the School List window, select the school. To delete a contact for a laboratory record : In the Labs List window, select the laboratory. To delete a contact for a pharmacy record : In the Pharmacy List window, select the pharmacy. To delete a contact for an account record : In the Account List window, select the account. To delete a contact for a claim record: In the Claim List window, select the claim. To delete a contact for a completed claim record: In the Completed Claim List window, select the claim. Select Contacts > By Date or Contacts > By Type from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Contacts for List window is displayed. Select the contact, and click Delete . A message asks you whether to delete the contact. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Duplicate Employers
topics.body: Deleting Duplicate Employers Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Employer Information Deleting Duplicate Employers To delete duplicate employer identification numbers: Select an option: In the Employer List window : Select the duplicate employer and select Replace Empl ID . In the Employer window : Select Replace Empl ID . The Replace Employer Id dialog box is displayed. Select the employer identification number with which to replace the duplicate number from the drop-down list. Click OK . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The duplicate employer is deleted. Insurance plans, accounts, or patients now reference the correct employer. Tip Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Duplicate Insurance Companies
topics.body: Deleting Duplicate Insurance Companies Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Company Information Deleting Duplicate Insurance Companies To delete duplicate insurance company identification numbers: Select List > Ins. Co . The Insurance Co List window is displayed. Select the duplicate insurance company and select Replace Ins Co ID . The Replace Insurance Company Id Dialog window is displayed. Select the replacement insurance company identification number from the drop-down list. To apply the correct identification number to completed claims, select Include Completed Claims . Click OK . A message is displayed, asking you to confirm the replacement. Click Yes . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + I to access the Insurance Company List window. You can also use the Find/Replace IDs utility to replace duplicate insurance company records. For more information, see Running the Find/Replace IDs Utility . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Duplicate Insurance Plans
topics.body: Deleting Duplicate Insurance Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Deleting Duplicate Insurance Plans To delete duplicate insurance plan identification numbers: Select List > Ins. Plan . The Insurance Plan List window is displayed. Select the duplicate insurance company and select Replace Plan ID . The Replace Plan Id Dialog window is displayed. Select the replacement number from the drop-down list. To apply the correct identification number to completed claims, select Include Completed Claims . Click OK . A message is displayed, asking you to confirm the replacement. Click Yes . Tip To delete a duplicate from the plan record, select Replace Plan ID . Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. You can also use the Find/Replace IDs utility to replace duplicate insurance company records. For more information, see Running the Find/Replace IDs Utility . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Delete Dangling TeamTalk Notes Utility
topics.body: Running the Delete Dangling TeamTalk Notes Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Delete Dangling TeamTalk Notes Utility The Delete Dangling TeamTalk Notes utility searches all TeamTalk notes and removes any dangling notes. To remove dangling TeamTalk notes: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Delete Dangling TeamTalk Notes . The Dangling TeamTalk Utility window is displayed. Ensure that you have a valid and complete backup of your SoftDent data files and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Documents
topics.body: Deleting Documents Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Deleting Documents You can delete clinical images, patient portraits, documents, and presentations in the Documents for window. To delete a document: In the Documents for window, select an option: To delete a clinical image or portrait : Select the image and select Delete from the Options section of the Express bar. A message asks you to confirm the deletion. T o delete a document : Select the document and select Delete Document from the Options section of the Express bar. A message asks you to confirm the deletion. T o delete a presentation : Select the presentation and select Delete Presentation from the Options section of the Express bar. A message asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . To delete archived letters, use the Delete Archived Letters utility. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting an Employer
topics.body: Deleting an Employer Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Employer Information Deleting an Employer To delete an employer record: Select List > Employer . The Employer List window is displayed. Select the a employer and click Delete . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Employer List window is displayed. Tip In any window, press Ctrl + E to access the Employer List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting a Patient Portrait
topics.body: Deleting a Patient Portrait Adding Patients > Patient Portraits Deleting a Patient Portrait You can delete a patient portrait from several areas in the software, including: Patient Chart Patient List Document Manager To delete a portrait in the patient chart: Click the Clinical Images tab at the bottom of the window. Select the portrait, right-click, and select Delete . A message asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . To delete a portrait in the Patient List: Select the patient and click Portrait > Create Portrait from the Options section of the Express Bar. The Patient Portrait window is displayed. Select the portrait at the bottom of the window. Right-click and select Delete . A message asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . To delete a portrait from the Document Manager: In the Patient List or the Patient Information window, click Documents from the Options section of the Express Bar. The Documents for window is displayed. In the document tree, click the plus sign in front of Portraits. Select the portrait you want to delete, right-click, and select Delete . A message asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Duplicate Patients
topics.body: Deleting Duplicate Patients Setting Up Patients Deleting Duplicate Patients Only employees with the appropriate security rights (typically front desk users or office managers) can delete patient records. To delete a duplicate patient record: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. In the Power Bar : Select Patient List Window . The Patient List window is displayed. In the Account List or Account window : Select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. A Patient List window containing all of the patients associated with the account is displayed. In the InOffice window : Select Patient from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Patient List window is displayed. In any window : Press Ctrl + O. The Patient List window is displayed. Determine which of the duplicate patient records contains the least amount of information (charges, procedures, etc.), and manually re-enter the work from that ledger onto the ledger of the record you want to keep. Be sure to move any charting information, documents, or images that might be attached to the record you are deleting, as these items will be deleted also. Return to the list window using one of the above options, select the record you do not want, and click Delete . The Delete Account or Patient Wizard window is displayed. Select Delete Account/Patient and click OK . A message that asks you whether to print the patient details is displayed. To print the details and transactions, click Yes . The details are printed. A message that informs you of the patient balance and asks you to confirm the deletion of the patient record, details, and transactions is displayed. To delete the patient and transactions, click Yes . When you delete a duplicate patient record, an audit trail entry is created. The name of the user who deleted the record is recorded. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Exams
topics.body: Deleting Exams Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Container > Handling Exams Deleting Exams To delete exam information on the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, or Treatment Planning tab in the Chart for window, click the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select Exams located in the History column of the Filter Dialog section. Select the exam, right-click, and select Delete . A prompt is displayed, asking if you are sure you want to delete the exam. Click Yes . You can delete multiple exams at one time by selecting the exams on the list and selecting Delete . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Pending Transactions from a Patient Chart
topics.body: Deleting Pending Transactions from a Patient Chart Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container Deleting Pending Transactions from a Patient Chart You can delete procedures you have accidentally added to a patient chart as long as you do not close the chart or click Save. These unposted procedures display a "Pending" status. If you click Save or close the chart, the procedures are posted, and the status is changed to "Actual." Posted procedures can only be deleted in the Transactions window. To delete pending procedures from a patient chart: In the patient chart, click the Clinical History tab at the bottom of the window. Select the procedure added in error. To highlight several contiguous procedures, click the first one, press Shift, and click the last one. To highlight several non-contiguous procedures, click the first one, press Ctrl , and click each of the others. Right-click and select Delete from the shortcut menu . A message asks if you are sure you want to delete the item. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Report Groups and Reports
topics.body: Deleting Report Groups and Reports Handling Reports > Generating Reports Using Report Manager Deleting Report Groups and Reports When you no longer need a particular report group or report, you can delete it. You can do the following: Delete a Report Group To delete a report group, follow these steps: Select Reports > Report Manager > Advanced Options . The Report Group window is displayed. Select the report group you want to delete and click Delete . The software asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . When you delete a report group, the individual reports that were part of the group are not deleted; the reports still appear in the Previously Set Up Reports window and can be added to another scheduled report group. Delete a Report from a Specific Report Group To delete a report from a specific report group, follow these steps: Select Reports > Report Manager > Advanced Options . The Report Group window is displayed. Select the report group you want to edit and click Edit Reports . The Report Group Members window is displayed. Select the report you want to remove and click Delete . The software asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . The report is deleted from this group only. You can still add the report to other scheduled groups using the Previously Set Up Reports window. Delete a Report from All Report Groups To delete a report from all report groups, follow these steps: Select Reports > Report Manager > Advanced Options . The Report Group window is displayed. Select a report group and click Edit Reports . The Report Group Members window is displayed. Click Add . The Previously Set Up Reports window is displayed. Select a report and click Delete . The software asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting User-Selected Reports
topics.body: Deleting User-Selected Reports Handling Reports > Generating User-Selected Reports Deleting User-Selected Reports To delete a user-selected report: Select Reports > Patient > User-Selected or Reports > Account > User-Selected . The User-Selected list window is displayed. Select the title of the report you want to delete, and click Delete . The software asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Delete History Appointments Utility
topics.body: Running the Delete History Appointments Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Delete History Appointments Utility The Delete History Appointments utility removes all history appointments prior to a date you specify. To remove history appointments: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Delete History Appointments . The Delete History Appointments window is displayed. The Before field is populated by default with a date that is three years prior to the current date. Click OK to run the utility, or click the drop-down arrow to display a calendar and select a different date. Click OK . The utility log file (sdutils.log) displays the date prior to which history appointments have been deleted and the number of appointments deleted. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Holidays
topics.body: Deleting Holidays Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Holidays Deleting Holidays To delete a holiday: Select Scheduler > Holidays . The Setup Holidays window is displayed. Select a holiday and click Delete . A message asking you whether to delete the selected holiday is displayed. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Images
topics.body: Deleting Images Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Images Deleting Images To delete an image: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tabs in the Chart for window, click the Clinical Images tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the image, right-click, and select Delete . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Organization Mode - Deleting Images
topics.body: Using Organization Mode - Deleting Images Handling Images While Charting > Using Organization Mode Deleting Images To delete a patient clinical image: Select buttons on the Clinical Imaging toolbar . Select images in the Clinical Detail Container. On the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select Organization Mode . Select the image on the Clinical Images tab and drag it to the Delete Image icon. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Insurance Plans
topics.body: Deleting Insurance Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Deleting Insurance Plans To delete an insurance plan: Select List > Ins. Plan . The Insurance Plan List window is displayed. Press Enter. The name of the selected insurance company is displayed in the title bar. Select an insurance plan and click Delete . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . If you are deleting a DMO/Capitation plan, a message is displayed, asking to delete plan transactions. Click No to save transactions. Several reports use this information. Click Close . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + P or select List Insurance Plans from the Power bar to access the Insurance Plan List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting an Insurance Company
topics.body: Deleting an Insurance Company Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Company Information Deleting an Insurance Company To delete an insurance company: Select List > Ins. Co . The Insurance Co List window is displayed. Press Enter. Select the insurance company and click Delete . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + I to access the Insurance Company List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting a Lab Record
topics.body: Deleting a Lab Record Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Labs Deleting a Lab To delete a lab record: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Labs . The Labs List window is displayed. Select the lab from the list. Click Labs List on the Power Bar : The Labs List window is displayed. Select the lab from the list. In any window : Press Ctrl + L. The Labs List window is displayed. Select the lab from the list. Click Delete . A delete confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Contact Lists
topics.body: Deleting Contact Lists Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert Deleting Contact Lists To delete a contact list: In the Contact Expert window, select Delete List from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message that asks you to confirm the deletion is displayed. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Delete Archived Letters Utility
topics.body: Running the Delete Archived Letters Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Delete Archived Letters Utility Run the Delete Archived Letters utility to delete archived patient, account, insurance company, referring doctor, insurance plan, and lab case letters. To delete archived Quick and Letter Expert letters: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Delete Archived Letters . The Delete Archived Letters Utility window is displayed. Select the record types or letters to delete. Tip You can delete letters between 1 and 60 months old. To delete letters created before a specific number of months, type the number in the Delete archived letters older than _ months field. To exclude letters attached to patients assigned a user code, type the code in the Exclude Patient User Codes field. To exclude letters attached to accounts assigned a user code, type the code in the Exclude Account User Codes field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Memos by Date Range
topics.body: Deleting Memos by Date Range Contacts and Memos > Using Memos Deleting Memos by Date Range To delete reminder memos within a specific date range: Select List > Memos . The Memos List window is displayed. Select the memo, and select Delete Range from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Delete Memos Between window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates from the drop-down lists. Click OK . A message asks you whether to delete the selected memos. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Patients
topics.body: Deleting Patients Setting Up Patients Deleting Patients Only employees with the appropriate security rights (typically front desk users or office managers) can delete patient records. To delete a patient record: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. In the Power Bar : Select Patient List Window . The Patient List window is displayed. In the Account List or Account window : Select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. A Patient List window containing all of the patients associated with the account is displayed. In the InOffice window : Select Patient from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Patient List window is displayed. In any window : Press Ctrl + O. The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient and click Delete . The Delete Account or Patient Wizard window is displayed. Select an option: To deactivate the patient record : Select Mark Account/Patient Inactive and click OK . The patient is inactive. I is displayed in the patient record. To delete the patient : Select Delete Account/Patient and click OK . A message that asks you whether to print the patient details is displayed. To print the details and transactions, click Yes . The details are printed. A message that informs you of the patient balance and asks you to confirm the deletion of the patient record, details, and transactions is displayed. To delete the patient and transactions, click Yes . Data stored in a deleted patient record is not maintained in the database. You can recover this data only from backup tapes or files. When you delete a patient record, an audit trail entry is created. The name of the user who deleted the record is recorded. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Pending Automated Statements
topics.body: Deleting Pending Automated Statements eServices > Using the Automated Statements Service > Using Automated Statements > Additional Automated Statements Options Deleting Pending Automated Statements To delete Automated Statements that are batched and waiting to be transmitted: In the eServices window, select Submit Data > Statement options > Delete pending stmt file . The Pending Statements window is displayed. Click Yes . The date on which the statement was last sent is included in the account records. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting a Prescription
topics.body: Deleting a Prescription Pharmacies and Prescriptions > Handling Prescriptions Deleting a Prescription To delete a prescription: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the patient chart: Hover over the prescription section of the patient banner and click the link, or click the Prescription button on the Common toolbar. In the Account List window : Select the patient and select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The list of patients associated with the account is displayed in the Patient List window. Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Account window : Select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The list of patients associated with the account is displayed in the Patient List window. Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The list of prescriptions for the selected patient is displayed in the Prescriptions for List window. Select the prescription and click Delete on the toolbar. You can also drag the prescription from the list to the Delete drag-and-drop icon at the bottom of the window. A message prompts you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Presentations
topics.body: Deleting Presentations Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Handling Case Presentations Deleting Presentations To delete a case presentation: In the Documents for window, select the presentation from the Case Presentation Module Presentations section of the tree, right-click, and select Delete Presentation . A message that asks you to confirm the deletion is displayed. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Providers
topics.body: Deleting Providers Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Provider Information Deleting Providers If a provider has never participated in any appointments or transactions, you can delete the provider�s record. To delete a provider record: Select List > Provider, or in any window, press Ctrl + D. The Provider List window is displayed. Press Enter , select the provider, and click Delete . A confirmation dialog box is displayed. Click Yes . The Provider List window is displayed. Tip When a provider leaves your practice, change the provider type to inactive in the provider record to prevent errors when posting payments for the provider. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Account Payments
topics.body: Deleting Account Payments Posting Payments > Posting Account Payments Deleting Account Payments You can delete payments that have been posted to an account. Only delete bulk payments if you post a bulk account payment to the wrong account or for the wrong amount. To delete an account payment: In the Payments window, select the payment and select Delete from the Options section of the Express bar. A message that asks you to confirm the deletion is displayed. Click Yes . Run the Account Aging report, including accounts with a credit balance, to generate a list of accounts that are due refunds. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting a Record
topics.body: Deleting a Record Getting Started > Using List Windows > Using Toolbar Options in a List Window Deleting a Record To delete a record in a list window: Select the record and click Delete . A message is displayed. To delete the record, click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Reminder Memos
topics.body: Deleting Reminder Memos Contacts and Memos > Using Memos Deleting Reminder Memos To delete a reminder memo: Select List > Memos . The Memos List window is displayed. Select the memo, and click Delete . A message asks you whether to delete the selected memo. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting a Pharmacy
topics.body: Deleting a Pharmacy Pharmacies and Prescriptions > Handling Pharmacies Deleting a Pharmacy To delete a pharmacy: Select an option: Select List > Pharmacy : The Pharmacy List window is displayed. Select the pharmacy from the list and click Delete . Click Pharmacy List Window on the Power Bar : The Pharmacy List window is displayed. Select the pharmacy from the list and click Delete . In the Pharmacy window : Click Delete . A message that asks you to confirm the deletion is displayed. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting a School
topics.body: Deleting a School Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling School Information Deleting a School To delete a school record: Select List > School . The School List window is displayed. Select a school and click Delete . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + T to access the School List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Allowance Tables
topics.body: Deleting Allowance Tables Setting Up Insurance > Using Allowance Tables Deleting Allowance Tables When you delete an allowance table, all bluebook entries for any plans associated with the table's entries are deleted. Likewise, when you delete a table entry, all bluebook entries for any plans associated with the entry are deleted. To delete an allowance table or entry: Select List > Allowance Table . The Allowance Table List window is displayed. Select an option: To delete an allowance table entry : Select View Codes . Select the code and click Delete . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . To delete an allowance table : Select it and click Delete . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Tip Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Time Card Entries
topics.body: Deleting Time Card Entries Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card > Handling Time Card Entries Deleting Time Card Entries To delete a Time Card entry: Select List > Employees . The Employees List window is displayed. Select a record and select Time Card . The Time Card List window is displayed. Select the entry and click Delete . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Tickler File Entries
topics.body: Deleting Tickler File Entries Scheduling Appointments > Using the Tickler Deleting Tickler File Entries To delete a tickler file entry: Select List > Tickler File . The Appt Ticklers For List window is displayed. Select an entry, and click Delete . A message that asks you whether to delete the selected entry is displayed. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Tickler Entries by Date Range
topics.body: Deleting Tickler Entries by Date Range Scheduling Appointments > Using the Tickler > Deleting Tickler File Entries Deleting Tickler Entries by Date Range To delete tickler file entries created within a specific date range: Select List > Tickler File . The Appt Ticklers For List window is displayed. Select Delete Range from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Delete Ticklers Between window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates from the drop-down lists. Click OK . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Treatment Plan Items
topics.body: Deleting Treatment Plan Items Charting Treatment Plans > Handling Treatment Plan Items Deleting Treatment Plan Items To delete a treatment plan item: Select an option: On the Treatment Planning tab : Select the treatment plan item, and select Click to delete selected item(s) on the Treatment Plan toolbar . In the Clinical Detail Tab Container : Select the treatment plan item, right-click, and select Delete . A message asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Treatment Plan Procedures
topics.body: Deleting Treatment Plan Procedures Treatment Planning Deleting Treatment Plan Procedures If the patient does not agree to all procedures in the group, you can delete the unaccepted procedures from the software. To delete a treatment plan procedure or group: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the appropriate patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the patient Transactions window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the InOffice window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options menu or the Options section of the Express bar. The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Select the treatment plan procedure, and click Delete . The Confirm Delete window is displayed. Select an option: To delete the procedure : Select Delete Single Item and click OK . To delete the treatment plan group : Select Delete Group . Click the drop-down arrow and select the type of treatment plan you want to delete. Click OK . For legal purposes, you can retain treatment plan transactions and note which procedures the patient refused. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting TeamTalk Note Conditions
topics.body: Deleting TeamTalk Note Conditions Using TeamTalk > Using TeamTalk Options Deleting TeamTalk Note Conditions To delete a TeamTalk Note condition: Select TeamTalk > General TeamTalk Lists . The TeamTalk - Notes window is displayed. Select the database from the List Notes for drop-down list. Select the note and click Edit . The TeamTalk window is displayed. The number of conditions that are associated with the note is displayed next to the Extra Settings button. Click Extra Settings . When the note is displayed, click Delete . A message asks you whether to delete the selected condition. Click Yes . The condition is deleted. Review your conditions, and click Finish . The TeamTalk - Notes window is displayed. For a list of database conditions, see Understanding TeamTalk Conditions . Click Save . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting TeamTalk Notes
topics.body: Deleting TeamTalk Notes Using TeamTalk > Using TeamTalk Options Deleting TeamTalk Notes To delete a TeamTalk note: Select TeamTalk > General TeamTalk Lists . The TeamTalk - Notes window is displayed. Select the database from the List Notes for drop-down list. Select the note and click Delete . A message asks you whether to delete the selected TeamTalk note. To delete expired notes, select Delete Expired from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Click Yes . The note is deleted, and the TeamTalk - Notes window is displayed. Click Save . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Delete Treatment Plans Utility
topics.body: Running the Delete Treatment Plans Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Delete Treatment Plans Utility Run this utility to delete treatment plans, created within a specific date range, that were either accepted but never scheduled or that were not accepted. To delete treatment plans: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Delete Orphaned Patients and Transactions . The Delete Treatment Plans Utility window is displayed. Select the dates between which to delete treatment plans. To delete treatment plans that were accepted but not scheduled, select Accepted, Not Scheduled . To delete treatment plans that were never accepted, select Not Accepted . Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Unposted Procedures
topics.body: Deleting Unposted Procedures Restorative Charting Deleting Unposted Procedures To delete an unposted procedure: Select the procedure on the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. To highlight several contiguous procedures, click the first one, press Shift, and click the last one. To highlight several non-contiguous procedures, click the first one, press Ctrl, and click each of the others. Right-click and select Delete from the shortcut menu. A message asks you whether to delete the transactions. Click Yes . Deleting Pending Transactions Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Deleting Website Addresses
topics.body: Deleting Website Addresses Using E-mail and WebLink > Accessing the Internet Deleting Website Addresses To delete a website: Select Internet > Other Web Sites . The Web Sites List window is displayed. Select the site, and click Delete . A message that asks you to confirm the deletion is displayed. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient List by Demographics Report
topics.body: Running the Patient List by Demographics Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Patient List by Demographics Report The Patient List by Demographics report generates an alphabetical list of patients by zip code. If you are considering opening a satellite office, run this report. Sample Report To generate a list of patients residing in each area code that your office services: Select Reports > Patient > Demographic . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patient Demographics Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient zip codes from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. To restrict the report to a summary of the percentage of patients per zip code only : Select Print Summary . If this checkbox is selected a patient list per zip code is not printed. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Deposit Slip Report
topics.body: Running the Deposit Slip Report Handling Reports > Accounting Reports Running the Deposit Slip Report The Deposit Slip report shows the total check and cash amounts, as well as personal credit card payment and insurance credit card payment amounts, posted for either your practice or a specific doctor. The CareCredit Transactions page lists all CareCredit transactions posted for either your practice or a specific doctor. Practice information automatically prints on the deposit slip when generated. This report might be useful for offices who post payments daily, but only make a bank deposit weekly. To run the Deposit Slip report: Select Reports > Accounting > Deposit Slip . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Deposit Slip Report window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows in the Starting and Ending fields to select the report dates from a calendar. In the Provider section, click the drop-down arrow to select the provider number from a list, or leave the first provider set to 999 to report on all providers. Click OK . Additional Details: This report lists the payment code, bank number, check number, and amount of each payment. In addition, the report lists the patient, employer, or plan ID; and the name associated with each payment. The credit card deposit slip page lists totals for personal credit card payments and insurance credit card payments. In addition, the credit card deposit slip lists the ID, name, and code. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Determining a Patient�s Normal Recall Date
topics.body: Determining a Patient�s Normal Recall Date Recalling Patients > Setting Up Recalls Determining a Patient�s Normal Recall Date The Normal Recall date is calculated by adding the values in the Frequency field and the Last Exam date field in the Patient window. The recall date is displayed in the Next Recall due on or after: field on the Dates tab and the Next Exam Due field on the patient Appointments and Transactions windows. If no recall interval is set, the default frequency is six months. If you change the name of your Normal Recall system, the name of the Last Exam fields and Last Exam section change. Example : I f the name of your Normal Recall system is Recalls, the Recalls section is displayed on the Recall tab, the Recalls date field is displayed on the Dates tab, and the Recall date field is displayed at the bottom of the Patient window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Determining a Patient�s User-Defined Recall Date
topics.body: Determining a Patient�s User-Defined Recall Date Recalling Patients > Setting Up Recalls Determining a Patient�s User-Defined Recall Date The User-Defined Recall date is calculated by adding the values in the Frequency and Perio date fields in the Patient window. If no User-Defined recall interval is set, the default frequency is six months. If you change the name of your User-Defined Recall system, the name of the Perio fields and Perio section change. Example : If the name of your User-Defined Recall system is Ortho, the Ortho section is displayed on the Recall tab, and the Ortho date field is displayed at the bottom on the Dates tab. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting the Default Blocked Slot Color
topics.body: Setting the Default Blocked Slot Color Setting Up the Scheduler > Using Blocked Time Slots Setting the Default Blocked Slot Color To set the default background and text colors for blocked time slots: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select Set Up Color Groups from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Scheduling Color Definitions window is displayed. Select Set Blocked Colors from the room the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Blocked Slot Colors window is displayed. Select an option: To set the blocked time slot default text color : Select the appropriate colored box. The Color window is displayed. Select a text color and click OK . To set the blocked time slot default background color : Select the appropriate colored box. The Color window is displayed. Select a background color and click OK . Click OK . Click Close twice . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

The Diagnosis Code Report Setup window is displayed. In the Diagnosis Codes section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending diagnosis codes from a list. The Account Budget Plan List window is displayed. Select Terms . The FIXED LOAN TERMS window is displayed. Click the CC Payment tab. The tab is the active window. topics.title: Disabling Credit Card Payment Plans
topics.body: Disabling Credit Card Payment Plans Budget Planning > Creating Credit Card Payment Plans Disabling Credit Card Payment Plans To disable a credit card payment plan: Do one of the following: To disable an account credit card payment plan, in the Account List window, select an account and select Credit Card Payment Plan . To disable a patient credit card payment plan, in the Patient List window, select a patient and select Credit Card Payment Plan . Click the CC Payment tab. The tab is the active window. Click Reset . The tab is the active window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Disabling Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payments
topics.body: Disabling Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payments Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Patient Budget Plans Disabling Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payments To disable recurring credit card payments for a patient budget plan: In the Patient List window, select a patient and select Budget Plan . topics.title: Applying any Discount to a Patient or Account
topics.body: Applying any Discount to a Patient or Account Posting Payments > Handling Discounts Applying Any Discount to a Patient or Account To apply further discounts to a patient or account: In the patient or account Transactions List windows, select View > Payment Bar . The Payment Bar is displayed in the window. Select Select a Discount from the Payment Bar and select the discount from the list. The estimated discount and discounted amounts are displayed in the Estimated Discount window. To accept the discount settings, click Accept . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Displaying the Assistant in the Transactions Window
topics.body: Displaying the Assistant in the Transactions Window Handling Transactions and Codes > Posting Patient Procedures > Using the Assistant in Transactions Windows Displaying the Assistant in the Transactions Window To display the Assistant and see transaction information: Open the patient or account Transactions window. Select Zoom from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Assistant window is displayed. "Pin" the Assistant window to use it for several transactions by clicking the push pin symbol in the upper-left corner of the window. The window is displayed as you select each transaction. If you pin the Assistant window, it is displayed each time you open the patient Transactions window. The Assistant window is organized into three pages. The following items are displayed at the top of each page: Patient name and identification number ADA code Date of service The following information is displayed on each page: The ADA Code description, number, and background color are displayed at the top of the first page. The insurance status is displayed at the bottom of the page. Tooth number, tooth surface, RTU, fee, tax, total amount of procedure, insurance payment, patient payment, write off, total amount paid, and transaction age information is also displayed on this page. Insurance claim information is displayed on the second page and includes transaction notes. Diagnostic codes and descriptions are displayed on the third page. The table includes the anesthesia units, follow up calls, PCIs (Prosthesis Crown Inlay), and PCI date information. Surface information is displayed at the bottom of the page. Tip Click the arrows to move from page to page. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Displaying Different Books
topics.body: Displaying Different Books Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows Displaying Different Books Every practice has a book that contains all the rooms in the office. This book is referred to as the Default Daybook. You can create additional books to help organize your scheduling information. For more information, see Setting Up Books . To display a different book: From a scheduler window, select Select Book from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu, or click Select Scheduler Book on the right side of the window. The Scheduler Books List window is displayed. The Default Daybook is displayed in blue text. Double-click a book. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

To disable Practice Central�s automatic display: Select System > Startup Preferences . The Startup Preferences window is displayed. To disable the automatic display, deselect Show Practice Central located in the Display at Startup section. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

Select Completed located in the Chart column of the Filter Dialog section. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a DMO/Capitation or Medicaid Plan Transaction
topics.body: Adding a DMO/Capitation or Medicaid Plan Transaction Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Transactions Adding a DMO/Capitation or Medicaid Plan Transaction To add a DMO/Capitation or Medicaid plan transaction: Select List > Ins. Plan . The Insurance Plan List window is displayed. Select a DMO/Capitation or Medicaid insurance plan and select Transactions . The Plan Transactions List window is displayed in SCAN mode. Click Add . The Plan Transactions window is displayed in ADD mode. Insurance plan transactions are displayed as separate line items on the daysheet and are not included in the current day�s receivables total. Select the code, collection doctor, date, and payment amounts from the drop-down lists. You can only select collection or adjustment codes. If you select a check payment code, type the check and bank numbers in the appropriate fields. Capitation payments are included in the total of the checks listed on the deposit slip, unless they are posted as separate checks. Type any notes in the Notes field and click OK . Click Close . Tip Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Submitting Dental And Medical Claims
topics.body: Submitting Dental And Medical Claims Submitting and Tracking Claims Submitting Dental and Medical Claims If a patient is covered by both dental and medical insurance, you can submit procedures to both insurance carriers, provided you post ADA codes that are linked to CPT medical codes. Example : ADA code 130 (emergency exam) might be linked to CPT code 99283 (emergency exam). When you submit the claim, the software creates a dental and a medical claim and submits them in the order you specify. If you are submitting medical claims, see Medical Cross-Coding . To submit dental and medical claims: After posting a linked ADA code in the patient�s Transactions window, click Submit Ins on the toolbar. If Ask to process or Automatically process is selected in the General System Settings window, a window prompting you to submit insurance, schedule an appointment, and print a walkout statement is displayed. A message prompts you for the range of transaction dates to print on the claim. The first date defaults to the service date of the oldest procedure that has not been submitted, and the second date defaults to the most current date of unsubmitted transactions. Click OK . A message is displayed that informs you that a linked claim is being processed. Click OK . The Order of Submission For Linked Claims window is displayed. By default, the primary dental claim is submitted first and the primary medical claim second. You can change the order of submission. When you post the insurance payment or denial to the dental claim, you are prompted to submit the claim to medical insurance, if the claim was not paid in full. Click OK . The Claim window is displayed for the linked claim. Verify that the information is correct and click OK . The First Sent field is not updated and the secondary insurance claims are not processed until you print the claim or submit it electronically. When you post an insurance check payment for primary insurance to the patient�s Transactions window, a message that asks if this is the final payment is displayed. Make sure you associate the payment with the claim number when posting, and click Yes . A message prompts you to submit the claim to secondary (medical) insurance. Click Yes . A message asks you whether to update the insurance bluebook. When you post a check payment from the secondary insurance plan to the patient�s Transactions window, a message is displayed that asks if this is the final secondary insurance payment. Click Yes . A message asks you whether to delete the claim. To delete the claim, click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Document Manager Features
topics.body: Document Manager Features What's New in Version 14.0 Document Manager Features The document manager has received enhancements in the following areas: New Document Tree Organization To give you more flexibility in grouping and sorting documents, the document tree has been reorganized: Document types have been replaced by document categories. The software includes several categories, and you can also create categories of your own. Category folders display in the tree based on the order you specify. Document labels have been changed to always contain the document description, the acquisition date, and the acquisition time. The description assigned to automatically archived documents has also been made more useful. Changes to the Document Manager Window Several sorting and grouping options have been created to make the structure of the document tree more meaningful. In addition, you can customize the way documents are saved and organized. Grouping Options The document manager now includes the following grouping options: Group By Category�You can now group documents by category, using predefined categories as well as categories you have added. Documents that are automatically archived (letters, billing statements, HIPAA acknowledgements, and so on) are automatically assigned a category. Documents that you scan or import are organized in categories that you create. Group By Date Acquired�You can now group documents by the date documents are created, enabling you to see every image or document that is saved for a patient on a specific date. All images and documents created on the same day are placed in the same date folder, followed by the document category folder. Sorting Options You can now sort documents within a category using the following options: Sort By Date Acquired�This option enables you to sort images or documents within a category by the date on which the documents or images are created. Documents are now sorted by date in descending order. The creation date and time, followed by the document description, are displayed for each document. Sort By Description�This option enables you to sort images or documents within a category alphabetically by description. The document description, followed by creation date and time, are displayed for each document. See Organizing Documents in the Document Tree for more information. Customizing Document Categories A Customize Document Categories option has been added to the System Settings menu ( System > Customize Document Categories ) and opens the Customize Document Categories window. This window enables you to add and edit categories, as well as change the order in which the categories display in the document manager. See Customizing Document Categories for more information. New Change Category Option Added to the Express Bar A Change Category menu item has been added to the Options section of the document manager Express bar and to the context menu that is displayed when you right-click on a document or image in the document tree. Clicking or selecting Change Category opens the Change Category window, which enables you to assign a different category to the document or image. See Changing a Document Category for more information. New Document and Image Icons Documents and images within a category are automatically assigned icons to help you identify the type of information the image or document contains. Icon types include: KDI images, portraits, STV-IntraOral images, third-party x-rays, Power

Case Presentations category icon. Click the document or image icon for a description. This icon is assigned to all dental imaging software images (including Trophy 5 images). Note: If the image is a portrait that is a CS Imaging software image, the CS Imaging software image icon is displayed, not the portrait icon. This icon is assigned to portraits. This icon is assigned to STV IntraOral images. This icon is assigned to third-party x-rays. This icon is assigned to PowerPoint presentations. This icon is assigned to MedVisor images. topics.title: Printing the Dot Matrix Daily Operatory Schedule Report
topics.body: Printing the Dot Matrix Daily Operatory Schedule Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Handling the Daily Operatory Schedule Report Printing the Dot Matrix Daily Operatory Schedule Report The daily operatory schedule lists all patients due in the office, their scheduled providers, and notes/alerts for all your operatories. Up to three operatories print on a page. Tooth number and surface information are also displayed, if requested. Sample Report To print the Dot Matrix Daily Operatory Schedule report: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Daily Op Schedule > Dot Matrix Daily Op Schedule > Dot Matrix Daily Op Schedule . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Daily Operatory Report Setup window is displayed. Click the drop-down arrow to select a date other than today's date in the Date section. Type the operatory number for the operatory on which you want to report, or leave 999 to report on all operatories. Select the Print Appointments & Medical Alerts checkbox to display additional information for specified patients. More... An "N" followed by a number (for example: N10) indicates an appointment note. An "A" followed by a number (for example: A5) indicates a medical alert. If there is a note and a medical alert, the "A" notation is displayed, since the medical alert is usually more important. Notes and alerts display at the end of the report. The patient name is displayed in capital letters if the appointment was confirmed. Select the Use Page Breaks checkbox to specify whether to repeat room titles if the report extends beyond one page. Type the number of copies of the schedule to print in the Copies section. Click OK . Related Topics Printing the Six Room Dot Matrix Operatory Schedule Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Disabling PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform
topics.body: Disabling PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform Using PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo System Settings Disabling PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform When you disable PracticeToGo with the Palm OS platform, the SoftDent conduit is removed from the HotSync Manager, and SoftDent data is no longer transferred during the HotSync operation. You will need to perform a new integration before you can use SoftDent on your Palm organizer again. To disable the PracticeToGo feature: Select System > PracticeToGo > Disable PracticeToGo . A message window is displayed, asking you to confirm that you want to disable the SoftDent integration with the Palm OS platform. Click Yes . Disabling the SoftDent feature on the PC does not remove SoftDent data already on the Palm organizer. If you want to remove SoftDent data from the Palm organizer, select the Remove all PracticeToGo data on next sync option on the Misc. tab in the PracticeToGo User Preferences window and perform a HotSync before disabling PracticeToGo. For more information about removing SoftDent data from the Palm organizer, see PracticeToGo Data Transfer Options . topics.title: Understanding Discount Types
topics.body: Understanding Discount Types Posting Payments > Handling Discounts > Defining a Discount Understanding Discount Types When you select the Percent of Amount Due discount type, a message notifies you of potential legal ramifications. In some legal jurisdictions, a discount applied only to the co-payment may constitute insurance fraud. If you use a discount based solely on the co-payment portion due, use ICM2. You can define the following discount types: Percent of Fee (Allowed Amount) : Apply a percentage discount to the allowed insurance plan coverage amount. When the patient is insured, the insurance estimation calculation is performed; then the allowed amount is calculated using the bluebook and the coverage information in the insurance plan record. The allowed amount for the same procedure can vary according to the patient's insurance type and other factors. Percent of Amount Due : Apply the discount to the patient's amount due. When the patient is insured, the insurance estimation calculation is performed; then the patient amount due is discounted by a percentage. The amount due from different patients for the same procedure can vary, depending upon whether the provider is a member of a PPO, and the patient's insurance type, deductible, and maximum. F ixed : Apply a specified dollar amount credit to the patient record. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Display Encoded Passwords Utility
topics.body: Running the Display Encoded Passwords Utility Utilities > Using File Menu Utilities Running the Display Encoded Passwords Utility Run this utility when you have forgotten your user name or password. The coded user names and passwords for all users in your system are displayed when you run the utility. If you do not have access to all of the utilities, you can still run this utility. Do not run this utility unless you are instructed to by a technical support representative. To run this utility: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select File > Display Encoded Passwords . The Userinfo window is displayed. The decoded user names and passwords for all users are displayed in the window. If you do not have access to the utility, type DECODE in the Name and Password fields to access it. Click Close . When the technical support representative tells you to, restart the software. Related Topics Recovering a Password Without Exiting SoftDent Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Activating PracticeToGo Data Transfer
topics.body: Activating PracticeToGo Data Transfer Using PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo User Preferences Activating PracticeToGo Data Transfer The PracticeToGo User Preferences Active tab enables you to specify which items you want to sync and which commands you want to enable. There are two options at the top of the window: To select a specific sync date, click the drop-down arrow in the Last Sync on: field and select the date from the calendar. Click the Restore Defaults button to restore system defaults for all PracticeToGo settings. To set your PracticeToGo user preferences: Select System > PracticeToGo > User Preferences . The PracticeToGo User Preferences Active tab is displayed. Select from the following options: Select the Active option next to each category of information you want to transfer the next time you perform a sync. Marking a category as Active will transfer current data and remove old data. This will remain set until you change it. Select the Private option next to each category of information you want to make private on the Palm organizer or in Microsoft Outlook when the data is transferred. Practice financials and patient information are made private by default. When you include financials as a syncing option, the information that is transferred includes receivables (production and collections) for a past or current day and goals and scheduled production for a future day or days. If you do not have security rights to transfer a category of information, the option is grayed out. Select the Active option next to each category of SoftDent commands that you want to be able to use on the Palm organizer or in Microsoft Outlook. For more information about SoftDent commands, see Sending Data Back to SoftDent . If you do not have security rights to transfer data from either the Palm OS Platform or Microsoft Outlook, the option is grayed out. In the Command Tag field, use the drop-down arrow to select the prefix you prefer for SoftDent commands on the Palm organizer or in Microsoft Outlook. The default prefix is \SD. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: PracticeToGo Data Transfer Options
topics.body: PracticeToGo Data Transfer Options Using PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo User Preferences PracticeToGo Data Transfer Options The PracticeToGo User Preferences Misc. tab contains choices that pertain to the kind of information included in the data transfer. There are two options at the top of the window: To select a specific sync date, click the drop-down arrow in the Last Sync on: field and select the date from the calendar. Click the Restore Defaults button to restore system defaults for all PracticeToGo settings. To set your PracticeToGo user preferences: Select System > PracticeToGo > User Preferences . The PracticeToGo User Preferences window is displayed. Click the Misc. tab. The PracticeToGo User Preferences Misc. tab is displayed. Select from the following options: Use extended phone information: Select this option to include the additional contact information (telephone numbers, beeper numbers, and e-mail addresses) when you transfer a data record. Codes on list line of appointments (Not patient): Select this option to see the ADA or office code�instead of the patient name�in the appointment summary for information that is transferred during a sync. Use office codes with appointments (Not ADA): Select this option to see office codes�instead of ADA codes�displayed in the appointment information that is transferred during a sync.
