Create Referred Patient List for Ref Drs: Select this option to create a referred patient list with the referring doctor information for ALL referring doctors. This means that a referred patient list for all the referring doctors in the SoftDent database will be transferred during a sync. If you want to transfer a referred patient list for a particular referring doctor, do not select the Create Referred Patient List for Ref Drs option. Instead, select the Include Patients Referred With PracticeToGo Entry option in the referring doctor record for the doctor(s) you want to include. See Creating a Patient List for Individual Referring Doctors for more information. Remove all PracticeToGo data on next sync: Select this option to remove all PracticeToGo data in the Microsoft Outlook software or in the Palm organizer during the next sync. No new data is brought over to these platforms during the next sync. This option is automatically reset after the sync is completed so that future syncs will transfer data as normal. You must have security rights to both patient and practice information to select this option. Include Inactive Patients: Select this option to include inactive patient records in the data transfer. Include Patients with user codes: Type patient user codes in this field to limit the number of patient records that transfer during a sync. If you enter a user code in this field, only patients with that user code are transferred. Exclude patients with user codes: Type a user code in this field to limit the number of patient records that transfer during a sync. Example: You might want to exclude patients in collections by typing a C in this field. This means that the next time you perform a sync, SoftDent software data for those patient records will not be transferred. For more information about patient user codes, see Understanding Patient User Codes . Practice: Select this option to transfer data for all providers in the practice. If you find that you are able to transfer data only for a single specific provider, the system administrator might have set this limitation in System Security. Only include pati

topics.title: Entering a Date Book Command on the Palm Organizer
topics.body: Entering a Date Book Command on the Palm Organizer Using PracticeToGo > Sending Data Back to SoftDent > SoftDent Commands and the Palm Organizer Entering a Date Book Command on the Palm Organizer Date Book commands enable you to transfer schedule information to the PC SoftDent software in the form of blocked slots, slot notes, memos, appointment cancellation notices, and appointment reminders. To view a list of available Date Book commands, click = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('DteBkPTG.htm');return false;">here . To transfer information to the PC SoftDent software with the Date Book commands: On the Palm Home screen, tap the SoftDent icon and select Appointments or Financials. The Date Book is displayed. In the Date Book, tap the SoftDent entry you want so that a cursor appears on the desired line, or tap a blank line to make a new entry. Tap the 123 dot in the graffiti writing area at the bottom of the screen to enter the Number Keyboard window. Begin by entering the \ character (unless you have selected something else as your command character in the Command Tag field in the PracticeToGo User Preferences window in the PC SoftDent software). Next, tap ABC at the bottom of the Number Keyboard window to switch to the Letter Keyboard window. Enter the rest of the SD Date Book command. Example: Enter \SDB# to update a blocked slot request. The command is case sensitive, so it must be entered in all capital letters. The command must also be entered without spacing between the characters. For online tips about ways to reach the onscreen Keyboard, tap the tips icon (the lowercase i) in the upper-right corner of the Keyboard window. Tap the Space bar on the Keyboard window and enter a description next to the command. When you are finished, tap Done to exit the Keyboard window and return to the Date Book. To enter a note about the command, tap the note icon to the right of the entry to enter the note field. If a note doesn�t exist, click here . Tap the entry, then tap Details at the bottom of the screen. The Event Details window for the entry is displayed. Tap Note at the bottom of this window to enter the note field. Once you enter the note field, tap either the ABC or 123 dot in the graffiti writing area at the bottom of the screen so that a Keyboard window is displayed. Enter a note about the entry. When you are finished, tap Done to return to the note field. Tap Done again to return to the Date Book. TIP: For easy viewing of your note in the PC SoftDent software, place the note text at the beginning of the note field. To ensure that the note will not be cut off in the PC SoftDent software, limit the text to two lines. Upon the next synchronization, information about the entries you marked with SoftDent Commands will be transferred to the PC SoftDent software in the form of memos, slot notes, or blocked slots, depending on the command you entered. Memos added to the PC SoftDent software from the Palm organizer display in the Memos list window in blue. Once the information is transferred to the PC SoftDent software, the SD command you entered on the Palm organizer is removed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering a Date Book Command in Microsoft Outlook
topics.body: Entering a Date Book Command in Microsoft Outlook Using PracticeToGo > Sending Data Back to SoftDent > SoftDent Commands and Microsoft Outlook Entering a Date Book Command in Microsoft Outlook Date Book commands enable you to transfer schedule information to the PC SoftDent software in the form of blocked slots, slot notes, memos, appointment cancellation notices, and appointment reminders. To view a list of available Date Book commands, click here . To transfer information to the PC SoftDent software using the Date Book commands in the Microsoft Outlook software: Open the Calendar. Place your cursor in the desired calendar entry. Type the command character tag, and then type the rest of the command. Example: Type \SDB# to update a blocked slot request. Because the command is case sensitive, it must be entered in all capital letters. The command must also be entered without spacing between the characters. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Date Book Commands
topics.body: Date Book Commands Date Book Commands The commands are case sensitive and must be entered on the Palm organizer or in the Microsoft Outlook software in all capital letters. Date Book Commands Command Function SD DateBook Command (from the Palm Date Book or the Microsoft Outlook Software Calendar) PC SoftDent Software Output To block a slot \SDB# [DESC] (Enter the command \SDB# and a description about the blocked slot on the appointment line.) [ NOTE ] (Enter a note in the note field on the Palm organizer.) Note: The # in the command stands for a specific operatory. An "A" may also be used in this position; the "A" represents all operatories. Places a blocked slot in the Daybook that includes the description and note information you entered. �or� If you place this command on an appointment entry, inserts a memo in the memo list that indicates a blocked slot request. The memo displays in blue. To place a slot note in the Daybook \SDN# [DESC] (Enter the command \SDN# and a description about the note on the appointment line.) [ NOTE ] (Enter a note in the note field on the Palm organizer.) Note: The # in the command stands for a specific operatory. An "A" may also be used in this position; the "A" represents all operatories. Places a slot note in the Daybook for the specified time that includes the description and note information you entered. To enter a memo \SDM[FREQ] [DESC] (Enter the command \SDM and a description about the memo on the appointment line. An optional frequency parameter can also be entered. Frequency parameters include: N: No Frequency D: Daily Frequency W: Weekly Frequency M: Monthly Frequency) [ NOTE ] (Enter a note in the note field on the Palm organizer.) Places a memo in the memo list for the date specified; memo content includes the memo description and note information you entered on the Palm organizer. The memo displays in blue. To remind you to cancel an appointment \SDC[N] [EXISTING APPT] (Enter the command \SDC and a description about the appointment cancellation on the address line.) The "N" is optional; if used, a specific request for patient notification will be made. Places a reminder memo in the memo list that specifies an appointment should be cancelled, based on the description and note information you entered on the Palm organizer. The memo displays in blue. If you enter the optional N, a memo displays in SoftDent, requesting that you notify the patient of the cancellation. The memo displays in blue. To remind you to make an appointment for a specific time slot \SDA# [DESC] (Enter the command \SDA# and a description about the appointment on the appointment line.) [ NOTE ] (Enter a note in the note field on the Palm organizer.) Note: The # in the command specifies the operatory. Places a reminder memo in the memo list AND a placeholder in the Daybook; they specify an appointment should be made. The memo displays in blue. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Daybook Options
topics.body: Understanding Daybook Options Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows Understanding Daybook Options In the Daily Appointments window, you can perform several functions. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu when using Enhanced Workspace are: Make Appt : Click an open time slot and select this command to make a patient appointment. The Patient List window is displayed. In this window, select the patient for whom you want to schedule the appointment. See Scheduling Patient Appointments . New Patient Appt: Click an available time slot and select this command to make an appointment for a new patient. The New Patient Appt window is displayed. In this window, you can enter new patient account information. See Setting the Auto Scheduling Book . AutoSched New Appt : Click an available time slot and select this command to auto schedule an appointment for a new patient. The Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters for window is displayed. In this window, you can enter auto scheduling restrictions for the new patient. View Appt : Click an appointment and select this command to display the Appointment Card window. In this window, you can view procedures scheduled for the selected appointment. Edit Appt: Click an appointment and select this command to display the Appointment Card window. In this window, you can view, add, edit, and delete procedures scheduled for the selected appointment. Confirm Appt : Click an unconfirmed appointment and select this command to display the Confirmed Appt for window. In this window, you can confirm patient information. After you confirm the appointment, you are prompted to log a contact for the call. AutoSched Appt : Click an appointment and select this command to display the Auto Scheduler Date Range window. In this window, you can set a date range within which to auto schedule a patient appointment. See Setting the Auto Scheduling Book . Cancel Appt: Click an appointment and select this command to cancel the appointment. You can immediately reschedule the appointment or place it in the Tickler file to reschedule later. Resched Appt : This command is enabled only after an appointment is canceled. Select a date and time slot to reschedule the appointment and select this command. AutoSched Canceled : This command is enabled only after an appointment is canceled. Select this command to display the Auto Scheduler Date Range window. In this window, you can set a date range within which to auto schedule the patient appointment. Resched Overlap : This command is enabled only when two appointments overlap. Select a date and time slot to reschedule the appointment and select this command. AutoSched Overlap : This command is enabled only when two appointments overlap. Click an appointment and select this command to display the Auto Scheduler Date Range window. In this window, you can set a date range within which to auto schedule the patient appointment. Shorten : Click an appointment and select this command to shorten the appointment by one time unit. Lengthen : Click an appointment and select this command to lengthen the appointment by one time unit. Zoom : Click an appointment and select this command to display the Assistant window. In this window, you can view additional patient and appointment information. You can also access this information from the Zoom appointment icon. Monitor KIOSK Check-In: Displays the Monitor KIOSK Check-In window. Use

topics.title: eForms
topics.body: eForms What's New in Version 14.0 eForms The eForms module uses electronic forms that eliminate the need for paper copies of documents used in your office every day. Using the eForms module, you can move closer to the goal of becoming a paperless office, as it eliminates the need for filling out paper forms and then entering the data into the patient's record via keyboard or scanner. Patients fill out and update forms electronically, and patients' signatures are captured electronically. This information is then added to the database with minimal effort on the part of the staff. To enroll in eForms, contact your Aftermarket Sales representative. See Using the eForms Module for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: eReminders
topics.body: eReminders What's New in Version 14.0 eReminders eReminders is an appointment reminder service that automatically contacts patients due for an appointment using the patient's preferred contact type (phone call, e-mail, or text message). After the appointment/reminder contact is made, the appointment information is automatically updated, enabling you to spend more time helping patients in the office. To enroll in the service, contact your eServices representative. See Using the eReminders Service for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Following Up on Electronic Claims
topics.body: Following Up on Electronic Claims eServices Following Up on Electronic Claims To check the status of an unpaid electronic claim: Review your transmission summary reports and verify that the claim was sent electronically. Review your daily reports and check for rejected claims, requests for additional information, or error messages. Check the payer ID number. If you find an error, enter the correct number and then cancel and resubmit the claim. Check the group number. If you find an error, enter the correct number and then cancel and resubmit the claim. Check the status of the claim online. Contact the insurance company and provide the following information: Verification that the claim was sent electronically Insured party�s first and last names Insured party�s insurance ID number For BlueCross BlueShield patients, the member number Patient�s first and last names Date of service Amount of claim Contact an eServices support representative and provide the following information: Your SoftDent account number Insured party�s first and last names Insured party�s insurance ID number For BlueCross BlueShield patients, the member number Patient�s first and last names Date of service Date the claim was submitted electronically Amount of claim Patient control number, which is a 17-digit number found on the Transmission Summary or Transmission Log reports Support representatives cannot check the status of claims that are more than 60 days old. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the eVerifications Claim Status Service
topics.body: Using the eVerifications Claim Status Service eServices > Using the eVerifications Service Using the eVerifications Claim Status Service The eVerifications Claim Status Service e nables you to check the payment status of an insurance claim. To enroll, contact your sales representative. To use the claim status service: Select an option: In the Treatment Plan List window : Select the item, and select eClaim Status from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Claim List window : Select the claim and select eClaim Status from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Claim window : Select eClaim Status from the Options section of the Express bar. The Claim Status Response window is displayed. You can also click eClaim Status or press I to access the service from the Claim List or Claim windows. To print the claim status information, click the Printer button. A copy of the response is saved as a patient document and can be displayed as a claim document. To view or print the claim document, see Viewing Claim Documents . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the eClaims Service
topics.body: Using the eClaims Service eServices Using the eClaims Service Before using the eClaims Service, you must: Set up your insurance companies Submit claims to the electronic batch Following transmission, eServices downloads the following reports: eClaims Transmission Summary : Lists all claims transmitted in the current session. Transmission Log : Lists all claims from the previous transmission that have been sent to the clearinghouse or payers. Review this report carefully; file processing errors are listed here. Account Balance Summary : Displays the current balance and history of your eServices account. Account statements are not mailed, so monitor this report frequently. If your account is overdrawn, you cannot submit claims or statements electronically. To avoid interruption of services, you can set up automatic credit card or check payments. Statement Audit : Lists the patients whose statements were printed and mailed at the clearinghouse. If an asterisk is displayed next to a patient name, the statement was not printed and mailed; this is typically due to an invalid mailing address. Claim Status reports : Include claim status, claim rejections, claim acceptances, and requests for additional information. These reports are generated by clearinghouses and insurance companies. R equests for Additional Information and Claim Settlement reports : Consist of messages returned by insurance companies requesting information or indicating that a claim has been paid. Some insurance companies send these reports by mail, rather than electronically. Related Topics Setting Up Insurance Companies Submitting Claims Sending Claims Suspending and Deleting Claims Transmitting Claims Automatically Printing Reports Using the Real Time Claim Submission Service Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the ePayments Service
topics.body: Using the ePayments Service eServices Using the ePayments Service The ePayments Service enables you to accept credit card payments using: SoftDent Card swiper The following are required to use this feature: Modem (if the Internet is not available) Telephone line or Internet line Mag-Tek card swiper To enroll, contact an eServices representative. These instructions are for the ePayments Service (version 1). If you are using ePayments Secure (version 2), see Using ePayments Secure for more information. Related Topics Setting Up Security Rights Installing the Card Swiper Setting Up the Modem Enabling Credit Card Processing by Provider Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Using a Card Swiper Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Manually Posting a Payment to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers Posting a Refund to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers Processing Transactions Offline Issuing Credit Card Refunds Adding Credit Card Payments Setting Options for Printing Receipts Printing Settlement Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Accounts
topics.body: Editing Accounts Setting Up Accounts Editing Accounts To edit an existing account record: Select an option: From the Main Menu bar : Select List > Account . The Account List window is displayed. Double-click the account from the list. From the patient chart: Click the link in the In Account section of the patient banner. The Account window is displayed. Make the changes and click OK . If you change the account guarantor�s address or phone number, you are prompted to update the information for all other patients in the account. Tip In any window, press Ctrl + A or select Account List Window from the Power bar to access the Account List window. In the Patient List and Patient windows, select Other Management > View Account to display the account. In the Inoffice window, select an appointment and select Account to display the account. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Allowance Tables
topics.body: Editing Allowance Tables Setting Up Insurance > Using Allowance Tables Editing Allowance Tables To edit an allowance table: Select List > Allowance Table . The Allowance Table List window is displayed. Select a table from the list, and click View Codes . The codes for the selected table are displayed in the Allowance Table List window. Select an option: To add a code to the table : Click Add . An allowance table entry window is displayed. Select the code, and type the allowed amount. Select Upgraded Procedure (write-off amount applied to patient balance) to add the write-off amount to the Pat. Pays amount. Click OK . To edit the fee for an existing code : Double-click the fee. An allowance table entry window is displayed. Type the new fee. Select Upgraded Procedure (write-off amount applied to patient balance) to add the write-off amount to the Pat. Pays amount. Click OK . To copy code information from another table : Select Copy From Existing Table . The Allowance Table window is displayed. Copy From Existing Table is selected. Select the table and click OK . To copy the codes from fee0 : Select the code and select Copy From Fee0 . Tip Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Annotation Text and Notes
topics.body: Editing Annotation Text and Notes Handling Images While Charting > Using Annotation Mode Editing Annotation Text and Notes To edit an annotation text or note: On any tab in the Chart for window, select the image to annotate on the Clinical Images tab in the Clinical Detail Container, right-click, and select Annotate . Select buttons on the Image Annotate toolbar . Select Annotation Text Tool s. To specify the text or note text color, click the color box in the Attributes section. The Color window is displayed. Select the color and click OK . Select the text or note, right-click, and select Edit Text . Type the correct text and click OK . To hide text and note annotations, deselect Show Annotations . Select Save Annotations . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Appointment Card Information
topics.body: Editing Appointment Card Information Scheduling Appointments > Editing Appointments Editing Appointment Card Information To edit procedures, tooth and surface numbers, providers, and operatory information for a scheduled appointment: Select an option: From the Daily Appointments window : Select an appointment, and click Edit Appt . From the Book At A Glance window : Select an appointment and click Edit Appt . From the Patient List window : Select a patient and select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select an appointment, and click Edit Appt . From the Patient window : Select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select an appointment, and click Edit Appt . The Appointment Card window is displayed. Make the changes. If you add a procedure to an appointment, the time slots and provider assignments are not changed automatically. You must adjust the length of the appointment and the provider assignments manually. Click OK . Tip -- To make a note about an appointment, type the information in the Appt Notes field. A small red triangle is displayed in the upper-right corner of the appointment to alert you that a note exists for the appointment. Place the cursor on the triangle to view the note. -- Click Print Routing Slip to print the patient's routing slip from this window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Appointments
topics.body: Editing Appointments Scheduling Appointments Editing Appointments You can: Edit appointment procedures. Change the scheduled date and time. Change the appointment length. Related Topics Editing Appointment Card Information Editing Appointment Date, Time, or Operatory Changing the Length of Appointments Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Books
topics.body: Editing Books Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Books Editing Books You can change the book title, book hours, rooms, and room colors at any time. To edit the book: Select Scheduler > Setup Books . The Scheduler Books List window is displayed. Select an option: To edit a book : Select it and click Edit . The book information is displayed in the Scheduler Books window. Make changes and click OK . To change the room or color : Select a book and select Assign Rooms from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Scheduler Book Definition Wizard is displayed. Make the changes and click Close twice . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Bluebook Entries
topics.body: Editing Bluebook Entries Setting Up Insurance > Using the Insurance Bluebook Editing Bluebook Entries The insurance plan�s estimation type dictates which fields are displayed when you open the Bluebook entries for window. If you change a code�s fee schedule 0 amount in the ADA/Transaction Codes window, all bluebook entries associated with the code are automatically updated. The insurance payment amount must be less than the allowed amount. To edit a bluebook entry: Select List > Bluebook . The Bluebook entries for Plan window is displayed. Select a plan from the Plan drop-down list. Click Edit . The Bluebook entries for window is displayed in EDIT mode. If you are editing a percentage, percentage PPO, or Medicare percentage plan, select an option: Type a new insurance payment amount in the Insurance Payment field : The Allowed Amount field is automatically populated with the new payment amount divided by the percentage. Type a new amount in the Allowed Amount field : The Insurance Payment field is automatically populated with the lowest amount: either the code�s fee schedule 0 amount or the allowed amount multiplied by the percentage covered value. Type a new percent in the Percent Covered field : The Insurance Payment field is automatically populated with the lowest amount: either the code�s fee schedule 0 amount or the allowed amount multiplied by the percentage covered value. If you are editing a flat fee plan, type a new insurance payment amount in the Insurance Payment field. If you are editing a DMO/Capitation or Medicaid plan, select an option: Type a new insurance payment amount in the Insurance Payment field : The Copay field is automatically populated with the plan fee amount, minus the insurance payment amount. Type a new plan fee amount in the Plan Fee field : The Copay field is automatically populated with the plan fee amount, minus the insurance payment amount. If you are editing a flat fee PPO or flat fee Medicare plan, select an option: Type a new insurance payment amount in the Insurance Payment field : The Copay field is automatically populated with the allowed amount, minus the insurance payment amount. Type a new allowed amount in the Allowed Amount field : The Copay field is automatically populated with the plan fee amount, minus the insurance payment amount. Select Apply to Deductible to apply the amount to the patient's deductible. Select Upgraded Procedure (write-off amount applied to patient balance) to add the write-off amount (the difference between the allowed amount and the fee) to the Pat. Pays amount. Type any notes in the Notes field. If an appointment is made for this procedure and for a patient covered by this plan, the note is displayed in the Assistant. Click OK . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + B to access the Bluebook entries for Plan window. In the Insurance Plan List or Insurance Plan windows, select Bluebook to display the Bluebook entries for window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Payments in a Credit Card Payment Plan
topics.body: Editing Payments in a Credit Card Payment Plan Editing Payments in a Credit Card Payment Plan Using the shortcut menu in the Credit Card Payment Plan�Details window, you can edit individual credit card payments in a payment plan. You cannot edit credit card payments for a payment plan that is linked to a budget plan. Options for editing payments include: Edit Amount To edit a credit card payment amount, follow these steps: In the Credit Card Payment Plan�Details window, click the CC Payment tab. Select the payment you want to change and right-click. The shortcut menu is displayed. Select Edit Amount . The Edit Payment Amount window is displayed. In the New Amount field, type the new amount. Click OK . The selected payment is updated with the new amount, and the Unallocated text at the bottom of the window is updated. Edit Date To edit a credit card payment date, follow these steps: In the Credit Card Payment Plan�Details window, click the CC Payment tab. Select the payment you want to change and right-click. The shortcut menu is displayed. Select Edit Date . The Edit Payment Date window is displayed. In the New Authorization Date field, type the new date. The new date must occur after today's date and must be within the previous payment and next payment date range. If you are changing the date of the first payment, the date you type must be after today�s date and before the next payment date. If you are changing the last payment date, the date must be after today�s date and after the previous payment. Click OK . The selected payment is updated with the new date. Split Amount To split a credit card payment amount, follow these steps: In the Credit Card Payment Plan�Details window, click the CC Payment tab. Select the payment you want to split and right-click. The shortcut menu is displayed. Select Split Amount . A new payment is inserted after the selected payment, and the original payment amount is split between the two payments. All other payments are advanced one month. Add Payment To add a credit card payment, follow these steps: In the Credit Card Payment Plan�Details window, click the CC Payment tab. Select the payment that should come before the new payment and right-click. The shortcut menu is displayed. Select Add Payment . A payment is added below the selected payment. It is dated to occur one month from the selected payment, and the authorization amount defaults to the amount of the selected payment. The authorization days for all payments below the newly added payment are advanced one month. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Codes
topics.body: Editing Codes Handling Transactions and Codes Editing Codes You must have security rights to edit or delete codes. To edit an existing code: Select List > ADA Codes . The ADA/Transaction Code List window is displayed. Select the code and click Edit . The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window is displayed. Make changes and click OK . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + C to access the ADA/CPT/Transaction Code List window. Related Topics Inactivating Codes Deleting Codes Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Generic Clinical Notes
topics.body: Editing Generic Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes > Handling Generic Clinical Notes Editing Generic Clinical Notes To edit a generic clinical note: Select an option: Select List > Clinical Notes > Generic Clinical Notes . In the patient chart, from the Overview, Restorative, Perio, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tabs, select Clinical Notes > Generic Notes . The Generic Clinical Notes List window is displayed. Select the note, and click Edit . The note is displayed in the Clinical Note window. Make the changes and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Color Group Definitions
topics.body: Editing Color Group Definitions Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Color Scheduling Editing Color Group Definitions To change a color group definition: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select Set Up Color Groups from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Scheduling Color Definitions window is displayed. Select a group definition and click Edit . Select an option: To change the minimum or maximum codes : Select the ADA or CPT codes from the Min Code and Max Code drop-down lists. To change the minimum or maximum dollar amounts : Type the price ranges in the Min $ and Max $ fields. To use the highest and lowest dollar amounts for the code range : Click Set $ Range on the toolbar. The lowest and highest dollar values for that code range are displayed in the Min $ and Max $ fields. To change the assigned color : Select Change Color from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Change Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Contacts
topics.body: Editing Contacts Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts Editing Contacts To edit a contact: Select an option: To edit a contact for a patient record : In the Patient List window, select the patient. To edit a contact for an insurance plan record : In the Insurance Plan List window, select the plan. To edit a contact for a referring doctor record : In the REFERRING DR List window, select the doctor. To edit a contact for a provider record : In the Provider List window, select the provider. To edit a contact for an employer record : In the Employer List window, select the employer. To edit a contact for an insurance company record : In the Insurance Company List window, select the company. To edit a contact for a school record : In the School List window, select the school. To edit a contact for a laboratory record : In the Labs List window, select the laboratory. To edit a contact for a pharmacy record : In the Pharmacy List window, select the pharmacy. To edit a contact for an account record : In the Account List window, select the account. To edit a contact for a claim record: In the Claim List window, select the claim. To edit a contact for a completed claim record: In the Completed Claim List window, select the claim. Select Contacts > By Date or Contacts > By Type from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Contacts for List window is displayed. Double-click a contact and make the changes. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Conditions
topics.body: Editing Conditions Handling Patient Medical History > Understanding Medical Conditions Editing Conditions When editing a medical condition, you can only select whether the condition triggers a medical alert. To edit a medical condition: Select System > Customize Medical History . The Medical Conditions List window is displayed. Select a condition and click Edit . The condition details are displayed in the Medical Conditions window. Select an option: To trigger a medical alert when the condition is added to a patient�s medical history : Select Should This Condition Set The Patient�s Medical Alert Flag? . To no longer trigger a medical alert when the condition is added to a patient�s medical history : Deselect Should This Condition Set The Patient�s Medical Alert Flag? . Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Appointment Date, Time, or Operatory
topics.body: Editing Appointment Date, Time, or Operatory Scheduling Appointments > Editing Appointments Editing Appointment Date, Time, or Operatory To change the date, time, or operatory for an appointment, select an option: From the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows : Drag the appointment to the appropriate time slot or operatory. From the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows : Select the appointment to move, and click Cancel . Click a time slot for the appointment and click Resched . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Document Descriptions
topics.body: Editing Document Descriptions Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Editing Document Descriptions To edit the description for account, patient, referring doctor, claim, insurance company, insurance plan, and lab case documents: In the Documents for window, select the document in the tree, right-click, and select Edit Document Description from the menu. The Edit Document Description window is displayed. Type the description in the New Document Description field, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Employer Information
topics.body: Editing Employer Information Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Employer Information Editing Employer Information To edit employer information: Select List > Employer . The Employer List window is displayed. Select the employer and click Edit . Make the changes and click OK . Click Close . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + E to access the Employer List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Employee Security Information in the Employee Windows
topics.body: Editing Employee Security Information in the Employee Windows Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Managing Employee Attendance > Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides Editing Employee Security Information in the Employee Windows Before an employee can access the software, you must assign a user name and password and assign system security permissions. When you add a new employee, enter this information in the Rights by User or User by Rights windows. After entering the employee and security permissions, you can display or edit this information in the Employee List and Employee windows. To display or edit employee security settings, do one of the following: In the Employee List window, select an employee and select Security . In the Employee window, select Security . The Rights By User window is displayed. To access System Security functions, you need the Modify System Security permission. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing a Facility Record
topics.body: Editing a Facility Record Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using Other Facilities Editing a Facility Record To edit a facility record: Select List > Other Facility . The Other Facility List window is displayed. Select the other facility, and click Edit . The Other Facility window is displayed. Make your changes, and click OK . The Other Facility List window is displayed. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Medical Histories Entered on the Current Date
topics.body: Editing Medical Histories Entered on the Current Date Handling Patient Medical History > Updating Medical Histories Editing Medical Histories Entered on the Current Date If you entered a medical history today and need to edit it: In the Patient List window, select a patient and select Clinical Management > Patient Medical History from the Options menu in the Express bar. OR In the patient chart, hover over the Medical Conditions, Allergies, or Medicines section in the patient banner, and click the link. The Medical History window is displayed. The medical history information for the patient is displayed. The current date is displayed in the Medical History Recorded On field. Click Edit located next to the Medical History Record On field. The Physician�s Name field is highlighted. Make changes and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Holidays
topics.body: Editing Holidays Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Holidays Editing Holidays To edit a holiday: Select Scheduler > Holidays . The Setup Holidays window is displayed. Select a holiday, and click Edit . Make changes and click OK . A warning is displayed when you attempt to schedule an appointment on a day defined as a holiday; you can, however, override the system settings and schedule the appointment. A warning is displayed if an appointment is already scheduled on a day specified as a holiday. You must either reschedule or cancel the appointment. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Images
topics.body: Editing Images Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Editing Images If you use Trophy or CS Imaging software, the images are displayed in that software. Annotate and save the image in the software before returning to SoftDent. To edit clinical images: In the Documents for window, select the document from the Portrait Image or Clinical image section of the tree, right-click, and select Edit from the menu. The Edit Image window is displayed. Select an option: To flip the image horizontally : Click Horizontally . To flip the image vertically : Click Vertically . To rotate the image by ninety degrees : Click 90 Degrees . To view the mirror image : Click Mirror . To reload the original image : Select Reload Image from the Edit Image toolbar. Select Save and click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing an Insurance Company
topics.body: Editing an Insurance Company Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Company Information Editing an Insurance Company To edit an insurance company: Select List > Ins. Co . The Insurance Co List window is displayed. Double-click the insurance company from the list. Make changes and click OK . Click Close . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + I to access the Insurance Company List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Insurance Plans
topics.body: Editing Insurance Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Editing Insurance Plans To edit insurance plan information: Select List > Ins. Plan . The Insurance Plan List window is displayed. Double-click an insurance plan in the list. The insurance plan is displayed in the Insurance Plan window. Make changes and click OK . If a history file is associated with the insurance plan, HIST is displayed in red text at the bottom of the Information, Coverage, and Claim Filing tabs. Click Close twice . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + P or select List Insurance Plans from the Power bar to access the Insurance Plan List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing a Clinical Condition
topics.body: Editing a Clinical Condition Restorative Charting > Using Clinical Conditions Editing a Clinical Condition To edit a clinical condition: Select List > Clinical Conditions . The Clinical Conditions List window is displayed. You cannot edit the clinical conditions provided with the software. Select a condition and click Edit . The Clinical Conditions window is displayed. Edit the condition description. Click OK . You cannot delete clinical conditions. Mark unused conditions inactive. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Images
topics.body: Editing Images Handling Images While Charting > Using Edit Mode Editing Images To rotate or flip clinical images: On the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select Edit Mode . Select buttons on the Clinical Imaging toolbar . Select images in the Clinical Detail Container. Select and drag the image from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. The image is displayed. Select an option: To flip the image horizontally : Click Horizontally . To flip the image vertically : Click Vertically . To rotate the image by ninety degrees : Click 90 Degrees . To view the mirror image : Click Mirror . To reload the original image : Select Reload Image . To convert a non-Carestream image to a dental imaging software, version 6.0, image : Right-click the image in the Clinical Detail Tab Container and select Convert to Clinical Image . A red border is displayed around an image that can be converted. Click Save . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Predefined User-Selected Reports
topics.body: Editing Predefined User-Selected Reports Handling Reports > Generating User-Selected Reports Editing Predefined User-Selected Reports To edit a report that has previously been created: Select Reports > Patient > User-Selected or Reports > Account > User-Selected . The User-Selected window is displayed. Select the title of the report you want to edit, and click Edit Only . The User-Selected report title window is displayed. Change the report name, if necessary. Click OK . The Selection Criteria window is displayed. Edit the report criteria, if necessary, and click OK . The software prompts you to save any report changes. Click Yes . See Creating User-Selected Reports . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Edit Mode
topics.body: Using Edit Mode Handling Images While Charting Using Edit Mode When the Clinical Imaging tab is displayed in edit mode, you can: Edit clinical images Save modifications Reload the image The Image Editor toolbar is displayed when this mode is selected. To view the tab in annotation mode, select Edit Mode on the Clinical Imaging toolbar . Related Topics Viewing Images in Edit Mode Editing Images Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Lab Information
topics.body: Editing Lab Information Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Labs Editing a Lab To edit lab information: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Labs . The Labs List window is displayed. Double-click the lab from the list. Click Labs List on the Power Bar : The Labs List window is displayed. Double-click the lab from the list. See Using the Power Bar for more information. In any window : Press Ctrl + L. The Labs List window is displayed. Double-click the lab from the list. The lab record is displayed in the Labs window. Make the changes and click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Patient Records
topics.body: Editing Patient Records Setting Up Patients Editing Patient Records To edit an existing patient record: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the Power Bar : Select Patient List Window . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the Account List or Account window : Select Patients from the Options menu of the Express bar or the Options menu. A Patient List window containing all of the patients associated with the selected account is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the InOffice window : Select Patient from the Options menu of the Express bar. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the patient chart: Hover over the patient name on the patient banner and click the link. In any window : Press Ctrl + O. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. The patient is displayed in the Patient window. Make changes and click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing a Pharmacy
topics.body: Editing a Pharmacy Pharmacies and Prescriptions > Handling Pharmacies Editing a Pharmacy To edit an existing pharmacy record: Select an option: Select List > Pharmacy : The Pharmacy List window is displayed. Double-click the pharmacy in the list. Click Pharmacy List Window on the Power Bar : The Pharmacy List window is displayed. Double-click the pharmacy in the list. The pharmacy record is displayed in the Pharmacy window. Make the appropriate changes and click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Patient Questionnaires
topics.body: Editing Patient Questionnaires Handling Patient Questionnaires Editing Patient Questionnaires You can edit patient questionnaires after they have been created and after responses have been entered into the software. To edit a patient questionnaire: Select List > Patient Questionnaire . The Patient Questionnaire List window is displayed in FIND mode. Double-click the questionnaire. Select an option: To add an existing question to the questionnaire : Select the question from the Questions section and click Selected in the Add >>>> section. To add a new question : Click Add Question . See Using the Questionnaire Editor . To edit a question : Select the question from the Questions Selected section and click Edit Question . If the question is the parent of a dependent (child) question, the dependency will be broken when you edit the parent question. The Question window is displayed. Make the changes and click OK . If the meaning of the question is changed, tabulated responses for the original question are retained and can be viewed in the Questionnaire Results window. INACTIVE is displayed in the Status column of the original question. If the meaning of the question is not changed, tabulated responses for both questions are stored together. You cannot edit questionnaires that contain incomplete response sets in the database. A list of patient records that contain incomplete responses is displayed. To remove a question : Select it from the Questions Selected section and click Remove . Any dependencies for the questionnaire are broken when a question is removed. To view or edit existing dependencies : Select the appropriate child question and click View . The Question Dependency window is displayed. Make the changes and click OK . The dependency information is displayed in the DEP column located in the Questions Selected section. To set a question dependency : Select the parent question from the Questions Selected section, press Ctrl, select the child question, and click Make in the Dependencies section. The Question Dependency window is displayed. Select the appropriate option and click OK . The dependency information is displayed in the DEP column located in the Questions Selected section. To remove a dependency : Select the child question from Questions Selected section and click Remove in the Dependencies section. To change the display order of a question : Select the question from the Questions Selected section of the window and click Top , Bottom , Up1 , or Dwn1 . Any dependencies for the questionnaire are broken when a question is moved. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Presentations
topics.body: Editing Presentations Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Handling Case Presentations Editing Presentations To edit a case presentation: In the Documents for window, select the presentation from the Case Presentation Module Presentations section of the tree, right-click, and select Edit Presentation . Microsoft PowerPoint is displayed. Edit the presentation and click Save . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Office Expert Item Prompts
topics.body: Editing Office Expert Item Prompts Using Experts > Using the Office Expert > Setting Up Office Expert Items Editing Office Expert Item Prompts You can edit the criteria for an office expert item prompt. If the security system is enabled, you must have the appropriate permission to access this feature. If the proper permissions are not set and you want to edit the criteria of an office expert item, see your software administrator. To edit office expert item prompts: From the Experts menu, select Office Expert . The Office Expert window is displayed. The Statements To Print, Yesterday�s Login Failures, and Yesterday�s Added Rx Count items do not have corresponding prompts. Select an option: To edit an item option in the Office Expert window : Select an item and select Edit Expert Options from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. To edit an item option in the Office Expert Setup window : Select the item and click Edit Prompts . A prompt is displayed. For a complete list of item prompts, see Using the Office Expert . Type the appropriate criteria, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Provider Information
topics.body: Editing Provider Information Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Provider Information Editing Provider Information To edit provider information: Select List > Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. Select the provider and click Edit . Make the changes and click OK . Click Close . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + D to access the Provider List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Incomplete Questionnaire Response Sets
topics.body: Editing Incomplete Questionnaire Response Sets Handling Patient Questionnaires > Entering Patient Questionnaire Responses Editing Incomplete Questionnaire Response Sets When a complete response set for a questionnaire is not entered , t he questionnaire results are saved, and Incomplete is displayed in the Complete? column in the Patient Questionnaire Results List window. To complete a questionnaire: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Other Patient Management > Patient Questionnaire from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Other Patient Management > Patient Questionnaire from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Patient Questionnaire Results List window is displayed. Select Edit an Incomplete Test from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Questionnaire Responses for window is displayed. Select the answer and click Next . Click OK . You cannot edit a questionnaire if there are incomplete response sets associated with it. A warning and a list of patients whose results are incomplete are displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Recall Postcards
topics.body: Editing Recall Postcards Recalling Patients > Setting Up Recalls Editing Recall Postcards You can customize the messages that print on your first, second, and third recall postcards for your normal and defined recall system. You can also edit the text for postcards not using motivated recall. To edit the text on recall postcards: Select System > Change System Settings > Customize Postcards > Recall Postcards . The Edit Recall Postcards window is displayed. Click the tab that corresponds to the text you want to edit. Select the text you want to change, and type the new message. You can type up to 8 lines of text on the postcard. Click OK . Run the Update Client Files Utility ( System > Update Client Files ) on all workstations to access your changes. Related Job Aid: Creating Recall Postcards Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing a Record
topics.body: Editing a Record Getting Started > Using List Windows > Using Toolbar Options in a List Window Editing a Record To edit a record in a list window: Select the record and click Edit. Type the modifications and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing And Deleting Referring Doctor Information
topics.body: Editing And Deleting Referring Doctor Information Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals > Handling Referring Doctor Records Editing and Deleting Referring Doctor Information To edit or delete referring doctor information: Select an option: Select List > Referring Dr . In any window, press Ctrl + F. Click List Referring Providers on the Power Bar. The REFERRING DR List window is displayed. Select the referring doctor and click Edit . The REFERRING DR window is displayed in EDIT mode. Make the changes and click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Reminder Memos
topics.body: Editing Reminder Memos Contacts and Memos > Using Memos Editing Reminder Memos To edit a reminder memo: Select List > Memos . The Memos List window is displayed. Select the memo, and click Edit . The Memo window is displayed. Make the changes, and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Reminder Postcards
topics.body: Editing Reminder Postcards Editing Reminder Postcards You can customize the messages that print on your reminder postcards. To edit the text on reminder postcards: Select System > Change System Settings > Customize Postcards > Reminder Postcards . The Edit Reminder Postcards window is displayed. Select the text you want to change, and type the new message. You can type up to 8 lines of text on the postcard. To insert the appointment date or time in the message, position the cursor where the date or time should display, select the merge field in the Placeholders section, and click Insert . Click OK . Run the Update Client Files Utility ( System > Update Client Files ) on all workstations to access your changes. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing School Information
topics.body: Editing School Information Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling School Information Editing School Information To edit school information: Select List > School . The School List window is displayed. Select a school and click Edit . Make the changes and click OK . C lick Close . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + T to access the School List window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing an E-mail Signature
topics.body: Editing an E-mail Signature Using E-mail and WebLink > Sending an E-mail Editing an E-mail Signature By default, a confidentiality notice and liability disclaimer are appended to all e-mail messages in the signature portion of an e-mail. You can edit the e-mail signature to suit the needs of your office. You need to modify the signature text only once. To edit the text in an e-mail signature, follow these steps: Select I nternet > E-Mail Signature . The E-Mail Signature window is displayed. Modify the existing confidentiality and disclaimer statements as necessary. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Employee Information
topics.body: Editing Employee Information Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Managing Employee Attendance Editing Employee Information After adding a new employee, you can edit the following information: Employee Name User Name Default Daybook Hire Date Status Pay Frequency To edit employee information: Select List > Employees . The Employees List window is displayed. Double-click an employee. The Employee window is displayed. Edit the information. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing an ePostings Adjustment Code
topics.body: Editing an ePostings Adjustment Code eServices > Using the ePostings Service > Using the ePostings Adjustments Window Editing an ePostings Adjustment Code To edit an ePostings adjustment code: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > ePostings > ePostings Adjustments . The ePostings Adjustments list window is displayed. Select the code you want to change. Click Edit . The ePostings Adjustments window is displayed. Make your changes. Click OK . Click Close . Tip To print the ePostings Adjustment Actions report, click Print , select an output option, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating and Editing ADA Code Presentation Links
topics.body: Creating and Editing ADA Code Presentation Links Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint Creating and Editing ADA Code Presentation Links To create or remove a link between a presentation and an ADA code: From the PowerPoint software, select Tools > Edit ADA Procedure Presentation Links . The Edit links between SoftDent ADA Codes and their PowerPoint presentations window is displayed. The case presentation icon is displayed next to codes that are associated with presentations. Select an option: To create a link between a presentation and an ADA code : Select the code and click Make Link . The Select a Presentation window is displayed. Select the presentation and click OK . To remove a link between a presentation and ADA code : Select the code and click Break Link . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Patient Clinical Notes
topics.body: Editing Patient Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes > Handling Patient Clinical Notes Editing Patient Clinical Notes You can edit a saved patient clinical note, provided you are editing the note on the day it was created and you have the proper security permissions. To edit a saved clinical note: While a patient�s chart is displayed in the Chart for window, do one of the following: Click the Notes tab. Select Clinical Notes > View Clinical Notes. C lick Add a descriptive note in the main Charting toolbar. The Patient Clinical Notes List window is displayed. Select the note to edit and click Edit Note Text . The current note text is displayed in the field at the bottom of the Info tab. Edit or add to the note text in the field. Select Reviewed by Dr. to indicate the doctor has seen this note. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Edit Recall Postcards Utility
topics.body: Running the Edit Recall Postcards Utility Utilities > Running Customize Menu Utilities Running the Edit Recall Postcards Utility Run t his utility to create and edit the text for the first, second, and third recall notices for your normal and user-defined recall system. You can also edit the text for postcards not using motivated recall. To edit recall postcard text: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Customize > Edit Recall Postcards . The Edit Recall Postcards window is displayed. Click the tab that corresponds to the letter to edit. Type up to 8 lines of text on the postcard. To edit text, select it, and type over the existing text. Click OK . Restart the software. Related Topics Editing Recall Postcards Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Referral Categories
topics.body: Editing Referral Categories Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals > Handling Referring Doctor Records Editing Referral Categories To edit referral categories: Select System > Referral Categories (1). The REFERRAL CATEGORIES list window is displayed. Double-click the category you want to change, or select the category and click Edit . The REFERRAL CATEGORIES window is displayed in EDIT mode. Type your changes and click OK . Click Close . It is recommended that you do not delete a referral category. If you attempt to delete a category, a message is displayed, suggesting that you mark the category Inactive instead. Related Topics Creating Referral Categories Running Referral Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing TeamTalk Note Conditions
topics.body: Editing TeamTalk Note Conditions Using TeamTalk > Using TeamTalk Options Editing TeamTalk Note Conditions To edit a TeamTalk Note condition: Select TeamTalk > General TeamTalk Lists . The TeamTalk - Notes window is displayed. Select the database from the List Notes for drop-down list. Select the note and click Edit . The TeamTalk window is displayed. The number of conditions that are associated with the note is displayed next to the Extra Settings button. Click Extra Settings . Click Edit . Make the changes and click Next . A message asks if you want to add another condition. To add another condition, click Yes and repeat this step. When you are finished adding conditions, click No . Review your conditions for accuracy, and click Finish . For a list of database conditions, see Understanding TeamTalk Conditions . Click Save . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing TeamTalk Notes
topics.body: Editing TeamTalk Notes Using TeamTalk > Using TeamTalk Options Editing TeamTalk Notes To view your TeamTalk notes: Select TeamTalk > General TeamTalk Lists . The TeamTalk - Notes window is displayed. To view all of the notes for a database, select the database from the List Notes for drop-down list. Select the note you want to change and click Edit . You can also add new notes from this window. Click Add and see Setting Up TeamTalk Notes from the TeamTalk Window . Make the changes and click Save . For more information, see Setting Up TeamTalk Notes from the TeamTalk Window . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing a User Login
topics.body: Editing a User Login System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Editing a User Login You can edit user names and passwords to increase or decrease the tasks that can be performed by the user, or assign the user to a different template. You cannot delete users, but you can recycle the user identification number and re-assign it or change the name and password. For a complete list of tasks by category, see Tasks with Limited Access . To edit a user login: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Select the user name and click Edit . The Rights By User window is displayed. Make the changes and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Editing Website Addresses
topics.body: Editing Website Addresses Using E-mail and WebLink > Accessing the Internet Editing Website Addresses To edit a website or change a note: Select Internet > Other Web Sites . The Web Sites List window is displayed. Select the site and click Edit . The Web Sites window is displayed. Make the edits to the Site Name or Notes field, and click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: eForms
topics.body: eForms System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security > Tasks with Limited Access eForms Tasks can be accessed from the following windows: Users by Rights: To access the Users By Rights window, select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Users by Rights . The Users By Rights window is displayed. Rights By User: To access the Rights by User window, select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Select the user name and click Edit . The Rights By User window is displayed. Security Settings - Security Overrides: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Security Overrides . The Security Overrides menu is displayed. The table describes each limited access task for the eForms category. Category Task Description eForms Review eForms Enables the user to review eForms. eForms Modify eForms Templates Enables the user to modify eForms templates. eForms Configure eForms Enables the user to configure eForms. eForms Add/Remove eForms for Appointment Enables the user to add or remove eForms for appointments. eForms Send/Receive eForms Enables the user to send or receive eForms. eForms Modify Patient eForms Setting Enables the user to modify patient eForms settings. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: eForms Security
topics.body: eForms Security eServices > Using the eForms Module eForms Security An eForms security option is in the Rights by User window. The following 6 eForms functions are security controlled: Review eForms Modify eForms Templates Configure eForms Add/Remove eForms for Appointment Send/Receive eForms Modify Patient eForm Setting All items are enabled for the User ID 1 (DOCTOR/DOCTOR12) user. For all other users, the Modify eForms Templates and Configure eForms items are disabled by default. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Sending an E-mail to Confirm an Appointment
topics.body: Sending an E-mail to Confirm an Appointment Sending an E-mail to Confirm an Appointment To send an e-mail appointment confirmation: In the Daily Appointments or By Book windows, select the appointment and select Confirm Appt . The Confirm Appt for window is displayed. Tip To carbon copy or blind carbon copy a recipient, type an e-mail in the CC or BCC field. Select an e-mail address in the Contact Info section of the window and click E-mail . The SoftDent E-Mail window is displayed. Type the e-mail subject and message. To attach a document or image, select the object and click Add . Click OK (Send) . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: E-mailing CS Imaging Software Images
topics.body: E-mailing CS Imaging Software Images Using E-mail and WebLink > Sending an E-mail E-mailing CS Imaging Software Images When e-mailing a CS Imaging software image from SoftDent, the following options are available: E-mailing the image and including a link to the downloadable imaging viewer E-mailing the image and attaching the viewer directly to the e-mail Converting the image to JPG format before e-mailing it Embedding annotations in the JPG image Tip Images in JPG format can be viewed in a variety of software applications. Related Topics Sending the CS Imaging Software Image Viewer to E-mail Recipients Converting CS Imaging Software Images to JPG Format Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: E-mailing Charts
topics.body: E-mailing Charts Using the SoftDent Charting Module E-mailing Charts You can e-mail a chart from the charting module or practice management software. You can include any patient images or documents that have been saved using the document manager. A 3D volume cannot be e-mailed, due to its size. To e-mail charts or images associated with a patient record: Select an option: On the Restorative, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tabs when a patient�s chart is displayed in the Chart for window : Select E-mail this patient�s chart from the Common toolbar. On the Restorative tab when a patient�s chart is displayed in the Chart for window : Select Chart > Patient Documents and Images . The Documents window is displayed. Select the image, and click E-Mail . On any tab in the Chart for window when a patient�s chart is displayed : Select File > E-Mail Current Chart . On any tab in the Chart for window when a patient�s chart is displayed : Press Ctrl + E. In the Patient List window : Select a patient and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Documents window is displayed. Select the image, and click E-Mail . In the Patient window : Select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Documents window is displayed. Select the image, and click E-Mail . In the Account List window : Select an account and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Documents window is displayed. Select the image, and click E-Mail . In the Account window : Select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Documents window is displayed. Select the image, and click E-Mail . The SoftDent E-Mail window is displayed. The patient�s name and e-mail address are displayed in the Recipient section of the window. Select an option: To send the e-mail to the primary insurance company, primary insurance plan, a lab that has an open lab case, or a referring doctor associated with the patient : Select the party from the Recipient drop-down list. The e-mail address associated with the party is displayed in the Address field. To send the e-mail to another party : Type the recipient�s name in the Name field and the e-mail address in the Address field. Type the information in the Subject and Message fields. Select the document or image to attach to the e-mail and click Add . Place your mouse over an image in the Attached Images section to view details. To remove an image from the Attached Images section, right-click the image and select Remove Image from the submenu. Click OK (Send) . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: E-mailing Contacts
topics.body: E-mailing Contacts Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Handling Contact Expert Tasks > Accessing the Auto Dialer E-mailing Contacts To e-mail a contact: In the Contact Expert window, select the record from the left pane. The contact information is displayed in the right pane. Select an e-mail address from the Auto Dialer drop-down list and click E-mail . The SoftDent E-Mail window is displayed. Type the e-mail subject and message. To attach a document or image, select the object and click Add . Click OK (Send) . Tip To carbon copy or blind carbon copy a recipient, type an e-mail in the CC or BCC field. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: E-mailing Documents
topics.body: E-mailing Documents Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents E-mailing Documents You can use the software to e-mail clinical images, claim documents, patient documents, portrait images, and case presentation module presentations. A 3D volume cannot be e-mailed, due to its size. Tip Before you e-mail documents, set up an E-mail Client outside of the software. To e-mail a document or image: In the Documents for window, select the document or image and click E-Mail , or select the command from the Options section of the Express bar. The SoftDent E-Mail window is displayed. Select an option: To send the e-mail to the primary insurance company, the primary insurance plan, a lab that has an open lab case, or a referring doctor associated with the patient : Select the party from the Recipient drop-down list. To send the e-mail to another party : Type the recipient�s name in the Name field and the e-mail address in the Address field. Type information in the Subject and Message fields. A tree view of all documents, images, and charts associated with this patient is displayed in the Documents and Images section. Place the mouse over an image or document to view details. To attach an additional document or image to the e-mail, select it from the Documents and Images section, and click Add . Place the mouse over an image in the Attached Images section to view details. To remove a document or image from the Attached Images section, right-click the document or image and select Remove Image . To send the e-mail, click OK (Send) . Related Topics E-Mailing Images in an FMS or Context Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: E-mailing from the Document Window
topics.body: E-mailing from the Document Window Using E-mail and WebLink > Sending an E-mail E-mailing from the Document Window To e-mail documents from the Documents window, see E-mailing Documents . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: E-Mailing Images in an FMS or Context
topics.body: E-Mailing Images in an FMS or Context Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents E-Mailing Images in an FMS or Context In both the Documents for window and the SoftDent E-mail window, images belonging to an FMS or context are displayed in a standard document tree. This example uses an FMS, but the software handles images in a context in the same way. To e-mail FMS images in the Documents for window: In the document tree, do one of the following: Click the plus sign in front of the FMS, select an image, and click E-Mail . The SoftDent E-Mail window is displayed. TIP: You can also select an image below the FMS, right-click, and select E-Mail . Select an FMS and click E-Mail. A message is displayed, stating that the FMS is too large to e-mail. Click OK . The SoftDent E-Mail window is displayed. In the Documents and Images section, the document tree is expanded and the FMS is selected. Select the image you want to attach and click Add . Select an option: To send the e-mail to the primary insurance company, the primary insurance plan, a lab that has an open lab case, or a referring doctor associated with the patient : Select the party from the Recipient drop-down list. To send the e-mail to another party : Type the recipient�s name in the Name field and the e-mail address in the Address field. Type information in the Subject and Message fields. A tree view of all documents, images, and charts associated with the patient is displayed in the Documents and Images section. Place the mouse over an image or document to view details. To attach an additional FMS image to the e-mail, select the image from the document tree in the Documents and Images section and click Add . Place the mouse over an image in the Attached Images section to view details. To remove an image from the Attached Images section, right-click the image and select Remove Image . To send the e-mail, click OK (Send) . Related Topics E-mailing Documents Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: E-mailing Presentations
topics.body: E-mailing Presentations Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Handling Case Presentations E-mailing Presentations To e-mail a case presentation: In the Documents for window, select the presentation, and select an option: Right-click and select E-Mail . Select E-Mail from the Options section of the Express bar. The SoftDent E-Mail window is displayed. Select an option: To send the e-mail to the patient, primary insurance company, primary insurance plan, a lab that has an open lab case, or a referring doctor associated with the patient : Select the party from the Recipient drop-down list. To send the e-mail to another party : Type the recipient�s name in the Name field and the e-mail address in the Address field. Type the appropriate information in the Subject and Message fields. A tree view of all documents, images, and charts associated with this patient is displayed in the Documents and Images section. Place the mouse over a presentation to view details.
