Select the presentation to attach to the e-mail and click Add . To remove a presentation from the Attached Images section, right-click the presentation and select Remove Image from the submenu. To attach the PowerPoint viewer to the e-mail, select Attach PowerPoint Viewer . To send the e-mail, click OK (Send) . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Sending E-mails from a Patient Chart
topics.body: Sending E-mails from a Patient Chart Using E-mail and WebLink > Sending an E-mail Sending E-mails from a Patient Chart To send an e-mail from a patient chart, see E-mailing Charts . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Employer List and Employer Window Options
topics.body: Using Employer List and Employer Window Options Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Employer Information Using Employer List and Employer Window Options In the Employer List or Employer window, you can print address labels and envelopes, log a contact, create TeamTalk notes, and view the phone numbers associated with an employer. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: Auto Dialer : Dials a specific employer number. Labels > Label : Prints a label or laser label for the selected employer. Labels > Envelope : Prints a label or laser envelope for the selected employer. TeamTalk : Adds, edits, and deletes TeamTalk notes for the employer; views conditions and deletes expired conditions. Contacts > By Date : Adds, edits, or deletes an employer contact; creates an employer memo. Contacts > By Type : Adds, edits, or deletes an employer contact; creates an employer memo. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using E-mail and WebLink
topics.body: Using E-mail and WebLink Using E-mail and WebLink The following features enable you to use the Internet without leaving the software interface: E-mail WebLink You can attach patient documents to a message and e-mail it to: Patients Pharmacies Other doctors The WebLink feature enables you to record and link to websites associated with your practice. Related Topics Sending an E-mail Message Accessing the Internet Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Managing Employee Attendance
topics.body: Managing Employee Attendance Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card Managing Employee Attendance Use the Employee database to update default daybook and hire date information. A list of all employees in the User Info database is listed in the Employee List window. The identification number, full name, user name, and date of hire for each employee are displayed in the list. In this window, you can search for a specific record and sort the employee records by full name or user name. You can also display or hide inactive records. You can track employee attendance using the Time Card feature. Each time a user adds, edits, or deletes a Time Card entry in the Time Card List or Time Card windows, an audit trail entry is created. You can review these entries in the Audit trail - Time Card Entry List window or print the information after generating the Time Card Audit Trail reports. Using Clock In and Clock Out does not create an audit trail entry. Related Topics Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides Editing Employee Information Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Employer/School Database Events
topics.body: Employer/School Database Events Using TeamTalk > Understanding TeamTalk Events Employer/School Database Events The employer or school record events that can be set up to display a TeamTalk note are: Editing Employer/School : Displays a TeamTalk note when editing an employer or school record. Calling Employer/School : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are calling an employer or school. Viewing Contact List : Displays a TeamTalk note when viewing the contact list associated with an employer or school. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Employer/School Database TeamTalk Conditions
topics.body: Employer/School Database TeamTalk Conditions Using TeamTalk > Understanding TeamTalk Events > Understanding TeamTalk Conditions Employer/School Database TeamTalk Conditions The employer or school record condition that can restrict the event that displays a TeamTalk note is: Name : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more employers or schools. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Employee Database and Time Card
topics.body: Employee Database and Time Card Getting Started Employee Database and Time Card This section includes the following topics: Managing Employee Attendance Using Time Card Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Employers by ID Report
topics.body: Running the Employers by ID Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Employers by ID Report The Employers by ID report generates a list of employers in the database. The list is sorted by identification number. Sample Report For each employer, the report includes: Identification number Name Address Telephone number Contact person ECS ID To run the Employers by ID report: Select Reports > Insurance > Employers By ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Employers By ID Report Setup window is displayed. Select the first and last employer identification number to include in the report from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Employer Plans
topics.body: Employer Plans Trojan > Handling Trojan Traditional Plans Employer Plans To view employer plan information: Select List > Trojan > Traditional Plans > Employer List . The Employer_plan List window is displayed. To display an employer insurance plan, double-click the insurance plan. The Employer Plan window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Employer Reports
topics.body: Running Employer Reports Running Employer Reports You can generate employer reports that are sorted alphabetically or by identification number. The reports contain the employer ID number, name, address, phone number, contact person, and ECS ID number. Related Topics Running the Employers by ID Report Running the Alphabetical Employers List Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling ePostings
topics.body: Enabling ePostings eServices > Using the ePostings Service Enabling ePostings In addition to subscribing to ePostings, there are several settings you must make in the software before you can use the ePostings feature. Settings are found in the following windows: Insurance Settings window: Select the Enable ePostings option. When you click the option, a message is displayed, asking you to contact eServices sales to enable ePostings. To access this window, select System > Change System Settings > Insurance . See Setting Insurance Defaults for more information. If one or more of the ePostings ADA codes is missing, a message is displayed, asking you to check the Default ADA ePostings Codes section of the Practice dialog. See Specific ADA Codes for Use with ePostings for more information. Insurance Setup Wizard: If you use the Insurance Setup wizard to set up default insurance settings, select the Enable ePostings option. When you click the option, a message is displayed, asking you to contact eServices sales to enable the ePostings service. To access the wizard, select Wizards > Insurance Wizard . If one or more of the ePostings ADA codes is missing, a message is displayed, asking you to check the Default ADA ePostings Codes section of the Practice dialog. See Specific ADA Codes for Use with ePostings for more information. Insurance Co window: Select the ePostings Capable option on the Information tab of the Insurance Co window. You must select this option for each insurance company that is using ePostings. To access this window, select List > Ins. Co. and double-click an insurance company. See Adding an Insurance Company for more information. Your eServices representative will assist you in setting up ePostings. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling Amazing Charts
topics.body: Enabling Amazing Charts Using Amazing Charts Enabling Amazing Charts You must install the Amazing Charts software on your server and enable the Carestream Dental interface before you can enable the integration with SoftDent. Contact Amazing Charts for information on purchasing the software and enabling the Carestream Dental interface. A mazing Charts is not currently available in SoftDent Cloud. To enable the Amazing Charts integration with SoftDent: On the server, select System > Amazing Charts > Enable . A message asking you to call Support for a license is displayed. Note the phone number, and click OK . Contact Support, and obtain an activation code. Select Help > About SoftDent . The About SoftDent window is displayed. Click Enter Activation Code . The Activation Code window is displayed. Type the activation code, and click OK . A message stating that Amazing Charts has been activated successfully is displayed. Click OK , close the About SoftDent window, and restart the SoftDent software. Select System > Amazing Charts > Enable . A message asking you to select the Configure option is displayed. Click OK . Select System > Amazing Charts > Configure . The configuration process begins. When the process is finished, a message stating that Amazing Charts has been configured successfully is displayed. Click OK . See the Amazing Charts help for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling and Editing Account Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payments
topics.body: Enabling and Editing Account Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payments Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans > Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Account Budget Plans Enabling and Editing Account Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payments When enabling or editing account budget plan recurring credit card payments, you can set up the authorization date and whether a billing statement, including payment details, is generated. Enable ePayments before setting up recurring credit card payments. For more information, see Using ePayments . To enable or edit recurring credit card payments for a fixed account budget plan: Select from the following options: To enable recurring credit card payments for an existing fixed account budget plan, in the Account window, select Acct Budget Plan . The Account Budget Plan List window is displayed. Skip to number 4. To enable recurring credit card payments for a new fixed account budget plan, in the Account window, select Transfer to BP . The Payments - Budget Plan Transfer window is displayed. The outstanding balance is displayed next to each patient. Do one of the following: To transfer the total amount due, select Post . To transfer a specific amount for each patient, type it in the Amount column and select Post . A budget plan selection message is displayed. To create a fixed budget plan, click No . The Account Budget Plan List window is displayed. A budget plan debit transaction (code 48) is displayed. Select Terms . The FIXED LOAN TERMS window is displayed. Do one of the following: To set up an existing plan, skip to number 6. To set up a new plan, type the annual interest rate and payment information. Click the CC Payment tab. The tab is the active window. Select the day on which the credit card payment is submitted from the Authorize this Day of Month drop-down list. To send budget plan billing statements indicating when the credit card is charged, select Send Statement . Billing statements indicate that a credit card was processed as a recurring credit card payment. Click Create . The Carestream ePayments Charge Authorization window is displayed. Do one of the following: Swipe the credit card to enter credit card information. Type the credit card information in the Credit Card section of the window, and Click OK . The Recurring Credit Card Payment section of the window is updated to reflect the payment plan information. This information cannot be edited. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling and Editing Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payments
topics.body: Enabling and Editing Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payments Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Patient Budget Plans Enabling and Editing Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payments Set up patient budget plan defaults before creating budget plans. For more information, see Setting Patient Budget Plan Defaults . To enable recurring credit card payments for a patient budget plan: In the Patient window, select Budget Plan . The Patient Budget Plan window is displayed. Click Edit Budget Plan . Do one of the following: If this is an existing budget plan, skip to number 4. If this is a new budget plan, enter the terms information on the Terms tab, and click OK . Click the CC Payment tab. The tab is the active window. Click Edit CC Payment and select the day on which the credit card payment is submitted from the Authorize this Day of Month drop-down list. Click Create . The Carestream ePayments Charge Authorization window is displayed. Do one of the following: Type the credit card information in the Credit Card section of the window. Swipe the credit card to enter credit card information. The Recurring Credit Card Payment section of the window is updated to reflect the payment plan information. This information cannot be edited. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up CareCredit
topics.body: Setting Up CareCredit eServices > Using CareCredit Setting Up CareCredit Even if your office is currently offering CareCredit, you must perform the following configuration steps to use the new integration. To set up CareCredit, you must enable the service and configure the CareCredit transaction codes and providers. Only a user with User ID 1 (the highest level of security rights) can enable CareCredit. Enabling CareCredit To enable CareCredit: Select System > CareCredit > Enable CareCredit . A message is displayed, telling you to continue with the configure options. Click OK . Configuring CareCredit Transaction Codes To configure the CareCredit transaction codes: Select System > CareCredit > Configure Tran Codes . A message is displayed, stating that you must configure the CareCredit transaction codes before you can enter CareCredit transactions and asking if you want to continue. Click Yes . The Practice Information window is displayed. On the Default ADA tab, select the CareCredit refund and payment codes by clicking the drop-down arrow next to the By CareCredit and CareCredit fields and selecting the codes from the ADA/Transaction Codes window. If you have not previously created CareCredit transaction codes, follow the steps described in Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes . Click OK . Click Close . Configuring CareCredit Providers To configure CareCredit providers: Do one of the following: Select System > CareCredit > Configure Providers . The Provider -- Configure the Provider for CareCredit Service list window is displayed. Double-click on a provider name. In the Provider List ( List > Provider ), select a provider name, and click Add CareCredit Service . The Provider -- Configure the Provider for CareCredit Service window is displayed. Type the CareCredit merchant number and practice code in the fields. If you are a new CareCredit client or if you do not know the merchant number, it can be found on the enrollment materials or your monthly statements. Contact CareCredit Provider Services to obtain the practice code. Click Add Service . A success message is displayed, and "Service Added" is displayed on the window. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting
topics.body: Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting Setting Up Insurance > Using CDT Codes Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting The CDT-2022 code set includes revisions and modifications to existing ADA codes. For information about specific changes, contact the American Dental Association for the CDT 2022 manual. You can enable the CDT-2022 code format for: An insurance plan All insurance plans associated with a specific insurance company All insurance plans Related Topics Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting for a Single Insurance Plan Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting for Multiple Insurance Plans Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting for a Single Insurance
topics.body: Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting for a Single Insurance Setting Up Insurance > Using CDT Codes > Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting for a Single Insurance Plan To enable the CDT-2022 code formatting and add the letter D prefix to codes for an insurance plan: Select List > Ins. Plan . The Insurance plan List window is displayed. Double-click the insurance plan. The insurance plan�s information is displayed in the Insurance plan window. Click the Claim Filing tab. The Claim Filing tab is the active window. Select Precede CDT Codes with the letter 'D.' A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Codes are preceded by the letter D when printing claims for the selected insurance plan. Select 2022 from the This plan accepts CDT Version: drop-down list. Click OK . A message is displayed, asking you if you want to change the CDT version for all plans belonging to the insurance company. Click Yes to change the CDT version for all plans, or click No to change the CDT version for only the selected plan. Click Close twice. Related Topics Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting Handling Insurance Plan Information Manually Installing CDT-2022 Codes Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting for Multiple Insurance Plans Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting for Multiple Insurance
topics.body: Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting for Multiple Insurance Setting Up Insurance > Using CDT Codes > Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting for Multiple Insurance Companies To enable the CDT-2022 code formatting and add the letter D prefix to codes for more than one insurance plan: Select System > Fees Maintenance > Enable CDT Code Formatting . The Enable CDT Code Formatting window is displayed. Select an option: To add the D prefix to codes used by plans associated with all insurance companies, select For ALL Plans . To add the D prefix to codes used by all plans associated with a specific company, select For Plans Belonging to the Following Company and select the company from the drop-down list. Click OK . Related Topics Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting Handling Insurance Plan Information Manually Installing CDT-2022 Codes Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting for a Single Insurance Plan Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Credit Card Payment Plans
topics.body: Setting Up Credit Card Payment Plans Budget Planning > Creating Credit Card Payment Plans Setting Up Credit Card Payment Plans To enable a credit card payment plan: Do one of the following: To set up a plan for an account, in the Account List window, select an account and select Credit Card Payment Plan . To set up a plan for a patient, in the Patient List window, select a patient and select Credit Card Payment Plan . The plan details are displayed. Click Edit and type the payment plan total in the Total Amount field. In the Down Payment field, type the down payment amount, if the patient wants to make a down payment. Select one of the following options: To have the patient specify the amount he wants to pay per month: Click Patient Picks Payment . Type the amount the patient wants to pay in the Monthly Payment field and press Tab . The No. of Payments field is automatically updated, as is the Last Payment field. To have the software determine the monthly payment amount based on the number of payments: In the No. of Payments field, type the number of payments and press Tab . The monthly payment amount is calculated and displayed in the Monthly Payment field. Select the day on which the credit card payment is submitted from the Day to Authorize drop-down list. If you entered a down payment and you select a date that is less than 20 days from the date of the down payment, the first payment is deferred until the next month. If the first payment date is 20 or more days from the down payment date, the first payment will occur on the date selected. Example: Select the provider from the Doctor drop-down list. Click Create . The Carestream ePayments Capture Credit Card window is displayed. Do one of the following: Swipe the credit card to enter credit card information. Type the credit card information in the Credit Card Info section of the window and click OK . The Credit Card Payment Plan Details section is updated to reflect the payment plan information. This information cannot be edited. If you entered a down payment amount, a message is displayed, stating that the down payment amount will be charged to the patient�s card today and asking if you want to continue. Click Yes to authorize the patient's credit card for the amount of the down payment. T he payment is treated as an automatic credit card payment, and the transaction does not display in the list of credit card records that are part of the payment plan. If the down payment is not authorized, a message is displayed, stating that the down payment was not authorized successfully and the payment plan will not be created. To continue, enter a valid credit card or click Cancel . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK to print the payment plan consent form. For more information, see Printing a Payment Plan Consent Form . Click OK . Click Close . Related Topics Reviewing and Editing Payments in a Credit Card Payment Plan Using the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details Window Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling eReminders
topics.body: Enabling eReminders eServices > Using the eReminders Service Enabling eReminders To enable eReminders: Select System > Modules > eReminders > Enable eReminders . A message is displayed, asking you to contact eServices sales. Note the phone number, click OK , and contact eServices sales to complete the process. For additional information, see the eReminders online help by clicking the ? button or pressing F1 in an eReminders window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling the Online Statement Service
topics.body: Enabling the Online Statement Service eServices > Using the Automated Statements Service > Using the Online Statement Service Enabling the Online Statement Service Before you can use the Online Statement Service, you must be enrolled in Automated Statements and enable the Online Statement option in the software. For more information, contact an eServices representative. To enable the Online Statement Service: Select System > Change System Settings > Billing Statements . The Billing Statement Settings window is displayed. In the Automated Statements Service Settings section, select Enable Online Statement Service . Click Close . To enable the Online Statement Service for a specific account: Select List > Account . The Account List Window is displayed. Double-click the account from the list. The Account window is displayed. Click the Contact tab, and ensure that Guarantor 1 has an e-mail address. At the bottom-right corner of the window, in the Send Automated Statements section, select By Mail and/or Online . Click Close . You can run a utility to enable the Online option for all accounts with valid e-mail addresses. See Running the Set Up Account Automated Statements Service Options Utility for more information. Related Topics Running the Set Up Account Automated Statements Service Options Utility Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling the ePrescriptions Module
topics.body: Enabling the ePrescriptions Module eServices > Using the ePrescriptions Module Enabling the ePrescriptions Module To enable the ePrescriptions module: Select System > Modules > ePrescriptions > Enable ePrescriptions . A message is displayed, asking you to contact eServices sales. Note the phone number, click OK , and contact eServices sales to complete the process. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: End of Year Reports
topics.body: End of Year Reports Handling Reports > Frequently Used Reports End of Year Reports The following table lists the recommended reports you should run at the end of every fiscal year. Although these reports are particularly useful at the end of the year, they can be run at any time throughout the year. These reports are not required to �close out� the year. Name and Path Description Insurance Plan Rollover Insurance > Insurance Plan Rollover � Resets insurance benefits for each patient with an insurance plan anniversary date of January. � Run prior to posting transactions or submitting insurance for the month of January. � Should be run at the start of each month. Treatment Plan Recalculation of Benefits Insurance > Treat Plan Recalc of Benefits � Recalculates estimated insurance portions for all treatment plans. � Should be run at the start of each month. Practice Summary Report Reports > Practice Management > Practice Summary � Compares nine key areas (such as production, receivables, referrals and so forth) for the past six months and forecasts the next six months. Practice Barometer Report Reports > Practice Management > Practice Barometers � Compares two date ranges for the number of patients seen, new patients, productions, goals, and receivables totals. Accountant�s Earnings Report Reports > Accounting > Accountant�s Earnings � Shows a breakdown of all transaction codes posted. � Calculates net collections, net productions, net charges, tax, and expected income. Account Aging Reports > Accounting > Account Aging � Shows ending receivables totals. � Identifies overdue accounts. � Lists all accounts that have an aged balance over a specified amount. Receivables Summary Reports > Practice Management > Receivables Reports > Summary � Lists prior receivables, changes in receivables, and new receivables totals for the current date, month-to-date, and year-to-date. Receivables Breakdown Reports > Practice Management > Receivables Reports > Breakdown � Lists monthly or yearly breakdown of receivables totals for the practice. Collection Summary Reports > Practice Management > Collection Reports > Summary � Summarizes all collections by cash, check, insurance check, and credit cards. Yearly Register Reports > Accounting > Registers > Yearly � Summarizes the number of patients seen, number of new patients, production, and collection amounts for the year. To view a printable job aid that lists the reports you should run at the end of the year, see End of Year Reports . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering Data
topics.body: Entering Data Getting Started Entering Data After you start the program, enter your practice information, and define any necessary settings, you are ready to enter data into the software. To use the recall and patient tracking features, enter each patient's first visit date and last exam date. Enter data in the following order: Provider information : This includes the personal information, identification number, specialty, and vacation schedule for each provider in your practice (dentists, assistants, and hygienists). Transaction codes and fee schedules : Basic ADA procedures are included with the software. Enter any other codes your office uses and assign fee schedule amounts for each procedure. If a procedure requires a tooth/surface/quadrant, include that information when you enter the code. Insurance plan and company data for the major plans in your area : Enter insurance company information, including address, default employer, and contact person. After you enter each insurance company, enter information for each insurance plan. All active accounts and patients with a current balance : An active account includes patients you have seen in the last two years. For your insured patients, assign an insurance plan and the patient to account guarantor relationship. Related Topics U sing Formatted Fields Using AutoCaps Selecting List Items Data Entry Tips Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the End Of Day Call Backs Report
topics.body: Running the End Of Day Call Backs Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports Running the End of Day Call Backs Report The End of Day Call Backs report g enerates a list of appointments that are flagged for a follow-up call at the end of the day. Appointments are flagged for a follow-up call when the Call Back Needed checkbox is selected in the ADA/Transaction Codes window for a scheduled procedure, or the Follow-Up Call checkbox is selected in the patient�s Transactions window. Sample Report To generate the End of Day Call Backs report: Select Reports > End of Day Call Backs or select Reports > Recall/Appt > End of Day Call Backs . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To restrict the report to a specific provider : Type the provider identification number in the Providers section. To include the account guarantor�s name and telephone numbers in the report : Select Include Guarantor�s Name and Phone Numbers in Report . Click OK . Related Topics Daily Reports: End of Day Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: End of Year Procedures End of Year Procedures Depending on your office practices, you might find it helpful to perform certain functions or procedures at the end of each fiscal year. The procedures you might want to perform include: Running End of Year Reports: There are several reports you should run at the end of the fiscal year. To view a list of the reports, click here . topics.title: Understanding the Enhanced Workspace
topics.body: Understanding the Enhanced Workspace Getting Started > Using the Workspace Understanding the Enhanced Workspace The Enhanced Workspace: Is the default workspace view Prevents window clutter Provides one-click access to previously active windows Much of the Enhanced Desktop Workspace is divided into two panes: The left pane contains the Express bar . Each window opened during the workday is represented by a button displayed in the Current Views section of the bar. The button for the active window is displayed in a distinct color to indicate that the window is selected. Window and feature-specific commands are displayed in the Options section of the bar. All the operations and functions that can be performed from the active window are displayed in this section of the bar. A button, enabling access to the CS Imaging software package from any window, is displayed in the General Operations section of the bar. You can also close all software windows from this section of the bar. By default, the right pane is the client area . The active window is displayed in the client area. The following are displayed at the bottom of the workspace in the Status bar: Person currently logged in System date and time Operating mode Reminder Notes for Today text Batch icons: Icons in the status bar indicate the presence of batched electronic statements, letters, reports, claims, statements, and memos for today. If the icons are not visible, floating over the empty space displays a message stating "No Batched Letters," "No Overdue Reports," and so on. Clicking on an icon displays the window associated with completing the particular task. The following icons can display: E: Electronic Statements�Click this icon to display the Electronic Services window ( Insurance > Electronic Services ). L: Batched Letters�Click this icon to display the Letter Expert Merge window ( Experts > Letter Expert > Merge All Batched Letters ). R: Overdue Reports�Click this icon to run overdue scheduled reports ( Reports > Report Manager > Run Scheduled Reports ). C: Batched Claims�Click this icon to display the Insurance Batch Run Setup window ( Insurance > Print Batched Claims ). S: Batched Statements�Click this icon to display the Statements in Batch window ( Reports > Accounting > Statements > Batch Count ). M: Reminder Notes for Today�Click this icon to display the Today's Memos window ( List > Today's Memos ). To switch from the Enhanced Workspace to the Classic Workspace, see Setting Workspace Preferences . Related Topics Using the Header Bar Using the Client Area Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering Practice Information
topics.body: Entering Practice Information Getting Started > Starting the Software Entering Practice Information Use the Practice Information window to enter practice information that can be printed on forms and statements distributed to patients, doctors, and insurance companies. To enter practice information: Select File > Practice . The Practice Information window is displayed. Type the practice name, address, city, state, and zip code. These default values are automatically displayed in all records that are added to the database. The first five digits of the zip code are used as the default value for any accounts and providers added to the database. Select the provider assigned to all new account records from the Set New Account Dr. to drop-down list. For more information, see Handling Provider Information . Type the practice bank account number and the minimum age for an adult patient in the appropriate fields. Primary teeth are automatically displayed in the charting module for patients younger than the specified age. Type your Type 2 (Group) ID number in the National Provider Id field. If your practice bills by doctor, leave this field blank. Type the office name and abbreviation in the Short Name and Abbreviated Name fields. If you purchased ePayments, Processing is enabled is displayed in the Credit Card Processing section. If you do not use this software package, Processing is not enabled is displayed in the Credit Card Processing section. For more information, see eServices . To purchase any electronic practice management service, contact an eServices representative. Type the practice�s federal tax identification number (TIN) in the Federal Tax field. The TIN is displayed on insurance forms. Click the ID#�s tab and type the practice identification numbers used by the specified insurance carrier in the (11) Blue Shield through (14) State Medicare fields. Type practice identification numbers that are required by other insurance companies or health organizations in the (15) through (22) fields. Click the Office Hours tab and type the time the office is opened and closed for each day of the week. Type a to indicate AM and p to indicate PM after each time. The office hours are used in the default Daybook. Click the Contact tab and type the phone numbers, fax numbers, and e-mail addresses in the appropriate fields. Click the Default ADA tab and select the ADA personal check payment, personal credit card payment, personal refund, insurance credit card payment, and CareCredit codes by clicking the drop-down arrow and selecting the codes from the ADA/Transaction Codes window. Only codes entered in the Insurance Payment by section of the window are available for credit card processing and claim attachment. If you subscribed to ePostings, the fields in the ePostings Codes section are automatically populated. If you purchased eServices software, select Enable Credit Card Processing from the Express bar or Options menu, select Enabled for Practice or Enabled for Providers , and click OK. Select Credit Card Options from the Express bar or Options menu to display the Carestream ePayments Setup window, where you can load or reload your VisaNet ini file, view or edit workstation settings, view or edit merchant information, and view or edit settings shared by the practice. Click OK . Click Close . Related Topics Creating a Treatment Location National Provider Identifier Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering Code Scheduling Information
topics.body: Entering Code Scheduling Information Handling Transactions and Codes > Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes Entering Code Scheduling Information Before attempting to enter ADA, CPT, or transaction code scheduling information, enter general information on the Information tab. For more information, see Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes . If you are adding a payment or adjustment code, you cannot change information displayed on the Scheduler/Fees tab. You can create up to 27 different fee schedules and set up different charges for the same code. This feature is useful for multiple-provider practices that use different fees for each doctor or unique requirements, such as senior citizen discounts. Fee schedule 0 can be used for bluebook entries. To enter code scheduling information for a new code: From the ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window, click the Scheduler/Fees tab or press Ctrl + Tab . To change the color group definition assignment, click OK and select Change Color . Type the number of time units required to perform the procedure in the Time Units field. Type the number of time units required when scheduling the procedure with other procedures in the Multi Time Units field and type the number of real time minutes it takes to complete the procedure in the RTU minutes field. To use the scheduler template, select Use Scheduler Template . If you use this feature, providers are automatically assigned to the code, and a warning is displayed if there are scheduling conflicts. Type provider codes for the personnel required for each time unit of an appointment in the Scheduler Template field. Example : If a procedure requires the assistant for the first time unit, the hygienist for the next two units, and the dentist for the last time unit, type AHHD in the Scheduler Template field. Type the doctor�s fee in the 0 field. Fields 0 through 26 specify charge amounts for up to 27 different fee schedules. To save the new procedure code information, click OK . Tip The code number is displayed in the Code field. After you save the code, the code description is displayed. If you are color scheduling, the code�s assigned color is displayed in the Color field. Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The real time unit is the actual time it takes to perform a procedure in minutes. For example, 20 RTU�s equal 20 minutes. To include a hygienist in the scheduler template, select Hygiene Code on the Information tab. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering an Address Command in Microsoft Outlook
topics.body: Entering an Address Command in Microsoft Outlook Using PracticeToGo > Sending Data Back to SoftDent > SoftDent Commands and Microsoft Outlook Entering an Address Command in Microsoft Outlook Address commands enable you to transfer information from the Microsoft Outlook software to the PC SoftDent software in the form of reminder memos, patient or referring doctor record updates, and prescription refill records. To view a list of available Address commands, click here . To transfer information to the PC SoftDent software using the Address commands in the Microsoft Outlook software: Open the Contacts list. Place your cursor in the desired Contacts list entry. Type the command character tag, and then type the rest of the command in the Last Name field. Example: Type \SDU to update a contact record. Because the command is case sensitive, it must be entered in all capital letters. The command must also be entered without spacing between the characters. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering Insurance Payment Notes
topics.body: Entering Insurance Payment Notes Posting Payments > Posting Insurance Payments Entering Insurance Payment Notes To enter notes for a claim, from the Insurance Payments window: Select Notes Mode from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Type the note. The information entered in this field is displayed in the Note field in the Transaction window for the write-off transactions (code 50.90), the risk write-off code (code 50.91), and the insurance check payment code. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Enhanced Workspace Express Bar
topics.body: Using the Enhanced Workspace Express Bar Getting Started > Using the Workspace Using the Enhanced Desktop Express Bar The Express bar: Is only displayed when you are using the Enhanced Workspace. Enables you to access a previously active window from the Current Views section of the bar. Enables you to launch CS Imaging software or close all windows from the General Operations section. When a window is displayed: A button is added to the Current Views section of the bar. Select a button to display a previously active window. This functionality enables you to quickly move from one window to another without cluttering your workspace. The commands displayed in the Options section of the bar: Are dictated by the currently active window. Enable you to access other features from the selected window. To select a command from the Options section of the Express bar command, select the command or type the hotkey associated with the command. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: ePayments Secure--Credit Card Voids and Refunds
topics.body: ePayments Secure--Credit Card Voids and Refunds eServices > ePayments Secure ePayments Secure--Credit Card Voids and Refunds If a transaction has not been settled, you can void it from the patient�s ledger. If a transaction has already been settled, use ePayments Secure to issue a refund. To refund a credit card payment: In the patient Transactions List window, select the credit card payment you want to refund, and click on the payment toolbar. The Refund window is displayed. Click OK . The ePayments Secure�Undo Payments window is displayed. Click Submit . The ePayments Secure service processes the request and returns a "Void Successfully Completed" notification. Click Finished . The ePayments Secure�Select a Print Device window is displayed. Select the device (System Tray App) if necessary, and click Print . The Payment Receipt window is displayed. Select a printer from the drop-down list, and click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: ePayments Secure--Enabling Credit Card Processing by Provider
topics.body: ePayments Secure--Enabling Credit Card Processing by Provider eServices > ePayments Secure ePayments Secure--Enabling Credit Card Processing by Provider If there are multiple providers in your practice using separate merchant accounts for credit card processing, you must enable processing for each provider. To enable credit card processing by provider: From the SoftDent main menu, select File > Practice . The Practice Information window is displayed. Select Enable Credit Card Processing from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The System Settings - Enable CC Processing window is displayed. Select Enabled for Providers , and then click OK . The Practice Information window is displayed. Select List > Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. Double-click a provider to display the provider�s settings. In the Credit Card Processing section, select Participating Provider and click OK . The Link TSYS Merchants window is displayed. The tray app device must be set up and running when credit card processing is enabled by provider. Select the merchant ID from the drop-down list, and click OK . Click OK . R epeat these steps for each provider. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: ePayments Secure--Generating a Credit Card Transactions By Day Report
topics.body: ePayments Secure--Generating a Credit Card Transactions By Day Report eServices > ePayments Secure > ePayments Secure--Printing Reports ePayments Secure--Generating a Credit Card Transactions By Day Report To view the credit card transactions in your office, you can run the Credit Card Transactions By Day report at any time. Sample Report To generate a Credit Card Transactions By Day Report: Select Reports > Accounting > Print CC Transactions By Day . The Select Date Range for CC Transactions window is displayed. Enter a date range and click OK . The ePayments Secure�Select a Print Device window is displayed. Select the device (System Tray App) if necessary, and click Print . The Credit Card Transactions by Day report is generated. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: ePayments Secure--Posting a Payment to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers
topics.body: ePayments Secure--Posting a Payment to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers eServices > ePayments Secure ePayments Secure--Posting a Payment to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers If you are using ePayments and have your credit card processing set to Enabled for Providers, you can post one credit card payment per billing provider with a single swipe/insertion/tap. To post a credit card payment for multiple providers: Select an option: In the Account List window: Select the account and click Transactions . In the Account window : Click Transactions . In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Click Personal in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Personal Payment window is displayed. Select the credit card code from the Code drop-down list and press Tab . Note the Amounts to be charged and Dr. # section that is displayed to the right of the Payment Information section. In the bottom section of the window, type the payment amount for each patient with an outstanding balance. Note that payment amounts for the same doctor are reflected in the Amounts to be charged section displayed to the right of the Payment Information section. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Click Post . The ePayments Secure�Capture Card Info window is displayed with the Device Selected field set to the default device (System Tray App) for this computer. Select Swipe/Insert/Tap Card , engage the card, and then click Submit . OR If the patient�s credit card number has been stored from a previous transaction, select the option to use that card, and then click Submit . The ePayments Secure service processes the transmission and returns a "Payment Successfully Completed" notification and an authorization number. Click Finished . The ePayments Secure�Select a Print Device window is displayed. Select the device (System Tray App) if necessary, and click Print . The Payment Receipt window is displayed. Print Receipt: Yes must be selected on the Practice tab of the ePayments Secure Settings window. For more information, see ePayments Secure--Printing Receipts . Select a printer from the drop-down list, and click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: ePayments Secure--Posting a Refund to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers
topics.body: ePayments Secure--Posting a Refund to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers eServices > ePayments Secure ePayments Secure--Posting a Refund to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers If you are using ePayments and have your credit card processing set to Enabled for Providers, you can post one refund per billing provider with a single swipe/insertion/tap. To post a refund for multiple providers: Select an option: In the Account List window : Select the account and click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Payments . In the Account window : Click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Payments . In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Payments . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Click Refunds in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Refund Credits window is displayed. The default credit refund code is displayed in the Code drop-down list. The total in the Amount field is updated as you add each refund amount to the list. Note the Amounts to be refunded section for each billing doctor is displayed to the right of the Payment Information section. In the bottom section of the window, type the refund amount for each patient due a refund. Note that refund amounts for the same doctor are reflected in the Amounts to be refunded section displayed to the right of the Payment Information section. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Click Post . The Carestream ePayments Undo Payment window is displayed. The Undo Charge Amount field is populated with the refund amount for the first provider. The Carestream ePayments Undo Payment window is displayed for each billing provider. Click Submit . The Carestream ePayments Secure Undo Payment window is displayed, and the Undo Charge Amount field is populated with the refund amount for the second provider. Since the refund is going on the same credit card, click Last Card Used to use the credit card information, and click Submit . Repeat for all remaining refunds. Click Finished . The ePayments Secure�Select a Print Device window is displayed. Select the device (System Tray App) if necessary, and click Print . The Payment Receipt window is displayed. Select a printer from the drop-down list, and click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: ePayments Secure--Printing Receipts eServices > ePayments Secure ePayments Secure--Printing Receipts If you set up the SoftDent software to print receipts for ePayments Secure transactions, you are prompted to print the receipt when the transaction is complete. This is a setting that your trainer will help you configure when you initially set up ePayments Secure, as it is found on a password-protected window. To print a receipt after the transaction is complete: In the Patient window, click Transactions . The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select the transaction for which you want to print a receipt. Select Credit Card Receipt from the Options section of the Express bar. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: ePayments Secure--Refunding Credit Card Transactions
topics.body: ePayments Secure--Refunding Credit Card Transactions eServices > ePayments Secure ePayments Secure--Refunding Credit Card Transactions To refund a credit card payment: In the Account List window, select an account. Click Payments . Select Refund . In the Code field, select the Refund ADA code. An Apply to Charge drop-down field is displayed. Select the payment you want to refund. A breakdown of the charges is displayed. Zero out the amount you DO NOT want to refund, leaving only the amount you want to refund. Click Post . The Undo Payment window is displayed. topics.title: Using ePayments Secure
topics.body: Using ePayments Secure eServices Using ePayments Secure ePayments Secure gives your practice and patients additional credit card payment security. Advantages of ePayments Secure include: No credit card numbers stored on your local computers Automatic settlement (no more manual settlement) New credit card chip reader/swiper/NFC machine Same workflow as existing ePayments You must purchase a new credit card chip reader/swiper/NFC device in order to use ePayments Secure. Contact your Carestream Dental representative for more details. New ePayments customers must use ePayments Secure. Existing ePayments users can continue to use version 1 and do not need to upgrade. These instructions are for ePayments Secure (version 2). If you are using the ePayments Service (version 1), see Using the ePayments Service for more information. Related Topics ePayments Secure--Enabling Credit Card Processing by Provider ePayments Secure--Adding Payments to Patient Transactions with the Card Reader ePayments Secure--Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Manually ePayments Secure--Posting a Payment to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers ePayments Secure--Posting a Refund to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers ePayments Secure--Credit Card Voids and Refunds ePayments Secure--Refunding Credit Card Transactions ePayments Secure--Printing Receipts ePayments Secure--Printing Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

To print the report: From the main menu, select Report Options > Electronic Payer Report . The Electronically Capable Payer Report window is displayed. Select an option: To view the report : Click Print Preview . To print the report : Click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: eServices
topics.body: eServices eServices eServices enable you to submit transactions electronically, using your computer and a modem or an Internet connection. eServices include the following: eClaims Service : A modem or Internet access is required. eVerifications Service: The eVerifications service includes the eVerifications insurance eligibility service and the eVerifications claim status service. Internet access is required. Automated Statements Service : A modem or Internet access is required. ePayments Secure: ePayments Secure is the second version of the ePayments service and offers additional benefits to your practice, including automatic settlement, a new credit card chip reader, and additional security features. New ePayments customers must use ePayments Secure. Existing ePayments users can continue to use version 1 and do not need to upgrade. ePayments Service : A modem or Internet access is required. ePostings Service: A modem or Internet access is required. eReminders Service: Internet access is required. eForms Module: Internet access is required. CareCredit : A modem or Internet access is required. ePrescriptions Module: Internet access is required. Before installing the software, ensure that each computer in your network meets the minimum system requirements. For more information on system requirements, see the System Requirements document shipped with your practice management software, or select System Requirements on the Web from the Help menu in your software. To access eServices from the software, select Insurance > eServices . The eServices window is displayed.
