topics.title: Running the ePostings Adjustment Actions Report
topics.body: Running the ePostings Adjustment Actions Report eServices > Using the ePostings Service > Using the ePostings Adjustments Window Running the ePostings Adjustment Actions Report The ePostings Adjustment Actions report contains a list of all of the ePostings adjustment codes in your database. The report includes details about the group code, reason code, description, and action, as well as whether the code will update the bluebook. Sample Report To run the ePostings Adjustment Actions report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > ePostings Service > Adjustments . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The ePostings Adjustment Actions window is displayed. You can also print the ePostings Adjustment Actions report by clicking the Print button in any of the ePostings Adjustments windows, selecting an output option, and clicking OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the ePostings Adjustments Window
topics.body: Using the ePostings Adjustments Window eServices > Using the ePostings Service Using the ePostings Adjustments Window When your office receives an ePostings payment file from the clearinghouse, adjustments are applied based on the codes found in the ePostings Adjustments window. The ePostings Adjustments window helps SoftDent apply adjustments to the appropriate amounts (Patient, Write-Off, Other Ins, and so on). If an ePostings adjustment code exists for the Group/Reason adjustment code combination, the adjustment is applied based on the action specified in the ePostings Adjustments window. Example: Group Code (CO), Reason Code (12), Action (Apply to Patient) causes the adjustment amount to be applied to the patient amount instead of the write-off amount. If no ePostings adjustment code exists for the Group/Reason adjustment code combination, the adjustment is applied to the Other Adj amount, which causes you to distribute the payment. Because offices can apply adjustments (Write-Off or Patient) in several ways, the ePostings Adjustments window enables you to change how adjustments are applied, instead of having to manually distribute the amount every time. Example: Contractual Obligations are usually considered a write-off; however, there might be instances, based on the reason code, where your office wants to apply the adjustment to the patient amount. Instead of having to manually do this every time you encounter this adjustment, you can use the ePostings Adjustments window to specify how to apply this adjustment (WO, Patient, Other) in the future. Several codes come pre-loaded. You can edit these codes and add your own codes to suit your practice's business needs. Related Topics Adding an ePostings Adjustment Code Editing an ePostings Adjustment Code Deleting an ePostings Adjustment Code Running the ePostings Adjustment Actions Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding the ePostings Claim Detail Window
topics.body: Understanding the ePostings Claim Detail Window eServices > Using the ePostings Service Understanding the ePostings Claim Detail Window The ePostings Claim Detail window lists all transactions of a claim selected in the ePostings Payment Detail window. The same claim information (for SoftDent and ePostings) displayed in the ePostings Payment Detail window is displayed in this window; however, in this window you can edit the Pat Resp. and Writeoff amounts for any of the transactions in the claim that are mismatched and require attention. Amounts in the columns from the SoftDent side are matched to the columns on the ePostings side and display in red when a mismatch occurs. To edit the payment distribution: Do one of the following: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > ePostings > ePostings Payment Viewer. Click in the ePostings Notifications section in Practice Central. Select the ePostings Notifications entry in the Office Expert Summary in Practice Central. The ePostings Payment Viewer window is displayed. Double-click a payment. The ePostings Payment Detail window is displayed. Select a claim with a status of Verify Distribution or Manual Distribution, and click the status link. Select Edit Distribution . The ePostings Claim Detail window is displayed. Click in the Pat Resp or Writeoff field containing red text, and type your changes. Increasing the Pat Resp and/or Writeoff amount decreases the Other Adjust amount in the same amount. Decreasing the Pat Resp and/or Writeoff amount increases the Other Adjust amount in the same amount. The Other Adjust amount must be zero before you can post the payment. After you have made your changes and the column amounts match, the color of the text changes. If you make a mistake, press Esc to return the field to the original amount. Click View/Add Notes . The ePostings - View/Add Notes window is displayed. Type any notes regarding the change, and click OK to close the ePostings Notes window. Any information displayed in the Remarks section comes from the Original ePostings Data file sent by the eServices clearinghouse and explains why a procedure was not fully paid. Click OK . Related Topics Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Window Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Detail Window Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing ePostings Documents in the Document Manager
topics.body: Viewing ePostings Documents in the Document Manager eServices > Using the ePostings Service Viewing ePostings Documents in the Document Manager When you post an ePostings payment, SoftDent saves a copy of the EOB in the ePostings EOB folder in the document manager. You can view the EOB in the document manager window for each of the areas related to the payment, including: Claim Insurance Company Insurance Plan Patient If the claim is for secondary insurance, the insurance company and insurance plan are based on the claim's secondary insurance. To view the EOB: Open the document manager window for the patient, claim, insurance company, or insurance plan. Use the option buttons at the top of the window to sort the EOB documents by Date Acquired, Type, or Description. In the ePostings EOB folder, select the EOB in the tree. The ePostings HTML data is displayed in the preview window. Right-click on an ePostings document in the document tree to display the standard document options . Edit Document Description Attach/Detach to Other Rec(s) E-Mail Print Document Delete Review Sort By Date/Time Captured Sort By Label Text Close Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing ePostings Information in Reports
topics.body: Viewing ePostings Information in Reports eServices > Using the ePostings Service Viewing ePostings Information in Reports ePostings information is displayed in several SoftDent reports. Reports containing ePostings information include: Alphabetical Insurance Companies: The last column of this report, eServices, displays the insurance company's Payer ID, as well as whether the company is ePostings Capable (Yes/No). See Running the Alphabetical Insurance Companies Report for more information. Insurance Companies by ID: The last column of this report, eServices, displays the insurance company's Payer ID, as well as whether the company is ePostings Capable (Yes/No). See Running the Insurance Companies by ID Report for more information. Daysheet: The total amounts for all transactions posted with the ePostings Payment by EFT ADA code are included in the Electronic Funds Transfer total. Transactions posted with the ePostings Payment by Check ADA code are included on the deposit slip. If some payments were marked Not Postable (for example: provider adjustments, or claims for another office), a note is displayed on the deposit slip, explaining the difference between the check amount (which will include the Not Postable amounts) and the total check payments from the daysheet itself (which only include the postable amounts). See Running the Daysheet Report for more information. Insurance Income: The total amounts for all write-off transactions posted with the ePostings Write-off ADA code are included in the Insurance Income report. The heading of the TOTAL ADJUSTMENTS column includes the ePostings Write-off code. See Running Insurance Practice Management Reports for more information. Register Reports (Daily, Monthy, Yearly, and Period): An EFT collections type�not specific to ePostings�is displayed on the register reports. The total amounts for all write-off transactions posted with the ePostings Write-off ADA code are included in the register reports (daily, monthly, yearly, and period) in the Amount due to PPO writeoffs field. Daily Production/Collection: The total amounts for all write-off transactions posted with the ePostings Write-off ADA code are included in the Write Off column of the Daily Production/Collection report. To access this report, select Reports > Accounting > Registers > Summary . Collection Summary: An EFT column�not ePostings specific�is displayed in the Collection Summary report. See Running the Collection Summary Report for more information. Outstanding Insurance Claims by Company: An ePostings Capable Companies Only option is found in the Insurance Companies section of the Outstanding Insurance Claims by Company window. If this option is selected, only those companies with the ePostings Capable flag checked and in the Starting/Ending range are included in the report. If the option is not selected, all companies, ePostings capable or not, that are in the Starting/Ending range are included in the report. The option is unchecked by default. See Generating Outstanding Insurance Claims Reports for more information. Practice Summary: The total amounts for all write-off transactions posted with the ePostings Write-off ADA code are included in the Practice Summary report. The heading of the Insurance write-offs row includes the ePostings Write-off code. See Practice Management Reports for more information. Writeoff Totals: The total amount for all write-off transactions posted with the ePostings Write-off code is included in the

topics.title: Understanding the ePostings Payment Detail Window
topics.body: Understanding the ePostings Payment Detail Window eServices > Using the ePostings Service Understanding the ePostings Payment Detail Window The ePostings Payment Detail window enables you to view the claims belonging to the ePostings payment you selected in the ePostings Payment Viewer window. This window displays the claim amount sent by the insurance company, as well as the amount calculated by SoftDent. The claim amounts must match on both sides of the window before you can post the payment. The Status field contains a link with menu options that will help you verify the claim details and match the claims. There are several scenarios that can occur: The SoftDent claim amount and the ePostings claim amount match when you receive the information from the clearinghouse. These claims have a status of Ready , and you can post the payment. You must verify the claim information by associating the payment with a claim or confirming the claim. You must verify the payment distribution. You must verify the claim and payment information. After you ensure t hat all the claims for the payment match up to the existing SoftDent claims, you can post the payment. To match claim amounts and post ePostings payments: Do one of the following: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > ePostings > ePostings Payment Viewer. Click in the ePostings Notifications section in Practice Central. Select the ePostings Notifications entry in the Office Expert Summary in Practice Central. The ePostings Payment Viewer window is displayed. Double-click a payment. The ePostings Payment Detail window is displayed. The upper portion of the ePostings Payment Detail window contains information about each of the claims associated with the payment. For a description of the fields in the ePostings Payment Detail window, click here . The ePostings Payment Detail window contains the following fields: Patient: Displays the patient last name, first name, and middle initial. If the line item is for a provider adjustment, there is no associated patient, and **Provider Adj** is displayed. Guarantor: Displays the Guarantor for the patient. If the line item is for a provider adjustment, this column is blank. From: Displays the start Date of Service for the claim. If the line item is for a provider adjustment, the column displays // . To: Displays the end Date of Service for the claim. If the line item is for a provider adjustment, this column displays // . Clm Amt: Displays the total amount of the individual claim. If the line item is for a provider adjustment, this column displays 0.00. Payment: Displays the total payment for the individual claim. If the line item is for a Provider Adjustment, this is the amount of the adjustment. Pat Amt: Displays the portion of this claim's total claim amount that is the patient's responsibility. If the line item is for a provider adjustment, this column displays 0.00 . WO Amt: Displays the write-off amount for the claim. If the line item is for a provider adjustment, this column displays 0.00 . Other Amt: Displays the portion of this claim's total claim amount that the software cannot assign to Patient Responsibility, Write Off, or Other Insurance. If a total is displayed in this field, you must manually distribute the adjustment amount. Claim ID: Displays the claim ID of the SoftDent claim associated with the ePostings payment. If the line item is for a claim that has not yet been matched to a SoftDent claim, this column displays NEED . If the line it

topics.title: Understanding the ePostings Payment Viewer Window
topics.body: Understanding the ePostings Payment Viewer Window eServices > Using the ePostings Service Understanding the ePostings Payment Viewer Window The ePostings Payment Viewer window displays payment information about electronic and paper claims. Each payment�electronic fund transfer (EFT), check, or non payment�is displayed on a separate line. A payment can include more than one claim. Typically, the ECS clearinghouse sends ePostings files to your office after you submit claims through eClaims. To receive ePostings files that are currently available without submitting claims, select Insurance > eServices > Receive Only . You can work with ePostings payments in several ways. You might post all of the payments with a Ready status first, and then go back and review the payments that need attention. Conversely, you could address all the payments with a status of Need Attn and then post. To work with payments in the ePostings Payment Viewer window: Do one of the following: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > ePostings > ePostings Payment Viewer . Click in the ePostings Notifications section in Practice Central. Select the ePostings Notifications entry in the Office Expert Summary in Practice Central. The ePostings Payment Viewer window is displayed. For a description of the fields in the ePostings Payment Viewer window, click here . The ePostings Payment Viewer window contains the following fields: Ins Co: The insurance company that sent the payment information. The text in this column displays exactly as it is received from the clearinghouse. Received: The date the ePostings payment was received, in MM/DD/YY format. Pay Date: The payment date, in MM/DD/YY format. For EFT payments, this is the date of the transfer of funds; for checks, this is the date the check is made out and mailed. Chk/Rt #: The check number or EFT reference number of the payment. The number is displayed exactly as it is received from the clearinghouse. Payment: The total payment amount for the claim. Not Cov'd: The total dollar amount of adjustments for all the claims that are part of this payment. Claims: The number of claims associated with the payment. If no claims are associated with the payment (for example, a provider-level adjustment), N/A is displayed. Status: The status of the payment (Ready, Waiting, Need Attn, Nothing to Post). W hen the list is displayed for the first time each day, any record with a status of Waiting is automatically changed to Ready if that day's date is the same as or later than the value in the Pay Date field. Type: The payment type�Check, EFT, or Non Pay. On Hold: The number of claims associated with this payment that have been placed on hold. Use the Sort By options at the upper right of the window to sort payments by pay date, check/routing number, payment type, status, date received, and insurance company. The search parameters used in the Find field vary, depending on the option you select. Select a payment from the list. Information about the payment�s posting status is displayed in blue text directly below the list. The following message types can display: EFT Posting Status Messages : This ePostings Payment has been electronically deposited. You may now post using the Post menu option. This message is displayed if the status is Ready (all claims are matched, all distributions are confirmed, and the payment date has been reached) and the payment has not been placed on Hold. This ePostings Payment is on HOLD and will not be posted. T

topics.title: Using ePostings Data in Utilities
topics.body: Using ePostings Data in Utilities eServices > Using the ePostings Service Using ePostings Data in Utilities ePostings data is handled by several SoftDent utilities. Utilities that use ePostings data include: Rebuild/Reindex Utility: The ePOSTINGS ADJUSTMENTS, ePOSTINGS CLAIM, ePOSTINGS PAYMENT, and ePOSTINGS TRANS data files display as selections in the Select Files to Rebuild/Reindex window. Find/Repair Corrupt Utility: The ePOSTINGS ADJUSTMENTS, ePOSTINGS CLAIM, ePOSTINGS PAYMENT, and ePOSTINGS TRANS data files display as selections in the Find/Repair Corrupt Records window. Edit System File Utility: ePOSTINGS Payment ID and ePOSTINGS Payment Detail ID fields are found on the Edit System File 3 tab in the Edit System File window. Find Largest ID Utility: The ePOSTINGS Payment File and ePOSTINGS Payment Detail File display as selections on the Find Largest ID window. For more information about using SoftDent utilities, see Utilities . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding eReminders Confirmations and Contacts
topics.body: Understanding eReminders Confirmations and Contacts eServices > Using the eReminders Service Understanding eReminders Confirmations and Contacts When the software receives a response from the eReminders module, a patient's appointment card is automatically updated with the response, and a contact is logged for the patient. A contact is added only if the Ask to log contact after confirming appointments option is selected in the Scheduler Settings window. The possible responses and the results include: Appointment was confirmed: When the software receives a response that the appointment is confirmed, the following information is updated in the Appointment Card window: The Confirmed On: field is updated with the date on which the reminder was delivered. The Confirmed By: field displays eReminder . The Confirmation Note field is updated to include the text: Confirmed on mm/dd/yyyy at hh:mm am/pm via xxxxx . If the Confirmation Note field already contains text, this text is appended. Reminder was Delivered (but not confirmed): When the software receives a response that the reminder was delivered but not confirmed, the following information is updated in the Appointment Card window: The Confirmed On: field is blank. The Confirmed By: field is blank. The Confirmation Note field is updated to include the text: Reminder delivered on mm/dd/yyyy at hh:mm am/pm via xxxxx . If the Confirmation Note field already contains text, this text is appended. Msg left on machine: When the software receives a response that the reminder was delivered by leaving a message on a machine, the following information is updated in the Appointment Card window: The Confirmed On: field is blank. The Confirmed By: field is blank. The Confirmation Note field is updated to include the text: Msg left on machine on mm/dd/yyyy at hh:mm am/pm . If the Confirmation Note field already contains text, this text is appended. Reminder attempted, no answer: When the software receives a response that the reminder was delivered but there was no answer, the following information is updated in the Appointment Card window: The Confirmed On: field is blank. The Confirmed By: field is blank. The Confirmation Note field is updated to include the text: Reminder attempted, no answer on mm/dd/yyyy at hh:mm am/pm . If the Confirmation Note field already contains text, this text is appended. Reminder undeliverable: When the software receives a response that the reminder was undeliverable, the following information is updated in the Appointment Card window: The Confirmed On: field is blank. The Confirmed By: field is blank. The Confirmation Note field is updated to include the text: Reminder undeliverable-xxxxxxxxx . Reasons for non-delivery can include: Unknown Reason, Insufficient Delivery Time, Invalid Phone Number, Call blocked by provider request, and Message segment not recorded. If the Confirmation Note field already contains text, this text is appended. A contact based on the information received from the eReminders module is recorded in the Contacts for window. The following contact information is recorded: Name and ID: Displays the name and ID number of the patient. Category: Displays Confirm Appointment . Re: Displays eReminders Confirmation . Status/By: Displays Made . The By: field displays one of the following: Phone , E-Reminder E-mail , or Text Message . By Whom: Displays eReminder . Date/Time: Displays the date and time the reminder was delivered. Expected by: Based on the status c

topics.title: Understanding the Electronic Router Window
topics.body: Understanding the Electronic Router Window Tracking Patients in the Office Understanding the Electronic Router Window The Electronic Router feature is accessible from the InOffice window, the Daily Appointments window, and the patient chart. The Electronic Router contains patient personal and financial information, which is typically printed on routing slips. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: Treatment Plan : Displays the patient Treatment Plan window. Use this command to view treatment plan procedures that have been entered for the patient. Contacts : Displays the patient Contacts for window. Use this command to view a list of contacts logged for the patient. You can also create reminder memos in this window. Edit Appt : Displays the Appointment Card window. Use this command to edit the patient appointment information. Complete : Toggles the appointment status to or from complete. Follow-Up : Displays the Electronic Router: Follow up window. Use this command to set the criteria for the patient's next appointment. Set Call Back: Displays the Set Patient Call Back window. Use this command to select the procedures to include in the End of Day Call Backs report. The report generates a list of appointments that are flagged for a follow-up call at the end of the day. Next Appointment : Displays the Appointment Card window. Use this command to view the patient's next appointment. If the patient does not have a scheduled appointment, this command is disabled. Chart : Displays the charting module. If the patient has a chart, the charting information is displayed. If the patient does not have a chart, a blank chart is displayed. Guarantors : Displays the Guarantors/Insurance window. Use this command to view and edit patient guarantor information. Referrals : Displays the Referred Patients window. Use this command to view information for all patients referred to you by a patient. Financial Info : Displays the patient Balance Information window. Use this command to view the patient's balance information. Lab Cases : Displays the Lab Case window. Use this command to view a list of lab cases associated with the patient. History : Displays the patient's history file in your word processor. Use this command to enter extensive notes about the patient. Other Programs : Displays the Program window. Use this command to launch other programs from within the software. View Patient: Displays the Patient Information window. Use this command to access patient demographic, treatment, and payment information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Call Back Reminders in the Electronic Router
topics.body: Setting Call Back Reminders in the Electronic Router Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions > Using the Electronic Router Setting Call Back Reminders in the Electronic Router While using the Electronic Router, you can set a reminder for office staff to follow-up on procedures performed during the appointment. To set a patient call back: In the InOffice or Daily Appointments window, select the appointment and select Electronic Router . The Electronic Router window is displayed. Select Set Call Back . The Set Patient Call Back window is displayed. Select the procedures to include in the End of the Day Call Back report and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Utility Error Log File Utility
topics.body: Running the Utility Error Log File Utility Utilities > Using Report Menu Utilities Running the Utility Error Log File Utility Run this utility to identify problems that occurred when running a utility. The log contains records that track each time a utility program was run, any errors that have occurred, and any changes made to your data files. To run the utility error log file utility: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Report > Utility Error Log File . The Action Options window is displayed. To print the file, select Print and click OK . Delete this file after you print it. Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the SoftDent Error Log File Utility
topics.body: Running the SoftDent Error Log File Utility Utilities > Using Report Menu Utilities Running the SoftDent Error Log File Utility Run this utility to correct a problem that occurred while running the software. This error log file contains records of any errors encountered while processing data files. If you encounter a problem, your technical support representative may suggest you print this file. To run the SoftDent error log file utility: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Report > SoftDent Error Log File . The Action Options window is displayed. To print the file, select Print and click OK . Delete this file after you print it. Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Estimating Insurance for Medicaid Plans
topics.body: Estimating Insurance for Medicaid Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information > Understanding Medicaid Plans Estimating Insurance for Medicaid Plans The calculations show the estimations when the deductible is subtracted first or last. The calculations are based upon the following example: The fee is $88, the allowed amount is $75, the bluebook insurance payment is $60, the coverage is 80%, the copayment is $30, and the deductible is $25. The insurance estimation calculations for dental and medical flat fee Medicaid plans are: Plan Type Calculations Dental Medicaid Plan Bluebook Entry No Deductible The estimated insurance payment equals the insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The estimated insurance payment is $60. Dental Medicaid Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the estimated insurance payment entered in the bluebook. The difference is added to the estimated write-off amount. In the example : The $25 deductible amount is subtracted from the $60, leaving $35. The $35 difference is added to the $13 estimated write-off amount ($88 fee - $75 allowed amount). The estimated insurance payment is $48. Dental Medicaid Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The deductible is automatically subtracted from the estimated insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The $25 deductible amount is subtracted from the $60, leaving $35. The estimated insurance payment is $35. Dental Medicaid Plan No Bluebook Entry No Deductible The copay is automatically subtracted from the fee. In the example : The $30 copay is subtracted from the $88 fee. The estimated insurance payment is $58. Dental Medicaid Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The copay is automatically subtracted from the fee. The $25 deductible is subtracted from the difference. In the example : The $30 copay is subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $58. The $25 deductible is subtracted from $58. The estimated insurance payment is $33. Dental Medicaid Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The copay is automatically subtracted from the fee. The $25 deductible is subtracted from the difference. In the example : The $30 copay is subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $58. The $25 deductible is subtracted from $58. The estimated insurance payment is $33. Medical Medicaid Plan Bluebook Entry Deductibles Do Not Apply The estimated insurance payment equals the insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The estimated insurance payment is $60. Medical Medicaid Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductibles Do Not Apply The copay is automatically subtracted from the fee. In the example : The $30 copay is subtracted from the $88 fee. The estimated insurance payment is $58. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Automated Statements
topics.body: Using Automated Statements eServices > Using the Automated Statements Service Using Automated Statements When you use Automated Statements, electronic billing statements are transmitted by eServices and then sent to a clearinghouse, where they are printed and mailed to your patients. You automatically receive a report, which lists the patients who will receive a statement. Each report contains the following information: Current balance Overdue balance Insurance balance Budget plan balance Prior to transmission, SoftDent displays a Statement Run Summary report, which lists the: Number of statements sent Total balances billed Outstanding insurance Amount due Related Topics Setting Up Automated Statements Generating Automated Statements Transmitting Automated Statements Additional Automated Statement Options Related Job Aid: Running Billing Statements Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Estimating Insurance for PPO Percentage Plans
topics.body: Estimating Insurance for PPO Percentage Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information > Understanding Preferred Provider (PPO) Plans Estimating Insurance for PPO Percentage Plans The following table explains the insurance estimation calculations used for dental and medical PPO percentage plans. The calculations show the estimations when the deductible is subtracted first or last. The calculations are based upon the following example: The fee is $88, the allowed amount is $75, the bluebook insurance payment is $60, the coverage is 80%, the copayment is $30, and the deductible is $25. T he insurance estimation calculations used for dental PPO percentage plans are: Plan Type Calculations Dental PPO Percentage Plan Bluebook Entry No Deductible The allowed amount is subtracted from the fee. The difference is the estimated write-off amount. The estimated write-off amount is added to the insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The $75 allowed amount is subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $13. The $13 difference is added to the $60 insurance payment entered in the bluebook. The estimated insurance payment is $73. Dental PPO Percentage Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the allowed amount. The difference is multiplied by the coverage percentage. The product is added to the write-off amount (allowed amount subtracted from the fee). In the example : The $25 deductible is subtracted from the $75 allowed amount, leaving $50. The $50 difference is multiplied by the 80% coverage percentage. The $40 product is added to the $13 estimated write-off amount ($88 - $75). The estimated insurance payment is $53. Dental PPO Percentage Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The allowed amount is multiplied by the coverage percentage. The deductible is subtracted from the product. The difference is added to the estimated write-off amount. In the example : The $75 allowed amount is multiplied by the 80% coverage percentage, leaving $60. The $25 deductible amount is subtracted from the $60, leaving $35. The $35 difference is added to the $13 estimated write-off amount ($88 - $75). The estimated insurance payment is $48. Dental PPO Percentage Plan No Bluebook Entry No Deductible The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the fee. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee. The estimated insurance payment is $70.40. Dental PPO Percentage Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the fee. The difference is multiplied by the coverage percentage. In the example : The $25 deductible is automatically subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $63. The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by $63. The estimated insurance payment is $50.40. Dental PPO Percentage Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the fee. The deductible is subtracted from the product. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee, leaving $70.40. The $25 deductible is subtracted from $70.40. The estimated insurance payment is $45.40. Tip If the plan pays something other than a percentage of your fees, set up bluebook entries. If the fees equal the percentage plan allotted amount, you do not need to set up a bluebook entry. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling System Security
topics.body: Enabling System Security System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Enabling System Security To enable system security, select an option: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Enable System Security . A checkmark is displayed next to the command to indicate the feature is enabled. In any of the Security Overrides windows, select Enable System Security . If system security is not enabled, all users can perform all tasks. The only task reserved for User ID 1 is to modify security settings. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Establishing a Backup Routine
topics.body: Establishing a Backup Routine Backing Up Data Establishing a Backup Routine A backup routine should include the following activities: Close all files before performing a backup. Perform backups at least once each working day. Verify the accuracy and completeness of the backup. Remove the latest backup from your office for safeguarding. Incremental backups are not recommended. Most backup applications do not save files that are open. If a file has not been closed, it might not be saved during the backup. This omission could result in essential data not being backed up. When this happens, systems usually provide a message indicating an error, and you can take corrective action. Be certain that every computer has been logged out and no one is using the system prior to performing backups. To capture records of each business day�s activities, you should perform backups routinely at the end of each working day. It is recommended that a separate medium be purchased and labeled for the day it will be used. Additional media can be used on a weekly or monthly basis. Some products may recommend more specific backup activities. A system crash, or hardware failure, can occur at any time. Your backup may be the only mechanism you have that enables you to recover your system data. Before a possible system crash, it is very important to verify the accuracy and completeness of the backup. Make sure you check your backup logs on a regular basis. The media containing the latest backup should be taken off site for safeguarding. Follow the media manufacturer�s guidelines for proper storage. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Estimating Insurance for DMO/Capitation Plans
topics.body: Estimating Insurance for DMO/Capitation Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information > Understanding DMO/Capitation Plans Estimating Insurance for DMO/Capitation Plans The calculations show the estimations when the deductible is subtracted first or last. The calculations are based upon the following example: The fee is $88, the allowed amount is $75, the bluebook insurance payment is $60, the coverage is 80%, the copayment is $30, and the deductible is $25. The insurance estimation calculations used for dental and medical DMO/Capitation plans are: Plan Type Calculations Dental DMO/Capitation Plan Bluebook Entry No Deductible The estimated insurance payment equals the insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The estimated insurance payment is $60. Dental DMO/Capitation Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the estimated insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The $25 deductible amount is subtracted from the $60. The estimated insurance payment is $35. Dental DMO/Capitation Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The deductible is automatically subtracted from the estimated insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The $25 deductible amount is subtracted from the $60. The estimated insurance payment is $35. Dental DMO/Capitation Plan No Bluebook Entry No Deductible The copay is automatically subtracted from the fee. In the example : The $30 copay is subtracted from the $88 fee. The estimated insurance payment is $58. Dental DMO/Capitation Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The copay is automatically subtracted from the fee. The $25 deductible is subtracted from the difference. In the example : The $30 copay is subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $58. The $25 deductible is subtracted from $58. The estimated insurance payment is $33. Dental DMO/Capitation Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The copay is automatically subtracted from the fee. The $25 deductible is subtracted from the difference. In the example : The $30 copay is subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $58. The $25 deductible is subtracted from $58. topics.body: Estimating Insurance for Flat Fee Medicare Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information > Understanding Medicare Plans Estimating Insurance for Flat Fee Medicare Plans To estimate insurance payments for flat fee Medicare plans: Set up accurate payment values in the bluebook for all procedures. If there is no bluebook entry for the flat fee PPO insurance plan, the percentages specified in the Coverage tab of the Insurance plan record are used. The calculations show the estimations when the deductible is subtracted first or last. The calculations are based upon the following example: The fee is $88, the allowed amount is $75, the bluebook insurance payment is $60, the coverage is 80%, the copayment is $30, and the deductible is $25. The insurance estimation calculations for dental and medical flat fee Medicare plans are: Plan Type Calculations Dental Flat Fee Medicare Plan Bluebook Entry No Deductible The allowed amount is subtracted from the fee. The difference is the estimated write-off amount. The estimated write-off amount is added to the insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The $75 allowed amount is subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $13. The $13 difference is added to the $60 insurance payment entered in the bluebook. The estimated insurance payment is $73. Dental Flat Fee Medicare Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the estimated insurance payment entered in the bluebook. The difference is added to the estimated write-off amount. In the example : The $25 deductible amount is subtracted from the $60, leaving $35. The difference is added to the estimated write-off amount. In the example : The $25 deductible amount is subtracted from the $60, leaving $35. The $35 difference is added to the $13 estimated write-off amount ($88 fee - $75 allowed amount). The estimated insurance payment is $48. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee. The estimated insurance payment is $70.40. In the example : The $25 deductible is subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $63. The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $63 product. The estimated insurance payment is $50.40. Dental Flat Fee Medicare Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the fee. The deductible is subtracted from the product. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee, leaving $70.40. The $25 deductible is subtracted from the $70.40 product. The estimated insurance payment is $45.40. Medical Flat Fee Medicare Plan Bluebook Entry Deductibles Do Not Apply The allowed amount is subtracted from the fee. The difference is the estimated write-off amount. topics.title: Estimating Insurance Coverage Information
topics.body: Estimating Insurance Coverage Information Trojan Estimating Insurance Coverage Information For plans linked to Trojan, the Trojan coverage estimates are used for insurance bluebook estimation. If a plan does not have a bluebook prior to linking it with a Trojan plan, and the Trojan plan has a Fees tab, Trojan data overwrites the plan data. If the Trojan plan does not have a Fees tab, the data is not overwritten. When an insurance plan record is displayed, you can view the bluebook entries by clicking Bluebook on the toolbar. The Bluebook Entries list window is displayed. If Trojan fees are used to update the bluebook, T is displayed in the tag field. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Estimating Insurance for Percentage Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Estimating Insurance for Percentage Plans Percentage or indemnity plans pay a percentage of the cost to perform a procedure. These percentages are defined for each procedural class. Example: A plan pays 50 percent of all preventive work completed on a covered patient. The calculations show the estimations when the deductible is subtracted first or last. The calculations are based upon the following example: The fee is $88, the allowed amount is $75, the bluebook insurance payment is $60, the coverage is 80%, the copayment is $30, and the deductible is $25. The insurance estimation calculations used for dental and medical percentage plans are: Plan Type Calculations Dental Percentage Plan Bluebook Entry No Deductible The estimated insurance payment equals the insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The estimated insurance payment is $60. Dental Percentage Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the allowed amount. The difference is multiplied by the procedural percentage amount. In the example : The $25 deductible is subtracted from the $75 allowed amount, leaving $50. The 80% coverage percentage for the procedure is multiplied by $50. The estimated insurance payment is $40. Dental Percentage Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the allowed amount. The deductible is subtracted from the product. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by $75, leaving $60. The $25 deductible is subtracted from $60. The estimated insurance payment is $35. Dental Percentage Plan No Bluebook Entry No Deductible The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the fee. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee. The estimated insurance payment is $70.40. Dental Percentage Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the fee. The difference is multiplied by the coverage percentage. In the example : The $25 deductible is automatically subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $63. The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by $63. The estimated insurance payment is $50.40. Dental Percentage Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the fee. The deductible is subtracted from the product. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee, leaving $70.40. The $25 deductible is subtracted from $70.40. The estimated insurance payment is $45.40. Medical Percentage Plan Bluebook Entry Deductibles Do Not Apply The estimated insurance payment equals the insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The estimated insurance payment is $60. Medical Percentage Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductibles Do Not Apply The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the fee. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee. The estimated insurance payment is $70.40. Tip If the plan pays something other than a percentage of your fees, set up bluebook entries. If the fees equal the percentage plan allotted amount, you do not need to set up a bluebook entry. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Estimating Insurance for Flat Fee PPO Plans
topics.body: Estimating Insurance for Flat Fee PPO Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information > Understanding Preferred Provider (PPO) Plans Estimating Insurance for Flat Fee PPO Plans The following table explains the insurance estimation calculations used for dental and medical flat fee PPO plans. The calculations show the estimations when the deductible is subtracted first or last. The calculations are based upon the following example: The fee is $88, the allowed amount is $75, the bluebook insurance payment is $60, the coverage is 80%, the copayment is $30, and the deductible is $25. The insurance estimation calculations used for dental and medical flat fee PPO plans are: Plan Type Calculations Dental Flat Fee PPO Plan Bluebook Entry No Deductible The allowed amount is subtracted from the fee. The difference is the estimated write-off amount. The estimated write-off amount is added to the insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The $75 allowed amount is subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $13. The $13 difference is added to the $60 insurance payment entered in the bluebook. The estimated insurance payment is $73. Dental Flat Fee PPO Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the estimated insurance payment entered in the bluebook. The difference is added to the estimated write-off amount. In the example : The $25 deductible amount is subtracted from the $60, leaving $35. The $35 difference is added to the $13 estimated write-off amount ($88 - $75). The estimated insurance payment is $48. Dental Flat Fee PPO Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The deductible is automatically subtracted from the estimated insurance payment entered in the bluebook. The difference is added to the estimated write-off amount. In the example : The $25 deductible amount is subtracted from the $60, leaving $35. The $35 difference is added to the $13 estimated write-off amount ($88 - $75). The estimated insurance payment is $48. Dental Flat Fee PPO Plan No Bluebook Entry No Deductible The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the fee. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee. The estimated insurance payment is $70.40. Dental Flat Fee PPO Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the fee. The difference is multiplied by the coverage percentage. In the example : The $25 deductible is automatically subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $63. The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by $63. The estimated insurance payment is $50.40. Dental Flat Fee PPO Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the fee. The deductible is subtracted from the product. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee, leaving $70.40. The $25 deductible is subtracted from $70.40. The estimated insurance payment is $45.40. Medical Flat Fee PPO Plan Bluebook Entry Deductibles Do Not Apply The allowed amount is subtracted from the fee. The difference is the estimated write-off amount. The estimated write-off amount is added to the insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The $75 allowed amount is subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $13. The $13 difference is added to the $60 insurance payment entered in the bluebook. The estimated insurance payment is $73. Medical Flat Fee PPO Plan No Blueb

topics.title: Estimating Insurance for PPO Percentage Plans
topics.body: Estimating Insurance for PPO Percentage Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information > Understanding Preferred Provider (PPO) Plans Estimating Insurance for PPO Percentage Plans The calculations show the estimations when the deductible is subtracted first or last. The calculations are based upon the following example: The fee is $88, the allowed amount is $75, the bluebook insurance payment is $60, the coverage is 80%, the copayment is $30, and the deductible is $25. T he insurance estimation calculations used for dental PPO percentage plans are: Plan Type Calculations Dental PPO Percentage Plan Bluebook Entry No Deductible The allowed amount is subtracted from the fee. The difference is the estimated write-off amount. The estimated write-off amount is added to the insurance payment entered in the bluebook. In the example : The $75 allowed amount is subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $13. The $13 difference is added to the $60 insurance payment entered in the bluebook. The estimated insurance payment is $73. Dental PPO Percentage Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the allowed amount. The difference is multiplied by the coverage percentage. The product is added to the write-off amount (allowed amount subtracted from the fee). In the example : The $25 deductible is subtracted from the $75 allowed amount, leaving $50. The $50 difference is multiplied by the 80% coverage percentage. The $40 product is added to the $13 estimated write-off amount ($88 - $75). The estimated insurance payment is $53. Dental PPO Percentage Plan Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The allowed amount is multiplied by the coverage percentage. The deductible is subtracted from the product. The difference is added to the estimated write-off amount. In the example : The $75 allowed amount is multiplied by the 80% coverage percentage, leaving $60. The $25 deductible amount is subtracted from the $60, leaving $35. The $35 difference is added to the $13 estimated write-off amount ($88 - $75). The estimated insurance payment is $48. Dental PPO Percentage Plan No Bluebook Entry No Deductible The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the fee. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee. The estimated insurance payment is $70.40. Dental PPO Percentage Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted First The deductible is automatically subtracted from the fee. The difference is multiplied by the coverage percentage. In the example : The $25 deductible is automatically subtracted from the $88 fee, leaving $63. The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by $63. The estimated insurance payment is $50.40. Dental PPO Percentage Plan No Bluebook Entry Deductible Subtracted Last The coverage percentage is automatically multiplied by the fee. The deductible is subtracted from the product. In the example : The 80% coverage percentage is multiplied by the $88 fee, leaving $70.40. The $25 deductible is subtracted from $70.40. The estimated insurance payment is $45.40. Tip If the plan pays something other than a percentage of your fees, set up bluebook entries. If the fees equal the percentage plan allotted amount, you do not need to set up a bluebook entry. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Exiting the Charting Module Using the SoftDent Charting Module Exiting the Charting Module To exit the charting module, select an option: To exit and return to the software : Select File > Close Charting Module or press Ctrl + F4. The Expert menu commands are: Office Expert : Run experts, set up expert options, take action for an expert, clear experts, and dictate when to run the Office Expert. Letter Expert : Add, edit, or delete Letter Expert templates. Contact Expert : Load a contact list or set the list as an Office Expert item. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Exam Options Toolbar

Click the icon for the description of the Exam Options toolbar buttons. Click an exam button and click this button to display a new exam. Click this button to edit the currently displayed exam. topics.body: Using Exams Tab Toolbars Charting Exams Using Exams Tab Toolbars The Common, Patient Exams, and Exam Options toolbars can be displayed in the Toolbars section on the Exams tab. To use the Common toolbar, see Using the Common Toolbar . Related Topics Using the Patient Exams Toolbar Using the Exam Options Toolbar Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Exporting Reports to Excel
topics.body: Exporting Reports to Excel Exporting Reports to Excel You can export several of the reports in the software to Excel. In many cases, you have the option of exporting the report information in the same format as the printed report, as well as exporting the information as raw data, which you can further manipulate in Excel. The following example describes how to export the Alphabetical Patient List report to Excel, but the process is similar for all reports that include the Excel output option. To export an alphabetical list of patients to Excel: Select Reports > Patient > Alphabetical . The Output Options window is displayed. Select Excel and click OK . The Patient List Report window is displayed. In the Patient Names section, select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. In the Patient User Codes section, select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. The following options are selected in the Excel Options section: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Click OK . The Select File Name window is displayed. Type a new file name, if necessary, or accept the default file name and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Exporting an Image
topics.body: Exporting an Image Exporting an Image To export a patient image to another location outside of the SoftDent database: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient. Click Show Images on the Express bar. The thumbnail viewer is displayed at the bottom of the screen. Right-click on the image you want to export. Select Export . The Save As window is displayed. Select a location and filename for the image you want to export. Select the image file type in which to save the exported image. Click Save . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Exiting the Software
topics.body: Exiting the Software Getting Started Exiting the Software Exit the program before turning off your computer. To exit the software: Select File > Exit . Click the Windows Close icon, or click the Exit SoftDent command located in the Practice Central window. A message is displayed. Click Yes to exit the application. If you have any scheduled reports that are overdue, you are prompted to print them before exiting the software. Click No to exit immediately. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding a Facility Record
topics.body: Adding a Facility Record Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using Other Facilities Adding a Facility Record To add a facility record: Select List > Other Facility . The Other Facility List window is displayed. Press Enter or Esc or click any list entry. Select Add on the toolbar. The Add Other Facility window is displayed. In the Facility Name field, enter the name of the facility you want to add. Click OK . The Other Facility record is displayed. The ID is assigned automatically and cannot be changed. Type the address and telephone numbers. To enter additional phone or fax numbers, or e-mail addresses, click More Phone Numbers . Type a contact name in the Contact field. Type the facility�s identification number, codes, and national provider ID in the Facility ID, Facility Codes, and Facility NPI fields. Type a note in the Notes field. The note is displayed in the Other Facility List window. The Claim Info field displays the name of the facility as it is displayed on insurance reports. This field defaults to the name you entered in the Facility Name field, but can be changed. Select the appropriate website address from the Web Link drop-down list. You cannot browse to a website to enter the address in this field. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Family Recall Appointments at Check Out
topics.body: Scheduling Family Recall Appointments at Check Out Scheduling Appointments > Scheduling Recall Appointments for the Family Scheduling Family Recall Appointments at Check Out If a patient wants to schedule recall appointments for the family while he or she is checking out: Drag the patient from the InOffice list to the Schedule Family icon at the bottom of the screen, or highlight the patient and double-click the icon. Follow the instructions in Scheduling Recall Appointments for the Family . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Fee Schedules
topics.body: Using Fee Schedules Handling Transactions and Codes Using Fee Schedules Use fee schedules to attach several charges to a code. In a fee schedule, you can: Assign a different fee for each doctor or incorporate unique requirements, such as a senior citizen discount. Enter up to 27 different fee schedules for each code. Fee schedule codes are between 102000.00 and 102026.00. You must match the schedule to the code. Example : Fee schedule four is coded 102004. To assign a fee schedule, see Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes . Related Topics Copying a Fee Schedule Changing a Fee Schedule Updating a Fee Schedule Updating Bluebook Fees Copying a Bluebook Between Plans Copying an Allowed Amount from Plan to Plan Need more information? topics.title: Fields in the ePostings Payment Detail Window
topics.body: Fields in the ePostings Payment Detail Window Fields in the ePostings Payment Detail Window The following fields are displayed in the ePostings Payment Detail window. ePostings Payment Detail Window Fields Field Description Patient Displays the patient last name, first name, and middle initial. If the line item is for a Provider Adjustment, there is no associated patient, and **Provider Adj** is displayed. Guarantor Displays the Guarantor for the patient. If the line item is for a Provider Adjustment, this column is blank. From Displays the start Date of Service for the claim. If the line item is for a Provider Adjustment, the column displays // . To Displays the end Date of Service for the claim. If the line item is for a Provider Adjustment, this column displays // . Clm Amt Displays the value in the Total Claim Amount field for each ePostings Payment Detail record in the list. If the line item is for a Provider Adjustment, this column displays 0.00. Payment Displays the value in the Claim Payment Amount field for each ePostings Payment Detail record in the list. If the line item is for a Provider Adjustment, this is the amount of the adjustment. Pat Amt Displays the value in the Patient Responsibility Amount field for each ePostings Payment Detail record in the list. If the line item is for a Provider Adjustment, this column displays 0.00 . WO Amt Displays the write off amount for each ePostings Payment Detail record in the list. If the line item is for a Provider Adjustment, this column displays 0.00 . Other Amt Claim ID Displays the claim ID for each ePostings Payment Detail record in the list. If the line item is for a claim that has not yet been matched to a SoftDent claim, this column displays NEED . If the line item is for a Provider Adjustment, the column displays N/A . Status Displays the following codes: Need Claim � Cnfirm Clm � Verify Distrib � Manual Distrib � Confirmed � Ready � Denied � Pre Auth � Reversal � Not Postable � Already Posted � Final Check this box to indicate that the ePostings payment associated with this claim is a final payment for that claim. All Final statuses receive a check by default. All Denied statuses receive a check by default and cannot be changed. Hold Check this option to prevent claims that are otherwise ready for posting from being processed. Initially, all ePostings Claim level entries will have a Hold status of OFF and the option is off. If you click on the option of an ePostings Claim level entry that is not on hold, the claim's hold status is set to ON, the option is toggled on, and the entry moves to the bottom of the list. As long as one associated claim is on hold, the ePostings Payment record associated with this claim is also on Hold. If you click on the option of an ePostings Claim level entry that is on hold, the claim's Hold status is set to OFF, the option is toggled off, and the entry moves back to its proper place in the current sort order. When no associated claims are on hold, the ePostings Payment record associated with this claim is no longer on Hold. For any sort option chosen, the ePostings records on "hold" will appear at the bottom -- then subsequently sorted by the selected option. Posting Status Displays the status of the selected entry and what you need to do before the payment can be posted. Distribution Status Displays the status of the match between the SoftDent estimation and the ePostings recommended adjustments. Claim # The claim number from the SoftDent data

topics.title: File Menu Commands
topics.body: File Menu Commands Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Main Menu Bar File Menu Commands Use the File menu to access the Find window for many features, view practice information, upload Trojan information, and exit the software. The File menu commands are: Account : Search for account records by the name, Social Security number, or account identification number for the guarantor 1 assigned to the account. Patient : Search for patient records by the name, Social Security number, chart number, or identification number of the patient. Appointments : Search for patient appointments by the name, Social Security number, chart number, or identification number of the patient. Provider : Search for a provider by name or identification number. Referring Dr : Search for a doctor by name or identification number. Employer : Search for an employer by name or identification number. Ins. Co : Search for an insurance company by name or identification number. Ins. Plan : Search for an insurance plan by group number, name, or identification number. Claim : Search for an insurance claim by the last name, patient identification number, or claim number. ADA Codes : Search for active or all ADA codes. Diagnosis : Search for a diagnosis code by code or description. Pharmacy : Search for a pharmacy by name or identification number. Labs : Search for a lab by name or identification number. Prescription Drugs : Search for a prescription drug by name or identification number. Practice : Display general information, office hours, contact information, default ADA codes, and identification numbers for your practice. Zip Codes : Search for a zip code or city. Trojan : Display the Monthly Update or Initial Upload commands; dictate whether uploaded information is to override the current company and plan information. Other Facility : Search for a facility by name or identification number. Exit : Exit the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using File Menu Utilities
topics.body: Using File Menu Utilities Utilities Using File Menu Utilities It is not recommended that you run SoftDent Utilities on a weekly, monthly, or yearly basis. You typically run utilities in case of database corruption, most commonly expressed as SDWin DBM Error, File No. ### Error = # . There might be other instances where utilities are used to resolve or troubleshoot an issue. You should run utilities only when you are instructed to do so by a SoftDent technical support representative. The following File menu utilities are available: Rebuild/Reindex Find/Repair Corrupt Find Largest ID Edit System File Display Encoded Passwords Reset Holiday.Def File Import Text File Recover From Faircom Crash Related Topics Running the Rebuild/Reindex Utility Running the Find/Repair Corrupt Utility Running the Find Largest ID Utility Running the Edit System File Utility Running the Display Encoded Passwords Utility Running the Reset Holiday.Def File Utility Running the Import Text File Utility Running the Recover From Faircom Crash Utility Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Files to Back Up Backing Up Data Files to Back Up The location of files varies according to your network setup. It is recommended that you use separate backup tapes for different applications. If you have multiple sets of data residing in multiple locations, be sure to back up each set. Need more information? topics.title: Filling the InOffice Window
topics.body: Filling the InOffice Window Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Filling the InOffice Window Before you can use InOffice , y ou must populate the window with patient and appointment information for the current day. The information from the scheduler is used to fill the InOffice window. To fill in the InOffice window: Select an option: Select List > InOffice. Click InOffice Window in the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. In any window, press Alt + F5. A blank InOffice window is displayed. Click Fill InOffice on the toolbar. The Fill InOffice window is displayed. The current date is displayed in the Fill For Date field. You can enter another date if necessary. Select an option: To print routing slips for each patient that is scheduled for an appointment for the current day : Select Printer Router? . If this option is selected, the Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . To print routing slips manually, see Manually Printing Routing Slips . If a provider-preferred routing slip is specified in the provider record, that form is printed. To remove any appointments left from the previous day : Select Clear InOffice and click OK . To restrict the appointments that are displayed in the window to a specific set of user codes : Type the codes in the Only include patient with the User Codes field and click OK . If the date in the Last History column is red, the patient is due for a new medical history. The medical history interval is set in the Recall Settings window. For more information, see Setting Up Recalls . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Finance Charge User Codes
topics.body: Using Finance Charge User Codes Setting Up Accounts > Understanding User Codes Using Finance Charge User Codes Use finance charge user codes in the Account window to override finance charge default system settings. When you enter a finance charge code (F3, F6, F9), the default finance charge settings entered in the Billing Statement Setup window are overridden. Example : Your system settings dictate all accounts that are 90 or more days overdue are charged a 2% monthly finance charge. Mr. Smith, however, has always been late with his payment, so you type F3 in the User Codes field in the Account window to charge Mr. Smith the 2% monthly finance charge after his account is overdue only 30 days. Check state regulations for finance charge guidelines. Assign a finance charge percentage in the Billing Statement Settings window before applying finance charges to an overdue account. The finance user codes are: F3 : Finance charges are based on an outstanding balance that is greater than 30 days old. F6 : Finance charges based on an outstanding balance that is greater than 60 days old. F9 : Finance charges based on an outstanding balance that is greater than 90 days old. User codes: Can be entered in any combination Must be entered as an unbroken string�no spaces or commas Enter finance charge user codes as characters; do not use the function keys. Example : F6MX To view a printable job aid that lists the default finance charge and account codes, and gives you a place to record user-defined codes for your practice, see Account User Codes . Related Topics Using Account User Codes Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Finding Answers
topics.body: Finding Answers Finding Answers Use the following resources to get answers to questions about your software: Online Help Your software includes online help. You can find answers through context sensitive topics by pressing F1. You can also use the Search function to look for information about specific features. Carestream Dental Institute You can access online training by logging in to the Carestream Dental Institute. The Exchange Ask a SoftDent expert a question, make a suggestion, or see how other dental offices use the software on The Exchange , Carestream Dental's online user community. Technical Support You can contact support representatives for assistance from 8 am to 8 pm EST Monday through Thursday, and from 8 am to 7 pm EST on Friday. topics.title: Finding a Record
topics.body: Finding a Record Getting Started > Using List Windows > Using Toolbar Options in a List Window Finding a Record To search for a record: Click Find. Type the search criteria in the Find field and press Enter, Tab, or Esc. The matching record is displayed and highlighted. By default, the Active Only checkbox is selected, limiting your search to active records. To include inactive records in your search, deselect the Active Only checkbox. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Find to Search for a Record
topics.body: Using Find to Search for a Record Getting Started > Searching for Records Using Find to Search for a Record To find a record using the List menu or Power bar button: From the List menu, select the menu item corresponding to the record type or select the Power bar button. The list window is displayed. Select the search method from the Sort By section. See Record Search Methods for a list of search methods for each record type. Type the search criteria in the Find field. The results of your search are displayed, and the matching record is highlighted. The Find field recognizes information separated by a comma when searching by name. Example : To search for a patient record by Social Security number, select S.S. No from the Sort By section, type the Social Security number in the Find field, and press Enter. By default, the Active Only checkbox is selected, limiting your search to active records. To include inactive records in your search, deselect the Active Only checkbox. To display the record, double-click it. Related Topics Changing Search Result Sort Order Record Search Methods Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Fix Accounts with Invalid Plans Utility
topics.body: Running the Fix Accounts with Invalid Plans Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Fix Accounts with Invalid Plans Utility Run this utility to update accounts with invalid insurance plan IDs that are created when you delete an insurance plan. To fix accounts with invalid insurance plans: Instruct the users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Fix Accounts With Invalid Plans . The Update Accounts With Invalid Insurance Plans window is displayed. Select an option: Set the Insurance Plan of all Accounts with Invalid Insurance Plans to : Click the drop-down arrow and select a plan from the list. Set the Insurance Plan of all Accounts with Invalid Insurance Plans to 0 . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Fix Dr $ Utility
topics.body: Running the Fix Dr $ Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Fix Dr $ Utility Run this utility to find all transactions for a specified production doctor and change the collection doctor. However, you cannot change the collection doctor in the provider record from this utility. Save a complete backup in addition to your regular backup before running this utility. To change production doctor information: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Fix Dr $ . The Transaction Doctor to Pay Fix window is displayed. Type the provider identification number for the production doctor for whom you want to change production information in the Enter Production Dr field. Type the provider identification number for the production doctor whom you want to be the collection doctor in the Enter Dr to Pay for Production Dr field. Click OK . A message asks you whether to run the utility. Click Yes . A message informs you how many transactions were scanned and changed. Click OK . A message informs you the process is complete. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting Budget Plan Coupon Formats
topics.body: Selecting Budget Plan Coupon Formats Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans Selecting Budget Plan Coupon Formats You can print budget plan coupons for fixed loan account and patient budget plans. To specify a budget coupon format: Select System > Form Selection . The Form Selection window is displayed in EDIT mode. Select the form from the Budget Plan Coupon drop-down list and click OK .
