topics.title: Using Formatted Fields
topics.body: Using Formatted Fields Getting Started > Entering Data Using Formatted Fields Formatted fields are fields used to enter phone numbers, zip codes, percentages, and dollar amounts without having to type dashes and parentheses. The punctuation marks are displayed in the field. As you type data in a formatted field, the punctuation marks function as follows: The punctuation mark acts as a placeholder that is skipped over as you type information. Example : In a phone number field, the cursor moves to the right of the parenthesis after you type the three-digit area code. The punctuation mark is added to the field after the cursor is moved to another field. Example : After you type 80 in the Percent field in the Coverage tab of the Insurance Plan window and move the cursor to the next field, the percent sign is automatically inserted at the end of the field. The following formatted fields are used in the software: Time : You do not need to type the colon that is displayed between the hour and minutes. Example: Type 012 and press Tab. 1:20p is displayed in the field. Date : You do not need to type the forward slash marks that are displayed between the month, day, and year. Type a zero before single-digit months. Example: Type 02 for February. Dollar Amount : You do not need to type the dollar sign in formatted fields used for dollar amounts. When you type a decimal point as part of the amount, the cursor is automatically moved to the right of the decimal point. Percentage : You do not need to type the percentage sign in formatted fields used for percentages. The sign is displayed after the cursor is moved to another field. Phone and Fax numbers : You do not need to type the parenthesis or hyphen when typing information into formatted fields that are used for phone numbers. Social Security numbers : You do not need to type the hyphens used to divide Social Security numbers. Z ip Code : You do not need to type the hyphen used to divide the standard five-digit code and the four-digit extension. When adding an account, patient, provider, or referring doctor, the City, State, and Zip Code fields default to the practice�s address. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Find Largest ID Utility
topics.body: Running the Find Largest ID Utility Utilities > Using File Menu Utilities Running the Find Largest ID Utility Run this utility t o determine the largest identification number in a data file. Running this utility ensures that the next entry made into the file has a unique identification number. If necessary, the system file is reset to ensure that the next identification number used is one greater than the largest existing identification number. When the account file is reset, the next identification number is 100 greater than the largest account identification number. Run this utility if an error message instructs you to run the FIND LARGEST IDs utility or when instructed to by a technical support representative. To find the largest identification number used for files: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select File > Find Largest IDs or select Find Largest Id . The Find Largest ID window is displayed. Select an option: To find the largest identification number used in specific files : Select the file from the list. To find the largest identification number used in all files : Click Select All . Click OK . A message asks you to run the utility. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Find/Replace IDs Utility
topics.body: Running the Find/Replace IDs Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Find/Replace IDs Utility Run this utility to delete duplicate employer, insurance company, or insurance plan identification numbers. To remove duplicate employer, insurance company, or insurance plan records: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Find/Replace IDs . The Find and Replace IDs window is displayed. Select an option: To delete duplicate employer records : Select Employer Id , type the identification number associated with the duplicate record in the Find field, and type the identification number associated with the master record in the Replace field. To delete duplicate insurance company records : Select Insurance Co Id , type the identification number associated with the duplicate record in the Find field, and type the identification number associated with the master record in the Replace field. To delete duplicate insurance plan records : Select Insurance Plan Id , type the identification number associated with the duplicate record in the Find field, and type the identification number associated with the master record in the Replace field. Click Apply . A message asks you to confirm the identification number replacement. Click Yes . A message informs you the process is complete. Click OK . Click Close . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Fingerprint Scanner
topics.body: Using the Fingerprint Scanner Using the Fingerprint Scanner You can scan and record employee fingerprints to use in the following situations: Logging in or out: Instead of typing a user name and password, an employee with a fingerprint in the database can scan his fingerprint to log in or out of the software. See Accessing the Software for more information. Clocking in or out: Instead of typing a user name and password, an employee with a fingerprint in the database can scan his fingerprint to clock in or out. See Clocking In and Out for more information. Changing login: Instead of typing a user name and password, an employee with a fingerprint in the database can scan his fingerprint to log in without exiting the software. See Changing Logins for more information. You must install the fingerprint scanner and software to use this feature. When the fingerprint scanner is attached to a workstation and there is at least one fingerprint in the database, the SoftDent Login window and SoftDent Clock In window instruct the employee how to proceed. You must have a license to use the fingerprint scanner. Related Topics Installing the M2SYS Fingerprint Software Fingerprint Registration Security Right Using the Fingerprint Maintenance Window Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Follow-Up Appointments
topics.body: Scheduling Follow-Up Appointments Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Scheduling Follow-Up Appointments After follow-up appointments have been entered using the Electronic Router in the operatory, front office staff can post the transactions performed during the visit. If any checkout option is selected, and a follow-up appointment is entered using the Electronic Router, the Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters window is displayed. To schedule a follow-up appointment: In the Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters window, specify the search criteria, and click OK . The first available time slot that meets the search criteria is displayed in the Daily Appointments window. Select an option: To schedule the patient appointment for the time slot : Click Make Appt . To find the next available time slot : Click Next . When a suitable time slot is displayed, click Make Appt . If more than one follow-up appointment was entered, the Setup Auto-Scheduling Parameters window is displayed. Repeat this step to schedule the appointment. If a follow-up visit was entered but not scheduled, a message that asks you to add the appointment to the Tickler file is displayed. You must either schedule the appointment or enter it into the Tickler file to exit the Auto-Scheduling window. Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Fingerprint Registration Security Right
topics.body: Fingerprint Registration Security Right Using the Fingerprint Scanner Fingerprint Registration Security Right The Fingerprint Registration security right enables you to access the Fingerprint Maintenance window, where you can acquire, verify, and delete employee fingerprints. This security right should not be assigned to all users. By default, the right is assigned to the person with User ID 1 security permissions, as well as the office manager. This right is available in the standard security windows ( System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User > Miscellaneous ) and can be used with both password and user login overrides. See Setting Up Security for more information about setting security permissions. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Fingerprint Maintenance Window
topics.body: Using the Fingerprint Maintenance Window Using the Fingerprint Scanner Using the Fingerprint Maintenance Window The Fingerprint Maintenance window enables you to scan, verify, or delete employee fingerprints and is accessible from the Employee List window and the Rights by User List security window. You must have the Fingerprint Registration security right to access the Fingerprint Maintenance window. To scan, verify, or delete a fingerprint: Select an option: In the Employee List window: Select List > Employees . The Employee List window is displayed. Select an employee and click Fingerprint . The Fingerprint Maintenance window is displayed. In the Rights by User List window: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights by User . The Rights by User List window is displayed. Select an employee and click Fingerprint . The Fingerprint Maintenance window is displayed. Select an option: To scan a new fingerprint: Click Scan . The Fingerprint Enrollment window is displayed. Place your finger on the scanner and click in the box at the right of the window. After you have scanned your finger, click Register . To re-scan an existing fingerprint: Click Scan . The Update Fingerprint window is displayed. Place your finger on the scanner and click in the box at the right of the window. After you have scanned your finger, click Update . To verify a fingerprint: Click Verify . The Identify Fingerprint window is displayed. Place your finger on the scanner. If the software finds a match for your fingerprint, a message is displayed, stating that the verification was successful. If the fingerprint record is not found, a message is displayed, asking you to verify the fingerprint again or delete and then re-scan the fingerprint. To delete a fingerprint: Click Delete . If the fingerprint is deleted, a message is displayed, stating that the fingerprint was successfully deleted. If the fingerprint is not deleted, a message is displayed, stating that the delete process failed and to try again. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Generic Clinical Notes
topics.body: Creating Generic Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes > Handling Generic Clinical Notes Creating Generic Clinical Notes To create a generic clinical note: Select an option: Select List > Clinical Notes > Generic Clinical Notes . In the patient chart, from the Overview, Restorative, Perio, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tabs, select Clinical Notes > Generic Notes . The Generic Clinical Notes List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The Clinical Note window is displayed. Complete the following information: Select the procedure code to which the note refers from the ADA Code drop-down list. You can select only a production or explosion code from the list. Select the provider from the list. If no provider is selected, the note is assigned to provider 0. Type a description of the note's contents in the Reference field. Type the text in the Note Text field. To use this note as a default when posting the procedure, check Use this text as the default for this ADA Code . Click OK . Need more information? topics.title: Generating a List of Inactive Patients
topics.body: Generating a List of Inactive Patients Generating a List of Inactive Patients To generate a list of inactive patients: Select Reports > Patient > Alphabetical . The Patient List Report window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . Type the letter I (I denotes a patient that is inactive) in the Include field under the Patient User Codes heading. Delete any user codes from the Exclude field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Generating a List of Patients Who are Members of Specific Insurance Companies Generating a List of Patients Who are Members of Specific Insurance Companies To generate an alphabetical list of patients assigned to plans offered by an insurance company, sorted by company ID: Select Reports > Insurance > Patients by Company . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patients By Insurance Company Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending identification numbers. To list patients for only one company, type the company ID number in both the Starting ID and Ending ID fields. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code: Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code: Type the code in the Exclude field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating Reports Using Report Manager
topics.body: Generating Reports Using Report Manager Handling Reports Generating Reports Using Report Manager The Report Manager enables you to manage and coordinate the reports your practice must generate. For example, if you need to run the Daily Operatory Schedule, Missed Appointments Report, and Routing Slips at the end of every business day, the Report Manager enables you to save your report settings and run all reports with one command. Whether you schedule reports to run daily, weekly, or monthly, the software prompts you when entering and exiting the program if you have a report group scheduled to be run. In addition, you can group reports by provider or by purpose. Related Topics Setting Up a Report Manager Report Modifying Reports and Report Groups Deleting Report Groups and Reports Running Scheduled Reports Running Overdue Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating the Credit Card Expiration Date Report
topics.body: Generating the Credit Card Expiration Date Report Generating the Credit Card Expiration Date Report The Credit Card Expiration Date report generates a list of credit cards that expire between a specified date range. The report results include the patient name and identification number, the credit card name and number, and the date on which the card expires. Sample Report To generate the Credit Card Expiration Date report: Select Reports > Accounting > Credit Card Expiration Date . The Report Setup window is displayed. Select the start and ending dates to include in the report, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating User-Selected Reports
topics.body: Generating User-Selected Reports Handling Reports Generating User-Selected Reports User-selected reports enable you to create customized reports containing information you specify�how many accounts have been sent to collection agencies, for example, or which patients have not had a full-mouth x-ray in the last five years. With this feature, you can establish the criteria for the report and specify the information contained in it. All user-selected report setups can be saved, so you have to define the selection criteria only once. User-selected reports can be set up for patient and account information. Related Topics Using the Selection Criteria Window Creating User-Selected Reports Running User-Selected Reports Editing Predefined User-Selected Reports Deleting User-Selected Reports Using the Report Manager Printing User-Selected Labels Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating Report Information
topics.body: Generating Report Information Generating Report Information SoftDent contains many reports to help you manage your practice, maintain financial information, and identify practice trends. Report Frequency Your practice will not need to run every report or might only need to run a particular report from time to time. The following list gives the suggested frequency for running some key reports: Reports set off with a ♦ are those that must be run in order for the software to calculate and maintain up-to-date financial totals. Reports You Should Run Every Day: Daily Operatory Schedule Daily Registers Report Daysheet ♦ End-of-Day Callback Report Insurance Batch Preview Report Routing Slips Reports You Should Run Every Week: Charting Audit Deposit Slip Missed Appointments Report ♦ Tickler File Report Unsubmitted Claims Report ♦ Reports You Should Run Every Month: Account Aging Report ♦ Accountant's Earnings Report Insurance Check Distribution Report Statements ♦ Transactions For A Period Report ♦ Report Accuracy To generate reports, the software sorts through the information stored in your databases to calculate the numbers on reports. To reduce the time required to run reports, the software stores many of the numbers needed for accounting reports in a single file named RCVTOT.DAT (receivables totals). This file is updated whenever you run a final daysheet. The following actions affect the accuracy of your reports, particularly the accounting and practice management reports: Running the final daysheet: Updates the RCVTOT.DAT file. If you forget to run the final daysheet, the receivables amounts and totals will be inaccurate on your reports. Deleting patients and/or accounts: Changes the information on reports. It is better to mark the account and/or patient as inactive. Most reports can be run to include or exclude patients and accounts that are marked inactive. Deleting providers or editing provider names with new provider information: Changes the numbers on reports. When a provider leaves the practice, mark the provider as inactive, and add new providers when new employees join the practice. Dangling Transactions Transaction information is saved in the TRANS.DAT file. Report accuracy can be affected if this file contains dangling transactions�transactions that are no longer associated with an account or patient. If your receivables totals do not match, or if transactions appear on a report without an associated patient, the TRANS.DAT file might contain dangling transactions. Reports that use information from the TRANS.DAT file�for example, the Transactions for a Period report, register reports, production reports, and collection reports�could contain dangling transactions. If you suspect your reports contain dangling transactions, contact Carestream support for assistance in resolving this problem. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: General System Settings
topics.body: General System Settings General System Settings The General System Settings window enables you to configure the settings for several areas of the software, including checkout preferences, default codes, service tax, patient budget plan, and account budget plan. To configure system settings: Select System > Change System Settings > General System . The General System Settings window is displayed. Click Edit and make changes to the following sections: System Date: To set the system date, click the drop-down arrow to select the system date from a calendar. Ledger Preferences: To specify whether the software should display insurance, walkout, or appointment prompts, click the drop-down arrow and select Ask to process , Do not process , or Automatically process . To automatically display the Payments window when you post a payment code during checkout or when manually adding a payment, select Automatically display the Payments screen when posting a payment code . Service Tax Settings: To specify whether the software should charge service tax, click the drop-down arrow in the Tax Method field, and select Pro Rata or Non-Pro Rata. Type the tax rate in the field. See Setting Up Service Tax for more information. Patient Budget Plan: To specify the patient budget plan code and default number of plan months, click the drop-down arrow in the Credit Code field and select the code from the ADA/Transaction Codes window. Type the number of months in the Default no. months field. Account Budget Plan: To enable revolving credit for account budget plans, select Enable REVOLVING CREDIT . Mobility: To enable Mobility for your SoftDent Cloud practice and allow users with a valid email address (defined in their Rights by User window) to access practice data from their mobile device and the web, select Enable Mobility . Note : This option is available only for SoftDent Cloud users. Click Printer Preferences to configure system printing preferences for routing slips, walkout statements, deposit slips, and so on. See Configuring System Printing Preferences . Click Phone Preferences to configure system phone preferences. See Configuring System Phone Preferences . Click OK . Related Topics Configuring System Printing Preferences Configuring System Phone Preferences Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating the Time Card Report
topics.body: Generating the Time Card Report Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card Generating the Time Card Report Generate the Time Card report to produce a list of Time Card entries for each employee. You can run the report by pay frequency and by start and end dates. The report also includes a summary of the type of hours worked (holiday, sick, vacation, and so on) for each employee on the report. The Time Card report can also include totals for unpaid hours and overtime hours if the Track Unpaid Time option and a track overtime option are selected in the Time Card Preferences window. See Setting Time Card Preferences for more information. Before generating the audit trail, see Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides . Sample Report To generate the Time Card report: Select Reports > Accounting > Time Card . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an option and click OK . The Time Card Report Setup window is displayed. Select the pay frequency and the start and stop dates. Select the employee identification numbers to include in the report. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected in the Excel Options section: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. To print an employee�s entries on a page, select Print each employee on single page . To include only the employee header information and the totals for each time type, select Print Totals Only . To include inactive employee information, select Include Inactive Employees . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Goal Scheduling
topics.body: Goal Scheduling Setting Up the Scheduler Goal Scheduling Scheduling by production goals enables you to: Schedule for 12 equally productive months. Create a consistent cash flow. Set a goal for the entire practice or for an individual provider. Set daily or monthly goals. Monitor your scheduled and production amounts to determine whether your office is meeting the goal. To set up production goals, set a daily goal for the practice or provider and then copy it to each business day or set a monthly goal and use the software to calculate your daily production goal. Before you set up goal scheduling, enter the following to ensure that the number of working days in the month is accurate: Your office hours Provider vacation days Holidays After you have set your goals and scheduled them, you can track the goal progress. Related Topics Setting Daily Goals Setting Monthly Production Goals Copying Goals Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating Electronic Billing Statements
topics.body: Generating Electronic Billing Statements Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Generating Electronic Billing Statements If you currently subscribe to Automated Statements to set up, generate, and transmit electronic billing statements, see Using Automated Statements . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating Automated Statements
topics.body: Generating Automated Statements eServices > Using the Automated Statements Service > Using Automated Statements Generating Automated Statements To generate Automated Statements: Select Reports > Accounting > Statements > Electronic > Electronic Batch Preview . The Electronic Statements window is displayed. Select the starting and ending names to include in the report using the drop-down lists in the Start and End fields. Select from the following options: To include billing statement information for accounts that contain a specific user code : Type the user code in the Only include Accounts with field. To exclude billing statement information for accounts that contain a specific user code : Type the user code in the Exclude Accounts with field. Accounts that have been referred to a collection agency, are inactive, involved in a legal case, or have been flagged for exclusion are not included in the billing statement results. The C, I, L, and N user codes are displayed in Exclude Accounts with field by default. To apply a different billing charge to the billing statements : Type the information in the appropriate fields. To apply a different finance charge to the billing statements : Type the information in the appropriate fields. To itemize transactions for a specific number of days : Type the number of days in the Itemize transactions for the last _ days field. The default value for this field is 45. To specify the maximum number of pages to send when using Automated Statements: Select Not to exceed and type the maximum number of pages to send. To avoid the additional page charge for Automated Statements, type 1 to minimize the number of statement pages sent to the clearinghouse. To send statements only to patients who owe more than a certain amount : Type the minimum amount in the Send for Amount due greater than or equal to field. The default value for this field is $0.01. To include a message in the billing statement results : Type the message in the Statement Message field. To generate billing statements only for a specific provider : Select the provider identification number from the Providers section of the window. By default, billing statements are generated for all providers. Click OK . The Electronic Statements List window is displayed and contains a list of the electronic statements that are ready to be transmitted. Accounts that are more than 90 days overdue are displayed in red text. Accounts that have an invalid address are displayed in blue text. Accounts that have previously had a statement sent to the batch are displayed in green text. Accounts that have been marked to receive statements online but have no e-mail address are unchecked, are displayed in blue text, and display an INVALID E-MAIL status when selected. Select from the following options: To sort the list by account name, account balance, or amount due: Click the column label. To enter a new address : Select the account, click Edit Acct and make the changes. To exclude an account : Deselect the checkbox. To preview a specific statement : Select the account and click Preview . To preview all statements: Click Preview All . To remove a statement from the list of statements to be sent to the clearinghouse : Deselect it. To add an individual note to a statement : Type the note in the Personalized Statement Note for Selected Account field. Click Send . The Output Options - Electronic Batch Summary window is displayed. Select the output option for the Statement Summa

topics.title: Generating Outstanding Claims Reports
topics.body: Generating Outstanding Claims Reports eServices Generating Outstanding Claims Reports Typically, it takes no longer than 30 days for insurance companies to process electronic claims. Outstanding claims are defined as claims for which no final insurance payments have been posted. Post all current insurance payments before running this report. To determine which claims are outstanding, run the Outstanding Claims report every 30 days for electronic and paper claims. You can generate several reports: Outstanding Claims by Company : The report lists all outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical insurance claims sorted by insurance company. This report includes total claim amounts submitted, total estimated payments due, and total pre-authorization amounts for both primary and secondary claims. Outstanding Claims by Patient : The report lists all outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical insurance claims sorted by patient. This report includes total claim amounts submitted, total estimated payments due, and total pre-authorization amounts for both primary and secondary claims. Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Co.: The report lists all outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical insurance claims with a benefit owed to the patient, sorted by insurance company. Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. By Patient: The report lists all outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical insurance claims with a benefit owed to the patient, sorted by patient name. To generate an Outstanding Claims report: Select an option to run the reports from the SoftDent main menu: To generate the Outstanding Claims by Company report : Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Outstanding Claims by Co . To generate the Outstanding Claims by Patient report : Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Outstanding Claims by Patient . To generate the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Co. report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Co. To generate the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Patient report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Patient. The Output Options window is displayed. Select Printer and click OK . The appropriate Outstanding Insurance Claims window is displayed. In the Options section, select the options to include. To include electronic claims only, deselect all options except Include ECS Claims. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Header Bar
topics.body: Using the Header Bar Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Understanding the Enhanced Workspace Using the Header Bar The Enhanced Workspace Header bar: Is displayed beneath the Main Menu bar Enables you to view the name of the currently active window When you are viewing a patient record, the header bar displays the following patient-specific information: Outside the chart , the header bar for a patient record or the phone center displays the patient name and the following (if applicable): HIST = There is information in patient history. TP = The patient has a treatment plan. LC = The patient has a lab case. PIX = The patient has a portrait. Recall Overdue = The patient is past due for his or her recall appointment. [Medical Alert] = If a medical alert is present, it is displayed in brackets. Inside the chart , the header bar (also called the patient banner in the chart) displays the following: Patient Information--Displays the patient's portrait or avatar; patient ID, name, and nickname. -- Click the portrait or avatar to display the Patient Portrait window, where you can capture a portrait. See Adding a Patient Portrait for more information. -- Hover over the patient name and click the link to display the Patient Information window. See Setting Up Patients for more information. Chart Mode--Displays the chart mode currently in view. Quadrant View--Displays the quadrant currently in view. Medical Alerts--Displays any patient medical alerts. If the patient has multiple alerts and "..." is displayed in the Medical Alerts section, hover over the Medical Alerts section to view all of the alerts in the tooltip. Hover over the Medical Alerts section and click the link to display the Conditions tab of the Medical History window. See Understanding Medical Conditions for more information. Allergies--Displays any patient allergies. If the patient has multiple allergies and "..." is displayed in the Allergies section, hover over the Allergies section to view all of the allergies in the tooltip. Hover over the Allergies section and click the link to display the General tab of the Medical History window. See Adding Medical Histories for more information. Meds--Displays the number of medications the patient is currently taking. Hover over the Medications section and click the link to display the General tab of the Medical History window. See Adding Medical Histories for more information. Prescr--Displays the number of prescriptions the patient has been prescribed by the office. An "*" indicates a currently prescribed prescription. Hover over the Prescriptions section and click the link to display the Prescriptions for List window. See Handling Prescriptions for more inform ation. Provider--Displays the patient's assigned (preferred) provider. Hover over the Provider section and click the link to display the Provider List window. See Selecting Providers for more information. In Acct--Displays the number of patients in the same account as the selected patient. Hover over the In Acct section and click the link to display a list of patients assigned to the account. See Viewing Patients in an Account from Account Windows for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling HIPAA Contacts
topics.body: Handling HIPAA Contacts System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features Handling HIPAA Contacts You can add HIPAA consents, authorizations, disclosures, and complaints to a patient or account record. This enables you to track all HIPAA related correspondence. Related Topics Adding HIPAA Acknowledgement Contacts Adding a HIPAA Contact Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Showing and Hiding Toolbars
topics.body: Showing and Hiding Toolbars Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using Toolbars Showing and Hiding Toolbars Using the charting module, you can dictate the toolbars displayed on each tab. To show or hide a toolbar: To hide a toolbar : Select the toolbar, right-click, and select Hide This Toolbar . To display a hidden toolbar : Select any toolbar displayed on the tab, right-click, select Show/Hide Toolbars . The list of toolbars is displayed. Select the toolbar. A checkmark next to a toolbar indicates that it is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Showing or Hiding Office Expert Items
topics.body: Showing or Hiding Office Expert Items Using Experts > Using the Office Expert > Setting Up Office Expert Items Showing or Hiding Office Expert Items You can show or hide items displayed in the Office Expert window. You can run an office expert item without displaying it in the office expert item list. All items are run at the specified time, but only those selected to be viewed are displayed in the list. To run an office expert item, see Running Office Expert Items . Example : You can run the Yesterday�s Login Failures item, but because this item does not require any action, you can choose not to display it in the Office Expert window. If the security system is enabled, you must have the appropriate permission to perform this task. If the proper permissions are not set and you want to edit the criteria of an office expert item, see your software administrator. To hide or show an item: From the Experts menu, select Office Expert . The Office Expert window is displayed. Select Setup Experts from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Office Expert Setup window is displayed. Select an option: To hide an item : Deselect the checkbox in the Show column for the item. To show an item : Select the checkbox in the Show column for the item. Click OK . Office expert items are not displayed in the list until they have been run. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the HIPAA Acknowledgement Received Report
topics.body: Running the HIPAA Acknowledgement Received Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Running HIPAA Reports Running the HIPAA Acknowledgement Received Report The HIPAA Acknowledgement Received report g enerates a list of patients who have received a HIPAA notification. Sample Report To generate a list of patients who have received a HIPAA notification: Select Reports > Patient > HIPAA Acknowledgement Received . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Patient List Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the HIPAA Acknowledgement Outstanding Report
topics.body: Running the HIPAA Acknowledgement Outstanding Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Running HIPAA Reports Running the HIPAA Acknowledgement Outstanding Report The HIPAA Acknowledgement Outstanding report g enerates a list of patients who have not received a HIPAA notification. Sample Report To generate a list of patients who have not received a HIPAA notification: Select Reports > Patient > HIPAA Acknowledgement Outstanding . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Patient List Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing HIPAA-Specific Contacts
topics.body: Viewing HIPAA-Specific Contacts Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts Viewing HIPAA-Specific Contacts After you have added HIPAA-specific contacts to a patient or account record , y ou can display a list of HIPAA contacts from the Contacts for List window. For more information, see Handling HIPAA Contacts . To view HIPAA-specific contact information: Select an option: From a patient record : Select Contacts > By Type from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. From an account record : Select Contacts > By Type from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Contacts for List window is displayed. Click HIPAA Only or select it from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. After you review the information, click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding HIPAA and the InOffice Feature
topics.body: Understanding HIPAA and the InOffice Feature Tracking Patients in the Office Understanding HIPAA and the InOffice Feature The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) contains provisions for privacy and security of health care information. To help your office comply with HIPAA Privacy and Transaction regulations, you can: Designate private workstations Set InOffice privacy features The following features help you comply with the HIPAA Transactional standards: Patient Insurance plan Claim Referring doctor ADA/transaction code Contacts For Related Topics Setting InOffice Privacy Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting HIPAA Security and Privacy Options
topics.body: Setting HIPAA Security and Privacy Options System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features Setting HIPAA Security and Privacy Options When the software is installed , User ID 1 is automatically assigned the user name Doctor and the password Doctor12. This user has the highest level of security rights. Only User ID 1 can set HIPAA Security and Privacy options for other users. If you are upgrading from a version of the software prior to v8.0, the login name and password of User ID 1 are the same as the previous Access Level 0. To set HIPAA Privacy and HIPAA Security options: Select System > Change System Settings > HIPAA Options . The HIPAA Options window is displayed. Click Edit . To enforce strong password protection for all users, select Enable Strong Passwords . When you enable strong password protection, the following restrictions are enforced for each user when they access the software: The password must be between seven and fifteen characters long. The password must contain at least one letter. The password is case sensitive. The password must contain at least one digit. The user name cannot be embedded in the password. The password must not contain the three consecutive instances of the same character. The password expires every 90 days. The password cannot be the same as the last two user passwords. The password can be changed only once daily. A user is locked out of the software and is unable to login for 10 minutes after five failed attempts to access the software. To log out each user automatically after the application is idle for a specified amount of time, select Enable Automatic Logoff and type the number of minutes in the (wait _ minutes) field. To create an audit file that tracks login attempts, select Enable Audit Logging . See Viewing Audit Trails . To enable the HIPAA Privacy Acknowledgement Notification reminder, select HIPAA Privacy Acknowledgement Notification . To add a HIPAA Acknowledgment contact, see Adding HIPAA Acknowledgement Contacts . Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the HIPAA Security Audit Trail By Status Report
topics.body: Running the HIPAA Security Audit Trail By Status Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running HIPAA Security Audit Trail Reports Running the HIPAA Security Audit Trail By Status Report The HIPAA Security Audit Trail by Status report generates a list of failed log-ins due to password errors, as well as override attempts for features requiring security passwords. The report is sorted by status. Sample Report To generate a list of HIPAA Security audit entries by the status results: Select Reports > Audit Trail > HIPAA Security Audit Trail > By Access . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The HIPAA Security Audit Trail By Access window is displayed. Select the access result from the Security Status section. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the HIPAA Security Audit Trail By Date Report
topics.body: Running the HIPAA Security Audit Trail By Date Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running HIPAA Security Audit Trail Reports Running the HIPAA Security Audit Trail By Date Report The HIPAA Security Audit Trail by Date report generates a list of failed log-ins due to password errors within a specified date range. The report also lists override attempts for features requiring security passwords. Sample Report To generate a list of HIPAA Security audit entries by the date on which they were created: Select Reports > Audit Trail > HIPAA Security Audit Trail > By Date . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The HIPAA Security Audit Trail By Date window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the HIPAA Security Audit Trail By User Report
topics.body: Running the HIPAA Security Audit Trail By User Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running HIPAA Security Audit Trail Reports Running the HIPAA Security Audit Trail By User Report The HIPAA Security Audit Trail by User report generates a list of failed log-ins due to password errors, as well as override attempts for features requiring security passwords. The report is sorted by user. Sample Report To generate a list of HIPAA Security audit entries by the user who performed the task: Select Reports > Audit Trail > HIPAA Security Audit Trail > By User . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The HIPAA Security Audit Trail By User window is displayed. Type the starting and ending users in the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling History Appointments
topics.body: Enabling History Appointments Using the Scheduler > Using the History Appointments Window Enabling History Appointments Before using the History Appointments window, you must: Enable and set the properties for the feature Run the Missed Appointments report After you run the Missed Appointments report, all posted appointments can be displayed in the History Appointments window when a date range is selected. This feature is enabled by default, but you can disable it if you do not use this information. When the feature is enabled: Posted appointments are displayed in the InOffice window and can be displayed in the Patient Appointments window. These appointments are displayed in purple text in both windows. For more information, see Viewing the History Appointments from a Patient Record and Viewing History Appointments from the InOffice List . If this feature is not enabled, posted appointments are permanently deleted from the system. To enable History Appointments, see Setting Scheduler Defaults . Related Topics Setting History Appointments Properties Setting Posted Appointments Colors Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding History Appointments Window Options
topics.body: Understanding History Appointments Window Options Using the Scheduler > Using the History Appointments Window Understanding History Appointments Window Options The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu when using Enhanced Workspace are: Change Date Range : Select this command to display the History Appointments Date Range window. In this window, you can select another date range of posted appointments to view. View Appt : Click an appointment and select this command to display the Appointment Card window in SCAN mode. In this window, you can view, add, edit, and delete procedures scheduled for the appointment. Zoom : Click an appointment and select this command to display the Assistant window. In this window, you can view additional patient and appointment information. Patient Information : Select this command to display the Patient window. In this window, you can view the patient record. P atient Chart : Click an appointment and select this command to display the patient chart in the charting module. Patient Portrait : Click an appointment and select this command to display the patient portrait. If no portrait exists, a message is displayed. Show Images/Hide Images : Click an appointment and select the Show Images command to display portraits associated with the patient at the bottom of the Book At A Glance window. Select the Hide Images command to hide the patient images. Dental Imaging : This command is enabled only if you are using CS Imaging software. Click an appointment or time slot to access the software. MedVisor: Starts the MedVisor software. This command is enabled only if you have installed the MedVisor software. Select Book : Click a time slot or appointment and select this command to display the Scheduler Rooms List window. In this window, you can select a book to view. See Displaying Different Books . Select Room # : Click a time slot or appointment and select this command to display the Scheduler Rooms List window. In this window, you can select a room to view. See Viewing Additional Rooms . Related Topics Using the History Appointments Scheduler Icon Bar Using the History Appointments Appointment Submenu Commands Using the History Appointments Time Slot Submenu Commands Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Hooking Up Your Palm Organizer
topics.body: Hooking Up Your Palm Organizer Using PracticeToGo > Installing PracticeToGo Hooking Up Your Palm Organizer If your Palm organizer is already connected to your computer, SoftDent will prompt you to perform an integration or update when it is started. Otherwise, after installing SoftDent, install the Palm Desktop software and the HotSync cradle on the PC according to the instructions that came with your Palm Computing connected organizer. It is recommended that you become familiar with the Palm Desktop software before using the features of PracticeToGo. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Help Menu Commands
topics.body: Help Menu Commands Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Main Menu Bar Help Menu Commands U se the Help menu to use Help and view software information. The Help menu commands are: SoftDent Help View the online help system. What's New Video View a video highlighting the new features and enhancements in this release. Session Information Review the current user and working office information. Backup Information View a list of folders and files to include when backing up your data. SoftDent on the Web Display the SoftDent practice management software website. System Requirements on the Web Display the system requirements for SoftDent. Online Resource Center Display the Online Resource Center website, where you can search the online support database, review product notifications, and display announcements, special offers, discounts, and special events. Carestream Dental Institute Display the Carestream Dental Institute website, where you can participate in interactive web training and learn about new features and enhancements added to the software. Install Adobe Acrobat Reader Install the Adobe Acrobat Reader so you can read the online documentation. About SoftDent Enter activation codes, install licenses, review license detail information, view physical memory and disk space, and review processor and operating system information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Reports - Audit Trail Reports
topics.body: Handling Reports - Audit Trail Reports Handling Reports Audit Trail Reports You can access the following reports from the Reports > Audit Trail menu: Report Name Description Frequency Transaction Audit Trail By Date Select this report to generate a list of every transaction, patient, or account record modification or deletion within a specified date range. The report is sorted by date. For more information, see Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Date Report . As Needed Transaction Audit Trail By User Select this report to generate a list of every transaction, patient, or account record modification or deletion performed by a specific user. For more information, see Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Patient Name Report . As Needed Transaction Audit Trail By Collection Doctor Select this report to generate a list of every transaction, patient, or account record modification or deletion for a specific collection doctor. For more information, see Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Collection Doctor Report . As Needed Transaction Audit Trail By Transaction Date Select this report to generate a list of every transaction, patient, or account record modification or deletion within a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Transaction Date Report . As Needed Scheduler Audit Trail By Date Select this report to generate a list of every appointment creation, modification, or deletion within a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Date Report . Weekly Scheduler Audit Trail By User Select this report to generate a list of every appointment creation, modification, or deletion, sorted by user. For more information, see Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By User Report . Weekly Scheduler Audit Trail By Patient Name Select this report to generate a list of every appointment creation, modification, or deletion, sorted by patient name. For more information, see Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Patient Name Report . The reported is sorted by user. For more information, see Running the Prescription Audit Trail By User Report . For more information, see Running the Prescription Audit Trail By Patient Name Report . Weekly Prescription Audit Trail By Doctor Select this report to generate a list of every prescription issued. The reported is sorted by doctor. For more information, see Running the Prescription Audit Trail By Doctor Report . Weekly Prescription Audit Trail By Drug Name Select this report to generate a list of every prescription issued. The reported is sorted by drug name. For more information, see Running the Prescription Audit Trail By Drug Name Report . Weekly Patient Information Audit Trail By Date Select

topics.title: Handling Patient Budget Plans
topics.body: Handling Patient Budget Plans Budget Planning Handling Patient Budget Plans Patient budget plans can be used for any procedure; if you use them for orthodontics, however, you can also create an ortho insurance case to track claim re-submission. The charges are posted monthly to the patient's Transactions window. There are no finance charges associated with patient budget plans. Each patient can have: One budget plan at a time associated with his or her record A patient budget plan and an account budget plan associated with his or her account Related Topics Using Patient Budget Plan Window Options Setting Patient Budget Plan Defaults Setting Up Patient Budget Plans Resetting a Patient Budget Plan Managing Ortho Cases Posting Payments to Ortho Claims Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Patient Budget Plans Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Handling Images While Charting Handling Images While Charting Use the charting module to capture images acquired from a live video source. This module is compliant with the following standards: VFW (Video for Windows) WDM TWAIN This interface supports video image capture using various software programs, including: Trophy for Windows X-ray capture using Schick Technologies The software also uses the Hauppauge Win-TV Radio Model 401 video card. topics.title: Handling Today's Memos
topics.body: Handling Today's Memos Contacts and Memos > Using Memos Handling Today's Memos When you access the software, any memos scheduled for the current date are displayed in the Today�s Memos window. To view or print memos for the current date: Select List > Today�s Memos. The Today�s Memos List window is displayed. Select an option: To view the details of a memo : Double-click it. After you finish reviewing the information, click Close . To delete all memos : Click Delete Today's . A message asks you whether to delete the memos. Click Yes . To print all memos : Click Print Today's . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option, and click OK . To edit a memo : Click Edit . The memo details are displayed in the Today�s Memos window. Make the changes, and click Close . To delete a specific memo : Select it and click Delete . A message asks you whether to delete the memo. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Default Clinical Notes
topics.body: Handling Default Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes Handling Default Clinical Notes Default clinical notes: Are used to speed up the note entry process. Are generic notes for the most common procedures that automatically display in the Note field each time a provider adds a patient clinical note for the same procedure. Can be associated with a provider or procedure. Example : If you set up a default note for code 3330, the note text is automatically displayed in each note for the same code and doctor. Can be edited and are displayed in orange text in the clinical notes list windows. Related Topics Creating Default Clinical Notes from Patient Clinical Notes Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Labs
topics.body: Handling Labs Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases Handling Labs You can add, edit, and delete your office's lab information. You can also run lab reports, sorting the labs alphabetically, by ID number, or by lab case. Related Topics Adding a New Lab Editing a Lab Deleting a Lab Running Lab Reports Using Labs List and Labs Window Options Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Discounts
topics.body: Handling Discounts Posting Payments Handling Discounts The discounts feature e nables you to apply discounts automatically while posting a transaction. Because the discount is applied as a separate credit transaction, the original transaction is unaltered. To set up a discount: Define the Discount : Define and name each discount. Assign the Discount : Select a discount and assign it to the patient record. You can also apply a discount from the patient Transactions window. A ssign a Discount to an ADA/Transaction Code : Associate the account with an ADA or transaction code. After setting up a discount, each time a transaction is posted, an automatic check is made to see whether the: Patient has an applicable discount Posted procedure is discountable If both are true, the discount is posted as a separate credit transaction. Related Topics Understanding Discount Security Features Defining a Discount Adding a Discount Setting the Current Discount Assigning a Discount to a Patient Assigning a Discount to an Account Assigning a Discount to a Procedure Code Applying the Current Discount to a Patient or Account Applying Any Discount to a Patient or Account Printing Discounts on a Claim Form Handling Refunds and Adjustments After Discounts Running the Patients By Discount Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Employer Information
topics.body: Handling Employer Information Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools Handling Employer Information For each patient's employer, use the employer database to store the following information: Personal Professional You can: Add, edit, or delete employer information Associate this information with account and patient records Employer information is used: For filing insurance forms To track provider goals and actual production amounts You can also associate an insurance plan with a default employer. If an insurance plan has a default provider, when you enter a plan for account guarantor 1, the employer information is automatically displayed in the appropriate fields. topics.body: Handling Generic Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes Handling Generic Clinical Notes Generic notes: Can be accessed from the List menu or from the Overview, Restorative, Perio, Exams, Clinical Imaging, Treatment Planning, or Notes tabs in the Chart for window Can be accessed from the Options menu of the ADA/Transaction Codes window Can be associated with a specific provider or procedure Can be added as often as needed to any patient�s chart Can be edited Are displayed in blue in clinical notes list windows Related Topics Creating Generic Clinical Notes from Patient Clinical Notes Creating Generic Clinical Notes Editing Generic Clinical Notes Need more information? topics.title: Handling Images
topics.body: Handling Images Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container Handling Images The following information is displayed beneath each image on the Clinical Images tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container: Image type Creation date You can do the following for each image on the tab: Preview Edit Annotate Print Save Convert to clinical image View image properties You can also do the following on the tab: Copy images to the clipboard Associate images with teeth Access the cosmetic imaging module If you use Trophy or CS Imaging, the images are displayed in that software. Edit and save the image in the software before returning to SoftDent. topics.title: Handling Insurance Company Information
topics.body: Handling Insurance Company Information Setting Up Insurance Handling Insurance Company Information You can add, edit, and delete insurance company records. You can also: Use the auto dialer Print address labels Print laser envelopes Receive Trojan updates Set up Trojan update options Merge insurance company information with a Letter Expert template View claim documents attached to the insurance company record View a list of insurance plans associated with the selected insurance company Related Topics Using Insurance Co List and Insurance Co Window Options Adding an Insurance Company Editing an Insurance Company Deleting Duplicate Insurance Companies Deleting an Insurance Company Setting Up An Umbrella Insurance Company Viewing Patients Associated with an Insurance Company Need more information? topics.title: Handling Insurance Plan Information
topics.body: Handling Insurance Plan Information Setting Up Insurance Handling Insurance Plan Information You can add, edit, and delete insurance plan records. To estimate patient and insurance payments, these records contain the following information: Deductible Coverage percentage Copay You can define up to four insurance plans per patient: Primary dental Secondary dental Primary medical Secondary medical You can set waiting period information associated with an insurance plan on the Coverage tab. Use the entry to track insurance plan waiting periods affecting insurance payment. When making a patient appointment or posting patient transactions, the following are used to dictate whether the patient is covered for the selected procedure: Patient insurance effective date set in the patient record Plan�s waiting period You can also set up: Flat fee plans Percentage plans DMO/capitation plans PPOs Medicare Medicaid plans Related Topics Setting Up Insurance Plans Understanding Percentage Plans Understanding Flat Fee Plans Understanding Preferred Provider (PPO) Plans Understanding DMO/Capitation Plans Understanding Medicare Plans Understanding Medicaid Plans Using Insurance Plan List and Insurance Plan Window Options Adding Insurance Plans Editing Insurance Plans Changing the Insurance Form Used by an Insurance Plan Deleting Duplicate Insurance Plans Deleting Insurance Plans Viewing Patients Associated with an Insurance Plan Checking Patient Benefits Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling InOffice Transactions
topics.body: Handling InOffice Transactions Tracking Patients in the Office Handling InOffice Transactions When the InOffice window is initially displayed: It is blank. You must fill the window with the scheduled appointments for the current date. After you fill the InOffice window, a color-coded system enables you to determine the following for each patient scheduled for an appointment on the current day: Status Location Select Change Location from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu to change the patient location from the waiting room to the operatory. After the patient has been seen by the specified provider, select Change Location from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu to return the patient to the waiting room. Related Topics Filling the InOffice Window Checking In a Patient Viewing Patient and Account Information Moving Patients to an Operatory Accessing the Dental Imaging Software in the InOffice Using the Electronic Router Returning Patients to the Waiting Room Checking Out Patients Automatically Posting Appointment Procedures Scheduling Follow-Up Appointments Clearing Patients from the InOffice Window Manually Printing Routing Slips Adding Unscheduled Patients to the InOffice Window Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Insurance Plan Transactions
topics.body: Handling Insurance Plan Transactions Setting Up Insurance Handling Insurance Plan Transactions Post payments from the insurance company to the following plans in the Plan Transactions window: DMO/Capitation Medicaid Example : If the contract specifies monthly payments, regardless of the number of eligible patients seen, post these payments in the Plan Transactions window. Post percentage, Medicare percentage, Medicare flat fee, flat fee, flat fee PPO, and percentage PPO plan transactions in the Patient Transactions or Account Transactions windows. For more information, see Posting Insurance Payments . Tip To compare payments received from the plan versus normal fees for the same procedures, run the Capitation Analysis report. Related Topics Adding a DMO/Capitation or Medicaid Plan Transaction Printing DMO/Capitation or Medicaid Plan Transactions Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Mixed Dentition
topics.body: Handling Mixed Dentition Restorative Charting Handling Mixed Dentition Using the charting module enables you to accurately chart the following for all patients: Procedures Conditions When you set up practice information in the Practice window, you dictate the age at which permanent teeth are displayed in the chart. Primary teeth with the molars unerupted are displayed in the chart for patients younger than the patient adult age indicated in the window. Permanent teeth are displayed, and all primary teeth are indicated as missing in the chart for patients equal to or older than the age indicated in the window. When both are located in one location: Permanent teeth are displayed if erupted teeth have been charted Primary teeth are displayed if no erupted teeth have been charted You can also chart conditions and procedures for both tooth types and the associated roots. Primary and permanent tooth toggles are located between the arch in the charting module. If primary teeth are displayed by default : Tooth letters are displayed in the chart. If permanent teeth are displayed by default : Tooth numbers are displayed. To toggle between a primary and permanent tooth when both share the same location : Click the tooth number or letter in the center of the tooth chart. If there is no mixed dentition noted for the tooth, only the primary or permanent tooth can be displayed. Related Topics Viewing Primary Teeth Viewing Permanent Teeth Charting Mixed Dentition Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

You can add, edit, and amend patient clinical notes, as well as view the history or images associated with patient clinical notes.
