topics.title: Handling Reports - Practice Management Reports
topics.body: Handling Reports - Practice Management Reports Handling Reports Practice Management Reports You can access the following reports from the Reports > Practice Management menu: Report Name Description Frequency Practice Summary Select this report to generate a summary of provider production, treatment plans, receivables, insurance collections, patient and professional referrals, and recall data. For more information, see Running the Practice Summary Report . Monthly Practice Barometers Select this report to compare critical statistics for your practice for two date ranges. For more information, see Running the Practice Barometers Report . As Needed Hygiene Analysis Select this report to compare the scheduled and available hours for hygienists. This report also includes the projected lost income. For more information, see Running the Hygiene Analysis Report . Monthly Production By ADA Code Select this report to generate a list of production codes posted during a specific date range. For more information, see Running the Production by ADA Code Report . As Needed Production By Class Select this report to generate a list of production codes posted during a specific date range by class. For more information, see Running the Production by Class Report . As Needed Payment Allocation Select this report to generate a list of income generated for the practice or a provider during a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Payment Allocation Report . As Needed RTU Production Select this report to generate a list of production codes and the associated RTU posted within a specified date range. For more information, see Running the RTU Production Report. As Needed Collection Summary Select this report to generate a list of collections by cash, personal check, insurance check, and credit cards within a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Collection Summary Report . Weekly Reconciliation Select this report to generate a list of daily totals and to ensure the validity of your bank statements. For more information, see Running the Reconciliation Report . Monthly Receivables Summary Select this report to generate a list of prior receivables for the current date, month-to-date, and year-to-date. For more information, see Running the Receivables Summary Report . Monthly Receivables Comparison Select this report to compare the change in receivables over two date ranges. For more information, see Running the Receivables Comparison Report . Weekly or Monthly Receivables Breakdown Select this report to generate a monthly or yearly breakdown of receivables. For more information, see Running the Receivables Breakdown Report . Monthly Receivables Ratio Select this report to generate the receivables for the previous twelve months and calculate a monthly average for each quarter. For more information, Running the Receivables Ratio Report . Monthly Insurance Batch Preview Listing Select this report to generate a list of all the insurance claims that are batched. Use this report if you process electronic claims. For more information, see Running the Insurance Batch Preview Report . Daily Outstanding Claims By Company Select this report to generate a list of outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical claims sorted by insurance company. For more information, see Running the Outstanding Claims by Company Report . As Needed Outstanding Claims By Patient Select this report to generate a list of outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical claims sorted by patient name. For more information, see Running the Outstanding Claims by Patient Report . As Needed Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Co. Select this report to generate a list of outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical claims with a benefit owed to the patient, sorted by insurance company. For more information, see Running the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Company Report . As Needed Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Patient Select this report to generate a list of outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical claims with a benefit owed to the patient, sorted by patient name. For more information, see Running the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Patient Report . As Needed Unsubmitted Claims Select this report to generate a list of insurable transactions that have not been submitted for payment. For more information, see Running the Unsubmitted Claims Report . Weekly Insurance Income Select this report to generate a list of how much income your practice has received from each insurance company. For more information, see Running the Insurance Income Report . As Needed Write-Off Totals Select this report to generate a list of codes that are associated with a write-off. For more information, see Running the Write-Off Totals Report . Monthly Contractual Plan Analysis Select this report to generate a list of all production codes that use a DMO/Capitation fee schedule and a comparison of the charges to the total for the same production for fee schedule 0. For more information, see Running the Contractual Plan Analysis Report . Monthly Contractual Plan Production Select this report to generate a list of all procedures posted using a DMO fee schedule. The quantity performed and the monies generated by the production are included in the report. For more information, see Running the Contractual Plan Production Report . Monthly Contractual Plan Production By Company Select this report to generate a list of all procedures posted using a DMO fee schedule by insurance company. The quantity performed and the monies generated by the production are included in the report. See Running the Contractual Plan Production by Company Report . Monthly Capitation Collections Summary Select this report to summarize the collections received for any capitation plan within a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Capitation Collections Summary Report . Monthly Capitation Plan Encounter Select this report to generate a list of patients with transactions posted for a selected capitation plan within a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Capitation Plan Encounter Report . Monthly Capitation Ins. Co. Encounter Select this report to generate a list of patients with transactions posted for a selected capitation plan within a specified date range. The report includes how often the patient was seen, the total number of performed procedures, and the total amount charged within the date range. For more information, see Running the Capitation Insurance Company Encounter Report . Monthly Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report Select this report to generate a list of Medicaid services provided during a specified time period. For more information, see Running the Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report . ePostings Adjustments Select this report to generate a list of all of the ePostings adjustment codes in your database. For more information, see Running the ePostings Adjustment Actions Report . As Needed Treatment Plan Summary Select this report to generate a summary of treatment plan information. For more information, see Running the Treatment Plan Summary Report . Monthly Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown Select this report to generate a breakdown of all treatment plans that meet the criteria. For more information, see Running the Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown Report . Monthly for Accepted and Unaccepted Plans Treatment Plan Transactions Select this report to generate a list of all posted treatment plan procedures and calculations of the percentages of transactions gained from treatment plans. For more information, see Running the Treatment Plan Transactions Report . Monthly Professional Referrals Select this report to generate a list of incoming and outgoing referrals for a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Professional Referrals Report. Weekly Patient Referrals Select this report to generate a list of patient referrals for a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Patient Referrals by Name Report . Monthly Who's Due Back Referrals Select this report to generate a list of patients who you have referred to other practices and are expected back in your office within a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Who's Due Back Referrals Report . Monthly Referral Production Analysis Select this report to generate a list of providers who have referred patients to your practice within a specified date range. The production and collections generated by the referral are included in the report. For more information, see Running the Referral Production Analysis Report . As Needed Referral Source Analysis Select this report to generate a list of providers�sorted by category�who have referred patients to your practice within a specified date range. The production generated by each referral is included in the report. For more information, see Running the Referral Source Analysis Report . As Needed Operatory Production Forecast By ADA Code Select this report to calculate which operatory brings in the largest percentage of income by procedure. The report also compares scheduled procedures for an operatory with the practice. For more information, see Running the Operatory Production Forecast by ADA Code Report . Monthly Provider Production Forecast By ADA Code Select this report to calculate which provider brings in the largest percentage of income by procedure. The report also compares scheduled procedures for a provider with other providers. For more information, see Running the Provider Productions Forecast by ADA Code Report . Monthly Provider Production Forecast By Class Select this report to calculate which provider brings in the largest percent of income by procedural class. The report also classifies scheduled procedures for a provider with other providers. For more information, see Running the Provider Productions Forecast by Class Report . Monthly Practice Summary Forecast By Class Select this report to generate a list of totals of scheduled charges for procedures by class. For more information, see Running the Practice by Class Forecast Report . Monthly Expected Response Contact Select this report to generate a list of contacts waiting for a response. You can run this report for patients, accounts, insurance plans, insurance companies, employers, referring doctors, providers, pharmacies , and claims . For more information, see Running the Expected Response Contact Report . Weekly Contacts Made Select this report to generate a list of contacts made within a specified date range. You can run this report for patients, accounts, insurance plans, insurance companies, employers, referring doctors, providers, pharmacies , and claims . For more information, see Running the Contacts Made Report . As Needed Contacts Pending Select this report to generate a list of contacts that need to be made. You can run this report for patients, accounts, insurance plans, insurance companies, employers, referring doctors, providers, pharmacies , and claims . For more information, see Running the Contacts Pending Report . Weekly Ortho Insurance Projected Income Select this report to calculate next year's expected monthly income to be generated from ortho insurance cases. For more information, see Running the Ortho Insurance Projected Income Report . Monthly Ortho Insurance Details Select this report to generate a list of ortho cases and budget plans set up for ortho cases. For more information, see Running the Ortho Insurance Details Report . Monthly Lab Case Select this report to generate a list of lab cases. For more information, see Running the Lab Case Report . As Needed Patient Production/Collection Select this report to generate a list of top production or collection patients. For more information, see Running the Patient Production/Collection Report . As Needed Patient Visit Statistics Select th

As a patient's medical health changes, you can update the information to ensure that the current information is available. topics.title: Handling Provider Information
topics.body: Handling Provider Information Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools Handling Provider Information You can add, edit, or delete provider information for each dentist, hygienist, and assistant in your practice and associate this information with account and patient records. Provider information can be used to: Set up the scheduler Schedule patient appointments Set up the auto scheduler File insurance claims Track provider goals and actual production amounts Payment allocation lets you specify which providers get paid for completed procedures. Use this feature when the provider performing the work is different from the provider getting paid for the work. You can allocate payment to any providers, not just those performing the work. Example : You can specify all provider revenue to one provider. You can also set up payment allocation so that revenues from hygienists go to the doctor performing the exams or that providers receive credit for whatever work they perform. In the Provider Preferences section of the provider record, you can specify the following for a new provider: Preferred assistant Scheduling book Auto scheduling book Routing slip Related Topics Using Provider List and Provider Window Options Adding a Provider Editing Provider Information Deleting Providers Marking Provider Records Inactive Setting Production Goals Tracking Goals National Provider Identifier Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Questionnaire Security
topics.body: Handling Questionnaire Security Handling Patient Questionnaires Handling Questionnaire Security When System Security is enabled, you can restrict access to patient questionnaire features. To set patient questionnaire security: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Users by Rights . The Users By Rights window is displayed. Select an option: To enable users to add or edit patient questionnaires : Select Add/Edit Questionnaires from the drop-down list and select the users from the bottom section of the window. To enable users to view patient questionnaire results : Select View Questionnaire Results from the drop-down list and select the users from the bottom section of the window. To enable users to enter patient questionnaire results : Select Enter Questionnaire Results from the drop-down list and select the users from the bottom section of the window. To enable users to delete patient questionnaire results : Select Delete Questionnaire Results from the drop-down list and select the users from the bottom section of the window. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Receivables
topics.body: Handling Receivables Billing and Receivables Handling Receivables At the end of the day, when you run the final daysheet, the daily receivables totals are calculated and the information is saved in the RCVTOT.DAT file. This file: Tracks your current and past receivables Can also be used to create summaries from past days and to generate reports that show receivables comparisons In addition, the receivables total is printed on the daysheet and can be printed on all register reports. Related Topics Viewing Daily Receivables Comparing Provider Goals to Provider Production Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Handling HIPAA Transactions Compliance System Security and HIPAA Compliance Handling HIPAA Transactions Compliance You can: Document patient/guarantor relationships : You can select from an Unknown, Self, Spouse, Child, Employee, Handicapped Dependant, Significant Other, Life Partner, Dependant, and Other relationship in the Patient�s Relationship to section of the Insur. tab of the patient record. topics.title: Handling Treatment Plan Items
topics.body: Handling Treatment Plan Items Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container Handling Treatment Plan Items The following information is displayed for each treatment plan item: Planned date Treatment plan type Procedure code Procedure code description Provider Estimated insurance payment Treatment plan group Number Surface number TP1, TP2, or TP3 is displayed in the Type column of each treatment plan item. Primary treatment plan items : Displayed in red on the chart. Secondary treatment plan items : Displayed in magenta. Tertiary treatment plan items: Displayed in dark green. Posted treatment plan items : Displayed in black. Pending transactions : Displayed in teal. Related Topics Viewing Treatment Plan Items Using Treatment Plan Item Options Displaying Treatment Plan Items in the Chart Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing Treatment Plan Acceptance States
topics.body: Changing Treatment Plan Acceptance States Treatment Planning Changing Treatment Plan Acceptance States After presenting the treatment plan to a patient , y ou can use the software to select the procedures accepted by the patient. You can also note patient acceptance in the Treatment Plan window as you enter the procedure. To change a treatment plan state: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the patient Transactions window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the InOffice window : Select the appropriate patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Select the treatment plan procedure from the list, and select Change State from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Change Treatment Plan State window is displayed. Do one of the following: To change the state of a group in the primary treatment plan, select the state from the drop-down list in the Primary Treatment Plan section, and type the group number. To change the state of a group in the secondary treatment plan, select the state from the drop-down list in the Secondary Treatment Plan section, and type the group number. To change the state of a group in the tertiary treatment plan, select the state from the drop-down list in the Tertiary Treatment Plan section, and type the group number. If the patient does not agree to all procedures in the group, delete the unaccepted procedures or reassign them to another group number. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Images While Charting - Viewing Images
topics.body: Handling Images While Charting - Viewing Images Handling Images While Charting > Using Layout Mode Viewing Images To display a specific layout view in the Chart for window when the Clinical Imaging tab is the active window, select an option: When ima ges are displayed in layout mode, an R located at the bottom left side of the window indicates that images are displayed normally. If the letter is located in the bottom right side of the window, the image is reversed. To display one image : Select Layout Mode , select Layout View 1 by 1 , and drag the image from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. To display two images side by side : Select Layout Mode , select Layout View 1 by 2 , and drag the images from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. To display three images, one image over two images : Select Layout Mode , select Layout View 1 Over 2 , and drag the images from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. To display two images, one image over another : Select Layout Mode , select Layout View 2 by 1 , and drag the images from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. To display four images in the window, two images over two images : Select Layout Mode , select Layout View 2 by 2 , and drag the images from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. To display ten images, two large images over eight small images : Select Layout Mode , select Layout View 2 Over 8 , and drag the images from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. To display nine images, three images in three rows : Select Layout Mode , select Layout View 3 by 3 , and drag the images from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. To display twelve images, four images in three rows : Select Layout Mode , select Layout View 4 by 3 , and drag the images from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. To display sixteen images, four images in four rows : Select Layout Mode , select Layout View 4 by 4 , and drag the images from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. To drag images from one pane to another, drag the image and drop it in the appropriate pane. Tip Select buttons from the Clinical Imaging or Image Layout toolbars. Drag images from the Clinical Detail Container. Related Topics Clearing Images from the Clinical Imaging Tab Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: How Often Should I Run Utilities?
topics.body: How Often Should I Run Utilities? Utilities How Often Should I Run Utilities? It is not recommended that you run SoftDent Utilities on a weekly, monthly, or yearly basis. You typically run utilities in case of database corruption, most commonly expressed as SDWin DBM Error, File No. ### Error = # . There might be other instances where utilities are used to resolve or troubleshoot an issue. You should run utilities only when you are instructed to do so by a SoftDent technical support representative. Related Topics Using File Menu Utilities Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running Customize Menu Utilities Using Report Menu Utilities Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features
topics.body: Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features System Security and HIPAA Compliance Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) contains provisions for privacy and security of healthcare information. Using HIPAA Privacy features, you can restrict the information that is: Displayed in the following windows: InOffice Daily Appointments Book At A Glance History Appointments Printed on the Daily Operatory report You can enable the HIPAA privacy settings to display a HIPAA reminder automatically to inform your patients of your efforts to comply with these regulations. Using HIPAA Security settings, you can: Enable security restrictions that help you enforce and protect user names and passwords Track user logins and activity Set the system to shut down automatically after a specified period of inactivity The following features also contain functions to help you comply with the HIPAA Transactional standards: Patient Insurance plan Claim Referring doctor ADA/transaction code Contacts For Related Topics Tracking HIPAA Privacy Compliance and Understanding HIPAA Security Setting HIPAA Security and Privacy Options Handling HIPAA Contacts Viewing HIPAA-Specific Contact Information Using the HIPAA Privacy Feature Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Customizing the Hour and Minute Markers
topics.body: Customizing the Hour and Minute Markers Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows Customizing the Hour and Minute Markers Each user can customize how items in the Daily Appointments, Book At A Glance, and History Appointments windows are displayed. To customize the color of the hour and minute markers, the lines between them, and the background color: In the appropriate scheduler window, right-click anywhere in the hour and minute marker section of the window (on the left side of the window, near the spiral binder) and select Properties from the menu. The Properties... window is displayed. Select an option: To change the background color for the time and minute marker section : Click the square next to Background. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the line color for the time and minute marker section : Click the square next to Line. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the color for the hour text displayed in the time and minute marker section : Click the square next to Hours. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the color for the minute text displayed in the time and minute marker section : Click the square next to Minutes. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the color for the text of the selected minute in the time and minute marker section : Click the square next to Selected Minutes. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the background color for the selected minute in the time and minute marker section : Click the square next to Selected Background. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the color for the a.m. and p.m. text displayed in the time and minute marker section : Click the square next to AM/PM. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To add a background image to the time and minute marker section : Select it from the Background Image drop-down list. To restore the default text colors : Click Restore Defaults . When selecting colors, ensure adequate contrast with the other scheduler window colors. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the History Appointments Appointment Submenu Commands
topics.body: Using the History Appointments Appointment Submenu Commands Using the Scheduler > Using the History Appointments Window > Understanding History Appointments Window Options Using the History Appointments Appointment Submenu Commands The commands available from the appointments submenu in the History Appointments window are: Appt Book : Select this command to display the Daily Appointments window. Book Quick Glance : Select this command to display the Book At A Glance window. Month At A Glance : Select this command to display the Month At A Glance window. Change Date Range: Select this command to display the History Appointments Date Range window. In this window, you can select another date range of posted appointments to view. View Appt : Select this command to display the Appointment Card window. In this window, you can view, add, edit, and delete procedures scheduled for an appointment. Zoom : Select this command to display the Assistant window. In this window, you can view additional patient and appointment information. You can also access this information from the Zoom appointment icon. Patient Information : Select this command to display the patient record in the Patient window. Patient Chart : Select this command to display the patient chart in the charting module. Patient Portrait : Select this command to display the patient portrait. If no portrait exists, a message is displayed. Show Images/Hide Images : Select the Show Images command to display portraits associated with the patient at the bottom of the Daily Appointments window. Select the Hide Images command to hide the patient images. Select Book: Click a time slot or appointment and select this command to display the Scheduler Rooms List window. In this window, you can select a book to view. See Displaying Different Books . Select Room #: Click a time slot or appointment and select this command to display the Scheduler Rooms List window. In this window, you can select a room to view. See Viewing Additional Rooms . Cancel : Select this command to close the appointments submenu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting History Appointments Properties
topics.body: Setting History Appointments Properties Using the Scheduler > Using the History Appointments Window > Enabling History Appointments Setting History Appointments Properties To set History Appointments window properties: Select an option: From the Daily Appointments, Book At A Glance, or Month At A Glance windows : Select History Appointments from the drop-down list at the top of the window. From the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows : Select an appointment or open time slot, right-click, and select History Appointments . From any window : Select Scheduler > History Appointments . The History Appointments Date Range window is displayed. The default settings for the date range fields are January 1 of the current year and yesterday. Select the starting and ending dates for which to view posted appointments from the appropriate drop-down lists and click OK . The History Appointments window is displayed. Select an empty cell, right-click, and select Properties . The Properties window is displayed. Select an option: To add a background image to the window : Select one from the Background Image drop-down list. To change the text font : Click Change Font . The Font window is displayed. Select the fonts from the Font, Font Style, and Size lists. To apply a specific character set to the selected font, select it from the Script drop-down list. Click OK . To show words in color : Select Show Color Wording . This box is checked by default. To change the background color for each time unit : Click the square next to Background Color. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . Grey is the default line color. Lilac is the default background color. To change the line color for each time unit : Click the square next to Line Color. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . Grey is the default line color. To change the line color that delineates each hour : Click the square next to Hourly Color. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . Red is the default hourly color. Set a default background color for this window to ensure that you do not confuse it with the Daily Appointments window. Click OK . You can also dictate how hour and minute markers are displayed in the window. See Customizing the Hour and Minute Markers . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing the History Appointments from a Patient Record
topics.body: Viewing the History Appointments from a Patient Record Using the Scheduler > Using the History Appointments Window Viewing the History Appointments from a Patient Record A list of all appointments that have been scheduled for a patient are displayed in the Patient Appointments window. When the History Appointments feature is enabled, you can display posted appointments in this window. To display posted appointments from the Patient Appointments window: From the Patient List window, select a patient and select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Include History Appointments . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the History Appointments Time Slot Submenu Commands
topics.body: Using the History Appointments Time Slot Submenu Commands Using the Scheduler > Using the History Appointments Window > Understanding History Appointments Window Options Using the History Appointments Time Slot Submenu Commands The commands available from the blank time slot submenu in the History Appointments window are: Appt Book : Select this command to display the Daily Appointments window. Book Quick Glance : Select this command to display the Book At A Glance window. Month At A Glance : Select this command to display the Month At A Glance window. Change Date Range: Select this command to display the History Appointments Date Range window. In this window, you can select another date range of posted appointments to view. Select Book : Select this command to display the Scheduler Book window. See Displaying Different Books . Select Room #: Click a time slot or appointment and select this command to display the Scheduler Rooms List window. In this window, you can select a room to view. See Viewing Additional Rooms . Properties : Select this command to display the Properties window. In this window, you can customize how information is displayed in the History Appointments window. Cancel : Select this command to close the time slot submenu. With the exception of the Properties command, these commands are also available for slot notes and blocked slots in the History Appointments window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the History Appointments Window
topics.body: Using the History Appointments Window Using the Scheduler Using the History Appointments Window From the History Appointments window, you can view past appointment records that have been saved for each patient, enabling you to access a complete record of each patient visit. This feature is enabled by default. If you do not want to save patient appointment histories, you can disable this feature. You can also view slot notes and blocked slots in the History Appointments window if your office does not use the Remove Slot Notes from Scheduler and the Remove Blocked Slots from Scheduler options. See Setting Scheduler Defaults for more information. Related Topics Enabling History Appointments Understanding History Appointments Window Options Changing the History Daybook Date Range Viewing the History Appointments from a Patient Record Viewing History Appointments from the InOffice List Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Transferring Data�The Data Sync
topics.body: Transferring Data�The Data Sync Using PracticeToGo Transferring Data�The Data Sync After the system administrator has made the PracticeToGo system settings and security settings, and you have set your PracticeToGo user preferences, you are ready to transfer data. You can sync data to either the Palm OS platform or the Microsoft Outlook software. To perform a HotSync using PracticeToGo and the Palm OS platform: Place the Palm organizer in the HotSync cradle. Press the HotSync button on the cradle. When the PracticeToGo HotSync status box on the PC indicates that the synchronization is finished, remove the Palm organizer from the cradle. To sync data from the PC SoftDent software to the Microsoft Outlook software: To manually sync between PracticeToGo and the Microsoft Outlook software, select System > PracticeToGo > Synchronize with MicroSoft Outlook . To sync with the Microsoft Outlook software automatically when you start or exit the SoftDent software, select System > PracticeToGo > System Settings and select the appropriate settings. See PracticeToGo System Settings for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Hygiene Analysis Report
topics.body: Running the Hygiene Analysis Report Handling Reports > Practice Management Reports Running the Hygiene Analysis Report The Hygiene Analysis report compares the hygienist�s scheduled hours to the available hours, analyzing the use of the hygienist�s time. The report generates information for one or all hygienists for the previous and next 12 months based on recall codes. Additional details: The report lists patients seen for recall appointments in the past 12 months, patients who were due and seen late, and patients who were due but not scheduled. Also displayed is the income generated by these recall appointments and the income lost because of unscheduled appointments. In addition, the report details the next 12 months of recall appointments, showing how many appointments are scheduled, the production dollars, and how many hours are required for the appointments. The accuracy of the report depends on the proper setup of the recall system in the following areas : Proper recall setup includes: Defining default recall codes and frequencies in System Settings. Setting up individual patient recall settings on the Recall tab of the patient record (code, frequency, time units, and hygienist). Marking transaction codes as hygiene procedures with time units and template information. Assigning recall appointment information on the scheduler template. To run the Hygiene Analysis report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Hygiene Analysis . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output type and click OK . The Hygiene/Recall Analysis Report Setup window is displayed. In the Exclude patients who had their Last Visit before: field, click the drop-down arrow to select a date from the calendar. Type the patient user codes to include or exclude in the report. To include inactive patients in the report, select Include Inactive Patients . Select the provider(s) you want to include in the report, or accept the default to report on all providers. The Normal Recall checkbox is selected by default. Deselect the checkbox to run the report for user-defined recalls. Click OK . Additional Hygiene Analysis Report Information: The report lists information for two periods: Previous 12 months: Month/Year�Name of month and year. Hours Used�Sum of the time units for each hygiene procedure for recall codes that have been posted to patients for each month. The sum of the time units used is multiplied by the default time unit (10 or 15 minutes), and rounded down to the nearest hour. Recall Pats Seen�Count of patients with hygiene procedures (as indicated in the Transaction Codes window) or recall codes (as defined in recall system settings) posted. A patient is counted once per day. When the report is run by provider, the service provider is used to calculate the number of patients seen . All Recall Income�Total dollar amount of all hygiene procedures and recall codes posted to the service provider each month. Pats Due Not Seen�Total number of patients due for recall who have not been seen. The patient�s last exam date plus the recall frequency determine the month the patient is due back. Income Not Realized�Pats Due Not Seen multiplied by the fee for the recall code on the Appts/Recall tab of the patient record. If no recall code is present, the system default is used. If an explosion code is present, the sum of the fees for all hygiene or recall codes within the explosion code is used. New Pats Seen�Count of all patients seen based on their first visit da

topics.title: Running the Assign Hygienist ID to Patient Utility
topics.body: Running the Assign Hygienist ID to Patient Utility Utilities > Running Customize Menu Utilities Running the Assign Hygienist ID to Patient Utility Run t his utility to add a default hygienist to all patient records or those associated with a specific provider identification number. To assign a hygienist identification number to patient records: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Customize > Assign Hygienist ID To Patient . The first window of the Hygienist ID Setup Program is displayed. Click Continue . The second window of the Hygienist ID Setup Program is displayed. Select an option: To assign the same default hygienist to all patients : Select (None) from the Choose doctor�s name or select none to assign all patients drop-down list. To assign the default hygienist to patients seen by a specific dentist : Select the provider�s name from the Choose doctor�s name or select none to assign all patients drop-down list. Select the hygienist from the Choose hygienist to assign drop-down list. Click Apply . A message asks you to confirm the selections. Click Yes . A message informs you the process is complete and specifies the number of modified records. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Images in Annotation Mode
topics.body: Viewing Images in Annotation Mode Handling Images While Charting > Using Annotation Mode Viewing Images in Annotation Mode To view an image in annotation mode: On the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select Annotation Mode . Select and drag the image from the Clinical Images tab of the Clinical Detail Container to the Clinical Imaging tab. The image is displayed. You can also select an image in the Clinical Detail Container, right-click, and select Annotate . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Image Annotate Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Image Annotate Toolbar Handling Images While Charting > Using Clinical Imaging Tab Toolbars Using the Image Annotate Toolbar Use the Image Annotate toolbar t o annotate images. This toolbar is displayed when the Clinical Imaging tab is displayed in Annotate mode. If you use Trophy, versions 4.2 or 5.0, or CS Imaging software, version 6.0, this toolbar is not enabled. Click the icon for the description of the Image Annotate toolbar buttons. Adds annotation text to an image. Adds an annotation shape to an image. Adds an annotation stamp to an image. Removes the last annotation made to an image. Reload the previous image. Saves the annotations to the image. Deletes the annotations. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Image Capture Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Image Capture Toolbar Handling Images While Charting > Using Clinical Imaging Tab Toolbars Using the Image Capture Toolbar Use the Image Capture toolbar commands to capture and import images. This toolbar is displayed when the Clinical Imaging tab is displayed in Capture mode. Click the icon for the description of the Image Capture toolbar buttons. Accesses live video. Captures live video. Saves an image. Deletes an image. Customizes camera or video card settings. Sets capture and color depth. Displays an image. Imports an image from a file. Imports an image from a digital camera. Imports an image from the selected TWAIN source. Selects a TWAIN source from which to import images. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Rebuild SoftDent Image Database Utility
topics.body: Running the Rebuild SoftDent Image Database Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Rebuild SoftDent Image Database Utility Run this utility to update image locations. When you move images to a new location or create a new image directory on your network, you should run this utility. Also, if you upgrade from an earlier version, run this utility if some of your existing images were missed during the conversion process. Do not run this utility unless you are instructed to by a technical support representative. To update image locations: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Rebuild SoftDent Image Database . The Rebuild Image Database Setup window is displayed. Select an option: To locate all your image directories : Click Scan Network . You can have more than one image directory, but each one must be named SDIMAGES and must be on a shared drive. A message informs you that the process is complete. Select the location checkboxes to include in the image database, and click OK (Rebuild) . To add a specific image directory to the database : Click Browse . The Browse For Folder window is displayed. Scroll to the bottom and open the Network Neighborhood directory before navigating to the image location. Select the image directory, and click OK . To add multiple locations, repeat the process. Select the location checkboxes to include in the image database, and click OK (Rebuild). After the images are added to the directory, the SoftDent utilities window is displayed. Click OK . When the technical support representative tells you to, restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Images in Edit Mode
topics.body: Viewing Images in Edit Mode Handling Images While Charting > Using Edit Mode Viewing Images in Edit Mode To view an image in edit mode: On the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select Edit Mode . Select and drag the image from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. You can also select an image in the Clinical Detail Container, right-click, and select Edit from the menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Image Editor Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Image Editor Toolbar Handling Images While Charting > Using Clinical Imaging Tab Toolbars Using the Image Editor Toolbar Use the Image Editor toolbar t o edit images. This toolbar is displayed when the Clinical Imaging tab is displayed in Edit mode. If you use Trophy software, versions 4.2 or 5.0, or CS Imaging software, version 6.0, this toolbar is not enabled. Click the icon for the description of the Image Editor toolbar buttons. Reloads the previous image. Saves an edited image. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Showing and Hiding Clinical Images in the InOffice
topics.body: Showing and Hiding Clinical Images in the InOffice Showing and Hiding Clinical Images in the InOffice To show or hide clinical images of a patient scheduled for an appointment in the InOffice window: Select List > InOffice . The InOffice window is displayed. Select Fill InOffice . To show patient images in the viewer at the bottom of the window, select an appointment and select Show Images . To hide patient images and the viewer, select Hide Images . If there are no patient images, the viewer is empty. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Image Layout Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Image Layout Toolbar Handling Images While Charting > Using Clinical Imaging Tab Toolbars Using the Image Layout Toolbar Use Image Layout toolbar commands t o select a view in which to display images. This toolbar is displayed when the Clinical Imaging tab is in Layout mode. Click the icon for the description of the Image Layout toolbar buttons. Displays one image. Displays two images side by side. Displays one image over two images. Displays one image over another image. Displays two images over two images. Displays two images over eight images. Displays three images in three rows. Displays fours images in three rows. Displays four images in four rows. Clears layout. E-mails layout. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Image Properties
topics.body: Viewing Image Properties Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Images Viewing Image Properties To view the image properties of an image: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, Clinical Imaging, or Treatment Planning tabs in the Chart for window, click the Clinical Images tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the image, right-click, and select Properties . The Image Properties window is displayed. In this window, you can edit the image label, make annotations, and associate the image with a tooth. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Associating Images with a Tooth or Region
topics.body: Associating Images with a Tooth or Region Handling Images While Charting > Using Organization Mode Associating Images with a Tooth or Region To associate an image with a tooth or region of the mouth: On the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select Organization Mode . Select buttons on the Clinical Imaging toolbar . Select images in the Clinical Detail Container. Select an option: To associate an image with a specific tooth : Drag the image from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. A yellow dot is displayed on the tooth to indicate that an image is associated with it. To associate an image with a specific region : Drag the image from the Clinical Images tab to the region text in the Clinical Imaging tab. A yellow dot is displayed next to the region to indicate that an image is associated with it. To use an image as a patient portrait : Drag the image from the Clinical Images tab to the Clinical Imaging tab. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Importing Images
topics.body: Importing Images Handling Images While Charting > Using Capture Mode Importing Images You can import images from a scanner or digital camera. You can import the following images: Bitmap (.bm and .bmp) JPEG PCX Tagged image file GIF Icon Macintosh image CAL Zsoft multi-page PCX Encapsulated post script GEM image Dr. Halo file Interchange file format Image object content architecture Laserview, Microsoft Paint Adobe Photoshop 3.0 Xpicmap Raster (.rast and .ras) Sun raster file (.im, .im1, .im8, .im24, .im32) Targa, Microsoft Window meta file Word Perfect Xbitmap To import an image on the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select an option: Select buttons on the Image Capture toolbar . To import an image that is saved on disk: Select Import an Image . The Import Images window is displayed. Select the image and click Open . To import an image from a TWAIN device : Select Acquire an Image from the currently Selected TWAIN source . The source software is launched. Acquire the image. To import an image from a digital camera : Select Import Images from Digital Camera . All digital camera images are displayed in the Dental Camera Images window. Select the image and click Import Images . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Importing an Image or X-ray to a Patient's Chart
topics.body: Importing an Image or X-ray to a Patient's Chart Importing an Image or X-ray to a Patient's Chart To import an image or x-ray to a patient's chart: Open the patient's chart. Select the Clinical Imaging tab. The Clinical Imaging window is displayed. Click Capture Mode in the Clinical Imaging toolbar. The Capture mode is displayed. Click Import an Image in the Image Capture toolbar. The Import Images window is displayed. The Import an Image button is available only when in Capture mode. Navigate to the file you want to import and highlight it. Click Open . The image is displayed in the patient's clinical images. OR Open the patient's chart. Click Video on the main menu bar. Select Live Video . Click Import an Image . The Import Images window is displayed. The Import an Image button is available only when in Capture mode. Navigate to the file you want to import and highlight it. Click Open . The image is displayed in the patient's clinical images. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: How Data Changes in SoftDent Affect Amazing Charts
topics.body: How Data Changes in SoftDent Affect Amazing Charts Using Amazing Charts How Data Changes in SoftDent Affect Amazing Charts Changes you make in SoftDent to the patient record or to an appointment can trigger the creation of data that will need to be imported into Amazing Charts. Here are some things to keep in mind about how changes you make in SoftDent affect Amazing Charts information: Changes that Will Require Data to be Imported Several conditions will trigger the process of creating data to later be imported into Amazing Charts, including: Adding a new patient Changing any of the following parts of the patient record: First Name Last Name Middle Initial (middle name's first letter only) Middle Name Address Zip Code Date of Birth Sex Home Phone Work Phone Cell Phone Email Marital Status SSN School/Employer Dr ID Referred In By Dr ID Primary Dental Insurance Holder Secondary Dental Insurance Holder Primary Dental Insurance ID Secondary Dental Insurance ID Primary Medical Insurance Holder (only if patient doesn't have both primary and secondary dental coverage) Primary Medical Insurance ID (only if patient doesn't have both primary and secondary dental coverage) Secondary Medical Insurance Holder (only if there is no secondary dental insurance) Secondary Medical Insurance ID (only if there is no secondary dental insurance) Patient's Relationship to Each Guarantor Scheduling or rescheduling an appointment Changing any of the following parts of the appointment : Lengthening the Appointment Shortening the Appointment Changing the Number of Slots in the Appointment Card Changing the Doctor Changing the Hygienist Changing the Assistant Changes that Cause Data Not to be Imported Creating a new patient and leaving the date of birth blank, despite the warning in SoftDent, results in no data being sent to Amazing Charts. Changes that Can Cause Errors Scheduling a new patient and leaving the date of birth blank, despite the warning in SoftDent, results in an error when SD data is imported into Amazing Charts. Scheduling an existing patient without a date of birth results in an error when SoftDent data is imported into Amazing Charts. General Info Providers (first name and last name) in SoftDent should match the providers entered in Amazing Charts prior to importing any data. Amazing Charts matches patients by their first name, last name, and date of birth. A patient with a date of birth that was left blank will not be imported into Amazing Charts. If a patient�s first or last name is changed in SoftDent, a new patient with the new name is added in Amazing Charts. If the patient (before the name change) had any appointments in Amazing Charts, they are deleted, and new appointment(s) are added for the patient with the new name. If you make several changes to a patient record in SoftDent �for example, changing an address and adding a Social Security Number�the same changes are found in Amazing Charts. If you make several changes to a patient appointment in SoftDent �for example, rescheduling, updating the date or time, or changing doctors�the same changes are found in Amazing Charts. Appointments that had been previously added to Amazing Charts and later cancelled in SoftDent are deleted from the Amazing Charts scheduler. Appointments that had been previously added to Amazing Charts and later checked out or posted in SoftDent will stay on the Amazing Charts scheduler. Information that is already in Amazing Charts cannot be deleted by deleting it in

topics.title: Importing Additional File Types
topics.body: Importing Additional File Types Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Importing Additional File Types You are not limited to importing only image files into the document manager. Documents created in Microsoft Word (.doc files), text files (.txt), HTML files, XML files, and so on, can also be imported. These files are displayed in the same manner as other files in the document tree, and a placeholder with an icon identifying the file type is displayed in the preview window. View the file by double-clicking on the placeholder, or right-click on the placeholder and select Open . To import additional file types: Select an option: To add an account document : In the Account List window, select the account to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a patient document : In the Patient List window, select the patient to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. OR In the patient chart, click the Documents tab. To add a referring doctor document : In the REFERRING DR List window, select the patient to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to an insurance company record : In the Insurance Co List window, select the company to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to an insurance company plan record : In the Insurance Plan List window, select the plan to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to an insurance claim record : In the Claim List window, select the claim to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a claim document to a completed claim record : In the Completed Claim List window, select the claim to which the document is to be added and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To add a lab case document : In the Labs List window, select the lab to which the case is to be added and select Multi-Case Document from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Multi Lab Case Capture window is displayed. In this window, you can attach a document to several lab cases. Click Add Lab Case , or select the command from the Options section of the Express bar. The Lab Case List window is displayed. Double-click the lab cases. A message asks you to confirm the lab case addition. Click Yes . The Lab Case List window is displayed. Select Capture Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar. The Documents for window is displayed. To append a page to an existing document, see Adding Pages to Documents . Select Capture New Document from the Options section of the Express bar. The Documents for add window is displayed. Type the document�s name in the Document Description field. To use the name as the default for all new documents of the selected type, select Make Default . For more information, see Setting Capture Preferences . The Category field defaults to a category based on where the capture process was started. Example: If you are capturing a new document from the Patient List window, the Category field defaults to

topics.title: Running the Import Text File Utility
topics.body: Running the Import Text File Utility Utilities > Using File Menu Utilities Running the Import Text File Utility Run this utility to import account and patient information previously stored in an ASCII text file. Each field must be separated by a semicolon before running the utility. To import account or patient records: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select File > Import Text File . The Import Data into SoftDent window is displayed. Select the location of the file from the drop-down list located in the middle of the window. Click Apply . Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Inactivating Codes
topics.body: Inactivating Codes Handling Transactions and Codes > Editing Codes Inactivating Codes To inactivate a code: Select List > ADA Codes . The ADA/Transaction Code List window is displayed. Select the code and click Edit . The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window is displayed. Select Inactive on the Information tab and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Including Inactive Records in Report Results
topics.body: Including Inactive Records in Report Results Including Inactive Records in Report Results To include inactive records in report results: Select the report from the Reports menu. The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The report window is displayed. Select the report criteria. Depending on the report you are generating, one or more of the following options is available: To include inactive patients, select Include Inactive Patients . To include inactive accounts, select Include Inactive Accounts . To include inactive providers, select Include Inactive Providers . To include inactive referring doctors, select Include Inactive Referring Doctors . To include inactive insurance companies, select Include Inactive Insurance Plans . To include inactive insurance plans, select Include Inactive Insurance Companies . To include inactive employers, select Include Inactive Employers . To include inactive schools, select Include Inactive Schools . To include inactive other facilities, select Include Inactive Record Type . To include inactive prescription drugs, select Include Inactive Drugs . To include inactive pharmacies, select Include Inactive Pharmacies . To include inactive laboratories, select Include Inactive Labs . To include inactive ADA or transaction codes, select Include Inactive Codes . To include inactive diagnosis codes, select Include Inactive Diagnosis Codes . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Insurance Plan Defaults for Plans That Require Claims
topics.body: Setting Insurance Plan Defaults for Plans That Require Claims Planning Budgets > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Managing Ortho Cases Setting Insurance Plan Defaults for Plans That Require Claims The Insurance Plan Wizard is displayed each time you add the first plan of a specific type. Example : The first time you add a percentage plan, the Wizard is displayed, prompting you to use that plan as a default template for all subsequent percentage plans. To set the insurance plan defaults for insurance plans that require a claim: Select an option: Select List > Ins. Plan . From any window, press Ctrl + P. Select List Insurance Plans from the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. The Insurance Plan List window is displayed in FIND mode. Select the insurance plan. Click Claim Filing , or press Ctrl + Tab until the Claim Filing tab is the active window. Select Resubmit Ortho Ins Form . Type the resubmission interval required by the plan in the Payment/Resubmission Frequency field. Example : If you are required to resubmit monthly, type 1 in the field. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Insurance Companies by ID Report
topics.body: Running the Insurance Companies by ID Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Company Reports Running the Insurance Companies by ID Report The Insurance Companies by ID report generates a list of insurance companies by identification number. Sample Report To generate a list of insurance companies by identification number: Select Reports > Insurance > Companies By ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Insurance Company by ID Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending identification numbers and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating an Individual Profile
topics.body: Creating an Individual Profile Getting Started > Using Profiles Creating an Individual Profile If your office does not use the Apply to all Users setting (which overrides all other profiles), you can configure the software to reflect your preferences as to how list windows are displayed. To set up an individual profile: Select View > List Preferences . The SoftDent List Preferences window is displayed. In the View Profile for: section, select your name from the drop-down list, if your office uses the system profile as the default. If your office has opted to use the system profile override, the drop-down list is disabled. In the Highlighting Method section, select an option to specify whether the selected entry in the list is highlighted by a colored solid bar or colored outline. In the Highlight/Border Color section, select the color to use for the solid bar or outline selected in the Highlighting Method section. Click Use a custom color to display a color chart, where you can select a specific color. In the Right Click Options section, select an option: Display dialog in view mode: When you right-click on an item in any list, the corresponding window opens in VIEW mode. Display Options menu: When you right-click on an item in any list, the corresponding Options menu is displayed. This is the same menu that is displayed if you press O while the list is open. In the Grid Line Options section, select whether you want to always display grid lines, never use grid lines, or use vertical lines only. Click OK . A window is displayed, asking if the current profile should be saved. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accessing Information from Practice Central
topics.body: Accessing Information from Practice Central Getting Started > Using Practice Central Accessing Information from Practice Central You can access information directly from Practice Central by clicking a link to display the corresponding window. Some list headings are also hyperlinks. Example : Click an item in the Office Expert Summary list to display that Office Expert item. Related Topics Switching Users Viewing Receivables Displaying Patient Records in Practice Central Printing Practice Central Entries Setting Practice Central Preferences Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Initial Chart
topics.body: Using the Initial Chart Restorative Charting > Understanding Restorative Tab Modes Using the Initial Chart When you first access a patient chart, the initial chart is displayed. This enables you to: Chart existing conditions Chart work done outside your practice Chart work done inside your practice Clinical notes associated with an initial chart are always displayed in green text. The dentition for the patient is automatically calculated and the appropriate set of teeth are displayed. When you set up practice information in the Practice window, you dictate the age at which permanent teeth are displayed in the chart. Primary teeth with the molars unerupted are displayed in the chart for patients younger than the patient adult age. Permanent teeth are displayed, and all primary teeth are indicated as missing in the chart for patients equal to or older than the adult age. After you save the initial chart, only a user with the security permissions to edit the initial patient chart can change the chart. To access the initial chart: Select Initial Chart from the Mode drop-down list. Select the appropriate button from the Chart Mode toolbar, or press F4. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Initializing the Scheduler
topics.body: Initializing the Scheduler Getting Started > Starting the Software Initializing the Scheduler Use the Scheduler Setup Wizard to design your scheduler to best meet your practice needs. If you performed a new installation and are accessing the software for the first time, the Startup Preferences window is displayed. If you upgraded the software, the previously active window is displayed. To open the wizard and initialize the scheduler: Select Wizards > Scheduler Wizard . The first window of the Scheduler Setup Wizard is displayed. Read the introduction and click Next . The next wizard window is displayed. Select an option: Enable Scheduler : Enables users to access the Daily Appointments, Book At A Glance, Month At A Glance, History Appointments, Tickler Entry, Scheduler Books, and Setup Holidays windows. Enable Color Scheduling : Enables color scheduling and goal scheduling. For more information, see Setting Up Color and Goal Scheduling . Click Next . The next wizard window is displayed. Select one of the following: Default Template to Provider : Assigns the patient's default doctor to appointments containing procedures that are not linked to a scheduler template. Default Template to Override : Assigns no doctor to appointments containing procedures that are not linked to a scheduler template. Click Next . The next wizard window is displayed. Type the number of days that must pass before an appointment is considered broken in the Broken appointment period field. If a patient does not show up for an appointment or call to cancel an appointment within the specified number of days, the appointment is considered broken. The Cancel/Broken Appointment Wizard is displayed when you cancel an appointment and reminds you of the broken appointment settings specified in this window. The default value for this field is 1. Type -1 if you do not track broken appointments. Click Next . The next wizard window is displayed. Select an option: Type the number of copies of the Daily Operatory Schedule report to print in the Print copies of Operatory Schedule field. The default value is 1. To print or display patient phone numbers on the Daily Operatory Schedule report : Select Print Phone Numbers on Operatory Schedule . Click Next . The next wizard window is displayed. Select an option: Display office codes instead of ADA codes on the Day Screen : Displays office codes in the Daily Appointments window and prints them in the Daily Operatory Schedule report results. For more information, see Setting Scheduler Defaults . Save appointment(s) to history when transaction is posted : Saves posted appointments to the History file after a transaction is posted. When this option is selected, you can view old appointments in the History Appointments window. If this option is not selected, all appointment information is deleted after the transactions for the day are posted. For more information, see Enabling History Appointments . Save slot notes and blocked slots to history: Displays slot notes and blocked slots in the History Appointments window. If you do not want slot notes and blocked slots to display in the History Appointments window, deselect this option. Click Next . The next wizard window is displayed. To display a message that asks you to log a contact after confirming an appointment, select Ask to log contact after confirming appointments . Click Next . The next wizard window is displayed. To automatically delete a new patient or account if they fail to show

topics.title: Internet Menu Commands
topics.body: Internet Menu Commands Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Main Menu Bar Internet Menu Commands U se the Internet menu to access and record URL addresses for the websites associated with pharmacies, labs, insurance companies, patient employers, and referring doctors. The Internet menu commands are: SoftDent on the Web : Display the SoftDent practice management software website. Carestream Dental Home Page : Display the Dental products and services page on the Carestream website. Product Education : Display the SoftDent practice management software: Training webpage. Online Resource Center: Display the Online Resource Center website, where you can search the online support database, review product notifications, and display announcements, special offers, discounts, and special events. Carestream Dental Institute: Display the Carestream Dental Institute website, where you can participate in interactive web training and learn about new features and enhancements added to the software. My Homepage : Display the website of your choice. Other Web Sites : Add, edit, and remove websites that you access. Setup My Homepage/URL's : Add or edit the Homepage and Carestream Dental Sitebuilder website. Bulk E-Mail : Type an e-mail message to distribute to groups of recipients. Compose E-Mail : Send e-mails to patients, pharmacies, labs, and other doctors. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Filling the InOffice Window
topics.body: Filling the InOffice Window Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Filling the InOffice Window Before you can use InOffice , y ou must populate the window with patient and appointment information for the current day. The information from the scheduler is used to fill the InOffice window. To fill in the InOffice window: Select an option: Select List > InOffice. Click InOffice Window in the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. In any window, press Alt + F5. A blank InOffice window is displayed. Click Fill InOffice on the toolbar. The Fill InOffice window is displayed. The current date is displayed in the Fill For Date field. You can enter another date if necessary. Select an option: To print routing slips for each patient that is scheduled for an appointment for the current day : Select Printer Router? . If this option is selected, the Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . To print routing slips manually, see Manually Printing Routing Slips . If a provider-preferred routing slip is specified in the provider record, that form is printed. To remove any appointments left from the previous day : Select Clear InOffice and click OK . To restrict the appointments that are displayed in the window to a specific set of user codes : Type the codes in the Only include patient with the User Codes field and click OK . If the date in the Last History column is red, the patient is due for a new medical history. The medical history interval is set in the Recall Settings window. For more information, see Setting Up Recalls . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding InOffice Color Coding
topics.body: Understanding InOffice Color Coding Tracking Patients in the Office > Using InOffice Window Options Understanding InOffice Color Coding The InOffice feature uses a color-coded system that enables you to track patient locations and statuses by the color in which the information is displayed in each column. The list of the colors used in the InOffice window and an explanation of the condition associated with each color are: Black : Text is displayed in black text when you click Fill InOffice. Red : Time in the Appt column is displayed in red text fifteen minutes before a scheduled appointment. Blue : Text of the row containing appointment information is displayed in blue after you click Check In. Fuchsia : The text is displayed in fuchsia for new patient appointments. Red : The text is displayed in red when the patient has a medical alert. Green : The text is displayed in green when you click Change Location to move the patient to t he operatory. Blue : The text is displayed in blue when you click Change Location to return the patient to the waiting room. Red : The text is displayed in red when you select Complete in the ELECTRONIC ROUTER window. Purple : The text is displayed in purple after the procedures are posted and the History Appointments feature is enabled. See Understanding History Appointments Window Options . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient Visit Statistics Report
topics.body: Running the Patient Visit Statistics Report Tracking Patients in the Office > Generating Patient Visit Statistics Reports Running the Patient Visit Statistics Report After consistent and reliable data about patient visits has been entered in the InOffice, you can track your practice�s efficiency by running the Patient Visit Statistics report. Sample Report To run the Patient Visit Statistics report: Select Patient Visit Statistics from the System menu. The Patient Visit Statistics window is displayed. Select the providers whose statistics you want to calculate from the Providers drop-down lists, or type 999 in the first drop-down list to run the report for all providers in your office. Select an option: To run the report for the current date : Click Today . To run the report from the beginning of the current week (Sunday) to the current date : Click WeekToDate . To run the report from the first day of the month to the current date : Click MonthToDate . To run the report for a specific date range : Select the start and end dates from the Date Range drop-down lists. Click Compute . If the patient average wait time is exceeded : The text of the row containing the provider information is blue. If the percentage of patients seated on time is not met : The text of the row containing the provider information is blue. The summary information is displayed in red. To print the Patient Visit Statistics report, click Print . To view a printable job aid about the Patient Visit Statistics Report, see Generating the Patient Visit Statistics Report . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting InOffice HIPAA Privacy
topics.body: Setting InOffice HIPAA Privacy System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features > Using the HIPAA Privacy Feature Setting InOffice HIPAA Privacy You can toggle HIPAA Privacy in the InOffice window. To enable HIPAA Privacy in the InOffice window only : Click Privacy Is Off . The feature is enabled, the button text changes to HIPAA Privacy On, and patient initials are displayed in the Name (Last, First) column for each appointment. If the global HIPAA Privacy feature is disabled, this feature is disabled when you close the window. T o disable HIPAA Privacy in the InOffice window only : Click Privacy Is On . The feature is disabled, the button text changes to HIPAA Privacy Off, and patient full names are displayed in the Name (Last, First) column for each appointment. If the global HIPAA Privacy feature is enabled, this feature is enabled when you close the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient Visit Statistics Detail Report
topics.body: Running the Patient Visit Statistics Detail Report Tracking Patients in the Office > Generating Patient Visit Statistics Reports Running the Patient Visit Statistics Detail Report The Patient Visit Statistics Detail report l ists all appointments within a specified date range for a provider. The columns displayed in the Patient Visit Statistics Detail report are: Appt Date : The calendar date of the appointment Appt Time : The appointment time Patient ID : The patient's ID number Patient Arrival : The patient's check in time (on time or late) Appt Length : The amount of time scheduled for the appointment Actual Appt Length : The amount of time the patient spends in the operatory Difference : The difference between the scheduled and actual appointment lengths To run the Patient Visit Statistics Detail report: Complete steps 1 through 4 in Running the Patient Visit Statistics Report . Click Details . The Output Options dialog box is displayed. Select an option: To print the report : Select Printer and click OK . To review the report : Select Print Review and click OK . To copy the report to a file : Select File and click OK . The Select File Name dialog box is displayed. Type the file name and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Insurance Plan Database Events
topics.body: Insurance Plan Database Events Using TeamTalk > Understanding TeamTalk Events Insurance Plan Database Events The insurance plan record events that can be set up to display a TeamTalk note are: Editing Insurance Plan : Displays a TeamTalk note when editing an insurance plan record. Updating from Trojan Data : If you subscribe to Trojan, displays a TeamTalk note when updating insurance plan information from the Trojan database. Viewing Contact List : Displays a TeamTalk note when viewing the contact list associated with an insurance plan. Viewing Transactions : Displays a TeamTalk note when viewing DMO/Capitation and Medicaid Insurance plan transactions.
