Making Appointment : Displays a TeamTalk note when making an appointment for a patient who is assigned to the insurance plan. Calling Administrator : Displays a TeamTalk note when calling the insurance plan administrator or contact. Checking-out Patient : Displays a TeamTalk note when checking out a patient who is assigned to the insurance plan. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting an Insurance Payment to an Ortho Case Claim
topics.body: Posting an Insurance Payment to an Ortho Case Claim Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Posting Payments to Ortho Claims Posting an Insurance Payment to an Ortho Case Claim To post an insurance payment to an ortho case claim: In the Patient Transactions List window, click ADD . The Patient Transactions window is displayed in ADD mode. Select the transaction code from the Code drop-down list. Select the insurance payment code from the Code drop-down list. Select the production doctor from the Production Dr drop-down list Select the collection doctor from the Collection Dr drop-down list. The Transactions - Insurance Payment window is displayed. Select the payment amount from the Amount drop-down list. Select the claim that is associated with the patient from the Claim drop-down list. Type the check number, bank number, and any notes in the appropriate fields, and click OK . A message asking you to update the bluebook is displayed. Select an option: To update the bluebook : Click Yes and see Using the Insurance Bluebook . To update the bluebook later : Click No . A message asks you whether this is the final insurance payment for the selected claim is displayed. Select an option: If this is the final insurance payment for the selected claim : Click Yes . If the patient has secondary insurance, a message asks you whether to open a secondary claim. If this is not the final insurance payment : Click No . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Insured Accounts Report
topics.body: Running the Insured Accounts Report Setting Up Accounts > Running Account Reports Running the Insured Accounts Report The Insured Accounts report generates an alphabetical list of insured accounts, including the account guarantor's home phone number and current balance. Sample Report To run the Insured Accounts report: Select Reports > Account > Insured . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Account List Report window is displayed. In the Account Names section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending names you want in the report. Select Include Inactive Accounts to include inactive accounts in the report. In the Account User Codes section, type the user codes you want to include or exclude from the report. If you selected the Merge Letter Expert output option, the Letter Expert Template ID field is active. Click the drop-down arrow to select a template from the list. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Insurance Batch Preview Report
topics.body: Running the Insurance Batch Preview Report Submitting and Tracking Claims > Running Claim Reports Running the Insurance Batch Preview Report The Insurance Batch Preview report lists all the insurance claims in the batch file. For each claim in the batch file, this report lists the patient's ID, name, insurance company, claim number, claim amount, and whether the claim can be submitted electronically. Code, description, tooth, and surface details are also included. Sample Report To run the Insurance Batch Preview report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Insurance Batch Preview Listing . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Batch Preview Report Setup window is displayed. All options are selected by default in the Claims section. Deselect a claim type, if necessary. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Carriers/Insurance Companies
topics.body: Carriers/Insurance Companies Trojan > Handling Trojan Traditional Plans Carriers/Insurance Companies To view a list of Trojan insurance companies associated with Trojan employer plans: Select List > Trojan > Traditional Plans > Carrier List . The Carrier list window is displayed, showing a list of insurance companies, or carriers. This list can be sorted by name or by Trojan ID number. The default is Name. To sort by ID, click ID . To see details about a particular insurance company, enter the plan�s name in the Find field and press Enter. The record is highlighted in the list window. Press Enter. The Carrier record is displayed and contains the following fields: Administration ID : Displays the insurance company's ID name or number assigned by Trojan. Phone Number : Lists the carrier's corporate telephone number. Company Name : Displays the insurance company's name. Address : Displays the corporate address. ID : Displays the ID number of the software insurance company that is linked to this Trojan insurance company. Payor ID : Displays the payor ID number used when filing insurance claims electronically. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Insurance Payment Distribution Report
topics.body: Running the Insurance Payment Distribution Report Handling Reports > Accounting Reports Running the Insurance Payment Distribution Report The Insurance Payment Distribution report lists all patients covered by insurance payments posted for a specific date range. If desired, you can print the report for EFT payments only. The Insurance Payment Distribution Report had previously been named the Insurance Check Distribution Report. To run the Insurance Payment Distribution report: Select Reports > Accounting > Insurance Payment Distribution . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Insurance Payment Distribution Report window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows in the Starting and Ending fields to select the report dates from a calendar. To print the report showing only EFT payments, click Print EFT Payments Only . Click OK . Additional Details: This report lists the code, bank number, check number, insurance company name and ID, and amount for each insurance payment posted. The report also lists the patients or plan names and IDs covered by each payment, and their covered portion. Note : The same information is contained in the report for EFT payments only. Sample Report Sample EFT Only Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Insurance Company Documents
topics.body: Viewing Insurance Company Documents Using the Document Manager > Viewing Documents Viewing Insurance Company Documents To view claim documents associated with an insurance company, select an option: In the Insurance Co List window : Select the company and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Insurance Co window : Select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Documents for window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Insurance Company Labels Report
topics.body: Running the Insurance Company Labels Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Company Reports Running the Insurance Company Labels Report The Insurance Company Labels report prints an alphabetical list of insurance company labels or a list of insurance company labels by identification number. Sample Label To print insurance company labels: Select an option: To print the labels in alphabetical order : Select Reports > Insurance > Labels > Alphabetical Company . The Output Options window is displayed. To print the labels by company identification number : Select Reports > Insurance > Labels > Companies by ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Insurance Company Labels Setup or Insurance Company Labels by ID Setup window is displayed. Select an option: If you are printing labels in alphabetical order : The Insurance Company Labels Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending names, type the number of lines per label, and click OK . If you are printing labels by identification number : The Insurance Company Labels By ID Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending identification numbers, type the number of lines per label, and click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Insurance Company Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Insurance Co List and Insurance Co Window Options
topics.body: Using Insurance Co List and Insurance Co Window Options Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Company Information Using Insurance Co List and Insurance Co Window Options Commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: 0 - Auto Dialer : Dials an insurance company number. If you do not have a modem, this information is displayed for your reference. Labels : Prints insurance company labels or laser envelopes. Update from Trojan : Updates insurance company information from the Trojan database. This command is enabled only if you subscribe to Trojan. Setup Trojan Update Options : Sets up options to update your insurance company information from the Trojan database. This command is enabled only if you subscribe to Trojan. Letter Expert : Merges insurance company information with a letter expert template. Replace Ins Co ID : Deletes duplicate insurance company records. Contacts > By Date : Adds, edits, prints, or deletes insurance company contact information; attaches a memo to a contact. Contacts > By Type : Adds, edits, prints, or deletes insurance company contact information; attaches a memo to a contact. Claim Documents : Views documents attached to insurance company claims. Show Insurance Plans : Views, adds, edits, or deletes insurance plans. Show Insured Patients : Views patients assigned to the insurance company. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Insurance Plan Defaults for Plans That Require Coupon Books or Continuous Care Forms
topics.body: Setting Insurance Plan Defaults for Plans That Require Coupon Books or Continuous Care Forms Planning Budgets > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Managing Ortho Cases Setting Insurance Plan Defaults for Plans That Require Coupon Books or Continuous Care Forms To set the insurance plan defaults for insurance plans that require a coupon book or continuous care form: Select an option: Select List > Ins. Plan . From any window, press Ctrl + P. Select List Insurance Plans from the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. The Insurance Plan List window is displayed in FIND mode. Select the insurance plan. Click Claim Filing , or press Ctrl + Tab until the Claim Filing tab is the active window. Select Resubmit Continuous Care Form . Type the monthly interval required by the plan for submitting the form in the Payment/Resubmission Frequency field. Example : If the plan requires you to resubmit monthly, enter 1. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Insurance Defaults in Billing and Receivables
topics.body: Setting Insurance Defaults in Billing and Receivables Billing and Receivables Setting Insurance Defaults in Billing and Receivables Before you can begin billing accounts, you must select a default insurance calculation method (ICM), which determines the amount due from each patient. Related Topics Selecting an Insurance Calculation Method Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Insurance Defaults
topics.body: Setting Insurance Defaults Setting Up Insurance Setting Insurance Defaults Before using the software to track insurance claims or entering insurance company and plan information, set up your default insurance settings. You can set the following insurance defaults: Default insurance calculation method (ICM) When a prompt is displayed for processing insurance claims When the bluebook is updated How benefits are distributed To set up your insurance defaults: Select System > Change System Settings > Insurance . The Insurance Settings window is displayed. Select an option from the Default Insurance Calculation Method for new accounts drop-down list: If your practice bills an account the full fee at the time of service and does not accept benefits assignment : Select ICM0 . ICM0 - The amount due ages immediately from the service date, and the estimated insurance payment is calculated. If your practice bills an account after billing and receiving payment from the insurance company : Select ICM1 . If ICM1 is assigned to an account with insurance, the Insurance Calculation wizard is displayed. The wizard reminds you that to bill the patient portion immediately, the account must be set to ICM2. ICM1 - Patient charges are held current until the final insurance payment is posted, and the account is billed the unpaid portion. This amount ages from the final insurance payment date. If your practice bills the estimated portion (the fee less any estimated insurance payment) at the time of service : Select ICM2 . The estimated insurance amount does not age until the final insurance payment is posted. The estimated patient portion ages from the service date. Select an option from the Process claims after submitting insurance drop-down list: To display a message that asks you to print or batch insurance claims : Select Ask Print or Batch . To send the claim automatically to the batch file after it is submitted : Select Send to Batch. To print the claim automatically after it is submitted : Select Print . Type the number of copies to print in the Default number of copies for each insurance form: field. To enable insurance estimation, select Enable Insurance estimation . The insurance payment is estimated when you schedule or post a procedure. The payment amount is determined by the insurance bluebook data, percentages, and copays entered on the Coverage tab of the Insurance Plan window. To display a message prompting you to update the bluebook after posting an insurance payment, select Update bluebook after insurance payments . To send insurance payments directly to the patient, select Default benefits to patient for new plans . To display any bluebook notes associated with the transaction code in the Assistant when scheduling appointments, select Display bluebook notes for appointments . To print practice information as the billing dentist on an insurance claim form, select Print Practice Name on ADA Forms . To display any bluebook notes associated with the transaction code in the Assistant when posting transactions, select Display bluebook notes for transactions . To enable ePostings, select Enable ePostings Service . If you use eClaims to submit claims electronically and want to print the claim after it is submitted, select Print copy of ECS claims after submitting . To enable the Real Time Claim Submission service, select Enable Real Time Claim Service . To use an existing dental plan as a template, select a plan from the Percentage, Flat Fee, Percenta

topics.title: Using the eVerifications Insurance Eligibility Service
topics.body: Using the eVerifications Insurance Eligibility Service eServices > Using the eVerifications Service Using the eVerifications Insurance Eligibility Service The eVerifications Insurance Eligibility Service: Enables you to verify a patient�s eligibility with an insurance company Requires enrollment and an active Internet connection To activate this option, contact an eServices representative. Responses from insurance companies vary and are restricted by what information can be returned. Related Topics Checking Patient Eligibility Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Insurance Features
topics.body: Insurance Features What's New in Version 14.0 Insurance Features Several enhancements have been made to insurance functions: Sorting Completed Claims By Patient Name The Completed Claim List window ( List > Completed Claim ) now has a Name sort option, enabling you to sort the completed claims by patient name. When the Name sort option is selected, the list is sorted by the last name of the patient associated with the claim. If there are claims for multiple patients with the same last name, the list is further sorted by ID. If there are multiple claims for the same patient, the list is further sorted by claim ID. Viewing a Log File for Claims Deleted After Running the Resubmit or Delete by Age Function You can now view and print a log file reflecting the claims that are deleted as a result of running the Resubmit/Delete by Age function. See Resubmitting Claims by Age and Deleting Claims By Age for more information. Contact Notes Available in the Claim List, Claim, and Completed Claim Windows You can now access contact notes for claims in the Claim List, Claim, and Completed Claim windows. The Contacts By Date and Contacts By Type options have been added to the Options menu for each of the above windows. Contact notes work the same way in these windows as they do in other areas of the software: Selecting Contacts By Date displays a list of contacts for the selected claim sorted by date. Selecting Contacts By Type displays a list of contacts for the selected claim sorted by type. Selecting Print prints a copy of the Contacts List. Double-clicking on a contact displays a window where you can edit the contact. When a claim is paid and marked complete, the contacts stay with the claim and can be accessed from the Completed Claim List window. See Using Contacts for more information. Estimating Secondary Insurance Benefits A new option�Coordinate benefits when used as secondary plan�has been added to the Claim Filing tab of the Insurance Plan window ( List > Insurance Plan > Double-click a plan > Claim Filing ). If you select this option, the software estimates the secondary insurance amount based on the total fee minus the deductible, minus the amount already estimated to be paid by the primary plan. Example : A primary plan pays 78% of a transaction after the patient pays a $50.00 deductible. $125.00 fee - $50.00 deductible x 78% = $58.50 The secondary plan pays 55% of a transaction after the patient pays a $50.00 deductible and the primary plan amount has been subtracted. $125.00 fee - $50.00 deductible - $58.50 primary plan amount x 55% = 9.07 If you do not select this option, the software estimates the secondary amount as it always has, using the total fee and not taking into account the amount already estimated to be paid by the primary plan. Example: A primary plan pays 78% of a transaction after the patient pays a $50.00 deductible. $125.00 fee - $50.00 deductible x 78% = $58.50 The secondary plan pays 55% of a transaction after the patient pays a $50.00 deductible. $125.00 fee - $50 deductible x 55% = $41.25 See Adding Insurance Plans for more information. Applying a Write-Off to a Patient Balance A new option� Upgraded Procedure (write-off amount applied to patient balance) �has been added to the Allowance Table window ( List > Allowance Table > View Codes > Add/Edit ) and the General tab of the Bluebook window ( List > Bluebook > Select Plan > Edit ). Whenever the software estimates primary insurance, it checks the bluebook entry to

topics.title: Running the Insurance Income Report
topics.body: Running the Insurance Income Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Running the Insurance Income Report The Insurance Income report tracks how much income your practice receives from each insurance company you deal with. It gives a breakdown of the total payments and adjustments for any insurance plan or company entered in your system. You cannot run this report for DMO/capitation plans. Sample Report To run the Insurance Income report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Insurance Income . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Insurance Income Report Setup window is displayed. In the Date Range section, click the drop-down arrows to select the dates from a calendar. In the Doctors section, use the drop-down arrows to select specific providers, or leave 999 to report on all providers. In the IDs section, use the drop-down arrows to select a specific insurance company or plan, or leave as 0 for all companies and plans. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Insurance Plan List and Insurance Plan Window Options
topics.body: Using Insurance Plan List and Insurance Plan Window Options Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Using Insurance Plan List and Insurance Plan Window Options In the Insurance Plan List or Insurance Plan window, you can: View insurance plan details. Access a history file. Use the auto dialer. Print address labels. Print laser envelopes. View transactions for Medicaid and DMO/capitation plans. Log and track insurance plan contact information. Receive Trojan updates. Print Trojan information associated with the insurance plan. Merge insurance company information with a Letter Expert template. View claim documents attached to the insurance plan record. View the insurance company associated with the selected insurance plan. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: History : Accesses a history file using your word processing software. In your word processing software, enter notes for the plan. The file name is plannumber.hst. History files are stored in the \SoftDent\History directory. Labels > Label : Prints an insurance plan label. Labels > Envelope : Prints a insurance plan laser envelope. 0 - Auto Dialer : Dials an insurance company number. If you do not have a modem, this information is displayed for your reference. Update from Trojan : Updates insurance plan information from the Trojan database. This command is enabled only if you subscribe to Trojan. Print Trojan Plan : Prints the Trojan information associated with the selected plan. Trojan Update Options : Sets up options to update your insurance plan information from the Trojan database. Letter Expert : Merges insurance plan information with a letter template. Replace Plan ID : Deletes duplicate insurance plans. BlueBook : Adds, edits, and deletes insurance plan bluebook notes. Transactions : Posts insurance company payments to the plan and prints plan transactions. This command is enabled only when a DMO/Capitation or Medicaid plan is selected. Contacts > By Date : Adds, edits, prints, or deletes insurance plan contact information; attaches a memo to the contact. Contacts > By Type : Adds, edits, prints, or deletes insurance plan contact information; attaches a memo to the contact. Claim Documents : Views claim documents associated with the insurance plan. Show Insurance Company : Views, adds, edits, or deletes information for the insurance company associated with the insurance plan. Show Insured Patients : Views a list of patients assigned to the insurance plan. TeamTalk : Views conditions associated with the note and deletes expired notes. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Insurance Menu Commands
topics.body: Insurance Menu Commands Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Main Menu Bar Insurance Menu Commands U se the Insurance menu to review insurance claims, print batched claims, delete completed claims, reprint claims, view a list of forms that have not been printed, select insurance forms, resubmit claims, transfer claim balances, reset patient benefits, and recalculate treatment plans. The Insurance menu commands are: Insurance Payments : Enter insurance company check payments. You can also access ePostings from this menu. Review Claim : Search for an insurance claim by the last name, patient identification number, or claim number. You can also print copies, perform a primary or secondary submittal, cancel a claim, review claim documents, and create or view claim attachments. Print Batched Claims : Print batched files. Delete Completed Claims : Delete all claims that were submitted before a specific date. Restart Batch Print : Print batch files by a specific number or date. Count of Forms not Printed : View the list of forms that have not been printed. Select Insurance Forms : Select an insurance form. Resubmit or Delete by Age : Resubmit or delete all claims submitted before a specific date. Transfer Balance of Old Claims to Patient : Transfer all claim balances from claims that were submitted before a specific date to a patient account. Insurance Plan Rollover : Reset insurance benefits. Treat Plan Recalc of Benefits : Recalculate treatment plan benefits. eServices : Display the eServices software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Associating Insurance Companies with NEA Payors
topics.body: Associating Insurance Companies with NEA Payors Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services Associating Insurance Companies with NEA Payors After you set NEA preferences, you must set up the participating insurance companies with the corresponding NEA payors. The insurance plan linked to any company associated with an NEA payor must also be ECS capable. To set up an insurance company with an NEA payor in the software: Select List > Ins Co . The Insurance Co List window is displayed. Double-click the insurance company to associate with a payor. The company record is displayed in the Insurance Co window. Type the information in the ECS Information section of the window. Click the NEA Info tab. Select a payer from the Payer drop-down list. The NEA payer ID is displayed next to the field, and information for sending attachments to the payor is displayed in the Message field. Click OK . Click Close . Repeat steps 2 through 7 for all of the insurance companies that work with NEA payors. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering Insurance Payment Notes
topics.body: Entering Insurance Payment Notes Posting Payments > Posting Insurance Payments Entering Insurance Payment Notes To enter notes for a claim, from the Insurance Payments window: Select Notes Mode from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Type the note. The information entered in this field is displayed in the Note field in the Transaction window for the write-off transactions (code 50.90), the risk write-off code (code 50.91), and the insurance check payment code. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Insurance Plan Documents
topics.body: Viewing Insurance Plan Documents Using the Document Manager > Viewing Documents Viewing Insurance Plan Documents To view claim documents associated with an insurance plan, select an option: In the Insurance Plan List window : Select the plan and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Insurance Plan window : Select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Documents for window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Insurance Plans By ID Report
topics.body: Running the Insurance Plans By ID Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Plan Reports Running the Insurance Plans by ID Report The Insurance Plans by ID report generates a list of insurance plans by insurance plan identification number. Sample Report To generate a list of insurance plans by identification number: Select Reports > Insurance > Plans By ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Insurance Plan ID Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending plan identification numbers and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Insurance Practice Management Reports
topics.body: Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports Running Insurance Practice Management Reports You can run the following insurance related practice management reports: Insurance Income report : Run this report to track how much income your practice receives from each insurance company. This report details the total payments and adjustments for any insurance plan or company. You cannot run this report for DMO/ Capitation plans. Write-Off Totals report : Run this report to generate a list of codes that have had a portion of their fee written off. This report includes the total write-offs posted in the specified date range, including those that may have been compressed, and a total of the actual write-offs. Contractual Plan Analysis report : Run this report to generate a list of productions posted using a DMO/capitation fee schedule and to compare the charges to the total of the same production for fee schedule 0. Contractual Plan Production report : Run this report to generate a list of all procedures posted using a DMO fee schedule. It details the quantity performed and the amount of money generated by the production of each procedure. Contractual Plan Production by Company report : Run this report to generate a list of all procedures posted using a DMO fee schedule. The quantity performed and the amount of money generated by production are included in the report. This report is sorted by insurance company. Capitation Collections Summary : Run this report to summarize the collections received for any capitation plan during a specific time period. Capitation Plan Encounter report : Run this report to generate a list of patients with transactions posted during a specific time period for the selected capitation plan. How many patients were seen within the specified time, the total number of procedures performed, and the total amount charged to these patients are included in the report. Capitation Insurance Company Encounter report : Run this report to generate a list of all patients with transactions posted for a specific time period for the selected capitation plan. How many patients were seen within the specified time, the total number of procedures performed, and the total amount charged to these patients are included in the report. This report is sorted by insurance company. Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report: Run this report to produce a list of Medicaid services provided during a given time period. Related Topics Running the Insurance Income Report Running the Write-Off Totals Report Running the Contractual Plan Analysis Report Running the Contractual Plan Production Report Running the Contractual Plan Production by Company Report Running the Capitation Collections Summary Report Running the Capitation Plan Encounter Report Running the Capitation Insurance Company Encounter Report Running the Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Insured Patients Report
topics.body: Running the Insured Patients Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Insured Patients Report The Insured Patient report generates an alphabetical list of insured patients. Sample Report To generate a list of insured patients: Select Reports > Patient > Insured . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patient List Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Insurance Payments Window Options
topics.body: Using Insurance Payments Window Options Posting Payments > Posting Insurance Payments Using Insurance Payments Window Options The Insurance Payments window Options commands are: Post : Posts claims to the Transactions window for each patient. Load Claims : Lists all outstanding claims for an insurance company and plan in the Insurance Payments window. Remove : Removes a claim from the list. Sort : Sorts the list of claims by patient last name, patient Social Security number, or date of service. Edit Write Off for Transfer : Displays the Claim Line Item Adjustment window. Use this to edit the write-off amount for a claim. Edit Check Info : Displays the Insurance Check Info window. Use this to edit the check information. Payment Denied : Indicates whether payment for a claim has been denied. A Y in the * column indicates that payment was denied. Mark as Final Payment : Indicates whether the current payment is the final payment for a claim. An asterisk (*) next to the patient's name indicates that the final payment has been collected. Payment Mode : Displays the Insurance Payments window in Payment mode. Notes Mode : Displays the Insurance Payments window in Notes mode. The Notes column is displayed. Type an appropriate note in the column. See Entering Insurance Payment Notes . Write Off Mode : Displays the Insurance Payments window in Write Off mode. Capture Claim Documents : Displays the Bulk Insurance Document Capture window. Use this to capture documents to attach to a claim. View Claim : Displays a claim. View EOB : Displays the Explanation of Benefits for a claim. This command is enabled only if your office scans and imports claims into the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Reset Insurance Rollover Date Utility
topics.body: Running the Reset Insurance Rollover Date Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Reset Insurance Rollover Date Utility Run t his utility to identify and reset accounts in which the last rollover date in the patient�s Guarantors/Insurance window is set to a future date. This problem can occur if a user forward-dates the computer and enters transactions for an account. If the last rollover date is set to a future date, the plan�s benefits will not roll over when the plan reaches its anniversary. As a result, insurance estimation may not take place when it should. Using this utility, you can search all accounts for guarantors whose last rollover date is greater than the current date and reset the date to the plan�s last anniversary date. To reset the insurance rollover dates for these accounts: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Reset Insurance Rollover Date . The Reset Scheduler Default Book window is displayed. Select an option: To identify and reset future rollover dates : Select Identify and reset future rollover dates . To identify the future recall dates only : Select Identify future rollover dates WITHOUT resetting them . A message asks you to confirm the current system date. Click Yes . A message informs you the process is complete and specifies the number of found future recall dates. Click OK . A message asks you to print a log. Select an option: To print the log : Click Yes . A message asks you to specify the print option. Select Print and click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . If you do not want to print the log : Click No . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Insurance Reports
topics.body: Insurance Reports Handling Reports Insurance Reports You can access the following reports from the Reports > Insurance menu: Report Name Description Frequency Alphabetical Insurance Company List Select this report to generate a list of insurance companies in alphabetical order. For more information, see Running the Alphabetical Insurance Companies Report . As Needed Insurance Companies By ID Select this report to generate a list of insurance companies by identification number. For more information, see Running the Insurance Companies by ID Report . As Needed Patients By Insurance Company By Company ID Select this report to generate an alphabetical list of patients assigned to plans offered by an insurance company, sorted by company ID. For more information, see Running the Patients By Insurance Company By Company ID Report . As Needed Patients By Insurance Company By Company Name Select this report to generate an alphabetical list of patients insured by a specific insurance company, sorted by company name. For more information, see Running the Patients by Insurance Company by Company Name Report . Alphabetical Insurance Company Labels Select this report to generate alphabetically-sorted insurance company labels. For more information, see Running the Insurance Company Labels Report . As Needed Insurance Company Labels By ID Select this report to generate insurance company labels sorted by identification number. For more information, see Running the Insurance Company Labels Report . As Needed Alphabetical Insurance Plan List Select this report to generate a list of insurance plans in alphabetical order. For more information, see Running the Alphabetical Insurance Plan List Report . As Needed Insurance Plans By ID Select this report to generate a list of insurance plans by identification number. For more information, see Running the Insurance Plans by ID Report . As Needed Patients By Insurance Plan By Plan ID Select this report to generate an alphabetical list of patients assigned to insurance plans, sorted by plan ID. For more information, see Running the Patients By Insurance Plan by Plan ID Report . As Needed Patients By Insurance Plan By Plan Name Select this report to generate an alphabetical list of patients assigned to insurance plans, sorted by plan name. For more information, see Running the Patients by Insurance Plan by Plan Name Report . BlueBook Select this report to generate bluebook dates for insurance plans. For more information, see Running the Bluebook Report . As Needed Alphabetical Employers List Select this report to generate a list of employers in alphabetical order. For more information, see Running the Alphabetical Employers List Report . As Needed Employer By ID Select this report to generate a list of employers by identification number. For more information, see Running the Employers by ID Report . As Needed Alphabetical School List Select this report to generate a list of schools in alphabetical order. For more information, see Running the Alphabetical School List Report . As Needed Schools By ID Select this report to generate a list of schools by identification number. For more information, see Running the Schools by ID Report . As Needed Alphabetical Other Facility List Select this report to generate a list of other facilities in alphabetical order. For more information, see Running the Alphabetical Other Facilities Report . As Needed Other Facility By ID Select this report to generate a list of other facilities by identification number. For more information, see Running the Other Facilities by ID Report . As Needed Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Installing SoftDent
topics.body: Installing SoftDent Getting Started Installing SoftDent You must install the server portion of SoftDent on a server class operating system before you can install the software on a workstation. You must install the workstation portion of the software on each computer that functions as a workstation. For more information, see the SoftDent Practice Management Software Installation Guide . Access this guide from your Windows task bar by selecting Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > Online Documentation > Installation Guide . The guide opens in a separate window using the Adobe Reader Software. The procedures in the Installation Guide are different for installing on a server or on a workstation, and for performing a new installation or an upgrade to a newer version. Read the applicable portion of the installation guide carefully before installing or upgrading the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Installing the M2SYS Fingerprint Software
topics.body: Installing the M2SYS Fingerprint Software Using the Fingerprint Scanner Installing the M2SYS Fingerprint Software You must install the M2SYS Fingerprint software on the server and the workstation computers that use it. You must have a license to use the fingerprint scanner. When you install SoftDent, shortcut icons that enable you to install the M2SYS Fingerprint software are added to the server and workstation desktops. To install the software: First, install the software on the server by double-clicking the Install M2SYS Fingerprint Server icon. Follow the instructions. Then double-click the Install M2SYS Fingerprint Client icon on each workstation that uses the fingerprint scanner. Follow the instructions. When prompted, install the fingerprint scanner by plugging the scanner into an empty USB port. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Inserting Images into Templates
topics.body: Inserting Images into Templates Using Experts > Working with Letter Expert Templates Inserting Images into Templates Embedding an image e nables you to insert images from the patient document tree and display them in the final letter. To create a template, see Creating Letter Expert Templates . To add an image to your template: While viewing a template, place the cursor where you want the image to appear and click Add New Image on the SoftDent toolbar. The Insert a Patient Image window is displayed. Select an option: If the image is required : Select Required . If the image is optional : Select Optional . Click OK . The template is displayed. The image place holder is embedded in the document and the image is added to the data when it is merged with this template. To add a merge field to the template : See Inserting Merge Fields into Templates . To add a list to the template : See Inserting Lists into Templates . Click Back to SoftDent to save the template and return to the software. The Template Information window is displayed. Select an option: To save your changes : Click Edit . To create a new template : Click Add . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Installing the KIOSK Check-In Module on a Workstation
topics.body: Installing the KIOSK Check-In Module on a Workstation Using the KIOSK Check-In Module Installing the KIOSK Check-In Module on a Workstation Install the KIOSK check-in module on the computer your patients will use to check in. This computer does not need to have the full version of SoftDent installed, only the KIOSK module. After the module is installed, you must activate the KIOSK from the SoftDent software on the SoftDent server. To install the KIOSK module on a computer: Verify that all programs are closed, navigate to softwaredownload.csdental.com , and download the SoftDent software. Click the downloaded ISO file. The Directory window is displayed. Double-click InstallMenu.exe . The SoftDent Installation window is displayed. Select Install SoftDent KIOSK . The installer program is launched. Follow the instructions in the installer. To activate the KIOSK module, see Activating and Deactivating the KIOSK Check-In Module . Related Topics Activating and Deactivating the KIOSK Check-In Module Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Installing PracticeToGo
topics.body: Installing PracticeToGo Using PracticeToGo Installing PracticeToGo Integration with the desired PracticeToGo platform is the first step in using PracticeToGo. PracticeToGo can be integrated with either the Palm OS platform or the Microsoft Outlook 2000 software or higher. PracticeToGo integrates with either the Palm OS platform or the Microsoft Outlook software platform. It will not integrate with both of these platforms simultaneously. See the following for more information: Hooking Up Your Palm Organizer Integrating PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform Integrating PracticeToGo with Microsoft Outlook Related Topics Hooking Up Your Palm Organizer Integrating PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform Integrating PracticeToGo with Microsoft Outlook After the Integration Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Inserting Lists into Templates
topics.body: Inserting Lists into Templates Using Experts > Working with Letter Expert Templates Inserting Lists into Templates You can add lists to a letter template. Example : You can add a list of scheduled appointments for the entire family to one letter and eliminate the need to send multiple appointment letters to the same guarantor. When you want to create a letter from a template that contains a merge list, the data is automatically merged with the list and creates a finished letter. To create a template, see Creating Letter Expert Templates . To add a list to your template: While viewing the template, place the cursor where you want the list to appear and click Add New List on the SoftDent toolbar. The SoftDent List Creation window is displayed. Select the category from the Category field in the List Selection Options section. Select an option: To view a list of data fields that typically appear in a letter : Select Display only common fields . To view a complete list of all data fields for the specified category : Select Display all fields . A list of categories and data fields is displayed in the Available List Fields section. A category contains a list of data fields that specifically relate to it. Expand the category containing the data field you want to add to the list. A small minus sign (-) is displayed left of the text when a category is expanded. A small plus sign (+) is displayed left of the text when a category is collapsed. To expand or collapse a category, click the category. Select an option to manipulate a data field: To add a field : Select it and click Add Field to List . To remove a field : Select it and click Remove Field From List . To clear all fields : Click Clear All Fields From List . To change the display order for a field : Select it and click Move Up or Move Down . After adding all of the fields that you want to include in the list, click OK . To add a merge field to the template : See Inserting Merge Fields into Templates . To add an image to the template : See Inserting Images into Templates . Click Back to SoftDent to save the template and return to the software. The Template Information window is displayed. Select an option: To save your changes : Click Edit . To create a new template : Click Add . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Installing Trojan
topics.body: Installing Trojan Installing Trojan To ensure that you do not impede normal work within the software, install Trojan on a weekend or at night. To install Trojan: Select File > Trojan > Initial Load . The Trojan Disk Load Options window is displayed. Select an option: To load traditional plan information : Select Load Traditional Plan Disks . To load managed care plan information : Select Load Managed Care Plan Disk . Select the letter that corresponds to the drive into which the Trojan disks are inserted in the Enter Disk Drive to Load from drop-down list. Click OK . A message prompts you to insert the disk into the drive. Insert the disk and press Enter. After the information is copied, a message informs you to insert another disk. Insert the next disk and click Yes . When you have installed all of the disks, click No . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Insurance Tips and Tricks
topics.body: Insurance Tips and Tricks Insurance Tips and Tricks Use these insurance tips to increase your productivity: Insurance Narrative Tip: When checking patients out, keep Microsoft Word minimized with a list of common insurance narratives, and copy and paste them onto the insurance claim. Insurance Plan Tip: How do you remember if you've entered the benefit information? Use all Caps to indicate the insurance plan record has been completed with the appropriate benefits. Guarantor Tip: In a situation where one person has 2 insurance policies in his/her name, it will be easier to link the insurance on the Patient window if you type the second guarantor�s first name in ALL CAPS. Then, when you are linking at the patient level, you will know that the uppercase/lowercase name is G#1 and the all uppercase name is G#2. = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('InsTpsTrick.htm');return false;">Insurance Tips and Tricks Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Instant Recalls for Patients with Set Recall Procedures
topics.body: Scheduling Instant Recalls for Patients with Set Recall Procedures Scheduling Appointments > Using Instant Recall Scheduling Instant Recalls for Patients with Set Recall Procedures To schedule an instant recall appointment for a patient who has a set recall date: From the Patient List window, select a patient, and select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Instant Recall from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The next recall date is displayed in the Daily Appointments window. The date is based on the recall frequency entered on the Appts/Recall tab of the patient�s record and the date of the patient�s last recall procedure. Example : If the frequency of recall is six, the date is the first available slot after six months from the last time a transaction was posted for this procedure. If your practice has a user-defined recall in addition to the normal recall, select the type of recall to schedule. If you cannot remember which recall type is due, click Next to schedule the next recall that is due. Select an option: If the day and time are acceptable to the patient : Skip to number 4. To display the next available time slot : Click Next Slot or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. To display the previous available time slot : Click Prev Slot or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. To display the next day : Click Skip Day or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. To display the previous day with an available time slot : Click Prev Day or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. When you have found a suitable slot, click Make Appt on the toolbar to schedule the appointment. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Instant Recall
topics.body: Using Instant Recall Scheduling Appointments Using Instant Recall Use the instant recall feature to schedule an appointment for a patient's next recall exam. To use this feature, you must first set up recall codes and frequency. To view or set the default recall settings, select System > Change System Settings > Recall > Recall Setup/Codes . Use this to: Set the default interval between recall appointments Set the procedures that trigger recalls When a patient is overdue for a recall appointment, Recall Overdue is displayed in the title bar of the Patient List, Patient, and Patient appointments windows. The patient's name is displayed in blue in the Patient List window, and the contents of the Next Exam Due field in the patient�s Transactions window is displayed in red. Related Topics Scheduling Instant Recalls for Patients with Set Recall Procedures Scheduling Instant Recall When the Patient Has No Set Recall Procedure Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Insurance Plan Database TeamTalk Conditions
topics.body: Insurance Plan Database TeamTalk Conditions Using TeamTalk > Understanding TeamTalk Events > Understanding TeamTalk Conditions Insurance Plan Database TeamTalk Conditions The insurance record conditions that can restrict the event that displays a TeamTalk note are: Insurance Co : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more insurance companies. Mail To ID : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more insurance company mail to addresses. Default Employer : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more employers. Plan Type : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for either dental or medical insurance plans. Estimation Type : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more insurance plan estimation types. ECS Primary : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for ECS primary enabled records. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Integrating PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform
topics.body: Integrating PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform Using PracticeToGo > Installing PracticeToGo Integrating PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform Before you can integrate PracticeToGo with the Palm OS platform, you must integrate the Palm organizer with SoftDent by installing the SoftDent-Palm application on the Palm organizer. Once the SoftDent-Palm application is installed on the Palm organizer, you can transfer SoftDent data to the Palm organizer. You only need to do this once. To integrate PracticeToGo with the Palm OS platform: After you have installed the PC SoftDent and Palm Desktop software and connected the HotSync cradle, PC SoftDent will detect the Palm Desktop software on your computer the next time you open SoftDent. A message is displayed, asking if you want PracticeToGo to integrate itself with the Palm OS platform. Click Yes . The SoftDent-Palm application is placed in the Install directory of your Palm Desktop software, and the SoftDent conduit is registered with the HotSync Manager. A message is displayed, verifying that the SoftDent conduit was registered successfully. Click OK . At this point, the Palm OS platform is enabled as the PracticeToGo platform. (To verify this setting, click System > PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo Platform . The Use the Palm OS Platform option is selected, signifying that it is your integration choice for PracticeToGo.) Insert the Palm organizer into the HotSync cradle and push the HotSync button on the cradle. The SoftDent-Palm Application ( SoftDent.prc ) is installed on the Palm organizer. No SoftDent data has been transferred to the Palm organizer at this point. When the first synchronization is complete, press the HotSync button again. A message is displayed, stating "Opening SoftDent, please wait.� The HotSync Manager checks the list of registered conduits and activates the SoftDent conduit, and PracticeToGo transfers SoftDent data to the Palm organizer. Y ou do not have to perform the first three steps each time you synchronize PracticeToGo with the Palm OS platform. Steps 1-3 are for initial integration of PracticeToGo to the Palm OS platform. The PC SoftDent login window is displayed. Enter your SoftDent user name and password. If your user name in the PC SoftDent program has security rights to transfer data to the Palm organizer, data is transferred according to the preferences you have set in the PC SoftDent program ( System > PracticeToGo > User Preferences ). The SoftDent user name and password you enter is stored on your Palm organizer. See After the Integration for additional information about logging in to perform a synchronization. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Interface to Palm OS Platform Command
topics.body: Using the Interface to Palm OS Platform Command Using PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo System Settings Using the Interface to Palm OS Platform Command Selecting this command re-integrates PracticeToGo with the current data directory you are using. This command is useful if you have more than one data directory and want to be sure you are integrating PracticeToGo with the current data directory you are running. To access this command: Select System > PracticeToGo > Interface to Palm OS Platform . A message window is displayed, stating that the SoftDent conduit has been registered successfully. Click OK. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Integrating PracticeToGo with Microsoft Outlook
topics.body: Integrating PracticeToGo with Microsoft Outlook Using PracticeToGo > Installing PracticeToGo Integrating PracticeToGo with Microsoft Outlook Before you can begin transferring SoftDent data to Microsoft Outlook, you must enable PracticeToGo to use the Microsoft Outlook software option. To enable PracticeToGo to use the Microsoft Outlook software option: Select System > PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo Platform > Use Microsoft Outlook 2000 . Select from the following transfer options: To transfer SoftDent data to Microsoft Outlook automatically on SoftDent startup and exit: Select System > PracticeToGo > System Settings . The PracticeToGo System Settings window is displayed. Select from the following options, or select both options: Sync with Outlook when entering the program on this machine . Sync with Outlook when exiting the program on this machine . Click OK . To transfer SoftDent data to Microsoft Outlook manually: Select System > PracticeToGo > Synchronize with MicroSoft Outlook . topics.title: Introducing SoftDent
topics.body: Introducing SoftDent Getting Started Introducing SoftDent SoftDent is a dental practice management program written for the Microsoft Windows operating system. You can use SoftDent to manage your front office activities, claims and billing, scheduling and appointments, patient accounts, and treatment plans. The charting module is interactive, enabling easy charting of the patient�s hard and soft tissue, including past, present, and future, or recommended procedures. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Inside Work Mode
topics.body: Using Inside Work Mode Restorative Charting > Understanding Restorative Tab Modes Using Inside Work Mode When the Restorative tab is displayed in inside work mode , y ou can chart work performed by your practice. By default, inside work procedures and the Header bar are displayed in blue on the chart. To view the chart in inside work mode: Select Inside Work from the Mode drop-down list. Select the appropriate button from the Chart Mode toolbar, or press F3. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting InOffice Privacy
topics.body: Setting InOffice Privacy Tracking Patients in the Office > Understanding HIPAA and the InOffice Feature Setting InOffice Privacy You can use the InOffice HIPAA Privacy feature to ensure that sensitive patient information is not displayed in the InOffice window. When this feature is enabled, patient initials are displayed instead of full names. To set the HIPAA Privacy feature for the InOffice, select an option: To enable the HIPAA Privacy feature : Click Privacy Is Off in InOffice window. The feature is enabled, and the menu command changes to HIPAA Privacy On. The patient initials for each patient scheduled for an appointment are displayed in the Name (Last, First) column of the window. To disable the HIPAA Privacy feature : Click Privacy Is On in the InOffice window. The feature is disabled, and the menu command changes to HIPAA Privacy Off. The full name of each patient scheduled for an appointment is displayed in the Name (Last, First) column of the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Patient Visit Statistics Report Properties
topics.body: Setting Patient Visit Statistics Report Properties Tracking Patients in the Office > Generating Patient Visit Statistics Reports Setting Patient Visit Statistics Report Properties The Patient Visit Statistics Properties window enables you to set: Time constraints for patient check in How early a patient must check in before an appointment Average time a patient should wait before being seated in the operatory To set Patient Visit Statistics report properties: Select Patient Visit Statistics from the System menu. The Patient Visit Statistics window is displayed. Click Properties . The Patient Visit Statistics Properties window is displayed. Type the seated late and seated very late minutes. A patient is considered seated on time : If they change locations to the operatory before the time entered in the Seated "Late" after field. The default value is 10. A patient is considered seated late : If they change locations to the operatory after the time entered in the Seated "Late" after field. A patient is considered seated very late : If they change locations to the operatory after the time entered in the Seated "Very Late" after field. The default value is 15. The seated late and seated very late times : Are used to calculate the percentage of patients seated on time, seated late, or seated very late. These calculations are displayed in the Seated "On Time," Seated "Late," and Seated "Very Late" columns of the Patient Visit Statistics report. Type how early a patient should check in before an appointment in the Patient Check-in Time field. The default value is 0. If the patient is not checked in before the value entered in the field : They are considered late. The check in time is used to calculate the percentage of on time and late patients. These calculations are displayed in the On Time and Late columns of the Patient Visit Statistics report. To exclude late patients from the report, check Exclude late arrival patients from the computation . If this checkbox is unchecked : All patients (on time and late) are displayed in the report. Type the expected average time a patient should wait before being seated in the operatory in the Avg Wait field. The default value is 10. If the patient average wait time is exceeded : The text of the row containing the provider information is blue in the report. Type the expected percent of patients who should be seated on time in the operatory in the % Seated On Time field. The default value is 50. If the percentage of patients seated on time is not met : The text of the row containing the provider information is blue in the report. Click OK . Related Job Aid: G enerating the Patient Visit Statistics Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using InOffice Window Options
topics.body: Using InOffice Window Options Tracking Patients in the Office Using InOffice Window Options In the InOffice window, you can perform several functions. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: Clear InOffice : Removes all entries from the InOffice window. Patient appointments are not cleared from the Daily Appointments window. Fill InOffice: Displays the Fill InOffice window. Use this command to load appointments for a specific date. InOffice Add : Displays the Patient List window. Use this command to select a patient to add to the InOffice window. Monitor KIOSK Check-In: Displays the Monitor KIOSK Check-In window. Use this command to view a list of patients who check in using the KIOSK module. See Monitoring Checked-In Patients . Check In: Checks in the selected patient for a scheduled appointment. eForms > Review eForms: Click an appointment and select this command to display a list of eForms completed by the patient. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Send eForms Now: Click an appointment and select this command to send eForms to the patient now. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Resend eForms: Click an appointment and select this command to resend eForms to the patient. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Add/Remove eForms: Click an appointment and select this command to display a window where you can add or remove eForms templates for the patient. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Patient Settings: Click an appointment and select this command to display a window where you can edit a patient's eForms settings. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. HIPAA Acknowledgement : Displays the Contacts for window. Use this command to add, edit, or delete HIPAA specific contacts for the patient. Change Location : Notes a patient's movement from the waiting room to the operatory or from the operatory to the waiting room. The patient location is displayed in the LOC column. The text color of the row that contains the patient appointment information indicates the patient location. See Moving Patients to an Operatory and Returning Patients to the Waiting Room . Check Out: Begins the check-out process for the selected patient. Remove : Clears an entry from the InOffice window. The patient appointment is not removed from the Daily Appointments window. Activate : Activates a new patient that is scheduled for an appointment. See Adding Unscheduled New Patients . Patient : Displays the Patient window. Use this command to view the patient record. Account : Displays the Account window. Use this command to view account information for a patient. M edical History : Displays the Medical History window. Use this command to view the medical history of a patient. View Appointment Card : Displays the Appointment Card window. Use this command to view the procedures scheduled for an appointment. Transactions : Displays the patient Transactions window. Use this command to add and view patient transactions. Treatment Plan : Displays the patient Treatment Plan window. Use this command to view treatment plan procedures that have been entered for the patient. Electronic Router : Displays the ELECTRONIC ROUTER window. Use this command to view patient personal and financial information. You can also enter follow up appointments in this window. Appt. Book : Displays the

topics.body: Accessing the Cosmetic Imaging Module Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Images Accessing the Cosmetic Imaging Module The cosmetic imaging module enables you to perform realistic simulations of aesthetic and restorative treatment plans. This command is enabled if you have purchased this module. For more information, contact your sales representative. topics.body: Accessing the Dental Imaging Software in the InOffice Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Accessing the Dental Imaging Software in the InOffice You can access the CS Imaging or Trophy software while working in SoftDent. To access imaging software, install the software and purchase a SoftDent imaging license. To access imaging software in the InOffice window: Select List > InOffice . The InOffice window is displayed. Select Fill InOffice . To display patient images, select a patient appointment and select Dental Imaging . If you use Trophy software, select Trophy . topics.title: Using the Keypad Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Keypad Toolbar Periodontal Charting > Using Perio Tab Toolbars Using the Keypad Toolbar The Keypad toolbar commands e nable you to enter pocket depth and move to the next or previous tooth. You can also chart blood and suppuration from this toolbar. Click the icon for the description of the Keypad toolbar buttons. Click this button and select a tooth to chart blood . Click this button and select a tooth to chart suppuration . Click this button to select the previous pocket . Click this button to select the next pocket . Numerical Buttons Click these buttons to enter values . Click this button and the appropriate numerical button to chart numerical values between 10 and 15. For example, click this button and 2 to chart a pocket depth of 12 . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Function Key and Keystroke Shortcuts
topics.body: Using Function Key and Keystroke Shortcuts Getting Started Using Function Key and Keystroke Shortcuts In addition to menu items and command buttons, you can use function key or keystroke shortcuts to access most features. Function keys are: Located on the top row of your keyboard Numbered from F1 to F12 Active Function Keys: F1 Opens the online help F3 Opens the Account search window F4 Opens the Provider search window F5 Opens the Patient search window F8 Opens the ADA Code search window Keystroke shortcuts are: One or more keyboard keys that are used to accomplish a task Shortcuts for function keys and keystrokes are: Displayed to the right of menu items on most menus Generally displayed on the File, Scheduler, Experts, and System menus Available for all menus The following tables list keystroke shortcuts you might find useful: Administrative Functions Ctrl A List > Account Ctrl O List > Patient Ctrl Q List > Phone Center Alt F5 List > InOffice Ctrl D List > Provider Ctrl F List > Referring Dr. Ctrl E List > Employer Ctrl T List > School Ctrl I List > Insurance Co Ctrl P List > Insurance Plan Ctrl B List > Bluebook Ctrl Y List > Claim Ctrl C List > ADA Codes Ctrl H List > Pharmacy Ctrl L List > Labs Ctrl X List > Meds Ctrl M List > Memos Ctrl N List > Today's Memos Ctrl J List > Tickler From Patient List: Ctrl K Launch DIS G View Insurance Info From Scheduler Window Ctrl G Select a Different Book Ctrl H Return to Today With an Appt. Selected: C Cancel Appt T Place Cancelled Appt. in Tickler R Reschedule Cancelled appt. V View Appt. E Edit Appt. Ctrl F Confirm Appt. S Shorten Appt. G Lengthen Appt. K Check Out Patient Ctrl N Appt. for New Patient Ctrl 1 Open Patient's Chart Ctrl K Launch DIS With an Open Slot Selected: B Block Slot Ctrl L Add a Slot Note With a Block Selected: B Unblock Slot From Patient's Chart Ctrl 1 View Quad 1 Ctrl 2 View Quad 2 Ctrl 3 View Quad 3 Ctrl 4 View Quad 4 Ctrl 7 View Full Mouth Ctrl D Provider List Ctrl N Patient's Clinical Notes Ctrl X Launch DIS Ctrl F4 Exit the Chart Restorative Tab Ctrl K Chart Decay Ctrl A Chart Abscesses Ctrl C Chart Fractures Alt C M Chart Missing Ctrl T Tooth Detail F2 Primary TP Ctrl F2 Secondary TP F3 Inside Work F4 Initial Chart F5 Current Chart Perio Tab Ctrl M New Perio Chart Ctrl G Display Numeric Chart From Anywhere in SoftDent: Ctrl F7 Opens the Log Out Window Alt F4 Exits SoftDent Ctrl F10 Launches Microsoft Word CTRL-TAB Cycles You Through the Colored Tabs ALT-ENTER Switches You Between a Common Area ALT- ↓ Opens Any List Space Bar Checks or Unchecks a Box Tab Forward Shift Tab Backward ENTER OK ESC Closes a Window Need more information?
