topics.title: Running the Lab Case Report
topics.body: Running the Lab Case Report Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Labs > Running Lab Reports Running the Lab Case Report The Lab Case report generates a list of lab cases, displaying the patient�s name and ID, invoice number, lab name, and lab phone numbers. The report also shows the opened, expected, and returned dates for each case, as well as the case status and fee. The total dollars charged in lab fees is also calculated. Additional details : If the case is currently at the lab, it is considered an open/expected case. If the case has been returned from the lab, it is an open/returned case. If the case has been returned and all clinical procedures associated with the case have been performed, the case is considered complete. Sample Report To run the Lab Case report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Lab Case Report . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Lab Case Report Setup window is displayed. In the Patient IDs section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending patient identification numbers from a list. To include inactive patients in the report, click the Include Inactive Patients checkbox. In the "Expected Back" Date Range section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending expected back dates from a list. In the Providers section, click the drop-down arrow to select specific providers from a list, or leave 999 to report on all providers. In the Patient User Codes section, type a user code in the Include or Exclude fields to include or exclude patients with a specific user code. In the Lab Cases section, select from the following: To restrict the report to a specific lab: Click the drop-down arrow in the Lab ID field to select the lab identification number from a list. To include open lab cases or completed lab cases: Click the Include Open Lab Cases or the Include Completed Lab Cases checkboxes. To use color/shading in the report, select Include Color/Shading . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Lab Case Options
topics.body: Using Lab Case Options Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Lab Cases Using Lab Case Options To use open lab case options: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, or Treatment Planning tab in the Chart for window, click the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the lab case, right-click, and select an option: To display the case details : Select View . To edit a lab case : Select Edit . The details of the prescription are displayed in the Lab Case window. Make the changes, and click OK . To indicate the lab case has been returned : Select Mark as Returned . The Enter Lab Fee and Invoice # window is displayed. Type the information, and click OK . To indicate the lab case is complete : Select Mark as Completed . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Lab Case List and Lab Case Window Options
topics.body: Using Lab Case List and Lab Case Window Options Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Lab Cases Using Lab Case List and Lab Case Window Options From the Lab case List and Lab case windows, you can: Mark a lab case as returned Access the auto dialer Schedule an appointment for the patient associated with a lab case The Lab case List and Lab case window options are: Mark Returned : Mark a lab case as returned to your office. Capture Lab Cases : View a list of documents associated with the lab case and associate captured documents with the lab case. 0 - Auto Dialer : If you have configured a modem for the software, you can dial a specific number associated with the lab. If you do not have a modem, the lab phone numbers are displayed for your reference. Schedule Appointments : Schedule an appointment for the patient associated with the lab case. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Lab Case Prompting
topics.body: Setting Up Lab Case Prompting Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Lab Cases > Using Lab Case Prompting Setting Up Lab Case Prompting Before you can use lab case prompting for procedural codes that do not use this feature by default , y ou must set up lab case prompting for specific procedure codes. Using Lab Case Prompting provides the default list of procedural codes that prompt you to open a lab case when posted. To set up lab case prompting for a procedural code: Select an option: Select List > ADA Codes . In any window, press Ctrl + C. Click ADA List Window on the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. The ADA/Transaction Codes List window is displayed. Double-click the code to prompt a lab case from the list. The ADA/Transaction Codes window is displayed in EDIT mode. Select an option: To prompt the user to open a lab case when the code is posted: Select Open lab case from the Lab Prep Code drop-down list. When the code is posted, a message that asks you whether to open a lab case for the posted procedure is displayed. If you click Yes, a message asks you to create a reminder memo for the day the lab case is expected to be returned to the office. If you click Yes, the reminder is automatically displayed in the Today�s Memos window on the date the lab case is expected to be returned to your office. This information also is displayed in the Memo window. To prompt the user to close a lab case when the code is posted : Select Close lab case from the Lab Prep Code drop-down list. When the code is posted, the Enter Lab Fee and Invoice # window is displayed. Type the information in the appropriate fields. Example : Code 2750 (Crown) usually requires a lab case. In the ADA/Transaction Codes window for code 2750, select Open lab case from the Lab Prep Code drop-down list. Whenever code 2750 is posted, you are prompted to open a lab case for the patient. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Labs by ID Report
topics.body: Running the Labs by ID Report Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Labs > Running Lab Reports Running the Labs by ID Report The Labs by ID report generates a list of labs, sorted by ID. It displays the lab ID, name, mailing address, telephone/fax numbers, and contact person. Sample Report To run the Labs by ID report: Select Reports > Rx/Pharmacies/Labs > Labs By ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Lab by ID Report Setup window is displayed. In the Lab IDs section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending lab identification numbers from a list. To include inactive labs in the report, select Include Inactive Labs . To use color/shading in the report, select Include Color/Shading . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Labs List and Labs Window Options
topics.body: Using Labs List and Labs Window Options Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Labs Using Labs List and Labs Window Options From the Labs List or Labs window, you can perform several functions. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: 0 - Auto Dialer : The telephone numbers associated with a lab are displayed in the window. If you have a modem set up, select a number and click Dial . If you do not have a modem, this window is displayed for your reference only. Labels : Select Label to print a lab address information on a label. Select Envelope to print a lab address on an envelope. Contacts By Date : Add, edit, or delete any contact information that your office has for a lab. Contacts are organized by date. Contacts By Type : Add, edit, or delete any contact information that your office has for a lab. Contacts are organized by type. Contacts : Records any contact your office has with the highlighted lab. You can indicate the type of contact, whether the contact was successful, who attempted the contact, and so on. Lab Cases : View a list of lab cases associated with the selected lab, mark lab cases returned, scan or import lab cases, use the auto dialer, and schedule an appointment for a patient associated with a lab case. Multi-Case Document : Add several lab cases and associate them with a scanned document. View Documents for Lab : View a list of all documents associated with a lab. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Using Layout Mode Handling Images While Charting Using Layout Mode When the Clinical Imaging tab is displayed in layout mode, you can select a view in which to display images. You can do the following with an image that is displayed on the tab: Annotate Edit Remove Print E-mail Associate a tooth or region Clear all or a selected image To view the tab in layout mode, select Layout Mode on the Clinical Imaging toolbar . topics.title: Running the Label Comp/Uncomp Utility
topics.body: Running the Label Comp/Uncomp Utility Utilities > Running Customize Menu Utilities Running the Label Comp/Uncomp Utility Run t his utility to compress or uncompress the font on the account, birthday, custom, employer, insurance company, patient, pharmacy, insurance plan, practice, recall, referring doctor, reminder, school, and tickler labels. To compress or uncompress label fonts: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Customize > Label Comp/Uncomp Utility . The Label Compress/Uncompress window is displayed. Select the files from the list and select Compress or Uncompress . Click Run . A message informs you the process is complete. Click OK . Click Close . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports
topics.body: Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports You can print several types of labels, postcards, and directory reports from the Recall/Appt menu. The following options are available: Appointment Remind Labels: This option generates appointment reminder labels for patients scheduled during a specified period. These labels include the patient's name and mailing address, and are formatted for a dot-matrix printer. You can define how many lines per label. Tickler Files Labels: This option generates labels for patients in the tickler file. These labels include the patient's name and mailing address, and are formatted for a dot-matrix printer. You can define how many labels to run and how many lines per label. Appointment Reminder Postcards: This option generates appointment reminder postcards to remind your patients of the date and time of their next scheduled appointment. Tickler File Postcards: This option generates postcards for patients in the tickler file, prompting them to schedule their missed, canceled, or treatment plan appointment. Recall All Phone Dir: This option generates a phone directory for all patients due for recall, including or excluding patients by their user codes. The directory lists the patient's name, telephone numbers, last exam date, and the number of recall notices sent. Confirm Phone Dir: This option generates a phone directory of patients that need their appointment confirmed. The directory lists the patient's name, telephone numbers, the next appointment date, and user codes. Related Topics Printing Appointment Reminder Labels Printing Tickler File Labels Printing Laser Labels Printing Appointment Reminder Postcards Printing Tickler File Postcards Printing Laser Postcards Printing the Recall Phone Directory Printing the Confirmation Phone Directory Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Letter Expert Templates1
topics.body: Creating Letter Expert Templates1 Using Experts > Working with Letter Expert Templates Creating Letter Expert Templates To create a letter expert template: Select Expert > Letter Expert > Letter Expert - MS Word Document Templates . The Letter Expert Template window is displayed. Click Add . The Template Information window is displayed. Type the template name. Press the Tab key to move from one field to the next. Select the type of record from the Type of record this template is for drop-down list. You can create templates for a patient, account, insurance company, insurance plan, referring doctor, pharmacy, lab case, employer, or provider record. Type the template description. Click Add Word Template . The document and the SoftDent toolbar are displayed. If the SoftDent toolbar is not displayed, resize your toolbars until it is. Type your letter. You must manually add all punctuation, spacing, and text. Select an option: To add a merge field to the template : See Inserting Merge Fields into Templates . To add a list to the template : See Inserting Lists into Templates . To add an image to a template : See Inserting Images into Templates . After you are finished adding merge fields, lists, and images to your template, click Back to SoftDent . The Save As window is displayed. Type the file name. Templates must be saved in the Letters directory. Click Save . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Working with Letter Expert Templates
topics.body: Working with Letter Expert Templates Using Experts Working with Letter Expert Templates To use the letter expert to merge letters, a template must be created in Microsoft Word software. Merge fields : See Inserting Merge Fields into Templates for more information. Lists : See Inserting Lists into Templates for more information. Images : See Inserting Images into Templates for more information. topics.title: Tasks with Limited Access - Accounting
topics.body: Tasks with Limited Access - Accounting System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security > Tasks with Limited Access Accounting Tasks can be accessed from the following windows: Users by Rights: To access the Users By Rights window, select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Users by Rights . The Users By Rights window is displayed. Rights By User: To access the Rights by User window, select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Select the user name and click Edit . The Rights By User window is displayed. Security Settings - Security Overrides: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Security Overrides . The Security Overrides menu is displayed. The table describes each limited access task for the Accounting category. Category Task Description Accounting Modify Balance Switch Enables the user to access the System > Modify Balance Mismatch command. The user can also modify the balances in patient and account records. Accounting Modify Fee Schedules Enables the user to modify fee schedules on the Scheduler/Fees tab of ADA and transactions code records. Accounting Modify Payment Allocation Enables the user to change the collection assignee in the provider record. Accounting Set Up/Transfer Money to Budget Plan Enables the user to set up account budget plans. The user can also transfer all or a portion of the account balance to the plan. Accounting Set Up/Edit Goals and Scheduling Colors Enables the user to set up practice production goals and specify color groups and scheduling. Accounting Show Production Amounts in Scheduler Enables the user to view the production amounts from a scheduler window. Accounting Delete Accounts/Patients Enables the user to delete account or patient records and all of the associated transactions. Accounting Move a Patient to a New Account Enables the user to move a patient to a new or existing account. Accounting View RCVTOT Totals Enables the user to display the RCVTOT list. Accounting Run the Credit Card Settlement Report Enables the user to run the Credit Card Settlement report. Accounting Access Employee/Time Card Info Enables the user to have full access to Employee/Time Card information. Accounting Always Allow Transfer Credits from Payment Dialog Enables the user to transfer funds via the Account Payment window. Accounting Add, Modify or Reset Payment Plans Enables the user to add, modify, or reset payment plans. Accounting Delete Completed Claim Records Enables the user to delete completed claims. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tasks with Limited Access - Clinical
topics.body: Tasks with Limited Access - Clinical System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security > Tasks with Limited Access Clinical Tasks can be accessed from the following windows: Users by Rights: To access the Users By Rights window, select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Users by Rights . The Users By Rights window is displayed. Rights By User: To access the Rights by User window, select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Select the user name and click Edit . The Rights By User window is displayed. Security Settings - Security Overrides: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Security Overrides . The Security Overrides menu is displayed. The table describes each limited access task for the Clinical category. Category Task Description Clinical View Patient Chart Enables the user to access patient charts in the SoftDent charting module. Clinical Edit Initial Patient Chart Enables the user to edit initial patient charts in the SoftDent charting module. Clinical Delete Patient Chart Enables the user to delete patient charts in the SoftDent charting module. Clinical View Prescriptions Enables the user to access patient prescription information. Clinical Write Prescriptions Enables the user to write patient prescriptions. Clinical Refill Prescriptions Enables the user to refill patient prescriptions. Clinical Delete Prescriptions Enables the user to delete patient prescriptions. Clinical Print the Prescription Audit Trail Enables the user to view or print the audit trail file. Clinical Delete the Prescription Audit Trail Enables the user to purge unwanted prescription records from the audit trail. Clinical Delete Providers Enables the user to delete providers. topics.body: Linking ADA and CPT Codes Medical Cross-Coding Linking ADA and CPT Codes To link ADA and CPT codes: Select an option: Select List > ADA --> CPT . In any window, press Ctrl +V. Co . Enter the insurance company name or ID in the Find field or highlight the insurance company in the list and press Enter. The Insurance Company record is displayed. Click the arrow in the Trojan ID field. Select the type of Trojan company you want to link to the insurance company and click OK . A list of Trojan carriers is displayed. For a managed care plan : Click Update from Trojan on the toolbar to make sure the name, address, and phone number are updated from the Trojan database. Click OK . Need more information? topics.title: Logging a Contact for All Patients Assigned to an Account
topics.body: Logging a Contact for All Patients Assigned to an Account Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Handling Contact Expert Tasks > Accessing the Auto Dialer Logging a Contact for All Patients Assigned to an Account After contacting an account member, you can log contact information for all patients assigned to that account and remove them from the list pane. To log a contact for all patients assigned to an account: Select the report from the Reports menu. The Output Options window is displayed. Select Contact Expert and click OK . The report window is displayed. Select the report criteria and click OK . The report results are displayed in the Contact Expert window. Pending contacts for all patients in the account that meet the report or recent report criteria are displayed at the top of the list. Select a patient in the left pane. A list of pending and made contacts are displayed in the Previous Contacts section. The contact details of the patient are displayed in the Current Contact section. Select the form of contact from the Auto Dialer drop-down list. You can select a phone number or e-mail address. Relate the communication and do one of the following: To add an existing note to the contact, select the note from the Notes drop-down list. To add a new note, type the text in the Notes field. If communication was related on another day and you are recording the information, select the date of communication from the Received On drop-down list. To defer or mark a contact for follow up, select Defer Contact and select the date you are to follow up. To apply the communication to all patients in the account, select Apply to pending account contacts in expert list and click Contact And Remove . The contact is logged, and all patients in the account are removed from the left pane. The status of all Previous Contact entries for this Contact Expert list is changed to Made. Tip To log other pending contacts, repeat steps 4 through 7. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Employee and Time Card Login Overrides
topics.body: Setting Employee and Time Card Login Overrides Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Managing Employee Attendance > Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides Setting Employee and Time Card Login Overrides To set Employee and Time Card login overrides: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Security Overrides > Transaction/Accounting Settings . The System Settings - Override Settings window is displayed. Click the Accounting 2 tab. Select Access Employee/Time Card Info and do one of the following: To enable a user to access the information after entering a password, select Enter Password from the drop-down list and type a password. To enable a user to access the information after logging in as a user with the correct security settings, select User Login . Click OK and click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

Plan : Search for, view, add, edit, or delete insurance plan records. Bluebook : Search for and view insurance plan Bluebook entries. Allowance Table : Search for, add, and edit allowance tables. topics.title: Letter Expert Features
topics.body: Letter Expert Features What's New in Version 14.0 Letter Expert Features The letter expert has received enhancements in the following areas: Change to How Letter Expert Displays Time The letter expert now displays times (for example, the time of the next scheduled appointment) in the HH:MM am/pm format. Also, the extra space that displayed before a single-digit time has been removed. Change to How Letter Expert Displays Dates The letter expert now displays dates in a text month, DD, YYYY format (for example, October 28, 2010) instead of the MM/DD/YYYY format. See Using the Letter Expert for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Letter Expert Preferences
topics.body: Setting Letter Expert Preferences Using Experts Setting Letter Expert Preferences Use the letter expert preferences t o dictate how quickly data merges with the template. This enables you to merge all fields, lists, and images to templates without looking at the data, or to stop the process before each merge to make minor adjustments to the template or data. To set letter expert preferences: Select Expert > Letter Expert > Letter Expert Preferences . The Letter Expert Preferences window is displayed. Select an option: To pause the process for each merge field : Select Pause For Each Field . The merge process stops after data is merged with a field. You must click the Proceed button to continue. To briefly delay the process between each merge : Select Delay Between Fields . There is a brief delay after data is merged with a field during the merge process. This enables you to view the merged data during the process. To continue the process until completion : Select No Pause Or Delay . The merge process continues until it is complete. You are unable to view each merge during the process. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Letter Expert Startup Preferences
topics.body: Setting Letter Expert Startup Preferences Using Experts > Using the Letter Expert Setting Letter Expert Startup Preferences You can edit contact information when sending a record to the letter expert. This option is set in the Startup Preferences window and enables you to edit contact information in the document. To edit contact information when sending a record to the letter expert: Select System > Startup Preferences . Select Edit the new contact when sending a record to the Letter Expert . This option is selected by default. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Maintenance Menu Utilities
topics.body: Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Utilities Using Maintenance Menu Utilities It is not recommended that you run SoftDent Utilities on a weekly, monthly, or yearly basis. You typically run utilities in case of database corruption, most commonly expressed as SDWin DBM Error, File No. ### Error = # . There might be other instances where utilities are used to resolve or troubleshoot an issue. You should run utilities only when you are instructed to do so by a SoftDent technical support representative. topics.title: Posting a CareCredit Payment
topics.body: Posting a CareCredit Payment eServices > Using CareCredit Posting a CareCredit Payment This topic explains how to post CareCredit payments in the following windows: Transactions List window Payments window Related Topics Posting a CareCredit Payment in the Transactions List Window Posting a CareCredit Payment in the Payments Window Reprinting CareCredit Receipts Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a CareCredit Payment in the Payments Window
topics.body: Posting a CareCredit Payment in the Payments Window eServices > Using CareCredit > Posting a CareCredit Payment Posting a CareCredit Payment in the Payments Window You can accept a patient's CareCredit card for payment just as you would any other credit card. If the patient does not have his CareCredit card with him, you can look up the patient's CareCredit account information and post the payment. To post a CareCredit payment in the Payments window: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select Payments . The Payments window is displayed. C lick on the Payment bar. OR Select Personal in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Personal Payment window is displayed. Select the CareCredit payment code from the Code drop-down list . Press Tab , and type the payment amount. Use the drop-down list to select the provider if your office has multiple CareCredit providers. Use the drop-down list to select the CareCredit payment plan. If there are multiple patients listed at the bottom of the window, enter zero amounts in the Amount fields for all patients other than the patient who will be using CareCredit. Type the CareCredit payment amount in the Amount field for the patient, and leave the field selected. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. CareCredit payments cannot be distributed. Do one of the following: If the patient has his CareCredit card: Click Post . The CareCredit Purchase Transaction window is displayed. Type the account number and click OK . A Payment Approved message is displayed. If the patient does not have his CareCredit card: Click the Look Up Account Number if Card Not Present button. The CareCredit Account Lookup window is displayed. Do one of the following: --If the Social Security Number and zip code fields are populated, click OK . If the patient has an active account, the account number and amount available sections are populated. --If the Social Security Number and zip code fields are not populated, ask for the Social Security Number and zip code of the person who owns the CareCredit account, type the information in the fields, and click OK . If the cardholder has an active account, the account number and amount available sections are populated. Click Post . A payment approval message is displayed. Click OK. T he CareCredit Transaction Receipt window is displayed. Click Print, have the patient sign the sales draft, and give the patient the other copy. Click Close to return to the Transactions window. Related Topics Reprinting CareCredit Receipts Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a CareCredit Payment in the Transactions List Window
topics.body: Posting a CareCredit Payment in the Transactions List Window eServices > Using CareCredit > Posting a CareCredit Payment Posting a CareCredit Payment in the Transactions List Window You can accept a patient's CareCredit card for payment just as you would any other credit card. If the patient does not have his CareCredit card with him, you can look up the patient's CareCredit account information and post the payment. To post a CareCredit payment in the Transactions List window: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select CareCredit Integration > Enter a Payment on the Express bar, or click on the Payment bar. The CareCredit Transactions window is displayed. Type the amount. Use the drop-down list to select the provider if your office has multiple CareCredit providers. Use the drop-down list to select the CareCredit payment plan. Do one of the following: If the patient has his card: Click OK . The CareCredit Purchase Transaction window is displayed. Type the account number and click OK . A Payment Approved message is displayed. If the patient does not have his CareCredit card: Click the Look Up Account Number if Card Not Present button. The CareCredit Account Lookup window is displayed. Do one of the following: --If the Social Security Number and zip code fields are populated, click OK . If the patient has an active account, the account number and amount available sections are populated. --If the Social Security Number and zip code fields are not populated, ask for the Social Security Number and zip code of the person who owns the CareCredit account, type the information in the fields, and click OK . If the cardholder has an active account, the account number and amount available sections are populated. Click OK . A payment approval message is displayed. Click OK. T he CareCredit Transaction Receipt window is displayed. Click Print, have the patient sign the sales draft, and give the patient the other copy. Click Close . Related Topics Reprinting CareCredit Receipts Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a CareCredit Refund in the Payments Window
topics.body: Posting a CareCredit Refund in the Payments Window eServices > Using CareCredit > Posting a CareCredit Refund Posting a CareCredit Refund in the Payments Window To post a CareCredit refund in the Payments window: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Select Refunds in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Refund Credits window is displayed. Select the CareCredit refund code from the Code drop-down list. The CareCredit Transactions adjustment window is displayed. Press Tab , and type the refund amount. Use the drop-down list to select the provider if your office has multiple CareCredit providers. Use the drop-down list to select the CareCredit payment plan. If you do not know which payment plan was used for this transaction, return to the Transactions window, select the transaction, and select CareCredit Integration > View the Payment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar. If there are multiple patients listed at the bottom of the window, enter zero amounts in the Amount fields for all patients other than the patient who will be receiving the CareCredit refund. Type the CareCredit refund amount in the Amount field for the patient, and leave the field selected. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Do one of the following: If the patient has his CareCredit card: Click Post . The CareCredit Refund window is displayed. Type the patient's account number and click OK . A refund approval message is displayed. If the patient does not have his CareCredit card: Click the Look Up Account Number if Card Not Present button. The CareCredit Account Lookup window is displayed. Do one of the following: --If the Social Security Number and zip code fields are populated, click OK . If the patient has an active account, the account number and amount available sections are populated. --If the Social Security Number and zip code fields are not populated, ask for the Social Security Number and zip code of the person who owns the CareCredit account, type the information in the fields, and click OK . If the cardholder has an active account, the account number and amount available sections are populated. Click Post . A refund approval message is displayed. Click OK . The CareCredit Refund Transaction Receipt window is displayed. Click Print, and give the patient one of the copies. Related Topics Reprinting CareCredit Receipts Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Posting a CareCredit Refund in the Transactions List Window eServices > Using CareCredit > Posting a CareCredit Refund Posting a CareCredit Refund in the Transactions List Window To post a CareCredit refund in the Transactions List window: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select CareCredit Integration > Enter a Refund on the Express bar. The CareCredit Transactions window is displayed. OR Click on the Payment bar. The CareCredit Transactions window is displayed. Select the CareCredit refund code from the drop-down list. Type the refund amount. Use the drop-down list to select the provider if your office has multiple CareCredit providers. Use the drop-down list to select the CareCredit payment plan. If you do not know which payment plan was used for this transaction, return to the Transactions window, select the transaction, and select CareCredit Integration > View the Payment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar. Do one of the following: If the patient has his card: Click OK . The CareCredit Refund window is displayed. Type the account number and click OK . A refund approval message is displayed. If the patient does not have his CareCredit card: Click the Look Up Account Number if Card Not Present button. The CareCredit Account Lookup window is displayed. Do one of the following: --If the Social Security Number and zip code fields are populated, click OK . If the patient has an active account, the account number and amount available sections are populated. --If the Social Security Number and zip code fields are not populated, ask for the Social Security Number and zip code of the person who owns the CareCredit account, type the information in the fields, and click OK . If the cardholder has an active account, the account number and amount available sections are populated. Click OK . A refund approval message is displayed. Click OK. T he CareCredit Transaction Receipt window is displayed. Click Print, and give the patient one of the copies. Related Topics Reprinting CareCredit Receipts Need more information? topics.title: Managing Frequency Templates
topics.body: Managing Frequency Templates eServices > Using the eReminders Service Managing Frequency Templates You can edit, create, or delete eReminders frequency templates. To manage frequency templates, follow these steps: Select System > Modules > eReminders > eReminders Settings . The eReminders Settings window is displayed. You must have the Modify eReminders Settings security permission to access this window. In the Frequency Templates section, click Manage Templates . The eReminders Frequency Templates window is displayed. Select an option: To add a template: Click Add . In the Template Details section, select the number of reminders you want in the template, and select the reminder type and the reminder timeframe from the drop-down lists. Note: Same Day delivery time is available only for Text Messaging/SMS. Click Close . A message is displayed, asking if you want to save the changes you have made. Click Yes . You must select this template from the Frequency Templates drop-down list in order to use it. To edit a template: In the Frequency Templates section, select the frequency template you want to edit. The details for that template are displayed. Select the number of reminders you want, and edit the template details, using the drop-down lists. Click Close . A message is displayed, asking if you want to save the changes you have made. Click Yes . You must select this template from the Frequency Templates drop-down list in order to use it. To delete a template: In the Frequency Templates section, select the template and click Delete . Click Close . You cannot delete a frequency template that is currently selected in the eReminders Settings window. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Assigning Payer ID Numbers Manually
topics.body: Assigning Payer ID Numbers Manually eServices > Using the eClaims Service > Setting Up Insurance Companies Assigning Payer ID Numbers Manually To assign payer ID numbers manually: Select an option: In the eServices window : Click Insurance Setup . From the main menu : Select Setup > Insurance Co / Plans . The Payer Setup window is displayed, listing all insurance companies in the database that accept claims electronically. For companies that accept electronic claims, select a company name and click Assign ID . The Insurance Company List window is displayed. Double-click the appropriate company name, or click the name and click Select . The value in the Payer column for the company is changed to the payer�s identification number. If the company is listed multiple times, select each one. BlueCross BlueShield, Delta Dental, and Medicaid are listed by state. Be sure to select the correct state. For the insurance companies that accept only paper claims, do the following in the Payer Setup window for each company you want to set up: To assign an identification number to companies that accept BlueCross BlueShield paper claims : Select A PAPER CLAIM � BC/BS and click Assign ID . In the Insurance Company List window, double-click all BlueCross BlueShield companies that do not have a payer ID number in the Payer column. Click Exit . To assign an identification number to companies that accept Delta Dental paper claims : Select A PAPER CLAIM � DELTA DENTAL and click Assign ID . In the Insurance Company List window, double-click all Delta Dental companies that do not have a payer ID number in the Payer column. Click Exit . To assign an identification number to companies that accept Medicaid paper claims : Select A PAPER CLAIM � MEDICAID and click Assign ID . In the Insurance Company List window, double-click all Medicaid companies that do not have a payer ID number in the Payer column. Click Exit . Paper electronic claims are printed on standard ADA forms. Not all Medicaid offices accept claims on the standard ADA form. To assign an identification number to companies that accept commercial paper claims : Select A PAPER CLAIM � COMMERCIAL and click Assign ID . In the Insurance Company List window, double-click all remaining companies that do not have a payer ID number in the Payer column. Click Exit . All companies other than BlueCross BlueShield, Delta Dental, and Medicaid are considered commercial companies. To view the dental eClaims payer list or the medical eClaims payer list, click here . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Manually Adding a Bluebook Entry
topics.body: Manually Adding a Bluebook Entry Manually Adding a Bluebook Entry To manually add a bluebook entry for an insurance plan: Select List > Ins. Plan . The Insurance Plan list window is displayed. Double-click the insurance plan for which you want to add a bluebook entry. Click the Bluebook button to display a list of bluebook entries associated with the plan. Click Add . Type the code for which the entry is to be applied. Type the coverage information. Click OK. Training is available on this topic in the Carestream Dental Institute (CDI). Class Name: "Utilizing the Bluebook - Creating an Insurance Bluebook." Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Manually Installing CDT-2022 Codes
topics.body: Manually Installing CDT-2022 Codes Setting Up Insurance > Using CDT Codes Manually Installing CDT-2022 Codes The CDT-2022 code set includes revisions and modifications to existing ADA codes. For information about specific changes, contact the American Dental Association for the CDT 2022 manual. To manually install the CDT-2022 codes: Select System > Fees Maintenance > Install/Update CDT-2022 Procedure Codes . The Install CDT-2022 Procedure Codes window is displayed. Click OK . OR If you did not agree to the End-User Agreement at startup, the CDT 2022 End User License Agreement window is displayed. Read the license agreement carefully. If you agree to the terms, click I Agree to load the new codes. Related Topics Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting Resetting Insurance Plan CDT Versions Viewing ADA Code Descriptions Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Manually Posting an Insurance Credit Card Payment
topics.body: Manually Posting an Insurance Credit Card Payment Posting Payments > Posting Insurance Payments > Posting Credit Card Insurance Payments Manually Posting an Insurance Credit Card Payment To post an insurance credit card payment if the credit card is not available: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Transactions from the toolbar. In the Patient window : Select Transactions from the toolbar. The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Click Payments from the Options section of the Express bar. The Payments window is displayed. Select Insurance in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Insurance Payment window is displayed. The default insurance check payment code is displayed in the Code drop-down list. Select the insurance credit card payment code from the Code drop-down list, and press Tab. Type the payment amount, and press Tab. In the Claim No field, select the claim from the drop-down list, and click Post . The Update Bluebook? message is displayed. Click Yes . The Update Bluebook Entry window is displayed. Complete the bluebook information, and click OK . The SoftDent Software ePayments Charge Authorization window is displayed. In the Cardholder Last and First fields, type the cardholder's name. In the Acct # field, type the credit card number. In the Exp Date (MM/YY) field, type the expiration date. In the Zip field, type the cardholder's ZIP code. In the CVD Number field, type the credit card verification number. If you do not enter a CVD number, a message requesting the number is displayed. To ignore this message, click OK . Click Submit . A fter you receive an authorization number, add notes, if necessary. To print a receipt, click OK at the prompt. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Marking Provider Records Inactive
topics.body: Marking Provider Records Inactive Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Provider Information Marking Provider Records Inactive To mark a provider record inactive: When you display previously inactive provider records, Unknown is displayed in the Provider Type drop-down list. Select List > Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. Double-click the record. Select Inactive . Click OK and click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Managed Care Plans
topics.body: Handling Managed Care Plans Trojan Handling Managed Care Plans You can view Trojan managed care plans using: A plan list An administrative list Related Topics Managed Care Plan List Managed Care Plan Fees Managed Care Plan Administrative List Need more information? topics.title: Managed Care Plan Administrative List
topics.body: Managed Care Plan Administrative List Trojan > Handling Managed Care Plans Managed Care Plan Administrative List To view a managed care plan administrative list: Select List > Trojan Managed Care Plans > Administrative List . A list of Trojan managed care administrators is displayed. To see details of a particular administrator, enter the administrator�s name or ID in the find field and press Enter. The record is highlighted in the list window. Press Enter. The Administrator Information window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Managed Care Plan Fees
topics.body: Managed Care Plan Fees Trojan > Handling Managed Care Plans Managed Care Plan Fees To access managed care plan fees: From the Managed Care list window or the Plan record, click Fees on the toolbar. Enter the ADA code in the Find field or highlight the code in the list and press Enter. The Managed Care Fee Schedule window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Managed Care Plan List
topics.body: Managed Care Plan List Trojan > Handling Managed Care Plans Managed Care Plan List To view a managed care plan list: Select List > Trojan > Managed Care Plans > Plan List . The Managed Care Plan list window is displayed. To see details of a particular plan, enter the plan�s name in the Find field and press Enter. The record is highlighted in the list window. Press Enter. The Managed Care Plan window is displayed and contains the following tabs: General : Displays general information about the plan. Codes : Displays benefit information about the plan. Notes : Displays additional information about the plan. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Medical History Details
topics.body: Viewing Medical History Details Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Medical Histories Viewing Medical History Details To display medical history details on the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, or Treatment Planning tab in the Chart for window, click the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the medial history, right-click, and select View . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Medical Cross-Coding
topics.body: Medical Cross-Coding Medical Cross-Coding If your practice submits procedures to both dental and medical insurance , y ou can use the Medical Cross-Coding feature to link a standard ADA procedure with an equivalent medical (CPT) code. In addition, you can specify up to four diagnosis codes for each medical procedure code. To link dental and medical codes, you must do the following: Add medical insurance plans and associate the appropriate patient with a specific plan. For more information, see Setting Up Insurance . Add the CPT medical codes. For more information, see Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes . Add the diagnosis codes. For more information, see Adding Diagnosis Codes . Link the ADA and CPT codes. For more information, see Linking ADA and CPT Codes . Link the CPT and diagnosis codes. For more information, see Linking ADA/CPT and Diagnosis Codes . Related Topics Specifying a Medical Insurance Plan Adding CPT Codes Adding Diagnosis Codes Linking ADA and CPT Codes Linking ADA/CPT and Diagnosis Codes Submitting Claims with Linked Codes Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Medical Conditions
topics.body: Understanding Medical Conditions Handling Patient Medical History Understanding Medical Conditions A list of 50 medical conditions are displayed on the Conditions tab in the Medical History window. You can: Display the medical conditions most often attributed to your patients. Determine the order in which medical conditions are displayed. Set medical conditions to trigger a medical alert. Example : You can customize the tab to display conditions exactly as on a printed form. If a patient indicates a specific condition, a medical alert can also be set automatically. If you manually edit the Medical History field, the system will: Continue to add medical alerts as they become true No longer automatically remove alerts that are no longer true Related Topics Adding Medical Conditions Selecting Conditions to Be Displayed Changing the Display Order of Conditions Editing Conditions Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Medical History Window Options
topics.body: Understanding Medical History Window Options Handling Patient Medical History Understanding Medical History Window Options In the Medical History window, you can print the following associated with a patient: Currently displayed medical history Blank medical history Prescriptions history The commands available from the Options menu on the Express bar when using the Enhanced Workspace are: Print Current Medical History : Prints the selected medical history. Print Blank Medical History : Prints a blank medical history form. Print Prescriptions History : Displays the Prescriptions For window for the selected patient. Use this window to refill, delete, and prescribe medications. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Medical History Security
topics.body: Setting Medical History Security Handling Patient Medical History Setting Medical History Security Before using the software to track patient medical histories, you should enable your system security and ensure that only the appropriate office personnel have access to patient medical history information. To set medical history security: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Select a user from the list. The security settings for the selected user are displayed in the Rights by User window. Select an option: To enable a user to view patient medical histories : Select View Patient Medical History . To enable a user to update patient medical histories : Select Update Patient Medical History . Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Medicaid Plans
topics.body: Understanding Medicaid Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Understanding Medicaid Plans Medicaid plan info: Plans provide medical or dental coverage for patients on medical assistance or welfare. Providers negotiate contracts with these plans for reimbursements. Plans might vary. Medicaid plans are always a patient�s primary insurance. These plans are similar to capitation plans because you submit procedures at a reduced fee rather than write off adjustments. If most services in each procedural class require the same copayment: Select Cov�d. T ype the amount in the Copay field on the Coverage tab of the Insurance Plan window. If only a few procedures are covered within a procedural class: Deselect Cov�d. E nter the covered codes in the plan�s bluebook. Related Topics Estimating Insurance for Medicaid Plans Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Medicare Plans
topics.body: Understanding Medicare Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Understanding Medicare Plans Some Medicare plans pay: Participating providers based on the plan�s allowed amounts. A flat fee per procedure. A percentage of the plan�s allowed amount. Write off the difference between the plan�s allowed amount and the practice fee. This amount is displayed as an adjustment in the patient's Transactions window. Medicare plans: Typically provide medical coverage. Often cover oral surgery procedures. Medicare is always a patient�s primary insurance coverage, even if the patient is covered by another plan. To estimate insurance payments for flat fee Medicare plans: Set up accurate payment values in the bluebook for all procedures. If there is no bluebook entry for the flat fee PPO insurance plan, the percentages specified in the Coverage tab of the Insurance plan record are used. Tip If the plan pays something other than a percentage of your fees, set up bluebook entries. If the fees equal the allotted amount, you do not need to set up a bluebook entry. Related Topics Estimating Insurance for Flat Fee Medicare Plans Estimating Insurance for Percentage Medicare Plans Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Specifying a Medical Insurance Plan
topics.body: Specifying a Medical Insurance Plan Medical Cross-Coding Specifying a Medical Insurance Plan Before you submit medical insurance claims for procedures performed by your providers , y ou must associate an account and patient record with a specific medical plan. If your office submits both dental and medical insurance claims, you must associate the account and patient records with both a medical and dental insurance plan. For more information, see Setting Up Insurance . To specify a medical plan: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Account . The Account List Window is displayed. Double-click the account that requires editing from the list. Click Account List Window on the Power Bar : The Account List Window is displayed. Double-click the account that requires editing from the list. In the Patient List or Patient window : Select Other Patient Management > View Account from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the InOffice window : Select Account from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In any window : Press Ctrl + A. The Account List Window is displayed. Double-click the account that requires editing from the list. Click OK . The account record is displayed in SCAN mode. Click Edit and select the medical insurance plan from the Medical drop-down list. Click OK to save the medical plan information in the account record. Select Patient from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Account Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. The patient record is displayed in the Patient window. To establish the relationship between the insured patient and the account guarantor for medical insurance claims, click the Insur. tab and select the account guarantor�s first name from the Primary drop-down list in the Medical Insurance section of the tab. Select the relationship between the patient and the account guarantor from the Guarantor 1 drop-down list in the Patient�s Relationship to section of the tab. Click OK . Click Close twice. Related Topics Power Bar Buttons Setting Up Insurance Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering a Memo Command in Microsoft Outlook
topics.body: Entering a Memo Command in Microsoft Outlook Using PracticeToGo > Sending Data Back to SoftDent > SoftDent Commands and Microsoft Outlook Entering a Memo Command in Microsoft Outlook The Memo command enables you to transfer memos to the PC SoftDent software. To view the available Memo command, click = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('MemoComPTG.htm');return false;">here . When you transfer a memo from the Microsoft Outlook software to the PC SoftDent software, you have the option of setting a frequency parameter for the memo. The frequency parameter determines whether the memo will display in the PC SoftDent software only once or at intervals. Example: If you place a daily frequency parameter on the SD Memo command, the memo you transfer will display daily in the PC SoftDent software. To view a list of the memo frequency parameters, click here . N�No Frequency D�Daily Frequency W�Weekly Frequency M�Monthly Frequency To transfer information to the PC SoftDent software using the Memo command in the Microsoft Outlook software: Open the Tasks list. Open the desired memo and place your cursor inside the memo. Type the command character tag, and then type the rest of the command and the memo frequency parameter. Example: Type \SDMD to update the memo in the SoftDent software every day. Because the command is case sensitive, it must be entered in all capital letters. The command must also be entered without spacing between the characters. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering a Memo Command on the Palm Organizer
topics.body: Entering a Memo Command on the Palm Organizer Using PracticeToGo > Sending Data Back to SoftDent > SoftDent Commands and the Palm Organizer Entering a Memo Command on the Palm Organizer The Memo command enables you to transfer memos to the PC SoftDent software. To view the available Memo command, click = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('MemoComPTG.htm');return false;">here . When you transfer a memo from the Palm organizer to the PC SoftDent software, you have the option of setting a frequency parameter for the memo. The frequency parameter determines whether the memo will display in the PC SoftDent software only once or at intervals. Example: If you place a daily frequency parameter on the SD Memo command, the memo you transfer will display daily in the PC SoftDent software. To view a list of the memo frequency parameters, click here . N�No Frequency D�Daily Frequency W�Weekly Frequency M�Monthly Frequency To transfer information to the PC SoftDent software using the Memo command: On the Palm home screen, tap the SoftDent icon, and then tap Memos on the SoftDent-Palm application menu to enter the Memo Pad. Tap the desired memo on the SD Memo list to open the memo. Tap inside the memo, at the beginning of the first text line, so the cursor displays there. Tap the 123 dot in the graffiti writing area underneath the screen. A Number Keyboard is displayed. Begin by entering the \ character (unless you have selected something else as your command character in the Command Tag field in the PracticeToGo User Preferences window in the PC SoftDent software). Next, tap ABC at the bottom of the keyboard window to open the Letter Keyboard. Enter the rest of the SD Memo Command and a frequency parameter. Example: Enter \SDMD to update the memo in the SoftDent software every day. The command is case sensitive, so it must be entered in all capital letters as shown. The command must also be entered without spacing between the characters. For online tips about ways to reach the onscreen Keyboard, tap the tips icon (the lowercase i) in the upper-right corner of the Keyboard window. When you are finished, tap Done to exit the keyboard window and return to the SD Memo. Tap Done again to return to the SD Memo list. Upon the next synchronization, the memo will be transferred to the PC SoftDent software. Memos added to the PC SoftDent software from the Palm organizer display in the Memos list window in blue. Once the information is transferred to the PC SoftDent software, the SD command you entered on the Palm organizer is removed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Memo Command
topics.body: Memo Command Memo Command The command is case sensitive and must be entered on the Palm organizer or in the Microsoft Outlook software in all capital letters. Memo Command Command Function SD Memo Command (from the Memo Pad Application on the Palm Organizer or the Microsoft Outlook Software Tasks Category) PC SoftDent Software Output To enter a memo \SDM[FREQ] [DESC] (Enter the command \SDM and a description about the memo on the appointment line. An optional frequency parameter can also be entered. Frequency parameters include: N: No Frequency D: Daily Frequency W: Weekly Frequency M: Monthly Frequency) Places a memo in the memo list for the date specified; memo content includes the memo description and information you entered on the Palm organizer. The memo displays in blue. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Merging Contact information to Letter Expert Templates
topics.body: Merging Contact information to Letter Expert Templates Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Merging Contact Letters Merging Contact Information to Letter Expert Templates To merge a contact letter: In the Contact Expert window, select a record from the list in the left pane. Select Send Entry to Letter Expert from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Letter Expert Merge window is displayed. Select the template to which you want to merge the contact from the list. To batch the letter, select Send To Batch . Skip to step 7. To merge batched letters, select Letter Expert > Merge All Batched Letters . Click OK . The Start Merge Wizard window is displayed. Follow the steps in Merging Letters . After the merge process is complete or the letter is batched, a message that asks you whether to remove the list is displayed. Select an option: To remove the contact from the list : Click Yes . To keep the contact in the list : Click No . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Merging Contact Letters

topics.title: Managing Lab Cases
topics.body: Managing Lab Cases Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases Handling Lab Cases You can: Track statistics for each lab your practice uses Avoid scheduling and resource problems caused by unreturned lab cases In addition to storing lab information, you are: Prompted to add a lab case when appropriate Reminded when an existing lab case has not been returned By entering and maintaining lab case data, you: Do not have to worry about scheduling a patient for an appointment before his or her lab work is done Can identify any potential problems with labs due to unacceptable turnaround times or high fees Can maintain and track fees for associates Related Topics Adding Lab Cases Tracking a Lab Case Status Viewing a Lab Case Status Marking Cases as Returned or Completed Using Lab Case Prompting Scheduling Appointments with Lab Cases Using Lab Case List and Lab Case Window Options Need more information? topics.title: Selecting Merge List Records
topics.body: Selecting Merge List Records Using Experts > Merging Letter Expert Documents > Merging Letters Selecting Merge List Records To select merge list records: Follow the instructions in Starting the Merge Process . If report results need to be merged into the document, the Merge List Status window is displayed. Click Select Record . The SoftDent List Creation window is displayed. Select an option: To select a list of records : Click the first record, hold down the Shift key, and click the last record. To select random records : Click the first record, hold down the Ctrl key, and click additional records. To select all records : Click Select All in the Selection Options section. Click OK . After the merge process is complete, select an option: To print the letters : Click the Print icon located on the Microsoft Word toolbar. To save the letters : Select Save As from the File menu. Type the file name in the appropriate field, and click Save . Click Back to SoftDent in the Microsoft Word toolbar to return to the application. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Merging Letters
topics.body: Merging Letters Using Experts > Merging Letter Expert Documents Merging Letters After you have started the merge process , t he document opens in Template Creation mode, and the SoftDent Merge Wizard is displayed. For more information, see Starting the Merge Process . To merge letters: Click Start Merge . When you need to select a record to merge with the template, the SoftDent Record Selection window is displayed. Select a record from the list. The record data replaces the merge field. Click OK . The Merge Field Status window is displayed. If No Pause Or Delay is the default merge speed : You cannot change any data. The merge continues until it is complete. If Pause For Each Field is the default merge speed : The process is paused for each merge field. You can change the data displayed in the Replacement Text field. If Delay Between Fields is the default speed : The process briefly delays between each merge. You can change the data displayed in the Replacement Text field. To create a template, see Setting Letter Expert Preferences . After the merge process is complete, select an option: To print the letters : Click the Print icon on the Microsoft Word toolbar. To save the letters : Select Save As from the File menu. Type the file name in the appropriate field, and click Save . Click Back to SoftDent on the Microsoft Word toolbar to return to the application. If the merge template contains a merge list, see Selecting Merge List Records . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Merging Letters for the Contact List
topics.body: Merging Letters for the Contact List Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Merging Contact Letters Merging Letters for the Contact List To merge a letter for the entire contact list: In the Contact Expert window, select Send List to Letter Expert from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Letter Expert Template Selection window is displayed. Select the template to which you want to merge the contact list. Click OK . The Letter Expert Merge window is displayed. Select the set of letters you want to merge from the list. Select an option: To merge and print the letters : Click Print . The Merge Wizard is displayed. The Start Merge Wizard window is displayed. To batch the letters to merge and print later : Click Save . Follow the steps in Merging Letters . After the merge process is complete, a message that asks you whether to remove the list is displayed. Select an option: To remove the contacts sent to the letter expert from the list : Click Yes . To keep the contacts in the list : Click No . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Merging Letter Expert Documents Using Experts Merging Letter Expert Documents To use the Merge features in the letter expert, a template must be created in the Microsoft Word software. Templates are composed of letter text and data fields, lists, or images that use data in merge letters. Related Topics Starting the Merge Process Merging Letters Need more information? topics.title: Managing Ortho Cases
topics.body: Managing Ortho Cases Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans Managing Ortho Cases In addition to setting up regular patient budget plans, you can also set up patient budget plans specifically for orthodontic procedures using the Ortho Insurance Tracking feature. You can resubmit ortho claims on a regular basis without marking them complete until the final payment has been received. If plans require payment coupons or continuous care forms , y ou are prompted on the days that these forms should be sent to the insurance company. Because insurance plans process ortho claims differently, you must specify how often to resubmit claims or continuous care forms. To use Ortho Case Tracking: The Orthodontics Maximum field in the Insurance Plan window must be set to something other than 0.00 Ortho procedures must be selected as covered on the Coverage tab When using the Ortho Insurance Tracking feature: The amount for the ortho code is subtracted from the production amount and added as a credit to the patient account The account balance and provider production amounts are increased monthly depending upon the monthly payment amount set up in the ortho budget plan Related Topics Setting Up Ortho Codes Setting Insurance Plan Defaults for Plans That Require Claims Setting Insurance Plan Defaults for Plans That Require Coupon Books or Continuous Care Forms Submitting an Ortho Case Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up a Minimum Payment Amount for Accounts
topics.body: Setting Up a Minimum Payment Amount for Accounts Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Setting Up a Minimum Payment Amount for Accounts As an alternative to creating a patient budget plan y ou can define a minimum payment amount for the account. When the statement is printed, the minimum monthly payment amount is billed. When setting up a minimum payment amount, the following is true: If the account balance is less than the minimum amount : The account balance is billed. If no payment is made : The amount due on the next billing statement is twice the minimum payment amount. The amount due increases by the minimum payment amount each cycle until a payment is received. The minimum payment amount is due : For each billing cycle or billing statement as long as the Min Payment field has a dollar amount and a personal payment for at least that amount has been posted within 45 days of the date the last statement was sent. When you no longer want the account to pay a minimum amount, you must disable this option. To set up a minimum payment amount for an account: Select an option: Select List > Account . Click Account List Window on the Power bar. In any window, press Ctrl + A. The Account List window is displayed in FIND mode. Click anywhere in the list area or press Enter to switch to SCAN mode. Type the account name in the Find field and press Enter. Double-click the account. Click OK . Type a minimum payment amount in the Min Payment field. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating the Missed Appointments Report
topics.body: Generating the Missed Appointments Report Generating the Missed Appointments Report Generate the Missed Appointments report to see a list of patients who missed appointments scheduled within a date range. When you generate the report, you can send missed appointments to the Tickler file and remove Scheduler colors and blocked slots. After you generate the report, old appointments are displayed in the History Appointments window. To generate the Missed Appointments report: Select Reports > Missed Appointments . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an option and click OK . The Missed Appointment Report Setup window is displayed. When Enter Missed Appointments into Tickler is not selected, missed appointment information is deleted. To add missed appointments to the Tickler file, select Enter Missed Appointments into Tickler . To remove Scheduler colors and blocked slots, select Remove Colors from Scheduler . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Fix Balance MisMatch Utility
topics.body: Running the Fix Balance MisMatch Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Fix Balance MisMatch Utility Run this utility to locate and correct patient and account balance mismatches. To correct patient and account balance mismatches: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Fix Balance Mismatch . After the process is complete, a message informs you of the number of patient mismatches found and corrected. Click OK . A message informs you of the number of account mismatches found and corrected. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Marking a Case Complete
topics.body: Marking a Case Complete Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Lab Cases > Marking Cases as Returned or Completed Marking a Case Complete Mark a lab case complete after it has been returned from the lab and all clinical procedures associated with the case have been completed. To mark a case complete: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Clinical Management from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A submenu is displayed. Select View Lab Cases . In the Patient window : Select Clinical Management from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A submenu is displayed. Select View Lab Cases . In the patient chart: Click the Labs tab, or click Display the Patient's Lab Cases on the Common toolbar. In the Labs List window : Select the lab and select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Labs window : Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the patient Transactions window : Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Electronic Router window : Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Lab case List window is displayed. Select the lab case and click Edit . The Lab case window is displayed. Select Complete and click OK . The lab case status is saved. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

Select View Lab Cases from the menu. In the patient chart: Click the Labs tab, or click Display the Patient's Lab Cases on the Common toolbar. In the Labs List window : Select the lab and select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Labs window : Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the patient Transactions window : Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Type the invoice number and lab fee in the appropriate fields. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!
