topics.title: Handling Claims With Multiple Diagnosis Codes
topics.body: Handling Claims With Multiple Diagnosis Codes Submitting and Tracking Claims > Submitting Medical Claims Handling Claims with Multiple Diagnosis Codes If you enter more than one medical procedure on the same claim and use more than four unique diagnosis codes for all procedures , t he Select Transactions For Claim window is displayed. Medical claim forms can only accommodate four unique diagnosis codes for all procedures on the claim. If you require more than four codes, you can use the Select Transactions For Claim window to change diagnosis codes or to submit the medical procedures on different claims. The four diagnosis codes are assigned numbers 1�4. The number is attached to each procedure associated with that diagnosis. The top of the Select Transactions For Claim window displays all submitted procedures and their diagnosis codes. The On Claim column displays the diagnosis code numbers (1�4) for each procedure on the claim. An asterisk is displayed for codes that will not fit on the claim form. To handle claims with multiple diagnosis codes: To change the diagnosis codes for a transaction, click Edit Diags on the toolbar and edit the Diagnosis Codes fields. If you need to use more than four diagnosis codes, submit the medical procedures on different claims. Highlight a procedure and click No (submit later) on the toolbar. The Submit checkbox for that procedure is no longer marked. In the patient�s Transactions window, that procedure is labeled Not Sent. Repeat this step until only those procedures using a total of four or fewer diagnosis codes are marked for submission. Click OK . The Claim record window is displayed for the procedures marked for submission. For more information, see Submitting Dental Claims . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Main Charting Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Main Charting Toolbar Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the Main Charting Toolbar Click the icon for a description of the Main Charting toolbar buttons, located at the top of the Charting for window. Displays the software online Help system. Closes the charting module to return to the previously opened window in the software. Saves changes made to the open chart. Prints a patient chart. See Printing Charts . Select Full Mouth to display all four quadrants. Select Quad 1 to display the first quadrant. Select Quad 2 to display the second quadrant. Select Quad 3 to display the third quadrant. Select Quad 4 to display the fourth quadrant. You can also access the quadrants by using Ctrl + the quadrant number or from the View menu. Maximizes the chart and hides the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Displays the Clinical History tab in the Clinical Detail Tab Container section. Displays the Clinical History tab in the Clinical Detail Tab Container section. Maximizes the Clinical History tab in the Clinical Detail Tab Container section. Maximizes the Clinical Images tab in the Clinical Detail Tab Container section. Displays the Provider List window. In this window, you can select the provider for the chart. You can also access this command from the Chart and Perio menus. Displays the Patient Clinical Notes List window. In this window, you can associate clinical notes with a chart. Accesses the CS Imaging software, where you can manipulate and re-save previously acquired images using the CS Imaging software tools. New images cannot be acquired using this button. This command is enabled only if you are using SoftDent Cloud. Accesses the CS Imaging software to acquire patient images. This command is enabled only if you are using SoftDent Cloud. Accesses the CS Imaging software. This command is enabled only if you use CS Imaging software. Accesses the CS Imaging software to capture a panoramic image. This command is enabled only if you use CS Imaging software, version 6 or higher, and have installed the 8000, 9000, or 9300 panoramic unit. Accesses the 3D Acquisition window of the 9000 3D system, enabling you to capture a 3D volume. This command is enabled only if you use CS Imaging software, version 6 or higher, and have installed the 9000, 9300, or 9500 3D system. Accesses the CS Imaging software to capture a cephalometric image. This command is enabled only if you use CS Imaging software, version 6 or higher, and have installed the 8000c, 9000c, or 9300c panoramic and cephalometric unit. Accesses the CS Imaging software to capture an RVG image. This command is enabled only if you use CS Imaging software, version 6 or higher, and have installed the RVG 6000 digital radiography system. Accesses the CS Imaging software to capture an intraoral image. This command is enabled only if you use CS Imaging software, version 6 or higher, and have installed the 1000, 1200, 1500, or 1600 intraoral video camera. Accesses the CS Imaging software to acquire a CR image. This command is enabled only if you use CS Imaging software, version 6 or higher, and have installed the Carestream Dental CR. Accesses the CS Imaging software to capture an intraoral 3D model using the CS 3500, CS 3600, or CS 3700. This command is enabled only if you use CS Imaging software and have installed the CS 3500, CS 3600, or CS 3700. Accesses the CS Imaging software to scan a 3D object using the CS 9000 or CS 9300. This command is enabled only if you use CS Imaging software

topics.title: Selecting Main Menu Items
topics.body: Selecting Main Menu Items Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Main Menu Bar Selecting Main Menu Items When you click a Main Menu bar heading, a drop-down list is displayed. The list contains the features and functions that can be accessed from the menu heading. Menu items can be selected using the mouse or keyboard. If a menu item is displayed in grey text, the option is not available or you do not have access to the feature. To select a Main Menu item, select an option: Place the mouse pointer on the menu heading, left-click, scroll down the menu, and left-click to select the menu item. Some menu items are assigned unrelated keystrokes. For example, to select the Missed Appointments report from the Reports menu, press Alt + R and then press 2. Hold down the Alt key and press the key of the underlined letter in the menu heading. Press the key of the underlined letter of the menu item. Example : To select Accounting from the Reports menu, press Alt + R, and then press A. If one or more menus or submenus are displayed from the Main Menu heading, press Esc to close one menu at a time. The triangle symbol next to a menu item indicates there is a submenu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

Select Schedule Appointment from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

The Experts menu commands are: Office Expert : Run experts, set up expert options, take action for an expert, clear experts, and dictate when to run the Office Expert. Letter Expert : Add, edit, or delete Letter Expert templates. Contact Expert : Load a contact list or set the list as an Office Expert item. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Using the Mobility Toolbar Restorative Charting > Using Restorative Tab Toolbars Using the Mobility Toolbar The Mobility toolbar commands enable you to chart the mobility value of teeth. To add a button to the toolbar, see Customizing Toolbars . Click the icon for the description of the Mobility toolbar buttons. Click this button and select a tooth to assign a mobility value of 0 . Click this button and select a tooth to assign a mobility value of 1 . Click this button and select a tooth to assign a mobility value of 2 . Click this button and select a tooth to assign a mobility value of 3 . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Mode Menu
topics.body: Using the Mode Menu Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the Mode Menu The commands available from the Mode menu are: Treatment Plan Primary : This command is enabled when the Restorative or Treatment Planning tabs are the active windows. Select this command to display the chart in treatment plan primary mode. Primary treatment plan items are displayed in red on the chart. Treatment Plan Secondary : This command is enabled when the Restorative or Treatment Planning tabs are the active windows. Select this command to display the chart in treatment plan secondary mode. By default, secondary treatment plan items are displayed in magenta on the chart. Treatment Plan Tertiary: This command is enabled when the Restorative or Treatment Planning tabs are the active windows. Select this command to display the chart in treatment plan tertiary mode. Tertiary treatment plan items are displayed in aqua on the chart. Complete Primary Treatment Plan Items : This command is enabled when the Restorative or Treatment Planning tabs are the active windows. Select this command to indicate that all primary treatment plan items are complete. You are prompted to post the items. Complete Secondary Treatment Plan Items : This command is enabled when the Restorative or Treatment Planning tabs are the active windows. Select this command to indicate that all secondary treatment plan items are complete. You are prompted to post the items. Complete Tertiary Treatment Plan Items: This command is enabled when the Restorative or Treatment Planning tabs are the active windows. Select this command to indicate that all tertiary treatment plan items are complete. You are prompted to post the items. Inside Work : This command is enabled when the Restorative tab is the active window. Select this command to chart work performed by your practice. By default, inside work procedures are displayed in blue on the chart. Outside Work : This command is enabled when the Restorative tab is the active window. Select this command to chart work performed outside of your practice. By default, outside work procedures are displayed in green on the chart. Use this mode with the initial chart or when a patient returns from a referral to a specialist. Initial Chart : This command is enabled when the Restorative tab is the active window. Select this command to display the initial chart associated with a patient. Current Chart : This command is enabled when the Restorative tab is the active window. Select this command to display the most up-to-date charting information. You can chart all ADA procedures and tooth conditions in the current chart. Start Florida Probe for : This command is enabled if Florida Probe software is installed. Select this command to access the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Using Case Presentation Module Presentation Options Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Case Presentation Module Presentations Using Case Presentation Module Presentation Options To use case presentation module presentation options: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, or Treatment Planning tab in the Chart for window, click the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. topics.title: Monitoring Checked-In Patients
topics.body: Monitoring Checked-In Patients eServices > Using the eForms Module Monitoring Checked-In Patients You can view a list of the patients who check in via the eForms-Enabled Kiosk by using the Monitor KIOSK Check-In window. The window displays the patient name, appointment time, operatory, and, if applicable, the reason the patient needs front-desk assistance. Depending on how you have configured the window, you can display all patients who have checked in using the Kiosk or only those patients who need a HIPAA acknowledgement or who have a check-in TeamTalk note. The office staff must complete the check-in process for all patients displayed in the Monitor KIOSK Check-In window. To view the Monitor KIOSK Check-In window: Select Monitor KIOSK Check-In on the Options menu while in the Scheduler or InOffice windows. Tip The window is initially positioned in the upper-right corner of the window, but you can drag it anywhere on the screen. To configure the Monitor KIOSK Check-In window: In the Monitor KIOSK Check-In window, click Settings and select one of the following: Monitor all patients: Displays all patients who have checked in using the eForms-Enabled Kiosk module. When this option is selected, the office staff must complete the check-in process for each patient by clicking Complete KIOSK Check-In . Monitor patients needing assistance: Displays only those patients who must sign a HIPAA acknowledgement or who have a check-in TeamTalk note. When this option is selected, the office staff must complete the check-in process for each patient needing assistance by clicking Complete KIOSK Check-In . Patients who do not need assistance are completely checked in, and no further action is required. The following reasons can display: HIPAA�The patient must sign a HIPAA acknowledgement. TT Notes�The patient has TeamTalk notes. HIPAA, TT Notes�The patient must sign a HIPAA acknowledgement and has TeamTalk notes. To complete the check-in process for a patient: Select a patient in the list and click Complete KIOSK Check-In , or double-click the patient's name. The patient is checked in for his appointment, exactly as if you performed the check-in from the InOffice or Scheduler window. If the patient has TeamTalk notes or a HIPAA Acknowledgement reminder, message windows informing you of this situation are displayed, and you would proceed as you typically would to resolve the issue. For additional information about the eForms-Enabled Kiosk, see the eForms online help. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Monthly Reports
topics.body: Monthly Reports Handling Reports > Frequently Used Reports Monthly Reports The following table lists the recommended end-of-month reports that you should run. Although recommended as monthly reports, these can be run at any time. These reports are not required to �close out� the month. Name and Path Description Accountant's Earnings Report Reports > Accounting > Accountant�s Earnings � Shows a breakdown of all transaction codes posted. � Calculates net collections, net productions, net charges, tax, and expected income. Account Aging Reports > Accounting > Account Aging � Shows ending receivables totals. � Identifies overdue accounts. � Lists all accounts that have an aged balance over a specified amount. � List can be sent to the Contact Expert. Insurance Payment Distribution Reports > Accounting > Insurance Payment Distribution � Lists all patients covered by insurance payments for the specified date range. Receivables Breakdown Reports > Practice Management > Receivables Reports > Breakdown � Lists monthly or yearly breakdown of receivables totals for the practice. Transactions for a Period Reports > Accounting > Trans for a Period � Details all transactions posted during a specified period and summarizes transactions by provider. Monthly Register Reports > Accounting > Registers > Monthly � Summarizes the number of patients seen, number of new patients, production, and collection amounts for the month. To view a printable job aid that lists the reports you should run at the end of the month, see Monthly Reports . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Motivated Recall
topics.body: Using Motivated Recall Recalling Patients > Running Recall Reports Using Motivated Recall The Motivated Recall system tracks the number of recall notices sent to patients and enables you to send a different postcard message for first, second, and third recall notices. You can make the wording stronger for each successive postcard to motivate patients to schedule their recall appointments. For information on editing postcard messages, see Editing Recall Postcards . To use the Motivated Recall system: Select an option: To generate a list of patients who are due for normal recall : Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Last Exam . To generate a list of patients who are due for user-defined recall : Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Perio . If you change the name of your Normal Recall or User-Defined Recall system, the name of the report changes. For example, if the name of your User-Defined Recall system is Ortho, select Reports > Recall/Appt > Ortho . The Report Format window is displayed. The Postcards option is selected by default. Click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an option: To print individual postcards on a dot-matrix printer : Select Printer and click OK . To display the postcards : Select Print Preview and click OK . To print the postcards on a laser printer : Select Laser Postcard File and click OK to send the postcards to the batch file. Note: After selecting the criteria for your postcards, you must print the postcards from the batch file. To print the postcards, s elect Reports > Print Laser Postcards . The Laser Postcards window is displayed. The number of postcards to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select the type of postcards you want to print, and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the postcards should be cleared. Verify that the postcards have printed correctly, and click Yes . The Recall Postcard Setup window is displayed. To change the report title, type a name in the Title field. In the Notices section, do the following: Select Update Notices Sent Count : Each time a patient is included in the Recall report, the Notices field on the Recall tab of the patient record is updated. This enables you to identify the number of notices sent to a patient. Select the patients to be included in the report: Select 0 Previous Notices to recall patients due to receive notice 1. Select 1 Previous Notice to recall patients due to receive notice 2. Select 2 Previous Notices to recall patients due to receive notice 3. Select 3 or more Previous Notices to recall patients who have already been sent 3 (or more) notices. Note : You can select more than one checkbox at a time. To recall patients who are due to receive their first, second, or third notice, select the 0 Previous Notices, 1 Previous Notice , and 2 Previous Notices checkboxes. In the Dates section, select a date range for the report. In the Patient Names section, select the start and stop last names from the drop-down lists to generate the recall list for a specific alphabetical range of patients. In the Providers section, select the provider identification number from the drop-down list to generate the recall list for a specific provider. To generate the recall list for all providers, select 999 from the drop-down list. To include only patie

topics.title: Moving Patients
topics.body: Moving Patients Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows > Handling Appointments in the Daily Appointments Window Moving Patients After checking a patient in and moving the patient to the operatory, select the appointment and select Change Location . The appointment is displayed in the default color for patients who are in the operatory. The Change Location command is disabled after you post the appointment. After the provider has completed the treatment and the patient is returned to the waiting room, select the appointment and select Change Location . The appointment is displayed in the default color for checked-in patients. Tip Generate the Patient Visit Statistics report to calculate check-in, operatory, and check-out information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Moving a Patient to His Own Account
topics.body: Moving a Patient to His Own Account Moving a Patient to His Own Account To move a patient from an existing account to his own account: Select List > Patient . Highlight the patient you want to move. On the Options Bar, click Move Patient. Click Yes on the message that reads Move this Patient? Click Yes on the window that prompts you to create a new account. If the patient has an outstanding insurance balance, the software will not allow the patient to be moved. Depending on your preference, the primary insurance information for the patient can be carried over to the new account. Click Yes when prompted to carry over the primary insurance to the new account. Otherwise, click No . Click OK on the message that reads Patient Successfully Moved... Press Any Key to Continue . The patient has now been moved from the previous account into their own account. Note that the patient name will still exist in the original account, though they will be marked as INACTIVE and their first name will be followed by an underscore ("_"). Additional Information: You should not move patients with active payment plans to their own account until the plan is cancelled or completed, as this can cause transaction problems when payment plans need to be run. While you can move a patient from one account to another, it is important to remember that for accounting reasons, the transactions are not moved to the new account with the patient. Only a single line item transaction indicating the balance will be present for the patient in the new account. You can view the patient's old transactions in the patient's Transaction History, which is accessed from the Transactions window. All of the patient's images, documents, history, charting, and contacts are moved as well. Patients cannot be moved until the outstanding insurance claims are finalized and estimated insurance amounts for the patient are taken care of. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Moving X-rays Accidentally Saved to the Wrong Chart
topics.body: Moving X-rays Accidentally Saved to the Wrong Chart Moving X-rays Accidentally Saved to the Wrong Chart If you saved x-rays to the wrong chart, you can copy or move the x-rays to another area on your computer and then import them into the correct chart. The instructions below reference specific windows in CS Imaging, but the steps are similar for other imaging products. To move x-rays that were accidentally saved to the wrong chart: Open CS Imaging for the patient where the x-rays are located. Click History Open . The Patient History window is displayed. Select the List tab. Highlight the x-rays that need to be moved, and click Send to . The Export window is displayed. Tip: Press and hold Shift and select the x-rays if they are grouped in succession, or press and hold Ctrl and select individual x-rays. Click Select next to the Folder location field, select Desktop from the drop-down list, and click OK . The File Format field defaults to Carestream files - Large format . Keep this setting and click OK . Open CS Imaging for the patient needing the x-rays and select File > Import Image File . The Import a File window is displayed. In the Look in field, select Desktop from the drop-down list. Select the x-rays you want to import and click Open . Tip: Press and hold Shift and select the x-rays if they are grouped in succession, or press and hold Ctrl and select individual x-rays. When you are satisfied that the x-rays are in the correct patient's chart, return to the patient's chart containing the incorrect x-rays, open CS Imaging, and select File > Delete . The Patient History window is displayed. On the List tab, select the incorrect x-rays, and click OK . A prompt asking you to confirm the deletion is displayed. Click Delete . DO NOT delete the x-rays until you are certain they are in the correct patient record. Deleted x-rays are not retrievable. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Starting the Merge Process from Batched Records
topics.body: Starting the Merge Process from Batched Records Using Experts > Merging Letter Expert Documents > Starting the Merge Process Starting the Merge Process from Batched Records You can merge data f rom several records to one letter template by adding them to a batch file. You can then merge the information from the batch file with a template. To start the merge process from batched records: Select Expert > Letter Expert > Merge All Batched Letters . The Letter Expert Merge window is displayed. Select the set of letters that you want to merge. Select an option: To merge and print the letters : Click Print . To merge and save the letters : Click Save . The Merge Wizard is displayed. For more information, see Merging Letters . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Starting the Merge Process from Letter Expert Templates
topics.body: Starting the Merge Process from Letter Expert Templates Using Experts > Merging Letter Expert Documents > Starting the Merge Process Starting the Merge Process from Letter Expert Templates If no template exists, you can type letter text, save the text as a template, and start the merge process from the template. To start the merge process from a letter expert template: Select Create Letter Now from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Starting the Merge Process from the Contact Expert
topics.body: Starting the Merge Process from the Contact Expert Using Experts > Merging Letter Expert Documents > Starting the Merge Process Starting the Merge Process from the Contact Expert To start the merge process from the Contact Expert. To start the merge process from the Contact Expert, select an option: To merge one contact with a letter : Select a contact and select Send Entry to Letter Expert from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. To merge the contact list with letters : Select Send List to Letter Expert from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. See Using the Contact Expert for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Inserting Merge Fields into Templates
topics.body: Inserting Merge Fields into Templates Using Experts > Working with Letter Expert Templates Inserting Merge Fields into Templates A merge field contains data to be displayed in a final document. When you insert merge fields into a template, the data is displayed in the finished letter. To create a template, see Creating Letter Expert Templates . To insert a merge field into a template: While viewing the template, place the cursor where you want the field to appear in the letter text and click Insert Merge Field on the SoftDent toolbar (located on the Add-Ins tab in Microsoft Word 2010). The Find Selector window is displayed. Select the category of data you want to insert. Select an option: To view a list of data fields that typically appear in a letter : Select Display only common fields . To view a complete list of all data fields for the specified category : Select Display all fields . A list of categories and data fields is displayed. Expand the category containing the data field you want to merge into the template. A small minus sign (-) is displayed left of the text when a category is expanded. A small plus sign (+) is displayed left of the text when a category is collapsed. To expand or collapse a category, click the category. Select the field that you want to insert into the template, and click Insert Field . Repeat steps 4 and 5 until you have inserted all merge fields that you want to include in the template. To add a list to the template, see Inserting Lists into Templates . To add an image to a template, see Inserting Images into Templates . Click Back to SoftDent to save the template and return to the software. The Template Information window is displayed. Select an option: To save your changes : Click Edit . To create a new template : Click Add . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Missed Appointments Report
topics.body: Running the Missed Appointments Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports Running the Missed Appointments Report The Missed Appointments report g enerates a list of patients who have missed a scheduled appointment. For each patient that has missed an appointment, the report includes: Name Identification number Phone numbers Appointment date Appointment time Provider identification number Operatory Reason for each missed appointment After you run this report, the missed appointments are no longer displayed in the Daily Appointments window. If the Save slot notes and blocked slots to history option is selected in the Scheduler Settings window, slot notes and blocked slots are not deleted from the database when you run the Missed Appointments report and will display in the History Appointments window. See Setting Scheduler Defaults for more information. Sample Report To generate the Missed Appointments report: Select Reports > Missed Appointments or select Reports > Recall/Appt > Missed Appointments . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Missed Appointment Report Setup window is displayed. Select the date before which to include all missed appointments from the List All Missed Appointments On Or Before drop-down list. Select an option: To add the missed appointments to the Tickler file : Select Enter Missed Appointments into Tickler File . To remove color coding from the report : Select Remove Colors From Scheduler . Click OK . Related Topics Daily Reports: Morning Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Placing Missed Appointments in the Tickler File
topics.body: Placing Missed Appointments in the Tickler File Scheduling Appointments > Using the Tickler Placing Missed Appointments in the Tickler File Missed appointments occur when a patient fails to cancel or attend an appointment. Run the Missed Appointments report to delete this information or enter the missed appointment information into the tickler file. To generate the report, see Running the Missed Appointments Report . To add appointments to the tickler file while running the Missed Appointments report: In the Missed Appointment Report Setup window, select Enter Missed Appointments into Tickler File . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Month At A Glance Window
topics.body: Using the Month At A Glance Window Using the Scheduler Using the Month At A Glance Window To display the Month At A Glance window, select an option: From the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows : Select Month At A Glance from the drop-down list at the top of the window. From the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows : Select an appointment or open time slot, right-click, and select Month At A Glance . From the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows : Select an appointment or open time slot and press Alt + M. From any window : Select Scheduler > Month At A Glance . The Month At A Glance window is displayed. Related Topics Navigating the Month At A Glance Window Using Practice and Provider Modes Printing Month At A Glance Window Information Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Goal Scheduling Setting a Monthly Production Goal
topics.body: Goal Scheduling Setting a Monthly Production Goal Setting Up the Scheduler > Goal Scheduling Setting Monthly Production Goals To set a monthly production goal for your office or a specific provider: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select an option: To set a monthly production goal for your practice : Select Select Practice from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To set a monthly production goal for a specific provider : Select Select Provider from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Select a month and select Distribute Monthly from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Monthly Goal Distribution window is displayed. Type the monthly goal amount in the Monthly Goal field and click OK . A message asking you whether to apply the monthly goal and daily average to each day is displayed. Select an option: To apply the monthly goal and daily average to each day : Click Yes . To save the monthly goal but not apply it each day of the calender month : Click No . After you have saved a monthly production goal, you can copy the goal to other months. For more information, see Copying Goals. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Monthly Production Goals
topics.body: Setting Monthly Production Goals Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Provider Information > Setting Production Goals Setting a Monthly Production Goal To set a monthly production goal for a specific provider: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select Select Provider . The provider name is displayed preceding the calendar. Select the month and select Distribute Monthly . The Monthly Goal Distribution window is displayed. Type the monthly goal amount in the Monthly Goal field and click OK . A dialog box is displayed, asking you to apply the monthly goal and daily average to each day. Select an option: To apply the monthly goal and daily average to each day : Click Yes . The production goal for each day of the month is displayed in the Month At A Glance (GOAL) window. To save the monthly goal but not apply it to each day of the calender month : Click No . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. After you have saved a monthly production goal, you can copy the goal to other days. For more information, see Copying Goals . Tip Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing the Monthly Production Goals
topics.body: Viewing the Monthly Production Goals Setting Up the Scheduler > Tracking Goals Viewing the Monthly Production Goals You can display a yearly view of goal and production information set and met for the practice in the Progress Toward Goals window. You can view the percentage amounts for: Monthly goals. Actual production amounts. Yearly production amounts. To view the yearly goal and production amounts for your practice from any window, click Production Goals in the Power bar. The Progress Toward Goals window is displayed. The following are displayed for the previous five and next six months: Daily goal achieved percentage Dollars scheduled or posted for the day Day�s production goal numbers To view the Progress Toward Goals window from the Month At A Glance (GOAL) window, select Year from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Goal Scheduling Setting a Monthly Production Goal
topics.body: Goal Scheduling Setting a Monthly Production Goal Setting Up the Scheduler > Goal Scheduling Setting Monthly Production Goals To set a monthly production goal for your office or a specific provider: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select an option: To set a monthly production goal for your practice : Select Select Practice from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To set a monthly production goal for a specific provider : Select Select Provider from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Select a month and select Distribute Monthly from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Monthly Goal Distribution window is displayed. Type the monthly goal amount in the Monthly Goal field and click OK . A message asking you whether to apply the monthly goal and daily average to each day is displayed. Select an option: To apply the monthly goal and daily average to each day : Click Yes . To save the monthly goal but not apply it each day of the calender month : Click No . After you have saved a monthly production goal, you can copy the goal to other months. For more information, see Copying Goals . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating Multi-Page Automated Statements
topics.body: Generating Multi-Page Automated Statements eServices > Using the Automated Statements Service > Using Automated Statements > Generating Automated Statements Generating Multi-Page Automated Statements If you use Automated Statements to process billing statements, you can send multi-page electronic statements. You can select this option while setting billing statement preferences, previewing electronic statements, or using the Billing Statements Wizard. Sending multiple pages results in additional charges. Setting Billing Statement Printing Preferences To send multi-page Automated Statements: Select System > Change System Settings > Billing Statements . The Billing Statement Settings window is displayed. Click Printing Preferences . The Printing Preferences window is displayed. Type the number of days to include in the report results in the Show transactions for the last _ days field. To specify the maximum number of pages to send when using Automated Statements, select Not to exceed and type the maximum number of pages to send. To avoid the additional page charge for electronic statements, type 1 to send only one statement page to the clearinghouse. Tip To print all transactions posted within the specified number of days, deselect Not to exceed . Click OK . Previewing Multi-Page Statements To preview a multi-page statement before transmitting the information: Select Reports > Accounting > Statements > Electronic > Electronic Batch Preview . The Electronic Statements window is displayed. Type the number of days to include in the report results in the Itemize transactions for the last _ days field. To specify the maximum number of pages to send when using Automated Statements, select Not to exceed and type the maximum number of pages to send. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Moving Among Pockets
topics.body: Moving Among Pockets Periodontal Charting Moving Among Pockets To move among pockets, select an option: To move between pockets : Click Previous Pocket or Next Pocket on the Keypad toolbar . If the Auto Advance feature is enabled : The application automatically advances from pocket to pocket when charting. If you have selected a custom pocket path, the software automatically advances to the selected pockets. To use keys to move between pockets : Press an arrow key in the correct direction. To dictate keyboard combinations, see Setting Perio Chart Options and Properties . To move to a particular tooth : Select it. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Moving Patients to an Operatory
topics.body: Moving Patients to an Operatory Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Moving Patients to an Operatory To move a checked-in patient to the operatory: Select the appropriate appointment. Select Change Location from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The operatory room number is displayed in the Loc column, and the text for the selected patient appointment is displayed in green. If you failed to check the patient in before selecting Change Location from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu, the current system time is displayed in the Arrive column. If the patient was in the operatory and is moved to the waiting room, W is displayed in the Loc column, and the text for the selected patient appointment is displayed in blue. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Moving Patients
topics.body: Moving Patients Setting Up Patients Moving a Patient to Another Account You can move a patient to a new or existing account. You can also move all or a portion of the patient balance; however, you cannot move a patient with an outstanding insurance balance. Appointments, contact notes, and history files are transferred with patient data to the new or existing account. Patient transactions are not moved with the patient record. To move a patient to a new or existing account: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient. In the Power Bar : Select Patient List Window . The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient. In the Account List or Account window : Select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. A Patient List window containing all of the patients associated with the account is displayed. Select a patient. In the InOffice window : Select Patient from the Options menu of the Express bar. The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient. In any window : Press Ctrl + O. The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient. Select Move Patient from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. A message is displayed. To move the patient, click Yes . A message is displayed. Select an option: To move the patient to a new account : Click Yes . If the patient has a balance, the patient�s current balance is displayed. Enter the amount to be transferred to the new account, and click OK . If the patient has insurance coverage, a message that asks to add the existing insurance information to the new account is displayed. Click the appropriate button. A message that informs you of the patient�s successful move is displayed. Click OK . A new account is created for the patient using the next available account identification number, and all personal information is copied to the new account. To move the patient to an existing account : Click No . The Account List window is displayed. Select the account to which to move the patient record from the list. A message that informs you of the move is displayed. Click OK . An underscore is added to the end of the patient�s first name (example: Sam_) to indicate that the record is moved. The old patient record is displayed after the new record when records are displayed alphabetically in the Patient List window. The date the record is moved and the new patient account identification number are displayed in the Notes field of the patient record. I is displayed in the User Codes field of the Patient window when the old record is displayed to indicate that it is inactive. Inactive records are not included in reports. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Move Selected Transactions Utility
topics.body: Running the Move Selected Transactions Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running the Transaction History Maintenance Utilities Running the Move Selected Transactions Utility Run this utility to compress paid transactions; balance transfers to the patient and claims that have not been sent or paid are not compressed into the transaction history. Actual productions, payments, and statement sent codes are stored. Other information, such as treatment plans, is deleted. Save a complete backup in addition to your regular backup before running this utility. To move selected transactions: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance >Transaction History Maintenance > Move Selected Transactions Utility . A message informs you to save a backup before running this utility. Click Yes . The Transaction Compressing window is displayed. Type the number of days before which transactions will be compressed in the All Transactions older than field. To exclude accounts associated with a specific user code from the compression, type the user code in the Do not archive transactions for field. The default value for this field is CL and indicates that accounts turned over for collection or associated with pending legal cases will not be compressed. Click OK . A message asks you whether to compress the selected files. Click Yes . A message informs you that the compression is complete. Click OK . Restart the software. Archiving a Previous Year's Transactions Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the New Area Code Boundaries Utility
topics.body: Running the New Area Code Boundaries Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the New Area Code Boundaries Utility Run this utility to update area codes if the phone company changes your regional area code. Contact the phone company to acquire a list of exchanges affected by the new area code. To update your area code boundaries: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > New Area Code Boundaries . The New Area Code Boundaries window is displayed. Type the current and new area codes in the appropriate fields. To add 1 as a prefix for the area code, select Add "1" prefix to this new area code . If the practice will use the new area code, select Will the practice be using the new area code . Type the name of the affected exchanges and select the appropriate checkbox to indicate which numbers are represented in the Exchange Information section. Click Apply . A message asks you whether to apply the changes. Click Yes . A message informs you of your selections. Click Yes . A message informs you of the number of phone numbers that were updated. Click OK . The New Area Code Boundaries window is displayed. Click Close . Restart the software. If your practice remains under the old area code and your exchange file lists numbers affected by the new area code : Exchanges in the file receive a new area code and a "1" prefix for long distance (only if specified). If your practice remains under the old area code and your exchange file represents numbers not affected by the new area code : All exchanges in the file stay the same, and all exchanges not in the file receive the new area code and a "1" prefix for long distance (only if specified). If your practice uses the new area code and your exchange file represents numbers changing to the new area code : All exchanges in the file have their area code field removed because they will be local to the practice. All exchanges not in the file are assigned the old area code and receive a "1" prefix for long distance (only if specified). If your practice uses the new area code and your exchange list represents numbers not affected by the new area code : All exchanges in the file receive the old area code and become long distance and receive a "1" prefix (only if specified). All exchanges not found in the file remain local and have the area code field removed. Any phone number with an area code different from the current or new area code will not be changed : All exchanges in the file receive the old area code and become long distance and receive a "1" prefix (only if specified). All exchanges not found in the file remain local and have the area code field removed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: National Provider Identifier
topics.body: National Provider Identifier Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Provider Information National Provider Identifier This topic explains where to enter NPI numbers in the software and how to select updated claim forms. For more information about NPI numbers, see the ADA website. Entering Type 1 NPI Numbers Type 1 NPI numbers identify doctors who operate independently as sole proprietors or as part of an organization. This type correlates with a doctor�s Social Security number. Type the number on each doctor�s provider record. Select List > Provider . Select a doctor and click Edit . Select the ID�s tab and type the NPI number in the Nat�l Prov ID field. Entering Type 2 NPI Numbers Type 2 NPI numbers identify organizations such as hospitals, clinics, group practices, or corporations. This type correlates with a practice�s Employer Identification Number (EIN). A user with appropriate security rights can enter this number on the practice record. Select File > Practice . Type the number in the National Provider Id field located in the middle of the window. Insurance Claim Forms To display an NPI number, you must select a 2006 claim form. Select Insurance > Select Insurance Forms . Select the applicable forms within the range of #165 - #175. On the 2006 Standard ADA forms, the provider, or Type 1, NPI number is displayed in box #54. The practice, or Type 2, NPI number is displayed in box #49. If no Type 2 NPI number exists, the software displays the Type 1 NPI number in both boxes #49 and #54 to identify the provider as both the billing and treating dentist. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Navigating the Month At A Glance Window
topics.body: Navigating the Month At A Glance Window Using the Scheduler > Using the Month At A Glance Window Navigating the Month At A Glance Window The commands available to help you navigate the Month At A Glance window are: : Press this key to select the same day for the following week. : Press this key to select the same day of the previous week. + : Press this key to display the next month. Press this key to display the previous month. : Press this key to select the next day. : Press this key to select the previous day. Ctrl + H : Press these keys to select the current day. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting NEA Preferences
topics.body: Setting NEA Preferences Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services Setting NEA Preferences Do not edit the Facility ID unless instructed to do so by NEA. To configure the NEA FastAttach service: From your desktop, select C:\SoftDent\neacomm.exe . The NEAComm window is displayed. Click Preferences . The Preferences tab is displayed. Type the NEA assigned facility identification number in the Facility ID field. This field is case sensitive. After you enter the number, it can only be edited from the .ini file. To view the NEA log, click Launch NEA Log . The NEA log lists NEA transmissions from the software by date, time, and file receipt. Select an option: To print the attachment file receipt upon successful transmission to NEA : Select Automatically print report . To send pending attachments to NEA when starting the software : Select Send attachments on startup . To make the Report tab of the NEAComm window the active window after the attachment is sent : Select Automatically switch to report tab . To save attachment file receipts from the NEA in the C:\SoftDent\NEACOMM\reports directory : Select Save Reports . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services
topics.body: Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services Submitting and Tracking Claims Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) is a clearinghouse that uses its FastAttach service to transmit attachments, in support of electronic claims, to payors via the Internet. Attachments include: Images X-rays Narrative explanations To use the FastAttach service with the software, you must perform the following: Enroll with NEA for the FastAttach service. Receive an NEA assigned Facility ID for your practice. Download the NEA software on each workstation where images will be attached to claims. SoftDent Cloud users must use the Configure NEA option found under System > Modules > NEA FastAttach > Configure NEA . You need to configure the software only on one machine. Associate each insurance company with an NEA Payor in the software. Call an NEA technical support representative with any questions about using the FastAttach service. Related Topics Associating Insurance Companies with NEA Payors Creating Attachments to Send to the NEA Viewing an NEA Attachment History Entry Transmitting Attachments to NEA Verifying an NEA Transmission in the Claim Viewing NEA Status for Claims Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Attachments to Send to the NEA
topics.body: Creating Attachments to Send to the NEA Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services Creating Attachments to Send to the NEA After you set up the insurance company and NEA payors, you can create and send attachments via the Internet. You can create NEA Attachments in the following windows: Claim window Transactions window In the Transactions window, add a transaction requiring an attachment and select Submit Insurance . Chart for window In the Chart for window, click the Treatment Planning tab, chart a procedure requiring an attachment, and select Submit Insurance from the Treatment Plan toolbar. During checkout Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Activating New Accounts
topics.body: Activating New Accounts Setting Up Accounts > Adding New Accounts from the Scheduler Windows Activating New Accounts When you add a new account or patient from the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows, the account information is stored in the database and can be viewed and edited from the Account List, Account, Patient, and Patient List windows. The title bar on these windows indicate that the account or patient is new. As long as the patient or account is considered new, most features in the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu are not available. To activate a new account or patient, select an option: From the New Patient window : Click OK . A message is displayed, asking you to activate the new account. Click Yes . From the New Account window : Click OK . A message is displayed, asking you to activate the new account. Click Yes . From the Daily Appointments window : Select the scheduled time, select Activate , right-click, select Activate Patient , and click OK . A message is displayed, asking you to activate the new account. Click Yes . Tip When you activate a new patient record, the associated account is also activated. After you activate a new account, you must activate each patient record. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the New Account List Report
topics.body: Running the New Account List Report Setting Up Accounts > Running Account Reports Running the New Account List Report The New Account List report generates a list of new patients with their first visit date within a specific date range. It includes patient ID, name, address, phone number, and the date of their first scheduled visit. You can set criteria to only show results from a specific date range, or set up criteria to compare results between two date ranges. Also, the report can generate all patient and/or doctor referral information. Sample Report To run the New Account List report: Select Reports > Account > First Visit . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The New Account List Report window is displayed. In the Date Range of First Visit section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending dates from a calendar. In the Comparison Range section, click the drop-down arrows to select a past date range to compare with the results from the date range selected in the Date Range of First Visit section. In the Provider section, click the drop-down arrow to select the provider number from a list, or leave the first provider set to 999 to report on all providers. Click the Include Referral Information checkbox to include all doctor and/or patient referral information in the report. Click the Compare Date Ranges checkbox to compare the date ranges you have selected. You must set up the past date range in the Comparison Range section. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. If you selected the Merge Letter Expert output option, the Letter Expert Template ID field is active. Click the drop-down arrow to select a template from the list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Additional Enhancements
topics.body: Additional Enhancements What's New in Version 12.5 Additional Enhancements SoftDent, version 12.5, includes the following additional enhancements: Enabling Electronic Statements Electronic Router Button Added to Charting Enabling Topaz Electronic Signature Pads Delete Dangling TeamTalk Notes Utility Referral Source Analysis Report Business Intelligence Shortcut Enabling Electronic Statements A new Enable e-statement service checkbox has been added to the Billing Statement Settings window. This checkbox must be selected before your office can send electronic statements. If you are an existing e-statement service customer, this setting is selected by default. For more information, see Setting Up Electronic Statements . Electronic Router Access Added to Charting You can now add an Electronic Router button to the Common toolbar in charting. This enables you to open the Electronic Router in the patient's chart and schedule a follow-up appointment without opening the InOffice or Daily Appointments windows. For more information, see Using the Common Toolbar . Enabling Topaz Electronic Signature Pads A new Use Topaz Electronic Signature Pad checkbox has been added to the Startup Preferences window. You must select this setting whether you are a new or existing user of a Topaz signature pad. For more information, see Setting Startup Preferences . Delete Dangling TeamTalk Notes Utility The Delete Dangling TeamTalk Notes utility has been added to the SoftDent Utilities Maintenance menu. When you run the utility, the software searches through all of the TeamTalk notes and removes any dangling notes. For more information, see Running the Delete Dangling TeamTalk Notes Utility . Referral Source Analysis Report The Referral Source Analysis report has been added to the Practice Management report menu. This report displays the number of patients�and their production amounts�who have visited your office in the specified time periods by each referring doctor, grouped by categories that you create. For more information, see Running Referral Reports . A Referral Categories database has been added, in which you can create your own categories�for example, a specific region or a specific hygienist�to track referral revenue. For more information, see Creating Referral Categories . Business Intelligence Shortcut A Business Intelligence shortcut is installed on your workstation�s desktop when you install SoftDent. Double-click the shortcut to access the website for Sikka Software, a provider of Business Intelligence software for dental professionals. Sikka Software products integrate with SoftDent and your practice�s financial software to give you critical information on vital areas of your practice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Credit Card Payment Plan Features
topics.body: Credit Card Payment Plan Features What's New in Version 12.5 Credit Card Payment Plan Features Credit card payment plans have received enhancements in several areas, including: Payment plan windows Reports Practice Central Security Credit Card Payment Plan Windows Enhancements Several windows dealing with credit card payments have changed. The Credit Card Payment Plan�Terms window has been updated. Enhancements include: The ability to have the patient pick a payment: A Patient Picks Payment checkbox has been added to the Credit Card Payment Plan�Terms window. This option enables the patient to specify the monthly payment he wants to make. For more information, see Setting Up Credit Card Payment Plans . The ability to accept a down payment and charge the patient's credit card immediately. A Down Payment field has been added to the Credit Card Payment Plan�Terms window. This field enables the patient to give you a down payment for the credit card plan. The down payment is charged to the patient's credit card when you create the payment plan. For more information, see Setting Up Credit Card Payment Plans . The Credit Card Payment Plan�CC Payment window has been updated. Enhancements include: The ability to edit individual payments using new Edit Amount, Edit Date, Add Payment, and Split Payment options: By right-clicking on a credit card payment, you can edit the amount or date, add a payment, or split a payment. For more information, see Reviewing and Editing Payments in a Credit Card Payment Plan . The ability to view unallocated payment amounts: A section has been added to the bottom of the Credit Card Payment Plan�CC Payment window that lists any unallocated payment amounts that result from editing payments. The following fields have been added: Payment Plan Amount�Displays the total amount of the payment plan. Sum of Payments�Displays the sum of all authorized, settled, and unauthorized payments. Payments that have been voided or which have failed authorization are not included in this total. Unallocated�Displays the difference between the payment plan amount and the sum of payments. If the sum of payments is greater, the unallocated amount is displayed as a negative number. These totals are updated as you edit payments. The Batch Authorization window has been renamed the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details window and has been updated. Enhancements include: The ability to view and sort credit card payments. You can view and sort credit card payments and access the payment window for a specific payment type. For more information, see Using the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details window . The ability to access the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details window from several areas in the software. The Recurring Credit Card Details window can be accessed using the following methods: By clicking the Recurring Credit Card Payments header in Practice Central. By selecting Reports > Accounting > Recurring Credit Card Details from the menu bar. By running the Credit Card Settlement report. For more information, see Using the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details window . Credit card payment plan indicators have been added to several windows in the software to let you know if the patient or account has an active payment plan. Areas with indicators include: Header bar: The letters RCCP (recurring credit card payment) are displayed next to the patient's name on the header bar. Patient list: If the selected patient has an active payment plan, the

topics.title: Document Manager Features
topics.body: Document Manager Features What's New in Version 12.5 Document Manager Features The Document Manager feature has received enhancements in the following areas: Viewing an FMS or Context E-mailing Images in an FMS or Context ePostings EOB Folder Importing Additional File Types Viewing an FMS or Context in the Document Manager Images belonging to an FMS or context are now displayed below the FMS or context in a standard document tree, enabling you to see which images belong to a particular FMS or context. For more information, see Viewing an FMS or Context . E-Mailing Images in an FMS or Context In the Documents for and SoftDent E-mail windows, images belonging to an FMS or context are now displayed below the FMS or context in a standard document tree. You can attach specific images from the FMS to an e-mail. For more information, see E-Mailing Images in an FMS or Context . ePostings EOB Folder An ePostings EOB folder is now displayed in the document tree. If you subscribe to the ePostings service, a copy of the electronic EOB is placed in this folder when you post an ePostings payment. You can view the EOB for each of the areas related to the payment, including Claim, Insurance Company, Insurance Plan, and Patient. For more information, see Viewing ePostings Documents in the Document Manager . Importing Additional File Types You can now import any file type into the Document Manager. Previously, you could import only image files, but now you can import documents created in Microsoft Word (.doc files), text files (.txt), HTML files, XML files, and so on. For more information, see Importing Additional File Types . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Fingerprint Scanner
topics.body: Fingerprint Scanner What's New in Version 12.5 Fingerprint Scanner SoftDent now integrates with software that enables you to scan and record employee fingerprints to use when logging in or out. If you use the Time Card feature, you can also use your fingerprint to clock in and out. You must install the fingerprint scanner and software in order to use this feature. For more information, see Using the Fingerprint Scanner . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up New Holidays
topics.body: Setting Up New Holidays Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Holidays Setting Up New Holidays To apply a new holiday to scheduler windows: Select Scheduler > Holidays . The Setup Holidays window is displayed. Click Add . A new identification number is displayed in a new row at the bottom of the list, and the Description field is enabled. Type a holiday description in the Description field and press Tab. The From Date field is enabled. Type the holiday start date in the From Date field and press Tab. The To Date field is enabled. Type the holiday end date in the To Date field and press Tab. The Annual option is enabled. If you are entering a one-day holiday, type the same date in both the From Date and To Date fields. You must type a date within 21 months of the current date in the From Date and To Date fields. If you leave either of the fields blank or type a date that does not fall within the 21-month date range, a warning message is displayed. Select an option: To enter a holiday that occurs on the same date every year : Select Annual . If this option is selected, the holiday is automatically rescheduled each year. If this option is not selected, you must manually reset the holiday each year. For example, Independence Day is observed on the 4th day of July every year. Select the option to indicate that the office is closed every year on this date. To enter a holiday that does not occur on the same date every year : Do not select Annual . For example, Thanksgiving Day. If the annual holiday falls on a weekend and your practice will be closed the Friday before or the Monday after the holiday, enter the observed day separately. Click OK . A warning is displayed when you attempt to schedule an appointment on a day defined as a holiday; you can, however, override the system settings and schedule the appointment. A warning is displayed if an appointment is already scheduled on a day specified as a holiday. You must either reschedule or cancel the appointment. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Insurance Features
topics.body: Insurance Features What's New in Version 12.5 Insurance Features Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA) Service The ERA service enables you to electronically receive insurance payment information from participating insurance companies. The information received through the ERA service functions as an electronic Explanation Of Benefits. After you have verified that the payment information you received matches the amount that SoftDent is expecting, you can post the payment. This feature has been renamed and is now called ePostings. See Using ePostings for more information. Enable Real Time Claims An Enable Real Time Claims checkbox has been added to the Insurance Settings window. This checkbox must be selected in order to use the Real Time Claims service. See Setting Insurance Defaults for more information. CDT-2009 Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2009) codes is included with this version of SoftDent. The CDT-2009 code set has been replaced by the CDT-2022 code set. See Using CDT Codes for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: KIOSK Check-In Module
topics.body: KIOSK Check-In Module What's New in Version 12.5 KIOSK Check-In Module You can now set up a KIOSK station for your waiting room that your patients can use to check themselves in when they arrive for appointments. You can customize the look and feel of your KIOSK with any message or instructions you want patients to see, as well as a picture or your office logo. The KIOSK feature also enables you to view a list of the patients who have checked themselves in or who need additional assistance�those patients with a check-in TeamTalk note or those who need to complete a HIPAA form, for example. For more information, see Using the KIOSK Check-In Module . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Features
topics.body: Scheduling Features What's New in Version 12.5 Scheduling Features Using Pre-Defined Books with the Auto Scheduler The method used to search for rooms when using the Auto Scheduler has changed. You can still randomly select from all available rooms by creating a temporary book, which you can make permanent, if necessary. You now also have the option of selecting a pre-defined book. For more information, see Auto Scheduling Patient Appointments . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Time Card Features
topics.body: Time Card Features What's New in Version 12.5 Time Card Features Two enhancements have been made to the Time Card function: Ability to clock in without clocking out for the previous work period Sick option added to the list of available time selections Clocking In Without Clocking Out In previous versions of SoftDent, you were not able to clock in if you failed to clock out for the previous work period, and the time clock administrator had to take care of the problem. In version 12.5, you can now clock in and enter a memo for the period for which you were not clocked out. For more information, see Clocking In Without Clocking Out . Using the Sick Time Option You can now select Sick as an option in the Time Card window to describe time missed from work due to illness. Sick time is also displayed on the Time Card report. For more information about recording time, see Handling Time Card Entries . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Normal Recalls for a Patient
topics.body: Setting Normal Recalls for a Patient Recalling Patients > Setting Up Recalls Setting Normal Recalls for a Patient When you add a new patient record, the adult or child default recall code procedure is displayed in the Last Exam section of the Recall Settings window. You can customize the recall information for each patient and override the default frequency, codes, notices, and time units. If you do not select a code that triggers a recall or an explosion code, the Last Exam date is not reset when the patient keeps a recall appointment, and the patient record remains overdue for recall until you manually change the date. If you enter 0 in the Code drop-down list, the patient is included in the Recall report at the specified interval, but no procedure is associated with the patient. To set Normal Recall information for a patient: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click a patient. The patient record is displayed in the Patient window. Type the patient date of birth in the DOB field on the Info tab and click the Recall tab. The default Normal Recall information is displayed in the Last Exam section. If you change the name of your Normal Recall system, the name of the Last Exam section in the Patient window changes. Select an option: To change the recall frequency : Type a new frequency in the Frequency field. To set a different Normal Recall procedure : Select the Code drop-down list. The ADA/Transaction Codes window is displayed. Double-click the code. To set the number of time slots the Normal Recall procedure requires in the scheduler : Type a number in the TU field. T o set an alternating recall procedure : See Setting Alternating Recalls . If you update the notices sent count when running the Normal Recall report, the value in the Notices field is updated every time a recall notice is sent to the patient. If this option is not selected when running the Recall report, the value in the Notices field is always 0. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up the Normal Recall System
topics.body: Setting Up the Normal Recall System Recalling Patients > Setting Up Recalls Setting Up the Normal Recall System To set up the Normal Recall system: Select System > Change System Settings > Recall > Recall Setup/Codes . The Recall Settings window is displayed in SCAN mode. Type the maximum number of recall notices to send to a patient before excluding him from the Recall reports or files in the Maximum number of recall notices field. To set the default Normal Recall system code for adults and children, click the appropriate drop-down list button. The ADA/Transaction Codes window is displayed. Double-click a code from the list. The code is displayed in the drop-down lists in the Default Recall Codes section. The patient is always recalled for this procedure unless you select a different recall procedure in the patient record. Type the default number of months between updating medical histories in the Medical History Recall Settings section. If a patient does not update his medical history in the specified number of months, the Last History date in the InOffice window is displayed in red text. Type the name of your Normal Recall system in the appropriate field. The default name of the Normal Recall system is Last Exam. Type the default number of months between Recalls in the appropriate field. When one of the recall procedures is posted to a patient record, the Last Exam date is modified, and the patient is recalled the specified number of months after the new Last Exam date in the patient record. Select the Normal Recall system codes in the Codes that set the Normal Recall System section. You can enter up to 10 codes that trigger a patient recall. Example : If you enter code 330 for a panoramic film, the Last Exam date field in the patient record is updated when you post a transaction for that code. Do not select explosion codes for the Normal Recall system. These codes are always posted as separate codes and are never displayed on the transactions record. To print the recall period and the last exam date on recall postcards or labels, select Show Recall information on Postcards/Labels . Select an option: To save your Normal Recall system settings : Click OK and Close . To set your User-Defined Recall system : See Setting Up the User-Defined Recall System . If you select a new adult or child recall from the Default Recall Codes section, the Recall Codes Wizard window is displayed after you click OK. You are reminded to run the Recall Setup/Utility Wizard to update all patients with the new codes. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Notes Tab
topics.body: Notes Tab Trojan > Handling Trojan Traditional Plan > Employer Plans Notes Tab The Notes tab lists any Trojan notes about the plan. Not all plans have notes. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!
