topics.title: Activating New Accounts
topics.body: Activating New Accounts Scheduling Appointments > Scheduling Appointments for New Patients Activating New Accounts When you add a new account or patient in the Daily Appointments Book At A Glance windows, the account information is stored in the database and can be viewed and edited from the Account List, Account, Patient, and Patient List windows. The title bar on these windows indicates that the account or patient is new. As long as the patient or account is considered new, most features in the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu are disabled. New patients added to existing accounts must be activated individually. To activate a new account: In the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows, select the scheduled time unit for the new patient and select Activate from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. If the patient to be activated is associated with an existing account : The New Patient window is displayed. If the patient to be activated is associated with a new account : The New Account window is displayed. Type the information in the fields and click OK . A message that asks you to activate the new patient or account is displayed. To activate the new account or patient record, click Yes . The record is displayed. When you activate a new patient, the associated account is automatically activated. If you activate a new account, however, you must individually activate each patient in the account. If you add a new patient to an existing account, you must activate that patient. Click Close . Tip To ensure that a new patient who does not keep an appointment does not adversely affect your bookkeeping , d o not activate the new patient or account records until the patient arrives for the appointment. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating New Books
topics.body: Creating New Books Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Books Creating New Books To create a new book: Select Scheduler > Setup Books . The Scheduler Books List window is displayed. Press Enter. Click Add . The Scheduler Books window is displayed. Type the name of the new book in the Book Title field. Type the earliest and latest hours to display the book. Click OK . The new office book is saved and the Scheduler Book Definition Wizard - Introduction window is displayed. Read the introduction and click Next . The Scheduler Book Definition Wizard - Options window is displayed. Select an option: To display the same rooms every day in the office book : Select The rooms will be the same every day and click Next . The Scheduler Book Definition Wizard - Room Selection (All Days) window is displayed. Select the rooms to include in the office book and click Next . The Scheduler Book Definition Wizard - Color Selection window is displayed. Go to step 11. To display different rooms on different days of the week : Select The rooms will depend upon the day of the week and click Next . The Scheduler Book Definition Wizard Day Selection window is displayed. Select the days of the week to set up and click Next . The Scheduler Book Definition Wizard - Room Selection (Selected Days) window is displayed. Select the checkbox for each room to display and click Next . The Scheduler Book Definition Wizard - Color Selection window is displayed. If you have not specified the room selection, the Scheduler Book Definition Wizard Day Selection window is displayed. Repeat steps 10 and 11 until the rooms are selected. Select the room, click Color , select a color for the selected room, and click OK . Select a text color for the room title. Repeat this step until a background and text color is selected. Click Next . The Scheduler Book Definition Wizard - Finished window is displayed. Select an option: To change your setting : Click Previous and repeat the appropriate steps. To complete the wizard : Click Finish . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Appointments for New Patients
topics.body: Scheduling Appointments for New Patients Scheduling Appointments Scheduling Appointments for New Patients You can add a new patient from the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance scheduler windows when a new patient calls to schedule an appointment. When the patient arrives for the appointment, enter the remainder of the information and activate the new patient and account records. To add a new patient from the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance scheduler windows: Select an option: In the Daily Appointments window : Select a time slot, and select New Patient Appt from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Book At A Glance window : Select a time slot, and select New Patient Appt from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The New Patient Appt window is displayed. Type the patient�s name and date of birth in the appropriate fields. Select a provider from the Doctor drop-down list. Type the fee schedule. Type the cell, home, and work phone numbers in the appropriate fields. If the new patient was referred to your office by an existing patient or another doctor, select the information from the drop-down lists in the Referral Info section. Select an option: To schedule the patient for an initial exam : Select Initial Exam . The default initial exam procedural code information is displayed in the Code, Description, T#, Su/Qd, Fee, and Slots fields. To change the default codes for initial exams, click Change Defaults . The New Patient Appt Defaults window is displayed. Select a procedural code and click OK . To schedule the patient for an emergency exam : Select Emergency Exam . The default emergency exam procedural code information is displayed in the Code, Description, T#, Su/Qd, Fee, and Slots fields. To change the default codes for emergency exams, click Change Defaults . The New Patient Appt Defaults window is displayed. Select a procedural code and click OK . To schedule the patient for a different exam : Select Other , and select a procedural code from the Code drop-down list. Type any notes in the Appt Notes field. These notes are displayed in the Appointment Card window. Type any medical alerts in the Medical Alert field. This patient is displayed in red text and a red cross is displayed in the Alerts column for the record in the Patient List window. If you are adding the new patient to an existing account, select an account from the Add patient to an existing account drop-down list. To add or view additional account information, click Additional Account Info . For more information, see Setting Up Accounts . Click OK . Related Topics Activating New Accounts Activating New Patients and Accounts Handling Broken Appointments for a New Patient Changing New Patient Appt Window Defaults Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Broken Appointments for a New Patient
topics.body: Handling Broken Appointments for a New Patient Scheduling Appointments > Scheduling Appointments for New Patients Handling Broken Appointments for a New Patient If a new patient does not keep the appointment, select one of the following options: Reschedule the appointment : You can cancel and reschedule the appointment. See Canceling and Rescheduling Appointments . Place the appointment in the Tickler for future rescheduling : You can cancel the appointment and place it in the tickler. See Using the Tickler . Delete the new patient : You can set up the software to delete the new patient and new account records automatically when the Missed Appointment Report is generated. Information referencing the missed appointment is included in the report. To delete this information automatically, select Delete New Patients/Accounts When Printing The Missed Appointment Report in the Scheduler Settings window. See Setting Scheduler Defaults . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Changing New Patient Appt Window Defaults
topics.body: Changing New Patient Appt Window Defaults Scheduling Appointments > Scheduling Appointments for New Patients Changing New Patient Appt Window Defaults While making an appointment for a new patient, you can change the default codes for initial and emergency exams. From the New Patient Appt window, click Change Defaults located next to the Appt Notes field. The New Patient Appt Defaults window is displayed. Select an option: To select a default initial exam procedure : Select a procedure code from the Initial Exam drop-down list. To select a default emergency exam procedure : Select a procedure code from the Emergency Exam drop-down list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Office Expert Item View Permissions
topics.body: Setting Office Expert Item View Permissions Using Experts > Using the Office Expert > Setting Up Office Expert Items Setting Office Expert Item View Permissions In the average office environment, the office manager delegates specific tasks for each employee. When each user logs into the software, only the selected items are displayed in the Office Expert window. If the security system is enabled, you must have the appropriate permission to perform this task. If the proper permissions are not set and you want to edit your active office expert items view, see your software administrator. To select which items each employee can display in the Office Expert window: Select Office Expert from the Experts menu. The Office Expert window is displayed. Select Setup Experts from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Office Expert Setup window is displayed. Select an item from the Active Office Expert Entries list. Click Show Users . The Office Expert User Administration window is displayed. Select an option: To enable users to view an item in the Office Expert window : Select the checkbox left of the appropriate user ID. To hide the item when the Office Expert window is displayed for a user : Deselect the checkbox left of the appropriate user ID. Click OK twice. Office expert items do not display in the list until they have been run. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Office Expert
topics.body: Using the Office Expert Using Experts Using the Office Expert The office expert a ssists you in identifying and performing daily tasks by displaying a user-specific list of important activities and daily issues, and provides quick access to the tools you need to complete each task. When you log in : Information is gathered and a list of tasks is displayed in the office expert, enabling you to see task summaries and priority levels. You can access the contact expert : From the office expert to automate communications with parties crucial to your practice. Example : To contact the patients who missed their scheduled appointments, set the office expert to run the Missed Appointments report every night. The next morning when you log in, the Office Expert window displays each item, which you can use to contact your patients. The Office Expert Setup window : Is divided into two panes and contains buttons that enable you to manipulate each item. A list of items that can be added to the office expert is displayed in the left pane. The list of active office expert items is displayed in the right pane. The description and prompts associated with each office expert item are: Item Description Prompt Overdue Accounts Runs the Account Aging report. The Account Aging Report Setup window is displayed. Credit Balances Runs the Account Aging report. The Account Aging Report Setup window is displayed. Statements To Print Generates a list of statements to be printed. There are no prompts associated with this item. TP Accepted, Not Scheduled Runs the Treatment Plan report. The Treatment Plan Detailed Report Setup window is displayed. TP Diagnosed Runs the Treatment Plan report. The Treatment Plan Detailed Report Setup window is displayed. Confirmation List Runs the Confirmation List report. The Confirmation List Report Setup window is displayed. Yesterday's Login Failures Generates a list of failed logins for the previous day. There are no prompts associated with this item. Yesterday's Added Rx Count Generates a list of prescriptions that were added the previous day. There are no prompts associated with this item. Pending Lab Cases Runs the Lab Case report. The Lab Case Report Setup window is displayed. Appointment List Runs the Appointment List report for a specified date range. The Appointment List Report Setup window is displayed. Tickler (Pending Appts) Runs the Tickler file report. The Tickler Report Setup window is displayed. Short Call List Runs the Short Call List report. The Short Call Report Setup window is displayed. Recall (Last Exam) Runs the Recall (Last Exam) report. The Recall Report Setup window is displayed. Missed Appointments Runs the Missed Appointments report. The Missed Appointments Report Setup window is displayed. End of Day Call Backs Runs the End-of Day Callback report. The Report Setup window is displayed. Outstanding Claims (Paper) Runs the Outstanding Claims By Company report. The Outstanding Insurance Claims By Company window is displayed. The Include regular claims and Include regular pre-authorization claims options are selected. Outstanding Claims (Electronic) Runs the Outstanding Claims By Company report. The Outstanding Insurance Claims By Company window is displayed. Outstanding Claims By Patient Runs the Outstanding Claims By Patient report. The Outstanding Insurance Claims By Patient Report Setup window is displayed. Unsubmitted Claims Runs the Unsubmitted Claims report. The Unsubmitted Insurance Report Setup window is

topics.title: Setting Office Hours
topics.body: Setting Office Hours Setting Up the Scheduler Setting Office Hours Office hours are generally set as part of the Getting Started procedures after the software is installed. You must set the office hours before using the scheduler feature to ensure that a warning is displayed when you attempt to schedule a patient appointment outside of normal office hours. To set or edit your office hours: Select File > Practice . The Practice Information window is displayed. Click the Office Hours tab. The current office hours are displayed. Type the time your office is opened and closed for each day of the week. Type a or p to indicate AM or PM. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Setting Provider Office Hours and Vacation Days Setting Up the Scheduler Setting Provider Office Hours and Vacation Days To use color and goal scheduling, you must enter the provider vacation days and office hours. To set the vacation days and office hours for your providers: Select List > Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. Select a provider and click Edit . The Provider window is displayed. Select an option: To set the provider vacation days : Select them from the Vacation/Days Off section of the Info tab. To set the provider office hours : Type them in the current hours section of the Current Hrs tab. Click OK . Click Close twice . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Setting Office Expert Item Frequency Using Experts > Using the Office Expert > Setting Up Office Expert Items Setting Office Expert Item Frequency You can specify how often a particular office expert should be run: On specific days of the week On or after the _ day of the month Manually If the security system is enabled, you must have the appropriate permission to perform this task. topics.title: Office Expert Features
topics.body: Office Expert Features What's New in Version 14.0 Office Expert Features The office expert has received enhancements in the following areas: Running Office Expert on Specific Days A Set Frequency button has been added to the Office Expert Setup window. When you select an expert and click Set Frequency , the Office Expert Item Frequency window is displayed. This window enables you to specify when the selected office expert should be run (every few days, after a particular day of the month, and so on). See Automatically Running Office Expert Items and Setting Office Expert Item Frequency for more information. Excluding Treatment Plan Transactions in the Unsubmitted Claims Report When you run the Unsubmitted Claims report from inside office expert, the Include Treatment Plan Transactions checkbox (which is selected by default) is no longer grayed out, enabling you to exclude treatment plan transactions from the report. If the Include Treatment Plan Transactions option is selected, all unsubmitted transactions�actual and treatment plan�are included in the report. If the Include Treatment Plan Transactions option is not selected, only actual unsubmitted transactions are included in the report. See Running the Unsubmitted Claims Report for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Office Expert Item Priorities
topics.body: Setting Office Expert Item Priorities Using Experts > Using the Office Expert > Setting Up Office Expert Items Setting Office Expert Item Priorities Each office expert item is displayed in the Office Expert window in order of priority, enabling you to quickly assess the importance of each item. You can set the priority of each item to suit the needs of your practice: Critical items : Displayed first. Urgent items : Displayed after critical items. Normal items : Displayed last. If the security system is enabled, you must have the appropriate permission to perform this task. If the proper permissions are not set and you want to edit the criteria of an office expert item, see your software administrator. To set office expert item priority: From the Experts menu, select Office Expert . The Office Expert window is displayed. Select Setup Experts from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Office Expert Setup window is displayed. Select the item from the Active Office Expert Entries list. Click Set Priority . The Office Expert Priority window is displayed. Select the desired priority for the item, and click OK twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Operating Modes
topics.body: Understanding Operating Modes Getting Started Understanding Operating Modes The software functions in different modes depending on what operation you are performing. Example : When you enter a new record, the software is operating in ADD mode. When you search for a record, the program operates in FIND mode. When you modify a record, the program operates in EDIT mode. The modes are: SCAN mode : The default mode for all data entry windows. You can view, but not edit, the information in the window. FIND mode : Used with the Find feature and enables you to search for and view records. ADD mode : Used after you click Add and enables you to enter new records. After you add the record or cancel the operation, the program returns to SCAN mode. EDIT mode : Used after you click Edit and enables you to modify existing records. After you edit the record or cancel the operation, the program returns to SCAN mode. The current operating mode is indicated in the lower-right corner of the Status bar. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Setting Daily Operatory Schedule Report Privacy System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features > Using the HIPAA Privacy Feature Setting Daily Operatory Schedule Report Privacy You can enable HIPAA Privacy when generating the Daily Operatory Schedule report. You can also customize how patient names and phone numbers are displayed when generating the report. The Daily Operatory Schedule Properties window is displayed. Click the Appt tab. Select the report template from the Report Template drop-down list. By default, the patient initials are displayed on the first line of the appointment time slot, and the patient home and work phone numbers are not displayed when either the Front Office - HIPAA or Back Office - HIPAA template is selected. The Daily Operatory Schedule Report window is displayed. Select the HIPAA template from the Use Daily OP Template drop-down list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Organizing Documents in the Document Tree
topics.body: Organizing Documents in the Document Tree Using the Document Manager Organizing Documents in the Document Tree You can use several grouping and sorting options to organize the documents in the document tree. In addition, you can customize the way documents are saved and organized. The following grouping and sorting options are available: Group By Category: This option groups documents by category (for example, RVG, Misc Patient Documents, and so on). The category folders display in the tree based on the order specified in the Customize Document Categories window. Several categories are predefined in the software, but you can also add categories of your own. Documents that are automatically archived (letters, billing statements, HIPAA acknowledgements, and so on) are automatically assigned a category. You can assign any category you wish to documents that you scan or import. See Customizing Document Categories for more information. Group By Date Acquired: This option groups documents by the date documents are created, enabling you to see every image or document that is saved for a patient on a specific date. All images and documents created on the same day are placed in the same date folder, followed by the document category folder. Sort By Date Acquired: This option enables you to sort images or documents within a category by the date on which the documents or images are created. Documents are sorted by date in descending order. The creation date and time, followed by the document description, are displayed for each document. Sort By Description: This option enables you to sort images or documents within a category alphabetically by description. The document description, followed by creation date and time, are displayed for each document. Related Topics Customizing Document Categories Changing a Document Category Document Tree Icons Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Delete Orphaned Patients and Transactions Utility
topics.body: Running the Delete Orphaned Patients and Transactions Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Delete Orphaned Patients and Transactions Utility Run this utility to delete orphaned entries from the patient, account, or transaction data files. Example : If you delete a patient�s record but do not delete the patient�s transactions, those transactions will reference a patient ID that no longer exists. These transactions become orphaned. Although orphaned entries do not affect your daily operations, they can alter report information. Save a complete backup in addition to your regular backup before running this utility. Do not run this utility unless you are instructed to by a technical support representative. To delete orphaned patients and transactions: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Delete Orphaned Patients and Transactions . A message informs you to save a backup before running this utility. Click OK . A message asks if you saved a backup. Click Yes . The Deleting Orphans window is displayed. When Complete is displayed in the Current Action field, click OK . When the technical support representative tells you to, restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Setting Up Ortho Codes Planning Budgets > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Managing Ortho Cases Setting Up Ortho Codes The software automatically prompts you to create an ortho insurance case when you post a transaction for an orthodontic production code. By default, codes 8000.00 through 8999.99 are ortho codes. To exclude an ortho code (8000 range) from the ortho insurance budget plan: Select an option: From the List menu, select ADA Codes . In any window, press Ctrl + C. Select ADA Code List Window from the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. The ADA/Transaction Codes List window is displayed in FIND mode. Select the code from the list. To exclude the code from the list of ortho insurance budget plan codes, deselect Ortho Code . Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? topics.body: Running the Ortho Insurance Details Report Budget Planning > Running Ortho Reports Running the Ortho Insurance Details Report The Ortho Insurance Details report d etails the insurance information for all ortho insurance cases. Sample Report To run the Ortho Insurance Details report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Ortho Insurance > Detail . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Orthodontic Report Setup window is displayed. Select an option: To restrict the report to a specific set of patients : Select the starting and ending patient identification numbers from the drop-down lists. To restrict the report to a specific patient user code : Type it in the Include field. To exclude a specific patient user code : Type it in the Exclude field. To include inactive patients in the report: Click the Include Inactive Patients checkbox. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resolving Overlapped Appointments
topics.body: Resolving Overlapped Appointments Scheduling Appointments > Rescheduling Appointments Resolving Overlapped Appointments You create an overlapped appointment when there is a provider scheduling conflict, the color scheduling requirements are not met, or there are not enough available Scheduler timeslots. To resolve an overlapped appointment: In the Daily Appointments window, select the overlapped appointment. To display the appointment card, select the appointment, right-click, and select View Appt . The appointment details are displayed in the Canceled Appt window. Review the details and click Close . To resolve the overlapped appointment, do one of the following: To reschedule the appointment, select the appointment, drag it to the new appointment time, and release the mouse. To add the appointment to the Tickler file, select the appointment, right-click, and select Place in Tickler . To add the appointment to the Short Call Tickler file, select the appointment, right-click, and select Place in Short Call Tickler . Tip To scan the Short Call Tickler or Tickler file, select Tickler from the Express bar. You can place an appointment in the Tickler files from this menu. To autoschedule the appointment, select the appointment, right-click, and select AutoSchedule Appt . Select the start and end dates and click OK . The first available slot is displayed. Find the correct slot and select Make Appt . To display the appointment card, select the appointment, right-click, and select View Appt . To delete the appointment, select the appointment, right-click, and click Delete . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: PracticeToGo and the Palm OS Interface
topics.body: PracticeToGo and the Palm OS Interface Using PracticeToGo PracticeToGo and the Palm OS Interface The SoftDent application that runs on the Palm-connected organizer adds a few icons to the normal Palm interface, but most of the data that is transferred from the PC SoftDent software to the Palm organizer is displayed using existing Palm features. To access appointments and practice financials: Open the Date Book. To access referring doctor information, end-of-day call-backs, the pharmacy list, and prescription information: Open the Address Book. To access memos: Open the Memo Pad. You can reach any of this information by going directly to the Palm application or by tapping the SoftDent icon and selecting from the menu that is displayed. The transfer of data from the Palm-connected organizer to the PC SoftDent software is accomplished through the SD Commands. The SD Commands are located on the SoftDent - Palm Application main menu. When you insert a SoftDent Date Book, Address, or Memo command on an entry, specific information will be sent back to the PC SoftDent software during the next synchronization. Related Topics Viewing SoftDent Data on the Palm Organizer Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Percentage Plans
topics.body: Understanding Percentage Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Understanding Percentage Plans Percentage or indemnity plans pay a percentage of the cost to perform a procedure. These percentages are defined for each procedural class. For example, a plan might pay 50 percent of all preventive work completed on a covered patient. For more information, see Estimating Insurance for Percentage Plans . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Perio Chart Options and Properties
topics.body: Setting Perio Chart Options and Properties Periodontal Charting Setting Perio Chart Options and Properties To set periodontal charting options and properties: On the Perio tab in the Chart for window, select Perio > Options . The Perio Properties window is displayed. In the Red line - mm section, type the red line values. In the Show section, select the checkboxes for the measurement types you want to display. In the Draw section, select whether you want to display a Bar Chart, a Line Chart, or Both. In the Data Display section, select how you want the data displayed on the chart. For more information, see Using the Perio Views Toolbar . In the Data Entry section, select how you want the entries to be recorded. In the Electronic Probe section, use the drop-down lists to select the probe and Com Port. You must attach the unit to a communications port on your computer before setting this option. See the manufacturer�s information to calibrate and use the probe. In the Keystroke Options section, select which method to use to navigate through the chart. If you use voice recognition, select the voice type from the Voice Response drop-down list. To set the chart and background colors, click the Colors tab. Click the colored button next to Pocket Depth : The Colors window is displayed. Select the color and click OK . Repeat for each measurement type. To select the background color : Click the colored button next to Background Color. The Colors window is displayed. Select the color, and click OK . To restore the software�s default colors : Click Restore Default Colors . To set the default pockets to measure and the tooth paths, click the Path tab, and select an option: To set the default pockets to measure : Select the information from the Pockets To Measure section of the window. To set the default tooth paths : Select the information from the Tooth Paths section of the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Perio Conditions Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Perio Conditions Toolbar Periodontal Charting > Using Perio Tab Toolbars Using the Perio Conditions Toolbar The Perio Conditions toolbar commands enable you to chart periodontal conditions. Click the icon for the description of the Perio Conditions toolbar buttons. Click this button and select a tooth to chart a periapical condition . Click this button and indicate the missing teeth . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Exams - Viewing and Comparing Perio Exams
topics.body: Charting Exams - Viewing and Comparing Perio Exams Charting Exams Viewing and Comparing Perio Exams To view and compare perio exams: In the Chart for window, click the Perio tab. Click Select periodontal exams to view and compare on the Perio Views toolbar . The View Perio Exams window is displayed. By default the date of the most recent exam is displayed in the View Exam drop-down list. Select the exam to view from the View Exam drop-down list. Select the exam to compare from the Compare With drop-down list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Using the View Perio History Chart Periodontal Charting > Understanding Perio Tab Modes Using the View Perio History Chart When the perio history chart is selected, you can: Display a saved periodontal chart associated with the patient Use this information to track treatment progress To view the perio numeric mode: Select View Perio History on the Perio Views toolbar . Select the dates associated with the charts to view. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Personal Payments
topics.body: Posting Personal Payments Posting Payments > Posting Account Payments Posting Personal Payments When your office receives a single cash, credit card, or check payment toward the balances of all patients in an account , y ou can post the payment directly to the account from the patient or account record. You can distribute the payment to each patient with an outstanding balance, based on the age of the transaction or by percentage of outstanding balance. To post a personal payment to an account: Select an option: In the Account List window : Select the account and select Payments . In the Account window : Select Payments . In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Transactions . The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments . In the Patient window : Select Transactions . The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Select Personal in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Personal Payment window is displayed. Select an option: To post a check payment : Type the check amount, check number, and bank number and verify the check date. To post a cash payment : Select the code from the Code drop-down list, type the cash payment amount, and verify the date. To post a credit card payment : Select the code from the Code drop-down list. Swipe the card. Select an option: To distribute the payment amount to transactions by the transaction creation date : Click By Age . The oldest transaction is paid off first. To distribute the payment amount evenly to all transactions : Click Evenly . Select an option to edit amount or percentage payment distributions: To edit the amount of a specific transaction and to display the Amount column for each transaction : Click Amount . Select the amount and type the correct distribution amount. To edit the percentage for a specific transaction and to display the % column for each transaction : Click Percentage . Select the percentage and type the correct distribution percentage. To add a note to a transaction, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Click Post . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Performing Occlusal Exams
topics.body: Performing Occlusal Exams Charting Exams > Understanding Exams Tab Exams Performing Occlusal Exams To perform an occlusal exam: On the Exams tab in the Charting for window, click Occlusal Exam on the Patient Exams toolbar . If no occlusal exam exists for the patient, skip to step 3. Select an option: To create a new exam : Click New Exam on the Exam Options toolbar . To edit the existing exam : Click Edit Exam on the Exam Options toolbar . Select an option: To indicate there are no occlusal conditions : Click All OK . Select the anterior teeth conditions in the Anterior Teeth section. Select the posterior teeth conditions in the Posterior Teeth section. Type the overjet amount in the Horizontal (Overjet) field : You can use the numerical keypad to enter information in this field. Type the overbite amount in the Vertical (Overbite) field : You can use the numerical keypad to enter information in this field. Type the open bite amount in the Open Bite field : You can use the numerical keypad to enter information in this field. Select the occlusion class in the Occlusion section. Click Save Exam . To display the next exam, click Next Exam . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Performing Periodontal Tissue Exams
topics.body: Performing Periodontal Tissue Exams Charting Exams > Understanding Exams Tab Exams Performing Periodontal Tissue Exams To perform a periodontal tissue exam: On the Exams tab in the Charting for window, click Perio Tissue Exam on the Patient Exams toolbar . If no periodontal tissue exam exists for the patient, skip to step 3. Select an option: To create a new exam : Click New Exam on the Exam Options toolbar . To edit the existing exam : Click Edit Exam on the Exam Options toolbar . Select an option: To indicate normal tissue color, contour, texture, and consistency : Click All OK . The appropriate options are selected. Select the tissue color in the Color section. Select the tissue contour in the Contour section. Select the tissue texture in the Texture section. Select the tissue consistency in the Consistency section. Click Save Exam . To display the next exam, click Next Exam . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Performing PSR Exams
topics.body: Performing PSR Exams Charting Exams > Understanding Exams Tab Exams Performing PSR Exams To perform a PSR exam: On the Exams tab in the Charting for window, click Perio Screening Exam on the Patient Exams toolbar . If no PSR exam exists for the patient, skip to step 3. Select an option: To create a new exam : Click New Exam on the Exam Options toolbar . To edit the existing exam : Click Edit Exam on the Exam Options toolbar . Select an option: To indicate the colored area of the probe remains visible in the deepest crevice of the sextant : Select 0 from the appropriate sextant drop-down list. To indicate bleeding after probing : Select 1 from the appropriate sextant drop-down list. To indicate supragingival or subgingival calculus or defective margins : Select 2 from the appropriate sextant drop-down list. To indicate the colored area of the probe remains partly visible in the deepest crevice of the sextant : Select 3 from the appropriate sextant drop-down list. To indicate a probing depth greater than 5.5 mm : Select 4 from the appropriate sextant drop-down list. To add a note to a sextant : Click the appropriate S # and Note button. Then type the note in the field beneath the buttons. Click Save Exam . To display the next exam, click Next Exam . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Performing Soft Tissue Exams
topics.body: Performing Soft Tissue Exams Charting Exams > Understanding Exams Tab Exams Performing Soft Tissue Exams When the soft tissue exam is selected , y ou can chart oral hygiene, gingival bleeding, and the condition of all soft tissues. To perform a soft tissue exam: On the Exams tab in the Charting for window, click Soft Tissue Exam on the Patient Exams toolbar . If no soft tissue exam exists for the patient, skip to step 3. Select an option: To create a new exam : Click New Exam on the Exam Options toolbar . To edit the existing exam : Click Edit Exam on the Exam Options toolbar . Select an option: To indicate no soft tissue pathology, excellent oral hygiene, no calculus, and no gingival bleeding : Click All OK . The appropriate options are selected. Select the appropriate checkbox to indicate soft tissue information in the Soft Tissue section. Select the oral hygiene level in the Oral Hygiene section. Select the calculus level in the Calculus section. Select the gingival bleeding amount in the Gingival Bleeding section. Type any notes in the Comments section. Click Save Exam . To display the next exam, click Next Exam . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Performing a Search from the File Menu
topics.body: Performing a Search from the File Menu Getting Started > Searching for Records Using the File Menu to Search for a Record To search for a record using the File menu: From the File menu, select the menu item corresponding to the record type. The Find window for the selected menu item is displayed. Type the search criteria in the top half of the window. Select the search method from the Find By drop-down list. See Record Search Methods for a list of search methods for each record type. Click OK . The matching record is displayed in SCAN mode. Example : To find the account record for Jane Doe, select Find > Account . The Find Account window is displayed. Type Doe in the G1 Last name field, select Name from the Find By drop-down list, and click OK . The account record for Jane Doe is displayed in SCAN mode. Tip If the correct record is not displayed, use the arrow buttons at the top of the window to display the next or previous record. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Performing a Search from a List Window
topics.body: Performing a Search from a List Window Getting Started > Searching for Records Using the List Menu to Search for a Record To search for a record using the List menu: From the List menu, select the menu item that corresponds to the record type you want to find. The appropriate list window is displayed. Select the search method from the Sort By section. See Record Search Methods for a list of search methods for each record type. Type the search criteria in the Find field. The results of your search are displayed, and the matching record is highlighted. To display the record, double-click it. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Performing a Search from the Power Bar Getting Started > Searching for Records Using the Power Bar to Search for a Record To search for a record using the Power Bar: From the Power bar, select the button that corresponds to the record type you want to find. The appropriate list window is displayed. Select the search method from the Sort By section. See Record Search Methods for a list of search methods for each record type. topics.title: Performing TMJ Exams
topics.body: Performing TMJ Exams Charting Exams > Understanding Exams Tab Exams Performing TMJ Exams To perform a TMJ exam: On the Exams tab in the Charting for window, click TMJ Exam on the Patient Exams toolbar . If no TMJ exam exists for the patient, skip to step 3. Select an option: To create a new exam : Click New Exam on the Exam Options toolbar . To edit the existing exam : Click Edit Exam on the Exam Options toolbar . Select an option: To indicate there are no TMJ conditions : Click All OK . Select the TMJ conditions from the top of the tab. Select the tenderness to palpation in the Tenderness to palpation section. Select the anterior guidance in the Adequate Anterior Guidance section. Type the deviation on closing in the Deviation on Closing field : You can use the numerical keypad to enter information in this field. Type the deviation on opening in the Deviation on Opening field : You can use the numerical keypad to enter information in this field. Type the lateral movement in the first Lateral Movement field : You can use the numerical keypad to enter information in this field. Type the maximum opening in the first Maximum Opening field : You can use the numerical keypad to enter information in this field. Click Save Exam . To display the next exam, click Next Exam . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Comparing Provider Goals to Provider Production
topics.body: Comparing Provider Goals to Provider Production Billing and Receivables > Handling Receivables Comparing Provider Goals to Provider Production You can compare a provider�s actual production to his or her goal. You must set up goal scheduling to compare production and goal information. For more information, see Setting Up Color and Goal Scheduling . To compare actual production to a goal: Select List > View Daily RCVTOT records > Goals/Prod > Provider Goals . The Select Provider Goals window is displayed. Select the provider from the drop-down list and click OK . If a production has both a production and collection doctor, receivables totals are only calculated for the collection doctor. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Purge Audit Trail Records Utility
topics.body: Running the Purge Audit Trail Records Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Purge Audit Trail Records Utility Run t his utility to purge unwanted information from the audit trail file by date range, information type, or provider. The audit trail file contains transactions, prescriptions, charting, security, scheduling information, report information, archived transactions, and Palm records. Over time, the file becomes large, and some of the information may no longer be needed. You must have the appropriate security rights to delete each type of data to run this utility. To purge audit trail files: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software> SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Purge Audit Records . The Audit Trail Purge Dialog window is displayed. Select the record type to delete from the Type of Record section of the window. Select the date range between which to delete records from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To purge files by production doctor : Select Production Dr and type the provider identification numbers in the Product Dr List section of the window. To purge files by collection doctor : Select Collection Dr and type the provider identification numbers in the Collection Dr List section of the window. Click OK . A message informs you that the selected records have been deleted. Click OK . Restart the software. Deleting Audit Trails Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Pharmacies by ID Report
topics.body: Running the Pharmacies by ID Report Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Running Pharmacy and Prescription Reports Running the Pharmacies by ID Report The Pharmacies by ID report generates a list of pharmacies sorted by ID. The report includes the pharmacy ID, name, mailing address, telephone numbers, and contact person. Sample Report To run the Pharmacies by ID report: Select Reports > RX/Pharmacies/Labs > Pharmacies by ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Pharmacy by ID Report Setup window is displayed. In the Pharmacy IDs section, click the drop-down arrows in the Start and End fields to select pharmacy identification numbers from a list. To include inactive pharmacies in the report, click the Include Inactive Pharmacies checkbox. To use color/shading in the report, select the Include Color/Shading checkbox. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Phone Center Window
topics.body: Using the Phone Center Window Using the Phone Center Using the Phone Center Window In the Phone Center window, you can perform several functions. The information available from each button in the Phone Center window: Information : Select a patient or account and click this button to display the Patient or Account window. You can view and edit patient or account information. Insurance Info : Select a patient and click this button to display the Patient Guarantors window. You can view, add, edit, and delete patient guarantor and insurance information. Scheduling : Select a patient and click this button to display the Patient Appointments window. You can view a list of appointments and schedule appointments. Prescription : Select a patient and click this button to display the Prescriptions For List window. You can view a list of drugs that have been or are currently prescribed for the patient. Contacts : Select a patient or account and click this button to display the Contacts for List window. You can view a list of contacts for the patient or account. New Patient : Select a patient or account and click this button to display the Daily Appointments book. You can add a new patient appointment. Transactions : Select a patient or account and click this button to display the Patient Transactions List or Account Transactions Lists window. You can view the transaction history for the patient or account. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!
