topics.title: Inserting Placeholders into Case Blueprints and ADA Procedure Presentations
topics.body: Inserting Placeholders into Case Blueprints and ADA Procedure Presentations Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint Inserting Placeholders into Case Blueprints and ADA Procedure Presentations You can insert the following information placeholders into a case blueprint or ADA procedure presentation: Patient portrait Name Address Phone number Chart Images Financial ADA code When the patient presentation is assembled : The placeholder is replaced with the appropriate patient data. You can insert placeholders for: Full or quadrant views of the current and treatment plans restorative chart Presentation manager images Full or quadrant views of the perio chart Perio numeric history chart Most recent: Third-party images Miscellaneous images Extraoral images Intraoral images Primary and secondary treatment plans Both treatment plans Summary financial information To insert a placeholder into a case blueprint or ADA procedure presentation: While viewing the case blueprint or ADA procedure presentation, select the placeholder from the Insert > SoftDent Placeholders menu. Move the placeholder to the appropriate location. Click Save and close the PowerPoint software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Planning Budgets
topics.body: Planning Budgets Budget Planning Budget plans are used for accounts or patients who find it difficult to pay their balance in full. Budget plan statements are sent monthly, in addition to regular account statements. You can set up three different types of budget plans: Account fixed loan budget plan : Applying an account balance or amount to a fixed loan budget plan enables you to define the amount, interest rate, and number of payments for the loan. Account revolving credit budget plan : Applying an account balance or amount to a revolving credit budget plan enables you to define a credit limit, interest rate, and what portion of the balance is due each month for the loan. This plan is similar to using a credit card. Before you set up this type of plan, make sure that it is in compliance with state law. Patient budget plan : Applying a patient procedural balance to a budget plan enables you to set up a regular payment schedule for the patient. Using patient budget plans for orthodontics enable you to create an orthodontics insurance case to track claim re-submissions. The charges are posted monthly. Patient budget plans do not accrue finance charges. Y ou can set up recurring credit card payments and automatically submit monthly credit card payments toward fixed loan account and patient budget plan balances. Related Topics Handling Account Budget Plans Handling Patient Budget Plans Creating Credit Card Payment Plans Running Budget Plan Reports Running Ortho Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Plan Information
topics.body: Plan Information Trojan > Managed Care Plans > Managed Care Plan Fees Plan Information The Plan Information section contains the following fields: Plan ID : Displays the plan's Trojan ID number. Code : Displays the ADA code for the procedure being viewed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Payment Allocation
topics.body: Understanding Payment Allocation Billing and Receivables Allocating Payments Payment allocation enables you to: Specify which providers get paid for completed procedures Assign payment to any provider Example : You can specify that revenues from all providers are allocated to one provider. You can also set up payment allocation so that revenues from hygienists go to the doctor performing the exams, or that providers receive payment for whatever work they personally perform. To allocate all revenues to the practice, you must add a provider to your database using the name of your practice and use its identification number for payment allocation. Related Topics Setting Up Payment Allocation Changing Payment Allocation While Posting Transactions Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Print Current Transaction History Utility
topics.body: Running the Print Current Transaction History Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running the Transaction History Maintenance Utilities Running the Print Current Transaction History Utility Run this utility to view or print compressed transactions. The transaction history includes the patient�s ID, transaction date, procedure code, service provider, collecting provider, tooth and surface (if applicable), and transaction amount. To print the transaction history: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Transaction History Maintenance > Print Current Transaction History . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Painting the Daybook
topics.body: Painting the Daybook Setting Up the Scheduler Painting the Daybook Painting the Daybook enables you to see which procedures should be scheduled during, or what production amount is targeted for any time slot. You can paint or mark color books. Colors are also visible in the By Book (Book-At-A-Glance) window. To paint the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance windows: Select Scheduler > Setup Goals and Colors . The Month At A Glance (GOAL) window is displayed. Select an option: Press Alt + D. Select Default Daybook from the drop-down list at the top of the window. The Daily Appointments (Goal) window is displayed. The provider identification number is displayed in the appropriate time slots. Patient names and procedures are not displayed in this view. Select an option: To paint or mark the Daily Appointments window by provider : Select Select Provider from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Provider List window is displayed. Double-click a provider. The Daily Appointments (Goal) window is displayed. When Daybook time slots are painted or marked by provider, the identification number and color associated with the provider are displayed in the specified time slots. Painting or marking by provider enables the auto scheduler to use color scheduling, when searching for time slots for a provider. To paint or mark the Daily Appointments window by practice : Select Select Practice from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Daily Appointments (Goal) window is displayed. Example : To indicate that room number one is used exclusively for hygiene procedures, select Select Practice , select Choose Color , and select the color scheduling group definition assigned to hygiene procedures from the list. Click Paint . The hygiene color is displayed in all time slots for room number one. To associate a color scheduling group definition with either the practice or a provider, select Choose Color from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Scheduling Color Definitions window is displayed. Double-click a color group definition. The Daily Appointments (Goal) window is displayed. Select an option: To mark one time slot with the selected color group definition : Select the time slot and select Mark from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To paint a group of time slots or the entire day with the selected color group definition : Select the first time slot and select Paint from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The selected scheduling color group definitions are displayed in all office books and the Book At A Glance window. If the selected color group definition is applied by provider, the provider identification number is displayed in the appropriate time slots. If the selected color group definition is applied by practice, 0 is displayed in the appropriate time slots. Click Close . Tip After you have customized your color group definitions, you can apply them to your Daily Appointments (Daybook) window by practice or provider. To prevent painting over time slots that have been marked, color-code the book from top to bottom. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Adjustments
topics.body: Posting Adjustments Posting Payments > Posting Account Payments Posting Adjustments Post a collection or production adjustment instead of editing and deleting transactions to: Maintain an accurate and detailed history of the payments Ensure the daysheet and deposit slip reflect accurate transaction posting Related Topics Posting Collection Adjustments Posting Production Adjustments Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a Cash Payment to a Patient Budget Plan
topics.body: Posting a Cash Payment to a Patient Budget Plan Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Posting Payments to Ortho Claims Posting a Cash Payment to a Patient Budget Plan To post a cash payment to a patient budget plan: In the Patient Transactions List window, click ADD . The Patient Transactions window is displayed in ADD mode. Select the cash payment code from the Code drop-down list. Select the production doctor from the Production Dr drop-down list. Select the collection doctor from the Collection Dr drop-down list. The Transactions - Cash Payment window is displayed. Select the payment amount from the Amount drop-down list. Type any notes in the Notes field, and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a Credit Card Payment to a Patient Budget Plan
topics.body: Posting a Credit Card Payment to a Patient Budget Plan Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Posting Payments to Ortho Claims Posting a Credit Card Payment to a Patient Budget Plan To post a credit card payment to a patient budget plan: In the Patient Transactions List window, click ADD . The Patient Transactions window is displayed in ADD mode. Select the credit card payment code from the Code drop-down list. Select the production doctor from the Production Dr drop-down list. Select the collection doctor from the Collection Dr drop-down list. The Transactions - Visa/Mastercard Payment window is displayed. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a Payment to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers
topics.body: Posting a Payment to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers eServices > Using the ePayments Service Posting a Payment to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers If you are using ePayments and have your credit card processing set to Enabled for Providers, you can post one credit card payment per billing provider with a single card swipe. To post a credit card payment for multiple providers: Select an option: In the Account List window: Select the account and click Transactions . In the Account window : Click Transactions . In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Click Personal in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Personal Payment window is displayed. Select the credit card code from the Code drop-down list and press Tab . Note the Amounts to be charged and Dr. # section that is displayed to the right of the Payment Information section. In the bottom section of the window, type the payment amount for each patient with an outstanding balance. Note that payment amounts for the same doctor are reflected in the Amounts to be charged section displayed to the right of the Payment Information section. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Click Post . The Carestream ePayments Capture Credit Card window is displayed. Swipe the card, or manually enter the information and click OK . A print preview of the receipt is automatically displayed for each provider being paid. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a Refund to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers
topics.body: Posting a Refund to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers eServices > Using the ePayments Service Posting a Refund to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers If you are using ePayments and have your credit card processing set to Enabled for Providers, you can post one refund per billing provider with one card swipe. To post a refund for multiple providers: Select an option: In the Account List window : Select the account and click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Payments . In the Account window : Click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Payments . In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Payments . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Click Refunds in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Refund Credits window is displayed. The default credit refund code is displayed in the Code drop-down list. The total in the Amount field is updated as you add each refund amount to the list. Note the Amounts to be refunded section for each billing doctor is displayed to the right of the Payment Information section. In the bottom section of the window, type the refund amount for each patient due a refund. Note that refund amounts for the same doctor are reflected in the Amounts to be refunded section displayed to the right of the Payment Information section. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Click Post . The Carestream ePayments Refund Authorization window is displayed. The Amount field is populated with the refund amount for the first provider. The Carestream ePayments Refund Authorization window is displayed for each billing provider. Type the card holder name, account number, expiration date, and zip code information and click OK . The Carestream ePayments Refund Authorization window is displayed, and the Amount field is populated with the refund amount for the second provider. Since the refund is going on the same credit card, click Last to use the credit card information you typed for the first provider, and click OK . Repeat for all remaining refunds. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting an Insurance Credit Card Payment Using the Card Swiper
topics.body: Posting an Insurance Credit Card Payment Using the Card Swiper Posting Payments > Posting Insurance Payments > Posting Credit Card Insurance Payments Posting an Insurance Credit Card Payment Using the Card Swiper Before you add a credit card payment, you must install the card swiper. See Installing the Card Swiper . To post an insurance credit card payment using the card swiper: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Transactions from the toolbar. In the Patient window : Select Transactions from the toolbar. The patient Transactions List window is displayed. In the patient Transactions List window, click Add . The Transactions window is displayed. Select the insurance credit card payment code from the Code drop-down list, and press Tab. The Transactions - Insurance Payment window is displayed. Type the payment amount, and press Tab. In the Claim No field, select the claim from the drop-down list, and click OK . The Update Bluebook? message is displayed. Click Yes . The Update Bluebook Entry window is displayed. Complete the bluebook information, and click OK . The SoftDent Software ePayments Charge Authorization window is displayed. Swipe the credit card. In the CVD Number field, type the credit card verification number. If you do not enter a CVD number, a message requesting the number is displayed. To ignore this message, click OK . Click Submit . After you receive an authorization number, add notes, if necessary. To print a receipt, click OK at the prompt. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Credit Card Insurance Payments
topics.body: Posting Credit Card Insurance Payments Posting Payments > Posting Insurance Payments Posting Credit Card Insurance Payments You can post a payment using a single-use credit card from an insurance company as you would a regular credit card. Insurance credit card payments can be posted using the card swiper, or you can post the payment manually, if a swiper is not available. The following codes have been added to the ADA code list for new offices (or offices who have not renamed codes): 11.93 MasterCard Insurance Payment 12.93 VISA Insurance Payment 13.93 Discover Insurance Payment 14.93 American Express Insurance Payment For offices that are using renamed credit card codes, the first available extension, starting with .93, will be used. For example: If your office uses code 17 for VISA, and you are not using code 17.93, the system will assign code 17.93 as the VISA Insurance Payment code for your practice. If code 17.93 is already in use, the next available extension (.94) is used, and so on. If your office has used all of the reserved extensions for a particular credit card type, a message is displayed, stating that you must manually set up the code � first in the ADA/Transactions Code List window and then in the Practice Information window. Only codes entered in the Insurance Payment by section on the Default ADA tab of the Practice Information window are available for credit card processing and claim attachment. Related Topics Posting an Insurance Credit Card Payment Using the Card Swiper Manually Posting an Insurance Credit Card Payment Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Prepayments
topics.body: Posting Prepayments Posting Payments > Posting Account Payments Posting Prepayments Post prepayments when patients or insurance companies send payments for procedures not yet performed. Example : John Doe agrees to a treatment plan totaling $500 and wants to pay for the procedures before scheduling an appointment. Post a $500 prepayment to his account. Each time a procedure is posted, the balance is decreased and the collections increased. To post a prepayment to an account or patient record: Do one of the following: In the Account window: Select Payments . In the Patient window: Select Transactions . The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Select Pre-Pay/Adjust in the Select Operation section of the window. The Payments - PrePayment or Adjustment window is displayed. Select the payment code from the Code drop-down list. Type the payment amount in the Amount column. To add a note to a transaction, select the Notes column, and type the information. Click Post . Code 98 is posted to the patient receiving the debit and code 96 to the patient receiving the credit. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Payments
topics.body: Posting Payments Posting Payments This topic explains how to post the following payments: Patient Account Related Topics Posting Patient Payments Posting Account Payments Posting Insurance Payments Posting Unassigned Plan Payments Handling Discounts Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Refunds
topics.body: Posting Refunds Posting Payments > Posting Account Payments Posting Refunds To post a refund to an account or patient record: Select an option: In the Account List window : Select the account and select Payments . In the Account window : Select Payments . In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Transactions . The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments . In the Patient window : Select Transactions . The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Select Refunds in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Refund Credits window is displayed. The default credit refund code is displayed in the Code drop-down list. The total for transactions displayed in the window is displayed in the Amount field. Type the refund amount associated with each transaction in the appropriate Amount column. To add a note to a transaction, select the Notes column for the transaction, and type the information. Click Post . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Power Bar
topics.body: Using the Power Bar Getting Started > Using the Workspace Using the Power Bar The Power bar: Can be positioned to display beneath the Main Menu bar or anywhere within the client area Provides one-click access to the most commonly used features of the application Use the Power bar to access the following windows: See Power Bar Buttons for details on the Power bar buttons. Patient List Account List Daily Appointments InOffice Progress Towards Goals Register Setup Insurance Payments Today's Memos Memos SoftDent E-mail Insurance Plans List Claim List Web_sites List Labs List Pharmacy List ADA/Transaction Codes List Referring Doctor List Batch File Form Summary Phone Center Team Talk - note settings Practice Central Time Clock Login Time Clock Logout From the Power bar, you can also: Access eServices Open the Microsoft Word software Run the Daily Scheduled report Generate the Electronic Statements report Switch log-ins Run the rollover insurance plans utility Access Trojan information Access CareCredit Related Topics Power Bar Buttons Viewing or Hiding the Power Bar Changing the Power Bar Location Adding and Removing Power Bar Buttons Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing a Payment Plan Consent Form
topics.body: Printing a Payment Plan Consent Form Budget Planning > Creating Credit Card Payment Plans Printing a Payment Plan Consent Form The Payment Plan Consent form is accessed from the Credit Card Payment Plan window and authorizes your office to keep a patient�s signature on file and charge his credit card on a recurring basis. The consent form is generated in two ways: The software prompts you to print a consent form immediately after you create or edit a payment plan. You must print the form before you can continue. If you have edited a payment plan, a modified version of the form is printed, including a new payment schedule. You can print the consent form at any time once a payment plan is created. The payment plan consent form is archived whenever it is printed and is displayed in the document tree for the patient or account. The document is labeled as Payment Plan Consent Form . If the consent form was printed because a user edited the payments, the text reads Payment Plan Consent Form (Modified) . To print an existing consent form: In the Account List or Patient List window, select a record and click Credit Card Payment Plan . The Credit Card Payment Plan window is displayed. Click Consent Form . The Output Options�Payment Plan Consent Form window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . Two copies of the consent form�an Office Copy and a Cardholder Copy�are printed. The consent form will not print if a payment plan is not set up. Additional information: The consent form lists the Monthly Payment, Last Payment, Start Date, Number of Payments, Financed Amount, and Down Payment. Each payment is displayed on a separate line. Payments are sorted in ascending order. The date to be authorized (or date authorized) and the amount are displayed for each payment. If a payment has already been authorized, the text (Completed) is displayed after the payment. If a payment has been voided, the text (Voided) is displayed after the payment. The payment amount is $0.00 . If a payment has failed authorization, the text (Failed) is displayed after the payment. The payment amount is the amount for which the payment failed to be authorized. If the form is being reprinted because the payment plan terms have been modified: The title reads PAYMENT PLAN CONSENT FORM (MODIFIED) . The text $XX.XX per payment on a monthly basis is replaced with unequal monthly payments . The note This is a modification from the original payment plan terms created on mm/dd/yy is displayed. Payment Schedule is changed to New Payment Schedule . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Preferred Provider (PPO) Plans
topics.body: Understanding Preferred Provider (PPO) Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Understanding Preferred Provider (PPO) Plans Some PPO plans pay: Participating providers based on the plan�s allowed amounts. A flat fee per procedure. A percentage of the allowed amount. Write off the difference between the plan�s allowed amount and the practice fee. Typically, you can bill the patient the difference between the insurance payment and the insurance plan�s allowed amount. You can also automatically handle any risk and development write-off required by a PPO. The write-off: Is subtracted from the claim estimated insurance payment. Does not change the allowed amounts for the plan. Does not affect your regular write-offs or patient copays. To estimate insurance payments for flat fee PPO plans: Set up accurate payment values in the bluebook for all procedures. If there is no bluebook entry for the flat fee PPO insurance plan, use the percentages specified on the Coverage tab of the Insurance plan window. Specify whether a deductible applies to each procedural class when entering dental PPO plans. Tip If the plan pays something other than a percentage of your fees, set up bluebook entries. If the fees equal the percentage plan allotted amount, you do not need to set up a bluebook entry. Related Topics Estimating Insurance for PPO Percentage Plans Estimating Insurance for Flat Fee PPO Plans Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting PPO Payments with Write-Offs
topics.body: Posting PPO Payments with Write-Offs Posting Payments > Posting Insurance Payments Posting PPO Payments with Write-Offs You can post PPO insurance payments in the Insurance Payments window. Write-offs are automatically posted with the claim payment. To post a PPO payment with a write-off: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > Bulk Insurance Payments . The Insurance Check Info window is displayed in EDIT mode. Select the insurance company, insurance plan, and transaction code from the drop-down lists. If the payment includes a DMO/capitation plan service fee, enter the plan, plan fee, and plan doctor information to display the plan fee in the correct plan Transactions window. The plan fee is subtracted from the check amount. You can distribute any remaining amount to the patients with outstanding claims. Type the amount of the insurance payment, the check number, and the bank number in the appropriate fields. Ensure the correct date is selected from the Date drop-down list. Select the billing and plan doctors from the drop-down lists. To submit completed claims covered by a secondary plan to the insurance batch file, select Print Secondary Claims . Click OK . The Insurance Payments window is displayed. The insurance check information is displayed at the top of the window. Select Load Claims from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Edit and remove claims as necessary. For a detailed list of Insurance Payments window options, see Using Insurance Payments Window Options . The write-off amount posted is the difference between the plan�s allowed amount and your fee schedule 0 amount for the code. The reserved code 50.90 is posted as the transaction write-off. If the payment amounts and estimates in the Payment column are not the same, select Write-Off Mode from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu to post the write-off. The Payment column replaces the Dr. Amt. column, the Write-off column replaces the Total column, and the Risk Woff column replaces the Payment column. Select Edit Write Off for Trans from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Claim Line Item Adjustment window is displayed for the transaction. The amount that must be written off is displayed in the Adjustments field. The patient portion (claim total minus insurance payment plus adjustments) is displayed in the Remaining field. Click Edit . The Write Off field is highlighted. Enter the appropriate write-off amount, based on the amount displayed in the Remaining field. The Adjustments field and the entered write-off amount should be the same. Click OK . To change the write-off amount for another claim, select Next from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The claim is displayed. Click Edit to change the write-off amount for the first transaction. You can press the down arrow key to move to the next transaction in the list. Click OK . Click Close . The Insurance Payments window is displayed. Some PPO plans require an additional risk and development write-off, and it may be necessary to type it in the Risk Woff column. When you post the insurance payment from this window, the risk write-off amount is automatically calculated. This amount is the risk write-off percentage multiplied by the claim�s estimated insurance payment. The write-off amount is subtracted from the insurance payment amount. Example : If the claim�s estimated insurance portion is $63 and there is a three percent risk write-off, $61.11

topics.title: Running the Practice Barometers Report
topics.body: Running the Practice Barometers Report Setting Up the Scheduler Running the Practice Barometers Report The Practice Barometers report compares critical statistics for your practice for two time periods. The report displays the: Goal for each period Percent of the goal attained Percentage change between the two periods The goal attained equals the production amount divided by the goals for the period. If you did not establish goals for the first period, no percentage change is calculated. To run the Practice Barometers report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Practice Barometers . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Practice Barometer Report window is displayed. In the Range One section, click the drop-down arrows in the Start Date and End Date fields to select the dates from a calendar. In the Range Two section, click the drop-down arrows in the Start Date and End Date fields to select the dates from a calendar. In the Providers section, click the drop-down arrows to select the providers from a list, or leave 999 to report on all providers. Click OK . Additional Practice Barometers Report Information: If the software is missing a number for one of the periods (for example, you set a goal for the first period but not the second), asterisks are displayed in the Change column. If your receivables totals are not current, the report will be inaccurate. The report contains the following sections: Critical Numbers/Measurement: Active Patients�Patients with production (codes 100+) posted in the last two years. Inactive Patients�Patients with no production posted in the last two years. Patients Seen�Patients with production posted within the specified date range. If you run this report for a single provider, the software uses the service, not the billing, provider. New Patients Seen�Patients with production posted and first visit date within the specified date range. If you run this report for a single provider, the software uses the service, not the billing, provider. New Patients Percentage�Percentage of patients seen who are new patients. Production Numbers: (Derived from service provider) Actual Production�Dollar amount of production for service provider. Missed Appt Prod.�Dollar amount of production not posted due to missed appointments. This number is updated when you run the Missed Appointments Report. Production Goal/Production Goal Attained�Dollar amount of production goal. Goal information initially is set up using the Month At A Glance window in Goal mode. The Production Goal Attained field gives the dollar amount of production posted and equals the production amount divided by the goals for the period. Estimated Hours Worked�Time units for all transactions posted during the indicated time period. This number may be higher than expected because explosion codes are counted as individual procedures. Production/Est Hours�Production for service provider divided by Estimated Hrs Worked. Production/Patients Seen�Production for the service provider divided by Patients Seen. Receivables Numbers: (Derived from collecting provider) Net Production$�Production +/- adjustments to production. Collections�Collections +/- adjustments to collections. Collection/Production Ratio�Collections divided by Net Production$. This ratio compares dollars collected to dollars produced for the specified date range. Plan Collections�Dollar amount posted to insurance plan transactions. Change in Receivables�The receivab

topics.title: Specifying Practice Central Content
topics.body: Specifying Practice Central Content Getting Started > Using Practice Central > Setting Practice Central Preferences Specifying Practice Central Content After setting security rights, configure Practice Central preferences to specify the content displayed for each user. To set Practice Central preferences: Select an option: Select View > Practice Central Preferences . Click My Preferences in the Practice Central window. The Practice Central Preferences window is displayed. In the Practice Central Content section, select the items to display, and deselect the items that you do not want to display. In the Content Settings section, select from the following options: To filter the patient list by appointment cost: Type the minimum cost in the High Dollar Amount field. To filter the patient list by a user-specified code: Type the code in the User Code field. To filter the patient list by a specific balance: Type the minimum balance in the High Balance Amount field. To filter the patient list based on how recently the patient has referred other patients: Type the number of months in the Referred Others Within the Last _ Month(s) field. To filter the patient list based on how soon patients are re-entering the practice: Type the number of months in the Patients Re-entering Practice After _ Month(s) field. To filter the patient list based on the length of time patients are out on referral: Type the number of weeks in the Patients Out on Referral for Over _ Week(s) field. To filter the patient list based on how recently the patient has missed an appointment: Type the number of days in the Appointments Missed Within Last _ Day(s) field. To filter the patient list based on the number of appointments for which patients were late in a specified number of months: Type the number of late appointments and the number of months in the Patients Were Late More Than _ Time(s) Within Last _ Months field. In the X-Rays Due section, type the number of years after which x-rays are due in the Bitewing at , Full Series at, and Panoral at fields. In the Show Totals For section, select an option: To display practice totals, click Practice . This information is displayed in the Production Goals section. To display totals for one provider, click Doctor , and select the doctor from the drop-down list. This information is displayed in the Production Goals section. In the Search the Scheduler for section, type or select the following information: Type the time unit interval in the Open _ Minute Intervals field. Select the provider in the For Doctor field. Type the number of days to search in the For the Next _ Days field. Select the rooms to search. To show the expansion icon when a section contains more than 10 records, select Show Expansion Icon . To specify how often the content in Practice Central should be updated, type the number of minutes in the Refresh Content Every _ Mins. field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Practice Central Items
topics.body: Practice Central Items Getting Started > Using Practice Central > Setting Practice Central Preferences Practice Central Items The items that can be displayed in the Practice Central window are: Available Scheduler Times : Displays a list of available scheduling time slots for the selected day range. Birthday Patients : Displays a list of patients who have a birthday that falls within the previous, current, or next calendar week. End of Day Call Backs : Displays a list of patients requiring a follow-up call at the end of the current date. High Dollar Patients : Displays a list of patients with appointments on the current date that cost more than the amount specified in the High Dollar Amount field. New Patients : Displays a list of patients with appointments for the current date who do not have a value in the First Visit field of their patient record. Office Expert Summary : Displays a list of Office Expert Summary items for the current date. Office Memos : Displays a list of office memos. Patients Needing Portraits : Displays a list of patients with appointments for the current date who require portraits. Patients Needing Perio Screening : Displays a list of patients with appointments for the current date who require perio screening. Patients Needing to be HIPAA Acknowledged : Displays a list of patients with appointments for the current date who have not received a HIPAA notification. Patients with Overdue Recall : Displays a list of patients with appointments for the current date who are overdue and have not been scheduled for a recall. Patients With User Codes : Displays a list of patients with appointments for the current date who are assigned the user code specified in the User Code field. Patients With Watches on a Tooth : Displays a list of patients with appointments for the current date who have a watch attached to one or more teeth. Production Goals : Displays the scheduled and actual goals for today, tomorrow, the current week, and current month. Startup Tasks : Displays startup tasks, such as Overdue Reports, Batched Statements , and Pending electronic statements file needs to be transmitted. Show Patients Needing Medical History Update : Displays a list of patients with appointments for the current date who require a medical history update. Today's High Balance Patients : Displays a list of patients due into the office on the current date that have a balance greater than that specified in the High Balance Amount field. Today's Memos : Displays memos for the current date. Today's Unconfirmed Appointments: Displays a list of unconfirmed appointments scheduled for the current date. Tomorrow's Unconfirmed Appointments: Displays a list of unconfirmed appointments scheduled for the next day. Patients Needing X-Rays: Displays a list of patients, scheduled for the current date, who are overdue for a bitewing, full series, or panoral x-ray. Icons are displayed next to patients to indicate which x-ray type is overdue. If a patient's insurance company allows for x-rays at an interval different from the one your office has set up, an "x" icon is displayed to the right of the x-ray icon. Float over the "x" icon with your mouse to display the time interval for x-rays as listed in the patient's insurance information. Patients who Referred Others: Displays a list of patients, scheduled for the current date, who have recently referred other patients to your office. Patients w/Outstanding TPs not Scheduled: Displays a list of patients, schedu

topics.title: Running the Create Practice Goal Utility
topics.body: Running the Create Practice Goal Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running Receivables Totals Utilities Running the Create Practice Goal Utility Run this utility to create a practice goal after you have set goals for each provider. The utility adds the provider goals to determine the practice goal amount. To create a practice goal: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > RCVTOT > Create Practice Goal . The Create Practice Goals window is displayed. Select the days from the appropriate drop-down lists, and click OK . A message asks you whether to create a practice goal for the selected dates. Click OK twice. Restart the software. To view the practice goal and compare it to scheduled or actual production amounts, select an option: To view the actual or production amounts for the practice : Select Scheduler > Month At A Glance . The practice goals are displayed. To view the practice production goals and actual production amounts for the past year : From any window, select Productions Goals in the Power bar . The Progress Towards Goals window is displayed. To view the daily practice goals : Select List > View Daily RCVTOT Records > Goals/Prod > Practice Goals . The Rcvtot window is displayed. You must have the appropriate security rights to view the production amounts and display these windows. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Practice Central Preferences
topics.body: Setting Practice Central Preferences Getting Started > Using Practice Central Setting Practice Central Preferences The content displayed in the Practice Central window is determined by the Practice Central preferences. Related Topics Setting Practice Central Security Rights Practice Central Items Specifying Practice Central Content Disabling Practice Central's Automatic Display Need more information? topics.title: Setting Practice Central Security Rights
topics.body: Setting Practice Central Security Rights Getting Started > Using Practice Central > Setting Practice Central Preferences Setting Practice Central Security Rights To set Practice Central security rights: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights by User . The Rights by User List window is displayed. Double-click the user. The user�s security settings are displayed in the Rights by User window. Click Practice Central . The Practice Central security options are displayed in the right pane of the window. See Practice Central Items for a list of Practice Central items. Select or deselect the items to display in the window. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Practice by Class Forecast Report
topics.body: Running the Practice by Class Forecast Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Practice Management Reports Running the Practice by Class Forecast Report The Practice by Class Forecast report lists the totals of scheduled charges for procedures by class. It calculates the percentage of income generated by each procedure class performed by your practice. To run the Practice by Class Forecast report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Sched/Appt Reports > Practice by Class . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Practice by Class Forecast Report Setup window is displayed. In the Forecast Dates section, click the drop-down arrows in the Starting and Ending fields to select dates from a calendar. Click OK . Additional Practice by Class Forecast Report Information: The report classifies procedures in the following classes: Diagnostic X-ray Preventive Restorative Endodontics Periodontics Prosthodontics Crowns Oral surgery Orthodontics Adjunctive services Medical codes Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Account Labels in Alphabetical Order
topics.body: Printing Account Labels in Alphabetical Order Setting Up Accounts > Running Account Reports > Printing Account Labels in Alphabetical Order Printing Account Labels in Alphabetical Order Account labels contain the name and address of each guarantor one. To print account labels in alphabetical order: Select Reports > Account > Labels > Alphabetical . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Account Labels window is displayed. Select the starting and ending account names from the drop-down lists. Select Include Inactive Accounts to print labels for inactive accounts. Select an option: To include labels for accounts with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude labels for accounts with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. Type the number of lines per label in the Lines Per Label field. Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Account Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Account Labels by ID Number
topics.body: Printing Account Labels by ID Number Setting Up Accounts > Running Account Reports > Printing Account Labels Printing Account Labels by ID Number To print account labels by identification number: Select Reports > Account > Labels > ID Order . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Labels by Account ID window is displayed. Select the starting and ending account identification numbers from the drop-down lists. Select Include Inactive Accounts to print labels for inactive accounts. Select an option: To include labels for accounts with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude labels for accounts with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. Type the number of lines per label in the Lines Per Label field. Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Account Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

Click OK . The Labels by Account Zip Code window is displayed. Select the starting and ending account zip codes from the drop-down lists. To exclude labels for accounts with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. Type the number of lines per label in the Lines Per Label field. Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Account Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . Need more information?
