topics.title: Using PracticeToGo
topics.body: Using PracticeToGo Using PracticeToGo The PracticeToGo feature transfers practice information in SoftDent to pocket PC devices by integrating with desktop organizer software. PracticeToGo also receives data from these programs through the SD Commands feature. The PracticeToGo feature has two integration options: The Palm OS platform Microsoft Outlook 2000 software or higher When you sync with PracticeToGo, the following information transfers from SoftDent to either the Palm organizer or Microsoft Outlook: Appointments Call-back list Patient list Memos Referring doctor list Referring doctor patient list Pharmacy list Prescription information Practice financials It is up to you to determine the parameters for the information that transfers; these parameters are your PracticeToGo user preferences. You must have security rights to use PracticeToGo, change PracticeToGo system settings, and set syncing preferences. To view a list of terms used in this section, click here: PracticeToGo: The SoftDent feature that enables you to transfer office information to the Palm OS platform or the Microsoft Outlook 2000 software. Palm-connected organizer: A hand-held electronic organizer that can hold information from your desktop PC or laptop computer. Pocket PC: A hand-held electronic organizer that can hold information from your desktop PC or laptop computer, for use with the PracticeToGo-Outlook platform. Palm Desktop software: A Palm software program that runs on the PC. The software enables you to work with your Palm organizer applications and to back up your Palm organizer data on your PC. It also contains the HotSync Manager, which enables you to synchronize data between your PC and your Palm organizer. Microsoft Outlook 2000: The desktop organizer software that runs on the PC. If enabled, Microsoft Outlook synchronizes data with SoftDent. Also, per your setup, Microsoft Outlook synchronizes data between your PC and your pocket PC device. SoftDent-Palm Application: The SoftDent software that runs on the Palm-connected organizer. This software must be installed on the Palm organizer before transfer of data can occur. SoftDent conduit: A small software program that runs on the PC to enable transfer of data from SoftDent to the Palm-connected organizer. This program is installed automatically when you perform the integration procedure with the Palm OS platform. PC SoftDent: The SoftDent software that runs on the PC, as distinguished from the SoftDent-Palm application that runs on the Palm-connected organizer. Synchronization: Another term for the transfer of data between the SoftDent software and the Palm-connected organizer or the Microsoft Outlook software. A synchronization or HotSync operation occurs when you press the HotSync button on the cradle. SD commands: PracticeToGo application Date Book, Address List, and Memo Pad commands that, when inserted in a selected Palm organizer or MicroSoft Outlook entry, allow for specific data to be transferred to the PC SoftDent software during synchronization. HotSync� Manager: A feature installed on the PC by the Palm Desktop software to manage the operation of the HotSync cradle. The HotSync Manager detects properly installed conduits, such as the SoftDent conduit, to enable transfer of data between SoftDent and the Palm-connected organizer. This feature is used only when PracticeToGo is enabled for use with the Palm OS platform. HotSync cradle: A device that is connected to your desktop PC or laptop compu

topics.title: Customizing Procedure Codes Toolbar Buttons
topics.body: Customizing Procedure Codes Toolbar Buttons Restorative Charting > Using Restorative Tab Toolbars > Using the Procedure Codes Toolbar Customizing Procedure Codes Toolbar Buttons There are 20 customizable buttons that can be displayed in the Procedure Codes toolbar. After you have added a button to the toolbar, you can assign a definition and parameters. To add a button to the toolbar, see Customizing Toolbars . To customize a Procedure Codes toolbar button: Select the button in the Procedure Codes toolbar, right-click, and select Customize Button from the submenu. The Customize Chart PowerBar Button window is displayed. Select an ADA code range to associate with the button from the Low and High drop-down lists. Type the text to display when your mouse is moved over the button in the Tool Tip Text field. Type the text to display in the status bar in the Status Bar field. Click OK and Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Procedure Codes Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Procedure Codes Toolbar Restorative Charting > Using Restorative Tab Toolbars Using the Procedure Codes Toolbar The Procedure Codes toolbar commands e nable you to chart common procedures and post recall and explosion codes. When you select these toolbar commands, the corresponding procedure code is selected based on the number of surfaces or roots you have selected on the chart. Example : If you click the amalgam button and then select two surfaces on a tooth, the procedure code for a two-surface amalgam is automatically selected. If a procedure encompasses multiple codes (like veneers), when you select the button a list from which you can specify the code is displayed. In addition, when you chart more than one instance of certain procedures, each is coded correctly. Example : The first time you chart an apicoectomy, the code to indicate the first root is automatically selected; when you chart the second apicoectomy, the correct code for each additional root is automatically selected. You can also select an ADA or transaction code from the ADA drop-down list located at the top of the toolbar. To add a button to the toolbar, see Customizing Toolbars . Click the icon for the description of the Procedure Codes toolbar buttons. Click this button and the appropriate surface to chart an amalgam. Click this button and the appropriate surface to chart a resin. Click this button and the appropriate tooth to chart a sedative filling. Click this button and select the code in the Crowns - Porcelain Fused to Metal window. Select the appropriate tooth to chart a porcelain fused to metal crown. Click this button and select the code in the Crowns - Resin Fused to Metal window. Select the appropriate tooth to chart a resin fused to metal crown. Click this button and the appropriate tooth to chart a temporary crown. Click this button and select the code in the Crowns - 3/4 Cast window. Select the appropriate tooth to chart a 3/4 metal crown. Click this button and select the code in the Crowns - Full-Cast Metal window. Select the appropriate tooth to chart a full cast metal crown. Click this button and select the code in the Crowns - Stainless Steel window. Select the appropriate tooth to chart a stainless steel crown. Click this button and the appropriate tooth to chart a core buildup. Click this button and select the code in the Cast Post and Core window. Select the appropriate tooth to chart a post and core. Click this button and the appropriate tooth to chart a sealant. Click this button and select the code in the Labial Veneer window. Select the appropriate tooth to chart a veneer. Click this button and the appropriate surface to chart a root canal. Click this button and the appropriate surface to chart an apicoectomy. Click this button and select the code in the Implants window. Select the appropriate tooth to chart an implant. Click this button and select the surface or quadrant to bleach. Select the code in the Quadrant or Tooth Needed window. Click this button and select the code in the Extractions window. Select the appropriate surface to chart an extraction. Click this button and the appropriate surface to chart a hemisection. Click this button to post a recall code to the patient's Transactions window. The codes specified in the Recall Settings window are used. Click this button to chart an intraoral periapical film. Click this button and select the appropriate tooth to display the Posted Work for Tooth window. View the details for

topics.title: Practice Management Reports
topics.body: Practice Management Reports Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports Practice Management Reports The following reports are found under Reports > Practice Management > Sched/Appt. Reports: Op by ADA Forecast : Shows you which operatory brings in the largest percentage of income by procedure. It compares procedures to be performed in a particular operatory to the same procedures to be performed by the entire practice. Provider by ADA Forecast : Shows you which provider brings in the largest percent of income by procedure. It compares procedures to be performed by a provider to the same procedures to be performed by the group of selected providers. Provider by Class Forecast : Shows you which provider brings in the largest percent of income for each class of procedures. Procedures are divided into the following classes: diagnostic, x-ray, preventive, restorative, endodontics, periodontics, prosthodontics, crowns, oral surgery, orthodontics, adjunctive services, and medical codes. Practice by Class: Lists the totals of scheduled charges for procedures by class. It calculates the percentage of income generated by each procedure class performed by your practice. Related Topics Running the Operatory Production Forecast by ADA Code Report Running the Provider Productions Forecast by ADA Code Report Running the Provider Productions Forecast by Class Report Running the Practice by Class Forecast Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Practice Central Features
topics.body: Practice Central Features What's New in Version 14.0 Practice Central Features Practice Central has received the following enhancements: Pending Electronic Statements File Link Added to the Startup Tasks Section A new link� Pending electronic statements file needs to be transmitted � has been added to the Startup Tasks section of Practice Central. If you use Automated Statements and have statements that need to be transmitted, this link is displayed. Click the link to open the eServices window, where you can transmit the statements. Missed Appointments Section Added to Practice Central A Missed Appointments section has been added to the Practice Central window. Each link under the Missed Appointments section contains the patient name, the doctor name, the date for which the appointment was scheduled and missed, and the portrait icon (if the patient has a portrait). Clicking the link opens the Tracked Appointments window ( List > Patient > Other Management > Missed/Broken/Late Appointments ). The following options have been added to the Practice Central Preferences window: A Missed Appointments option has been added to the Practice Central Content section. This option is selected by default. An Appointments Missed Within Last _ Days option has been added to the Content Settings section. This field is set to 7 by default. An entry for a particular patient/appointment is displayed in the Missed Appointments section if the patient has missed an appointment within the number of days specified in this field. A Show Missed Appointments security permission has been added to the Entire List and Practice Central lists of the Rights by User window, and the permission is also found in the Users by Rights window. This permission is assigned to all users and templates by default. Patients Who Have a History of Being Late Section Added to Practice Central A Patients Who Have a History of Being Late section has been added to the Practice Central window. Each link under the Patients Who Have a History of Being Late section contains the patient name and the portrait icon (if the patient has a portrait). Clicking the link opens the Tracked Appointments window ( List > Patient > Other Management > Missed/Broken/Late Appointments ). The following options have been added to the Practice Central Preferences window: A Patients Who Have a History of Being Late option has been added to the Practice Central Content section. This option is selected by default. A Patients Were Late More Than _ Time(s) Within Last _ Months option has been added to the Content Settings section. The values in these fields are set to 2 and 18 by default. An entry for a particular patient/appointment is displayed in the Patients Who Have a History of Being Late section if the patient has been late more than twice in the last 18 months. A Show Patients Who Have a History of Being Late security permission has been added to the Practice Central list of the Rights by User window, and the permission is also found in the Users by Rights window. This permission is assigned to all users and templates by default. Insurance X-Ray Interval Displayed in Patients Needing X-Rays Section When SoftDent indicates it is time to take new x-rays�by listing patients under the Patients Needing X-Rays section�the software now checks the patient's insurance information to see how often the insurance company pays for x-rays. For example: Your office has set the X-Rays Due field in the Practice Central Preferenc

topics.title: Running the Practice Summary Report
topics.body: Running the Practice Summary Report Handling Reports > Practice Management Reports Running the Practice Summary Report The Practice Summary report summarizes several important areas of your practice. By default, the report is generated for the previous six months, while forecast information is projected for the next six months. Depending on your selection criteria, this report summarizes your provider production, treatment plans, receivables, insurance collections, patient and professional referrals, and recall data. To run the Practice Summary report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Practice Summary . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Practice Summary Report Setup window is displayed. In the Sections portion of the window, select the areas of your practice on which you want to report. Click OK . Additional Practice Summary Report Information: The report contains the following sections: Class Production Summary: This section displays production totals for each class of dental and medical codes. The Actual Dollars column shows the production amounts posted for the past six months. The Forecast Dollars column lists the scheduled production amounts for the next six months. The forecasted amount is generated from scheduled appointments. The Totals/Goals field displays the goals set up for your practice for the previous six months (if you have set up the goal scheduling feature). Provider Production Summary: This section compares the provider's production totals (work actually done) for the previous six months in relation to the practice�s production. The provider production amount includes all production posted to the provider in the past six months. Practice production includes all production codes (100.00-99999.99) posted for all providers. This number is the same for all providers in your database. The % of Prod. field is provider production divided by the practice production. Treatment Plan Summary: This section summarizes treatment plans for dental and medical procedures. Only treatment plan transactions designated as primary, secondary, or estimate are included. The report also lists the number of treatment plan transactions with the specified code types and the dollar amount of accepted, unaccepted, scheduled, and completed primary and secondary treatment plans. The software determines the treatment plan status using the Accept field in the Treatment Plan window. Receivables Summary: This section lists your net production, net collections, and the change in receivables for the previous six months for both dental and medical codes. For these numbers to be accurate, your receivables totals must be current and correct�you need to run a final daysheet for all dates included in the report. Net Production includes productions (codes 100.00-9999.99), charges (codes 61.00-91.99), and adjustments to productions (codes 40.00-59.99) posted in the past six months. Net Collections includes payment codes (codes 1.00-20.99) and collection adjustments (codes 21.00-39.99). Change in Receivables includes the practice�s receivables from the previous six months minus the practice�s current receivables total. This amount is not necessarily the net production minus the net collections, because net collections also contain finance and billing charges. Insurance Summary: This section lists the total of all unsubmitted, outstanding, and pending claims, and includes totals for pre-authorization and ortho claims

topics.title: Processing Transactions Offline
topics.body: Processing Transactions Offline eServices > Using the ePayments Service Processing Transactions Offline If your office is without a modem or Internet connection, use this procedure. To process a transaction offline: In the patient Transactions List window, click Add . The Transaction window is displayed. Select the credit card payment and press Tab. The Payment window is displayed. Select Off Line in the Credit Card section. When prompted, swipe the credit card through the card swiper. See the diagram on the card swiper for the correct position of the card. If the card is unavailable, enter the card information manually. See Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Manually . Contact the bank for the authorization number. See the documentation provided by TransFirst for more information. After you receive the authorization number, enter it in the Authorization # field. Add notes, if necessary. To print a receipt, click OK at the prompt. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Processing Treatment Plans
topics.body: Processing Treatment Plans Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint Processing Treatment Plans To mark a proposed treatment plan as accepted by the patient: While viewing the patient presentation, select Process Treatment Plan . The Place a checkmark beside the treatment plan items this patient has accepted window is displayed. Select the items in the Accept column and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Production by ADA Code Report
topics.body: Running the Production by ADA Code Report Billing and Receivables > Using Billing and Receivables Reports > Running Production Reports Running the Production by ADA Code Report The Production by ADA Code report g enerates a list of production codes posted during a specific date range. The practice and individual provider totals and percentages are included in the report results. To generate a list of production amounts by ADA code: Select Reports > Practice Management > Production Reports > By ADA . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Production/Transactions by Code Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending transaction dates from the appropriate drop-down lists. Select an option: To restrict the report to a specific set of ADA or transaction codes : Select the codes from the drop-down lists in the Codes section. To include information for accounts that contain a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for accounts that contain a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. To include information for inactive patients: Select Include Inactive Patients . To generate production information for a specific provider : Select the provider identification number from the Doctors section. Click OK . Additional Production by ADA Code Report Information: Information on this report includes the code number, description, and fee, as well as how many procedures were performed by each provider and by all providers in the report. Also included is the amount generated by the provider or the practice for each procedure code. This report includes every transaction. This means that any "dangling" transactions will be included in the totals. Code/Description�The transaction code number and description. Fee $�Dollar amount of fee schedule 0 in the Transaction Codes Scheduler/Fees tab. An asterisk (*) appears for payment and adjustment codes. Prvdr Qty�Number of transactions posted for the service provider (Dr on production codes 100+). Group Qty�Total number of transactions posted for providers included in report parameters. % Qty�Percent of transactions posted for the service provider (Dr on production codes 100+). Prvdr Amnt $�Dollar amount posted for the service provider (Dr on production codes 100+). Provider UCR Amnt�The provider�s usual and customary rate or fee 0. Group Amnt $�Total dollar amount posted for providers included in report parameters. Group UCR Amnt�The provider�s usual and customary rate or fee 0. % Amnt $�Percent of dollar amount posted for service providers included in report parameters. Prvdr Prod Rate $/hr�Prvdr Amnt $ multiplied by the number of RTUs. Group Prod Rate $/hr�Group Amnt $ multiplied by the number of RTUs. Totals�Totals for quantities and amounts. No totals for payment and adjustment codes. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Prescribing Drugs Using the ePrescriptions Service
topics.body: Prescribing Drugs Using the ePrescriptions Service eServices > Using the ePrescriptions Module Prescribing Drugs Using the ePrescriptions Service When the ePrescriptions service is enabled, ePrescriptions options are enabled for prescription-related functions in the Prescriptions for list window. To prescribe a drug: Select an option: In the Patient List window: Select the patient and select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window: Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options menu. In the patient chart: Click the Prescription button on the Common toolbar. In the Account List window: Select the patient and select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patients associated with the account are displayed in the Patient List window. Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Account window: Select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patients associated with the account are displayed in the Patient List window. Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patient prescriptions are displayed in the Prescriptions for list window. Click Prescribe Drug on the toolbar. The ePrescribing window is displayed. Drugs prescribed using ePrescriptions are displayed in blue. Click ePrescription History in the Options section of the Express bar to display the ePrescribing Rx history window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Prescribing a Drug
topics.body: Prescribing a Drug Pharmacies and Prescriptions > Handling Prescriptions Prescribing a Drug To prescribe a drug: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options menu. In the Account List window : Select the patient and select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patients associated with the account are displayed in the Patient List window. Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Account window : Select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patients associated with the account are displayed in the Patient List window. Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the patient chart: Hover over the Prescriptions section of the patient banner, and click the link. The patient prescriptions are displayed in the Prescriptions for List window. Click Prescribe Drug on the toolbar. The Add A Prescription window is displayed. Select the drug from the Drug ID drop-down list. If the drug you are prescribing is not displayed in the Drug List window, see Adding Prescription Drugs . Select the provider from the Doctor ID drop-down list. The patient�s preferred doctor is the default value for the Doctor ID drop-down list. Click OK . To modify the prescription, dispense, or refill information, type the information in the appropriate fields. You cannot edit the Med Alerts field in this window. Medical alert information must be entered in the Medical History window. Click OK . The Prescription Options window is displayed. Select an option: To use the auto dialer to call the preferred pharmacy : Select Call Preferred Pharmacy and click OK . The Pharmacy Phone Numbers window is displayed. Click Dial . To call another pharmacy : Select Choose another Pharmacy and click OK . The Pharmacy List window is displayed. Double-click the pharmacy. The Pharmacy Phone Numbers window is displayed. Click Dial . To print the prescription : Select Print Prescription and click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Case Presentation Dates
topics.body: Updating Case Presentation Dates Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint Updating Case Presentation Dates To update the date the case presentation was shown to the patient to the current date: While viewing the patient presentation, select Tools > Update Date Presented . A message that asks you whether to update the presentation date to the current date is displayed. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Previously Entered Medical Histories
topics.body: Updating Previously Entered Medical Histories Handling Patient Medical History > Updating Medical Histories Updating Previously Entered Medical Histories If more than 24 hours have passed since you entered the medical history information and you need to update the information: In the Patient List window, select a patient and select Clinical Management > Patient Medical History from the Options menu or from the Options section of the Express bar. OR In the patient chart, hover over the Medical Conditions, Allergies, or Medicines section in the patient banner, and click the link. The medical history information for the selected patient is displayed. To search for the appropriate history, click Find and select a date from the Date field. Click Update . The Physician�s Name field is highlighted. Make changes and click OK . If nothing has changed, click Update , and then click OK . A new medical history with the current date is created. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Purging Bluebook Entries
topics.body: Purging Bluebook Entries Setting Up Insurance > Using the Insurance Bluebook Purging Bluebook Entries You can purge all bluebook entries before a date you specify. The Last Update field at the bottom of the Bluebook entries for window shows you the last time bluebook entries have been updated. You must have the appropriate security rights to perform this function. To purge all bluebook entries before a specific date: Select List > Bluebook . The Bluebook entries for Plan window is displayed. Select a plan from the Plan drop-down list. Click Bluebook from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Bluebook entries for window is displayed. Click Purge from the Options section of the Express bar or from the top of the Bluebook entries for window. The Purge Blue Book window is displayed. In the Start Purge Date field, type the date or click the drop-down arrow to display a calendar and select the date. All bluebook entries before the start purge date will be deleted. Click OK . The utility log file (sdutils.log) displays the plan names for which entries are deleted and the start purge date. The total number of all bluebook entries scanned and deleted is also displayed. A separate log file (PurgeBlueBook.log) contains an itemized list of purged bluebook entries. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Purge Bluebook Utility
topics.body: Running the Purge Bluebook Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Purge Bluebook Utility The Purge Bluebook utility enables you to purge all bluebook entries before a date you specify. The Last Update field at the bottom of the Bluebook entries for window shows you the last time bluebook entries have been updated. You must have the appropriate security rights to run this utility. To purge all bluebook entries before a specific date: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Purge Blue Book . The Purge Blue Book window is displayed. Select One Plan or All Plans . If you select One Plan, select the plan name from the drop-down list. In the Start Purge Date field, type the date or click the drop-down arrow to display a calendar and select the date. All bluebook entries before the start purge date will be deleted. Click OK . The utility log file (sdutils.log) displays the plan names for which entries are deleted and the start purge date. The total number of all bluebook entries scanned and deleted is also displayed. A separate log file (PurgeBlueBook.log) contains an itemized list of purged bluebook entries. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Primary Teeth
topics.body: Viewing Primary Teeth Restorative Charting > Handling Mixed Dentition Viewing Primary Teeth To display only primary teeth when charting, select Chart > Chart All Permanent Teeth as Unerupted . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing a Complete Refer Work Letter
topics.body: Printing a Complete Refer Work Letter Setting Up Patients > Patient List and Patient Window Options > Using Quick Letters Printing a Complete Refer Work Letter You can print a letter informing a referring doctor that your office has completed the specified work for a patient using the Quick Letters feature. To print a complete refer work letter for a doctor who has referred a patient to your office: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the Power Bar : Select Patient List Window . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the Account List or Account window : Select Patients from the Options menu of the Express bar or the Options menu. A Patient List window containing all of the patients associated with the selected account is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the InOffice window : Select Patient from the Options menu of the Express bar. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the patient chart: Hover over the patient name on the patient banner and click the link. In any window : Press Ctrl + O. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. The patient is displayed in the Patient window. Select Letters and Labels > Quick Letters > Complete Refer Work from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output type and click OK . The Referring Doctor Correspondence window is displayed. Select a date range using the drop-down lists, and click OK . The referring doctor thank you letter is displayed. Click Zoom In or Zoom Out to view the letter. Click Print , select a printer, and click OK . Sample Letter Related Topics Using Quick Letters Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing a Delinquency Notice
topics.body: Printing a Delinquency Notice Setting Up Patients > Patient List and Patient Window Options > Using Quick Letters Printing a Delinquency Notice You can print a delinquency notice for a patient using the Quick Letters feature. To print a Delinquency Notice letter for a patient: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the Power Bar : Select Patient List Window . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the Account List or Account window : Select Patients from the Options menu of the Express bar or the Options menu. A Patient List window containing all of the patients associated with the selected account is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the InOffice window : Select Patient from the Options menu of the Express bar. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the patient chart: Hover over the patient name on the patient banner and click the link. In any window : Press Ctrl + O. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. The patient is displayed in the Patient window. Select Letters and Labels > Quick Letters > Delinquency Notice from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output type and click OK . The Statement of Delinquency letter is displayed. Click Zoom In or Zoom Out to view the form. Click Print , select a printer, and click OK . Sample Letter Related Topics Using Quick Letters Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing a Medical History Form
topics.body: Printing a Medical History Form Setting Up Patients > Patient List and Patient Window Options > Using Quick Letters Printing a Medical History Form You can print the Confidential Medical-Dental History Form for a patient using the Quick Letters feature. To print a Medical History form for a patient: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. topics.title: Printing Discounts on a Claim Form
topics.body: Printing Discounts on a Claim Form Posting Payments > Handling Discounts Printing Discounts on a Claim Form Some insurance companies are requesting that discounts posted to the patient's ledger be printed on the claim form. For example: A patient comes in for a given procedure and is charged a fee but is also given a discount to waive the patient copay. In this case, the claim form would need to include the procedure being submitted as well as the line item for the discount that was provided. To print a discount transaction on a claim form, you must ensure that the following conditions are met: The Print discount transactions on claim option is selected on the Claim Filing tab of the Insurance Plan window. See Adding Insurance Plans for more information. The discount transaction must be listed in the Discount Schedule window. See Adding a Discount for more information. The date of service of the discount transaction must fall within the claim submission dates. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing eServices Reports
topics.body: Printing eServices Reports eServices > Using eServices Reports Printing eServices Reports Printing Available Reports The number of reports available for each report type for any given day is displayed on the Electronic Services screen in the Report List window. You should use the PRINT ALL button to print all reports on the day you transmit your claims. To print the available reports: From the SoftDent main menu, select Insurance > Electronic Services . The Electronic Services screen is displayed. Select Report Options > Reports , or click the Reports button on the right-hand side of the screen. The Report List window is displayed. Select the reports you want to print by holding down the Ctrl key and clicking the file name of each report. Click PRINT ALL . The Report List window is automatically displayed after a transmit or receive-only session. Printing Previous Reports To print reports from a previous date: From the SoftDent main menu, select Insurance > Electronic Services . The Electronic Services screen is displayed. Select Report Options > Reports , or click the Reports button on the right-hand side of the screen. The Report List window is displayed. In the Date Range Start and End fields, click the drop-down arrows to select the start and end dates from a calendar. The reports for the requested date range are displayed in the top portion of the window. Select the reports you want to print by holding down the Ctrl key and clicking the file name of each report. Click Print File or PRINT ALL . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing User-Selected Labels
topics.body: Printing User-Selected Labels Handling Reports > Generating User-Selected Reports Printing User-Selected Labels In addition to generating and printing user-selected reports, you can generate and print user-selected labels. You can select criteria to determine which user-selected labels will print; however, the information on the labels is limited. The name and address are displayed by default, and you can opt to include the hygiene recall date or the next appointment date on the label. To print user-selected labels: Select Reports > Patient > Labels > User-Selected or Reports > Account > Labels > User-Selected . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Selection Criteria window is displayed. Verify the selection criteria. If necessary, set up the label criteria, following the steps used to create a user-selected report. See Creating User-Selected Reports . Click OK . The software searches the database for patients who match the specified criteria and displays a status window, indicating the number of patients searched and the number of matches found. Click OK . The User-Selected Labels window is displayed. In the Options section, click the checkboxes to have the hygiene recall date, the next appointment date, or both, display on the labels. In the Lines Per Label field, specify the number of lines per label (the recommended default setting is 6). Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select the type of label you want to print, and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the HIPAA Privacy Feature
topics.body: Using the HIPAA Privacy Feature System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features Using the HIPAA Privacy Feature You can use the HIPAA Privacy feature to hide patient names and phone numbers in the InOffice, Daily Appointments, and Book At A Glance windows. You can hide this information when printing the Daily Operatory Schedule report. To enable HIPAA Privacy in the InOffice, Daily Appointments, and Book At A Glance windows : Select HIPAA Privacy Off from the View menu. The HIPAA feature is enabled, and the menu command changes to HIPAA Privacy On. The patient�s initials are displayed for each appointment. No patient phone numbers are displayed in the windows. To disable HIPAA Privacy features : Select HIPAA Privacy On from the View menu. The HIPAA feature is disabled, and the menu command changes to HIPAA Privacy Off. Related Topics Setting InOffice HIPAA Privacy Setting Daily Appointments Window Privacy Setting Daily Operatory Schedule Report Privacy Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Printing Custom Labels Printing Custom Labels You can create labels containing any information you type. To print custom labels: Select Reports > Practice/Custom Labels > Custom Labels . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Custom Labels window is displayed. Type the number of labels you want to print. Type the number of lines per label. Standard labels can hold three lines of text. Type the text as it should display on the label. Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Custom Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . Need more information? topics.title: Printing Laser Labels
topics.body: Printing Laser Labels Printing Laser Labels When you print labels and select the Laser Label File option, the labels are sent to a file where they are stored until you are ready to print them. This is useful when you are creating a few labels at a time and you want to wait until you have a complete sheet (30 labels) before you print them. This example uses Alphabetical Patient labels. The steps used to print laser labels are the same, regardless of the type of labels you are printing. To print laser labels: Select Reports > Patient > Labels > Alphabetical . The Output Options window is displayed. Select Laser Label File and click OK . The Patient Label Setup window is displayed. Select the criteria for the labels, and click OK . Select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. Select an option: To print a specific label type: Select the checkbox next to the type of labels you want to print (Patient Labels in this example), and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. To print all label types on the same page: Select the checkboxes next to the types of labels you want to print, and click Print All on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly and click Yes . TIP : To increase the horizontal or vertical offsets, click Settings and type the values, in inches, in the fields. Related Topics Printing Laser Postcards Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Printing a Billing Statement After Posting the Final Insurance Payment Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Printing a Billing Statement After Posting the Final Insurance Payment You can customize the software to prompt you to send a billing statement i f there is no secondary insurance and a balance remains after posting a final insurance payment to the patient Transactions window. topics.title: Using the Probe Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Probe Toolbar Periodontal Charting > Using Perio Tab Toolbars Using the Probe Toolbar The Probe toolbar commands enable you to chart pocket depth, FGM, MGJ, and furcation grade. You can also start and stop voice software. Click the icon for the description of the Probe toolbar buttons. Click this button and a tooth to chart pocket depth . Click this button and a tooth to chart FGM . Click this button and a tooth to chart furcation grades . Click this button and a pocket to chart MGJ . Click this button to resume using Auto Advance . Click this button to start or pause your voice activation software . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Production Adjustments
topics.body: Posting Production Adjustments Posting Payments > Posting Account Payments > Posting Adjustments Posting Production Adjustments Post a production adjustment when the production doctor or the fee is changed. Use production adjustments to post provider allocation changes, courtesy credits, insurance adjustments, and write-offs. Example : A patient has an outstanding balance of $300 and your office sends the account to a collection agency. The collection agency eventually sends a check for $200. In this scenario, you have the following options: Apply the payment to the patient account when you receive a payment and write off the remaining account balance. To post the payment and write off the remaining balance, post a $200 check payment (codes 3�10) and post a production code (codes 50�59) for $100. Post a $300 write-off (codes 50�59) to the patient account when you send the account to the collection agency. After payment is received, post $200 (codes 3�10) to the patient account and post a debit production adjustment (codes 40�47) equal to the amount of the collection agency check. To post a production adjustment to an account or patient record: Select an option: In the Account List window : Select the account and select Payments . In the Account window : Select Payments . In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Transactions . The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments . In the Patient window : Select Transactions . The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Select Production Adj. in the Select Operation section. The Payments � Production Adjustments window is displayed. Select the charge adjustment code from the Code drop-down list. The transactions total is displayed in the Amount field. Type the collection doctor�s identification number in the Dr $ column for each transaction. Type the adjustment amount in the Amount column for each transaction. To add a note to a transaction, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Click Post . Click Close . Tip If you select a write-off code (codes 50.90 to 50.91), select the claim from the Claim drop-down list. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Production by Class Report
topics.body: Running the Production by Class Report Billing and Receivables > Using Billing and Receivables Reports > Running Production Reports Running the Production by Class Report The Production by Class report g enerates a list of production code classes posted during a specific date range. To generate a list of production amounts by procedural class: Select Reports > Practice Management > Production Reports > By Class . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Production by Class Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending transaction dates from the appropriate drop-down lists. To generate production information for only a specific provider, select the provider identification number from the Doctors section. Click OK . Additional Production by Class Report Information: This report includes every transaction. This means that any "dangling" transactions will be included in the totals. Class�The transaction codes grouped by class and description. Provider Dollars�Total dollar amount posted for individual provider of service for each class. Group Dollars�Total dollars posted for service providers included in report parameters. % Dollars Provider/Group�Percent of transaction dollars posted for individual service provider divided by all providers included in report parameters. % Dollars Provider Class/Provider Total�Percent of dollar amount posted for individual service provider divided by total Provider Dollars. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Production Goals
topics.body: Setting Production Goals Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Provider Information Setting Production Goals You can define daily or monthly production goals for every provider in your practice and compare them to the provider�s scheduled production, actual production, and actual collections. For more information, see Goal Scheduling . Before setting up goal scheduling, enter your office hours, provider vacation days, and holidays to ensure the number of working days in the month is accurate. Related Topics Setting a Daily Production Goal Setting a Monthly Production Goal Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Professional Referrals Report
topics.body: Running the Professional Referrals Report Patient and Doctor Referrals > Running Referral Reports Running the Professional Referrals Report The Professional Referrals report lists incoming or outgoing referrals made during a specific period. To run the Professional Referrals report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Referral Reports > Professional . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Professional Referral Report Setup window is displayed. In the Format section, type the number that corresponds to the report format you want. In the Dates section, cliick the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending dates from a calendar. In the Doctors section, click the drop-down arrow to select the referring doctor, or leave 999 to report on all referring doctors. Click OK . Additional Professional Referrals Report Information: The Professional Referral report has four formats: Format 1�Lists work performed on patients referred to your practice by another provider. This format includes the patient's name, last exam date, referral reason, expected return date (if specified), the date, code, and description of work completed, and any additional work planned. Format 2�Lists patients referred to your practice by another provider. This format includes the total production generated by the referrals and the average production per referral. Format 3�Lists patients referred to your practice by another provider. This format includes the number of patients referred to your practice by each provider on the report, the date of their last referral, and the total and average production generated by the referral. Format 4�Lists work done on patients referred to other providers, including the referral reason and expected return date, if specified. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Provider Reports
topics.body: Running Provider Reports Running Provider Reports You can run reports that generate lists or labels for each provider assigned to your practice. You can run the following reports: Alphabetical Provider List Provider by ID Alphabetical Provider Labels Provider Labels by ID Payment Allocation Provider Productions Forecast by Class Provider Productions Forecast by ADA Code Collection Summary Patient Production/Collection Related Topics Running the Alphabetical Provider List Report Running the Provider by ID Report Running the Alphabetical Provider Labels Report Running the Provider Labels by ID Report Running the Payment Allocation Report Running the Provider Productions Forecast by Class Report Running the Provider Productions Forecast by ADA Code Report Running the Collection Summary Report Running the Patient Production/Collection Report Need more information? topics.title: Printing Patient Labels in Alphabetical Order
topics.body: Printing Patient Labels in Alphabetical Order Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports > Printing Patient Labels Printing Patient Labels in Alphabetical Order Patient labels contain the name and address of each patient. To print patient labels in alphabetical order: Select Reports > Patient > Labels > Alphabetical . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Patient Label Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. To include the patient�s last exam date on the label : Select Show Last Exam Date . To include the patient�s next appointment date on the label : Select Show Next Appointment Date . Type the number of lines per label in the Lines Per Label field. Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Patient Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the printer, and click OK . A message is displayed. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Patient Labels by Zip Code

The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Patient Labels by Zip window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient zip codes from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include labels for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude labels for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. To include the patient�s last exam date on the label : Select Show Last Exam Date . To include the patient�s next appointment date on the label : Select Show Next Appointment Date . Type the number of lines per label in the Lines Per Label field. Click OK . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Patient Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. topics.title: Printing Charts
topics.body: Printing Charts Using the SoftDent Charting Module Printing Charts On the Restorative tab , y ou can print the initial, current, or treatment plan chart. You can also print treatment plan information, exam information, and clinical notes with the chart. Charts print on standard 8.5� x 11� paper on a variety of laser and inkjet printers and can be printed in color. To print a chart, a printer must be set up. To set up your printer, select File > Print Setup . The Print Setup window is displayed. Select the printer options and click OK . To print the chart: On the Restorative tab, select a chart (initial, current, or treatment plan), and select File > Print or click Print in the Main Charting toolbar. The Printing Information window is displayed. Select an option: To print the group number, date planned, planning doctor, procedure code and description, tooth/surface, estimated patient and insurance payments, and acceptance status associated with the treatment plan beneath the patient chart for both primary and secondary treatment plans : Select Treatment Plans . Select the appropriate checkboxes to customize the treatment plan information. To print information, including comments from the Soft Tissue exam and PSR data for exam histories beneath the patient chart : Select Exams . To print the note creation date, patient and doctor identification numbers, procedure code, tooth and surface, note reference and text, and whether the note is associated with a treatment plan beneath the patient chart : Select Clinical Notes . To only print the chart : Select None . Click OK . The Print window is displayed. Select the printer and the number of copies to print. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Claims
topics.body: Printing Claims Submitting and Tracking Claims Printing Claims The software comes with many standard forms. The insurance forms are cross-referenced so you can order forms from a new vendor without changing the form number associated with each plan. First, specify up to 10 forms that are used in your office. Then, when you print the claims, select one of the forms you specified. topics.title: Printing Contacts
topics.body: Printing Contacts Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts Printing Contacts You can print a list of contacts logged during a specific period for an individual or company . To print a record contact: Select an option: To print a contact for a patient record : In the Patient List window, select the patient. To print a contact for an insurance plan record : In the Insurance Plan List window, select the plan. To print a contact for a referring doctor record : In the REFERRING DR List window, select the doctor. To print a contact for a provider record : In the Provider List window, select the provider. To print a contact for an employer record : In the Employer List window, select the employer. To print a contact for an insurance company record : In the Insurance Company List window, select the company. To print a contact for a school record : In the School List window, select the school. To print a contact for a laboratory record : In the Labs List window, select the laboratory. To print a contact for a pharmacy record : In the Pharmacy List window, select the pharmacy. To print a contact for an account record : In the Account List window, select the account. To print a contact for a claim record: In the Claim List window, select the claim. To print a contact for a completed claim record: In the Completed Claim List window, select the claim. Select Contacts > By Date or Contacts > By Type from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Contacts for List window is displayed. Select the contact and click Print , or select Print from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Contact List Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates from the drop-down lists. Select Print one contact note per page to print the entire content of each note on a separate page. This option is disabled if you are exporting to Excel. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

Select Print Transactions . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output options and click OK . The Plan Transaction List Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates and click OK . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + P or select List Insurance Plans from the Power bar to access the Insurance Plan List window. Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

Select an option: To include labels for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . Select Patient Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. topics.title: Printing Laser Postcards
topics.body: Printing Laser Postcards Printing Laser Postcards When you print postcards and select the Laser Postcard File option, the postcards are sent to a file where they are stored until you are ready to print them. This is useful if you prefer to print all of your postcards at one time�to avoid tying up a printer throughout the day or to keep from having to switch between paper types. Four postcards are printed per page. This example uses Appointment Reminder postcards. The steps used to print laser postcards are the same, regardless of the type of postcards you are printing. To print laser postcards: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Labels/Postcards/Dir > Appt Remind Postcards . The Output Options window is displayed. Select Laser Postcard File and click OK . The Confirmation List Report Setup window is displayed. Select the criteria for the postcards, and click OK . Select Reports > Print Laser Postcards . The Laser Postcards window is displayed. Select an option: To print a specific postcard type: Select the checkbox next to the type of postcards you want to print (Appt Remind Postcards in this example), and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. To print all postcard types: Select the checkboxes next to the types of postcards you want to print, and click Print All on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the postcards should be cleared. Verify that the postcards have printed correctly, and click Yes . TIP: To increase the horizontal or vertical offsets, click Settings and type the values, in inches, in the fields. Related Topics Printing Laser Labels Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Month At A Glance Window Information
topics.body: Printing Month At A Glance Window Information Using the Scheduler > Using the Month At A Glance Window Printing Month At A Glance Window Information To print the Month At A Glance window: From the Month At A Glance window, display the information to print, and click Print . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Monthly Billing Statements
topics.body: Printing Monthly Billing Statements Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements > Printing Billing Statements Printing Monthly Billing Statements To print monthly billing statements: Select Reports > Accounting > Statements > Paper > Monthly . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Billing Run Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending names to include in the report using the drop-down lists in the Start and End fields. Select from the following options: To include billing statement information for accounts that contain a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude billing statement information for accounts that contain a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. Accounts that have been referred to a collection agency, are inactive, involved in a legal case, or have been flagged for exclusion are not included in the billing statement results. The C, I, L, and N user codes are displayed in the Exclude field by default. To apply a different billing charge to the billing statements : Type the information in the appropriate fields. To apply a different finance charge to the billing statements : Type the information in the appropriate fields. To itemize transactions for a specific number of days : Type the number of days in the Itemize transactions for the last _ days field. The default value is 45. To send statements only to patients who owe more than a certain amount : Type the minimum amount in the Send for Amount due greater than or equal to field. The default value is $0.01. To include a message in the billing statement results : Type the message in the Message field. To generate billing statements only for a specific provider : Select the provider identification number from the Doctors section. By default, billing statements are generated for all providers. Click OK . Related Job Aid: Running Billing Statements Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing the Daily Operatory Schedule Report
topics.body: Printing the Daily Operatory Schedule Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Handling the Daily Operatory Schedule Report Printing the Daily Operatory Schedule Report The Daily Operatory Schedule report lists all patients due in the office, their scheduled providers, and notes or alerts for all operatories. Sample Report To print the Daily Operatory Schedule report: Select Reports > Daily Op Schedule or select Reports > Recall/Appt > Daily Op Schedule > Print Daily Op Schedule. The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Daily Operatory Schedule Report window is displayed. Click the drop-down arrow to select a date other than today's date in the Date section. To print the report for a day on which your office is typically closed, click the arrow in the Date field and double-click the day on the calendar. Press Tab . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Type the number of copies of the schedule and notes to print in the Copies section. Select a template and book from the drop-down lists in the Options section. Type the number of rooms to print per page. Click Specify Rooms to select the rooms to include on the report. Type the number of hours to print per page. Click Specify Hours to indicate the range of hours to print on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Practice Central Entries
topics.body: Printing Practice Central Entries Getting Started > Using Practice Central > Accessing Information from Practice Central Printing Practice Central Entries To print Practice Central window content: Select View > Practice Central . The Practice Central window is displayed. Click Print . The Output Options window is displayed. Do one of the following: To preview the contents before printing, select Print Preview and click OK . To print the entries, click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!
