topics.title: Printing Recall Postcards
topics.body: Printing Recall Postcards Recalling Patients > Running Recall Reports Printing Recall Postcards You can print recall postcards to send to patients, notifying them to schedule an appointment for their next recall. You can also use the Motivated Recall option, which enables you to send postcards with different messages to patients for first, second, and third recall notices. The wording for each successive message can be made stronger to motivate patients to schedule their recall appointments. See Editing Recall Postcards for more information on postcard messages. Sample Postcard To print recall postcards: Select an option: To generate a list of patients who are due for normal recall : Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Last Exam . To generate a list of patients who are due for User-Defined recall : Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Perio . If you have not set up your Recall System or have upgraded from a previous version of the software and are running the first Recall report, the System Setting - PostCard Options window is displayed. Select an option and click OK . For more information, see Setting Up Recalls . If you change the name of your Normal Recall or User-Defined Recall system, the name of the report changes. For example, if the name of your User-Defined Recall system is Ortho, select Reports > Recall/Appt > Ortho . The Report Format window is displayed. The Postcards option is selected by default. Click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an option: To print individual postcards on a dot-matrix printer : Select Printer and click OK . To display the postcards : Select Print Preview and click OK . To print the postcards on a laser printer : Select Laser Postcard File and click OK to send the postcards to the batch file. Note: After selecting the criteria for your postcards, you must print the postcards from the batch file. To print the postcards, s elect Reports > Print Laser Postcards . The Laser Postcards window is displayed. The number of postcards to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select the type of postcards you want to print, and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the postcards should be cleared. Verify that the postcards have printed correctly, and click Yes . The Recall Postcard Setup window is displayed. To change the report title, type a name in the Title field. In the Notices section, select from the following options: To update the patient record each time the patient is included in the Recall report: Select Update Notices Sent Count . Each time a patient is included in the Recall report, the Notices field on the Recall tab of the patient record is updated. This enables you to identify the number of notices sent to a patient. Note : Select this option when using the Motivated Recall system. To indicate which patients should be included in the report: Select 0 Previous Notices to recall patients due to receive notice 1. Select 1 Previous Notice to recall patients due to receive notice 2. Select 2 Previous Notices to recall patients due to receive notice 3. Select 3 or more Previous Notices to recall patients who have already been sent 3 (or more) notices. Note : You can select more than one checkbox at a time. To recall patients who are due to receive their first, second, or third noti

topics.title: Printing Recall Labels
topics.body: Printing Recall Labels Recalling Patients > Running Recall Reports Printing Recall Labels You can print mailing labels for the list of patients needing recalls. Sample Label To print labels from the recall list: Select an option: To generate a list of patients who are due for normal recall : Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Last Exam . To generate a list of patients who are due for User-Defined recall : Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Perio . If you have not set up your Recall System or have upgraded from a previous version of the software and are running the first Recall report, the System Setting - PostCard Options window is displayed. Select an option and click OK . For more information, see Setting Up Recalls . If you change the name of your Normal Recall or User-Defined Recall system, the name of the report changes. For example, if the name of your User-Defined Recall system is Ortho, select Reports > Recall/Appt > Ortho . The Report Format window is displayed. Select Labels and click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an option: To print individual labels on a label printer : Select Printer and click OK . To display the labels : Select Print Preview and click OK . To print labels on a laser printer : Select Laser Label File and click OK to send the labels to the batch file. Note: After selecting the criteria for your labels, you must print the labels from the batch file. To print labels, s elect Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Recall Reminder Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . The Recall Label Setup window is displayed. To change the report title, type a name in the Title field. In the Notices section, select an option: To update the patient record each time the patient is included in the Recall report: Select Update Notices Sent Count . Each time a patient is included in the Recall report, the Notices field on the Recall tab of the patient record is updated. This enables you to identify the number of notices sent to a patient. To indicate which patients should be included in the report: Select 0 Previous Notices to recall patients due to receive notice 1. Select 1 Previous Notice to recall patients due to receive notice 2. Select 2 Previous Notices to recall patients due to receive notice 3. Select 3 or more Previous Notices to recall patients who have already been sent 3 (or more) notices. Note : You can select more than one checkbox at a time. To recall patients who are due to receive their first, second, or third notice, select the 0 Previous Notices, 1 Previous Notice , and 2 Previous Notices checkboxes. In the Dates section, select a date range for the report. In the Patient Names section, select the start and stop last names from the drop-down lists to generate the recall list for a specific alphabetical range of patients. In the Providers section, select the provider identification number from the drop-down list to generate the recall list for a specific provider. To generate the recall list for all providers, select 999 from the drop-down list. To include only patients assigned to t

topics.title: Printing Reports
topics.body: Printing Reports eServices > Using the eClaims Service Printing Reports To print reports: Select an option: In the eServices main window : Click Reports . From the main menu : Select Report Options > Reports . The Report List window is displayed. Select an option: To view a report : Click Preview File . To print individual reports : Select the report name and click Print File . To print all reports : Click PRINT ALL . Print all reports after every transmission. The reports include important account balance, claim status, rejection, or settlement information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Manually Printing Routing Slips
topics.body: Manually Printing Routing Slips Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Manually Printing Routing Slips Information printed on a routing slip: Scheduled procedures Treatment plan information Insurance information Reason for patient visit Account status Information about other patients assigned to the account Manually print routing slips for: Patients scheduled for an appointment for the current day. Walk-in appointments. Same-day appointments. Offices most often print routing slips the night before or day of an office visit. To print a routing slip automatically for each patient that is scheduled for an appointment on the current day, see Filling the InOffice Window . To print a routing slip: Select an option: From the Patient List window: -- Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. -- Type the patient name in the Find field and press Enter . Tip: To print the provider on a routing slip, select Reports > Recall/Appt > Routing Slips By Date . -- Select the record and select Patient Info/Forms > Routing Slip from the Options menu. From the Daybook window: Select an appointment in the Daybook, and select Print Routing Slip from the Express Bar. From the appointment submenu: Right-click on an appointment in the Daybook, and select Print Routing Slip from the appointment submenu. From the appointment card: Access the appointment card using a method described in Editing Appointment Card Information , and click Print Routing Slip . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Routing Slips
topics.body: Printing Routing Slips Printing Routing Slips The scheduled procedures, treatment plan information, insurance information, account status, and other information is printed on a routing slip. Related Topics Setting Routing Slip Preferences Manually Printing Routing Slips Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Settlement Reports
topics.body: Printing Settlement Reports eServices > Using the ePayments Service Printing Settlement Reports To reconcile credit card transactions , a Credit Card Settlement report must be generated at the end of each day. Credit card payment plan transactions display on settlement reports with an asterisk next to the credit card type. Sample Report To generate a Credit Card Settlement report: From the main menu, select Reports > Accounting > Credit Card Settlement . If there are pending authorizations, the Recurring Credit Card Payments window is displayed, and the authorization process with the credit card processor begins immediately. When the authorization is complete, the list is updated to display the status of each authorization: Red authorizations have failed. Blue authorizations have been authorized. Green authorizations have been settled. Click Print to print the list, if needed, then click Close . The Carestream ePayments Pending Transactions window is displayed. Select an option: To view the details of an authorization : Select the authorization and click View . To print the pending settlement transactions : Click Print . To view old credit card settlements : Click View Settled . Click the drop-down arrow in the Date field and select the date from the calendar. Click View List . Click Settle . The Carestream ePayments Settling With Bank window is displayed. To print a confirmation of the transaction, click Print Confirmation . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing a Single Billing Statement from the Patient Account
topics.body: Printing a Single Billing Statement from the Patient Account Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements > Printing and Batching Statements from the Account Window Printing a Single Billing Statement from the Patient Account To print a billing statement from the account record: Select an option: Select List > Account . Click Account List Window on the Power bar. In any window, press Ctrl + A. The Account List window is displayed in FIND mode. Click anywhere in the list area or press Enter to switch to SCAN mode. Type the account name in the Find field and press Enter. Select Billing Statement from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message that asks you if you want to print or batch the statement for the selected account is displayed. To print a billing statement for the selected account, click Yes . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Single Billing Statement Setup window is displayed. Select an option: To include a message in the billing statement results : Type the message in the Message field. To apply a different billing charge to the billing statements : Type the information in the appropriate fields. To apply a different finance charge to the billing statements : Type the information in the appropriate fields. To itemize transactions for a specific number of days : Type the number of days in the Itemize transactions for the last _ days field. The default value is 45. Select the guarantor to which the statement is addressed from the Send To Guarantor drop-down list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Treatment Plans
topics.body: Printing Treatment Plans Treatment Planning Printing Treatment Plans After entering a treatment plan in the software, you can print a copy of the plan, including estimated insurance amounts, for the patient to approve. To print a treatment plan: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the patient Transactions window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the InOffice window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Select Print Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Patient Treatment Plan Report window is displayed. Select the treatment plan you want to print from the drop-down list. Select an option: To print the report for all groups : Type 0 in the Select Treatment Group fields. To print a single group : Type the group number in the first Select Treatment Group field and 0 in the second. To print the report for several groups : Type the starting group number in the first Select Treatment Group field and the ending number in the second field. To print the fees by group : Select Break down fees by Group . To use insurance estimation amounts without including the patient�s year to date benefits : Select Print using insurance estimation amounts that were calculated without taking into account the patient�s used YTD benefits . If this checkbox is selected, the patient, insurance, and total fees are printed. If this checkbox is not selected, only the total fees are printed. To print the amount for each procedure : Select Print the fee for each procedure listed on the printout . Click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an option and click OK . The Treatment Plan Additional Setup window is displayed. Select from the following options: To add a pre-defined comment to the treatment plan: In the Additional Comments section, select the comment title from the Pre-defined comments drop-down list. Click OK . To add a new comment to the treatment plan that will not be used in the future: In the Additional Comments section, type the comment text in the text field. Click OK . To add a new comment to the treatment plan and save it for future use: In the Additional Comments section, type the comment in the text field, and select Save this comment for future use . Click OK . The Save Treatment Plan Comment window is displayed. Type a title for the comment, and click OK . To add a pre-defined financial arrangement note to the treatment plan : In the Financial Arrangements section, select the note title from the Pre-defined messages drop-down list. Click OK . To add a new financial arrangement note to the treatment plan that will not be used in the future : In the Financial Arrangements section, type the financial arrangement note text in the text field. Click OK . To add a new financial arrangement note to the treatment plan and save it for future use : In the Financial Arrangements section, type the comment in the text field, and select Save this comment for future use . Click OK . The Save Treatment Plan Financial Arrangement Text window is displayed. Type a title for the comment, and click OK . These

topics.title: Running the Provider Daily Summary Report
topics.body: Running the Provider Daily Summary Report Handling Reports > Accounting Reports Running the Provider Daily Summary Report The Provider Daily Summary report generates a list of charges, production adjustments, payments, balances, and collection to production ratios for the specified doctors. Sample Report To generate the Provider Daily Summary report: Select Reports > Accounting > Provider Daily Summary . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Provider Daily Summary Setup window is displayed. In the Through Date section, click the drop-down arrow to select the last date to include in the report from a calendar. In the Providers section, click the drop-down arrow to select the provider number from a list, or leave the first provider set to 999 to report on all providers. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools
topics.body: Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools You can enter provider, employer, and school information and associate it with an account, patient, and insurance claims record. Related Topics Handling Provider Information Handling Employer Information Handling School Information Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Account Payments
topics.body: Posting Account Payments Posting Payments Posting Account Payments You can post the following payments to an account record: Personal Insurance Adjustment Prepayments Credits Refunds Related Topics Posting Personal Payments Posting Adjustments Posting Refunds Transferring Credits Posting Prepayments Deleting Account Payments Posting Returned Checks Posting a Payoff Amount Due Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Posted Appointments Colors
topics.body: Setting Posted Appointments Colors Using the Scheduler > Using the History Appointments Window > Enabling History Appointments Setting Posted Appointments Colors The background color associated with posted appointments in the Daily Appointments window is also the background color used for posted appointments displayed in the History Appointments window. To set the background color associated with posted appointments, see Setting Appointment Properties . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Account Budget Plan Payments
topics.body: Posting Account Budget Plan Payments Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans Posting Account Budget Plan Payments To post an account budget plan payment: Select an option: In the Patient Transactions window : Select Account Budget Plan from the Options menu or Acct Budget Plan from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Account Transactions window : Select Account Budget Plan from the Options menu or Budget Plan from the Options section of the Express bar. In the Account List or Account windows : Select Account Budget Plan from the Options menu or Budget Plan (Ctrl+G) from the Options section of the Express bar. The Account Budget Plan List window is displayed. Click Add . The Account Budget Plan window is displayed in ADD mode. Type the last two digits of the identification number for the patient who is making a payment in the ID field. Select the payment code from the Code drop-down list. Select the collection doctor from the Dr$ drop-down list. Select the payment amount from the Amount drop-down list. If you selected a check payment : The Check No and Bank No fields are enabled. Type the check information in the appropriate fields. If you selected a Visa or MasterCard payment : The Account Budget Plan Visa/Mastercard Payment window is displayed. Select the charge amount from the Amount drop-down list. If your are using ePayments , type the appropriate information in the Credit Card section. Click OK . The Account Budget Plan window is displayed. Type any notes in the Notes field, and click OK . If you post a payment for the loan payoff amount, a message that prompts you to confirm the payment is displayed. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Bulk Insurance Payments
topics.body: Posting Bulk Insurance Payments Posting Payments > Posting Insurance Payments Posting Bulk Insurance Payments You can post insurance payments covering multiple patients that are not assigned to the same account. The bulk insurance payment process does not currently support credit card insurance payments. To post bulk insurance payments: Select Insurance > Insurance Payments > Bulk Insurance Payments . The Insurance Check Info window is displayed. Select the insurance company, insurance plan, and transaction code from the drop-down lists. If the payment includes a DMO/capitation plan service fee, enter the plan, plan fee, and plan doctor information to display the plan fee in the correct Plan Transactions window. The plan fee is subtracted from the check amount. You can distribute any remaining amount to the patients with outstanding claims. Type the amount of the insurance payment, the check number, and the bank number in the fields and ensure that the correct date is selected from the Date drop-down list. Select the billing and plan doctors from the drop-down lists. To submit completed claims covered by a secondary plan to the insurance batch file, select Print Secondary Claims . Click OK . The Insurance Payments window is displayed. Select an option: For a detailed list of Insurance Payments window options, see Using Insurance Payments Window Options . To edit the check information : Select Edit Check Info from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Insurance Check Info window is displayed. Make the changes and click OK . The Insurance Payments window is displayed. To list all outstanding claims with the specified insurance company and plan : Select Load Claims from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. All claims that meet the criteria are displayed. The number of loaded claims is displayed in the Claims field, and the difference between the total payments for each claim and the check amount is displayed in the Remaining field. If your office uses payment allocation, a claim might be displayed more than once if the payment is disbursed to more than one provider. If more than one doctor performed procedures on a claim, a separate entry is displayed for each doctor. To list the claims in order by patient last name, the date of service, or the patient Social Security number : Select Sort from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. To remove claims that are not included on the Explanation of Benefits (EOB) : Highlight the claim and select Remove from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. To edit the payment amount : Click Edit . Type the amount in the Payment column and click OK . I f this is not the final payment for the claim : Select Mark As Final Payment from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The asterisk (*) is removed from the Patient Name column. Click Post . The Insurance Payments window is displayed. The Remaining amount must be 0.00 to post the payment. If Update bluebook... in the Insurance System Settings window is selected : The Bluebook Update Method window is displayed. If any claims selected for final payment have secondary coverage and Print Secondary Claims in the Insurance Check Info window is selected : The secondary claim is printed. If any secondary claims are submitted : A query box prompting you to print all batched billing statements is displayed. Click Yes to print the statements and No to send the statements to a bat

topics.title: Posting Account Budget Plan Payments from the Account Payments Window
topics.body: Posting Account Budget Plan Payments from the Account Payments Window Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans > Posting Account Budget Plan Payments Posting Account Budget Plan Payments from the Account Payments Window To post an account budget plan payment from the Account Payments window: From the Account List window, select Edit from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Account Payments window is displayed in Payment View. Select Edit from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Payment column is enabled. Type the payment amount in the column and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a Patient Cash Payment
topics.body: Posting a Patient Cash Payment Posting Payments > Posting Patient Payments Posting a Patient Cash Payment When your office receives a single cash, credit card, or check payment for a patient�s balance, you can post the payment directly to the patient�s record. To post a cash payment: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Select Personal in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Personal Payment window is displayed. Select the payment code from the Code drop-down list, type the cash payment amount, and verify the date. Press Tab . To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Click Post . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Posted Transaction Options
topics.body: Using Posted Transaction Options Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Posted Transactions Using Posted Transaction Options You can d isplay the details, view attached note text, delete a transaction, and display the chart from the day the transaction was posted for each transaction displayed on the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. To use posted transaction options: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, or Treatment Planning tabs of the Chart for window, click the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select a posted transaction, right-click, and select an option: To display the transaction details : Select View . To view the clinical notes associated with the transaction : Select View Attached Note . To amend the note, click Amend Note Text , type the text, and click OK . To delete the transaction : Select Delete . A message asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . To view the chart details for the date on which the transaction was posted : Select View Chart as of . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting an Insurance Payment to an Account
topics.body: Posting an Insurance Payment to an Account Posting Payments > Posting Insurance Payments Posting an Insurance Payment to an Account To post an insurance payment to an account: Select an option: In the Account List window : Select the account and select Payments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Account window : Select Payments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Transactions from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Transactions from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Payments window is displayed. Select Insurance in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Insurance Payment window is displayed. The default insurance check payment code is displayed in the Code drop-down list. Type the check amount, check number, and bank number in the fields and verify the check date. This information is printed on the deposit slip. Select the claim number from the Claim drop-down list. You must select a valid outstanding claim. Select an option: If this is the final insurance payment : Select Final? . If this is not the final insurance payment : Deselect Final? . If the claim is denied : Select Denied? . If the claim is associated with a PPO plan, the Write Offs column is displayed in the window. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction, and type the information. Click Post . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting an Insurance Payment to a Patient
topics.body: Posting an Insurance Payment to a Patient Posting Payments > Posting Insurance Payments Posting an Insurance Payment to a Patient To post an insurance payment to a patient: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Transactions from the toolbar. In the Patient window : Select Transactions from the toolbar. The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Click Payments from the Options section of the Express bar. The Payments window is displayed. Select Insurance in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Insurance Payment window is displayed. The default insurance check payment code is displayed in the Code drop-down list. Type the check amount, select the claim number, verify that this is the final payment for this claim, and type the check number and bank number in the fields. Click Post . If the Update bluebook after insurance payments checkbox is selected in the Insurance Settings window, a message is displayed, asking if you want to update the bluebook. Click Yes or No . A message is displayed, asking if you want to send a billing statement. Click Yes or No . If you selected Yes, you are given the option of printing the statement now or printing to a batch. To print a statement now, click Yes . To print to a batch, click No . If you click Yes, the Output Options window is displayed. Select the option you want, and click OK . If you select Printer, the Single Billing Statement Setup window is displayed. When you have made your choices, click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Insurance Payments
topics.body: Posting Insurance Payments Posting Payments Posting Insurance Payments You can post insurance payments for: A patient An account You can also post PPO payments that contain: Write-offs Unassigned plan payments Adjustments Related Topics Using Insurance Payments Window Options Posting an Insurance Payment to a Patient Posting an Insurance Payment to an Account Posting Credit Card Insurance Payments Posting Bulk Insurance Payments Posting PPO Payments with Write-Offs Entering Insurance Payment Notes Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a Payoff Amount Due
topics.body: Posting a Payoff Amount Due Posting Payments > Posting Account Payments Posting a Payoff Amount Due You can post a check for the total amount due for an account. To post a payoff amount for an account: Select an option: In the Account List window : Select the account and select Payoff Amount Due from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Account window : Select Payoff Amount Due from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Payment Distribution window is displayed. If there is a budget plan balance, the Account Payments window is displayed. Select the code and type the check number and bank number in the appropriate fields. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a Patient Procedure on the Date of a Scheduled Appointment
topics.body: Posting a Patient Procedure on the Date of a Scheduled Appointment Handling Transactions and Codes > Posting Patient Procedures Posting a Patient Procedure on the Date of a Scheduled Appointment To post a patient procedure on the date of a scheduled appointment: In the Patient List window, select a patient and click Transactions on the toolbar. The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Click Add . The Appointment Card window is displayed. To edit the appointment card, click Edit , make changes, and click OK . To post the scheduled procedures, click Post . The procedures scheduled for the current date are posted, and the Transactions List window is displayed. The posted procedures are displayed in the list. Tip To post procedures from the patient record, click Transactions . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting a Patient Procedure from the Transactions Window
topics.body: Posting a Patient Procedure from the Transactions Window Handling Transactions and Codes > Posting Patient Procedures Posting a Patient Procedure from the Transactions Window You can post procedures to a patient record when the patient is not scheduled for an appointment on the current date. When posting a partial dental procedure, type tooth numbers in the Note field. To post a patient procedure from the Transactions window: In the Patient List window, select a patient and click Transactions on the toolbar. The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Click Add . The Transactions window is displayed. The office and patient identification numbers are displayed. Select the code to post from the Code drop-down list. If you use color-scheduling, the Description field is displayed in the assigned color. Select any tooth and surface information. If you are posting an ADA code linked to a CPT medical code, select the codes from the Diagnosis Codes drop-down lists. Select the production and collection doctors from the Production Dr and Collection Dr drop-down lists. Select the place and type of service from the Place of Service and Type of Service drop-down lists. These drop-down lists are only enabled when a medical code is selected. The default values for these fields are entered in the Medical Information section of the ADA/Transaction Codes window. If the code is insurable, select Insurable. If the procedure is posted to a Medicaid or DMO/capitation plan patient, this field defaults to the Cov�d field value in the Insurance Plan Coverage window. Type any notes in the Notes field and click OK . Tip To post procedures from the patient record, click Transactions . The default production doctor is the patient�s default doctor. The default collection doctor is the assign-to doctor in the provider record. This option is not selected if Submit To Insurance is not selected in the code record or if the patient is uninsured. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Patient Checks
topics.body: Posting Patient Checks Posting Payments > Posting Patient Payments Posting Patient Checks To post a check payment: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions. In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Select Personal in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Personal Payment window is displayed. To post a check payment, type the check amount, check number, and bank number and verify the check date. This information is printed on the deposit slip. Press Tab . To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction, and type the information. Click Post . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Patient Procedures
topics.body: Posting Patient Procedures Handling Transactions and Codes Posting Patient Procedures You can post patient procedures directly: To the Transactions window In the InOffice window In the Appointment Card window Related Topics Using the Patient Transactions Window Posting a Patient Procedure on the Date of a Scheduled Appointment Posting a Patient Procedure from the Transactions Window Auto Posting from the InOffice Removing a Chart Graphic and Re-Charting a Procedure Using the Assistant in Transactions Windows Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Patient Payments
topics.body: Posting Patient Payments Posting Payments Posting Patient Payments You can post the following payments to a patient record: Cash Check Credit card payments Related Topics Posting a Patient Cash Payment Posting Patient Checks Posting Patient Credit Card Payments Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting A Scheduled Treatment Plan Procedure
topics.body: Posting A Scheduled Treatment Plan Procedure Treatment Planning > Posting Treatment Plan Procedures Posting a Scheduled Treatment Plan Procedure When you post a scheduled treatment plan procedure in the patient�s Transactions window: It is no longer displayed in the Treatment Plan List window. The transaction type is changed from primary to actual. A notation is displayed in the Transactions window. When you check out patients with treatment plans : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu to display the Treatment Plan List window. In this window: You can see whether any of the procedures are treatment plan items. If you don't check this information at the time of checkout : You may post invalid treatment plan items because the procedures were changed at the time of service. For more information, see Posting Patient Procedures . If you post a transaction that matches a treatment plan procedure, that procedure is automatically deleted from the treatment plan. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Treatment Plan Procedures
topics.body: Posting Treatment Plan Procedures Treatment Planning Posting Treatment Plan Procedures Use the software to post scheduled and unscheduled: Treatment groups Procedures Related Topics Posting a Scheduled Treatment Plan Procedure Posting an Unscheduled Treatment Plan Procedure Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Unassigned Plan Payments
topics.body: Posting Unassigned Plan Payments Posting Payments Posting Unassigned Plan Payments To post a DMO/capitation or Medicaid plan that is not assigned to a specific patient or claim: Select List > Ins Plan . The Insurance Plans list window is displayed in FIND mode. Select the plan and double-click or press Enter. The Insurance Plan window is displayed. Press Enter. The window is displayed. Select Transactions from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Plan Transactions List window is displayed. Click Add . The Plan Transactions window is displayed. Insurance plan transactions are displayed as separate line items on the daysheet and are not included in the current day�s receivables total. Select the code, collection doctor, date, and payment amounts from the drop-down lists. You can select collection or adjustment codes only. If you selected a check payment code, type the check and bank numbers in the appropriate fields. Capitation payments are included in the total of the checks listed on the deposit slip, unless they have been posted as a separate check. Type any notes in the Notes field and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient First Visit Report
topics.body: Running the Patient First Visit Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Patient First Visit Report The Patient First Visit report generates a list of new patients with the dates of the first visits. Sample Report To generate a list of patients� first visits: Select Reports > Patient > First Visit . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The New Patient List Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending first visit date ranges from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To compare the first visit information entered in the Date Range of First Visit section to a previous date range : Type the starting and ending dates in the Comparison Range section, and select Compare Date Ranges . To restrict the report to a specific provider : Type the provider identification number in the Provider field. To include all doctor and patient referral information in the report : Select Include Referral Information . If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Patient and Account Information
topics.body: Viewing Patient and Account Information Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Viewing Patient and Account Information You can view and update patient and account records from a patient appointment that is displayed in the InOffice window. To view patient information from the InOffice window: Select the appropriate entry. Select Patient from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The record for the patient is displayed in the Patient window. You can also update patient information from this window. To view account information from the InOffice window: Select the appropriate entry. Select Account from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The account record for the patient is displayed in the Patient window. You can also update patient account information from this window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding the Patient Appointments Window
topics.body: Understanding the Patient Appointments Window Scheduling Appointments Understanding the Patient Appointments Window If you are scheduling an appointment from the Patient List or Patient windows, the Patient Appointments window is displayed. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu when using Enhanced Workspace are: Edit Appt: Displays the patient's Appointment Card window in edit mode. Use this command to change a patient's appointment. Cancel Appt: Use this command to cancel the selected appointment. Appt Book : Displays the current date in the Daily Appointments window. Schedule Family : Displays the Family Recall window. Use this command to schedule recall appointments for everyone associated with the patient's account. Auto Schedule : Displays the Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters for window. Use this command to set a date range within which to auto schedule a patient appointment. See Setting the Auto Scheduling Book . Instant Recall : Displays the first suitable open time slot after the next recall due date in the Daily Appointments window. Go To Recall Date : Displays the first suitable recall date in the Daily Appointments window. The date displayed in the window is dependant upon the interval of recall and the date of the patient's last exam. If the patient is overdue, the next day is displayed in the window. Put in Tickler : This command is enabled only after an appointment is canceled. Use this command to place the appointment in the Tickler list. Place in Short Call Tickler : This command is enabled only after an appointment is canceled. Use this command to place the appointment in the Tickler list, and mark it as "short call" to indicate that the patient is willing to be rescheduled on short notice if an appointment becomes available. T reatment Plan : Displays the patient's treatment plan items in the Treatment Plan List window. Use this command to schedule any or all treatment plan procedures. Confirm : Displays the Confirm Appt window. Use this command to confirm patient information. After you confirm the appointment, you are prompted to log a contact for the call. Print Appointments : Select an appointment and use this command to print the appointment details. Print Reminder : Select an appointment and use this command to print a patient reminder postcard. Make Referral : Displays the Patient Referral window. Use this command to record an outgoing referral for the patient. The information you enter here is displayed in the Patient Appointments window and on the Info tab of the patient record. Auto Dialer : Displays the Patient Phone Numbers window. If you have a modem set up in the software, you can dial a specific number associated with a patient. If you do not have a modem, this information is displayed for your reference. Related Topics Viewing Appointments for a Family Scheduling Patient Appointments Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Patient Budget Plan Defaults
topics.body: Setting Patient Budget Plan Defaults Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans Setting Patient Budget Plan Defaults To set patient budget plan defaults: Select System > Change System Settings > General System . The General System Settings window is displayed in SCAN mode. Double-click in a field or click Edit to switch to EDIT mode. Select the appropriate credit code from the Credit Code drop-down list. The default code for this field is 58.90, but you can use any code between 50.00 and 59.89. The code enables the software to transfer the charges for the procedure to the credit amount for the patient balance when you set up a patient budget plan. The credit amount is then divided by the number of payments set for the patient budget plan to determine the payment amount. Type the typical patient budget plan duration (in months) in the Default No. Months field. Example : If most patients pay for treatment over a year, type 12. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient Budget Plan Report
topics.body: Running the Patient Budget Plan Report Budget Planning > Running Budget Plan Reports Running the Patient Budget Plan Report The Patient Budget Plan report generates a list of patients with budget plans. The report includes the patient's ID number; name and address; and specific budget plan information, such as cash price, down payment, monthly payment, and so on. Sample Report To run the Patient Budget Plan report: Select Reports > Accounting > Budget Plan Reports > Patient . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Patient Budget Plan Report window is displayed. Select an option: To restrict the report to a specific patient user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude a specific patient user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. To restrict the report to a specific set of patients : Select the starting and ending patient identification numbers from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Patient Budget Plan Window Options
topics.body: Using Patient Budget Plan Window Options Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans Using Patient Budget Plan Window Options The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: Edit Ortho Case : Use this command to edit the fields that are located in the Ortho Insurance Case Information section of the Patient Budget Plan window. Edit Budget Plan : Use this command to edit the fields that are located in the Patient Budget Plan section of the Patient Budget Plan window. Coupons : Prints payment coupons for the patient budget plan. Truth in Lending Form : Prints the lending agreement information for the patient to sign. Post Adjustment : Posts an adjustment to the Patient Transactions window when the patient is paying a down payment on the patient budget plan. Reset Budget Plan : Resets the information that is displayed in the window. Use this command only if you make a mistake before posting the adjustment. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Billing Statements
topics.body: Printing Billing Statements Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Printing Billing Statements Billing statements detail transactions posted to each account within a specific date range. Sample Report When generating billing statements, you can also apply the following charges against overdue accounts: Billing Finance You can generate the following billing statements: Monthly Electronic Batched You can also regenerate the following billing statements for a specific date: Regular Electronic Related Topics Printing Monthly Billing Statements Reprinting Billing Statements Need more information? To generate a list of patients assigned to insurance companies by company name: Select Reports > Insurance > Patients by Company > By Company Name . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patients By Insurance Company Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending company names. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patients by Insurance Plan by Plan Name Report
topics.body: Running the Patients by Insurance Plan by Plan Name Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Plan Reports Running the Patients by Insurance Plan by Plan Name Report The Patients by Insurance Plan by Plan Name report generates an alphabetical list of patients assigned to insurance plans, sorted by plan name. To generate a list of patients assigned to insurance plans by plan name, follow these steps: Select Reports > Insurance > Patients by Plan > By Plan Name . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patients By Insurance Plan Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending plan names. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Patient Credit Card Payments
topics.body: Posting Patient Credit Card Payments Posting Payments > Posting Patient Payments Posting Patient Credit Card Payments To process credit card payments directly through the software, you must use ePayments. Contact your sales representative for more information. To post a patient credit card payment: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select Payments . The Payments window is displayed. Select Personal in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Personal Payment window is displayed. Select the code from the Code drop-down list. Press Tab . To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction and type the information. Click Post . The Carestream ePayments Charge Authorization window is displayed. Swipe the card, or manually enter the information and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Patient Check Out Options
topics.body: Setting Patient Check Out Options Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions > Checking Out Patients Setting Patient Check Out Options To set up patient check out options: Select System > Change System Settings > General System . The General System Settings window is displayed. Click Edit . Select an option: To display the InOffice window after posting appointment procedures and receiving any patient payment when checking out a patient from the InOffice window : Select Do not process from the Insurance/Walkout/Appointment questions after transaction screen drop-down list. To display a window that enables you to select whether to submit an insurance claim, s chedule the next patient appointment, print a walkout statement, or reconcile treatment plan items when checking out a patient in the InOffice window : Select Ask to process from the Insurance/Walkout/Appointment questions after transaction screen drop-down list. To submit an insurance claim, schedule the next patient appointment, print a walkout statement or reconcile treatment plan items when checking out a patient in the InOffice window : Select Automatically process from the Insurance/Walkout/Appointment questions after transaction screen drop-down list. To automatically display the Payments window when you post a payment code during checkout in the InOffice window: Select Automatically display the Payments screen when posting a payment code . Click OK . Click Close . Related Topics Reconciling Treatment Plans Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing and Reviewing Patient Clinical Notes
topics.body: Viewing and Reviewing Patient Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes > Handling Patient Clinical Notes Viewing and Reviewing Patient Clinical Notes A clinical note is displayed in black text. If the note is associated with an initial chart, it is displayed in green text. Primary treatment plan notes are displayed in red. Secondary treatment plan notes are displayed in Magenta. Tertiary treatment plan notes are displayed in Aqua. Y is displayed in the TP column of treatment plan notes. To view patient clinical notes: Select an option: To view the patient�s clinical notes : Click the Notes tab, or select Clinical Notes > View Clinical Notes . The Patient Clinical Notes List window is displayed. In this window, you can add and amend clinical notes. You can also view the note history and images associated with the notes. To review the patient�s clinical notes in the order in which they were created : Select Clinical Notes > Review > Patient Notes by Date . The Patient Clinical Notes by Date List window is displayed. In this window, you can add and amend clinical notes. You can also view the note history and images associated with the notes. To review the patient�s clinical notes in order by tooth : Select Clinical Notes > Review > Patient Notes by Tooth . The Patient Clinical Notes by Tooth No. List window is displayed. In this window, you can add and amend clinical notes. You can also view the note history and images associated with the notes. To view the images associated with a note, select the note and click View Images . The Images for Note window is displayed. To amend the notes, see Amending Patient Clinical Notes . To print clinical notes, see Printing Clinical Notes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

Calling Patient : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are calling a patient. Checking-in Patient : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are checking in a patient. Confirming Appointment : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are confirming a patient appointment. Editing Patient : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are editing a patient appointment. Making New Patient Appointment : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are making an appointment for a new patient. Entering Appointment Book : Displays a TeamTalk note when you access the appointment book from a patient record. Entering Chart : Displays a TeamTalk note when you access the charting module from a patient record. Moving Patient : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are moving a patient record. Prescribing Drug : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are prescribing a drug for a patient. Printing Routing Slip : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are printing a routing slip for a patient. Printing Walkout Statement : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are printing a walkout statement for a patient. Refilling Drug : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are refilling a drug for a patient. Removing from In-Office : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are removing a patient from the InOffice window. Submitting Insurance : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are submitting an insurance claim for a patient. Viewing Budget Plan : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing a budget plan associated with a patient. Viewing Contact List : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing a contact list associated with a patient. Viewing Guarantor/Ins. Info : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing guarantor or insurance information associated with a patient. Viewing Medical History : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing a patient medical history. Viewing Prescriptions : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing a patient prescription. Viewing Questionnaires : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing a patient's questionnaire. Viewing Scheduled Appointments : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing a patient's scheduled appointment. Viewing Tickler Entries : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing Tickler entries associated with a patient. Viewing Transactions : Displays a TeamTalk note when you are viewing patient transactions. topics.title: Setting User-Defined Recalls for a Patient
topics.body: Setting User-Defined Recalls for a Patient Recalling Patients > Setting Up Recalls Setting User-Defined Recalls for a Patient You can recall patients for non-routine procedures like orthodontics, customize the User-Defined Recall information for each patient, and override the default frequency, codes, notices, and time units. If you do not select a code that triggers a recall or an explosion code, the Perio date is not reset when the patient keeps a recall appointment, and the patient record remains overdue for recall until you manually change the date. To set a User-Defined Recall procedure for a patient: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click a patient. The patient record is displayed in the Patient window. Click Recall . The User-Defined Recall information is displayed in the Perio section. Select an option: To set the User-Defined Recall frequency for the user : Type a frequency in the Frequency field. If you change the name of your User-Defined Recall system, the name of the Perio field and Perio section in the Patients window change. To set a different User-Defined Recall procedure : Select the Code drop-down list. The ADA/Transaction Codes window is displayed. Double-click a User-Defined Recall code. The code is displayed in the Code drop-down list in the patient record. To set the number of time slots the User-Defined Recall procedure requires in the scheduler : Type a number in the TU field. I f you update the notices sent count when running the User-Defined Recall report, the value in the Notices field is updated every time a recall notice is sent to the patient. If this option is not selected when running the Recall report, the value in the Notices field is always 0. If any procedures selected in the User-Defined Recall System section of the Recall Settings window are posted for the patient, a recall is triggered. Click OK . To generate a list of overdue patients, run the User-Defined Recall report. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patients by Discount List Report
topics.body: Running the Patients by Discount List Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Patients by Discount List Report The Patients by Discount List report generates a list of patients that are using a specified discount plan. Sample Report To generate a list of patients that are using a discount program: Select Reports > Patient > With Discounts . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patient By Discount List window is displayed. Select a discount to generate results for from the Discount drop-down list. Select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patients By Discount Report
topics.body: Running the Patients By Discount Report Posting Payments > Handling Discounts Running the Patients By Discount Report The Patients By Discount report g enerates a list of patients assigned to a specific discount schedule. For each patient, the report includes: Identification number Name Address Phone number Balance Insurance information User code information To run the Patients By Discount report: Select Reports > Patient > With Discounts . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option, and click OK . The Patient List Report window is displayed. Select the discount, and select the starting and ending patients from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . The ADA/Transaction Codes report contains a column that indicates if the code is included in the discount schedule. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: PracticeToGo Audit Trail
topics.body: PracticeToGo Audit Trail Using PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo System Settings PracticeToGo Audit Trail If you have enabled the PracticeToGo audit trail in the System Settings window, you can use the audit trail to track usage of the PracticeToGo feature, including access violations. For information on enabling the PracticeToGo audit trail, see PracticeToGo System Settings . You can view the PracticeToGo audit trail on your computer screen or print it out. To view the audit trail: Select System > View Audit Trails > PracticeToGo Audit Trail . The Audit Trail window is displayed. Use the scroll bar to scroll through the list, and double-click an entry to view the detail for the entry. Click Close twice. To print the audit trail: Select Reports > Audit Trail > PracticeToGo Audit Trail > By Date or By User. The Output Options window is displayed. Click OK . The Audit Trail List Report window is displayed. Depending on the report you selected, type either the starting and ending audit entry dates or the starting and ending user names. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!
