topics.title: PracticeToGo and the Microsoft Outlook Interface
topics.body: PracticeToGo and the Microsoft Outlook Interface Using PracticeToGo PracticeToGo and the Microsoft Outlook Interface The SoftDent data that transfers to Microsoft Outlook is displayed within Microsoft Outlook program categories. To access appointments and practice financials: Open the Outlook Calendar. To access referring doctor information, end-of-day call-backs, the pharmacy list, and prescription information: Open Outlook Contacts. To access your memos: Open Outlook Tasks. You can reach any of this information when you are running Microsoft Outlook. The transfer of data from Microsoft Outlook to SoftDent is accomplished through the SD Commands. When you insert a SoftDent Date Book, Address, or Memo command on the appropriate entry in Microsoft Outlook, specific information is sent back to SoftDent during the next synchronization. Related Topics Viewing SoftDent Data in Microsoft Outlook Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: PracticeToGo Operation
topics.body: PracticeToGo Operation Using PracticeToGo PracticeToGo Operation The PracticeToGo feature has two integration options: PracticeToGo with the Palm OS platform: The Palm-connected organizer and the PC SoftDent program integrate through two software pieces: SoftDent conduit: Part of the SoftDent program, the SoftDent conduit is registered with the HotSync Manager during the initial SoftDent-Palm OS integration. HotSync Manager: Part of the Palm Desktop software that runs on the PC. Both software pieces are activated during data synchronization. If you are running the Palm desktop software and have a HotSync cradle hooked up to your PC, SoftDent will automatically detect the Palm OS platform and prompt you to integrate it with PracticeToGo. PracticeToGo with Microsoft Outlook 2000: Microsoft Outlook 2000 and the PC SoftDent software integrate directly with each other when you select the Use Microsoft Outlook 2000 option with PracticeToGo. You must have the HotSync cradle connected to your desktop PC or laptop computer to perform integration or synchronization between PracticeToGo and the Palm OS Platform. PracticeToGo Security Features PracticeToGo security settings include rights to change PracticeToGo system settings and transfer data. Further, PracticeToGo user preferences enable the user to mark data-syncing options as "private." Practice financial information and the patient list are automatically marked Private on the Palm organizer and in the Microsoft Outlook software; however, it is up to you to set up security on your Palm organizer or in the Microsoft Outlook software so that others cannot view private information. Tip It is a good idea to limit PracticeToGo System Setting rights to the system administrator (user ID 1). Additionally, with the Palm OS platform, the user name is matched with a user name and password in SoftDent, and the Palm security rights of the SoftDent user are checked before any data is transferred. This ensures that only the authorized user can transfer data to a specific Palm organizer and that no other Palm organizer can be used to retrieve your data. Related Topics SoftDent-Palm Integration Security PracticeToGo User Preferences Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: PracticeToGo User Preferences
topics.body: PracticeToGo User Preferences Using PracticeToGo PracticeToGo User Preferences The PracticeToGo User Preferences window enables you to control settings for data transfer with the PracticeToGo feature. Every user of the PC SoftDent software who has security rights to use PracticeToGo can set user preferences for the data he/she syncs. Related Topics Activating Data Transfer to the Palm Organizer Transferring Data by Date Range or Interval Data Transfer Options to the Palm Organizer Creating a Patient List for Individual Referring Doctors Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: PracticeToGo Security
topics.body: PracticeToGo Security Using PracticeToGo PracticeToGo Security After you have integrated PracticeToGo with either the Palm OS platform or Microsoft Outlook, you should make security settings to indicate who is authorized to change PracticeToGo settings and/or transfer data. Both the Palm organizer and SoftDent have built-in security settings. Some settings apply to all users, and some are based on the user name. Before any data is transferred, the user name on the Palm organizer is matched with a user name and password in SoftDent, and the Palm privileges of the SoftDent user are checked. This ensures that only the authorized user can transfer data to a specific Palm organizer and that no other Palm organizer can be used to retrieve your data. To mark a data category on your Palm organizer or in the Microsoft Outlook as private, select System > PracticeToGo > User Preferences and select the appropriate settings. Practice financial and patient information is marked Private by default. It is up to the user to set up security on the Palm organizer and in Microsoft Outlook so that others cannot view private information. Related Topics Setting PracticeToGo Security Rights Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: PracticeToGo System Settings
topics.body: PracticeToGo System Settings Using PracticeToGo PracticeToGo System Settings There are several options that affect the use of the PracticeToGo feature on this computer. You must have Change PracticeToGo System Settings security rights to access these settings, and it is recommended that these rights be limited to the system administrator (user ID 1). To make changes to PracticeToGo system settings: Select System > PracticeToGo > System Settings . The PracticeToGo System Settings window is displayed. Select from the following options: PracticeToGo audit trail enabled: Select this option to have the PC SoftDent software create an audit trail showing all users who logged on and what data was transferred. Only log errors or access violations: Select this option to have the audit trail record only login errors and access violations. Make users login every sync with SoftDent: Select this option to force the user to log in when he or she presses the HotSync button. Otherwise, after the initial synchronization, a synchronization occurs automatically each time the HotSync button is pressed. Enable new memo notification on the network: Select this option to have memo notification messages display in PC SoftDent software after PracticeToGo memos are transferred from the Palm organizer or the Microsoft Outlook software. For more information about SoftDent Commands, see Sending Data Back to the SoftDent Software . Enable new memo notification on this machine: Select this option to have memo notification messages display on this specific PC after PracticeToGo memos are transferred from the Palm organizer or the Microsoft Outlook software. For more information about SoftDent Commands, see Sending Data Back to the SoftDent Software . Sync with Outlook when entering the program on this machine: Select this option to automatically activate syncing between PracticeToGo and the Microsoft Outlook software upon SoftDent software startup. This option is only available when using the Microsoft Outlook software setup. Sync with Outlook when exiting the program on this machine: Select this option to automatically activate syncing between PracticeToGo and the Microsoft Outlook software upon exiting SoftDent software. This option is only available when using the Microsoft Outlook software setup. Socket Notification Port: If you have more than one data set in your SoftDent software installation (for example, two doctors in an office maintaining separate records), the port numbers can be set differently so that memo notifications from a sychronized Palm organizer working off one data set will not be displayed on computers working off the other data set. The port numbers can be set arbitrarily in the range of 10000 � 65534. The default is 13005. Time out for loading SoftDent: Enter a time limit in this field. This ensures that if the SoftDent conduit is not recognized by the HotSync Manager, or the connection fails for any other reason, the HotSync Manager will cease trying to connect with the PC SoftDent software after the time limit you set is reached. This option is available only when using the Palm OS platform setup. Click OK . Related Topics PracticeToGo Audit Trail Disabling PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform Re-Integrating PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform Using the Interface to Palm OS Platform Command Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patients by Insurance Company by Company ID Report
topics.body: Running the Patients by Insurance Company by Company ID Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Company Reports Running the Patients by Insurance Company by Company ID Report The Patients by Insurance Company by Company ID report generates an alphabetical list of patients assigned to plans offered by an insurance company, sorted by company ID. Sample Report To generate a list of patients assigned to insurance companies by company ID: Select Reports > Insurance > Patients by Company . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patients By Insurance Company Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending identification numbers. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patients by Insurance Plan by Plan ID Report
topics.body: Running the Patients by Insurance Plan by Plan ID Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Plan Reports Running the Patients by Insurance Plan by Plan ID Report The Patients by Insurance Plan by Plan ID report generates an alphabetical list of patients assigned to insurance plans, sorted by plan ID. Sample Report To generate a list of patients assigned to insurance plans by plan ID: Select Reports > Insurance > Patients by Plan . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patients By Insurance Plan Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending plan identification numbers. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient Instant Recall Procedures Utility
topics.body: Running the Patient Instant Recall Procedures Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running the Instant Recall Codes Setup Utilities Running the Patient Instant Recall Procedures Utility Run this utility to modify the normal frequency and procedure, the alternating procedure, the user-defined frequency and procedures (if applicable), and the time units for recall codes for all patients at one time. Adult and child recall information is handled separately. To run the patient instant recall procedures utility: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Instant Recall Codes Setup > Patient Instant Recall Procedures . The Patient Instant Recall Setup window is displayed. Select an option: To set recall values in all patient records that do not already have recall settings : Select Set All Patient Recall Procedures . To set these values in all patient records : Select Replace Patient Recall Procedures . To modify recall information for adults, click the Adult tab, select the appropriate checkboxes, and type a new default number in the To column. To modify recall information for children, click the Child tab, select the appropriate checkboxes, and type a new default number in the To column. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Lab Case Status by Patient
topics.body: Viewing Lab Case Status by Patient Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Lab Cases > Viewing a Lab Case Status Viewing Lab Case Status by Patient To view a case status by patient, select an option: From the Patient List window : Select the appropriate patient and select Clinical Management from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A submenu is displayed. Select View Lab Cases . From the Patient window : Select Clinical Management from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A submenu is displayed. Select View Lab Cases . From the patient chart: Click the Labs tab, or click Display the Patient's Lab Cases on the Common toolbar. From the patient Transactions window : Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. From the Electronic Router window : Select Lab Cases from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Lab case List window is displayed. Each lab case associated with the selected patient is displayed in the list. Lab cases that are at the lab : Displayed in green text. Lab cases that have been returned to your office : Displayed in blue text. Lab cases that are complete : Displayed in red text. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient List by Birthday Report
topics.body: Running the Patient List by Birthday Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Patient List by Birthday Report The Patient List by Birthday report generates a list of patients who celebrate a birthday within a specific date range. Sample Report To generate a list of patients by birthday: Select Reports > Patient > Birthday . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The List Patients by Birthday Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. To restrict the report to a specific birthday range : Select the starting and ending dates from the Birthdates section. To restrict the report to a specific age group : Select the starting and ending ages from the Age section. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient List by ID Report
topics.body: Running the Patient List by ID Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Patient List by ID Report The Patient List by ID report generates a list of patients by identification number. Sample Report To generate a list of patients by identification number: Select Reports > Patient > ID Order . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The List Patients by ID Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient identification numbers from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Patient List and Patient Window Notations
topics.body: Understanding Patient List and Patient Window Notations Setting Up Patients > Patient List and Patient Window Options Understanding Patient List and Patient Window Notations Notations are displayed in the Patient List and Patient window title bars to provide additional information about a patient. The notations that can be displayed in the Patient List and Patient window title bars are: HIST : A patient history file exists. LC : An open patient lab case exists. OBP : An ortho budget plan or patient budget plan exists. TP : A treatment plan exists. MOVED : The patient record has been moved from one account to another. BP : An account budget plan exists. PIX: The patient has a portrait. R elated Topics Patient List and Patient Window Options Using Quick Letters Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Patient List and Patient Window Options
topics.body: Understanding Patient List and Patient Window Options Setting Up Patients Understanding Patient List and Patient Window Options In the Patient List and Patient windows, you can view a list of transactions, appointments, images, documents, guarantors, and contacts associated with a patient. Use the drop-down list in the upper-right of the window to view the other patients assigned to the account. Tip Patients' names are displayed in several colors: Black�Normal Red�Medical alert Blue�Overdue recall Fuchsia�New patient Note: A CareCredit icon displayed in color in the upper-right of the Patient window indicates that the patient is a CareCredit customer; an icon displayed in B&W indicates that the patient is not a CareCredit customer. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu when using Enhanced Workspace are: Transactions : Views the patient transaction history. Eligibility : Checks the patient on-line insurance eligibility information. This command is only enabled if you use eVerifications insurance eligibility. CareCredit Integration : Access the CareCredit integration to perform account lookups, complete online applications, and use a payment calculator. eReminders Settings: Displays the permitted reminder delivery methods for the patient. This command is enabled only if you have enrolled in eReminders. Appointments : Views scheduled patient appointments; schedules patient or family appointments. Treatment Plan : Sets up and prints treatment plan procedures, accepts or declines treatment; and submits transactions for pre-authorization. Appt Book > Patient's Recall : Views the patient's next recall appointment. Appt Book > Patient's Next Appt : Views the patient's next appointment. Appt Book > Today : Views the current date in the Daily Appointments window. Portrait > Show Portrait : Select this command to view the portrait associated with the patient. Portrait > Create Portrait : Adds a patient portrait. Documents : Views existing or captures new documents to attach to the patient's record. eForms > Review eForms: Displays completed eForms for the selected patient. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. eForms > Patient Settings: Enables or disables eForms delivery for the patient or responsible party. This option is enabled only if you purchased eForms. Clinical Management > Patient Medical History : Views patient medical history for the selected appointment. Clinical Management > Update Med Alert Date : Updates the Alert Updated On field on the Info tab of the Patient window with the current date. Clinical Management > Chart : Views the patient chart. Clinical Management > View Clinical Notes : Views, adds, edits, and deletes patient clinical notes. Clinical Management > View Lab Cases : Views, edits, and deletes patient lab cases. Clinical Management > Prescription History : Views patient prescriptions. Clinical Management > Outgoing Referring History : Views patient referral history; refers patient to another doctor. Clinical Management > Missing Teeth : Views missing teeth and annotates additional missing teeth. Show Images : Displays patient images at the bottom of the window. Dental Imaging : Enabled if you have purchased CS Imaging software. MedVisor: Starts the MedVisor software. This command is enabled only if you have installed the MedVisor software. Other Management > Other Patients Referred : Views a list of patients referred by the selected patient; adds, edits, a

topics.title: Running the Patient Telephone Directory Report
topics.body: Running the Patient Telephone Directory Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Patient Telephone Directory Report The Telephone Directory report generates an alphabetical list of patients and their phone numbers. Sample Report To generate a patient telephone directory: Select Reports > Patient > Phone Directory . The Select Patients window is displayed. Select an option: To include information for patients associated with a specific user code from the directory : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients associated with a specific user code from the directory : Type the code in the Exclude field. Click OK . A message is displayed. Click OK . A message is displayed. topics.title: Running the Patient Production/Collection Report
topics.body: Running the Patient Production/Collection Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Patient Production/Collection Report The Patient Production/Collection report generates a list of production patients and collection patients for a provider during a specific date range. Sample Report To run the Patient Production/Collection report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Patient Production/Collection Report . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Production/Collection Report window is displayed. Select the identification numbers for the providers to include in the report from the Provider section. Type the percentage of patients to include in the report in the Percentile section. Select the start and stop date range from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include patients that have no production or collection amounts during the time frame: Select Include Patients with $0.00 Productions/Collections . To generate production percentage information in the report : Select Run by Production . To generate collection percentage information in the report : Deselect Run by Production . Click OK . Tip Type 999 in the first field to include production forecast information for all providers. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient Prescriptions Report
topics.body: Running the Patient Prescriptions Report Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Running Pharmacy and Prescription Reports Running the Patient Prescriptions Report The Patient Prescriptions report generates a list of prescribed drugs according to their issue dates. The report includes the prescription ID, name, description, date issued, quantity, and refill number. Sample Report To run the Patient Prescriptions report: Select Reports > RX/Pharmacies/Labs > Patient Prescriptions . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Patient Prescriptions Report window is displayed. In the Prescription Dates section, click the drop-down arrows in the First and Last fields to select prescription dates from a calendar. To use color/shading in the report, select the Include Color/Shading checkbox. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Entering Patient Questionnaire Responses
topics.body: Entering Patient Questionnaire Responses Handling Patient Questionnaires Entering Patient Questionnaire Responses After a questionnaire has been completed by a patient, you can enter the results into the software for tabulation. To enter patient questionnaire responses: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Other Patient Management > Patient Questionnaire from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Other Patient Management > Patient Questionnaire from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Patient Questionnaire Results List window is displayed. Select Enter New Test Results from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Patient Questionnaire Results window is displayed. Select the questionnaire from the Questionnaire drop-down list. Select the administration date from the Date Administered drop-down list. Click OK . The Questionnaire Responses for window is displayed. Select the answer and click Next . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient Referrals by Name Report
topics.body: Running the Patient Referrals by Name Report Patient and Doctor Referrals > Running Referral Reports Running the Patient Referrals by Name Report The Patient Referrals by Name report generates a list of referrals made by patients during a specified period. It includes the production generated by each patient�s referrals, as well as the total and average production for all referred patients. Sample Report To run the Patient Referrals by Name report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Referral Reports > Patient . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Patient Referrals By Name Report Setup window is displayed. In the Referring Source Name section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending referring patient names. In the Referral Date section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending dates from a calendar. In the Patient User Codes section, type the user codes to include or exclude from the report. To include inactive patients in the report, click the Include Inactive Patients checkbox. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tracking Patients Referred to an Outside Doctor
topics.body: Tracking Patients Referred to an Outside Doctor Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals Tracking Patients Referred to an Outside Doctor The Patient Tracking feature enables you to track outgoing referrals by patient. To track a patient that has been referred to an outside doctor: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select Clinical Management > Outgoing Referral History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Select the referred out patient and select Clinical Management > Outgoing Referral History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Clinical Management > Outgoing Referral History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Outgoing Referrals Tracked List window is displayed. Select an option: To edit the referral reason : Select the referral and click Edit . The Outgoing Referrals Tracked window is displayed. Edit the referral reason and click OK . Click Close . The Outgoing Referrals Tracked List window is displayed. To delete a referral : Select the referral and click Delete . A message that asks you to confirm the deletion is displayed. Click Yes . To print, preview, or save a referral : Select a referral and select Print from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Patient Referral Tracking Report window is displayed. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Patient Reports
topics.body: Patient Reports Handling Reports Patient Reports You can access the following reports from the Reports > Patient menu: Report Name Description Frequency Alphabetical Patient List Select this report to generate an alphabetical list of patients. For more information, see Running the Alphabetical Patient List Report . As Needed Patient List By ID Select this report to generate a list of patients by identification number. For more information, see Running the Patient List by ID Report . As Needed Patient List By Zip Code Select this report to generate a list of patients by zip code. For more information, see Running the Patient List by Zip Code Report . As Needed User-Selected Select and customize this report to generate information based on the criteria you specify. For more information, see Creating User-Selected Reports . As Needed Patient List By Birthday Select this report to generate a list of patients who celebrate a birthday within a specific date range. For more information, see Running the Patient List by Birthday Report . As Needed Patient First Visit Report Select this report to generate a list of new patients. For more information, see Running the Patient First Visit Report . As Needed Patient Last Visit Report Select this report to generate a list of patients having a last visit date within a specific date range. For more information, see Running the Patient Last Visit Report . As Needed Insured Patients Select this report to generate an alphabetical list of insured patients. For more information, see Running the Insured Patients Report . As Needed Uninsured Patients Select this report to generate an alphabetical list of uninsured patients. For more information, see Running the Uninsured Patients Report . As Needed HIPAA Acknowledgement Received Select this report to generate a list of patients who have received a HIPAA notification. The report information includes the name, identification number, address, phone number, balance, insurance status, and user codes associated with each patient. For more information, see Running the HIPAA Acknowledgement Received Report . As Needed HIPAA Acknowledgement Outstanding Select this report to generate a list of patients who have not received a HIPAA notification. For more information, see Running the HIPAA Acknowledgement Outstanding Report . As Needed Patients By Discount List Select this report to generate a list of patients that are using a specified discount plan. For more information, see Running the Patients by Discount List Report . As Needed Patient List By Demographics Select this report to generate an alphabetical list of patients by zip code. If you are considering opening a satellite office, run this report. For more information, see Running the Patient List by Demographics Report . As Needed Patient Labels You can print patient labels in alphabetical order, and by identification number, zip code, and birthday. You can also print user-selected labels. For more information, see Printing Patient Labels . As Needed Patient Telephone Directory Select this report to generate an alphabetical list of patients and their phone numbers. For more information, see Running the Patient Telephone Directory Report . As Needed Patient Questionnaire by Question Select this report to generate statistical information about the response sets entered for a question. For more information, see Running Patient Questionnaire Reports . As Needed Patient Questionnaire by Questionnaire Select this report

topics.title: Patient Reports
topics.body: Patient Reports What's New in Version 14.0 > Reports Patient Reports Patient reports have received enhancements in the following areas: Patient Reports Export to Excel The following patient reports can now be exported to Excel: Alphabetical Patient List Patients By ID List Patients By ZIP Code List Birthday List New Patient List Insured Patient List Uninsured Patient List Patients By Discount List HIPAA Acknowledgement Received HIPAA Acknowledgement Outstanding In addition, you can specify how you want the information exported to Excel: Print Raw Data �Exports the report data to a tab in Excel where each piece of data in the report is in a separate column, enabling you to further manipulate the data using Excel. The report information is displayed on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data �Exports the report data to a tab in Excel where the report data is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer. The report information is displayed on the Formatted tab. You can also select both options. See Running Patient Reports for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Patient Transactions List Window Options
topics.body: Using Patient Transactions List Window Options Handling Transactions and Codes > Understanding the Account and Patient Transactions List Windows Using Patient Transactions List Window Options The patient Transactions List window options are: Outstanding Ins: Displays the Claim List window. A list of all open claims for the patient is displayed. Claim Status : Displays the transaction claim status. Eligibility : Displays the patient's insurance eligibility. This command is enabled only if you use eVerifications insurance eligibility. eClaim Status: Displays a list of electronic claims. To use this feature, you must purchase eClaims. Submit Insurance: Displays the Transaction Submission window, which enables you to submit insurance claims immediately after posting transactions or at any time later. CareCredit Integration: Access the CareCredit integration to perform account lookups, complete online applications, use a payment calculator, accept payments, enter adjustments, reprint a receipt, or view the payment plan. Payments : Enters bulk account payments (single payments that cover multiple patients in the account). Guarantors: Displays, adds, edits, and deletes patient guarantor and insurance information. View Insurance Company: Displays insurance information for the patient's primary or secondary insurance company. Appointments : Schedules the patient appointment. Appt Book : Displays the Daily Appointments window, where you can schedule, cancel, and reschedule patient appointments. Treatment Plan : Adds, deletes, and posts treatment plan procedures or groups. Lab Cases : Displays a list of outstanding lab cases. Missing Teeth : Denotes missing permanent or primary teeth. View Claim for Transaction : Edits claim information. You can also reprint or resubmit primary and secondary claims from this window. Documents : Displays any documents attached to the claim. Completed Claims : Displays a list of completed claims for the transaction. X-fer to Account Budget Plan : Transfers any part of the account balance to a fixed or revolving loan budget plan. Acct Budget Plan : Adds, edits, and deletes budget plans. Patient Budget Plan : Displays the Patient Budget Plan window. Attending Dr Statement : Prints a valid statement of treatment the patient can submit to an insurance company if your office does not submit insurance claims. Account Walkout : Prints a walkout statement showing the account transactions for the current date. Patient Walkout : Prints a walkout statement showing the patient transactions for the current date. Print Transactions : Prints a list of patient transactions posted within a specific date range. Print Appointments : Prints the next scheduled appointment and procedural details for the patient. Credit Card Receipt : Displays a credit card receipt. To use this feature, you must purchase ePayments. Print Signature Form : Processes credit card payments and prints the Signature on File form. View Credit Card Number: Displays credit card information. To use this feature, you must purchase ePayments. Auto Dialer : Contacts a patient via your computer modem. Zoom : Displays the transaction details. Trans History : Displays compressed patient transactions. Compression : Compresses transactions to increase file space. Use this option only if the patient has more than 400 transactions. All transactions posted within a selected date range are replaced with a compression debit or credit balance entry. Clinical Notes : Adds a clinic

topics.title: Using the Patient Transactions Window
topics.body: Using the Patient Transactions Window Handling Transactions and Codes > Posting Patient Procedures Using the Patient Transactions Window In the Treatment Plan window, the patient Transactions window is displayed when you add or edit: Patient transactions Treatment plan procedures The information is transaction-specific. The office and patient identification numbers are displayed in the following fields: Office Pat/Acct ID: These fields cannot be edited from this window. The following information is displayed in the Charges/Aging section: Charge Date of service Taxable is only enabled if a non-prorated tax method and a tax rate is selected in the General System Settings window. The patient fee schedule amount is displayed in the Fee field. If a patient is covered by DMO/Capitation plans : This field defaults to the bluebook cap fee. If there is no bluebook entry : The fee defaults to the fee schedule 0 amount. The real time units are displayed in the RTU�s field. The current date is displayed in the DOS field. The Aged field is only displayed when a procedure or charge code is selected. If a non-insurable transaction is selected or the account uses ICM0, the default value for this field is the current date, and the transaction starts aging immediately. If you leave this field blank : The transaction remains current until you select a date. If you select a future date for this field : The transaction remains current until the selected date and then starts aging. When an insurable transaction is selected, the default aged date is dictated by the ICM for the account. If the account ICM is 1 : This field is blank because all charges are kept current until an insurance payment is received and posted against the claim. If the account ICM is 2 : This field is blank to keep the deferred insurance portion of the charge from aging. The following boxes are checked if the corresponding options are selected in the ADA Transaction Codes window: Follow-up Call Select this option if the patient requires a call at the end of the day following the procedure. This is selected automatically if Call Back Needed is selected in the ADA/Transaction Codes window. PCI The PCI (Prosthesis/Crown/Inlay) option is selected by default if the code is in the PCI code range or Pros/Crown/Inlay is selected in the ADA/Transaction Codes window. When selected, it indicates the procedure is an initial placement. If this PCI procedure is not an initial placement, deselect the PCI checkbox and type the initial placement date in the field. Insurance information is displayed in the Insurance section. Insurable is only selected if the procedure is covered by insurance. A claim number is displayed in the Claim # field if a claim has been filed for the procedure. When posting against a claim, select the claim number. The insurance coverage information is displayed in the Type field. The portion of the fees used to meet the primary and secondary dental plan deductible is displayed in the Deductible field. The estimated insurance payment is displayed in the Insurance Pays field. The Remove Chart Graphic option enables you to re-chart an existing procedure in the chart to accurately depict the current status of patient dentition. (For example: an implant fails and needs to be retreated; an extraction was charted for the wrong tooth, etc.) When you select Remove Chart Graphic, the following things occur: A grey asterisk (indicating the procedure has been removed) is displayed next to th

topics.title: Understanding Patient User Codes
topics.body: Understanding Patient User Codes Setting Up Patients Understanding Patient User Codes Patient user codes are displayed in the User Code column of the Patient List window and entered in the patient record. Use these codes to group patients according to specific criteria. Example : Type X to turn off the insurance estimation for the patient. The pre-defined user codes are: A : The patient accepts assignment of all benefits. The Accept Assignment option (number 27) is selected on the patient's HCFA form. This code overrides the value entered in the Accept Allowed field in the Insurance Plan window. I : The patient is inactive. C : The patient account has been turned over to a collection agency. L : The patient account is involved in a legal case. M : The second line of text entered in the Notes field in the Patient window is printed in the recall list and on recall postcards addressed to the patient. N : The patient is excluded from receiving recall notices. P : Prints PREMED (premedicate patient) on recall letters and postcards sent to the patient. PREMED is also displayed in the patient's appointment card. RN : This code specifies the monthly interval of recalls. Type R6, for example to indicate a six-month interval between recalls. W : The patient receives medical assistance. X : This code turns off the insurance estimation. T ip A warning is displayed when you attempt to schedule a patient with the following user codes: C, I, or L. A warning is also displayed when you attempt to add a transaction to a patient record that contains C or I user codes. To view a printable job aid that lists the default patient user codes and gives you a place to record user-defined codes for your practice, see Patient User Codes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating Patient Visit Statistics Reports
topics.body: Generating Patient Visit Statistics Reports Handling Reports Generating Patient Visit Statistics Reports Generate the Patient Visit Statistics report after completing the checkout process. The report generates a list of patient appointments scheduled within a specified date range; shows the expected and actual patient arrival, wait, and operatory times; and calculates the percentage of patients that arrived on time. Tip Clear the InOffice before generating the report. Related Topics Setting Patient Visit Statistics Report Properties Generating the Patient Visit Statistics Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Patients Associated With an Insurance Plan
topics.body: Viewing Patients Associated With an Insurance Plan Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Viewing Patients Associated with an Insurance Plan To view a list of patients associated with an insurance plan: Select List > Ins. Plan . The Insurance Plan List window is displayed. Select an insurance plan and select Show Insured Patients . Select an option: To display the patient�s account record : Select a patient and select View Account . The Account window is displayed. After you view the details, click Close . To display the patient�s record : Select a patient and select View Patient . The Patient window is displayed. After you view the details, click Close . To display the patient�s guarantor information : Select a patient and select Guarantors/Insurance . The Patient Guarantor window is displayed. After you view the details, click Close . Tip In any window, press Ctrl + P or select List Insurance Plans from the Power bar to access the Insurance Plan List window. To view patients in the plan record, select Show Insured Patients . Select options from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Patients Associated With an Insurance Company
topics.body: Viewing Patients Associated With an Insurance Company Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Company Information Viewing Patients Associated with an Insurance Company To view a list of patients associated with an insurance company: In the Insurance Co List window, select an insurance company and select Show Insured Patients . The Patients Insured By List window is displayed. Select an option: To display the patient�s account record : Select a patient and select View Account . The Account window is displayed. After you view the details, click Close . To display the patient�s record : Select a patient and select View Patient . The Patient window is displayed. After you view the details, click Close . topics.title: Running the Patient List by Zip Code Report
topics.body: Running the Patient List by Zip Code Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Patient List by Zip Code Report The Patient List by Zip Code report generates a list of patients by zip code. Sample Report To generate a list of patients by zip codes: Select Reports > Patient > Zip Code Order . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The List Patients by Zip Code Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient zip codes from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Previously Accessed Patient Records
topics.body: Viewing Previously Accessed Patient Records Setting Up Patients Viewing Previously Accessed Patient Records You can view a list of the last ten patient records accessed from the Patient List window. To view previously accessed patient records: In the Patient List window, click the Find drop-down list. A list of previously accessed patient records is displayed. Select a record from the drop-down list. To display the record, double-click it. The Patient window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Provider Mode
topics.body: Using Provider Mode Using the Scheduler > Understanding Book At A Glance Window Options Using Provider Mode When the Book At A Glance window is displayed in provider mode: All provider assignments are displayed in the color assigned to the provider enabling you to quickly see when and where providers are scheduled. Appointments and slot notes are represented by small boxes. Appointments that are not displayed in a color indicate that no provider is assigned to the appointment. Provider colors are assigned by slot. If the provider is not assigned for the entire appointment, part of an appointment is not displayed in the provider�s assigned color. To view information in the Book At A Glance window in color mode, click View Providers at the top of the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Provider and Referring Doctor Reports
topics.body: Provider and Referring Doctor Reports Handling Reports Provider and Referring Doctor Reports You can access the following reports from the Reports > Provider/Referring Dr menu: Report Name Description Frequency Alphabetical Provider List Select this report to generate a list of providers in alphabetical order. For more information, see Running the Alphabetical Provider List Report . As Needed Providers By ID Select this report to generate a list of providers by identification number. For more information, see Running the Provider by ID Report . As Needed Alphabetical Provider Labels Select this report to generate alphabetically-sorted provider labels. topics.title: Using the Provider Detail Bar
topics.body: Using the Provider Detail Bar Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows Using the Provider Detail Bar The Provider Detail bar enables you to display a provider�s availability a week at a time in the following windows: Daily Appointments Book At A Glance History Appointments You can dock this bar anywhere in these windows. The Provider Detail bar is a grid that displays days and hours. After you select a provider, the color associated with the provider is displayed in the time slots to which he or she is assigned. Room information is not displayed in the Provider Detail bar. When you select a slot in the scheduler window, the slot is also selected in the Provider Detail bar. If you click a slot in the Provider Detail bar, the same slot is selected in the scheduler window. To use the Provider Detail bar, select an option: To display the Provider Detail bar : From the appropriate scheduler window, select View > Provider Detail Bar . A checkmark is displayed next to the menu item to indicate that the bar is displayed. To hide the bar : From the appropriate scheduler window, select View > Provider Detail Bar or click the exit button displayed in the Provider Detail bar title bar. To move the Provider Detail bar : Double-click the title bar or drag the title bar toward the middle of the scheduler window. To dock the Provider Detail bar : Select the title bar and drag the bar to the appropriate side of the scheduler window. When the bar is docked, the hour markers in the bar are aligned with the scheduler window�s information, even when scrolling. To select a provider : Click Select Provider in the title bar or select a grid, right-click, and select Select Provider . The Provider List window is displayed. The provider�s name is displayed in the Provider Detail bar title bar. To toggle between the bar and scheduler window : Press the Tab key. When the Provider Detail bar is selected, the title bar is enabled. To customize how information is displayed in the Provider Detail bar and scheduler window : From the Provider Detail bar, select a grid, right-click, and select Properties . The Properties window is displayed. To use this window, see Setting Daily Appointments Window Properties , Setting Book At A Glance Window Properties , or Setting History Appointments Properties . Tip If you use color scheduling, lunch times are displayed in the appropriate color. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Provider Productions Forecast by ADA Code Report
topics.body: Running the Provider Productions Forecast by ADA Code Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Provider Productions Forecast by ADA Code Report The Provider Productions Forecast by ADA Code report identifies which production doctor brings in the largest percentage of income for each procedure. The report includes: Procedure identification number Procedure name Procedural fee Number of procedures scheduled by the selected provider Number of procedures scheduled for the selected group Percentage of group income generated by a specified provider Income generated by the selected provider Usual and customary rate charged by the selected provider Income generated by the group Usual and customary rate charged by the group Percentage of group usual and customary rate income generated by the selected provider Production rate for the doctor and group Sample Report To run the Provider Productions Forecast by ADA Code report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Sched/Appt Reports > Provider By ADA Forecast . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Provider Forecast Report Setup window is displayed. Select the production date range from the drop-down lists. Select the identification numbers for the providers to include in the report from the Providers section. Click OK . Tip Type 999 in the first field to include production forecast information for all providers. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Running the Provider by ID Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Provider by ID Report The Provider by ID report generates a list of providers in the database. The list is sorted by identification number. For each provider, the report includes: Identification number Name Address Social Security number Tax identification number License number Sample Report To run the Provider by ID report: Select Reports > Provider/Referring Dr > Provider > ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Provider By ID Report Setup window is displayed. Select the start and end provider identification numbers. To print the provider�s home address in the report, select Use Provider�s Home Address . To use color shading in the printed report, select Include Color Shading . Click OK . topics.title: Running the Provider Labels by ID Report
topics.body: Running the Provider Labels by ID Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Provider Labels by ID Report The Provider Labels by ID report generates labels for providers in the database. The labels are sorted by identification number and include the name and address of each provider. Sample Label To run the Provider Labels by ID report: Select Reports > Provider/Referring Dr > Provider > Labels > ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Provider by ID Label Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending provider identification numbers. To print the provider�s home address on the labels, select Use Provider�s Home Address . Type the number of lines to print per label in the Lines Per Label field. Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Provider Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . Tip If the Use Provider's Home Address option is not selected, the office address is printed on the labels. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Provider List and Provider Window Options
topics.body: Using Provider List and Provider Window Options Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Provider Information Using Provider List and Provider Window Options In the Provider List or Provider window, you can print address labels and envelopes, log a contact, create reminder notes, and view the phone numbers associated with a provider. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: Labels : Prints a label or laser envelope for the provider. You can print office or home addresses on the labels. 0 - Auto Dialer : Dials a specific number associated with the selected provider. Choose Color : Assigns a color with the provider. This color is displayed in the Account, Add a Prescription, Tickler Entry, and Rcvtot windows. Letter Expert : Merges the provider information with a letter template. Contacts By Date : Adds, edits, or deletes a provider contact; creates a provider memo. Contacts By Type : Adds, edits, or deletes a provider contact; creates a provider memo. Add CareCredit Service: Opens the Configure the Provider for CareCredit Service list window to enable you to set up providers to use CareCredit. Need more information? topics.title: Using Practice and Provider Modes

The default in this window is the practice mode, but you can toggle between modes. To display production and goal information for a single provider or for the entire practice, select an option: To display production and goal information for the practice in the Month At A Glance window : Click Practice or select Select Practice from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A green box is displayed around the button to indicate that practice information is displayed in the window. To display production and goal information for a specific provider in the Month At A Glance window : Click Provider , click S , or select Select Provider from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Provider List window is displayed. Double-click a provider. A green box is displayed around the button to indicate that provider specific information is displayed in the window. topics.title: Running the Provider Productions Forecast by Class Report
topics.body: Running the Provider Productions Forecast by Class Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Provider Productions Forecast by Class Report The Provider Productions Forecast by Class report identifies which production doctor brings in the largest percentage of income for each class of procedure. The report classifies all procedures to be performed by a provider and compares them to the procedures to be performed by the group of selected providers. The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Provider Forecast Report Setup window is displayed. Select the production date range from the drop-down lists. topics.title: Creating Case Presentations using PowerPoint
topics.body: Creating Case Presentations using PowerPoint Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint PowerPoint and SoftDent are integrated using the SoftDent toolbar in PowerPoint. The toolbar enables you to: Access each presentation type Integrate new presentations into SoftDent Insert placeholders into case blueprints while working with PowerPoint Case blueprints : Presentations that contain placeholders that denote where to import patient and treatment plan information. Blueprints are used as outlines for creating treatment plan case presentations. Example : You can create a blueprint with slides that contain placeholders for patient chart, procedure code, and proposed treatment cost information. Related Topics Displaying the SoftDent Toolbar Creating Case Blueprints Opening Case Blueprints Saving Presentations as Case Blueprints Maintaining Case Blueprints Inserting Placeholders into Case Blueprints and ADA Procedure Presentations Opening ADA Procedure Presentations Saving Presentations as ADA Procedure Presentations Creating and Editing ADA Code Presentation Links Opening Patient Case Presentations Changing the Order of ADA Procedure Presentations Within a Patient Presentation Updating Case Presentation Dates Processing Treatment Plans Specifying the SoftDent Data Directory Need more information?
