topics.title: Payment Information
topics.body: Payment Information Trojan > Managed Care Plans > Managed Care Plan Fees Payment Information The Payment Information section contains the following fields: Modifier : Lists any Trojan ADA code extensions for the procedure being viewed. CoPay : Displays the dollar or percentage amount that the patient is responsible for. Lab Payment : Displays the dollar or percentage amount the plan pays for any lab procedures associated with the ADA code. Lab Paid By : Displays the code for the party who is paying the lab fees. The following codes are used: P : Patient is responsible for the laboratory fees. A : Administration or the insurance company is responsible for the laboratory fees. B : Both are responsible for the payment. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Payment Plan Report
topics.body: Running the Payment Plan Report Budget Planning > Running Budget Plan Reports Running the Payment Plan Report The Payment Plan report generates a list of credit card payment plan information, including a summary of the outstanding payment plan balance, the number of payments due, and the average monthly income generated from payment plan payments. Sample Report To generate the Payment Plan report: Select Reports > Accounting > Budget Plan Reports > Payment Plans . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Payment Plan Report Setup window is displayed. Select an option: To restrict the report results to a specific account user code: Type the code in the Include field. To exclude a specific account user code: Type the code in the Exclude field. To restrict the report results to a specific set of accounts: Select the starting and ending account numbers from the drop-down lists. To include inactive accounts in the report: Select Include Inactive Accts . To restrict the report results to a specific patient user code: Type the code in the Include field. To exclude a specific patient user code: Type the code in the Exclude field. To restrict the report results to a specific set of patients: Select the starting and ending patient numbers from the drop-down lists. To include inactive patients in the report: Select Include Inactive Pats . Click OK . The Payment Plan Report is displayed. The Payment Plan report contains information similar to that of the Account Budget Plan report. The last page of the Payment Plan report is a summary report. The following sections of the summary report might require some explanation: Outstanding Balance on Payment Plans: The sum of the balance (Amount Financed - Amount Paid) of all active payment plans. No. of Payments: The sum of the payments remaining of all active payment plans. Average Monthly Income: The Outstanding Balance on Payment Plans amount divided by the No. of Payments. The totals for Month 1, Month 2, and so on, will be the sum of each payment for that month for all active payment plans. Example: If two payment plans are active and one payment plan has 10 payments for $50.00 and the other has six payments for $30, then Months 1-6 will have an amount of $80 ($50 + $30). Months 7-10 will have an amount of $50, and months 11 and 12 will have an amount of $0. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Payment Allocation Report
topics.body: Running the Payment Allocation Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Payment Allocation Report The Payment Allocation report g enerates a list of provider income information for a specified time period. For each provider, the report includes: Direct income sources : Direct income is generated when the service provider and production doctor are the same. Indirect income sources : Indirect income is generated for the production doctor when the service provider and production doctor are not the same. Every transaction Plan transactions for collection or collection adjustment information All income is calculated by adding the production amounts and charges, adding or subtracting the production adjustments, and adding the tax for production amounts. The total amount of direct income sources is calculated by adding the cash and credit card payments and adding or subtracting the collection adjustments. Sample Report To run the Payment Allocation report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Production Reports > Payment Allocation . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Payment Allocation Report Setup window is displayed. Select the production date range from the drop-down lists. Select the identification numbers for the providers to include in the report from the Doctors section. Click OK . Tip Type 999 in the first field to include payment allocation information for all providers. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Manually
topics.body: Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Manually eServices > Using the ePayments Service Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Manually If a credit card is not available, use this procedure to add a payment. To add a credit card payment manually: In the patient Transactions List window, click Add . The Transaction window is displayed. Select the credit card payment code from the Code drop-down list and press Tab. The Payment window is displayed. Type the payment amount, and click OK . The Carestream ePayments Charge Authorization window is displayed. In the Cardholder Last and First fields, type the cardholder's name. In the Acct # field, type the credit card number. In the Exp Date (MM/YY) field, type the expiration date. In the Zip field, type the cardholder's ZIP code. In the CVD Number field, type the credit card verification number. If you do not enter a CVD number, a message requesting the number is displayed. To ignore this message, click OK . Click Submit . After you receive an authorization number, add notes, if necessary. To print a receipt, click OK at the prompt. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Patient Questionnaire Creation Wizard
topics.body: Using the Patient Questionnaire Creation Wizard Handling Patient Questionnaires > Creating a Patient Questionnaire Using the Patient Questionnaire Creation Wizard To modify existing questionnaires, see Editing Patient Questionnaires . To create a new patient questionnaire: Select Wizards > Questionnaire Creation Wizard . Read the introductory statement and click Next . Type the question title in the field and click Next . To add a question that exists on another questionnaire to the new questionnaire, select I would like to add an existing question to the questionnaire and click Next . Select a question category from the list and click Next . Select a question from the list and click Next . Go to step 10. To add a new question to the questionnaire, select I would like to create a new question and add it to the questionnaire and click Next . Select an option: To create a new category : Type the category name in the top field and click Next . To use a previously created category : Double-click the category name in the bottom section of the window and click Next . The category name is displayed on the questionnaire preceding the question. Type the question in the field as it is to print on the questionnaire and click Next . Select an option: If the answer to the question is yes or no : Select Yes/No - This is a "Yes/No" question and click Next . Go to step 10. If the answer to the question is free form : Select Free Form - This is a "fill-in-the-blank" question and click Next . Go to step 9. If the answer to the question is numeric : Select Numeric - The answer can be a positive number and click Next . Go to step 10. If the answer to the question is to be selected from a list of responses : Select List - The user can pick one answer from a list of responses and click Next . If the patient can select more than one answer from a list of responses : Select Multiple List - The user can pick zero or more answers from a list of responses and click Next . Select an option: To enter an answer to the question : Type it in the field in the top section of the window and click Add or press Enter. The answer is displayed in the list in the bottom section of the window. To edit an answer to the question : Select it from the bottom section of the window and click Edit . Make the changes and click Add . To delete an answer from the list : Select it from the bottom section of the window and click Remove . After you have entered all of the possible answers to the question, click Next . Review the questionnaire and click Next . Repeat steps 4 through 11 until you have added all of the questions to the questionnaire. After you have added all of the questions to the questionnaire, in the third window of the Patient Questionnaire Creation Wizard select I am done adding questions to the questionnaire and click Next . Select an option: To complete the patient questionnaire : Click Finish . Go to step 19. To create question dependencies : Click Next . The text and type of each question are displayed in the list. Select the parent question from the list, press Ctrl, and select the child question from the list. The child question is dependent upon the answer of the parent question. Example : Question 10 is asked only if the answer to question 9 is Yes. You can have multiple parent/child relationships, but you must set each one up separately. Click Next . To set the dependency, select an option and click Next . Repeat steps 4 through 17 until you have added all of th

topics.title: Using the Questionnaire Editor
topics.body: Using the Questionnaire Editor Handling Patient Questionnaires > Creating a Patient Questionnaire Using the Questionnaire Editor To create a new patient questionnaire using the Questionnaire Editor: Select List > Patient Questionnaire . The Patient Questionnaire List window is displayed in FIND mode. Click Add . The Patient Questionnaire window is displayed in ADD mode. The questions for all categories in other questionnaires are displayed. Type the questionnaire title in the Test Name field. Select the category of questions to display in the Questions section from the Category drop-down list. Select All to include questions from all categories in the list. Select an option: To add an existing question to the new questionnaire : Select the question from the Questions section and click Selected in the Add >>>> section. To add consecutive questions to the new questionnaire : Select the first question, press Shift, and select the last question. Click Selected in the Add >>>> section. To select non-consecutive questions to the new questionnaire : Select the first question, press Ctrl, and select each question. Click Selected in the Add >>>> section. To add all existing questions to the new questionnaire : Click >>All in the Add >>>> section. To remove a question from the Questions Selected section, select the question and click Selected in the Remove < section. To remove all questions from the Questions Selected section, click <<All in the Remove < section. To create a new question, click Add Question . The Question window is displayed. Type the question in the Question field. Select an option: To add the new question to a new category : Type the category name in the Category drop-down list. To add the new question to an existing category : Select the category from the Category drop-down list. The category name is displayed on the questionnaire preceding the question. Select an option: If the answer to the question is yes or no : Select Yes/No . If the answer to the question is free-form : Select Free Form . If the answer to the question is numeric : Select Numeric . If the answer to the question is to be selected from a list of responses : Select List . The Enter a Response section of the window is displayed. Type a response in the field located at the top of the section, and click Add Response To List . Repeat until all response choices are entered. To edit or delete a response, select it and click the appropriate button. To change the display order of the responses, select a response and click the appropriate button. If the patient can select more than one answer from a list of responses : Select Multiple List . Type a response in the field located at the top of the section, and click Add Response To List . Repeat until all response choices are entered. To edit or delete a response, select it and click the appropriate button. To change the display order of the responses, select a response and click the appropriate button. After you have finished editing the question, click OK . The Patient Questionnaire window is displayed. To add the new question to the questionnaire, select it from the Questions section of the window and click Selected in the Add >>>> section. To edit an existing question, see Entering Patient Questionnaire Responses . Select an option: If you do not want to set any dependencies in your questionnaire : Go to step 16. To set a question dependency : Select the parent question from the Questions Selected section, press Ct

topics.title: Printing the Recall Phone Directory
topics.body: Printing the Recall Phone Directory Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports Printing the Recall Phone Directory You can print a phone directory for all patients due for recall, including or excluding patients by their user codes. The directory lists the patient's name, telephone numbers, last exam date, and the number of recall notices sent. Sample Report To generate a recall phone directory: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Labels/Postcards/Dir > Recall All Phone Dir . The Select Patients window is displayed. Select an option: To include patients associated with a specific user code in the directory : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude patients associated with a specific user code from the directory : Type the code in the Exclude field. To include inactive patients in the directory: Click the Include Inactive Patients checkbox. Click OK . A message that informs you of the number of patients added to the directory is displayed. Click OK . A message that asks you to print or delete the patient directory is displayed. Select Print and click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Recall Reports
topics.body: Recall Reports What's New in Version 14.0 > Reports Recall Reports Recall reports have received enhancements in the following areas: Hygienist ID Added to the Confirmation List Report The hygienist ID has been added to the Confirmation List report ( Reports > Recall/Appt > Confirmation List > By Date and Time or By Patient Name ). The Dr ID column heading has been changed to Dr/Hyg on the Confirmation List by Patient Name and the Confirmation List by Date and Time reports. The Dr ID from the appointment card is always displayed in the Dr/Hyg column in the first row of the appointment information. The hygienist ID from the appointment card is displayed in the Dr/Hyg column in the second row of the appointment information. Keep the following in mind: Any non-zero hygienist ID is displayed, even if there are no H's in the template. If the hygienist ID from the appointment card is zero and there are H's in the template, two asterisks are displayed instead of zero. If the hygienist ID from the appointment card is zero and there are no H's in the template, nothing is displayed. This scenario includes non-hygiene appointments as well as hygiene appointments where the office does not use templates. See Running the Confirmation List Report for more information. Cell Phone and E-Mail Address Added to the Recall Reports The Recall reports for patients now contain the patient's cell phone number and e-mail address, if available. The patient's cell phone number is displayed directly under the home and work numbers, and is labeled with a "C:." The e-mail address is displayed directly under the patient's name and address, if available. See Running Recall Reports for more information. Recall Reports Export to Excel The Patient Recalls and User-Defined Recalls reports can now be exported to Excel. In addition, you can specify how you want the information exported to Excel: Print Raw Data �Exports the report data to a tab in Excel where each piece of data in the report is in a separate column, enabling you to further manipulate the data using Excel. The report information is displayed on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data �Exports the report data to a tab in Excel where the report data is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer. The report information is displayed on the Formatted tab. You can also select both options. See Running Recall Reports for more information. Option to Print Icons on the Daily Operatory Schedule Report You now have a choice as to whether or not to print appointment icons on the Daily Operatory Schedule report. A Show Icons option has been added to the Appt tab of the Daily Operatory Schedule Properties window ( Reports > Recall/Appt > Daily Op Schedule > Set Daily Op Properties > Appt ). When the Show Icons option is selected (the default), the icons displayed at the bottom of patient appointments are printed on the schedule. If the Show Icons option is not selected, no icons are printed on the schedule. See Setting Up the Daily Operatory Schedule Report for more information. New Format and Setup Windows for the Recall Reports The windows used to set up a recall report have changed. Each report format (Postcards, Labels, and so on) now has a setup window that contains only the fields used for that type of report. When you select the recall report ( Reports > Recall/Appt > Last Exam ), the Report Format window is now displayed. Use this window to select the format for the report�Postcards, Letter Expert, Labe

topics.title: Using the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details Window
topics.body: Using the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details Window Budget Planning > Creating Credit Card Payment Plans Using the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details Window The Recurring Credit Card Payment Details window enables you to view and sort the payments from your practice�s credit card payment plans, based on the date range and option filters you select. This window is also displayed when you run the Credit Card Settlement report, although the window is display-only. Sample Report To use the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details window: Do one of the following: Select Reports > Accounting > Recurring Credit Card Details . Click the Recurring Credit Card Payments header in Practice Central. The Recurring Credit Card Payment Details window is displayed. In the Display Credit Card Payments section, type a date range in the From Date and To Date fields, or click the arrows to select the dates from a calendar. Click Update . Select additional options at the bottom of the window to display additional payments. To help you differentiate between the various credit card payment status types, payments are displayed in several colors, and the Status column lists the associated status codes . S -- Settled (Green) F -- Failed Authorization (Red) A -- Authorized Successfully (Blue) V -- Voided (Orange) Blank -- Not authorized, settled, voided, or failed authorization (Black) Double-click a payment in the list. If the payment is part of an account or patient payment plan, the Recurring Credit Card Payment�CC Payment window is displayed. If the payment is part of a patient or account budget plan, the Patient Budget Plan�CC Payment window or Account Budget Plan�CC Payment window is displayed. See Reviewing and Editing Payments in a Credit Card Payment Plan for information on editing credit card payments. To print the Recurring Credit Card Payments report, click Print . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The contents of the Recurring Credit Card Payments report depend on the settings you made in the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details window. Example: If you select the Display Authorized option to display authorized payments in the window, authorized payments are printed on the report. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Recall and Appointment Reports
topics.body: Recall and Appointment Reports Handling Reports Recall and Appointment Reports You can access the following reports from the Reports > Recall/Appt menu: Report Name Description Frequency Daily Operatory Schedule Select this report to print a list of all patients due in the office, their scheduled providers, and notes and alerts for all operatories. For more information, see Handling the Daily Operatory Schedule Report . Daily Recall Select this report to generate all recall notices from one window. You can print postcards and mailing labels, and merge letters for each patient due for recall, or you can generate Contact lists of patients due for recalls. For more information, see Running Recall Reports . Monthly User-Defined Recalls Select this report to generate all recall notices from one window. You can print postcards and mailing labels, and merge letters for each patient due for recall, or you can generate Contact lists of patients due for recalls. For more information, see Running Recall Reports . Monthly Missed Appointments Select this report to generate a list of patients who have missed a scheduled appointment. After you run this report, the missed appointments are no longer displayed in the Daily Appointments window. For more information, see Running the Missed Appointments Report . Weekly Tickler File Select this report to generate a list of patients in the tickler file. For more information, see Running the Tickler File Report . Weekly Confirmation List Select this report to generate a list of the confirmation statuses of patient appointments for a specified date. For more information, see Running the Confirmation List Report . As Needed Appointment List Select this report to generate a list of all appointments for a specified date range. For more information, see Running the Appointment List Report . As Needed End-Of-Day Callback List Select this report to generate a list of appointments tagged for a follow-up call at the end of the day. For more information, see Running the End Of Day Call Backs Report . Daily Short Call List Select this report to generate a list of scheduled patients who are willing to move their appointments if there is an opening earlier in the schedule or patients with Tickler entries who are willing to schedule an appointment on short notice. For more information, see Running the Short Call List Report . As Needed Labels/Postcards/Dir You can print appointment and Tickler postcards and labels. You can also print recall and confirmation phone directories. For more information, see Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports . As Needed Routing Slips By Date You can print routing slips for a specific date for one or all providers. For more information, see Running the Routing Slips by Date Report . As Needed Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Recalculating Benefits
topics.body: Recalculating Benefits Treatment Planning Recalculating Benefits Once a month, you should reset insurance benefits for all patients based on their anniversary date and recalculate estimated insurance for all treatment plan procedures. To recalculate benefits: Select an option: Select Insurance > Insurance Plan Rollover . Click the Insurance Plan Rollover Power bar button. The Insurance Rollover window is displayed. To run the utility and rollover insurance plans, click OK . When the rollover is complete, a message that informs you that the process is complete is displayed. Click OK . The previously active window is displayed. Select Insurance >Treat Plan Recalc of Benefits . A message that asks you to rollover insurance plans is displayed. Click No . Related Topics End of Year Reports Related Job Aid: End of Year Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Instant Recall Codes Setup Utilities
topics.body: Running the Instant Recall Codes Setup Utilities Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Instant Recall Codes Setup Utilities You can run the Instant Recall System Setup and Patient Instant Recall Procedures to: Set up a user-defined recall system Set new recall codes Related Topics Running the Instant Recall System Setup Utility Running the Patient Instant Recall Procedures Utility Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Recalling Patients
topics.body: Recalling Patients Recalling Patients You can: Specify different recall procedures for adults and children. Use alternating recalls for procedures performed on alternating visits. You can set up: Separate recalls for a different class of dental care; such as, orthodontics. Set up specific recalls for individual patients. Using the Recall feature, you can generate: Letters Postcards Labels Merge files Telephone lists Example : You might call patients who are due to receive their first recall notice, send postcards to those due for a second notice, and send a letter to those patients due for a third notice. You must generate a separate report for each format and indicate which patient group is due to receive that notice. Using Instant Recall, you can schedule a patient�s recall visits before he leaves your office. The following factors determine the time slot for a recall appointment: Last exam date for the patient Recall interval Recall procedure set in the patient record Time units required for the appointment Patient's preferred hygienist Doctor's availability Office hours Preferred days and times for the patient Goal or procedure parameters, if you are using color scheduling When a patient is overdue for recall, the following reminders are available: The patient record is displayed in blue text and the Overdue for Recall button is displayed in the Patient List window Alerts column. The date in the Next Recall due on or after: field is displayed in red text in the Patient window. The Next Exam Due field is displayed in red text in the Transactions window. An ***Overdue*** alert is displayed next to the Last Exam field in the Patient Appointments window. The Recall Overdue reminder is displayed in the title bar of the Patient List and Patient Appointments windows. If a patient has a red medical alert and is overdue for recall, the record text is displayed in red in the Patient List window. Related Topics Setting Up Recalls Running Recall Reports Scheduling Recall Appointments Clearing Recall Notices Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Instant Recall System Setup Utility
topics.body: Running the Instant Recall System Setup Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running the Instant Recall Codes Setup Utilities Running the Instant Recall System Setup Utility Run this utility to set up the instant recall system. To run this utility: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Instant Recall Codes Setup > Instant Recall System Setup . The Instant Recall Setup window is displayed. Type the name, hot key letter, and number of months in the fields. Type the codes that trigger an instant recall in the Codes that set the Normal Recall section. These codes are associated with procedures used to recall adults and children for routine preventive visits. If you use a user-defined recall system, type the name, hot key letter, and number of months in the fields, and type the codes that trigger a user-defined recall in the Codes that set the User-Defined Recall section. Set default procedures for a separate class of dental care, such as orthodontics or periodontics in this section. Select the appropriate options in the Options section. Click OK . A message informs you that the process is complete and asks you to exit the utility. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Instant Recalls for Patients Without Set Recall Procedures
topics.body: Scheduling Instant Recalls for Patients Without Set Recall Procedures Scheduling Appointments > Using Instant Recall Scheduling Instant Recalls for Patients Without Set Recall Procedures If the patient does not have a recall procedure set up on the Appts/Recall tab of the patient record, an error message is displayed when you attempt to schedule an instant recall appointment. To schedule a recall appointment for a patient who does not have an instant recall set: In the Patient List window, select a patient, and select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The patient Appointments window is displayed. Select an option: To make an exam appointment : Select Go ToRecall Day > Last Exam from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To make a user-defined recall appointment : Select Go ToRecall Day > User- Defined from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To make a recall appointment : Select Go ToRecall Day > Next from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Select an option: If the selected day and time are acceptable to the patient : Skip to number 4. To display the next available time slot : Click Next Slot or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. To display the previous available time slot : Click Prev Slot or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. To display the next day : Click Skip Day or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. To display the previous day with an available time slot : Click Prev Day or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. When you have found a suitable slot, click Make Appt on the toolbar to schedule the appointment. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Record Search Methods
topics.body: Record Search Methods Getting Started > Searching for Records > Using Find to Search for a Record Record Search Methods The search methods for each list window are: Account : Name, identification number, and Social Security number. Patient : Name, identification number, chart number, Social Security number, and first name. Phone Center : Name, identification number, chart number, Social Security number, and first name. Provider : Name and identification number. Referring Doctor : Name and identification number. Employer : Name and identification number. School : Name and identification number. Insurance Company : Name and identification number. Insurance Plan : Name and identification number. Insurance Claim : Claim number, patient identification number, and patient name. ADA Code : All codes or active codes. Diagnosis : Code or description. CPT ADA Codes : All codes or active codes. CPT Diagnosis Codes : All codes or active codes. Pharmacy : Name and identification number. Labs : Name and identification number. Prescription Drugs : Name and identification number. Zip Codes : Zip code or city. Tickler File : File date, patient name, doctor name, procedure length, or by short calls. Completed Claim : Claim identification number, patient identification number, or submission date. Patient Questionnaire : Name or identification number. Receivable Totals : Date. Trojan : In the Employer_plans window, by group number, name, or identification number. In the Carriers window, by name or identification number. In the Managed Care Plan window, by group number, name, or identification number. In the Plan Administrators window, by name or identification number. Other Facilities : Name and identification number. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Receivables Breakdown Report
topics.body: Running the Receivables Breakdown Report Billing and Receivables > Using Billing and Receivables Reports > Running Receivables Reports Running the Receivables Breakdown Report The Receivables Breakdown report g enerates a monthly or yearly breakdown of receivables for your practice. To generate the Receivables Breakdown report: Select Reports > Practice Management >Receivables Reports > Breakdown . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Receivables Breakdown Report Setup window is displayed. To generate the report for a specific provider, select the provider�s identification number from the Providers field, or accept the default of 999 to include information for all providers. To generate a yearly breakdown of receivables, select Yearly Breakdown (Blank implies Monthly Breakdown) . If this checkbox is not selected, a monthly receivables breakdown is generated. Click the drop-down arrow in the Through Date field to select a different date from a calendar. Click OK . Additional Receivables Breakdown Report Information: Each month when you run this report, you should run both the monthly and yearly breakdowns. The monthly breakdown shows your beginning and ending accounts receivables numbers, productions, charges, adjustments, and payments for each day of the month. The yearly breakdown shows the same information for each month of the current year. Figures on this report are accurate only if a final daysheet has been run for every date included in the report. If your receivables totals are not current, the report information will be inaccurate. The Receivables Breakdown report contains the following sections: Day or Month�The setup screen for the Receivables Breakdown Report has an option for Yearly Breakdown. If you mark this option, the report prints every month of the year within the specified date range. If you do not mark this option, the report prints every day of the month. The rest of the report remains the same. Beginning A/R�Receivables totals for the previous day or month (depending on the breakdown selected). Production$�Production dollar amounts (codes 100.00+) for the collecting doctor (Dr$). Charges�Total dollar amount for charges posted (codes 61.00- 95.99, 97.00-97.99, 99.00-99.99). Charge Adjustments�Total dollar amount for charge adjustments posted (codes 40.00-60.99) Net Charges�Net Charges = Production$ + Charges +/- Charge Adjustments. Payments�Dollar amount of cash, check, and credit card payments posted (codes 1.00-20.99). Payment Adjustments�Dollar amount of adjustments to payments posted (codes 40.00-60.99). Net Payments�Net Payments = Payments +/- Payment Adjustments. Ending A/R�Ending AR = Beginning A/R +/- Net Charges +/- Net Payments. Part of A/R on Bgt Plans�Total outstanding amount for account budget plans. Percent Collect�Percent Collected = Net Payments divided by Net Charges. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Receivables Comparison Report
topics.body: Running the Receivables Comparison Report Billing and Receivables > Using Billing and Receivables Reports > Running Receivables Reports Running the Receivables Comparison Report The Receivables Comparison report e nables you to compare the change in receivables over two specific date ranges. To generate the Receivables Comparison report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Receivables Reports > Summary . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Receivables Comparison Report Setup window is displayed. To generate the report for a specific provider, select the provider�s identification number from the Providers field, or accept the default of 999 to include information for all providers. Select the date ranges from the Ranges section. Click OK . Additional Receivables Comparison Report Information: The Change In Receivables amount includes the practice�s receivables for the first period minus the practice�s receivables for the second period. This figure is not necessarily net production minus net collections because net collections also includes billing and finance charges. If your receivables totals are not current, the report information will be inaccurate. The Receivables Comparison report contains the following sections: Production$�Production dollar amounts (codes 100+) for the collecting provider (DR$). Charges�Dollar amounts for charges posted (Codes 61.00-95.99, 97.00-97.99, 99.00-99.99). Adjustments to Production�Dollar amounts of adjustments to posted production (codes 40.00-60.99). Tax Liability�% of tax on production codes (if tax feature is enabled). Net Production$�Net Production$ = Production$ + Charges +/- Adjustments to Production Cash�Dollar amounts posted for cash payments (codes 1.00-1.99). Check�Dollar amounts posted for check payments (codes 3.00-10.99). Credit Cards�Dollar amounts posted for credit card payments (codes 11.00-20.99). Adjustment to Collections�Dollar amounts posted for collections adjustments (codes 21.00-39.99, 96.00-96.99, 98.00-98.99). Net Collections�Net Collections = Cash + Check + Credit Cards +/- Adjustments to Collections. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Receive Only
topics.body: Using Receive Only eServices > Using Additional eServices Options Using Receive Only Use Receive Only to: Verify the connection between your computer�s modem or Internet connection and the eServices network Download reports and notices without transmitting claims To use the Receive Only option, select an option: In the eServices window : Click Receive Only . From the main menu : Select Submit Data > Receive Only . Pending reports are downloaded to your computer, and the Report List window is displayed. You can preview and print reports from this window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Receivables Ratio Report
topics.body: Running the Receivables Ratio Report Billing and Receivables > Using Billing and Receivables Reports > Running Receivables Reports Running the Receivables Ratio Report The Receivables Ratio report generates the receivables totals for the previous 12 months and calculates a monthly average for three-month periods. To generate the Receivables Ratio report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Receivables Reports > Ratio . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Receivables Ratio Report Setup window is displayed. To generate the report for a specific provider, select the provider�s identification number from the Providers field, or accept the default of 999 to include information for all providers. Click OK . Additional Receivables Ratio Report Information: Tips for analyzing report results: If Ending Receivables is relatively stable and the Production on Books Ratio rises, then production is falling . If Ending Receivables is rising and the Production On Books Ratio is stable, then production is rising proportionately . If Ending Receivables is falling and the Production On Books Ratio rises, then production is falling proportionately . If Ending Receivables is approximately equal to Monthly Avg for the last 3 months, then collections are keeping pace with productions . If your receivables totals are not correct, the report information will be inaccurate. The Receivables Ratio report contains the following sections: Monthly Avg for last 3 months�Net Production$ stored in RCVTOT.DAT file for three-month period, divided by three. Ending Receivables�Ending balance for the last day of the month for the receivables stored in the RCVTOT.DAT file. Production On Books Ratio�Ending Receivables divided by Monthly Avg for last 3 months. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Receivables Summary Report
topics.body: Running the Receivables Summary Report Billing and Receivables > Using Billing and Receivables Reports > Running Receivables Reports Running the Receivables Summary Report The Receivables Summary report g enerates a list of prior receivables, the change in receivables, and new receivables for the current date, month-to-date, and year-to-date. To generate the Receivables Summary report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Receivables Reports > Summary . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Receivables Summary Report Setup window is displayed. Type the last date on which to summarize receivables in the Summarize Receivables Through field. To generate the report for a specific provider, select the provider�s identification number from the Providers field, or accept the default of 999 to include information for all providers. To include the average production figures in the report results, select Include Average Production Figures . Click OK . Additional Receivables Summary Report Information: For each period (Today, MTD, YTD), totals are calculated as follows: Net Production$ = Productions (production $ amounts for Dr$ for codes 100+) + Charges (61.00-95.99, 97.00-97.99, 99.00-99.99) +/- Production/Adjustments (40.00-60.99) Net Collections = Cash (1.00-1.99) + Check (3.00-10.99) + Credit Card payments (11.00-20.99) = Collections +/- Collection Adjustments (21.00-39.99, 96.00-96.99, 98.00-98.99) The Production$ and Net Production$ amounts only appear for providers collecting income for their own or others� productions. These fields will be $0.00 for providers whose collections are allocated to someone else. If your receivables totals are not current, the report information will be inaccurate. The Receivables Summary report contains the following sections: Prior Receivables TODAY�Beginning until previous day MTD�Beginning until last day of previous month YTD�Beginning until last day of previous year Change in Receivables TODAY�Receivables total for today ONLY MTD�Receivables total for first day of current month until today YTD�Receivables total for first day of current year until today If the Include Average Production Figures setting is set to Y , the following prints: Patients TODAY�Patients with transaction dates of today MTD�Patients with transaction dates of current month YTD�Patients with transaction dates of current year New Patients TODAY�Patients w/first visit date of today MTD�Patients w/first visit date of current month YTD�Patients w/first visit date of current year Avg Production/Patient Production$ divided by Patients. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Calculate Running RCVTOT Balance Utility
topics.body: Running the Calculate Running RCVTOT Balance Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running Receivables Totals Utilities Running the Calculate Running RCVTOT Balance Utility Run this utility to review transactions and recalculate your balances. Use this utility if you have not run a final daysheet in more than seven days. The running balance for seven days of receivables is recalculated when you run a final daysheet. To create a daily record for all transactions: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > RCVTOT > Calculate Running Balance . A message asks you whether to run the utility. Click Yes . A message informs you when the process is complete. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Compress RCVTOT Records Utility
topics.body: Running the Compress RCVTOT Records Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running Receivables Totals Utilities Running the Compress RCVTOT Records Utility Run this utility to compress your receivables totals. You can compress all receivables entries and set totals to a starting balance by provider. Compressed receivables information is stored in the RCVTOT file. The date range and totals for each entry is a single line entry by doctor. You should compress receivables totals every few years, depending how much information you want to keep available. Do not run this utility unless you are instructed to by a technical support representative. To compress your receivables totals: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > RCVTOT > Compress . The RCVTOT Date Range window is displayed. Select the appropriate dates from the drop-down lists, and click OK . The default values for the date fields are the oldest transaction date and three years prior to the most recent transaction date. Click Yes . After the compression is complete, a message is displayed. Click OK . When the technical support representative tells you to, restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Re-Creating Attachments Using Attachment History
topics.body: Re-Creating Attachments Using Attachment History Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services Re-Creating Attachments Using Attachment History If you created an attachment history, you can easily re-create attachments, if necessary. To re-create attachments: In the Transactions window, re-create the claim by clicking Submit Insurance . The software displays a prompt, asking if you want to add an electronic attachment. Click OK . The Transaction Submission window is displayed. Select the date range for the transactions you want to submit and click OK . The Claim window is displayed. Verify the claim information and click OK . The Submit window is displayed. Click OK . The Send Attachments to NEA window is displayed. Select the claim document that was associated to the original claim from the list of images and documents at the top of the window and click Add . The images from the attachment history are added to the thumbnail viewer, and each image retains its original type, orientation, and date of x-ray information. You can edit this information by selecting an image and changing the values in the Selected Image section. The original narrative text associated with the attachment is also automatically populated. You can edit the narrative by clicking in the Narrative field and typing your changes. Select one of the following: Click Launch NEA . A message is displayed, asking if you want to add an attachment history for the claim. Click Yes or No . The Fast Attach program is opened. It is recommended that you always add an attachment history for a claim. If you click Yes, the images and the narrative you typed are saved and attached to the claim document. Click Send Later to batch the attachments and send at a later time. A message is displayed, asking if you want to add an attachment history for the claim. Click Yes or No . If you click Yes, the images and the narrative you typed are saved and attached to the claim document. Tip Access batched attachments through the Fast Attach software or by selecting Send NEA Attachments from the Express bar. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the eVerifications Service
topics.body: Using the eVerifications Service eServices Using the eVerifications Service The eVerifications Service includes the eVerifications Insurance Eligibility Service and the eVerifications Claim Status Service. eVerifications Insurance Eligibility Service The eVerifications Insurance Eligibility Service enables you to get real-time insurance benefit and eligibility information directly from insurance companies. Patients' benefit information is accessed directly from the patient record, and the benefit information is saved to the patients' Documents window for future use. For more information, see Using the eVerifications Insurance Eligibility Service . eVerifications Claim Status Service The eVerifications Claim Status Service enables you to verify the current status of outstanding claims you have filed with the insurance companies. For more information, see Using the eVerifications Claim Status Service . Related Topics Using the eVerifications Insurance Eligibility Service Using the eVerifications Claim Status Service Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Rebuild/Reindex Utility
topics.body: Running the Rebuild/Reindex Utility Utilities > Using File Menu Utilities Running the Rebuild/Reindex Utility When a data file or its index is damaged or out of order, run this utility to check the integrity of the file and to rebuild the associated index files. Do not run this utility unless you are instructed to by a technical support representative. To rebuild or reindex files: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select File > Rebuild/Reindex or select Rebuild/Reindex . The Select Files to Rebuild/Reindex window is displayed. Select an option: To rebuild or reindex a specific file : Select it from the list. To rebuild or reindex all files : Click Select All . Click OK . A message asks you to rebuild or reindex the files. Click Yes . A message is displayed when the process is complete. Click OK . Click Close . When the technical support representative tells you to, restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Merging the Recall List Using the Letter Expert
topics.body: Merging the Recall List Using the Letter Expert Recalling Patients > Running Recall Reports Merging the Recall List Using the Letter Expert You can merge the list of patients needing recalls with specific letter types using the Letter Expert. To merge the recall list with letters: Select an option: To generate a list of patients who are due for normal recall : Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Last Exam . To generate a list of patients who are due for User-Defined recall : Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Perio . If you have not set up your Recall System or have upgraded from a previous version of the software and are running the first Recall report, the System Setting - PostCard Options window is displayed. Select an option and click OK . For more information, see Setting Up Recalls . If you change the name of your Normal Recall or User-Defined Recall system, the name of the report changes. For example, if the name of your User-Defined Recall system is Ortho, select Reports > Recall/Appt > Ortho . The Report Format window is displayed. Select Letter Expert and click OK . The Recall To Letter Expert Setup window is displayed. To change the report title, type a name in the Title field. In the Notices section, select an option: To update the patient record each time the patient is included in the Recall report: Select Update Notices Sent Count . Each time a patient is included in the Recall report, the Notices field on the Recall tab of the patient record is updated. This enables you to identify the number of notices sent to a patient. To indicate which patients should be included in the report: Select 0 Previous Notices to recall patients due to receive notice 1. Select 1 Previous Notice to recall patients due to receive notice 2. Select 2 Previous Notices to recall patients due to receive notice 3. Select 3 or more Previous Notices to recall patients who have already been sent 3 (or more) notices. Note : You can select more than one checkbox at a time. To recall patients who are due to receive their first, second, or third notice, select the 0 Previous Notices, 1 Previous Notice , and 2 Previous Notices checkboxes. In the Dates section, select a date range for the report. In the Patient Names section, select the start and stop last names from the drop-down lists to generate the recall list for a specific alphabetical range of patients. In the Providers section, select the provider identification number from the drop-down list to generate the recall list for a specific provider. To generate the recall list for all providers, select 999 from the drop-down list. To include only patients assigned to the doctor specified in this section : Select Search Patient�s Doctor . If you do not select this option, the report includes patients assigned to all providers. To include only patients assigned to a specific hygienist : Select the hygienist from the drop-down list, and select Search Patient�s Hygienist . In the Patient User Codes section, type the user codes for patients you want to include or exclude from the report. By default, user codes C (collections), I (inactive), L (legal case), and N (do not recall) are displayed in the Exclude field. In the Recall Tracking section, select an option: To post recall code 103000.92 to the ledger of each patient included in the recall report: Select Post a RECALL SENT code to the patient's ledger . To add a Recall Sent contact to the contact list of each patient included in the recall report: Select Add a REC

topics.title: Viewing Record Contacts
topics.body: Viewing Record Contacts Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Handling Contact Expert Tasks > Accessing the Auto Dialer Displaying Contacts You can view a list of previous and current contacts made for a record. To view a list of contacts for a record: In the Contact Expert window, select a record from the list in the left pane. Select a contact from the Previous Contacts section. Select Contacts > By Date or Contacts > By Type from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Contacts for window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Receiving and Viewing Alerts
topics.body: Receiving and Viewing Alerts Getting Started > Using the Software Update Service (SUS) Receiving and Viewing Alerts You might receive an SUS alert containing information about the service; for example, a message about low disk space or a server connection issue. To view alerts: Do one of the following: Click the alerts message. Right-click the triangle logo in the system tray, and select View Alerts . The Carestream Alerts window is displayed. Read the alert and click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Go To Recall Day
topics.body: Using Go To Recall Day Recalling Patients > Scheduling Recall Appointments Using Go To Recall Day Use Go To Recall Day t o recall a patient who does not have a recall procedure associated with his patient record. To use Go To Recall Day: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click a patient. The patient record is displayed in the Patient window. Select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Go To Recall Day from the Options menu. The Go To Recall Day submenu is displayed. Select an option: To schedule the patient for a normal recall appointment : Select Last Exam from the menu. To schedule the patient for an user-defined recall appointment : Select Perio from the menu. To schedule the patient for the next recall appointment : Select Next from the menu. If you change the name of your Normal Recall or User-Defined Recall system, the submenu command changes. For example, if the name of your User-Defined Recall system is Ortho, select Instant Recall > Ortho . The Daily Appointments window, or Daybook, is displayed. Search for and select a date and time slot and click Make Appt . A message asking if you want to make the appointment is displayed. Click Yes . The Appointment Card window is displayed. Select the recall procedures from the Code drop-down list. Click OK . The Daily Appointments window is displayed. Click Close . The Patient Appointments window is displayed. The unconfirmed appointment is displayed in red text. Select an option: To print the appointment : Select Print Appointments from the Options menu. The Output Options dialog box is displayed. Select Print and click OK . The Patient Appointments List Report Setup window is displayed. Select Print Next Appointment Only and click OK . To print an appointment reminder : Select Print Reminder from the Options menu. The Output Options dialog box is displayed. Select Print and click OK . Select Confirm from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The appointment is confirmed and displayed in black text. Click Close twice. If you are using the Motivated Recall system, the Notices field on the Recall tab of the patient record is reset to zero after the patient is checked out on the appointment date for the recall procedure. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Recall Report in Office Expert
topics.body: Running the Recall Report in Office Expert Recalling Patients > Running Recall Reports Running the Recall Report in Office Expert You can generate the recall report as part of your Office Expert To-Do list. To run the recall report in Office Expert: Select Experts > Office Expert . The Office Expert Setup window is displayed. Click Setup Experts . The Office Expert Setup window is displayed. Select Recall (Last Exam) in the Available Office Expert Items list and click Add Selected Item . The Recall Office Expert Setup window is displayed. To change the report title, type a name in the Title field. In the Dates section, select a date range for the report. In the Patient Names section, select the start and stop last names from the drop-down lists to generate the recall list for a specific alphabetical range of patients. In the Providers section, select the provider identification number from the drop-down list to generate the recall list for a specific provider. To generate the recall list for all providers, select 999 from the drop-down list. In the Patient User Codes section, type the user codes for patients you want to include in the report. In the Options section, select the priority level from the drop-down list. Click OK . See Setting Up Office Expert Items for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Reconciliation Report
topics.body: Running the Reconciliation Report Billing and Receivables > Using Billing and Receivables Reports > Running Collection Reports Running the Reconciliation Report The Reconciliation report g enerates a list of your collections by cash, personal check, insurance check, and credit card charges during a specific date range. The report also includes the daily total for each date, listing any discrepancies between the calculated amount and the receivables file amount. Run this report for the previous month�s receivables to ensure the validity of your bank statements. Additional Details: An asterisk is displayed for dates where there is a mismatch between the transaction and receivables files. There are several reasons for a mismatch: Whenever transactions are posted to an insurance plan, a mismatch occurs because insurance plan transactions are not stored in the receivables file. A final daysheet has not been run. Dangling transactions are present. Sample Report To generate the Reconciliation report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Collection Reports > Reconciliation . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Reconciliation Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates from the drop-down lists. Select the provider(s) you want to include in the report, or accept the default of 999 to include information for all providers. To include the details for each transaction in the report, select Include Detail Transactions . Click OK . Additional Reconciliation Report Information: The Reconciliation report contains the following fields: Date�Date of payment. Cash�Dollar amount for cash payments posted (codes 1.00-1.99). Check�Dollar amount for personal check and insurance check payments posted (codes 2.00-2.99 and codes 3.00-10.99). Credit Card�Dollar amount for credit card payments posted (codes 11.00-20.99). Daily Total�Total payments posted for each date. Subtotals�Totals for cash, check, and credit card payments for the specified date range. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reconciling Treatment Plans
topics.body: Reconciling Treatment Plans Treatment Planning Reconciling Treatment Plans If a different procedure was performed from what is listed in the treatment plan, you can reconcile the treatment plan at checkout by deleting the unnecessary procedures. To use the Reconcile Treatment Plan Items option during checkout, the following conditions must be met: Ask to Process must be selected in the Ledger Preferences section of the General System Settings window. See Setting Patient Checkout Options for more information. The patient you are checking out must have a treatment plan with pending procedures. If the procedures you post match the treatment plan items exactly, the procedures are automatically marked as completed and will not display in this window. To reconcile a treatment plan: Post the procedures for the appointment, and click OK or Close . The checkout options window is displayed. Select Reconcile Treatment Plan Items , and click OK (Process) . Depending on the options you have selected in the checkout options window, several checkout windows are displayed (for example: Transaction Submission, Claim, Submit Primary, Appointment Card, Print Walkout Statement). Complete the information in the windows, and click Close or OK . The Treatment Plan window is displayed. Select the procedures to delete, and click Delete on the toolbar in the middle of the window. The Confirm Delete window is displayed. Do one of the following: Select Delete Single Item and click OK. Select Delete Group , s elect the group from the drop-down list, and click OK . You can right-click on a treatment plan item to edit the item, but editing an item does not reconcile it. Click Close on the toolbar in the middle of the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Recovering a Password Without Exiting SoftDent
topics.body: Recovering a Password Without Exiting SoftDent System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Recovering a Password Without Exiting SoftDent You can run the Display Encoded Passwords utility without having all staff members log out of SoftDent. This utility produces a list of encrypted numbers that you read to a SoftDent support technician, who will identify the staff member's password. You can run the utility from the SoftDent Login window from the following locations: At SoftDent startup At Change Login At HIPAA Automatic Logoff To run the Display Encoded Passwords utility without having all staff members log out of SoftDent: In the SoftDent Login window, type DECODE in the Name and Password fields. Click OK . The Decode Passwords window is displayed. The Password column displays a series of numbers that represent the encrypted password. Contact SoftDent Technical Support, and read the numbers to the support representative. The User ID 1 (DOCTOR/DOCTOR12) password information is displayed in blue text. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Referring Doctor List by ID Report
topics.body: Running the Referring Doctor List by ID Report Patient and Doctor Referrals > Running Referral Reports Running the Referring Doctor List by ID Report The Referring Doctor List by ID report generates a list of incoming and outgoing referring doctors by identification number. Sample Report To run the Referring Doctor List by ID report: Select Reports > Provider/Referring Dr > Referring Doctor > ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Referring Doctor by ID Report window is displayed. In the Referring Doctors section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending doctor identification numbers from a list. To include inactive referring doctors, click the Include Inactive Referring Doctors checkbox. Select the appropriate option from the Options section. To use color shading in the report, click the Include Color Shading checkbox. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Referring Doctor Labels by ID Report
topics.body: Running the Referring Doctor Labels by ID Report Patient and Doctor Referrals > Running Referral Reports Running the Referring Doctor Labels by ID Report The Referring Doctor Labels by ID report generates labels for referring doctors sorted by identification number. Sample Label To run the Referring Doctor Labels by ID report: Select Reports > Provider/Referring Dr > Referring Doctor > Labels > ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Referring Doctor Labels by ID Report Setup window is displayed. In the Referring Doctors section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending doctor identification numbers from a list. To include inactive referring doctors, click the Include Inactive Referring Doctors checkbox. Select the appropriate option from the Options section. Click OK . If you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Referring Doctor Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly, and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Referral Production Analysis Report
topics.body: Running the Referral Production Analysis Report Patient and Doctor Referrals > Running Referral Reports Running the Referral Production Analysis Report The Referral Production Analysis report lists all providers who have referred patients to your practice. It includes the referring doctor�s name and the patient�s name. To run the Referral Production Analysis report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Referral Reports > Production . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Referral Analysis Report Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending patient visit dates from a calendar. In the Options section, select Include Patient Details to list the production and collection amounts for each referred patient seen. In the Referring Doctor ID section, click the drop-down arrow to select the providers from a list, or leave 999 to report on all providers. Click OK . Additional Referral Production Analysis Report Information: The report includes the following information: Referral�If the Print Patient Details option is checked, all referred patients are listed under the provider, and the name of the referring provider is displayed in bold. Number New�Number of new patients referred by the provider with production posted during the specified date range. Number Seen�Number of patients who were referred by the provider with production posted during the specified date range. Production�Total dollar amount posted for service codes 100.00 + on patients referred by the provider. Collection�Total dollar amount posted for collection codes 1.00-20.99 on patients referred by the provider. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Referral Source Analysis Report
topics.body: Running the Referral Source Analysis Report Patient and Doctor Referrals > Running Referral Reports Running the Referral Source Analysis Report The Referral Source Analysis report displays the number of patients�and their production amounts�who have visited your office in the specified time periods by each referring doctor, grouped by categories that you create. You must set up referral categories to use this report properly. For more information, see Creating Referral Categories . Sample Report To run the Referral Source Analysis report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Referral Reports > Referral Source Analysis . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Referral Source Analysis Report Setup window is displayed. In the Options section, de-select Include Patient Referrals if you do not want to include patients who referred other patients in the report. If included in the report, patient referrals have a category of 0. In the Date Ranges section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending patient visit dates from a calendar. In the Include Analysis for Provider(s) section, click the drop-down arrow to select specific providers from a list, or leave 999 to report on all providers. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Registering for The Exchange
topics.body: Registering for The Exchange Finding Answers > Accessing The Exchange Registering for The Exchange You can view all available content on The Exchange without an account. But you need a free account if you want to ask or answer questions, maintain a profile, or interact with other SoftDent users, as well as support representatives and trainers. To register an individual for The Exchange: Click The Exchange , or type https://community.carestreamdental.com/t5/SoftDent-Discussion/bd-p/softdentdiscussion into the Internet browser address field. The SoftDent Discussion page is displayed. Click Sign In . The Sign In window is displayed. Click the New User? Register here link. The Register window is displayed. Complete all fields on the window, and click Register . Once you have an account and log in, click the avatar at the upper-right corner of the window, and click My Settings to add more information about yourself and set your preferences on the Personal, Preferences, Avatars, Tagging, Macros, and Subscriptions and Notifications tabs. Related Topics Accessing The Exchange Logging In to The Exchange Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Registering for a Carestream Dental Institute Account
topics.body: Registering for a Carestream Dental Institute Account Finding Answers > Accessing the Carestream Dental Institute Registering for a Carestream Dental Institute Account B efore you can access an interactive web training course, you must register with the Carestream Dental Institute. To register an individual for an interactive web training course: Click Carestream Dental Institute or type http://www.carestreamdental.com/cdi into the Internet browser address field. Click Register . In the Registration User Information sheet, type the information for your office. E-mail or fax the form to Carestream Dental. Related Topics Accessing the Carestream Dental Institute Logging In to the Carestream Dental Institute Reviewing Information in the Carestream Dental Institute SoftDent Practice Management Software Learning Resources Need more information? topics.title: Running Register Reports
topics.body: Running Register Reports Handling Reports > Accounting Reports Running Register Reports Register reports summarize receivables and production information. There are six types of register reports: Daily, Monthly, Yearly, and Period The Daily, Monthly, Yearly, and Period Register reports summarize receivables information for a specific period and include the number of patients seen and how many of those patients were new. These reports also give the total production amount for the period; a breakdown of charges, adjustments, and collections; average production per patient; and average production per hour for the time period. Cumulative The Cumulative Register summarizes production information by provider for a specific date and includes the number of patients seen, theoretical, and actual time spent with patients. It also gives the total production amount for the period, a breakdown of average production, and average production per hour for the time period. Summary The Register Summary report summarizes receivables information for a specific period and includes the number of patients seen and referred out. It also gives the total production amount for the period; a breakdown of charges, adjustments, and collections; average production per patient; and average production per hour for the time period. Related Topics Running Daily, Monthly, Yearly, or Period Register Reports Running a Cumulative Register Report Running a Register Summary Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running a Register Summary Report
topics.body: Running a Register Summary Report Handling Reports > Accounting Reports > Running Register Reports Running a Register Summary Report To run a register summary report: Select Reports > Accounting > Registers > Summary . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Register Summary Report window is displayed. In the Options section, click the drop-down arrow in the Report Date field to select a date from the calendar. Select the Roll Indirect Production into the Collection Dr. checkbox if you want to include all indirect sources in the report. In the Providers section, click the drop-down arrow to select the provider number from a list, or leave the first provider set to 999 to report on all providers. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Place Excel Chart on separate sheet: The Gross Production By Provider information is displayed in chart form. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave all options selected, the report information is displayed on three tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Click OK . Additional Details: This report includes every transaction, including = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('Dangl.htm');return false;">dangling transactions. Report Elements: FF�Number of patients seen with Flat Fee insurance coverage. DMO�Number of patients seen with DMO/Capitation insurance coverage. PPO�Number of patients seen with Preferred Provider insurance coverage. Medicare�Number of patients seen with Medicare insurance coverage. Medicaid�Number of patients seen with Medicaid insurance coverage. No Cov�Number of patients seen with no insurance coverage. Patients Seen�Total number of patients seen during the specified date range. Gross Prod./Charges�Total dollar amount posted to charges (codes 61.00-95.89, 97.00-97.89, 99.00-99.89, 100.00+). Aver $ Per Patient�Net Productions divided by Patients Seen. Adjustments To Production�Total dollar amount posted to production adjustments (codes 40.00-60.89). Adjustments to Collections�Total dollar amount posted to collections adjustments (codes 21.00-39.89, 96.00-96.89, 98.00-98.89), including plan transactions. Amount due to PPO write-offs�Total dollar amount posted to PPO write-offs (code 50.90). Net Productions�Net Productions = Gross Productions Charges +/- Adjustments to Production - Tax. Collections�Total collections = Cash + Check + Credit Cards. Collections %�Collections divided by the Gross Production/Charges for the provider. Patients Refd Out�Number of patients referred to outside providers. Sample Report Related Topics Running Daily, Monthly, Yearly, or Period Register Reports Running a Cumulative Register Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Re-Integrating PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform Using PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo System Settings Re-Integrating PracticeToGo with the Palm OS Platform To re-integrate PracticeToGo with the Palm OS platform after you have disabled it: Select System > PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo Platform > Use the Palm OS Platform .
