topics.title: Running the Remake Scheduler File Utility
topics.body: Running the Remake Scheduler File Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Remake Scheduler File Utility Run this utility to erase the sched.dat file and recreate it. You can also use this utility to reconfigure the number of operatories and time units for the appointment scheduler. You must run the Scheduler Repair utility after running this utility. Do not run this utility unless you are instructed to by a technical support representative. To delete and recreate the scheduler file: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Remake Scheduler File . A message informs you that the scheduler will be deleted if you run this utility. Click Yes . The Remake Scheduler File window is displayed. Click OK . A message informs you that the scheduler will be deleted if you run this utility. Click Yes . After the utility is complete, the SoftDent utilities window is displayed. When the technical support representative tells you to, restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Remap ADA Codes Utility
topics.body: Running the Remap ADA Codes Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Remap ADA Codes Utility Run this utility to convert old ADA codes to new ADA codes for appointments already scheduled. Current appointments and tickler file entries are updated with the new ADA code information. To remap ADA codes: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Remap ADA Code . The Remap ADA Code window is displayed. Type the current and new codes in the appropriate fields. You can remap up to 10 ADA codes at one time. Click Apply . To remap another set of codes, repeat steps 4 and 5. Click Close . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Remap Provider Utility
topics.body: Running the Remap Provider Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Remap Provider Utility Run this utility to substitute a new provider in patient and account records. Appointment, treatment plan transaction, and tickler entry records are updated with the new doctor�s identification number. To replace an existing doctor�s identification number with a new one: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Remap Provider . The Remap Provider ID window is displayed. Select an option: To remap provider identification numbers : Select Remap Doctor ID from the Pick an option to run this utility drop-down list. To remap hygienist identification numbers : Select Remap Hygienist ID from the Pick an option to run this utility drop-down list. Type the new and old provider identification numbers in the appropriate fields. Click OK . A message informs you that the process is complete. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Removing Image Associations
topics.body: Removing Image Associations Handling Images While Charting > Using Organization Mode Removing Image Associations To remove all tooth and regions associations with an image: On the Clinical Imaging tab in the Chart for window, select Organization Mode . Select buttons on the Clinical Imaging toolbar . Select images in the Clinical Detail Container. Select the image on the Clinical Images tab and drag it to the Remove Associations icon. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Removing a Chart Graphic and Re-Charting a Procedure
topics.body: Removing a Chart Graphic and Re-Charting a Procedure Handling Transactions and Codes > Posting Patient Procedures Removing a Chart Graphic and Re-Charting a Procedure You might encounter a situation where you need to remove an existing graphic from the chart and re-chart a new procedure. (For example: an implant fails and needs to be retreated; an extraction was charted for the wrong tooth, etc.) In a case like this, you can use the Remove Chart Graphic option on the Patient Transactions window. You must have the " Modify Remove Chart Graphic Checkbox" Transactions security permission to use the Remove Chart Graphic feature. When you edit a procedure code transaction and check the Remove Chart Graphic option: A grey asterisk (indicating the procedure has been removed) is displayed next to the tooth number with the removed graphic. A *Removed graphic for [ada code description] entry is placed in the Description column of the Clinical History tab for the transaction with the "Remove Chart Graphic" checkbox selected. A Rem Cht Graph -- Checked entry is made in the Transaction Audit Trail report. A Rem Cht Graph--Unchecked entry is made in the Transaction Audit Trail report w hen charting graphics have been added back into the chart (when the Remove Chart Graphic checkbox is deselected). To remove an existing charted procedure from the chart: In the Patient List window, select a patient and click Transactions on the toolbar. The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select the transaction for the procedure you want to remove from the chart, and click Edit. The Patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Remove Chart Graphic , and type a reason for the change in the Note field. Click OK . Click Close . Open the patient's chart and chart the new procedure. (Note the asterisk next to the tooth number and the "Removed Graphic" entry on the Clinical History tab.) Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Replacing Backup Media
topics.body: Replacing Backup Media Backing Up Data Replacing Backup Media Regular use of backup media eventually degrades the quality of the media. The more often backups are used, the quicker they degrade. The only way to know if media is no longer functional is to attempt to restore data from it. By periodically replacing the media, you can avoid deterioration problems. Follow the replacement schedule recommended by the media manufacturer. To keep track of the replacement dates, mark them on the disk or tape label for easy reference. Follow the manufacturer�s maintenance instructions. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reprinting Billing Statements
topics.body: Reprinting Billing Statements Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements > Printing Billing Statements Reprinting Billing Statements If printer problems occur while generating billing statements, you can reprint the statements that did not print correctly. To reprint billing statements: Select Reports > Accounting > Statements > Paper > Rerun Paper Statements . The Paper Statements window is displayed. In the Account Search section, type the start and ending account names for the statements you need to print. In the Date section, click the arrow button and use the calendar to select a date. In the Statement Message field, type a note, if necessary. Click OK . The Paper Statements List window is displayed. Select from the following: To change the sort order: Click the column label. To exclude a statement: Deselect the checkbox in the Account Name column. To display additional information: Select the account. The information is displayed in the Additional Information section of the window. To include a statement note: Select the account and type the note in the Personalized Statement Note for Selected Account field. To edit the account information, select the account and click Edit Acct . The Account window is displayed. Edit the record and click OK . Select from the following: To preview statements for specific accounts: Select the account and click Preview . To preview statements for the selected accounts: Click Preview All . Click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reprinting CareCredit Receipts
topics.body: Reprinting CareCredit Receipts eServices > Using CareCredit Reprinting CareCredit Receipts You can reprint a CareCredit sales draft or refund receipt for a customer, if necessary. To reprint a CareCredit receipt: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and click Transactions . In the Patient window : Click Transactions . The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select the CareCredit transaction (payment or refund) from the list. Select CareCredit Integration > Print Receipt from the Options section of the Express bar. If your security permissions enable you to edit transactions, you can also reprint the receipt by clicking Reprint Receipt in the Transactions window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Canceling and Rescheduling Appointments
topics.body: Canceling and Rescheduling Appointments Scheduling Appointments > Rescheduling Appointments Canceling and Rescheduling Appointments To cancel and reschedule a patient appointment: In the Daily Appointments window, select the appointment and select Cancel Appt. The appointment is canceled and displayed, floating on the left side of the window. The default background color is orange, and Reschedule Cancelled Appt is displayed at the bottom of the appointment. Reschedule Cancelled Appt is only displayed if the appointment is four slots or more. To display the appointment card, select the appointment, right-click, and select View Appt. Review the details and click Close. Tip After you display the appointment details, you must resolve the appointment. To resolve the cancelled appointment, do one of the following: To reschedule the appointment, select the appointment, drag it to the new appointment time, and release the mouse. To send the appointment to the Tickler file, select the appointment, right-click, and select Place in Tickler . To send the appointment to the Short Call Tickler file, select the appointment, right-click, and select Place in Short Call Tickler . To autoschedule the appointment, select the appointment, right-click, and select AutoSchedule Appt . Select the start and end dates and click OK . The first available slot is displayed. Find the correct slot and select Make Appt . To delete the appointment, select the appointment, right-click, and click Delete . Tip To scan the Short Call Tickler or Tickler file, select Tickler from the Express bar. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Reset Scheduler Default Book Utility
topics.body: Running the Reset Scheduler Default Book Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Reset Scheduler Default Book Utility Run this utility to reset the default book to the office book displayed in the Scheduler Books List window and to properly use the auto scheduler. A default book is set for users and for the practice. To view the default book setting for the practice: From the software, select File > Practice . The Practice window is displayed. Click the Office Hours tab. The information is displayed in the Default Book field. To view the default book for users: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security and select an item on the submenu. When the System Security window is displayed, click Rights By User . Double-click the appropriate user. To reset the scheduler default book: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Reset Scheduler Default Book . The Reset Scheduler Default Book window is displayed. Click Yes . A message informs you the process is complete. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resending eForms
topics.body: Resending eForms eServices > Using the eForms Module Resending eForms The Resend eForms function deletes all the forms that have been sent for an appointment and resends them. This function can be helpful in several instances: You sent forms to the wrong e-mail address. The patient never received an e-mail. A particular form has been updated. The patient deleted the e-mail, etc. The patient's appointment is rescheduled for a day/time outside of the parameters in the eForms Configuration window. Example: You add two forms to an appointment from the scheduler and click Send eForms Now . Then you add one more form. If you click Send eForms Now , only the form you added last will be sent. If you click Resend eForms , all of the attached forms will be sent again. To resend eForms to a specific patient: In the scheduler, right-click on an appointment, and select eForms > Resend eForms . Related Topics Sending and Receiving eForms Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the View/Reset Last DaySheet Date Utility
topics.body: Running the View/Reset Last DaySheet Date Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running Receivables Totals Utilities Running the View/Reset Last DaySheet Date Utility Run this utility to reset your final daysheet date to the correct date. Run this utility after running a final daysheet for a future date. Only users with the proper security settings can run this utility. To reset your final daysheet day: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > RCVTOT > View/Reset Last Daysheet Date . The Daysheet Date for an Office window is displayed. Select the date. Click Reset . A message asks you whether to reset the date. Click Yes . Click OK . Click Cancel . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resetting Holidays
topics.body: Resetting Holidays Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Holidays Resetting Holidays At the end of the year, you must reset all holiday dates for the next year. To update the annual holiday schedule: Select Scheduler > Holidays . The Setup Holidays window is displayed. Select an option: To update annual holidays : Select Reset Dates from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The year portion of the date for each annual holiday is changed to the next year. To update holidays that fall on a different day each year : Select a holiday and click Edit . Make changes and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resetting a Patient Budget Plan
topics.body: Resetting a Patient Budget Plan Resetting a Patient Budget Plan If you enter an incorrect amount while establishing a patient budget plan, you can reset it from within the patient budget plan options. Note that if the patient budget plan is being terminated early and there is a remaining balance on the patient's budget plan, a code 48 in the amount of the remaining balance should be posted to the patient Transactions window in order to bring that balance back to the patient. To reset a patient budget plan: In the patient Transactions list window, select Patient Budget Plan from the Express bar. The Patient Budget Plan window is displayed. Select Reset Budget Plan from Options menu. Click Yes to confirm resetting the budget plan. Close the patient budget plan window. On the patient Transactions window, post code 40.xx for the �Balance to Bill� amount. Additional Information: Note the Balance to Bill and monthly payments. The Balance to Bill amount will become zero when you select Reset Budget Plan . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resetting Insurance Plan CDT Versions
topics.body: Resetting Insurance Plan CDT Versions Setting Up Insurance > Using CDT Codes Resetting Insurance Plan CDT Versions You can reset the CDT code version for: An insurance plan All insurance plans associated with a specific insurance company All insurance plans Related Topics Resetting the_CDT_Version_for_an_Insurance_Plan Resetting_the_CDT_Version_for_Insurance_Plans Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resetting the CDT Version for an Insurance Plan
topics.body: Resetting the CDT Version for an Insurance Plan Setting Up Insurance > Using CDT Codes > Resetting Insurance Plan CDT Versions Resetting the CDT Version for an Insurance Plan To reset the CDT code version used with any single insurance plan: Select List > Ins. Plan . The Insurance plan List window is displayed. Double-click the insurance plan. The insurance plan�s information is displayed in the Insurance Plan window. Click the Claim Filing tab. The Claim Filing tab is the active window. Select 2022 from the This plan accepts CDT Version: drop-down list. Click OK . A message is displayed, asking you if you want to change the CDT version for all plans belonging to the insurance company. Click Yes to change the CDT version for all plans, or click No to change the CDT version for only the selected plan. Click Close twice. Related Topics Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting Resetting Insurance Plan CDT Versions Viewing ADA Code Descriptions Viewing Claim Documents Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resetting the CDT Version for Insurance Plans
topics.body: Resetting the CDT Version for Insurance Plans Setting Up Insurance > Using CDT Codes > Resetting Insurance Plan CDT Versions Resetting the CDT Version for Insurance Plans To reset the CDT version associated with insurance plans: Select System > Fees Maintenance > Reset Insurance Plan CDT Version . The Reset CDT Version window is displayed. Select 2022 from the Reset CDT Version to field. Do one of the following: To reset the CDT code format to the 2022 codes for plans associated with all insurance companies, select For ALL Plans . To reset the CDT code format to the 2022 codes for plans associated with a specific company, select For Plans Belonging to the Following Company and select the company from the drop-down list. Click OK . A message is displayed, asking you to confirm the operation. Click Yes . Related Topics Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting Resetting Insurance Plan CDT Versions Viewing ADA Code Descriptions Viewing Claim Documents Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resetting the Color Group Definition for an Individual Procedure to the Daybook Color
topics.body: Resetting the Color Group Definition for an Individual Procedure to the Daybook Color Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Up Color Scheduling Resetting the Color Group Definition for an Individual Procedure to the Daybook Color To reset the color of a procedure contained within a color group definition to the color used in the Daily Appointments window, select a code in the ADA/Transaction Codes List window, and select Reset Color . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Reset Schedule Colors Utility
topics.body: Running the Reset Schedule Colors Utility Utilities > Running Customize Menu Utilities Running the Reset Schedule Colors Utility Run this utility to return your scheduler colors to the original defaults. To reset scheduler colors: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Customize > Reset Schedule Colors . The Reset Schedule Colors window is displayed. Select an option: To reset ADA colors to the defaults : Select Reset ADA Schedule Colors . To reset scheduler color defaults : Select Reset Schedule Colors . Click OK . A message asks whether to run the utility. Click Yes . A message informs you that the process is complete. The Reset Schedule Colors window is displayed. Click Cancel . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Restoring Data
topics.body: Restoring Data Backing Up Data Restoring Data A step-by-step restore process varies according to your system manufacturer. Be sure to validate your individual data restore plan to assure a prompt recovery of your business records, if necessary. It is recommended that you contact your hardware vendor or system administrator before attempting to restore data. Your office�s restore plan should include copying the current data before completing a restore. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Restorative Tab Toolbars
topics.body: Using Restorative Tab Toolbars Restorative Charting Using Restorative Tab Toolbars The following toolbars can be displayed in the Toolbars section on the Restorative tab: Common Procedure Codes Chart Mode Conditions Mobility You can customize each toolbar to display the commands that you most often use. To add a button to the toolbar, see Customizing Toolbars . To use the Common toolbar, see Using the Common Toolbar . Related Topics Using the Procedure Codes Toolbar Using the Chart Mode Toolbar Using the Conditions Toolbar Using the Mobility Toolbar Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resubmitting a Previously Printed Claim Electronically
topics.body: Resubmitting a Previously Printed Claim Electronically Submitting and Tracking Claims > Resubmitting and Deleting Claims Resubmitting a Previously Printed Claim Electronically If a claim was not initially sent electronically (you submitted a paper copy), it can be re-sent electronically, if necessary. To resubmit a previously printed claim electronically: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient, and click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Outstanding Insurance . The Claims window is displayed. Select the claim, and click View Claim . The Claim window is displayed. Click the Status tab. Select the ECS option, and click OK . Click Submit Primary . If a "This claim was sent electronically, Send Again?" message is displayed, click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling the Revolving Credit Plan Feature
topics.body: Enabling the Revolving Credit Plan Feature Planning Budgets > Handling Account Budget Plans > Setting Up Account Balances for a Revolving Credit Budget Plan Enabling the Revolving Credit Plan Feature To enable the revolving credit plan feature: Select System > Change System Settings > General System . The General System Settings window is displayed. Click Edit and select Enable Revolving Credit . Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reviewing eForms
topics.body: Reviewing eForms eServices > Using the eForms Module Reviewing eForms Review a patient's eForms using a computer loaded with both the SoftDent and eForms software. When you review completed eForms, several scenarios are possible: The patient has made no changes, and you click Accept . The patient has made changes, and you review the changes and click Accept . The patient has made changes to insurance or employer information, and the software has indicated that several possible matches exist. You use the matching window to select the correct option or add the new information to the database and click Accept . The patient has made changes, and you need further information or clarification and click Reject . When you reject a form, the software removes the form from the list. Any changes to the patient data are ignored, and the information is not merged into the database; however, the rejected eForm is archived in the patient's Documents folder. To review eForms: Select an option: From the System menu: Select System > Modules > eForms > Review eForms . The eForms Review window is displayed. Click Select Patient . The Select Patient search window is displayed. Do one of the following: Type the patient�s name in the field. Click the Calendar button, and select the patient�s appointment date from the calendar. From the daybook or Book Quick Glance view: Right-click on an appointment, and select eForms > Review eForms . In the daybook, you can also click the Forms Need to be Completed/Signed icon or the Forms Need to be Reviewed icon to display the eForms Review window. From the Patient List or Patient windows: Select eForms > Review eForms from the Options section of the Express bar. The completed eForms for the patient are displayed in the eForms Review window. If a patient has not submitted a form, it will not display in the list. For additional information, see the eForms online help. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reviewing Account Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Transactions
topics.body: Reviewing Account Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Transactions Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans > Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Account Budget Plans Reviewing Account Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Transactions To view recurring credit card payment transactions: In the Account window, select Acct Budget Plan . The Account Budget Plan List window is displayed. Select Terms . The FIXED LOAN TERMS window is displayed. Click the CC Records tab. The tab is the active window. Select from the following options: To display settled payments, select Display Settled . To display authorized payments, select Display Authorized . To display payments that failed authorization, select Displayed Failed Authorization . To display payments that are not yet authorized, select Display Not Authorized . To display voided payments, select Display Voided . After reviewing the transactions, click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reviewing and Editing Payments in a Credit Card Payment Plan
topics.body: Reviewing and Editing Payments in a Credit Card Payment Plan Budget Planning > Creating Credit Card Payment Plans Reviewing and Editing Payments in a Credit Card Payment Plan To view or edit credit card payment plan payments: Do one of the following: In the Account List window, select an account and select Credit Card Payment Plan . In the Patient List window, select a patient and select Credit Card Payment Plan . Click the CC Payment tab. The tab is the active window. Select from the following display options: To display settled payments, select Display Settled . To display authorized payments, select Display Authorized . To display payments that failed authorization, select Displayed Failed Authorization . To display payments that are not yet authorized, select Display Not Authorized . To display voided payments, select Display Voided . Tip Deselect an option to hide payments of that type. To edit a payment, right-click on the payment to display the shortcut menu. Select from the following options: Edit Amount To edit a credit card payment amount, follow these steps: In the Credit Card Payment Plan�Details window, click the CC Payment tab. Select the payment you want to change and right-click. The shortcut menu is displayed. Select Edit Amount . The Edit Payment Amount window is displayed. In the New Amount field, type the new amount. Click OK . The selected payment is updated with the new amount, and the Unallocated text at the bottom of the window is updated. Edit Date To edit a credit card payment date, follow these steps: In the Credit Card Payment Plan�Details window, click the CC Payment tab. Select the payment you want to change and right-click. The shortcut menu is displayed. Select Edit Date . The Edit Payment Date window is displayed. In the New Authorization Date field, type the new date. The new date must occur after today's date and must be within the previous payment and next payment date range. If you are changing the date of the first payment, the date you type must be after today�s date and before the next payment date. If you are changing the last payment date, the date must be after today�s date and after the previous payment. Click OK . The selected payment is updated with the new date. Split Amount To split a credit card payment amount, follow these steps: In the Credit Card Payment Plan�Details window, click the CC Payment tab. Select the payment you want to split and right-click. The shortcut menu is displayed. Select Split Amount . A new payment is inserted after the selected payment, and the original payment amount is split between the two payments. All other payments are advanced one month. Add Payment To add a credit card payment, follow these steps: In the Credit Card Payment Plan�Details window, click the CC Payment tab. Select a payment from the list and right-click. The shortcut menu is displayed. Select Add Payment . A payment is added to the end of the payment plan. It is dated to occur one month from the current last payment, and the authorization amount defaults to the amount of the selected payment. Select Edit Amount from the shortcut menu to edit the payment amount, if necessary. You cannot edit credit card payments for a payment plan that is linked to a budget plan. Click Close . Related Topics Using the Recurring Credit Card Payment Details Window Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reviewing Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Transactions
topics.body: Reviewing Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Transactions Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Patient Budget Plans Reviewing Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Transactions To view recurring credit card payment transactions: In the Patient List window, select the patient and select Budget Plan . The Budget Plan window is displayed. Click the CC Records tab . The tab is the active window. Click Edit CC Payment and select from the following options: To display settled payments, select Display Settled . To display authorized payments, select Display Authorized . To display payments that failed authorization, select Displayed Failed Authorization . To display payments that are not yet authorized, select Display Not Authorized . To display voided payments, select Display Voided . After reviewing the transactions, click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reviewing Time Card Audit Trail Entries
topics.body: Reviewing Time Card Audit Trail Entries Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card Reviewing Time Card Audit Trail Entries Generate the Time Card Audit Trail to create and review a list of entries. Before generating the audit trail, see Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides . To generate the Time Card Audit Trail: Select System > View Audit Trails > Time Card Audit Trail . The Audit trail - Time Card Entry List window is displayed. To display entry details, double-click the entry. The Audit trail - Time Card Entry window is displayed. Review the information and click Close . The Audit trail - Time Card Entry List window is displayed. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Refilling a Prescription
topics.body: Refilling a Prescription Pharmacies and Prescriptions > Handling Prescriptions Refilling a Prescription To refill a prescription: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the patient chart: Hover over the Prescriptions section of the patient banner and click the link, or click the Prescription button on the Common toolbar. In the Account List window : Select the patient and select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patients associated with the account are displayed in the Patient List window. Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Account window : Select Patients from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patients associated with the account are displayed in the Patient List window. Select Clinical Management > Prescription History from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patient�s prescriptions are displayed in the Prescriptions for List window. Select the prescription to refill from the list and select Refill from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Prescriptions for window is displayed. Type the number of refills in the Refills field. Click OK . The Prescription Options window is displayed. Select an option: To use the auto dialer to call the pharmacy : Select Call Preferred Pharmacy and click OK . The Pharmacy Phone Numbers window is displayed. Click Dial . If the patient has no preferred pharmacy, this option is disabled. To call another pharmacy : Select Choose another Pharmacy and click OK . The Pharmacy List window is displayed. Double-click the pharmacy. The Pharmacy Phone Numbers window is displayed. Click Dial . To print the prescription : Select Print Prescription and click OK. The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor from the Patient Appointments Window
topics.body: Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor from the Patient Appointments Window Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals > Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor from the Patient Appointments Window To refer one of your patients to an outside doctor from the Patient Appointments window: Select Make Referral from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Patient Referral window is displayed. Select the doctor to whom you are referring the patient from the Referred to drop-down list. Type the reason for the referral in the Reason field. Select the date on which you expect the patient to return to your practice from the Expected Back drop-down list. If no return date is selected, you are prompted to use the current date when a production code is posted after the patient�s expected Due Back date. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor
topics.body: Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor You can refer a patient to an outside doctor from the following windows: Patient Patient Appointment Related Topics Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor from the Patient Window Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor from the Patient Appointments Window Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor from the Patient Window
topics.body: Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor from the Patient Window Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals > Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor Referring a Patient to an Outside Doctor from the Patient Window To refer one of your patients to an outside doctor from the patient record: In the Patient window of the patient you want to refer, select the doctor from the Doctor drop-down list in the Referred Out To section of the Info tab. Type the reason for the referral in the For field. Select the date on which you referred your patient and the date you expect the patient to return to your practice from the date drop-down lists. If no return date is selected, you are prompted to use the current date when a production code is posted after the patient�s expected Due Back date. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Referring Dr List and Referring Dr Window Options
topics.body: Using Referring Dr List and Referring Dr Window Options Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals Using Referring Dr List and Referring Dr Window Options From the REFERRING DR List and REFERRING DR windows, you can: Use the auto dialer Print labels Print envelopes Merge referring doctor information with a letter template Add a contact to the referring doctor View a list of incoming or outgoing referrals for the doctor The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: 0 - Auto Dialer : Displays the Referring Provider Phone Numbers window. If you have a modem set up in the software, you can dial a specific number associated with the referring doctor. If you do not have a modem, the referring doctor phone numbers are displayed for your reference. Labels > Envelope : Display the Output Options window. Select the output option and click OK . The Referring Doctor Envelope Setup window is displayed. Click OK . Labels > Label : Displays the Output Options window. Select the output option and click OK . The Referring Doctor Label Setup window is displayed. Click OK . Letter Expert : Displays the Letter Expert Merge window. Use this to merge the referring doctor information to a letter template. Contacts By Date : Displays the Contacts for window for the referring doctor by date. Use this to add, edit, or delete information for the referring doctor. You can also print contact information or print memos for the referring doctor. Contacts By Type : Displays the Contacts for window for the referring doctor by type. Use this to add, edit, or delete information for the referring doctor. You can also print contact information or print memos for the referring doctor. Incoming Referrals : Displays the Incoming Referrals window. Use this to view a list of patients referred to your office by the doctor. Outgoing Referrals : Displays the Outgoing Referrals window. Use this to view a list of patients referred to the doctor by your office. Documents : Displays the Documents for window. Use this to view existing documents or capture new documents to attach to the referring doctor. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Refreshing a Window
topics.body: Refreshing a Window Getting Started Refreshing a Window When running the software across a network, more than one user can access the same information or make changes to a record that is being viewed. To ensure the most current information is displayed, click Refresh on most toolbars to update data that is displayed in open windows. Refresh is not displayed on the toolbar for features and windows that permit only one user at a time to make changes. If you do not manually update the display, an automatic update of all visible data is performed at specified intervals. To set the refresh interval time: Select System > Startup Preferences . The Startup Preferences window is displayed. Type the refresh interval in the Refresh view every _ minutes field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Regenerating Automated Statements
topics.body: Regenerating Automated Statements eServices > Using the Automated Statements Service > Using Automated Statements > Additional Automated Statements Options Regenerating Automated Statements To regenerate Automated Statements for a specific day: Select Reports > Accounting > Statements > Electronic > Rerun Automated Statements . The Automated Statements window is displayed. In the Account Search section, type the start and ending account names for the statements you need to print. In the Date section, click the arrow button and use the calendar to select the date. In the Statement Message field, type a note, if necessary. Click OK . The Automated Statements List window is displayed. Select from the following options: To change the sort order: Click the column label. To exclude a statement: Deselect the checkbox in the Account Name column. To display additional information: Select the account. The information is displayed in the Additional Information section of the window. To include a statement note: Select the account and type the note in the Personalized Statement Note for Selected Account field. To edit the account information: Select the account and click Edit Acct . The Account window is displayed. Edit the record and click OK . To preview statements for specific accounts: Select the accounts and click Preview . To preview statements for the selected accounts: Click Preview All . Click Send . The Output Options - Electronic Batch Summary window is displayed. Select Printer and click OK . The Electronic Batch Summary report is printed, and a message, informing you the report is generated and asking you to transmit the statements, is displayed. Click Yes . To transmit the statements again, see Transmitting Automated Statements . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Logging and Removing Contacts
topics.body: Logging and Removing Contacts Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert > Handling Contact Expert Tasks > Accessing the Auto Dialer Logging and Removing Contacts To log contact information and remove a record from the list displayed in the Contact Expert window: In the Contact Expert window, select a record from the list in the left pane. Click Log Contact And Remove on the Log Contact tab. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Removing Office Expert Items
topics.body: Removing Office Expert Items Using Experts > Using the Office Expert > Setting Up Office Expert Items Removing Office Expert Items You can remove office expert items from the active list. If the security system is enabled, you must have the appropriate permission to perform this task. If the proper permissions are not set and you want to remove an office expert item, see your software administrator. To remove an office expert item: From the Experts menu, select Office Expert . The Office Expert window is displayed. Select Setup Experts from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Office Expert Setup window is displayed. Select the item to remove from the Active Office Expert Entries list. Click Remove Selected Item . The confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Removing Payer ID Numbers
topics.body: Removing Payer ID Numbers eServices > Using the eClaims Service > Setting Up Insurance Companies Removing Payer ID Numbers If you choose not to submit electronic or paper electronic claims after an insurance company has been set up, the payer ID must be removed. To remove a payer ID number: Select an option: In the eServices window : Click Insurance Setup . From the main menu : Select Setup > Insurance Co/Plans . The Payer Setup window is displayed, listing all insurance companies in the database that accept claims electronically. Click the + sign next to a payer to display the payers that are set up under that name. Select the company, and click Remove ID . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Account Budget Plan Report
topics.body: Running the Account Budget Plan Report Budget Planning > Running Budget Plan Reports > Running Account Budget Plan Reports Running the Account Budget Plan Report The Account Budget Plan report generates a list of fixed or revolving account budget plans. The report includes a summary of the outstanding budget plan balances, the number of payments due, and the average monthly income generated from budget plan payments. Sample Report To run the Account Budget Plan report: Select Reports > Accounting > Budget Plan Reports > Account . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Account Budget Plan Report Setup window is displayed. Select an option: To restrict the report to a specific account user code : Type it in the Include field. To exclude a specific account user code : Type it in the Exclude field. To restrict the report to a specific set of accounts : Select the starting and ending account numbers from the drop-down lists. To restrict the report to account fixed budget plans : Deselect Include Revolving Budget Plans . To restrict the report to account revolving budget plans : Deselect Include Fixed Budget Plans . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Account Reports
topics.body: Running Account Reports Setting Up Accounts Running Account Reports You can run reports that generate lists of each account associated with your practice by: Guarantor Name Identification number The account-related reports are available from the Reports menu. In addition to the reports provided with the software, you can run user-selected reports based on criteria that you define. You can run reports and labels that list accounts: Alphabetically By account ID By zip code You can also run reports to: See which accounts are insured See which accounts are uninsured Related Topics Running the Alphabetical Account List Report Running the Account List by ID Report Running the Account List by Zip Code Report Running the Insured Accounts Report Running the Uninsured Accounts Report Running the New Account List Report Printing Account Labels Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Add/Delete User Codes Utility
topics.body: Running the Add/Delete User Codes Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Add/Delete/Replace User Codes Utility This utility enables you to add, delete, or replace user codes for all your patients and accounts. Run this utility to remove a user code that you no longer use or to add a specific user code to all patients or accounts in your system. When you replace an existing code, all patient and account records containing the code are updated with the new code. To add or delete a user code: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Add/Delete User Codes . The Add or Delete User Codes window is displayed. Select an option: To add a code : Type the code in the Add field. To add the code to the patient database, select Patient Data . To add the code to the account database, select Account Data . Click OK . To delete a code : Type the code in the Delete field. To remove the code from the patient database, select Patient Data . To remove the code from the account database, select Account Data . Click OK . To replace a code: Type the code to replace in the Replace field and the new code in the with field. To update patient records containing the code, select Patients . To update account records containing the code, select Accounts . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . A message informs you that the selected operation is complete. Click Close . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Claim Reports
topics.body: Running Claim Reports Submitting and Tracking Claims Running Claim Reports The software offers several practice management reports that contain useful insurance claim information. Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports to run the following reports: Insurance Batch Preview Listing : Lists all the insurance claims in the batch file. Outstanding Claims By Company : Lists all outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical insurance claims sorted by insurance company. Outstanding Claims By Patient : Lists all outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical insurance claims sorted by patient name. Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Co.: Lists all outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical insurance claims with a benefit owed to the patient, sorted by insurance company. Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Pat. by Patient: Lists all outstanding primary and secondary dental and medical insurance claims with a benefit owed to the patient, sorted by patient name. Unsubmitted Claims : Lists all unsubmitted charges for patients sorted by their service dates. You can also print reports that list insurance companies and plans sorted alphabetically and by ID, and the Bluebook report. Related Topics Running the Insurance Batch Preview Report Running the Outstanding Claims by Company Report Running the Outstanding Claims by Patient Report Running the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Company Report Running the Outstanding Claims with Benefit to Patient by Patient Report Running the Unsubmitted Claims Report Need more information? topics.title: Running Customize Menu Utilities
topics.body: Running Customize Menu Utilities Utilities Running Customize Menu Utilities It is not recommended that you run SoftDent Utilities on a weekly, monthly, or yearly basis. You typically run utilities in case of database corruption, most commonly expressed as SDWin DBM Error, File No. ### Error = # . There might be other instances where utilities are used to resolve or troubleshoot an issue. You should run utilities only when you are instructed to do so by a SoftDent technical support representative. The Customize menu contains several utilities. Related Topics Running the Assign Hygienist ID to Patient Utility Running the Change All Account ICMs Utility Running the Modify Routing Slip Codes Utility Running the Define User Quick Letters Utility Running the Edit Recall Postcards Utility Running the Reset Schedule Colors Utility Running the Clear Scheduler Block Utility Running the Label Comp/Uncomp Utility Running the Capital Letter Format Utility Need more information? topics.title: Running Recall Reports
topics.body: Running Recall Reports Recalling Patients Running Recall Reports Recall reports generate lists of patients due for recall. Reports can be merged with letters, sent to the Contact Expert, and printed on labels or postcards. The following criteria determine the patients included in a Recall report: The date in the Next Recall due on or after: field in the Patient window must fall between the two dates specified in the Recall Report Setup window. Any patients with the user code N (Do not recall) are omitted. If the patient is included in the Recall report more times than allowed by the Maximum number of recall notices field in the Recall Settings window, the patient is not included in the Recall report. If the patient has an appointment scheduled for a recall code, the patient is not included in the Recall report. You can generate the Recall reports in several ways: Printing Recall Postcards Merging the Recall List Using Letter Expert Printing Recall Labels Running the Recall Report Running the Recall Report in Office Expert Related Topics Editing Recall Postcards Related Job Aid: Creating Recall Postcards Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Recall Report
topics.body: Running the Recall Report Recalling Patients > Running Recall Reports Running the Recall Report You can generate a list of patients due for recall. Sample Report To run the recall report: Select an option: To generate a list of patients who are due for normal recall : Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Last Exam . To generate a list of patients who are due for User-Defined recall : Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Perio . If you have not set up your Recall System or have upgraded from a previous version of the software and are running the first Recall report, the System Setting - PostCard Options window is displayed. Select an option and click OK . For more information, see Setting Up Recalls . If you change the name of your Normal Recall or User-Defined Recall system, the name of the report changes. For example, if the name of your User-Defined Recall system is Ortho, select Reports > Recall/Appt > Ortho . The Report Format window is displayed. Select Reports and click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an option: To print the report : Select Printer and click OK . To display the report : Select Print Preview and click OK . To save the report : Select File and click OK . To generate the recall list and merge the patient information with a Letter Expert template: Select Merge Letter Expert and click OK . To send the report to the Contact Expert: Select Contact Expert and click OK . To export the report data to Excel: Select Excel and click OK . The Recall Report Setup window is displayed. To change the report title, type a name in the Title field. In the Notices section, select an option: To update the patient record each time the patient is included in the Recall report: Select Update Notices Sent Count . Each time a patient is included in the Recall report, the Notices field on the Recall tab of the patient record is updated. This enables you to identify the number of notices sent to a patient. To indicate which patients should be included in the report: Select 0 Previous Notices to recall patients due to receive notice 1. Select 1 Previous Notice to recall patients due to receive notice 2. Select 2 Previous Notices to recall patients due to receive notice 3. Select 3 or more Previous Notices to recall patients who have already been sent 3 (or more) notices. Note : You can select more than one checkbox at a time. To recall patients who are due to receive their first, second, or third notice, select the 0 Previous Notices, 1 Previous Notice , and 2 Previous Notices checkboxes. In the Dates section, select a date range for the report. In the Patient Names section, select the start and stop last names from the drop-down lists to generate the recall list for a specific alphabetical range of patients. In the Providers section, select the provider identification number from the drop-down list to generate the recall list for a specific provider. To generate the recall list for all providers, select 999 from the drop-down list. To include only patients assigned to the doctor specified in this section : Select Search Patient�s Doctor . If you do not select this option, the report includes patients assigned to all providers. To include only patients assigned to a specific hygienist : Select the hygienist from the drop-down list, and select Search Patient�s Hygienist . In the Patient User Codes section, type the user codes for patients you want to include or exclude from the report. By default, user codes C (collections), I (inactive)

topics.title: Running Referral Reports
topics.body: Running Referral Reports Patient and Doctor Referrals Running Referral Reports Both general and practice management referral reports are available from the Reports menu. You can also run user-defined reports. The following reports are available by selecting Reports > Provider/Referring Doctor: Alphabetical Referring Doctor List: Generates an alphabetical list of incoming and outgoing referring doctors. Referring Doctor List by ID: Generates a list of incoming and outgoing referring doctors by identification number. Alphabetical Referring Doctor Labels: Generates alphabetically sorted labels for referring doctors. Referring Doctor by ID Labels: Generates labels for referring doctors sorted by identification number. The following reports are available by selecting Reports > Practice Management > Referral Reports: Professional Referral: Generates a list of incoming or outgoing referrals for a specific referring doctor and a specific date range. Patient Referrals By Name: Generates a list of referrals made for a specific patient and a specific date range. Who's Due Back: Generates a list of all patients your practice has referred to other providers. Referral Analysis: Generates a list of all providers who have referred patients to your practice. Referral Source Analysis: Generates a list of all providers belonging to a specific category who have referred patients to your practice. Related Topics Running the Alphabetical Referring Doctor List Report Running the Referring Doctor List by ID Report Running the Alphabetical Referring Doctor Labels Report Running the Referring Doctor by ID Labels Report Running the Professional Referrals Report Running the Patient Referrals by Name Report Running the Who's Due Back Referrals Report Running the Referral Production Analysis Report Running the Referral Source Analysis Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Pharmacy and Prescription Reports
topics.body: Running Pharmacy and Prescription Reports Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases Running Pharmacy and Prescription Reports Several reports that provide pharmacy and prescription information are available from the Reports menu. You can also run user-defined reports. You can run the following pharmacy- and prescription-related reports: Alphabetical Drugs: Generates an alphabetical list of prescription drugs. Drugs By ID: Generates a list of prescription drugs sorted by identification number. Patient Prescriptions: Generates a list of prescription drugs issued during a specific date range. Alphabetical Pharmacies: Generates an alphabetical list of pharmacies. Pharmacies by ID: Generates a list of pharmacies sorted by identification number. You can run the following prescription audit trail reports: Prescription Audit Trail By Date: Generates an audit trail report for every prescription issued within a specific date range. Prescription Audit Trail By User: Generates an audit trail report for every prescription drug entered by a specific user. Prescription Audit Trail By Patient Name: Generates an audit trail report for every prescription issued for a specific patient. Prescription Audit Trail By Doctor: Generates an audit trail report for every prescription issued by a specific provider. Prescription Audit Trail By Drug Name: Generates an audit trail report for every prescription issued for a specific drug. Related Topics Running the Alphabetical Drugs Report Running the Drugs by ID Report Running the Patient Prescriptions Report Running the Alphabetical Pharmacies Report Running the Pharmacies by ID Report Running the Prescription Audit Trail By Date Report Running the Prescription Audit Trail By User Report Running the Prescription Audit Trail By Patient Name Report Running the Prescription Audit Trail By Doctor Report Running the Prescription Audit Trail By Drug Name Report Need more information?
