topics.title: Setting Routing Slip Preferences
topics.body: Setting Routing Slip Preferences Setting Routing Slip Preferences To set routing slip preferences: Select System > Change System Settings > General System . The General System Settings window is displayed. Click Printer Preferences . The System Printing Preferences window is displayed. Select from the following options: To use a provider�s customized routing slip, select Use provider preferred routing slips . See Running the Modify Routing Slip Codes Utility for more information on creating a customized routing slip. To print treatment plan codes on routing slips if the patient has treatment plan procedures, select Include Treatment Plan . To print last exam information in the Patient Information section of the routing slip, select Include Exam History . To print patient referral information on routing slips, select Include Patient Referrals and Acct Aging . Click OK . The General System Settings window is displayed. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reports Menu Commands
topics.body: Reports Menu Commands Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Main Menu Bar Reports Menu Commands U se the Reports menu to view and set up reports. The Reports menu commands are: Daily Op Schedule : Print, review, or save the Daily Op Schedule report. Missed Appointments : Print, review, or save the Missed Appointments report. You can also merge the report with a Letter Expert template or send it to the Contact Expert. Confirmation List : Print, review, or save the Confirmation List report. You can also merge the report with a Letter Expert template or send it to the Contact Expert. End of Day Call Backs : Print, review, or save the End of Day Call Backs report. You can also merge the report with a Letter Expert template or send it to the Contact Expert. Daysheet : Print, review, or save the Daysheet report. You can also view or print the report in a Microsoft Excel software spreadsheet. Daily Register : Print, review, or save the Daily Register report. Accounting : Print, review, or save account reports. Patient : Print, review, or save patient reports. Account : Print, review, or save account reports. Recall/Appt : Print, review, or save recall and appointment reports. Provider/Referring Dr : Print, review, or save provider and referring doctor reports. Insurance : Print, review, or save insurance reports. Trojan Plan Reports : Print, review, or save traditional or managed care reports. ADA/Trans/Diag codes : Print, review, or save code reports. Rx/Pharmacies/Labs : Print, review, or save prescription and lab reports. Practice Management : Print, review, or save practice management reports. Report Manager : Set up report groups, run scheduled reports, and configure report advanced options. Practice/Custom Labels : Print practice labels and create custom labels. Print Laser Labels : Print labels and set printer offsets. Print Laser Postcards : Print postcards and set printer offsets. Audit Trail : Print, review, or save audit trail reports. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reprinting Batch Files
topics.body: Reprinting Batch Files Submitting and Tracking Claims > Printing Claims Reprinting Batch Files To reprint batched files: Select Insurance > Restart Batch Print . The Reprint Insurance Batch Run Setup window is displayed. Type the insurance form number, the insurance company, and claim number in the fields. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reprinting Settlement Reports
topics.body: Reprinting Settlement Reports eServices > Using the ePayments Service > Printing Settlement Reports Reprinting Settlement Reports To reprint a settlement report: Select Reports > Accounting > Reprint Credit Card Settlement . Select a settlement date and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Find/Repair Corrupt Utility
topics.body: Running the Find/Repair Corrupt Utility Utilities > Using File Menu Utilities Running the Find/Repair Corrupt Utility Run this utility t o locate, repair, and reindex corrupted data records. Run the Find Largest ID utility before repairing corrupt records. Do not run this utility unless you are instructed to by a technical support representative. To find and repair corrupt files: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select File > Find/Repair Corrupt . The Find/Repair Corrupt Records window is displayed. Select an option: To find and correct a specific corrupted file : Select it from the list. To run the corruption utility for all files : Click Select All . Click OK . A message asks you to run the utility. Click Yes . A message asks you to pause between files. Select an option: To pause between files : Click Yes . After each file is checked, a window that details the number of records, number of corrupt records, the number of corrected records, and the number of deleted files is displayed. Click OK to check the next file. To run the utility without pausing between files : Click No . The files are checked, and corrupt files are corrected. A message informs you that the utility is finished. Information on corrupt data found by this utility is stored in the SDUTILS.LOG file. Click OK . Click Close . When the technical support representative tells you to, restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reprinting a Claim
topics.body: Reprinting a Claim Submitting and Tracking Claims > Printing Claims Reprinting a Claim To reprint a claim: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Select the patient and click Transactions on the toolbar. The patient�s Transactions window is displayed. Click Outstanding Ins on the toolbar. All outstanding claims associated with the patient are displayed in the Claim List window. Select a claim, and click View Claim on the toolbar. The Claim window is displayed. Click Print copy on the toolbar to reprint a copy of the claim without updating the date on which it was last sent. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reprinting Receipts
topics.body: Reprinting Receipts eServices > Using the ePayments Service > Setting Options for Printing Receipts Reprinting Receipts To reprint a receipt, from the patient Transactions List window: Select the transaction. Select Credit Card Receipt from the Options section of the Express bar. The receipt is printed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Report Menu Utilities
topics.body: Using Report Menu Utilities Utilities Using Report Menu Utilities It is not recommended that you run SoftDent Utilities on a weekly, monthly, or yearly basis. You typically run utilities in case of database corruption, most commonly expressed as SDWin DBM Error, File No. ### Error = # . There might be other instances where utilities are used to resolve or troubleshoot an issue. You should run utilities only when you are instructed to do so by a SoftDent technical support representative. The following Report menu utilities are available: SoftDent Error Log File Utility Error Log File Related Topics Running the SoftDent Error Log File Utility Running the Utility Error Log File Utility Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resubmitting Claims by Age
topics.body: Resubmitting Claims by Age Submitting and Tracking Claims > Resubmitting and Deleting Claims Resubmitting Claims by Age You can automatically resubmit any outstanding claims that were last submitted within a certain date range without the need for resubmitting claims one at a time. Using the Resubmitting Claims by Age function updates the Last Sent field of the claim status window. To resubmit claims by age: Select Insurance > Resubmit or Delete by Age . The Resubmit/Delete Old Insurance Claims window is displayed. Select Paper , ECS only , or All . Select the first or last date on which the claim or electronic claim was submitted. The Process All Claims With field is typically set to First Sent Date. To set the date range, select an option: Select Older than __ days : Enter the number of days (Older than __ days) or a cutoff date (Prior to __). Select In Date Range : Enter the beginning and ending dates. If the last date or first date on which the claim was sent is within the date range, the claim is resubmitted. Click OK . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . A message is displayed listing the number of records that have been processed. Click OK . A message is displayed, asking if you want to print the log file. Click Yes . The Action Options window is displayed. Select Print and click OK . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Resubmitting and Deleting Claims
topics.body: Resubmitting and Deleting Claims Submitting and Tracking Claims Resubmitting and Deleting Claims You can resubmit or delete the following claims: Electronic Paper You can select the type of claims you want to resubmit or delete. Related Topics Cancelling and Resubmitting an Insurance Claim Resubmitting Claims by Age Deleting Claims by Age Canceling a Claim Resubmitting a Previously Printed Claim Electronically Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Rescheduling Appointments
topics.body: Rescheduling Appointments Scheduling Appointments Rescheduling Appointments When you cancel an appointment: The information is temporarily stored. If you reschedule the appointment immediately, you are not required to re-enter the information. If you do not reschedule the appointment immediately, you can store the appointment information by placing it in the Tickler file. Related Topics Rescheduling Using Drag and Drop Canceling and Rescheduling Appointments Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Rescheduling Using Drag and Drop
topics.body: Rescheduling Using Drag and Drop Scheduling Appointments > Rescheduling Appointments Rescheduling Using Drag and Drop The quickest way to reschedule an appointment is to drag it to the appropriate location in the scheduler window. A warning is displayed if there is a conflict. If there are not enough slots to accommodate the appointment, an overlap error message is displayed, and the appointment is moved to the overlap bin. If you close the scheduler window before rescheduling a docked appointment, a message that asks you to add the appointment to the Tickler file is displayed. To drag and drop a patient appointment, select an option: To drag and drop a patient appointment to another time on the same day : In the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows, click and hold the left mouse button on the appointment, and drag it to the new time slot. T o drag and drop a patient appointment to another date and time : In the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows, click and hold the left mouse button on the appointment, and drag it to the far right or left side of the window to dock it. Locate the new date and time, then click and drag the docked appointment to the time slot. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Pausing and Resuming Auto Advance
topics.body: Pausing and Resuming Auto Advance Periodontal Charting > Using Auto Advance for Perio Charting Pausing and Resuming Auto Advance When using Auto Advance for periodontal charting: The advance feature is temporarily paused when you move to another pocket. To return to the pocket and tooth in the normal auto sequence and continue using the feature, click Resume Perio on the Probe toolbar . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Expected Response Contact Report
topics.body: Running the Expected Response Contact Report Contacts and Memos > Using Contacts > Running Contact Reports Running the Expected Response Contact Report The Expected Response Contact report generates a list of all contacts from whom you are awaiting a response. You can run this report for: Patients Accounts Insurance plans Insurance companies Employers Referring doctors Providers Pharmacies Claims The report includes the following information for each contact listed in the report: Identification number Name Address Phone numbers Date on which the contact was made Name of the user who made the contact Contact method Expected response date Sample Report To run the Expected Response Contact report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Contact Reports > Expected Response . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option, and click OK . The Contacts - No Response Received Report Setup window is displayed. Select the type of contact from the drop-down list. Select the dates between which contact is expected from the appropriate drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Full Resource Scheduling
topics.body: Using Full Resource Scheduling Scheduling Appointments Using Full Resource Scheduling Full-Resource Scheduling: Lets you schedule providers for individual time slots, instead of entire appointments. Enables you to allocate the necessary providers for an appointment and minimize scheduling conflicts. Example : For a routine exam, you might assign the hygienist for the first and second time slots and the dentist for the third. You can do this by manually entering the proper codes into the Assignment field in the Appointment Card window while making the appointment, but it can be done automatically by setting up a scheduler template for each procedure. Related Topics Setting Up a Scheduler Template Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Reset Holiday.Def File Utility
topics.body: Running the Reset Holiday.Def File Utility Utilities > Using File Menu Utilities Running the Reset Holiday.Def File Utility Run this utility to r eset the holiday.def file and clear any dates marked as annual holidays. To reset system holidays: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select File > Reset Holiday.Def File . A message informs you that running the utility will update the file to your current scheduler settings. Click OK . The holidays are reset. A message informs you to reset annual holidays. Click OK and exit the software. Select C:\SofDent\resethol.txt . This file contains a list of annual holidays. For example, you must reset Thanksgiving as it does not fall on the same date each year. Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Restorative Charting
topics.body: Restorative Charting Restorative Charting Using the charting module, you can chart the following from the Restorative tab: Restorative procedures Tooth conditions Treatment plans Procedures charted and posted on this tab are updated in the patient's Transactions and Treatment Plan windows. Teeth charted as missing are also marked as missing in the application. Tooth conditions, such as decay or diastema, are not posted. When you open a chart, all chartable transactions posted in the software are displayed. Related Topics Setting Charting Options and Properties Understanding Restorative Tab Modes Charting ADA Procedures Using Clinical Conditions Handling Mixed Dentition Removing a Chart Graphic and Re-Charting a Procedure Deleting Unposted Procedures Using Restorative Tab Toolbars Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Restorative Tab Modes
topics.body: Understanding Restorative Tab Modes Restorative Charting Understanding Restorative Tab Modes When the Restorative tab is the active window, you can work in the following modes: Initial chart Current chart Treatment plan primary Treatment plan secondary Treatment plan tertiary Complete primary treatment plan items Complete secondary treatment plan items Complete tertiary treatment plan items The various modes enable you to chart patient conditions or completed work. Related Topics Using the Initial Chart Using the Current Chart Using Inside Work Mode Using Outside Work Mode Using the Treatment Plan Modes Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Real Time Claim Submission Service
topics.body: Using the Real Time Claim Submission Service eServices > Using the eClaims Service Using the Real Time Claim Submission Service The Real Time Claim Submission service enables you to instantaneously transmit and adjudicate claims with participating insurance companies. You must be enrolled in eClaims and have an Internet connection in order to use the Real Time Claim Submission service. To enroll in eClaims, contact your sales representative. Before using the Real Time Claim Submission service, select the Enable Real Time Claims checkbox in the Insurance Settings window. See Setting Insurance Defaults for more information. To use the real time claim submission service: Select Insurance > eServices . The eServices window is displayed. Click Load . The Claim Submission List window is displayed with real time claims highlighted in blue. Select a claim highlighted in blue (the highlight color changes to light blue), and click Send Selected Claim , or click Send RT Claim on the menu at the right. The claim status changes from Waiting validation to Provider Info OK if the claim passes validation, and the status bar at the bottom of the window displays a Transmitting Claim message. When the claim has been successfully transmitted, the payer sends a Claim Status response. The Claim Status response is saved to the patient's Documents window. Click the printer icon in the upper-left corner of the window to print the response. Select the next real time claim and repeat steps 3 and 4. Related Topics Sending All Real Time Claims at One Time Submitting Claims Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting Returned Checks
topics.body: Posting Returned Checks Posting Payments > Posting Account Payments Posting Returned Checks If a check posted to an account is returned due to insufficient funds, you can post the returned check and any fees. To post a returned check: To post a returned check from the Payments window, select Adjustment in the Select Operation section. The Payments - Make an Adjustment window is displayed. 0 is displayed in the Code drop-down list. Select an adjustment to collections code (codes 30.00 � 39.89) to increase the account�s balance and decrease the provider collections for the amount of the returned check credit from the Code drop-down list. You can also post a charge for the returned check (codes 61.00 � 90.89). Type the check amount in the Amount column for the transaction. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction, and type the information. Click Post . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Returning Patients to the Waiting Room
topics.body: Returning Patients to the Waiting Room Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Returning Patients to the Waiting Room After the provider has completed the treatment and the patient is returned to the waiting room: Select the appointment. Select Change Location from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. W is displayed in the Loc column, the amount of time that the patient was in the operatory is displayed in the Op Time column, and the text for the patient appointment is displayed in blue. After the provider or front office employee selects Complete from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu in the ELECTRONIC ROUTER window, Complete is displayed in the Code column, and the text in each column of the row that contains the patient appointment information is displayed in red to indicate that the patient can now be checked out. If the appointment card is posted from the charting module, Posted is displayed in the Code column. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Modify Routing Slip Codes Utility
topics.body: Running the Modify Routing Slip Codes Utility Utilities > Running Customize Menu Utilities Running the Modify Routing Slip Codes Utility Run the Modify Routing Slips utility to add procedural codes that can be printed on provider preferred and office routing slips. To customize routing slips: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Customize > Modify Routing Slip Codes . The Routing Slip Code Editor window is displayed. Select the routing slip to edit from the Office Routing Slip drop-down list. Select from the following options: To customize a provider�s preferred routing slip, select Use provider preferred routing slips and select the provider for whom routing slips are being customized from the Customizing Routing Slip for Provider drop-down list. To print treatment plan codes on routing slips, select Include Treatment Plan . To print last exam information in the Patient Information section, select Include Exam History . To print patient referral information, select Include Patient Referrals and Acct Aging . To add a new code, click Insert . The Edit Routing Slip Code window is displayed. Select the code to add, type a description and press Tab. Click OK . To edit a code, click Edit . The Edit Routing Slip Code window is displayed. Type a description, select or deselect Include Tooth and Include Surface , and press Tab. Click OK . To delete a code, click Delete . Click Save . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Routing Slips By Date Report
topics.body: Running the Routing Slips By Date Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports Running the Routing Slips By Date Report This option prints routing slips for all patients scheduled on a specified date. You can also print routing slips for a specific doctor's patients on a given date. Sample Report To run the Routing Slips By Date report: Select Reports > Recall/Appts > Routing Slips By Date . The Routing Slip Setup window is displayed. In the Date section, click the drop-down arrow to select the date from a calendar. In the Providers section, click the drop-down arrow to select the doctors from a list, or leave 999 to report on all doctors. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the RTU Production Report
topics.body: Running the RTU Production Report Billing and Receivables > Using Billing and Receivables Reports > Running Production Reports Running the RTU Production Report The RTU Production report generates a list of production codes and the associated RTU minutes posted within a specified date range. Information on this report includes code number, description, and RTUs, as well as the numbers performed by each provider and by all providers in the report. The report also includes practice and individual provider RTU totals and percentages. Sample Report To generate the RTU Production report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Production Reports > RTU Production . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The RTU Production/ Transactions by Code Report window is displayed. Select the transaction date range from the drop-down lists. To run the report for a single date, type the same date in the Starting and Ending fields. Select the code range from the drop-down lists. To run the report for a single code, type the same code in both fields. Type the patient user codes to include or exclude. To include inactive patients in the report, select Include Inactive Patients . Select the identification numbers for the providers to include in the report results from the Doctors section. Type 999 in the first field to include payment allocation information for all providers. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Rights by User Window
topics.body: Using the Rights by User Window System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Using the Rights by User Window In the Rights by User window, you can: Assign security rights that enable each user to perform specific tasks within the software Create and modify user profiles that contain a set of security rights for a specific job description Enable Mobility ( Note : This option is available only to SoftDent Cloud users.) The following buttons are displayed at the bottom of the window: Select All Select from Template OK Cancel To assign or restrict rights for a user: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Select the user name and click Edit . The Rights By User window is displayed. In the User Rights Selection section, select an option: To restrict the user�s access : Select the appropriate category from the left side of the window, and select the checkbox next to each task the user can perform in the right side of the window. To give the user rights to perform all the tasks : Click Select All . To copy the task permissions from a previously created template : Click Select from Template . The User Template List window is displayed. Double-click the template. The Rights By User window is displayed. For a complete list of tasks by category, see Tasks with Limited Access . Click OK . Related Topics Users By Rights Window Tasks with Limited Access Rights by User List Window Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: System Security and HIPAA Compliance - Running HIPAA Reports
topics.body: System Security and HIPAA Compliance - Running HIPAA Reports System Security and HIPAA Compliance Running HIPAA Reports After informing the patient, you can add HIPAA acknowledgement, consent, authorization, disclosure, and complaint contacts that enable you to track and report this information. You can run the following reports: HIPAA Acknowledgement Received HIPAA Acknowledgement Outstanding Related Topics Running the HIPAA Acknowledgement Received Report Running the HIPAA Acknowledgement Outstanding Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Insurance Reports
topics.body: Running Insurance Reports Setting Up Insurance Running Insurance Reports You can generate several reports that provide useful information about the following areas of your practice: Insurance companies Insurance plans Insurance practice management Related Topics Running Insurance Company Reports Running Insurance Plan Reports Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Running the Bluebook Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running CareCredit Reports
topics.body: Running CareCredit Reports Using CareCredit Running CareCredit Reports There are two CareCredit-specific practice management reports you can run to track CareCredit transactions in your practice. The available reports include: CareCredit Transactions for a Period Treatment Plans for CareCredit Patients In addition, the Daysheet now includes a separate page for CareCredit transactions, and the deposit slip also has a separate CareCredit page. Related Topics Running the CareCredit Transactions for a Period Report Running the Treatment Plans for CareCredit Patients Report Running the Daysheet Report Running the Deposit Slip Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running eReminders Reports
topics.body: Running eReminders Reports eServices > Using the eReminders Service Running eReminders Reports The eReminders service contains two reports that enable you to monitor your practice's eReminders activity: eReminders report: Lists the reminders sent to patients, as well as additional details about each patient's appointment. Revenue Analysis report: Displays the number of missed appointments, the percentage of total appointments, and the lost revenue for a specified date range. Important: The Save appointment(s) to history when transaction is posted option in the Scheduler Settings window ( System > Change System Settings > Scheduler ) must be selected in order to use the Revenue Analysis report. You must also run the Missed Appointments Report at the end of each day (or the beginning of the next day) in order to commit any posted appointments to history. You can quickly access reports with pre-defined eReminders criteria in Practice Central. Click the Delivery report or No Confirmation report links under the eReminders section. To run eReminders reports: Select System > Modules > eReminders > eReminder Reports . The eReminders Reporting window is displayed. For additional information, see the eReminders online help by clicking the ? button or pressing F1 in an eReminders window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Overdue Reports
topics.body: Running Overdue Reports Handling Reports > Generating Reports Using Report Manager Running Overdue Reports If the system date is later than the next run date for a scheduled report, a message is displayed when you enter and exit the software, prompting you to run any overdue reports or a report scheduled for the current date. If you click Yes , another window is displayed, prompting you for the run date for the overdue reports. This date defaults to yesterday�s date. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Patient Information Audit Trail Reports
topics.body: Running Patient Information Audit Trail Reports System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails Running Patient Information Audit Trail Reports You can generate the Patient Information Audit Trail reports by: Date User The Patient Information Audit Trail reports generate lists of changes to patient demographic data (excluding changes to clinical data). The following information is included in all reports: Date Time Name of the user who deleted the demographic data Task description Related Topics Running the Patient Information Audit Trail By Date Report Running the Patient Information Audit Trail By User Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Scheduled Reports
topics.body: Running Scheduled Reports Handling Reports > Generating Reports Using Report Manager Running Scheduled Reports In addition to being prompted to run scheduled reports when entering and exiting the software, you can print the reports on demand or print only specific reports or report groups. To run reports for the current date, select Reports > Report Manager > Run Scheduled Reports . The software displays a window, asking if you want to print today�s scheduled reports. Click Yes . To run a report group regardless of the next run date, select Reports > Report Manager > Advanced Options . The Report Group window is displayed. Select a report group and click Run Now . To run a specific report regardless of the next run date, select Reports > Report Manager > Advanced Options . The Report Group window is displayed. Select the report group and click Edit Reports . The Report Group Members window is displayed. Select a report and click Run Now . Reports that are run as part of a batch are still subject to system security. A user must have security rights specified in the System Security window to run a report group containing the daysheet or a report containing financial information. Overdue Reports: If the system date is later than the next run date for a scheduled report, a message is displayed when you enter and exit the software, prompting you to run any overdue reports or a report scheduled for the current date. If you select Yes, another window is displayed, prompting you for the run date for the overdue reports. This date defaults to yesterday's date. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the CareCredit Transactions for a Period Report
topics.body: Running the CareCredit Transactions for a Period Report Using CareCredit > Running CareCredit Reports Running the CareCredit Transactions for a Period Report This report details all CareCredit transactions posted during a specific period and summarizes transactions by provider. You also can find out how many adjustments were made in a month, year, and so on. You can select individual patient details/summaries and totals. To run the CareCredit Transactions for a Period report: Select Reports > Practice Management > CareCredit Reports > Transactions for a Period . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output type and click OK . The Transactions for a Period Report window is displayed. In the Dates section, select the start and end date for the report. In the Format section, type a format for the report. 1. List Each Transaction Separately�Lists every transaction posted during the specified date range. 2. Summarize Patient Transactions�Lists the patient only once for the day and groups the dollar amounts by production, charges, and so on. 3. Only List Totals�Lists only the totals for the specified date range, and groups the totals by production, charges, and so on. 4. Group by Payment Plan�Lists every transaction grouped according to payment plan. Select the Include Indirect Sources for Doctor (if Applicable) option to have the report show all of the production dollars for the specified doctor(s). More This means that the indirect production will be rolled over into the doctor�s production. If you do not select the option, only the production done solely by the doctor(s) is shown. Select the Include Inactive Accounts/Patients option to include inactive accounts or patients in the report. In the Code Types section, set the type of transactions to include in the report. In the Accounts With User Codes section, type the user codes for the accounts you want to include or exclude. In the Patients With User Codes section, type the user codes for the patients you want to include or exclude. In the Transactions section, select whether to include CareCredit payments, CareCredit refunds, or both. In the Doctors section, click the drop-down arrow to select the doctors for the report, or leave the default of 999 to report on all doctors. Deselect the Include Color/Shading checkbox if you do not want shading to display on the report. Click OK . Additional Details: The first section of the report lists transactions within the date range and whether they were CareCredit charges or adjustments. Report Details: Date�Date of transaction. ID and Name�Patient or account ID and name. Transactions posted to the account will have the account's first name. D$�The collecting provider. Dr�The service provider. Code�The posted transactions code. Prod�Dollar amount of production codes posted (codes 100.00+). Charges�Dollar amount of charges posted (codes 61.00-95.89, 97.00-97.89, 99.00-99.89). Prod Adj�Dollar amount of production adjustments posted (codes 40.00-60.00). Cash�Dollar amount of cash payments (codes 1.00-1.89). Check�Dollar amount of check payments (codes 2.00-10.89). Credit�Dollar amount of credit card payments (codes 11.00-20.89). Pay Adj�Dollar amount of collection adjustments posted (codes 21.00-39.89, 96.00-96.89, 98.00-98.89). The provider summary portion of the report lists all specified providers with transactions for the date range, which are classified as collections and collections adjustments. Report Details: Dr�The doctor who col

topics.title: Running the Fix Patient Insurance Link Utility
topics.body: Running the Fix Patient Insurance Link Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Fix Patient Insurance Link Utility Run this utility to ensure that all patients linked to a particular insurance plan are displayed in the Patients Insured By List window. To view the Patients Insured By List window, click Show Insured Patients in the Insurance Plan or Insurance co. List window. To fix patient and insurance links: Instruct the users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Fix Patient Insurance Link . The Repair Patient Insurance Links window is displayed. After the process is complete, a message is displayed. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Fix Phone Records Utility
topics.body: Running the Fix Phone Records Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Fix Phone Records Utility Run this utility to repair phone number information associated with practice, account, patient, dentist, employer, insurance company, insurance plan, pharmacy, referring doctor, and other facility records. To correct phone number information: Instruct the users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Fix Phone Records . The Repair Phone Records window is displayed. After the process is complete, a message is displayed. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Medicaid Encounter Report Utility
topics.body: Running the Medicaid Encounter Report Utility Running the Medicaid Encounter Report Utility The Medicaid Encounter Report utility measures the percentage of your office's patients that were Medicaid patients over a specified period. The number of Unique Medicaid Encounters and the number of Total Unique Encounters are also included. To run the Medicaid Encounter Report utility: On the server, navigate to and open the SoftDent folder, and double-click MedicaidEncounter.exe . The Medicaid Encounter Report Criteria window is displayed. Select a start and end date. Type the provider ID, or type 0 to include all providers. Click Run . A prompt asking if you want to run the Medicaid Encounter Report is displayed. Click Yes . A message stating that the process has been successfully completed is displayed. Click OK . Click View Results . The results display in a Notepad window. To continue using the Medicaid Encounter Report utility, close the Notepad window, or the utility will appear to be non-responsive. Click Exit and select Yes to exit the utility. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report
topics.body: Running the Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Running the Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report The Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report lists Medicaid services provided during a given time period, as required by some states' Medicaid offices. To run the Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Setup window is displayed. In the Date Range section, click the drop-down arrows to select the date of service range from a calendar. In the Plan ID section, click the drop-down arrows to select the insurance plan. Do not leave this field as zero. You must select a Medicaid insurance plan. In the Doctors section, use the drop-down arrows to select specific providers, or leave 999 to report on all providers. Click OK . Additional Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report Information : This report will not run unless you specify a valid Medicaid insurance plan ID. Information on this report includes: Billing Provider NPI -- the practice's National Provider ID Billing Provider Medicaid ID -- the practice's state Medicaid ID Rendering Provider Medicaid ID -- the claim's treating doctor's state ID Member First Name -- the patient's first name Member Last Name -- the patient's last name MCO Member ID -- the patient's Medicaid ID Name of MCO Billed -- the insurance company name Date of Service -- the claim's date of service Procedure Code -- displays T1015, the HCPCS clinic visit/encounter all-inclusive code Date Payment Received from MCO -- the date on which the claim has been paid MCO Paid Amount -- the claim's paid amount Other Primary Insurance Paid Amount -- the amount paid by a primary insurance carrier The total of all MCO Paid Amounts and Other Primary Insurance Paid Amounts is also given. Sample Report : Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient Information Audit Trail By Date Report
topics.body: Running the Patient Information Audit Trail By Date Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Patient Information Audit Trail Reports Running the Patient Information Audit Trail By Date Report The Patient Information Audit Trail By Date Report generates a list of every change to patient demographic data within a specified date range. The report records the date, time, user name, and description of the change. Sample Report To generate a list of changes made to patient demographic data for a specific period: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Patient Information Audit Trail > By Date . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Patient Information Audit Trail By Date window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient Information Audit Trail By User Report
topics.body: Running the Patient Information Audit Trail By User Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Patient Information Audit Trail Reports Running the Patient Information Audit Trail By User Report The Patient Information Audit Trail By User Report generates a list of every change to patient demographic data made by a specific user. The report records the date, time, user name, and description of the change. Sample Report To generate a list of changes made to patient demographic data for a specific user: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Patient Information Audit Trail > By User . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Patient Information Audit Trail By User window is displayed. Type the starting and ending user names in the appropriate fields. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Patient Last Visit Report
topics.body: Running the Patient Last Visit Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Patient Last Visit Report The Patient Last Visit report generates a list of patients with a last visit date within a date range. Sample Report To generate a list of patients� last visits: Select Reports > Patient > Last Visit . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patient Last Visit List Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending last visit dates from the drop-down lists. To restrict the report to a specific provider, type the provider identification number in the Provider field. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Recover From Faircom Crash Utility
topics.body: Running the Recover From Faircom Crash Utility Utilities > Using File Menu Utilities Running the Recover From Faircom Crash Utility Run this utility when you are using the Client/Server version of SoftDent and have a Faircom crash. This utility combines the actions of the Rebuild/Reindex, Remake Scheduler File, and Scheduler Repair utilities. Be certain you want to run this utility. When you select this option, the utility starts immediately. To recover from a Faircom crash: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select File > Recover From Faircom Crash . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Set Up Account Automated Statements Service Options Utility
topics.body: Running the Set Up Account Automated Statements Service Options Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Set Up Account Automated Statements Service Options Utility This utility has two options. The first option enables you to quickly set up your practice to send statements online instead of by mail. The second option turns off the "By Mail" checkbox on the Account window, enabling you to quickly set up your practice to no longer send statements by mail to those accounts who are already set up to receive them online. To set up account Automated Statements options: Ensure that you have a valid backup of your data files. Instruct the users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select 2 Set Up Account Automated Statements Options . The Set Up Account Automated Statements Service Options window is displayed. Select an option: To set up accounts to receive statements online: Select Check the "Online" option for any account whose Guarantor 1 has an e-mail address . To set up accounts to no longer receive automated statements by mail: Select Uncheck the "By Mail" option for any account whose "Online" option of the "Automated Statements Service" options is checked . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Treatment Plans for CareCredit Patients Report
topics.body: Running the Treatment Plans for CareCredit Patients Report Using CareCredit > Running CareCredit Reports Running the Treatment Plans for CareCredit Patients Report The Treatment Plans for CareCredit Patients report generates a breakdown of treatment plans for your CareCredit patients. This report lists all patients with a treatment plan who have at one time used CareCredit. To generate a breakdown of treatment plan information for CareCredit patients: Select Reports > Practice Management > CareCredit Reports > Treatment Plans. The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Treatment Plans for CareCredit Patients Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending date range from a calendar. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code: Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code: Type the code in the Exclude field. To include inactive patients in the report: Click the Include Inactive Patients checkbox. To restrict the report to a specific provider: Select the provider identification number from the Doctors section. Leave 999 to report on all providers. To include information for a specific plan in the report results: Type the information in the Options section. To restrict the report to a specific procedural code range: Select the codes from the fields in the Codes section. Note : To specify a single code, type it in the left Codes column and type 0 in the right field. Type the number that corresponds to the appropriate report format in the Report Format section. Click OK . Additional Treatment Plans for CareCredit Patients Report Information: There are five formats for this report: List All Patient Treatment Plans�Lists all patient treatment plans. Under the patient name, the treatment plan items are sorted/listed by group number. For each patient, the total dollar value is listed for each treatment plan group, as well as total dollar value for the group�s estimated insurance. This format shows if the group is accepted/unaccepted, scheduled/not scheduled, and the appointment date and time if scheduled. Unaccepted Treatment Plans Only�Lists only the patients with unaccepted treatment plans. Accepted But Not Scheduled Treatment Plans�Lists only the patients with accepted treatment plans who have not been scheduled. All Not Scheduled And Not In Tickler�Lists only the patients with treatment plans who have not been scheduled or placed in the tickler file. All Scheduled Treatment Plans�Lists only the patients with scheduled treatment plans. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running User-Selected Reports
topics.body: Running User-Selected Reports Handling Reports > Generating User-Selected Reports Running User-Selected Reports To run a user-selected report that has previously been created: Select Reports > Patient > User-Selected or Reports > Account > User-Selected . The User-Selected list window is displayed. Select the report you want to print and click Run/select . The User-Selected report title window is displayed. Change the title of the report, if necessary, and click OK . The Selection Criteria window is displayed. Verify the selected categories on the Info and Trans/Category tabs, and click OK . The software prompts you to save the report setup changes. Click Yes . A message is displayed, indicating the number of matches that were found. Click OK . The software prompts you to print the report. Click Yes . The Output Options window is displayed. Make a selection and click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reviewing Claims
topics.body: Reviewing Claims Submitting and Tracking Claims > Reviewing and Tracking Claims Reviewing Claims You can display any claim record using this method: Select Insurance > Review Claim . The Find Claim window is displayed. Select a record and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reviewing Generic and Default Clinical Notes
topics.body: Reviewing Generic and Default Clinical Notes Working with Clinical Notes Reviewing Generic and Default Clinical Notes To view generic or default clinical notes: Select an option: To view all of the generic and default clinical notes associated with a provider in the order in which they were created : Select Clinical Notes > Review > Provider Notes by Date . The Provider Clinical Notes by Date List window is displayed. In this window, you can add and amend clinical notes. You can also view the note history and images associated with the notes. To view all of the generic and default clinical notes associated with a provider in the order of patient identification number : Select Clinical Notes > Review > Provider Notes by Patient . The Provider Clinical Notes by Patient List window is displayed. In this window, you can amend clinical notes. You can also view the note history and images associated with the notes. To view all of the generic and default clinical notes associated with an ADA procedure code : Select Clinical Notes > Review > Notes by ADA Code . The Clinical Notes by ADA Code window is displayed. In this window, you can amend clinical notes. You can also view the note history and images associated with the notes. Generic notes are displayed in blue text, and Y is displayed in the Generic column. Default notes are displayed in orange text, and a Y is displayed in the Default column. To view the images associated with a note, select it and click View Images . The Images for Note window is displayed. After you have reviewed the images, click Close . To amend the notes, see Amending Patient Clinical Notes . To print clinical notes, see Printing Clinical Notes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Reviewing Documents
topics.body: Reviewing Documents Using the Document Manager Reviewing Documents You can assign account, patient, referring doctor, claim, ePostings, insurance company, insurance plan, and lab case documents to another user to review. topics.body: Reviewing Information in the Carestream Dental Institute Finding Answers > Accessing the Carestream Dental Institute Reviewing Information in the Carestream Dental Institute To review information in the Carestream Dental Institute: Log in to the Carestream Dental Institute . Click Catalog . The Catalog page is displayed. Type the keyword in the Learning Resource field, and click Search . Select a learning resource and click one of the following options: Detail Provides a description and specific information about a learning resource. Preview View the learning resource. Add to My Learning Adds a learning resource to your My Learning page. topics.title: Reviewing Queued Documents
topics.body: Reviewing Queued Documents Using the Document Manager > Reviewing Documents Reviewing Queued Documents When you access the software and you are assigned a document to review, the document is displayed in the Documents to be Reviewed By section of the Practice Central window. To review a document assigned to you: Select List > Employees . The Employees List window is displayed. Select the record and select Documents . The Document Review window is displayed. Select the document and select Review . The Document Review window is displayed. Select your name in the Users Assigned To Review List. Mark Reviewed is enabled. Type any notes in the Current Note field and click Mark Reviewed . Click Close . The reviewed document is displayed in the Recently Reviewed Documents section of the Document Review window. To review another document, repeat steps 2 through 6. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

Contacts By Date : Select this command to view a list of contacts organized by date. You can add, edit, and delete pharmacy contacts. Contacts By Type : Select this command to view a list of contacts organized by type. You can add, edit, and delete pharmacy contacts. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Prescription Options

To use prescription options: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, or Treatment Planning tab in the Chart for window, click the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the prescription, right-click, and select an option: To display the prescription details : Select View . To refill a prescription : Select Refill . Type the information and click OK . The Prescription Options window is displayed. Select the option, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Prescription Security
topics.body: Setting Prescription Security Pharmacies and Prescriptions > Handling Prescriptions Setting Prescription Security To set prescription security: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Select the user from the list. Select an option: To enable the user to view prescription records : Select View Prescriptions . To enable the user to write prescriptions : Select Write Prescriptions . To enable the user to delete prescriptions : Select Delete Prescriptions . Click OK . Click Close . To ensure that the system security is enabled, select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Enable System Security . When system security is enabled, a checkbox is displayed next to the command. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Saving Treatment Plan Comments
topics.body: Saving Treatment Plan Comments Treatment Planning Saving Treatment Plan Comments You can use the software to save predefined treatment plan comments and print them on treatment plans. You can save, edit, and delete treatment plan comments in the Treatment Plan Comment window or while printing a treatment plan. To save treatment plan comments while printing a treatment plan, see Printing Treatment Plans . To save, edit, or delete a treatment plan comment: Select System > Change System Settings > Treatment Plan Comments . The Treatment Plan Comment window is displayed. Select an option: To add a new treatment plan comment : Click Add . The Treatment Plan Comment window is displayed. Type the comment title and text in the appropriate fields, and click OK . Click Close . To edit a treatment plan comment : Select the comment and click Edit . The comment information is displayed in the Treatment Plan Comment window. Type the changes in the appropriate fields, and click OK . Click Close . To delete a treatment plan comment : Select the comment and click Delete . A message asking if you want to delete the selected comment is displayed. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Submitting Claims with Linked Codes
topics.body: Submitting Claims with Linked Codes Medical Cross-Coding Submitting Claims with Linked Codes If a patient is covered by both dental and medical insurance , y ou can submit procedures to both insurance carriers when posting ADA codes that are linked to CPT medical codes. When you submit the claim, either a dental or a medical claim is created and submitted in the order you specify. To submit a claim that contains a linked CPT code: In the patient Transactions List window, click Add to add a transaction that contains a linked CPT code to the patient transaction log. To post transactions automatically during the check out process from the InOffice or Daily Appointments windows, see Automatically Posting Appointment Procedures . Select the code from the Code drop-down list and click OK . Select an option: If the code requires a tooth number or surface : Select it from the drop-down lists. If a diagnosis code is associated with the code : Select it from the Diagnosis Codes section. Click OK . Click Close . The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select Submit Insurance from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Transactions Submission window is displayed. To submit a claim for transactions, select the appropriate dates and click OK . A message that informs you that a linked claim is being processed is displayed. Click OK . The Order of Submission For Linked Claims window is displayed. When you post the insurance payment or denial to the dental claim, a message prompts you to submit the claim to medical insurance, if the claim was not paid in full. To submit the primary medical claim first, type C in the Insurance Carrier #1 field. If the patient also has dental insurance, type A in the Insurance Carrier #2 field. Click OK . The Claim window is displayed. Type the appropriate information and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Submitting Claims
topics.body: Submitting Claims eServices > Using the eClaims Service Submitting Claims To submit claims electronically: In the Claim window, click OK . The Submit Primary window is displayed, prompting you to submit the claim electronically. When you assign payer ID numbers to insurance companies, ECS Capable Primary in the Insurance Plan window is selected, and you are prompted to submit a claim electronically for that plan. Click OK . The claim is sent to the ECS batch file to be transmitted later. Continue adding claims to the batch. OR If you use the Real Time Claim Submission service and the claim is for an insurance plan that is ECS capable, a Submit Real-Time for immediate payor response option is selected. More: If you use the Real Time service, select this option, and click OK. The eServices window is immediately opened, and the Real Time claim submission process begins. When the process is complete, a Claim Status window is displayed, showing whether the claim has been paid and the amount that was paid. The report that is returned from the insurance company during Real Time Claim processing is added to the patient's document tree in the Real Time Claim Responses folder. Click OK . The eServices window is displayed, and the Real Time Claim Submission process begins. Related Topics Submitting and Tracking Claims Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Submitting Dental Claims
topics.body: Submitting Dental Claims Submitting and Tracking Claims Submitting Dental Claims You can submit insurance claims immediately after posting transactions, or at any time later. To submit a dental claim: After posting or auto-posting transactions in the patient�s Transactions window, select Submit Insurance from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Transaction Submission window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates between which to submit transactions on the claim. The default value for the Start Date field is the service date of the oldest procedure that has not been submitted, and the default value for the End Date field is the most current date of unsubmitted transactions. Click OK . The Claim window is displayed. The Claim No is assigned automatically and cannot be changed. When you post an insurance payment for the claim, the Paid field is populated with the check amount and the Date field with the check�s post date. When the claim is completely paid and marked as the final payment for the claim, Complete is automatically selected. Click OK . A message is displayed, prompting you about the method you want to use to submit the claim: A message prompts you to print the claim. By default, the claim is sent to a batch file for later printing. Click OK to print the claim. If you use the Electronic Claim Service (ECS), you are prompted to submit the claim electronically. Click OK to submit the claim electronically. If you use the Real Time Claim Submission service and the carrier is ECS capable, you can submit the claim for immediate payor response. Click OK . The eServices window is displayed, and the Real Time Claim Submission process begins. Related Topics Checking Claim Status Information Submitting Secondary Insurance Claims Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Submitting an Ortho Case
topics.body: Submitting an Ortho Case Planning Budgets > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Managing Ortho Cases Submitting an Ortho Case When you submit a claim for an ortho insurance case , y ou can simultaneously set up an ortho budget plan. Before using this feature , y ou must set up the budget plan defaults. See Setting Patient Budget Plan Defaults for more information. To submit an ortho case: From the Patient Transactions window, click ADD . The Transactions window is displayed in ADD mode. Select a transaction that includes an orthodontic procedure code and click OK and Close . The Patient Transactions window is displayed. Click Submit Insurance . The Claim window is displayed. Type the number of months that remain in the treatment process and click OK . The insurance is submitted, and the Patient Transactions window is displayed. A message is displayed. Click Yes . The Patient Budget Plan window is displayed. The estimated amount for the insurance company's first payment is displayed in the Periodic Pymt field. This amount is calculated by dividing the expected payment amount by the number of treatment months and multiplying the sum by the submission frequency. Example : If the expected insurance payment is $200, the months of treatment is 6, and the payment frequency is 3. The estimated periodic payment would be $100 ($200 divided by 6 = $33.333; 3 x $33.333 = $99.999, or $100). Select Edit Budget Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Patient Budget Plan window is displayed in EDIT mode. Select the patient budget plan effective date from the Start Date drop-down list. The first budget plan charge will be posted on the date. The next charge will be posted one month after the start date. Type the patient portion of the budget plan (the amount to be billed over time) in the Cash Price field. If the patient is uninsured, type the full amount of the procedure in the field. If the patient makes a down payment, type the down payment amount in the Cash Down field. The cash price minus any cash down payment is displayed in the Total on Plan field. When you enter a down payment in this window, you must also post it to the Patient Transactions window. If you forget, the program reminds you when you exit the window. Click OK . To print patient ortho budget plan coupons, select Coupons from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message that asks you to confirm your request is displayed. Click Yes . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the appropriate option and click OK . To specify a budget plan coupon format, see Selecting Budget Plan Coupon Formats . To print a statement specifying the loan details for the patient�s signature, select Truth in Lending Form from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message that asks you to confirm your request is displayed. Click Yes . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the appropriate option and click OK . To post the cash down payment amount to the Patient Transactions window, select Post Adjustment from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A credit adjustment code selected in the General System Settings window for patient budget plans is posted to the Patient Transactions window, and the plan balance is decreased by the financed amount. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Submitting and Tracking Claims
topics.body: Submitting and Tracking Claims Submitting and Tracking Claims Dental and medical insurance claims can be submitted from the following windows: Patient Transactions Treatment Plan Claims List Insurance balances can be transferred back to the patient. To generate claims, the following conditions must exist: The patient�s account must have at least one valid insurance plan ID entered for guarantor 1 or guarantor 2. The account guarantor holding the primary insurance coverage must be specified in the Dental Insurance/Primary or Medical Insurance/Primary field on the Insurance tab of the Patient record. Either guarantor 1 or guarantor 2 can provide primary insurance coverage for the patient. The patient�s relationship to each policy holder is also indicated on the Insurance tab. Any plans specified in the Guarantor 1 window must be displayed in the Insurance Plan window and must be associated with an insurance company entered in your database. Related Topics Submitting Dental Claims Using the Claim Record Using Claim List and Claim Window Options Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services Using Other Facilities Submitting Medical Claims Submitting Dental and Medical Claims Reviewing and Tracking Claims Resubmitting and Deleting Claims Transferring Claim Balances to the Patient Printing Claims Running Claim Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Appointments from the Contact Expert
topics.body: Scheduling Appointments from the Contact Expert Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert Scheduling Appointments from the Contact Expert You can schedule an appointment manually or automatically for the patient associated with a contact in the Contact Expert window. Related Topics Manually Scheduling an Appointment Automatically Scheduling an Appointment Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Appointments with Lab Cases
topics.body: Scheduling Appointments with Lab Cases Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Lab Cases Scheduling Appointments with Lab Cases When you are scheduling a patient appointment for a patient that has an open lab case associated with his or her patient record, a message asks you to display the open lab cases. Click Yes . The Lab Cases List window is displayed. All open lab cases for the patient are displayed in the list. From the Daily Appointments window: The Lab Case is outstanding icon : Displayed at the bottom of all scheduled appointments for patients who have open lab cases that have not been returned from the lab. The Lab Case has been returned icon : Displayed at the bottom of all scheduled appointments for patients who have open lab cases that have been returned from the lab. When you are confirming an appointment in the Daily Appointments window for a patient that has open lab cases that have not been returned from the lab, a message asks you to display the unreturned lab cases. From this window, you can: Reschedule the patient Verify that the appointment is confirmed The open lab case associated with the appointment is displayed in the confirmation report. When you are viewing a list of patient appointments in the Patient Appointments window for a patient who has an open lab case associated with his or her record, ***Pending Labs*** is displayed preceding the patient appointment list. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Patient Name Report
topics.body: Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Patient Name Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Scheduler Audit Trail Reports Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Patient Name Report The Scheduler Audit Trail by Patient Name report generates a list of every appointment creation, modification, or deletion, sorted by patient. Sample Report To generate a list of appointment entries and modifications by the patient for whom the appointment was created: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Scheduler Audit Trail > By Patient Name . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Scheduler Audit Trail By Patient Name window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Date Report
topics.body: Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Date Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Scheduler Audit Trail Reports Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Date Report The Scheduler Audit Trail by Date report generates a list of every appointment creation, modification, or deletion within a specified date range. Sample Report To generate a list of appointment entries and modifications by the date on which they were performed: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Scheduler Audit Trail > By Date . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Scheduler Audit Trail By Date window is displayed. Select the starting and ending appointment dates from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By User Report
topics.body: Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By User Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Scheduler Audit Trail Reports Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By User Report The Scheduler Audit Trail by User report generates a list of every appointment creation, modification, or deletion, sorted by user. Sample Report To generate a list of appointment entries by the user who performed the task: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Scheduler Audit Trail > By User . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Scheduler Audit Trail By User window is displayed. Type the starting and ending user names in the appropriate fields. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Recall Appointments for the Family
topics.body: Scheduling Recall Appointments for the Family Scheduling Appointments Scheduling Recall Appointments for the Family Use family scheduling to schedule recall appointments for everyone associated with an account at one time. To schedule recall appointments for the entire family: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select a member of the family to schedule and select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In a Patient window : Select Appointments from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The patient Appointments window is displayed. Click Schedule Family or select the command from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Family Recall window is displayed. Each member of the account and their recall due date is displayed in the list. If you do not want to schedule an appointment for a specific family member, deselect the checkbox in the Schedule column. If a recall appointment is already set for a family member, the checkbox is automatically deselected. To schedule recall appointments for the family members, click Family Scheduling . The Daily Appointments window is display. A series of floating appointments is displayed in the window, one for each family member. Drag each appointment to a time slot. When you release the mouse button, the appointment is scheduled. A warning is displayed if there is a conflict. You can change days, if necessary. The unscheduled appointments will continue to float. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Recall Appointments Using Instant Recall
topics.body: Scheduling Recall Appointments Using Instant Recall Recalling Patients > Scheduling Recall Appointments Using Instant Recall To recall a patient who has a recall procedure associated with his patient record: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click a patient. The patient record is displayed in the Patient window. Select Schedule appointment from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Instant Recall from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Instant Recall submenu is displayed. Select an option: To schedule the patient for a normal recall appointment : Select Last Exam from the menu. To schedule the patient for a user-defined recall appointment : Select Perio from the menu. To schedule the patient for the next recall appointment : Select Next from the menu. If you change the name of your Normal Recall or User-Defined Recall system, the submenu command changes. For example, if the name of your User-Defined Recall system is Ortho, select Instant Recall > Ortho . The Daily Appointments window, or Daybook, is displayed. The day and time of the next available appointment are selected. If the patient�s last exam was more than six months ago, tomorrow�s Daybook is displayed. Select an option: To view the next available time slot : Select Next Slot from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. To view the next available time slot on the next business day : Select Skip Day from the Options menu. To schedule the appointment, select Make Appt from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Click Close . The Patient Appointments window is displayed. The unconfirmed appointment is displayed in red text. Select an option: To print the appointment : Select Print Appointments from the Options menu. The Output Options dialog box is displayed. Select Print and click OK . The Patient Appointments List Report Setup window is displayed. Select Print Next Appointment Only and click OK . To print an appointment reminder : Select Print Reminder from the Options menu. The Output Options dialog box is displayed. Select Print and click OK . Select Confirm from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The appointment is confirmed and displayed. Click Close three times. If you are using the Motivated Recall system, the Notices field on the Recall tab of the patient record is reset to zero after the patient is checked out on the appointment date for the recall procedure. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Multiple Procedures
topics.body: Scheduling Multiple Procedures Scheduling Appointments > Handling Appointment Conflicts Scheduling Multiple Procedures When scheduling a patient for multiple procedures , t he default number of time units needed for the subsequent procedures is 1. You can specify the number of time slots required to complete the selected procedure in conjunction with other procedures. To set the number of time units required to perform a procedure when it is scheduled with another procedure: Select an option: Select List > ADA Codes . From any window, press Ctrl + C. Select ADA Code List Window from the Power bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. The ADA/Transaction Codes List window is displayed. Select a code and press Enter. The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window is displayed. Click Scheduler/Fees or press Ctrl + Tab to make the Scheduler/Fees tab the active window. Type the number of time units required to perform the procedure when combined with other procedures in the Multi Time Units field. Example : While four time units are required to perform one amalgam, you only need two additional time units to perform two amalgams. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduler Menu Commands
topics.body: Scheduler Menu Commands Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Main Menu Bar Scheduler Menu Commands Use the Scheduler menu to view scheduling and production information, schedule appointments, set new production goals, view appointment histories, add patients to Tickler files, set up books, and add holidays to the scheduler. The Scheduler menu commands are: Daily Appointments : Schedule and display patient appointments for a 24-month period. Book At A Glance : Schedule and display patient appointments. Month At A Glance : View goals and production totals for a calendar month. History Appointments : View the appointment history for the selected date range. Set up Goals and Colors : Add, edit, or delete color groups. Tickler File : Search for, view, add, edit, or delete a Tickler File entry. Tickler Short Call List : Display and automatically schedule patients who can reschedule on short notice. Set up Books : Search for, add, edit, delete, add rooms to, or view rooms for a book. Holidays : Add, edit, or delete a holiday. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Order for Multiple Procedures
