topics.body: Scheduling Order for Multiple Procedures Scheduling Appointments > Handling Appointment Conflicts Scheduling Order for Multiple Procedures The order in which procedures are entered in the Appointment Card window might affect the time units allotted for the appointment. Example : When an appointment consists of an amalgam and an x-ray, the time needed for the amalgam is four units, two if it is posted with another procedure. The x-ray needs one time unit, zero if it is posted with another procedure. If you schedule the amalgam first, the total time units needed is four, because the x-ray was posted as an additional procedure needing zero time units. If you schedule the x-ray first, the total time units needed is three, because the x-ray requires one, and the amalgam was posted as an additional procedure needing two time units. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Patient Appointments
topics.body: Scheduling Patient Appointments Scheduling Appointments > Understanding the Patient Appointments Window Scheduling Patient Appointments To schedule an appointment for an existing patient: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select a patient, and select Appointments . The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Appt Book . The Daily Appointments window is displayed. Select the time slot, or right-click, and select Make Appointment . A message asking you to make an appointment is displayed. Click Yes . On the Overview tab in the patient chart: Click Add New . The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Appt Book . The Daily Appointments window is displayed. Select the time slot, or right-click, and select Make Appointment . A message asking you to make an appointment is displayed. Click Yes . In the Book At A Glance window : Select the time slot, right-click, and select Make Appointment . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click a patient. In the Daily Appointments window : Select the time slot, right-click, and select Make Appointment . In the Phone Center window : Select a patient, and click Scheduling . The Patient Appointments window is displayed. Select Appt Book . The Daily Appointments window is displayed. Select the time slot, right-click, and select Make Appointment . A message asking you to make an appointment is displayed. Click Yes . If you are scheduling a patient with tickler entries, a message asks whether to display the entries before displaying the Appointment Card window. You can schedule the appointment using the tickler entry information. The Appointment Card window is displayed. Select the first appointment procedure from the Code drop-down list. Press Tab to view the description, fee, and time units associated with the procedure. The Assistant window is displayed if a bluebook entry is associated with the procedure in the insurance plan that covers the patient. If color scheduling is enabled, the Description field is highlighted in the color assigned to this type of procedure. If necessary, type the tooth number and surface/quadrant information in the appropriate fields. Select any other procedure codes to schedule for the appointment. The total appointment fees and any applicable sales tax are displayed in the Payment Summary section. If the patient does not have any previously scheduled appointments : The insurance estimation amounts are displayed in the Pat Pays and Exp Ins fields. If the patient does not have insurance coverage : No Ins is displayed. If a patient has two or more appointments (on different days), the insurance amount is estimated for the first appointment only. After a transaction is posted for the first appointment, the estimated insurance payment for the second appointment is displayed. If a patient has two appointments scheduled for the same day, the insurance coverage is estimated for both appointments. Type the number of time slots required for the procedures in the Slots field. The default value for this field is the number of time units specified on the Scheduler/Fees tab in the ADA/Transaction Codes window. The maximum number of time units available is displayed to the right of the field. You cannot schedule the patient for more than the available number of time units. Type the letter associated with the provider types required to perform the procedures in the Assignment field. The following letters are associated with each provider types: D : Dentist

topics.title: Running the Edit Scheduler Parameters Utility
topics.body: Running the Edit Scheduler Parameters Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Edit Scheduler Parameters Utility Run this utility to change the settings established during the scheduler initialization. You can change the number of operatories and time units for your practice in this utility. You should have a valid backup before running this utility. Do not run this utility unless you are instructed to by a technical support representative. To edit scheduler parameters: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Edit Scheduler Parameters . The Edit Scheduler Parameters window is displayed. Type the number of operatories in the Number of Operatories field and select the number of time slots from the Number of Time Slots drop-down list. Click OK . A message asks you whether to run a backup. Select an option: If you have a valid backup : Click Yes . A message informs you that the changes are saved. Click OK . If you do not have a valid backup : Click No . The Edit Scheduler Parameters window is displayed. Click Cancel . When the technical support representative tells you to, restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Recall Appointments
topics.body: Scheduling Recall Appointments Recalling Patients Scheduling Recall Appointments In the Patient Appointments window, you can schedule recall appointments using the following options: Instant Recall : Use Instant Recall to schedule recall appointments for a patient who has a recall procedure associated with his patient record. Go To Recall Day : Use Go To Recall Day to schedule recall appointments for a patient who does not have a recall procedure associated with his patient record. Related Topics Using Instant Recall Using Go To Recall Day Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Scheduler Room Names
topics.body: Setting Scheduler Room Names Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Scheduler Defaults Setting Scheduler Room Names You enter the number of operatories using the Scheduler Setup Wizard after the software is initially installed. You can reset the number of operatories using the SoftDent Utilities application. The number of rooms are the same as the number of operatories set up for your practice. To set the name of a room: Select System > Change System Settings > Scheduler . The Scheduler Settings window is displayed. Select Rooms from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Scheduler Rooms List window is displayed. Double-click a room. The Scheduler Rooms window is displayed. Type a name in the Room Title field. The room title is displayed as a column header in the Daily Appointments (Daybook) window. Type an abbreviated name in the Short Title field. This title is displayed as a column heading in the Daily Appointments (Daybook) window when the column width is too small to display the room title. Click OK . Click Close . Tip To adjust the width of columns in the scheduler, place the cursor between two room tabs. Click and hold the left mouse button and drag to the left or right. Release the button. Right-click on the same column and select Column Properties > Make All Rooms This Width to set the same width for the rest of the rooms. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting up the Scheduler Tracking Goals
topics.body: Setting up the Scheduler Tracking Goals Setting Up the Scheduler Tracking Goals You can view and track the progress of practice and provider daily and monthly goals in the Month At A Glance window. The following are displayed in each day: Daily goal achieved percentage Dollars scheduled or posted for the day Production goal numbers You must have the appropriate security rights to see goal and production information. To access the monthly or daily goal information, select the Show Production Amounts In Scheduler from the options in the Rights by User window. Using Goal Scheduling, you can: Track scheduled and production amounts in relation to your production goal. View your progress in the Month At A Glance window. Related Topics Viewing the Monthly Production Goals Viewing Daily Production Goals Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Treatment Plans
topics.body: Scheduling Treatment Plans Treatment Planning Scheduling Treatment Plans After the patient has agreed to a treatment group or procedure, you can use the software to schedule appointments to complete work. To schedule a treatment group or procedure: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the patient Transactions window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the InOffice window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Select a procedure from the treatment group and select Schedule Appointment from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Treatment Plan Scheduling window is displayed. Click OK . Select an option: To schedule an appointment for the treatment plan group : Select Autosched from the Options section of the Express bar or click Autosched on the toolbar. The Daily Appointments window is displayed in auto schedule mode. Select a time slot, and select Make Appt from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To schedule an appointment for the treatment plan procedure : Select Day Book from the Options section of the Express bar or click Daybook on the toolbar. The Daily Appointments window is displayed. Select a time slot, and select Make Appt from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. A message that asks you to confirm the appointment is displayed. Click Yes . The Appointment Card window is displayed. Select the procedures to schedule from the Code drop-down list, and click OK . Click Close twice. If you try to exit the Treatment Plan window without scheduling treatment plan items (or placing them in the tickler file), the Treatment Plan Scheduling Wizard is displayed. Click OK to display the Treatment Plan Scheduling window. If you don't want to schedule the procedures, click Cancel . To never display the window again, select Don't show this window again and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Appointments
topics.body: Scheduling Appointments Scheduling Appointments You can: Track broken, missed, or canceled appointments Build call-back and confirmation lists Check for double-booking and provider availability Monitor your production goals For your convenience, you can schedule an appointment from several windows within the application. You can access special scheduling features, such as: Auto-scheduling Scheduling from the tickler file Family scheduling Instant recall Related Topics Understanding the Patient Appointments Window Scheduling Appointments for New Patients Handling Appointment Conflicts Using Full Resource Scheduling Editing Appointments Canceling Appointments Rescheduling Appointments Tracking Missed, Broken, and Late Appointments Using the Tickler Confirming Appointments Auto Scheduling Patient Appointments Using Instant Recall Scheduling Recall Appointments for the Family Appointment Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Scheduler Audit Trail Reports
topics.body: Running Scheduler Audit Trail Reports System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails Running Scheduler Audit Trail Reports You can generate the Scheduler Audit Trail reports by: Date User Patient name The Scheduler Audit Trail reports generate lists of the dates and times when appointments are: Made Shortened Lengthened Canceled Entered into the tickler file Use these reports to restore information that was accidentally deleted. The following information associated with each appointment modification is included in all report results: Date Time Name of the user who performed the task Task description Patient identification number Patient�s name These reports can be generated only if system security is enabled. Related Topics Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Date Report Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By User Report Running the Scheduler Audit Trail By Patient Name Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Scheduler Defaults
topics.body: Setting Scheduler Defaults Setting Up the Scheduler Setting Scheduler Defaults To set the scheduler defaults: Select System > Change System Settings > Schedule r . The Scheduler Settings window is displayed in SCAN mode. Click Edit . Select an option: To enable the Daily Appointments, Book At A Glance, Month At A Glance, and Tickler Entry windows and the Scheduler menu items : Select Enable Scheduler . If this option is not selected, the scheduler windows are not displayed and the menu items are disabled. This option is selected by default. To enable Color and Goal Scheduling : Select Enable Color Scheduling . If this option is not selected, you cannot set up color groups or schedule by color or production goals. To display a message that prompts you to log a contact after confirming a patient appointment : Select Ask to log contact after confirming appointments . To delete the new patient and account records if the patient does not show up for the appointment when you generate the Missed Appointment report : Select Delete new patients/accounts when printing the Missed Appointment Report . To schedule appointments in time slots designated for lunch when using the auto scheduler : Select Auto schedule in slots marked for lunch . To track late appointments in the Tracked Appointments window: Select Track late appointments . To have the software prompt you when a patient is late and ask if you want to track the appointment as a late appointment: Select Show warning message for late arrivals . Note: A Late appointment tracking record is still added to the database if you opt to only disable the warning message. To display office codes instead of ADA codes in the Daily Appointments (Daybook) and InOffice windows and print office codes instead of ADA codes on the Daily Operatory report : Select Display office codes instead of ADA codes on Day Screen and InOffice . If no office code is associated with an ADA code, the ADA is always displayed. To enable the History Appointments feature and save appointments : Select Save appointment(s) to history when transaction is posted . When this option is selected, appointments can be viewed in the History Appointments window. If the option is not selected, appointment information is deleted from the Daily Appointments and InOffice windows when transactions are posted. T o save slot notes and blocked slots to history: Select Save Slot Notes and Blocked Slots to History . If this option is not selected, slot notes and blocked slots are not displayed in the History Appointments window. To display patient information in the Assistant window when scheduling a patient appointment from the Patient List or Patient windows : Select Show patient information when scheduling from the patient screen . To enable the Check Out feature : Select Enable Check Out Feature . If this option is not selected, the Check Out option is disabled in the Daily Appointments window shortcut menu and the Check Out command is disabled in the InOffice window. Select an option: To assign the patient's default provider to all of the appointment time slots in the Daily Appointments window when the selected procedure is not associated with the scheduler template : Select Default Template to Provider from the Default for Provider Template drop-down list. To assign no default provider to any of the appointment time slots in the Daily Appointments window when the selected procedure is not associated with the scheduler template : Select Default

topics.title: Auto Scheduling Appointments for Existing Patients from the Scheduler Windows
topics.body: Auto Scheduling Appointments for Existing Patients from the Scheduler Windows Scheduling Appointments > Auto Scheduling Patient Appointments Auto Scheduling Appointments for Existing Patients from the Scheduler Windows To auto schedule a new, canceled, or overlapped appointment for an existing patient from the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows: Select an option: To auto schedule an existing patient appointment from the Daily Appointments window : Select the appointment, right-click, and select AutoSched Appt . To auto schedule an existing patient appointment from the Book At A Glance window : Select the appointment, right-click, and select AutoSched Appt . To auto schedule a canceled appointment for an existing patient from the Daily Appointments window : After canceling the appointment, select AutoSched Canceled from the Options section of the Express bar. To auto schedule a canceled appointment for an existing patient from the Book At A Glance window : After canceling the appointment, select AutoSched Canceled from the Options section of the Express bar. To auto schedule an overlapped appointment for an existing patient in the Daily Appointments window : Select AutoSched Overlap from the Options section of the Express bar. To auto schedule an overlapped appointment for an existing patient in the Book At A Glance window : Select AutoSched Overlap from the Options section of the Express bar. The Auto Scheduler Date Range window is displayed. The default value for the Start Date field is tomorrow�s date, and the default value for the End Date field is sixty days from tomorrow. Select the dates within which to search for an appointment, and click OK . The first available time slot that meets the criteria is displayed in the Daily Appointments window. To edit the auto scheduler parameters set for the patient, select Edit Other Auto Sched Parameters . The Setup AutoScheduling Parameters window is displayed. Make changes, and click OK . (See Auto Scheduling Appointments from Patient or Account Records .) The first available time slot that meets the criteria is displayed in the Daily Appointments window. Select an option: If the selected day and time are acceptable to the patient : Skip to number 4. To display the next available time slot that meets the criteria : Click Next Slot or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. To display the previous available time slot that meets the criteria : Click Prev Slot or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. To display the next day that contains a time slot that meets the criteria : Click Skip Day or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. To display the previous day with an available time slot that meets the criteria : Click Prev Day or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. After you have found a suitable time slot, click Make Appt or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Scheduler Icon Bar
topics.body: Using the Scheduler Icon Bar Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows Using the Scheduler Icon Bar These icons are not enabled from the Month At A Glance window. Information is maintained for one appointment at a time. The drag and drop icons are displayed on the right side of the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance windows. Click the icon for the description. Drag and drop an appointment to this icon to cancel it. After the appointment is canceled, the appointment information is held until you reschedule the appointment or place it in the Tickler file. While the appointment is held, the icon is an open book and you can display the appointment information by clicking the icon. You can reschedule the canceled appointment by dragging the icon to the appropriate slot. Drag and drop an overlapped appointment to this icon to reschedule it. Overlapped appointments occur when you move or reschedule an appointment into a time slot that is too small to accommodate it, and it overlaps with another scheduled appointment. The appointment information is held internally, and you can reschedule it by dragging the icon to the appropriate time slot. While the appointment is held, the icon is an open book and you can display the appointment information by clicking the icon. Drag and drop an appointment to this icon to place the appointment in the Tickler List. Click this icon to display the Appt Ticklers for List window. A list of all appointments in the Tickler is displayed in this window. You can sort the list by date of entry, patient name, appointment length, or short call patients. Right-click an entry to display the appointment information. Select an entry and select Make Appt from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu to schedule the appointment in the slot selected in the scheduler window. Drag and drop an appointment to this icon to place the appointment in the Short Call Tickler List. Click this icon to display the Appt Ticklers for List window. A list of all appointments in the Tickler is displayed in this window. You can sort the list by date of entry, patient name, appointment length, or short call patients. Right-click an entry to display the appointment information. Select an entry and select Make Appt from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu to schedule the appointment in the slot selected in the scheduler window. Drag and drop an appointment to this icon to display the patient's chart. If no chart exists, a blank initial chart is displayed. Drag and drop an appointment to this icon to display the patient's portrait. Click this icon to display the Scheduler Books list window. To display a specific book, double-click it. The book is displayed in the scheduler window. Click this icon to display the total dollar amount scheduled for all rooms for the day. To display this information, you must have security rights to show production amounts in the scheduler window. This icon is enabled only when you access the Daily Appointments window from the Patient List or Patient window. The Assistant window is displayed to provide helpful information. If you close this window, click this icon to display it. Click this icon to apply or remove HIPAA privacy feature settings to the appointment information displayed in the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Scheduler Repair Utility
topics.body: Running the Scheduler Repair Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Scheduler Repair Utility Run t his utility to repair a corrupt scheduler data file. Example : If you have phantom appointments, you may be instructed to run this utility. Run the Missed Appointments report and save a backup before running this utility. Do not run this utility unless you are instructed to by a technical support representative. To repair a corrupt scheduler data file: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > Scheduler Repair . A message informs you to run the Missed Appointments report and save a valid backup before running this utility. Click OK . The Scheduler Repair window is displayed. Verify the current date and click OK . Select the date range to repair from the drop-down lists. To update the patients' next appointment dates, select Update the Patients Next Appointment Dates . Click OK twice. When the technical support representative tells you to, restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up a Scheduler Template
topics.body: Setting Up a Scheduler Template Scheduling Appointments > Using Full Resource Scheduling Setting Up a Scheduler Template You can set up a scheduler template for each procedure. When the procedure is scheduled, providers are automatically assigned. To use this feature, you must define the provider type in each provider�s record. To set up a scheduler template: Select an option: Select List > ADA Codes . From any window, press Ctrl + C. Select ADA Code List Window from the Power bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. The ADA/Transaction Codes List window is displayed. Select a code and press Enter. The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window is displayed. If the procedure requires a hygienist, select Hygiene Code . You cannot assign a hygienist to the scheduler template if this checkbox is not selected. Click Scheduler/Fees or press Ctrl + Tab to make the Scheduler/Fees tab the active window. Type the number of time units required to perform the procedure when combined with other procedures in the Multi Time Units field. Example : While four time units are required to perform one amalgam, you only need two additional time units to perform two amalgams. Type the number of real time minutes it takes to complete the coded procedure, in the RTU minutes field. The real time unit is the actual time it takes you to perform a procedure in minutes. For example, 20 RTUs equal 20 minutes. To use the scheduler template, select Use Scheduler Template . If you use this feature, the selected providers are automatically assigned to the code. A message that warns you of scheduling conflicts is also displayed. Type the provider assignment code for each time unit in the Scheduler Template field. Example : If you enter HHDB, a hygienist is assigned for the first two time units, a doctor for the third time unit, and a doctor and assistant or hygienist for the fourth time unit. Click OK . The Slots and Assignment fields in the Appointment Card window are automatically populated when this procedure is scheduled. Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Scheduler Warnings
topics.body: Setting Scheduler Warnings Setting Up the Scheduler > Setting Scheduler Defaults Setting Scheduler Warnings To dictate the warning and reminder messages displayed in the scheduler windows: Select System > Change System Settings > Scheduler . The Scheduler Settings window is displayed. Select Warnings from the Options section of the Express bar or from the Options menu. The Scheduler Warning Settings window is displayed. Type the number of days before which color scheduling warnings are disabled in the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance windows in the Disable Color Scheduling warning period window. To fill last minute gaps in the schedule regardless of color parameters, without triggering color-mismatch warnings, type 1 in the field. Example : If you have assigned the color blue to all hygiene procedures, and then try to schedule one of those procedures in a time slot designated as yellow for the current day, the warning is not displayed if 1 is entered in this field. If 0 is entered in this field, a warning is displayed. To limit the number of appointments that can be scheduled for patients on medical assistance or capitation plans per day, type the maximum number of appointments in the Maximum number of Cap/Medicaid/PPO Appts Per Day Before Warning field. CAP is displayed in the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance windows when this limit is reached. To display a warning before deleting the previous appointment cancelation in the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance windows, select Display Warning before erasing previous cancellation . When you cancel an appointment, the appointment information is temporarily stored to enable you to reschedule it or place it in the Tickler. If you cancel another appointment before rescheduling the appointment or placing it in the Tickler, the first appointment is deleted. When this checkbox is selected, a warning is displayed before the first appointment is deleted. To display a warning before scheduling a provider who does not meet the appointment�s requirements in the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance windows, select Display warning if provider type is incorrect when scheduling . Example : If this checkbox is selected, a warning is displayed if you try to schedule a hygienist for a procedure that requires a dentist. This warning is displayed only when the provider type is associated with the provider�s record and a scheduler template is associated with the procedure. To display a warning if the patient is inactive, select Display warning if patient is Inactive . To display a warning when an appointment is scheduled for a patient who has certain codes associated with his patient record, type the codes in the Display warning if patient has any of the following user codes: field. The default values for this field are C (collection), I (inactive patient), L (legal cases), W (medical assistance), and P (premedicate). See Understanding Patient User Codes . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the History Appointments Scheduler Icon Bar
topics.body: Using the History Appointments Scheduler Icon Bar Using the Scheduler > Using the History Appointments Window > Understanding History Appointments Window Options Using the History Appointments Scheduler Icon Bar The following drag and drop icons are displayed on the right-side of the History Appointments window. Only the following commands are enabled in this window: Drag and drop an appointment to this icon to display the patient's chart. If no chart exists, a blank initial chart is displayed. Drag and drop an appointment to this icon to display the patient's portrait. Click this icon to display the Scheduler Books list window. To display a specific book, double-click it. Click this icon to display the total dollar amount scheduled for all rooms for the selected day. To display this information, you must have security rights to show production amounts in the scheduler window. Click this icon to apply or remove HIPAA privacy feature settings to the appointment information in the window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduler Features
topics.body: Scheduler Features What's New in Version 14.0 Scheduler Features The Scheduler has received enhancements in the following areas: Daily Appointments Window Maximized in the Classic Workspace If you use the Classic Workspace, the Daily Appointments window now fills the entire screen when Daily Appointments is selected in the Display At Startup section of the Startup Preferences window. The size of this window is identical to the size of the window that is displayed by selecting Scheduler > Daily Appointments . Slot Notes and Blocked Slots Available in the History Appointments Window You now have the option of viewing slot notes and blocked slots in the History Appointments window of the scheduler. A new option�Save slot notes and blocked slots to history�is now available in the Scheduler Settings window and the Scheduler Setup Wizard window. This option is not selected by default, meaning that slot notes and blocked slots will not be displayed in the History Appointments window. If you want slot notes and blocked slots to display in the History Appointments window, select this option. Slot notes and blocked slots in the History Appointments window cannot be edited. Use the same methods to view slot notes and blocked slots in the History Appointments window as you would in the Daybook. See Setting Scheduler Defaults and Initializing the Scheduler for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running School Reports
topics.body: Running School Reports Running School Reports You can generate school reports that are sorted alphabetically or by identification number. The reports contain the school ID number, name, address, phone number, and contact person. Related Topics Running the Alphabetical Schools List Report Running the Schools by ID Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using School List and School Window Options
topics.body: Using School List and School Window Options Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling School Information Using School List and School Window Options In the School List or School window, you can print address labels and envelopes, log a contact, create TeamTalk notes, and view the phone numbers associated with a school. The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu when using the Enhanced Workspace are: 0 - Auto Dialer : Dials a school number. If you do not have a modem, this information is displayed for your reference. Label : Prints a label or laser label for the selected school. Envelope : Prints a label or laser envelope for the selected school. TeamTalk : Adds, edits, and deletes school TeamTalk notes; views and deletes expired school conditions. Contacts By Date : Adds, edits, or deletes school contacts; creates school memos. Contacts By Type : Adds, edits, or deletes school contacts; creates school memos. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Auto Scheduling New Patient Appointments from the Scheduler Windows
topics.body: Auto Scheduling New Patient Appointments from the Scheduler Windows Scheduling Appointments > Auto Scheduling Patient Appointments Auto Scheduling New Patient Appointments from the Scheduler Windows To auto schedule a new patient appointment from the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows: Select an option: To auto schedule a new patient appointment from the Daily Appointments window : Select AutoSched New Appt from the Options section of the Express bar. To auto schedule a new patient appointment from the Book At A Glance window : Select AutoSched New Appt from the Options section of the Express bar. The Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters window is displayed. Click OK , select a date range, type a time range, select the procedures for which the appointment is to be scheduled, and type the number of time slots and the slot usage schedule in the appropriate fields. The Time Slots and Slot Usage Schedule fields are populated automatically if the procedure is associated with a scheduler template. If a bluebook note is associated with the procedure in the patient's insurance plan, the Assistant window is displayed. Click the Preferred Days tab or press Ctrl + Tab to make the tab the active window. The default value for the Dr field is the default new account doctor. If the procedure includes a hygiene code : The default value for the HYG field is the hygienist specified in the patient record. The default values in the Days to Search section are the days and hours in which the office is open. Select the patient�s provider from the Dr drop-down list. If the procedure includes a hygiene code, select a hygienist from the drop-down list in the Hyg field. Select the days preferred by the new patient from the Days to Search section. In the Search Rooms in Book section, click the drop-down arrow to select a book. If the procedures to be scheduled do not include a hygiene code: The default book is the book in the Auto Sched Book field in the dentist�s Provider window. If the dentist does not have a preferred auto scheduling book: The default book for the practice is displayed. If the procedures to be scheduled include a hygiene code: The default book is the book in the Auto Sched Book field in the hygienist's Provider window. If the hygienist does not have a preferred auto scheduling book: The default book for the practice is displayed. OR Click Specify Rooms . The Specify Rooms window is displayed. Do the following: Select the rooms you want to search to create a temporary schedule book. A temporary schedule book is automatically deleted after the appointment is scheduled. To save the room selections for future use, click Save this book for future use . In the Book Title field, type the name for the book. If you attempt to add a book with a name that is already in use, a message is displayed, stating that you can select the book from the drop-down list or continue adding the book. If you click Yes , a book with the same name is added to the list of available books. Click OK . Click Schedule Appt or select the command from the Options section of the Express bar. The first available time slot that meets the criteria is displayed in the Daily Appointments window. Select an option: If the day and time are acceptable to the patient : Skip to number 10. To display the next available time slot that meets the criteria : Click Next Slot or select it from the Options section of the Express bar. To display the previous available time slot that meets the

topics.title: Running the Schools by ID Report
topics.body: Running the Schools by ID Report Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Running Provider, Employer, and School Reports Running the Schools by ID Report The Schools by ID report generates a list of schools in the database. The list is sorted by identification number. Sample Report For each school, the report includes: Identification number Name Address Contact person To run the Schools By ID report: Select Reports > Insurance > Schools By ID . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Schools By ID Report Setup window is displayed. Select the first and last school identification numbers to include in the report from the drop-down lists. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scrolling Through Records in a List Window
topics.body: Scrolling Through Records in a List Window Getting Started > Using List Windows Scrolling Through Records in a List Window To scroll through the records that are displayed in a list window, select an option: To display the previous record : Click Previous or press the up arrow key. To display the next record : Click Next or press the down arrow key. To display the previous page of results : Press Page Up . To display the next page of results : Press Page Down. To display the first record : Press Home. The first record is highlighted. To display the last record : Press End. The last record is highlighted. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting PracticeToGo Security Rights
topics.body: Setting PracticeToGo Security Rights Using PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo Security Setting PracticeToGo Security Rights Before a user of the PC SoftDent software can synchronize SoftDent data with a Palm organizer or the Microsoft Outlook software, he or she must have security rights set by the system administrator in the PC SoftDent software. Using the security settings, the system administrator specifies which users can transfer SoftDent data, as well as the categories of information the user is allowed to transfer. The settings also determine which users can transfer SoftDent data from the Palm organizer or the Microsoft Outlook software back to the PC SoftDent software. If a user has no PracticeToGo security rights assigned, he or she does not have the ability to transfer SoftDent data. To view a list of the PracticeToGo security settings, click here . PracticeToGo security settings include: Print the PracticeToGo Audit Trail Delete the PracticeToGo Audit Trail PracticeToGo can access end of day call backs PracticeToGo can access office financial information PracticeToGo can access provider schedules PracticeToGo can access office reminders PracticeToGo can access referring doctor information PracticeToGo can access pharmacy information PracticeToGo can access Rx information PracticeToGo can access patient information PracticeToGo Date Book commands active PracticeToGo Address commands active Change PracticeToGo system settings PracticeToGo has access to practice information PracticeToGo Memo commands active You assign security rights for PracticeToGo in the same manner as you would any other security rights for a user. See Setting Up Security for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the SoftDent Charting Module
topics.body: Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the SoftDent Charting Module The charting module is an advanced dental charting feature that enables you to do the following for the visit: Chart patient conditions and procedures Perform mucogingival junction (MGJ) and free gingival margin (FGM) charting Record a variety of special exams (including periodontal screening and recording (PSR)) Post all transactions You can use the following to perform all restorative and periodontal charting: Mouse Keyboard Light pen Touch-screen This module also supports voice input for periodontal charting. Using this module while creating and storing the chart, you have instant access to: Patient data Transaction information Treatment plans When you open a chart, it is automatically updated to reflect all applicable transactions posted in the practice management software since the chart was last viewed. Related Topics Using the Main Charting Toolbar Using the File Menu Using the Chart Menu Using the Perio Menu Using the Mode Menu Using the View Menu Using the Video Menu Using the X-ray Menu Using the Clinical Notes Menu Accessing the Charting Module Viewing Quadrants E-mailing Charts Viewing the Assistant's Info Window Selecting Providers Selecting Teeth Using Toolbars Printing Charts Viewing Images Associated with a Tooth Using the Clinical Detail Tab Container Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container Using the Clinical Images Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container Working with MEDVISOR Dental Software Exiting the Charting Module Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: SoftDent Commands and Microsoft Outlook
topics.body: SoftDent Commands and Microsoft Outlook Using PracticeToGo > Sending Data Back to SoftDent SoftDent Commands and Microsoft Outlook The SoftDent Commands available through the PracticeToGo feature enable you to transfer specific information to the PC SoftDent software from the Microsoft Outlook software. You can enter the following commands: Date Book Commands Address Commands Memo Command Related Topics Entering a Date Book Command in Microsoft Outlook Entering an Address Command in Microsoft Outlook Entering a Memo Command in Microsoft Outlook Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: SoftDent Commands and the Palm Organizer
topics.body: SoftDent Commands and the Palm Organizer Using PracticeToGo > Sending Data Back to SoftDent SoftDent Commands and the Palm Organizer SoftDent Commands for the Date Book, Address List, and Memo Pad enable you to transfer data to the PC SoftDent software. You can view an explanation of all the SD Commands on the screen of your Palm organizer. To view the SoftDent Commands: On the Palm Home screen, tap the SoftDent icon and select SD Commands . The SoftDent Commands screen is displayed. Each command, along with information about it, is displayed. These are for reference only. These commands are entered in the Palm Date Book, Address List, and Memo Pad applications next to SoftDent data entries. To scroll through the commands, tap the up- or down-pointing arrow in the bottom-right corner of the screen. To return to the SoftDent Menu, tap Back . Related Topics Entering a Date Book Command on the Palm Organizer Entering an Address Command on the Palm Organizer Entering a Memo Command on the Palm Organizer Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Specifying the SoftDent Data Directory
topics.body: Specifying the SoftDent Data Directory Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Creating Case Presentations Using PowerPoint Specifying the SoftDent Data Directory If you run multiple instances of the software used to create case presentations , y ou can select a directory to run each PowerPoint session. To specify a data directory: From PowerPoint, select Tools > Specify Data Directory . The Specify the SoftDent Data Directory window is displayed. Click Browse . The Browse for Folder window is displayed. Navigate to and select the correct folder to use as the data directory. Click OK twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Security Overrides
topics.body: Setting Security Overrides System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Setting Security Overrides You can assign an override to any of the tasks with restricted access . T his feature enables a user who does not typically have access to the task to perform it. The restricted access tasks are arranged in four System Security windows. A list of tasks is displayed on tabs in each window. For a complete list of tasks by category, see Tasks with Limited Access . To set a security override: Select an option: To set a transaction or accounting override : Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Security Overrides > Transactions/Accounting Settings . To set a report override : Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Security Overrides > Report Settings . To set a clinical note override : Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Security Overrides > Clinical Settings . To set a miscellaneous override : Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Security Overrides > Miscellaneous . The appropriate System Settings - Override Settings window is displayed. Press Enter or click Edit . Select an option: To disable system security : Deselect Enable System Security . To enable system security : Select Enable System Security . To apply an override to a task, select Override next to the task. Select an option: To enable any user to perform the task after entering a password that you assign : Select Enter Password from the Override Type drop-down list and type the password in the Password field. When a user who does not have permission attempts to perform the task, a message informs the user to enter the password. To enable any user to perform the task after entering the user name and password of a user who can perform the task : Select User Login from the Override Type drop-down list. When a user who does not have permission attempts to perform the task, a message informs the user to enter the user name and password of someone who does have permission. The security rights of the user are not changed by using this feature. The user only has temporary access to the task. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Security Rights
topics.body: Setting Up Security Rights eServices > Using the ePayments Service Setting Up Security Rights To view the Practice Information window and to access credit card information, a user must have security rights. For more information, see Setting Up Security . To provide security rights to other users, the user with the highest security must follow these steps: From the SoftDent main menu, select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights by User . The Rights by User window is displayed. Double-click the user name. In the User Rights Selection section, click the Accounting button. To enable the user to run settlement reports, select Run the Credit Card Settlement Report . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting Conditions to Be Displayed
topics.body: Selecting Conditions to Be Displayed Handling Patient Medical History > Understanding Medical Conditions Selecting Conditions to Be Displayed To select and dictate the display order for conditions: Select System > Customize Medical History . The Medical Conditions List window is displayed. Press Enter, and click Change Order. The Select and Sort Conditions To Be Displayed window is displayed. Select an option: To display a hidden condition on the Conditions tab of the Medical History window : Select it from the Hidden Conditions section, and click Add in the Display section. To hide a condition that is currently displayed on the Conditions tab of the Medical History window : Select it from the Displayed Conditions section, and click Remove in the Display section. Add is disabled after fifty conditions are displayed in the Displayed Conditions section. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting a Default Patient Portrait
topics.body: Selecting a Default Patient Portrait Adding Patients > Patient Portraits Selecting a Default Patient Portrait Only one patient portrait can be displayed at a time; however, if you retain past patient portraits, you can select the portrait you want to display. To select the default patient portrait: In the patient chart, click the Clinical Imaging tab to display the Clinical Imaging toolbar. Click the Organization Mode button. The Image Maintenance window is displayed, with the currently selected patient portrait displayed in the upper-right of the window. In the Clinical Images tab at the bottom of the window, click the portrait you want as the new default, and drag the image until it is over the current portrait and your cursor displays a plus sign. Release the mouse. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting Insurance Forms
topics.body: Selecting Insurance Forms Getting Started > Starting the Software Selecting Insurance Forms Many standard insurance forms are included with this software. The insurance forms are cross-referenced so you can order them from a new vendor without changing the form number. To review or select insurance forms: Select Insurance > Select Insurance Forms . The Insurance Forms Selection window is displayed. Select the form from the drop-down list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting from a List While Editing
topics.body: Selecting from a List While Editing Getting Started > Using List Windows Selecting from a List While Editing When you are adding or editing a record, you often need to access other records to locate pertinent information. Example : To change a patient�s default doctor, you need to select information from the provider database while working in the patient database. Using the preceding example, while editing the patient record: Select the doctor from the Doctor drop-down list. The Providers List window is displayed. The current provider is highlighted. Double-click a provider as the patient�s doctor. The name of the doctor is displayed in the Doctor field in the patient record. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting List Items
topics.body: Selecting List Items Getting Started > Entering Data Selecting List Items Click the button next to a drop-down list to select an entry from a predefined or default list. To select a value from the drop-down list: Select an option or highlight it. Press Enter . Several windows contain drop-down lists that enable access to another database. Example : In the Patient window, select the Doctor drop-down list to display the Providers List window in FIND mode. Select a provider, and the Providers List window is closed. The selected provider is displayed in the Doctor drop-down list. Many drop-down lists contain a list of predefined selections. Select a record by typing the first letter of the entry. After you type the first letter, an entry that begins with that letter is inserted in the field. If it is not the correct entry, type the letter again until the correct entry is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting Printers
topics.body: Selecting Printers Getting Started > Starting the Software Selecting Printers The default Microsoft Windows operating system software printer is set as the default printer for this software. Example : If an HP LaserJet 5L is your default printer in Microsoft Windows, all settings in the System Settings - Printer and Word Processor window default to that printer. Printer selections are workstation specific. To designate a different printer for a specific document type: Select System > Change System Settings > Printer And WP . The System Settings - Printer and Word Processor window is displayed. Select an option: To change the default printer for a specific report category : Select the printer from the drop-down list of printers. To print a specific report category in color : Select Print in Color . To set the printer offsets for a specific report category : Click Set Offsets . The Set Printer Offsets window is displayed. Type the horizontal and vertical offset information and click OK . The System Settings - Printer and Word Processor window is displayed. To display a window before printing : Select Display printer selection before printing . Select the word processor from the drop-down list, and type any word processor startup codes in the field. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting Providers
topics.body: Selecting Providers Using the SoftDent Charting Module Selecting Providers If the service provider is not performing the exam or procedures , y ou can select the provider. If you select a hygiene procedure such as a prophy, the default hygienist is automatically selected. If no hygienist is specified, the doctor is selected for the procedure. A warning is displayed when someone other than a hygienist is selected to perform a hygiene procedure or if a hygienist is selected to perform a non-hygiene procedure. To select a provider: Do one of the following: Select Chart > Provider List. Select Provider List from the main charting toolbar. Click the link in the Provider section of the patient banner. The Provider List window is displayed. Double-click the provider. To select different providers to perform different procedures within the same chart, select the provider from the Providers List window before charting the procedure. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting Prescription Forms
topics.body: Selecting Prescription Forms Pharmacies and Prescriptions > Handling Prescriptions Selecting Prescription Forms To print a prescription form, you must first select the form. You can also phone in prescriptions. To choose a prescription form: Select System > Form Selection . The Forms Selection window is displayed. Select the prescription form from the Prescription Form drop-down list and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting a Billing Statement
topics.body: Selecting a Billing Statement Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Selecting a Billing Statement You can use a variety of statement forms. To select a billing statement form: Select System > Form Selection . The Form Selection window is displayed. Select the billing statement and walkout statement forms from the Billing Statements and Walkout Statements drop-down lists. If you process claims electronically, select the form from the Electronic Statements drop-down list. For more information, see Setting Up Automated Statements . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Selecting Teeth
topics.body: Selecting Teeth Using the SoftDent Charting Module Selecting Teeth To chart any ADA procedure or tooth condition, select the procedure or condition and select the tooth, teeth, surface, or surfaces with the mouse, light pen, or touch-screen. If a graphical representation is associated with the procedure or condition, it is displayed on the tooth. If the Use Pointing Device checkbox is selected in the Chart Options Properties window and you are charting a procedure that requires a surface; you must point to or select the surface when selecting a tooth. Example : To chart an amalgam, you must select a tooth surface with your input device�selecting the tooth or the root will not chart the procedure. If the Use Pointing Device checkbox is not selected in the Chart Options Properties window, the Tooth window is displayed. In this window, you must select the tooth surface or quadrant to chart the procedure or condition. Example : When you click a tooth to chart an amalgam, the Tooth window is displayed. To chart the procedure, you must select the appropriate surface. Procedures requiring the following can only be applied to one tooth at a time: Surface Root Quadrant Conditions can be applied to multiple teeth. You cannot select multiple teeth in perio mode. Related Topics Viewing Tooth Details Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Sending All Real Time Claims at One Time
topics.body: Sending All Real Time Claims at One Time eServices > Using the eClaims Service Sending All Real Time Claims at One Time To send all real time claims at one time: Select Insurance > eServices . The eServices window is displayed. Click Load . The Claim Submission List window is displayed with real time claims highlighted in blue. Click Send All Real Time Claims . The claim status changes from Waiting validation to Provider Info OK if the claim passes validation, and the status bar at the bottom of the window displays a Transmitting Claim message. When the claims have been successfully transmitted, the payer sends a Claim Status response. The Claim Status response is saved to the patients' Documents window. Click the printer icon in the upper-left corner of the window to print the response. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Sending Claims
topics.body: Sending Claims eServices > Using the eClaims Service Sending Claims After you submit claims, you must load, validate, and transmit the claims. To access eServices from the software main menu , select Insurance > eServices . The eServices window is displayed. Related Topics Loading Claims Validating Claims Transmitting Claims Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Sending the CS Imaging Software Image Viewer to E-mail Recipients
topics.body: Sending the CS Imaging Software Image Viewer to E-mail Recipients Using E-mail and WebLink > Sending an E-mail > E-mailing CS Imaging Software Images Sending the CS Imaging Software Image Viewer to E-mail Recipients You can attach a dental image and the image viewer to an e-mail. Recipients can use the viewer to display the attached image and annotations. When you e-mail a CS Imaging software image, a link to a downloadable version of the viewer is automatically included in the e-mail. To attach the viewer: In the patient record, select Documents . The Documents window is displayed. Patient documents, images, and charts are displayed in the window. Expand the clinical images, select the image, and select E-Mail . The SoftDent E-Mail window is displayed. The image is displayed in the Attached Items section of the window, and the patient name and an annotation is displayed in the Message section of the window. To send the e-mail to a different recipient, select the addressee from the Recipient drop-down list. Tip To carbon copy or blind carbon copy a recipient, type an e-mail in the CC or BCC field. Type the e-mail subject and message. Select Attach Image Viewer . Click OK (Send) . Tip To attach the Microsoft PowerPoint software viewer, select Attach PowerPoint Viewer. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Sending and Receiving eForms
topics.body: Sending and Receiving eForms eServices > Using the eForms Module Sending and Receiving eForms Typically, you set up the times to automatically send and receive eForms ahead of time, but you can manually send and receive eForms, if necessary. Sending eForms To send eForms to a specific patient: In the Daybook or Book Quick Glance view, right-click on an appointment and select eForms > Send eForms Now . To send eForms to all patients who meet the criteria you previously specified: Select System > Modules > eForms > Send eForms . This command causes all qualifying eForms to be sent and received. This command overrides the send and receive time set in the eForms Configuration window. Receiving eForms To receive eForms: Select System > Modules > eForms > Receive eForms . This command causes all completed eForms to be received. This command overrides the send and receive time set in the eForms Configuration window. Once you have received eForms, you need to review the completed forms to see what patient information might have changed. See Reviewing eForms for more information. Related Topics Resending eForms Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Searching for ADA Codes
topics.body: Searching for ADA Codes Handling Transactions and Codes Searching for ADA Codes To search for an ADA or transaction code: Select List > ADA Codes . The ADA/Transaction Codes List window is displayed. Select a sort option: Select All Codes , and type the ADA, transaction, or office code number in the Find field. Select Description , and type the description for the ADA, transaction, or office code in the Find field. Tip The number of characters that must be typed before a search is conducted is set in the Startup Preferences window. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Patient Budget Plans
topics.body: Setting Up Patient Budget Plans Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans Setting Up Patient Budget Plans To set up a patient budget plan: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Click the patient and select Budget Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Budget Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Patient Budget Plan window is displayed in SCAN mode. Select Edit Budget Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Patient Budget Plan window is displayed in EDIT mode. To enter an Ortho budget plan, see Managing Ortho Cases . Select the effective date from the Start Date drop-down list. The first budget plan charge will be posted on the date. The next charge will be posted one month after the start date. Type the balance, to be billed over time, in the Cash Price field. If the patient is uninsured, type the full amount of the procedure in the field. If the patient makes a down payment, type the down payment amount in the Cash Down field. The cash price minus any cash down payment is displayed in the Total on Plan field. When you enter a down payment in this window, you must also post it to the Patient Transactions window. In the Day to Post field, select the day of the month to post the budget plan charge back to the patient. Select an option: To calculate the monthly payment amount automatically : Type the number of payments in the No. of Payments field. To enter the patient�s preferred monthly payment : Select Patient Picks Payment and type the preferred amount in the Monthly Payment field. If you need to specify the next posting date, enable the Next Charge to be Posted field by selecting System > Special Edit Field Enable and type the date in the field. You must have the appropriate security permissions to access the Special Edit Field Enable feature. Click OK . If you make a mistake while setting up the patient budget plan, select Reset Budget Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. You cannot reset the budget plan after you have posted the adjustment. To print patient budget plan coupons, select Coupons from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message is displayed. Click Yes . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the appropriate option and click OK . To specify a budget plan coupon format, see Selecting Budget Plan Coupon Formats . To print a statement specifying the loan details for the patient�s signature, select Truth in Lending Form from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A message is displayed. Click Yes . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the appropriate option and click OK . To post the cash down payment amount to the patient Transactions window, select Post Adjustment from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A credit adjustment code selected in the General System Settings window for patient budget plans is posted to the Patient Transactions window, and the plan balance is decreased by the financed amount. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up the Modem
topics.body: Setting Up the Modem eServices > Using the ePayments Service Setting Up the Modem To configure the modem: From the SoftDent main menu, select System > Change System Settings > General System . The General System Settings window is displayed. Select Modem Settings from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Modem Settings window is displayed. Enter the settings for your modem. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides
topics.body: Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Managing Employee Attendance Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides When System Security is enabled, access to the Employee database is restricted. Enable the setting to allow a user to access the Employee database to edit employee information, add and review Time Card entries, review the Time Card audit trails, and generate the Time Card report. You can also set a login and password override to enable an employee to edit employee information and add and review Time Card entries. By default, new user security settings created from the Office Manager Template include access to the Employee and Time Card databases. Related Topics Setting Employee and Time Card Security Setting Employee and Time Card Login Overrides Editing Employee Security Information in the Employee Windows Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Communications
topics.body: Setting Up Communications eServices Setting Up Communications To set up communications: Select Insurance > eServices . The eServices window is displayed. On the menu bar at the top of the window, select Setup > Modem . The Modem Settings window is displayed. In the Provider TIN field, type the provider�s tax ID number (TIN). The provider TIN is the practice tax ID number, the provider�s tax ID number, or the provider�s Social Security number. If the TIN is not the same number indicated on the eServices enrollment form, contact an eServices representative. In the Account No. field, type your eServices account number. Select from the following: To enable eServices submissions over the Internet: Select Internet File Transfer settings . Each computer using eServices must have an active Internet connection. Internet security in the Microsoft Windows operating system should not be set higher than medium, and cookies should be enabled. To enable eServices submissions using a modem: In the Com Port field, select the modem�s communications port from the drop-down list. In the Baud Rate field, select the modem�s speed from the drop-down list. Note: Transmissions are accepted from modems with Baud rates of 2400 through 57600. In the following fields, keep the default settings: Parity : None Data Bits : 8 Stop Bits : 1 Transmit : 4000 Receive : 100 Script Match : 9 Retries : 40 In the Phone Number field, verify the eServices telephone number. If you must dial 9 to make an outside call, type 9 and a comma before the number; for example, 9,1-800-000-0000. Note: The default setting is the toll-free eServices network number. In the Phone Type field, select Tone or Pulse , depending on your telephone service. In the remaining fields, keep the default settings unless instructed to change them by a technical support representative. Click OK . Related Topics Testing Communications Testing the Printer Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Automated Statements
topics.body: Setting Up Automated Statements eServices > Using the Automated Statements Service > Using Automated Statements Setting Up Automated Statements To enroll in Automated Statements, contact an eServices representative. To set up Automated Statements: From the SoftDent main menu, select System > Change System Settings > Billing Statements . The Billing Statement Settings window is displayed. Click Edit , and in the Cycle Billing section, do the following: Verify that Enable Cycle Billing is selected. In the Cycle Billing Period field : Type the number of days in the billing period. The recommended setting is 25, which means SoftDent will not generate a statement within 25 days of the previous statement transmission. In the Automated Statements Service Settings section, do the following: Click Enable Automated Statements Service . A message is displayed, asking you to contact eServices sales to enable the option. Click OK. You must have Run the Billing Statements security rights to select this option. Click Enable Online Statement Service if you want to send online statements to patients. You must be enrolled in Automated Statements in order to use this service. Click OK . To print a message on the billing statement, click Messages . The Statement Messages window is displayed. Type the text for each type of message and click OK . The Billing Statement Settings window is displayed. To select the form on which the statement is printed, from the SoftDent main menu, select System > Form Selection . The Forms Selection window is displayed. Select an option from the Automated Statements drop-down list: Statement WITHOUT Credit Card Information Statement w/Credit Card: (VISA/MC) Statement w/Credit Card: (VISA/MC/Discover) Statement w/Credit Card: (VISA/MC/Discover/American Express) Statement w/Credit Card: (VISA/MC/American Express) Click OK . To remove a minimum payment amount as the amount due on a billing statement, access the Account window for the patient and delete the amount from the Min Payment field. Tip Select this form on every workstation computer. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Insurance Companies
topics.body: Setting Up Insurance Companies eServices > Using the eClaims Service Setting Up Insurance Companies To submit claims electronically: You must assign a 5-digit payer ID number to the insurance companies that are capable of accepting electronic claims. The Auto IDs feature matches the insurance companies in your database with the appropriate payer ID numbers. To submit claims electronically to insurance companies that do not accept electronic claims: You have the option to submit claims electronically through SoftDent to a clearinghouse. The clearinghouse prints and mails the claims to the insurance companies. These claims are called "paper electronic" claims. Some insurance companies require: Additional enrollment forms before accepting electronic claims : These companies are marked with a + sign in the Insurance Payer Setup window. A provider number on file before accepting electronic claims : These companies are marked with ID in the Insurance Payer Setup window. These insurance companies send written confirmation after completed enrollment; without confirmation, the claims are rejected. To submit claims to companies requiring enrollment, contact a technical support representative to obtain the enrollment forms. Related Topics Assigning Payer ID Numbers Automatically Assigning Payer ID Numbers Manually Removing Payer ID Numbers Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Treatment Plans
topics.body: Setting Up Treatment Plans Treatment Planning Setting Up Treatment Plans To set up a treatment plan: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the patient Transactions window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the InOffice window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Click Add on the toolbar. The Treatment Plan window is displayed. Select the treatment plan type from the Type drop-down list. Select the procedural code from the Code drop-down list and press Tab. The code description and fee are displayed in the appropriate fields, and the insurance totals are calculated and displayed. If the Diagnostic Codes fields are enabled, you are entering a medical procedure code, or the Use Diagnosis Codes field is selected in the Insurance Plan Claim Filing window. If the procedure requires a tooth, quadrant, or surface; select the information from the Tooth or Surface drop-down lists. These fields are enabled only if required by the procedure. Select the doctor from the Dr drop-down list. The default value of this field is the treating doctor specified in the common area of the patient record. Use the Group field for grouping treatment plan procedures. All treatment plan procedures default to group 1. You can schedule 12 procedures per appointment. Do not add more than 12 procedures to any group. Enter a new group number if you cannot schedule all treatment plan work in one appointment. Select the procedural acceptance state from the drop-down list in the State field. If the procedure is not covered by the patient�s insurance, deselect Insurable . No claim can be associated with this procedure. If the patient is not using the year to date estimation for the procedure, select Do not use YTD estimation . Click OK . The Treatment Plan window is displayed. To enter the same treatment plan item again, select DITTO from the Options section of the Express bar. The information for the previously entered transaction is displayed. Make the appropriate changes to the transaction, and click OK . The Treatment Plan window is displayed. After you enter all procedures for this treatment plan, click Close twice. If Do not process is selected from the Insurance/Walkout/Appointment questions after transaction screen drop-down list in the General System Settings window : The Treatment Plan Scheduling Wizard window is displayed. To schedule the treatment group, click OK . The Treatment Plan Scheduling window is displayed. Click OK , select the appropriate information, and click Close . If Automatically process is selected from the Insurance/Walkout/Appointment questions after transaction screen drop-down list in the General System Settings window : The Treatment Plan Scheduling Wizard window is displayed. To schedule the treatment group, click OK . The Treatment Plan Scheduling window is displayed. Click OK , select the appropriate information, and click Close . The Submit Pre-Auths window is displayed. Enter the appropriate information, and click OK . The Transaction Submission window is displayed. Select the appropriate dates, and click OK . The Claim window is disp

topics.title: Setting Up Practice and Provider Information
topics.body: Setting Up Practice and Provider Information eServices Setting Up Practice and Provider Information Before you can use eServices, you must set up the following information that will appear on claims and billing statements: Provider Practice To set up or verify this information, see Entering Practice Information and Handling Provider Information . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Payment Allocation
topics.body: Setting Up Payment Allocation Billing and Receivables > Understanding Payment Allocation Setting Up Payment Allocation To set up payment allocation: Select an option: Select List > Provider . In any window, press Ctrl + D. The Provider List window is displayed in FIND mode. Type the provider�s name in the Find field and press Enter. Double-click the provider record. The Provider window is displayed. On the Information tab, select the doctor to receive collections for this provider from the Assign Collections to drop-down list. Example : To assign all collections produced by a doctor to the doctor who owns the practice, select the practice owner from the list. Select the doctor identification number to print on insurance forms as the billing (collecting) provider from the Dr on Insurance forms drop-down list. If you do not assign a provider, the doctor associated with the patient record is printed on the forms. Click OK . For more information, see Handling Provider Information . Click Close twice . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Recalls
topics.body: Setting Up Recalls Recalling Patients Setting Up Recalls You can set up the following systems: Normal Recall : Use the Normal Recall system to recall patients for routine exams. User-Defined : Use the User-Defined Recall system to recall patients for exams that are not routine to your practice. Motivated Recall : Use the Motivated Recall system to send different notices to patients based on the recall notice number. Related Topics Setting Up the Normal Recall System Setting Up the User-Defined Recall System Determining a Patient�s Normal Recall Date Determining a Patient�s User-Defined Recall Date Setting Normal Recalls for a Patient Setting User-Defined Recalls for a Patient Setting Alternating Recalls Editing Recall Postcards Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Security
topics.body: Setting Up Security System Security and HIPAA Compliance Setting Up Security When the software is installed , U ser ID 1 is automatically assigned the name Doctor and the password Doctor12. This user has the highest level of security rights. Only User ID 1 can change security settings and add users. When you decide who will be responsible for creating and maintaining user login information, change the name and password of User ID 1. If you are upgrading from a version of the software prior to v8.0, the login name and password of User ID 1 are set the same as the previous Access Level 0.
