To properly set up your security system, you must perform the following: Enable system security : If the system security is not enabled, all users can perform any action, regardless what other security settings you have made. Add users : Assign each system user a unique login and password. This process enables you to restrict the actions each user may perform and to track actions using audit trails. Set up user�s rights : Assign a set of rights to each user. For example, the program includes a set of templates already prepared for positions such as Front Desk User, Office Manager, and Clinical Staff. You can alter these templates to suit your needs or create your own templates. Set up security overrides (Optional) : You can set up an override that enables users to perform restricted functions. To access the security windows, s elect System > Change System Settings > System Security . The Security menu is displayed. Tip If the doctor has top-level security privileges and uses the computer throughout the day, create a second log-in name with lower-level security for everyday use. Save the top-level login for the times when you need it, and safeguard your data! Related Topics U sing the Rights by User List Window Using the Rights by User Window Using the Users By Rights Window Tasks with Limited Access Enabling System Security Creating and Modifying User Templates Adding Users Editing a User Login Viewing Users by Security Task Access Setting Security Overrides Changing Logins Changing User Passwords Recovering a Password Without Exiting SoftDent Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting a Default Printer
topics.body: Setting a Default Printer Setting a Default Printer To select a default printer for a specific report category: Select System > Change System Settings > Printer And WP . The System Settings - Printer and Word Processor window is displayed. Select the printer from the Available Printers drop-down list next to the type of report needed. Click OK . topics.title: Setting Up a Report Manager Report
topics.body: Setting Up a Report Manager Report Handling Reports > Generating Reports Using Report Manager Setting Up a Report Manager Report Setting up a report involves specifying the report criteria and output destination (screen, printer, file, and so on) in the Report Manager. Once a report has been set up, it can be added to any report group you want to create. You can include user-selected patient and account reports in your report groups. To create a report group: Select Reports > Report Manager > Set up a Report Group . A status window is displayed, giving a brief explanation of the Report Manager feature. Click OK . The Report Group Setup window is displayed. Type the name of the new report group and click OK . The Reports drop-down list is displayed. Note that Batch Select Mode is marked. This indicates that you are defining reports which will run as part of a batch and that you are still in the Report Manager feature. Select a report. The Output Options window is displayed. Verify that Printer is selected and click OK . The report setup window is displayed in batch mode. Even though you are specifying the printer as the output destination, the report will not print immediately because you are in Batch Select mode. Type the information. Note that some reports such as accounting reports, recall/appointment reports, and practice management reports enable you to enter a date to determine what information is included in the report. To avoid having to change the date each time one of these reports is run, use the following values in the date fields: Type To Include Information MM/DD/YY for the current run date MM/01/YY for the first day of the current month MM/99/YY through the last day of the current month MM/-1/YY from the previous day MM/+1/YY for the following day +1/01/YY through +1/99/YY (The monthly variable can be set up to nine months in advance.) for the following month (EXAMPLE: To include information for six months from now, enter +6/01/YY through +6/99/YY.) -1/01/YY through -1/99/YY (The monthly variable can be set up to nine months prior to the current date.) for the previous month (EXAMPLE: To include information that occurred three months ago, enter -3/01/YY through -3/99/YY.) Here are some examples of how you might set up your reports: If the Daily Operatory Schedule is part of your daily batched reports, set the date to MM/+1/YY, since you want this report to print for the following day. To run next month�s recall reports as part of a batch file, set the date range from +1/01/YY to +1/99/YY. If you are running a Missed Appointments Report and need data from the previous day, enter MM/-1/YY. After providing the necessary information, click OK . The Report Output window is displayed. Type the number of copies you want to print and click Next . A message window is displayed, asking if you want to add another scheduled report to the group. If you select Yes , repeat Steps 4 through 6. If you select No , the Group Setup window is displayed. In the Frequency field, select Daily , Weekly , or Monthly from the drop-down list. In the Next Run field, click the arrow to display a calendar and select the date when the report group should run next (the field defaults to the current date), or type the date. The software automatically enters the day of the week. Click OK . The date you type here should not be confused with the date you entered in step 5 above. The date in step 5 applies to a single report; the date in this step applies to a

topics.title: Setting Up An Umbrella Insurance Company
topics.body: Setting Up An Umbrella Insurance Company Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Company Information Setting Up An Umbrella Insurance Company This topic lists the steps for adding an umbrella insurance company and for linking new and existing insurance plans to that company, which is helpful in posting bulk insurance payments. Adding an Umbrella Insurance Company To add an umbrella insurance company: Open the Insurance Company List window. Click Add . Add a new insurance company record and name it the same as the company name followed by a generic, capitalized identifier such as �MAIN, MASTER, or BULK.� Click OK . It is not necessary to enter any other information in the Insurance Company window of an umbrella company. Linking an Insurance Plan to an Umbrella Insurance Company To link an insurance plan to an umbrella insurance company: Open an Insurance Plan window. Select the umbrella insurance company from the Insurance Co: drop-down list. Select the insurance company with the address to which you mail claims from the Mail To: drop-down list. Linking Existing Plans to Umbrella Insurance Companies Linking umbrella insurance companies to existing insurance plans is helpful when posting bulk checks from companies that send checks from one central location but have multiple locations to which claims are submitted. To link an existing plan to an umbrella insurance company: From the Insurance Company List window, type the name of the insurance company in the Find field (for example, Aetna). If you have several listings for the insurance company, you will need to repeat the following steps for each one. From the Express bar, select Show Ins Plans . Highlight the insurance plan and click Edit . In the Insurance Plan window, select the umbrella insurance company from the Insurance Co: field drop-down list. Select the insurance company with the address to which you mail claims from the Mail To: field drop-down list. Click OK . Click Close . Repeat these steps for each insurance plan listed for this company. To view a printable job aid about the setting up an umbrella insurance company, see Setting Up Umbrella Insurance Companies . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Insurance Plans
topics.body: Setting Up Insurance Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Setting Up Insurance Plans This topic identifies the fields you should complete when adding various insurance plan types in SoftDent. The information required for each insurance plan type�Indemnity, PPO, DMO/Capitation, or Medicaid�varies when combined with the insurance estimation type. For All Plan Types Set up the bluebook with plan fees. In the Patient window, type the Insurance ID in the Insurance ID field. Check with eServices for specific Sub Type. Flat Fee Plans On the Information tab, complete the following fields: Estimation Type�Select Flat Fee . Sub Type�Select Indemnity . Percentage Plans On the Information tab, complete the following fields: Estimation Type�Select Percentage . Sub Type�Select Indemnity . PPO Plans On the Information tab, complete the following fields: Estimation Type�Select either % PPO or Flat Fee PPO . Sub Type�Select Preferred Provider Organization . Risk Write-Off�Type the write-off percentage. Post Write Off at time of service�Select this option to post the write-off amount when a procedure is posted. Link an allowance table to the plan to create the bluebook automatically. DMO Plans On the Information tab, complete the following fields: Estimation Type�Select DMO . Sub Type�Select Dental Maintenance Organization . Anniversary Month�Select None if there is no anniversary month. Maximum Coverage�Deselect this option if there is no maximum coverage. Charge Per Visit�Type the charge per visit as specified by the plan. On the Coverage tab, select each procedure code range that is covered, and type each copay amount. On the Claim Filing tab, deselect the Print Form option if no insurance form is required to print. Medicaid Plans On the Information tab, complete the following fields: Estimation Type�Select Medicaid . Sub Type�Select Medicaid . Anniversary Month�Select None if there is no anniversary month. Maximum Coverage�Deselect this option if there is no maximum coverage. On the Coverage tab, select each procedure code range that is covered, and enter each copay amount. On the Claim Filing tab, complete the following fields: Print UCR�Select this option to print your usual fees on the claim form. Treat Patient as Guarantor�Select this option to have the patient display as the responsible party on the insurance claims. To view a printable job aid about setting up insurance plans, see Setting Up Insurance Plans . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Account Budget Plans
topics.body: Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Account Budget Plans Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Account Budget Plans When using ePayments, you can set up recurring credit card payments for account budget plans to automatically submit monthly credit card payments toward fixed loan account budget plan balances. After setting up recurring credit card payments, a transaction for each credit card payment is created and displayed on the CC Records tab of the loan window. Additionally, you can void payments on this tab. Recurring credit card payments are sent for authorization when you generate the Credit Card Settlement report. topics.title: Setting Up Books
topics.body: Setting Up Books Setting Up the Scheduler Setting Up Books The rooms in your practice can be grouped into books for convenience in scheduling. When you open the Daily Appointments (Daybook) window, the rooms that are displayed are determined by the open book. Auto-Scheduling is more effective when you use books. Example : If you set up a book with the preferred rooms and hours for a doctor, the auto scheduler finds only time slots within those parameters. Every office has a default book, created by the Scheduler Setup Wizard, that runs automatically when the software is installed. The default book for each office contains: All the rooms for the office. Hours the office is open. You can create other books to facilitate the specific needs of your office. Example : You can create a book that contains different rooms for different days of the week. Each person in the office can have a book containing only the information he needs to access. You can: Color-code books to ensure that different rooms are easily identified. Use room colors to identify special purpose rooms, such as hygiene rooms or rooms assigned to a particular doctor. In a multi-office installation: Room colors can be used to identify the office to which the room is assigned. The same room can be identified by different colors in different books. An appointment can be scheduled in any book, and shows up in all books that contain the room in which the appointment is scheduled. Related Topics Creating New Books Editing Books Viewing Room Settings Deleting Books Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Holidays
topics.body: Setting Up Holidays Setting Up the Scheduler Setting Up Holidays You can set up to 19 holidays. In the Setup Holidays window, you can specify: A range of dates that your office is closed Whether the holiday is recurring Holidays are displayed in the following windows: Daily Appointments (Daybook) By Book (Book At A Glance) Month At A Glance History Appointments A warning is displayed when you attempt to schedule an appointment on a holiday. If color scheduling is enabled, holidays are displayed in the assigned color. Related Topics Setting Up New Holidays Editing Holidays Deleting Holidays Resetting Holidays Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up the Scheduler
topics.body: Setting Up the Scheduler Setting Up the Scheduler Before scheduling patient appointments, you must define your default views for the following windows: Daily Appointments (Daybook) By Book (Book At A Glance) Month At A Glance History Appointments Tickler Entry Scheduler Books InOffice Setup Holidays Before scheduling patient appointments, you must also define your default views for: Office hours Operatory information (rooms) Screen displays Example : To display the Daily Appointments (Daybook) window when you start the software, select Daily Appointments in the Startup Preferences window. If you performed a new installation, the Scheduler Setup Wizard is displayed the first time you access the software. Related Topics Setting Scheduler Defaults Setting Office Hours Setting Up Holidays Setting Up Books Setting the Auto-Scheduling Book Setting Provider Office Hours and Vacation Days Using Blocked Time Slots Setting Up Color and Goal Scheduling Setting Up Color Scheduling Painting the Daybook Goal Scheduling Copying Colors and Goals Tracking Goals Limiting Access to Goal Information Running the Practice Barometers Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Sharing Feedback
topics.body: Sharing Feedback Finding Answers Sharing Feedback You can provide feedback about our products or our documentation in the following ways: Connect with us on The Exchange! Use The Exchange to find answers to questions about our software, make suggestions, and interact with other members of the community, including support representatives and trainers. Complete the Product Documentation and Online Help questionnaire Fill out our questionnaire to tell us about your experiences with our documentation and share ideas on how we could improve it. Related Topics Finding Answers Using Online Help Accessing the Carestream Dental Institute Accessing The Exchange Making a Support Call Related Resources SoftDent Practice Management Software Tutorial Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Displaying Clinical Notes
topics.body: Displaying Clinical Notes Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Clinical Notes Displaying Clinical Notes To display clinical note information on the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container: On the Restorative, Perio, Exams, or Treatment Planning tab in the Chart for window, click the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select Other located in the History column of the Filter Dialog section. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Showing Presentations
topics.body: Showing Presentations Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Handling Case Presentations Showing Presentations To show a case presentation from the Documents for window: Select the presentation from the Case Presentation Module Presentations section of the tree. Right-click, and select Show Presentation . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Lengthening and Shortening Appointments
topics.body: Lengthening and Shortening Appointments Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows > Handling Appointments in the Daily Appointments Window Lengthening and Shortening Appointments You can lengthen or shorten an appointment time slot allocation. To change the length of an appointment in the Daily Appointments window: Select the appointment. A small black square is displayed in the lower-right corner of the appointment. Select an option: To reduce the appointment time by one time slot : Select Shorten from the Options section of the Express bar or from the Options menu. To lengthen the appointment by one time slot : Select Lengthen from the Options section of the Express bar or from the Options menu. To change the appointment start time : Drag the square to the appropriate start time. To extend the appointment length : Drag the square to the appropriate end time. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Short Call List Report
topics.body: Running the Short Call List Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports Running the Short Call List Report The Short Call List report generates a list of patients willing to move the scheduled appointment time if an earlier appointment is available. Select the Short Call checkbox in the Appointment Card or Tickler Entry windows. Sample Report To generate the Short Call List report: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Short Call List . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Short Call Report Setup window is displayed. Select an option: To include normal recall patients: Select Hyg Recall . To include other recall patients: Select Other Recall . To include non-recall patients: Select Non Recall . To include short recall tickler entries: Select Tickler File . To include appointment short call tickler entries: Select Appointments . To restrict the results to a specific appointment length: Type the number in the Include Entries with Appointment Length field. To restrict the results to a provider: Type the identification number in the Providers section. Click OK . Related Topics Daily Reports: Morning Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Signing In to The Exchange
topics.body: Signing In to The Exchange Finding Answers > Accessing The Exchange Signing In to The Exchange To sign in to The Exchange: Click The Exchange or type https://community.carestreamdental.com/t5/SoftDent-Discussion/bd-p/softdentdiscussion into the Internet browser address field. The SoftDent Discussion page is displayed. Click Sign in . The Sign In window is displayed. Type your user name in the Username field. Type your password in the Password field. Click Sign In . You can now participate in the community by asking/answering questions, making suggestions, and so on. Related Topics Accessing The Exchange Registering for The Exchange Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing the Six Room Dot Matrix Operatory Schedule Report
topics.body: Printing the Six Room Dot Matrix Operatory Schedule Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Handling the Daily Operatory Schedule Report Printing the Six Room Dot Matrix Operatory Schedule Report The Six Room Dot Matrix Operatory Schedule report prints the appointments for up to six operatories in a compressed format. Medical alerts and appointment notes can be printed on a separate page. Sample Report To print the Six Room Dot Matrix Operatory Schedule report: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Daily Op Schedule > Dot Matrix Daily Op Schedule > Six Room Dot Matrix Op Schedule . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Six Room Operatory Schedule Report Setup window is displayed. Click the drop-down arrow to select a date other than today's date in the Date section. Type the operatory number for the operatory on which you want to report, or leave 999 to report on all operatories. Select the Print Appointments & Medical Alerts checkbox to display additional information for specified patients. More... An "N� followed by a number (for example: N10) indicates an appointment note. An "A" followed by a number (for example: A5) indicates a medical alert. If there is a note and a medical alert, the "A" notation is displayed, since the medical alert is usually more important. Notes and alerts display at the end of the report. The patient name is displayed in capital letters if the appointment was confirmed. Select the Use Page Breaks checkbox to specify whether to repeat room titles if the report extends beyond one page. Type the number of copies of the schedule to print in the Copies section. Click OK . Related Topics Printing the Dot Matrix Daily Operatory Schedule Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Slot Notes
topics.body: Using Slot Notes Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows Using Slot Notes Slot notes c an be added only from the Daily Appointments window and are created and handled like blocked slots, but the note is not displayed when an appointment is scheduled in the slot. You can auto schedule appointments in slots that contain notes, and these slots are included in time usage reports. You can add a note to open, scheduled, or blocked slots. If a note is associated with a slot that contains an appointment, a small red mark is displayed at the right side of the slot. You can also view slot notes in the History Appointments window if your office does not use the Remove Slot Notes from Scheduler option. See Setting Scheduler Defaults for more information. To add, edit, or delete a note to a time slot: Select a time slot in the Daily Appointments window, right-click, and select Add Note from the submenu; or select Slot Notes > Add Note from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Assistant window is displayed. Type a note in the window. Select an option: To change the note text color for the slot : Click the square next to Foreground. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . To change the background for the slot : Click the square next to Background. The Color window is displayed. Select a color and click OK . Click OK . To display the note in a series of slots, select it and drag the square in the lower-right corner to the appropriate location. Drag the corner down to extend the note downward, or up to extend the note upwards. To edit a slot note, select a time slot, right-click, and select Edit Note ; or select Slot Notes > Edit Note from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Assistant window is displayed. Make changes and click OK . To delete a slot note, right-click, and select Delete Note from the submenu; or select Slot Notes > Delete Note from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Statement Printing Preferences
topics.body: Setting Statement Printing Preferences Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Setting Statement Printing Preferences To set billing statement printing preferences: Select System > Change System Settings > Billing Statements . The Billing Statement Settings window is displayed. Click Printer Preferences . The Printing Preferences window is displayed. Select an option: To print the practice name on statements, deselect Practice name is preprinted on statements . To print 30-, 60-, and 90-day dunning messages for overdue accounts, select Print 30, 60, 90 day dunning messages . The message is determined by the oldest overdue amount on the account. Example: If an account has 30- and 90- day overdue amounts, the 90-day dunning message is printed. To print the insurance claim number, select Display Claim Number next to transactions involving insurance . To print the running balance, select Display Running Balance . To print the insurance claim status next to billing statement transactions, select Display "Waiting Ins" or "Ins Complete" next to transactions involving insurance. To automatically post transaction code 103000 in the Transactions window, select Post a statement sent code to the account . To print sent codes on statements, select Display statement sent codes on statements . Selecting this option gives the patient a record of the statements sent. To print statements by zip code, select Print statements in zip code order (otherwise name order) . Deselect the option to print statements alphabetically. To print transaction code 103000.50 if the insurance balance is transferred to the account, select Show old Insurance Transfer Code (103000.50) on statements . To print the usual and customary rate (UCR), walk-out statements, and treatment plans for DMO/HMO, select For DMO/HMO plans, show UCR on bills, walkouts, and treatment plans . To consolidate distributed insurance check payments into a single line entry, select Make distributed insurance checks one entry on statement . Type the number of days within which posted transactions are printed in the Show transactions for the last _ days field. To specify the maximum number of pages to send when using Automated Statements, select Not to exceed and type the maximum number of pages to send. To avoid the additional page charge for electronic statements, type 1 to minimize the number of statement pages sent to the clearinghouse. Click OK . The Billing Statement Settings window is displayed. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Sending Bulk E-mails
topics.body: Sending Bulk E-mails Using E-mail and WebLink > Sending an E-mail Sending Bulk E-mails You can send a bulk e-mail to all individuals who have addresses stored in the software. To send a single e-mail to multiple recipients: Select I nternet > Bulk E-Mail . The Bulk E-Mail - Mass Distribution window is displayed. Select the record types to which the e-mail is addressed from the Recipient Types drop-down list. To attach a document to the e-mail, click Attached . The Attach File window is displayed. Search for and select the document to attach, and click Open . To send all records without an address to the Contact Expert, select Send Messages w/o Addresses to Contact Expert , and type a title in the Title field. To exclude inactive records from the bulk e-mail, select Exclude Inactive Records . Type information in the Subject and Message fields. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Sending Data Back to SoftDent
topics.body: Sending Data Back to SoftDent Using PracticeToGo Sending Data Back to SoftDent Through the SoftDent Commands feature, PracticeToGo can transfer blocked slots, slot notes, appointment cancellations, reminder memos, patient and referring doctor record updates, and prescription refill requests back to SoftDent from the Palm organizer or Microsoft Outlook. When you insert a SoftDent Command in an appointment, patient, referring doctor, or prescription record line in the Palm device or Microsoft Outlook, PracticeToGo will transfer information from the record to the PC SoftDent software during the next synchronization. Related Topics SoftDent Commands and the Palm Organizer SoftDent Commands and Microsoft Outlook Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Sending an E-mail Message
topics.body: Sending an E-mail Message Using E-mail and WebLink Sending an E-mail You can e-mail p atient images, charts, and other documents to labs, referring doctors, insurance companies, and patients. You can send an e-mail from any of the following windows: Account Patient Provider Referring Doctor Employer Insurance Company Insurance Plan Claim Pharmacy Documents For Chart For Other Facility Before attempting to send e-mail messages and attachments : You must establish your e-mail program as the Messaging Application Program Interface (MAPI) client. For more information, contact your system administrator. To send an e-mail message or attachment: Select an option: In the appropriate window : Select Send an e-mail in the Power Bar. In the appropriate List window : Select a record and select Send an e-mail in the Power Bar. The SoftDent E-Mail window is displayed. Select an option: To send the e-mail to the primary insurance company, primary insurance plan, a lab that has an open lab case, or a referring doctor associated with the patient : Select the party from the Recipient drop-down list. The Recipient drop-down list is only enabled if you access this window from the Patient List, Patient, Claim List, or Claim windows. When this drop-down list is enabled, the recipient�s name and e-mail address are automatically displayed in the appropriate fields. To send the e-mail to another party : Type the recipient�s name in the Name field and the e-mail address in the Address field. To carbon copy or blind copy another e-mail recipient, type the e-mail address in the appropriate field. Type the information in the Subject and Message fields. If a recipient is selected, a recipient greeting and a list of attachments and file locations are automatically added to the field. A tree view of all documents, images, and charts associated with the record is displayed in the Documents and Images section. Hold the cursor over an image or document to view details. Select an option: To attach a document or image to the e-mail : Select the object and click Add . Hold the cursor over an image in the Attached Images section to view details. You can attach documents to an e-mail only if the feature is accessed in the Patient List, Patient, Claim List, or Claim windows. To remove an image from the Attached Images section : Right-click the image and select Remove Image . To view the e-mail, click MAPI Client. To send the e-mail, click OK (Send) . Select an option: To add a contact for the e-mail : Click Yes . The Contacts for window is displayed. Type the appropriate information in the fields, and click OK . To close the message : Click No . Related Topics S ending E-mails from a Patient Chart E-mailing from the Document Window Sending Bulk E-mails Editing an E-mail Signature E-mailing Dental Imaging Software Images Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Single Fee Schedule Report
topics.body: Running the Single Fee Schedule Report Handling Transactions and Codes > Transaction Code Reports Running the Single Fee Schedule Report The Single Fee Schedule report lists transaction codes and fees for a fee schedule. The transaction code, office code, description, and fee for the selected codes in the fee schedule are included in the list. Sample Report To run the Single Fee Schedule report: Select Reports > ADA/Trans/Diag Codes > Single Fee Schedule . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The ADA Transaction Codes Report Setup window is displayed. In the Subtitle section, type a subtitle for the report, if desired. In the Codes section, type the code ranges to include in the report. To specify a single code, type the code in the left field and type 0 in the right field. Select an option: To include inactive codes in the report: Select Include Inactive Codes . To exclude codes with no fee: Select Exclude codes with a fee of $0 . In the Fee Schedule field, type a fee schedule number, if necessary. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Notes for a Specific Record
topics.body: Viewing Notes for a Specific Record Using TeamTalk Viewing Notes for a Specific Record To view a list of TeamTalk notes in the Account List, Account, Patient List, Patient, Employer List, School List, Employer, School, Insurance Plan List, or the Insurance Plan window, select an option: To view the TeamTalk notes associated with the account database : In the Account List or Account window, select TeamTalk from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The TeamTalk - Account notes window is displayed. To view the TeamTalk notes associated with the patient database : In the Patient List or Patient window, click TeamTalk located at the top of the window. The TeamTalk - Patient notes window is displayed. To view the TeamTalk notes associated with the insurance plan database : In the Insurance Plan List or Insurance Plan window, select TeamTalk from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The TeamTalk - Plan notes window is displayed. To view TeamTalk notes associated with the employer/school database : In the Employer List, School List, Employer, or School windows, select TeamTalk from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The appropriate TeamTalk notes window is displayed. In each TeamTalk note window, you can view the notes associated with a record or for a database. You can also edit notes and any associated conditions in these windows. For more information see Using TeamTalk Options . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Suspending and Deleting Claims
topics.body: Suspending and Deleting Claims eServices > Using the eClaims Service Suspending and Deleting Claims When you suspend a claim , y ou remove it from the current transmission, and the claim is displayed in the Claim Submission List window the next time you load claims. To suspend a claim, select an option: In the Claim Submission List window : Select the claim, and click Stop Claim . Select the claim in the Claim Submission List window and from the main menu : Select Submit Data > Suspend . To delete a claim and place it in the batch print file, select an option: In the Claim Submission List window : Select the claim, and click Delete Claim . Select the claim in the Claim Submission List window and from the main menu : Select Submit Data > Delete . A message asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . The claim is removed from the batch. You can print deleted claims later. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Searching the Online Help
topics.body: Searching the Online Help Finding Answers > Using Online Help Searching the Online Help To search for information in the online help: Select Help > SoftDent Help . The SoftDent Practice Management Software Help window is displayed. Click Search . The Search tab is displayed. Type the keyword for which you want to search and press Enter . Select a topic from the list and click Display . Related Topics Using Online Help Accessing Online Help Printing an Online Help Topic Adding a Topic as a Favorite Viewing Help Demos Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Searching for and Viewing Patient Information
topics.body: Searching for and Viewing Patient Information Using the Phone Center Searching for and Viewing Patient Information To access additional patient information using the phone center: Type the patient name in the Find field, or scroll through the list to locate the patient. A patient list is displayed. Select the patient from the list. Select an option: To view the patient record : Click Information . To view patient guarantor or insurance information : Click Insurance Info . To view a list of patient appointments (past, scheduled, and missed) : Click Scheduling . To view a list of patient prescriptions : Click Prescription . To view a list of patient contacts : Click Contacts . To add an appointment for a new patient : Click New Patient . To view the patient transactional history : Click Transactions . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Searching for Records
topics.body: Searching for Records Getting Started Searching for Records You can search for a record from the: List menu Power bar button File menu When you search for a record from the List menu or Power bar button, Find is displayed at the top of the window. For more information, see Using Find to Search for a Record . When you search for a record from the File menu, select the record type from the list. The Find window for the record type is displayed. For more information, see Using Find to Search for a Record . Related Topics Using Find to Search for a Record Using the File Menu to Search for a Record Using the List Menu to Search for a Record Using the Power Bar to Search for a Record Need more information? topics.body: Starting the Merge Process from Records Using Experts > Merging Letter Expert Documents > Starting the Merge Process Starting the Merge Process from Records You can combine data in a record with a previously created template and create quick letters for a specific record without having to retype letter text. To start the merge process from a record: Select Letter Expert from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Starting the Merge Process from Reports
topics.body: Starting the Merge Process from Reports Using Experts > Merging Letter Expert Documents > Starting the Merge Process Starting the Merge Process from Reports You can merge report results with a template. To start the merge process from a report: Select the report from the Reports menu. The Output Options window is displayed. Select Merge Letter Expert . Click OK . Need more information?
