topics.title: Setting Patient Visit Statistics Report Properties
topics.body: Setting Patient Visit Statistics Report Properties Setting Patient Visit Statistics Report Properties You can set patient check-in time constraints and the expected wait average before a patient is seated in the operatory. When the report is generated, these settings are compared to actual office performance. To set Patient Visit Statistics report properties: Select Reports > Practice Management > Patient Visit Statistics . The Patient Visit Statistics window is displayed. Click Properties . The Patient Visit Statistics Properties window is displayed. Type the seated late and seated very late minutes. These times are used to calculate the percentage of patients seated on time, seated late, or seated very late. Move a patient to the operatory before the seated late time to seat them on time. Type the expected number of minutes a patient should arrive before an appointment in the Patient considered tardy if not checked in_ minutes prior to appointment time. field. The check-in time is used to calculate the percentage of on time and late patients. The patient is late if not checked in before the check-in time. To exclude late patients, select Exclude tardy patients from the computation . Type the expected average time a patient waits before being seated in the operatory in the Avg Wait field. If the average wait time is exceeded or the percentage of patients seated on time is not met, the provider information is displayed in blue text. Type the expected percentage of patients who are seated on time in the operatory in the % Seated On Time field. Click OK . The Patient Visit Statistics window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Billing Statement Defaults
topics.body: Setting Billing Statement Defaults Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Setting Billing Statement Defaults To set billing statement defaults: Select System > Change System Settings > Billing Statements . The Billing Statement Settings window is displayed. Click Edit . Type the minimum amount due for which statements are generated in the Minimum Amount Due to send statement field. Select your preferences: To generate statements for accounts with outstanding insurance claims and at least the minimum amount due, select Send Statement if there is at least the minimum amount due. To generate statements for accounts with outstanding insurance claims but no amount due, select Send Statement if there is no amount due. To generate a statement asking for payment of the amount due, select Ask for payment of amount due ; (otherwise ask for full account balance). Type the annual percentage rate to calculate the monthly percentage rate automatically and vice versa. To set up billing charges for overdue accounts, type the amount in the Apply to overdue accounts field and select the number of days an account must be overdue before the charge amount is applied from the For accounts overdue more than _ days drop-down list. Example: If you type 10.00 and 60, a $10.00 billing charge is applied to accounts that are overdue more than 60 days. To set up finance charges, do the following: Type the monthly or annual percentage rate. Select the number of days accounts must be overdue before charges are applied in the For accounts overdue more than _ days drop-down list. To disable finance charges, select 0 . Charges are not disabled for account user codes F3, F6, or F9. For more information, see Using Finance Charge User Codes . Type the minimum account balance to which charges are applied in the Minimum Account Balance to Apply field. The minimum balance must be at least $0.01 more than the minimum amount due. To bill only the largest amount, select Use Billing OR Finance Charge, whichever is greater . To apply both charges, deselect the option. Do one of the following: To request payment within a specific number of days of the billing date, select days from billing date _ and type the number of days in the field. To request payment on a specific day of the month, select day of the month _ and type the day in the field. To exclude a date by which payment is due, select do not show Due By date . To send one statement within a specified number of days, select Enable Cycle Billing and type the number of days between billing statements in the Cycle Billing Period (minimum number of days between statements for an account) field. Billing Statement Generation Cycle Cycle Billing Period Statement Receipt Weekly 25 - 28 days If you send out billing statements every week, type a number within this range in the Cycle Billing Period (minimum number of days between statements for an account) field to ensure that a patient does not receive two billing statements within a 25- to 28-day cycle. Bi-Monthly 15 - 16 days If you send out billing statements every two weeks, type a number within this range in the Cycle Billing Period (minimum number of days between statements for an account) field to ensure that a patient does not receive two billing statements within a 30-day cycle. Monthly 7 days or less If you send out billing statements once a month, type a number within this range in the Cycle Billing Period (minimum number of days between statements for an acco

topics.title: Creating Statement Messages
topics.body: Creating Statement Messages Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Creating Statement Messages You can create messages that can be printed on billing and walkout statements for accounts with current, 30-, 60-, and 90-day overdue balances. To print these messages the Print 30, 60, 90 day dunning messages checkbox must be selected in the Printing Preferences window. See Setting Statement Printing Preferences . To create a message to print on billing and walkout statements: Select System > Change System Settings > Billing Statements . The Billing Statement Settings window is displayed. Select Messages from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Statement Messages window is displayed. Type a message for each statement in the appropriate fields. Only the Top form #1210 and laser statements have enough space to print two lines of message text. If you use any other statement, limit your message to one line. Click OK . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Storing Backup Media
topics.body: Storing Backup Media Backing Up Data Storing Backup Media Make sure you store your backups in a safe place. For all types of media, you should avoid excessive heat or moisture. Avoid magnets of any type; they can erase data. Always follow the manufacturer�s backup, storage, and replacement guidelines. It is recommended that you keep one or more backups off site. This should be your most recent backup and should be rotated on a daily basis. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Startup Preferences
topics.body: Setting Startup Preferences Getting Started > Starting the Software Setting Startup Preferences You can set the default startup window, office expert item display frequency, patient and account amounts due when posting personal payment codes, appointment reminder printer, window refresh frequency, printer for labels, and what information is displayed on the first Patient window tab. To set startup preferences: Select System > Startup Preferences . The Startup Preferences window is displayed. Select the window to display when you access the software from the Display at Startup section. Select an option: To display the SoftDent Clock In and Clock Out windows after logging in or out of the software: Select Clock In (and Clock Out on exit) . To display the Statement Batch Count window if there are batched statements when you access the software : Select Statement Batch Count Window . To display the Practice Central window when you access the software : Select Show Practice Central . To display the Office Expert window when you have office expert items requiring action, select Display Office Expert when items are available . When there are no office expert items requiring attention, the window selected from the Display at Startup section is displayed after log-in. To automatically display a default value, other than $0.00, in the Amount Due field of the Payments window when posting a personal payment, select Default the amount due for patient/account when posting personal payment codes . For more information, see Setting Insurance Defaults . To use a Costar SE250 or EL60 model printer to print appointment reminder cards, select Use Costar SE250 or EL60 Appointment Reminder Printer . If this option is selected, Prompt To Print Reminder Card When Exiting Transaction Screen is enabled. Select this option to display a message, prompting you to print a patient appointment reminder card, after scheduling the next appointment. To dictate which tab is first displayed in the Patient window, select it from the Patient Tab to Display Initially section. Select the tab that contains the information that you most often use. To set how often information is updated in the active window, type the refresh interval in the Refresh view every __ minutes field. To dictate how many characters must be typed in the Find field before starting a search for matching records, type the number of characters in the Start Find after __ characters typed field. If your office uses a Topaz signature pad to capture digital signatures on a patient�s Medical History window, select the Use Topaz Electronic Signature Pad checkbox. To display the Contacts For window when merging a patient record with a letter expert template, select Edit the new contact when sending a record to the Letter Expert . Select the printer used to print labels from the Printer For Labels section of the window. To ensure that information displayed on your workstation is synchronized with information contained on the server upon log-in, select Update Client Files at Startup . This option is only displayed if you are running a Client/Server or Multi-User version of the software and are setting the startup preferences for a workstation. Click OK . The Practice Information window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Time Card Preferences
topics.body: Setting Time Card Preferences Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card > Setting Up Time Card Setting Time Card Preferences Use the Time Card Preferences window to specify the default pay frequency; select how hours worked are displayed; and track lunch, unpaid time, and overtime. You must have Profile Administrator security rights to set time card preferences. To set time card preferences: Select System > Change System Settings > Time Card > Time Card Preferences . The Time Card Preferences window is displayed. In the Defaults section, select a pay frequency from the drop-down list. The frequency you select is the default pay frequency for all employees. If specific employees should not have this frequency, you can select a different frequency in the Employee window for those employees. In the Formatting Options section, select the format you want to use for displaying employee time in the Time Card list and on the Time Card report. In the Tracking Options section, select from the following options: Track Lunch: Select this option to add Out for Lunch and Return From Lunch buttons to the SoftDent Clock In window. Employees can clock out for lunch and clock back in using these buttons, and a Lunch entry is recorded on the time card. In the Lunch Type field, select a lunch type from the drop-down list. Track Unpaid Time: Select this option to display unpaid time on the Time Card report. In the Overtime section, select from the following options: Do not track Overtime: Select this option if you do not want to include overtime on the Time Card report. Track Overtime per Day: Select this option and enter a value in the Overtime begins at: __ hours field to include a subtotal of overtime hours in the Time Card report whenever an employee's total hours for a day exceed the hours you specified. Note : The default value is 8.0. Track Overtime per Week: Select this option and enter a value in the Overtime begins at: __ hours field to include a subtotal of overtime hours in the Time Card report whenever an employee's total hours for the week exceed the hours you specified. Note : The default value is 40.0. In the Week begins on a: field, select the day of the week on which your workweek begins. Note : This field is set to Sunday by default. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Service Tax
topics.body: Setting Up Service Tax Handling Transactions and Codes Setting Up Service Tax SoftDent enables you to apply service tax to ADA procedure codes as they are posted. Before enabling service tax in SoftDent, make sure that service taxes are legal in your state. Two processes are involved when setting up service tax: Enabling Service Tax To enable service tax: Click System > Change System Settings > General System . The General System Settings window is displayed. In the Service Tax Settings section, click the drop-down arrow and select a tax method: Pro Rata�No patients or charges are exempt; tax is assessed on all. Non-Pro Rata�Some patients or charges can be exempt. In the Tax Rate field, type the tax rate. Example: Type 7.00 for a 7% service tax. When you enable service tax, a Taxable field is added to the Information tab for each ADA procedure code. This option is selected by default for all codes. You must deselect the option for codes that should not be taxed. Click OK . Specifying Which ADA Codes Should Not Be Taxable When you enable service tax, a Taxable field is added to the Information tab for each ADA procedure code. This option is selected by default for all codes. To specify that an ADA code should not be taxable: Select List > ADA Codes . The ADA/Transaction Codes List window is displayed. Double-click a code in the list. The Information tab of the ADA/Transaction Codes window is displayed. At the bottom-right of the window, click the Taxable checkbox to remove the check. Click OK . Click Close . If you are using the non-pro rata tax method, you can mark a transaction as taxable or not "on the fly" in the Transaction Detail window by checking or unchecking the Taxable check box in the Charges/Aging section. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up Time Card
topics.body: Setting Up Time Card Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card Setting Up Time Card When setting up the Time Card function, you select a time card type and then select what time card information is tracked and how it is displayed. Related Topics Customizing Time Card Types Setting Time Card Preferences Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Submitting Medical Claims
topics.body: Submitting Medical Claims Submitting and Tracking Claims Submitting Medical Claims Some patients may have medical insurance in addition to, or instead of, dental coverage . Medical insurance often covers oral surgery, TMJ and periodontal procedures, and emergency exams. To submit medical claims, you must use a Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) code and assign a diagnosis code. CPT codes are 10000�99999. Because reimbursement for medical claims is dependent on both diagnosis codes and CPT codes, you can specify up to four diagnosis codes per claim. To submit medical claims: Select List > Patient . Enter the patient�s name in the Find field and press Enter. Click Transactions on the toolbar. The patient�s Transactions window is displayed. From the patient Transactions window, click Submit Insurance on the toolbar. A message prompts you for the range of transaction dates to print on the claim. The first date defaults to the service date of the oldest procedure that has not been submitted, and the second date defaults to the most current date that has not been submitted. Verify the date range and click OK . A message is displayed that informs you that a claim is being processed. Click OK . Related Topics Handling Claims with Multiple Diagnosis Codes Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Submitting Secondary Insurance Claims
topics.body: Submitting Secondary Insurance Claims Submitting and Tracking Claims > Submitting Dental Claims Submitting Secondary Insurance Claims You might encounter a situation where a patient has an existing claim for which a secondary claim can be submitted, but no secondary insurance was applied to the patient before the initial claim was sent. SoftDent enables you to submit insurance claims to both primary and secondary insurance companies. Before you can submit a claim to a secondary insurance company, the primary claim must first be paid and completed. Patient Already Assigned Secondary Insurance To submit a claim for a patient who has already been assigned secondary insurance: Enter the insurance check payment for the patient. Attach the insurance payment to the appropriate primary claim. Submit secondary insurance. Patient Has Not Been Assigned Secondary Insurance, but a Claim is Outstanding To assign secondary insurance for a patient and submit an outstanding claim: Select List > Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click on the patient having secondary insurance coverage. The Patient window is displayed. Click the Insur. tab. Click the Secondary dental insurance drop-down menu. Select the name of the guarantor under which secondary insurance is carried. Ensure that the patient's relationship to guarantor 1 and 2 are selected. Click OK to save the changes. Click Transactions . The Transactions window is displayed. Click Outstanding Ins . Select the claim that needs to submitted to the secondary insurance. Click View Claim . Click Cancel Claim . Click Yes to cancel the claim. Click Yes to delete the claim. Click Close in the Outstanding Insurance window. Click Submit Insurance . This will resubmit the primary insurance. If you have received the final insurance payment, post that payment, or if already posted, post a $0.00 payment and associate it with the new claim number. When the Submit Secondary Insurance Form? prompt displays, click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Submitting Treatment Plans for Pre-Authorization
topics.body: Submitting Treatment Plans for Pre-Authorization Treatment Planning Submitting Treatment Plans for Pre-Authorization You can use the software to submit treatment plans for pre-authorization just as you would posted procedures. This enables you to give the patient an accurate estimate of the patient portion for all procedures before treatment begins. To submit a treatment plan for pre-authorization: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the patient Transactions window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the InOffice window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Click Submit Ins on the toolbar. The Submit Pre-Auths window is displayed. Type the treatment group number in the Treatment Group Number field. The default value for this field is the group number of the selected procedure. To submit all groups for pre-authorization, type 0. Select the treatment items and click OK . The Transaction Submission window is displayed. Select the starting and ending entry dates for procedures being submitted to insurance and click OK . The Claim window is displayed. The Preauthorization field is selected. If the treatment group includes a prosthesis, crown, or inlay, these fields are also selected. Click OK . The Submit Primary window is displayed. Select an option: To print the insurance claim now : Select Do you want to print it now? , type the number of copies to print in the Number of copies to print field, and click OK . To print the insurance claim later : Click OK . The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. An asterisk is displayed in the TP column in the Claims List window for treatment plan pre-authorization claims. These claims are also displayed in blue text. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Summary Report
topics.body: Using the Summary Report eServices > Using Additional eServices Options Using the Summary Report Use the Summary Report option to review the following: Provider information Insurance company information Insurance plans Prosthesis/crown/inlay procedure codes To print a report: In the eServices window, select Report Options > Summary Report . Select the report you want to print. Select an option: To view the report : Click Print Preview . To print the report : Click Print . To print all summary information: Select Report Options > Summary Report > All Summary Info . Click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Suspending a Statement Preview to Resume Later
topics.body: Suspending a Statement Preview to Resume Later Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Suspending a Statement Preview to Resume Later If, while previewing paper or electronic statements, you need to suspend the process, you can close the Paper Statements List or Electronic Statements List window and resume work later. To suspend a statement preview: Follow the steps described under Previewing and Printing Statements . While previewing statements, click Close . A message is displayed, asking if you want to save your changes and resume later. Click Yes . To discard your changes, click No . When you reopen the statement preview, a message is displayed, stating that the last preview session was suspended and asking if you want to resume the suspended session. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Previewing and Printing Statements
topics.body: Previewing and Printing Statements Billing and Receivables > Handling Billing Statements Previewing and Printing Statements While previewing paper and Automated Statements, you can exclude accounts, edit account information, and add notes. To preview and print statements: To preview Automated Statements, select Reports > Accounting > Statements > Electronic > Electronic Batch Preview . The Electronic Statements window is displayed. To preview paper statements, select Reports > Accounting > Statements > Paper > Statement Preview . The Paper Statements window is displayed. Select the starting and ending names to include in the report using the drop-down lists in the Start and End fields. Select from the following options: To apply a different billing charge to overdue accounts: Type the amount in the Billing Charge Setup section. To apply a different finance charge to overdue accounts: Type the amount in the Finance Charge Setup section. To itemize transactions for a specific number of days: Type the number in the Transaction Detail Setup section. To specify the maximum number of pages to send when using eClaims: Select Not to exceed and type the maximum number of pages to send. To avoid the additional page charge for electronic statements, type 1 to minimize the number of statement pages sent to the clearinghouse. To include only accounts owing more than a specific amount: Type the amount in the Send for amount due greater than or equal to _ dollars field. To include only accounts with a specific user code: Type the user code in the Only include Accounts with field. To exclude accounts with a specific user code: Type the code in the Exclude field. Accounts containing C, I, L, and N user codes are not included in report results. To generate statements for a specific provider, select the provider identification number. To include a message, type it in the Statement Message field. Click OK . The Electronic Statements List or Paper Statements List window is displayed. Blue text indicates accounts with invalid addresses, and fuchsia text indicates accounts with no balances. No statements are printed for these accounts. Select from the following options: To sort the list by account name, account balance, or amount due: Click the column label. To exclude a statement: Deselect the checkbox in the Account Name column. To display the additional information: Select the account. The information is displayed in the Additional Information section. To display account user codes attached to the account: Select the account. Any account user codes attached to the account are displayed in the bottom-left corner of the window. To include a statement note: Select the account and type the note in the Personalized Statement Note for Selected Account field. To edit account information, select the account and click Edit Acct . The Account window is displayed. Edit the record and click OK . Select from the following options: To preview statements for specific accounts: Click Preview . To preview statements for all accounts: Click Preview All . To print statements: Click Print . Related Topics Suspending a Statement Preview to Resume Later Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Switching Users
topics.body: Switching Users Getting Started > Using Practice Central > Accessing Information from Practice Central Switching Users To switch users: In the Practice Central window, click Logout/Switch User . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: System Menu Commands
topics.body: System Menu Commands Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Main Menu Bar System Menu Commands U se the System menu to configure system settings, change your log-in and password, switch license files, select forms, set up and maintain fees, configure Patient Statistics report settings, review data file statuses, reset Wizards, update client files, and dictate what special fields can be updated by users. You can also customize medical history, title bars, background colors, discount schedules, and PracticeToGo. The System menu commands are: Change System Settings : Configure settings and options for the following areas: general system, security, billing statements, insurance, recall, scheduler, HIPAA options, contact notes, treatment plan financial arrangement notes, treatment plan comments, customize postcards, CPT code classes, category codes, time card preferences, printer and WP, video capture options, and KIOSK configuration options. Clock In: Display the Clock In window, so you can clock in. Clock Out: Display the Clock Out window, so you can clock out. Change Login : Log into the software as another user. Change User Password : Change the password of the currently logged-in user. Change Working Office : Toggle between the working offices. This command is enabled only if you purchased a multi-office license. Switch License Files : Switch license files. This command is enabled only if your configuration uses multiple license files. Form Selection : Select billing statement, walkout statement, budget coupons, prescription forms, routing slip, treatment plan, electronic statement, and daysheet forms. Fees Maintenance : Copy, change, and update fee schedules; update Bluebook fees, copy Bluebook fees between plans, copy the allowed amount from plan to plan, install or update CDT procedure codes, enable CDT code formatting, and reset the insurance plan CDT version. Patient Visit Statistics: Configure the Patient Visit Statistics reports. File Status : View a list of software data files. Startup Preferences : Select which window is automatically displayed when you start the program and set additional default settings. Reset Wizard Setting : Select which wizards are displayed. Modify Balance : Edit account balance amounts. Special Edit Field Enable : Edit fields that are protected by the software. View Audit Trails : View audit trails for transactions, scheduling, prescriptions, charting, security, PracticeToGo, reports, and archived transactions. View Network Users : View machines used to access the software. Customize Medical History : Add, edit, and delete conditions. Customize Document Categories: Create, edit, and change the display order of document manager categories. Customize Titlebars : Change the appearance of the titlebars. Customize Background : Select the background color displayed in the workspace. PracticeToGo : Select a platform, set user preferences, and configure system settings for PracticeToGo. Amazing Charts: Enable and configure the Amazing Charts software. Modules: Enable, select options, and run reports for additional modules. Referral Categories (1): Create and edit referral categories for use in the Referral Source Analysis report. Discount Schedule : Add, edit, and delete discount schedules. Update Client Files : Select directories that need to be updated. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: System Security and HIPAA Compliance
topics.body: System Security and HIPAA Compliance System Security and HIPAA Compliance Using SoftDent's system security feature enables you to safeguard the following data used to run your practice: Confidential Financial You can: Set up a login name and password for each person who uses your system Control who has rights to perform various actions within the program Specify to which parts of the program each user has access Additionally, certain actions, such as modifying transactions, are recorded in a log by user identification number, enabling you to track when the action was performed and by whom. When a user logs into the program, his or her user name is displayed in the Status bar at the bottom of the window. To fully take advantage of the program�s security features and to ensure audit trail report accuracy, users should always log in using their assigned name. Related Topics Setting Up Security Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features Handling HIPAA Transactions Compliance Running HIPAA Reports Using Audit Trails Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Edit System File Utility
topics.body: Running the Edit System File Utility Utilities > Using File Menu Utilities Running the Edit System File Utility Run this utility to edit the system file. Do not run this utility unless you are instructed to by a technical support representative. To edit the system file: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select File > Edit System File or select Edit System File . The Edit System File window is displayed. Type the correct information in the appropriate fields. After you have made the corrections, click OK . When the technical support representative tells you to, restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating a System Profile
topics.body: Creating a System Profile Getting Started > Using Profiles Creating a System Profile System profiles can be used in two ways: You can create a system profile that functions as the default in your office. All list windows will initially display the same way for all employees, but each employee can set up individual profiles, if desired. You can create a system profile that overrides all other profile types. All list windows display the same way for all employees, and employees cannot make any changes. You must have the Profile Administrator security permission to create or modify system profiles. The Profile Administrator security permission is located in the Miscellaneous security permissions list in the Rights By User window. The permission is also available in the Users By Rights window. This security permission should be assigned only to the user with the highest security permissions (typically User ID 1) and the office manager. To set up a system profile: Select View > List Preferences . The SoftDent List Preferences window is displayed. In the View Profile for: section, click the drop-down arrow and select System from the list. The Apply to all Users option is displayed. Select an option: To prevent employees from making any changes to the profile settings: Select Apply to all Users . A message is displayed, stating that the settings will override any user-defined profiles and asking if you want to continue. Click Yes . To set up a system profile as a default and still allow employees to create individual profiles: Do not select the Apply to all Users option. In the Highlighting Method section, select an option to specify whether the selected entry in the list is highlighted by a colored solid bar or colored outline. In the Highlight/Border Color section, select the color to use for the solid bar or outline selected in the Highlighting Method section. Click Use a custom color to display a color chart, where you can select a specific color. In the Right Click Options section, select an option: Display dialog in view mode: When you right-click on an item in any list, the corresponding window opens in VIEW mode. Display Options menu: When you right-click on an item in any list, the corresponding Options menu is displayed. This is the same menu that is displayed if you press O while the list is open. In the Grid Line Options section, select whether you want to always display grid lines, never use grid lines, or use vertical lines only. Click OK . A window is displayed, asking if the current profile should be saved. Click Yes . To turn off the system profile override and enable employees to create individual profiles: Select View > List Preferences . The SoftDent List Preferences window is displayed. Deselect the Apply to all Users option. A message is displayed, stating that users will be allowed to customize their own settings and asking if you want to continue. Click Yes . Click OK . Related Topics Creating an Individual Profile Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Configuring System Printing Preferences
topics.body: Configuring System Printing Preferences General System Settings Configuring System Printing Preferences The System Printing Preferences window enables you to set preferences for the type of information printed on routing slips, walkout statements, deposit slips, and so on. To configure system printing preferences: Select System > Change System Settings > General System . The General System Settings window is displayed. Click Printer Preferences . The System Printing Preferences window is displayed. In the Routing Slip Preferences section, select from the following options: Use provider preferred routing slips: Select this option to use a provider�s customized routing slip. See Running the Modify Routing Slip Codes Utility for more information. Include Exam History: Select this option to print a patient's exam history on the routing slip. Include Treatment Plan: Select this option to print treatment plan codes on the routing slip if the patient has treatment plan procedures. Include Patient Referrals and Acct Aging: Select this option to include sections with referral and account information on the routing slip. In the Walkout Statement Preferences section, select from the following options: Print Patient Portion for Next Appointment on Walkout: Select this option to print the estimated amount the patient will pay at the next appointment on the walkout statement. Post PATIENT WALKOUT GIVEN code to patient's ledger: Select this option to post code 103000.90�Patient Walkout Given�to the patient's ledger. Post ACCOUNT WALKOUT GIVEN code to account's ledger: Select this option to post code 103000.91�Account Walkout Given�to the account's ledger. Print walkout given codes on account billing statement: Select this option to print the walkout given codes on account billing statements. In the Address Preferences section, click the drop-down arrow next to each type of correspondence and select the address type that should be used. In the ADA Description Preference section, click the drop-down arrow and select the ADA description type to use in correspondence. The office description is printed on insurance forms, statements, and reports and is displayed when you schedule appointments and post transactions. The alternate description is printed on walkouts, statements, and treatment plans. See Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes for more information. In the Archive Preference section, select Archive Printed Letters to keep copies of the letters you print. In the Default Path for Excel Files field, type the file path where Excel files should be stored. In the Deposit Slip Preferences section, select how the check and credit card information is displayed on the deposit slip. Click OK . Related Topics General System Settings Configuring System Phone Preferences Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Enabling System Security
topics.body: Enabling System Security System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Enabling System Security To enable system security, select an option: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Enable System Security . A checkmark is displayed next to the command to indicate the feature is enabled. In any of the Security Overrides windows, select Enable System Security . If system security is not enabled, all users can perform all tasks. The only task reserved for User ID 1 is to modify security settings. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Tabbed Windows
topics.body: Using Tabbed Windows Getting Started Using Tabbed Windows Most data entry is completed in tabbed windows. Tabbed windows divide information into logical categories and have a common area for general information. The Window keystrokes that enable navigation through tabbed windows in ADD or EDIT mode are: Tab : Moves the cursor to the next field. Shift + Tab : Moves the cursor to the previous field. Alt + Enter : Toggles between the active tab and the common area. Ctrl + Tab : Displays the next tab. Enter : Saves new information or settings. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Exams Tab Exams
topics.body: Understanding Exams Tab Exams Charting Exams Understanding Exams Tab Exams When the Exams tab is the active window, you can chart the following exams: Periodontal tissue Occlusal Soft tissue TMJ PSR You can also view an exam summary. Related Topics Viewing the Exam Summary Performing Soft Tissue Exams Performing Periodontal Tissue Exams Performing Occlusal Exams Performing TMJ Exams Performing PSR Exams Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tabulating Questionnaire Results by Question
topics.body: Tabulating Questionnaire Results by Question Handling Patient Questionnaires > Running Patient Questionnaire Reports Tabulating Questionnaire Results by Question To tabulate patient questionnaire results by question and run the Tabulate Results by Question report: Select Reports > Patient > Patient Questionnaire > Tabulate Results By Question . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Questionnaire Results window is displayed. To view a list of questions from a specific category, select the category from the Category drop-down list. Select the question. Select an option: To view the summary for all responses : Select Summary . To view the summary and details for responses : Select Summary and Details . The Show Detail for: section is displayed. Select the option. To restrict the report to a specific administration date, select the dates from the From and To date fields. Click OK . Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Taking Action for Office Expert Items
topics.body: Taking Action for Office Expert Items Using Experts > Using the Office Expert Taking Action for Office Expert Items After you have successfully run an office expert item, you can take action for the records included in the item. Taking Action for an item opens the Contact Expert window. To access the contact expert from the office expert: Select an item from the list. Click Take Action . The Contact Expert window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding the SoftDent Task Manager
topics.body: Understanding the SoftDent Task Manager Using Experts > Using the Office Expert Understanding the SoftDent Task Manager The SoftDent Task Manager: Is installed during the installation process Includes an icon in the lower-right corner of your desktop The Task Manager processes office expert queries. Query results are displayed in the Office Expert Summary section of the Practice Central window and in the Office Expert window. Example : If you set the office expert to run the Account Aging item every night at 11:00 p.m., the office expert contacts the Task Manager to gather the item information from the server. The Task Manager returns the results to the office expert. When you log in the following day, the Office Expert window is displayed, and the item results are displayed in the window. The Task Manager requires a TCP/IP connection. If the Task Manager is not running, you must restart it from the server. The SoftDent Task Manager window is displayed when you click the SoftDent Task Manager�s icon in the lower-right corner of the desktop, and enables you to view the SoftDent Task Manager log. Related Topics Viewing the SoftDent Task Manager Log Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Toolbar Options in a List Window
topics.body: Using Toolbar Options in a List Window Getting Started > Using List Windows Using Toolbar Options in a List Window The following toolbar buttons are displayed at the top of most list windows: Edit Add Delete Find These buttons enable you to add, modify, delete, and view database records. Related Topics Adding a Record Editing a Record Deleting a Record Finding a Record Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tabulating Questionnaire Results by Questionnaire
topics.body: Tabulating Questionnaire Results by Questionnaire Handling Patient Questionnaires > Running Patient Questionnaire Reports Tabulating Questionnaire Results by Questionnaire To tabulate patient questionnaire results by questionnaire and run the Tabulate Results by Questionnaire report: Select Reports > Patient > Patient Questionnaire > Tabulate Results By Questionnaire . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Select a Questionnaire window is displayed. Select the questionnaire from the Questionnaire drop-down list. Select an option: To view the summary for all responses : Select Summary . To view the summary and details for responses : Select Summary and Details . Select an option: To restrict the report to responses entered since the questionnaire was last edited : Select Show Responses Since the Questionnaire was Last Modified . To restrict the report to a specific date : Select the dates from the From and To date fields. To include all responses : Select Show All Responses . Click OK . Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Generating Time Card Audit Trail Reports
topics.body: Generating Time Card Audit Trail Reports Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card Generating Time Card Audit Trail Reports Generate Time Card Audit Trail reports to view a list of changes made to employees' time cards. You can generate a report by date or by user. Before generating the audit trail, see Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides . Related Topics Running the Time Card Audit Trail By Date Report Running the Time Card Audit Trail by User Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Handling Time Card Entries
topics.body: Handling Time Card Entries Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card Handling Time Card Entries Employees can review their Time Card entries. Some employees, with specific security settings, can add, edit, and delete Time Card entries for other employees. To add, edit, or delete an entry, see Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides . Related Topics Reviewing Time Card Entries Adding and Editing Time Card Entries Deleting Time Card Entries Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Tickler File Labels
topics.body: Printing Tickler File Labels Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports Printing Tickler File Labels You can print labels for patients in the tickler file. The labels include the patient's name and mailing address. You can also define the number of lines to print per label. Sample Label To print tickler file labels: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Labels/Postcards/Dir > Tickler File Labels . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual labels on a label printer, select Printer . To print labels on a laser printer, select Laser Label File . Click OK . The Patient Tickler Label Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the Start and Stop dates from a calendar. In the Provider section, click the drop-down arrow to select the provider from a list, or leave 999 to report on all providers. In the Options section, deselect the appointment type checkboxes for which you do not want to print labels. In the Lines Per Label section, type the number of lines to print on each label. Click OK . I f you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK. OR If you selected the Laser Label File option, select Reports > Print Laser Labels . The Laser Labels window is displayed. The number of labels to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Tickler Entry Labels , and click Print Selected on the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the labels should be cleared. Verify that the labels have printed correctly and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Employee and Time Card Security
topics.body: Setting Employee and Time Card Security Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Managing Employee Attendance > Setting Employee and Time Card Security and Login Overrides Setting Employee and Time Card Security To set Employee and Time Card security: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Double-click the employee. The Rights By User window is displayed. Select Accounting . A list of accounting-type security settings is displayed. Select Access Employee/TimeCard Info and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Testing the Printer
topics.body: Testing the Printer eServices > Setting Up Communications Testing the Printer To ensure the printer is configured correctly: Select File > Print Setup . The eServices Software window is displayed. Select a printer from the Select new default printer drop-down list, and click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Testing Your Backup System
topics.body: Testing Your Backup System Backing Up Data Testing Your Backup System The installer of the backup system should test the system to confirm that it operates properly and saves all the necessary data. Make sure you understand the methods for verifying your backup system. Consult your hardware technician to provide specific steps for your office. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Text Colors in SoftDent
topics.body: Understanding Text Colors in SoftDent Understanding Text Colors in SoftDent Text in SoftDent is displayed in several colors and can mean different things, depending on the window in which the text is displayed. The following table lists the text color and its meaning in a specific window. Text Colors in SoftDent Window Color Description Account List Red Account has user code C or L (account sent to collection agency or involves a legal case). Gray Account has user code I (inactive). Fuchsia New account (has not been activated). Scheduler Books List Blue Default or Office book. Claims List Blue Treatment Plan claim (pre-authorization). InOffice List Red Medical claims. Black Patient initially displays in window. Blue * Patient arrival time is updated. (Patient has been checked in.) * Patient's location has changed from Operatory back to Waiting Room. Green Patient's location has changed to an Operatory. Fuchsia (Name) New patient (has not been activated). Red * Name - Patient has medical alert. * Last History date - Patient is due for updated medical history. * Appt time - Appointment time color changes to red 15 minutes before scheduled appointment. * Entire entry - Appointment marked "complete" in Electronic Router window. Lab Case List Purple Patient has been checked out (if history appointments is enabled). Red Case complete. Blue Case returned but still open. Green Case open and expected back. Patient List Red Patient has a medical alert. Blue Patient is overdue for recall (overridden if patient has a medical alert). Fuchsia New patient (has not been activated). Charting Clinical Notes Black Active patient. Red Primary Treatment Plan notes. Purple Secondary Treatment Plan notes. Teal Tertiary Treatment Plan notes. Green Initial Chart notes. Blue Generic notes. Orange Default notes. Statement Preview Green Statement was manually batched. Blue Account has an invalid address. Purple Account has an invalid e-mail address. Red Account is overdue more than 90 days. Tickler File Red Short Call tickler entry. Transaction Window & Treatment Plan Window Red Credit items. Blue Debit items. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Tickler File Report
topics.body: Running the Tickler File Report Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports Running the Tickler File Report The Tickler File report lists patients in the tickler file based on the selection criteria. It includes patient name, phone numbers, the original appointment date, and the date the patient was last contacted. It also shows the reason the patient is in the tickler file, and appointment information such as production codes, number of time units, the provider for the service, and the expected appointment fees. Sample Report To run the Tickler File report: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Tickler File . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Tickler Report Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the Start and Stop dates from a calendar. In the Provider Entries section, do the following: Click the drop-down arrow to select the provider from a list, or leave 999 to report on all providers. Type an appointment length, or leave 0 to report on appointments of any length. In the Options section, click the checkboxes for the types of appointments you want to include on the report. If you selected the Merge Letter Expert output option, the Letter Expert Template ID field is active. Click the drop-down arrow to select a template from the list. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Scheduling Tickler Appointments
topics.body: Scheduling Tickler Appointments Scheduling Appointments > Using the Tickler Scheduling Tickler Appointments To schedule an appointment that is stored in the Tickler file: In the Daily Appointments or Book At A Glance windows, select the time slot within which to schedule the appointment. Select an option: To schedule a Tickler file entry : Select Tickler > Scan Tickler from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To schedule a Short Call Tickle file entry : Select Tickler > Scan Tickler Short Calls from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The entry for the appointment that has been in the file the longest time and meet the requirements for the selected time slot is displayed in the Tickler Entry window. Click + or - to locate a different entry. To edit the appointment information, click Edit . You cannot edit the operatory number. To schedule the appointment, click Make Appt . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tips and Tricks
topics.body: Tips and Tricks Tips and Tricks There are many "tips and tricks" that our trainers recommend to help you use the SoftDent software efficiently. Insurance Tips and Tricks: = 4 && typeof(BSPSPopupOnMouseOver) == 'function') BSPSPopupOnMouseOver(event);" class="BSSCPopup" onclick="BSSCPopup('ChartTpsTricks.htm');return false;">Charting Tips and Tricks: Scheduler Tips and Tricks: General Office Tips and Tricks: Insurance Narrative Tip: When checking patients out, keep Microsoft Word minimized with a list of common insurance narratives, and copy and paste them onto the insurance claim. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Printing Tickler File Postcards
topics.body: Printing Tickler File Postcards Scheduling Appointments > Appointment Reports > Running Label, Postcard, and Directory Reports Printing Tickler File Postcards You can print postcards for patients in the tickler file that prompt them to schedule their missed, canceled, or treatment plan appointment. Sample Postcard To print tickler file postcards: Select Reports > Recall/Appt > Labels/Postcards/Dir > Tickler File Postcards . The Output Options window is displayed. Select from the following: To print individual postcards on a dot-matrix printer, select Printer . To print postcards on a laser printer, select Laser Postcard File . Click OK . The Tickler Report Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the Start and Stop dates from a calendar. In the Provider Entries section, do the following: Click the drop-down arrow to select the provider from a list, or leave 999 to report on all providers. Type an appointment length, or leave 0 to report on appointments of any length. In the Options section, deselect the appointment type checkboxes for which you do not want to print postcards. Click OK . I f you selected the Printer option, the Printer Selection window is displayed. Verify the selected printer, and click OK. OR If you selected the Laser Postcard File option, s elect Reports > Print Laser Postcards . The Laser Postcards window is displayed, and the number of postcards to be printed is displayed in the Count column. Select Tickler Postcards , and select Print Selected from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Print window is displayed. Verify the selected printer and click OK . A message is displayed, asking if the postcards should be cleared. Verify that the postcards have printed correctly, and click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Showing and Hiding Toolbar Titles
topics.body: Showing and Hiding Toolbar Titles Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using Toolbars Showing and Hiding Toolbar Titles To show or hide a toolbar title: To hide a toolbar description : Select any toolbar, right-click, and select Show/Hide Toolbar Titles . To display hidden toolbar titles : Select any toolbar, right-click, select Show/Hide Toolbar Titles . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Time Card Audit Trail By Date Report
topics.body: Running the Time Card Audit Trail By Date Report Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card > Generating Time Card Audit Trail Reports Running the Time Card Audit Trail By Date Report The Time Card Audit Trail by Date report lists changes that have been made to employees' time cards. The report is sorted by date. Sample Report To run the Time Card Audit Trail by Date report: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Time Card Audit Trail > By Date . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Audit Trail List Report (By Date) window is displayed. In the Audit Entry Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the start and end dates from a calendar. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Time Card Audit Trail by User Report
topics.body: Running the Time Card Audit Trail by User Report Getting Started > Employee Database and Time Card > Using Time Card > Generating Time Card Audit Trail Reports Running the Time Card Audit Trail by User Report The Time Card Audit Trail by User report lists changes that have been made to employees' time cards. The report is sorted by user name. Sample Report To run the Time Card Audit Trail by User report: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Time Card Audit Trail > By User . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Audit Trail List Report (By User) window is displayed. In the User section, type the starting and ending user names to include in the report. Select an option: To exclude added transactions: Select Exclude added transactions . To exclude deleted patient or account transactions: Select Exclude deleted patients/accounts . Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Time Card Features
topics.body: Time Card Features What's New in Version 14.0 Time Card Features Several enhancements have been made to the Time Card function: Customizing Time Card Preferences A Time Card Preferences option has been added to the System Settings menu ( System > Change System Settings > Time Card > Time Card Preferences ) and opens the Time Card Preferences window. This window enables you to specify pay frequency; select how hours worked are displayed; and track lunch, unpaid time, and overtime. See Setting Time Card Preferences for more information. Pay Frequency Added to Time Cards If you have Profile Administrator security rights, you can now specify the default pay frequency for all employees (weekly, twice a month, monthly, and so on). A Pay Frequency field has been added to the Employee window and the Rights by User window, and you can include pay frequency information on the Time Card report as well. Clocking Out for Lunch and Clocking In after Lunch When Track Lunch is selected in the Tracking Options section of the Time Card Preferences window ( System > Change System Settings > Time Card > Time Card Preferences ), an Out for Lunch button is added to the SoftDent Clock Out window. Click the button to record the time you are out to lunch. When you are ready to clock in again, the SoftDent Clock In window contains a Return From Lunch button. Click the button to clock back in. See Clocking In and Out for more information. Customizing Time Card Types A Customize Time Card Types option has been added to the System Settings menu ( System > Change System Settings > Time Card > Customize Time Card Types ) and opens the Time Card Types window. This window enables you to customize the types that you assign to an employee's time. In past versions of the software, the types were limited to Regular, Vacation, Holiday, and Sick. Now you can create any type you need, such as Training (Paid), Errand (Unpaid), and so on. The types you create display in the Time Card report as well. See Customizing Time Card Types for more information. Changes to the Employee Window Two fields have been added to the Employee window ( List > Employees > Double-click an employee ): Pay Frequency: The drop-down list contains the following options: Default (xxxxxxx)�The default for any existing and newly added employees. This is the value selected in the Time Card Preferences window. Weekly Every Two Weeks Twice a Month Monthly Status: The drop-down list contains the following overtime status options: Exempt, Non Exempt. See Editing Employee Information for more details. Changes to the Rights by User Window Two fields have been added to the Rights by User window ( System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User ): Pay Frequency: The drop-down list contains the following options: Default (xxxxxxx)�The default for any existing and newly added employees. This is the value selected in the Time Card Preferences window. Weekly Every Two Weeks Twice a Month Monthly Status: The drop-down list contains the following overtime status options: Exempt, Non Exempt. See Adding Users for more information. Changes to the Time Card Window The following changes have been made to the Time Card window ( List > Employees > Select an employee > Time Card ): Clicking the drop-down arrow in the Type field now displays the Time Card Types window, where you can select default and customized time card types. Start Date/End Date fields will now display when adding a time card entry, enabling

topics.title: Using Time Slot Submenu Commands
topics.body: Using Time Slot Submenu Commands Using the Scheduler > Using Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance Windows > Setting Daily Appointment Window Properties and Using Profiles Using Time Slot Submenu Commands The commands available from the blank time slot submenu in the Daily Appointments window are: Book Quick Glance : Select this command to display the Book At A Glance window. Month At A Glance : Select this command to display the Month At A Glance window. History Appointments : Select this command to display the History Appointments Date Range window. In this window, you can select a specific date range of appointments to display. Make Appointment : Select this command to make a patient appointment. The Patient List window is displayed. In this window, select the patient for whom you want to schedule the appointment. See Scheduling Patient Appointments . New Patient Appointment : Select this command to make an appointment for a new patient. The New Patient Appt window is displayed. In this window, you can enter new patient account information. See Setting the Auto Scheduling Book . AutoSched New Patient Appointment : Select this command to auto schedule an appointment for a new patient. The Setup Auto Scheduling Parameters for window is displayed. In this window, you can enter auto scheduling restrictions for the new patient. Select Patient : Select this command to display the Patient List window. In this window, you can select a patient for whom to make an appointment. Select Book : Select this command to display the Scheduler Books window. See Displaying Different Books . Tickler > Scan Tickler : Select this command to display the Tickler Entry window for the patient who has been in the Tickler list the longest and whose procedures require the same amount of time that is available in the schedule. Tickler > Scan Tickler Short Calls : Select this command to display the Tickler Entry window for patients whose entries are marked "short call" and whose procedures require the same amount of time that is available in the schedule. Add Note : Select this command to display the Assistant window. In this window, you can associate a note with an appointment. Only one note can be associated with an appointment. Block Slot : Select this command to display the Blocked Slots Properties window. In this window, you can block the time slot as unavailable for scheduling. See Using Blocked Time Slots . Column Properties > Save Width as Default : Select this command to save the width of a column as the default. Column Properties > Make All Rooms Default Width : Select this command to make the width of all columns the default width. Column Properties > Make All Rooms This Width : Select this command to make the width of all columns the width of the selected column. Column Properties > Save Column Order For Daily Op Schedule : Select this command to print the current column order on the Daily Operatory report. Properties : Select this command to display the Properties... window. See Setting Daily Appointment Window Properties and Using Profiles . Cancel : Select this command to close the time slot submenu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing Images Associated with a Tooth
topics.body: Viewing Images Associated with a Tooth Using the SoftDent Charting Module Viewing Images Associated with a Tooth You can display an image that is associated with a tooth in the Clinical Detail Container when the Restorative tab of the Chart for window is the active tab. To display the image, click the button located at the bottom right of the tooth. The V button : Indicates that an intraoral image is associated with the tooth. The X button : Indicates that an x-ray is associated with the tooth. The image is displayed in the Clinical Detail Container section. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Treatment Plans
topics.body: Charting Treatment Plans Charting Treatment Plans Using the charting module, you can: Chart primary, secondary, and tertiary treatment plan items Create treatment plan groups Enter clinical notes for plans Post completed treatment plan procedures When the Treatment tab is the active window : Existing treatment plan procedures are displayed. If you chart and post treatment plan items in the charting module, they are displayed in the patient's Treatment Plan window. Related Topics Organizing the Treatment Planning Tab Using Treatment Planning Tab Toolbars Creating Treatment Plan Groups Handling Treatment Plan Items Using the Presentation Manager Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating Treatment Plan Groups
topics.body: Creating Treatment Plan Groups Charting Treatment Plans Creating Treatment Plan Groups You can create a treatment plan c onsisting of up to 50 procedures. You can organize the procedures in up to 99 groups. Each treatment group can contain primary, secondary, and tertiary treatment plan items. To create a treatment group: On the Restorative tab in the Charting for window, select an option: To create a primary treatment plan group : Select Mode > Treatment Plan Primary . To create a secondary treatment plan group : Select Mode > Treatment Plan Secondary . To create a tertiary treatment plan group: Select Mode > Treatment Plan Tertiary . To create a treatment plan group, chart the items to include in the group, and click Save . To edit a treatment plan group, click the Treatment Planning tab. Select the treatment plan item, and select Click to change treatment plan group on the Treatment Plan toolbar; or right-click, and select Change TP Group from the menu. The Modify Group Number window is displayed. Select the group number from the Group Number drop-down list. If you do not select a group number, all procedures are charted to Group 1. Click OK . Related Topics Changing Treatment Groups Accepting Treatment Plan Groups Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Treatment Plans - Handling Treatment Plan Items
topics.body: Charting Treatment Plans - Handling Treatment Plan Items Charting Treatment Plans Handling Treatment Plan Items On the Treatment Planning tab in the Charting For window, for each treatment plan item you can: View the details of each treatment plan item associated with a patient Switch treatment plans Change the treatment group numbers Delete Build a presentation Accept Complete Print Schedule an appointment Re-estimate insurance View the online eligibility You can also access the presentation manager to display treatment plan information for the patient�s review. Related Topics Charting Treatment Plan Items Viewing Treatment Plan Items Deleting Treatment Plan Items Completing Treatment Plan Work Changing Treatment Plan Groups Building Case Presentation Module Presentations Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Charting Treatment Plans - Viewing Treatment Plan Items
topics.body: Charting Treatment Plans - Viewing Treatment Plan Items Charting Treatment Plans > Handling Treatment Plan Items Viewing Treatment Plan Items To view a list of treatment plan items, click the Treatment Planning tab. Primary treatment plan items are displayed in red text. Secondary treatment plan items are displayed in magenta. To view treatment plan items, on the Restorative tab in the Charting for window, select an option: To view primary treatment plan items : Select Treatment Plan Primary from the Mode drop-down list, or select Treatment Plan 1 from the Chart Treatment Plans button on the Chart Mode toolbar . To view secondary treatment plan items : Select Treatment Plan Secondary from the Mode drop-down list, or select Treatment Plan 2 from the Chart Treatment Plans button on the Chart Mode toolbar . When you display a specific treatment plan, only procedures in that plan are displayed in the window. Primary and secondary plan procedures are never displayed on the same chart. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Training on the Web
topics.body: Training on the Web Getting Started Training on the Web Update training is included with your software as part of your Advantage plan. Log in to the Carestream Dental Institute to participate in interactive web training and learn about new features and enhancements added to the software. To log in to the Carestream Dental Institute: Select Help > Carestream Dental Institute . The Carestream Dental Institute login webpage is displayed. Do one of the following: If you registered for other online training, type your user name and password and click Enter . Example: User Name: 99.99999joe If you have not previously registered for online training, your user name and your password is the combination of your customer ID number and the word update . Tip To display your customer ID number, select Help > About SoftDent , and click Show License Details . Example: User Name: 99.99999update Password: 99.99999update Type this combination in each field and click Enter . To view a list of courses, click the My Learning tab. To view the list of training resources assigned to a learning track, click Start in the Progress column of the track. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Transferring Data by Date Range or Interval
topics.body: Transferring Data by Date Range or Interval Using PracticeToGo > PracticeToGo User Preferences Transferring Data by Date Range or Interval The PracticeToGo User Preferences Intervals tab enables you to limit the amount of data transferred during a sync. The Daily Options section enables you to set a range of days or an interval. The amount of data that transfers during a sync is determined by the settings you make here. Things to keep in mind about the PracticeToGo data transfer: To transfer only today�s data, set the range of days to zero. The more data that is transferred, the longer the sync. Therefore, do not transfer more data than necessary. All old data is automatically purged from the Palm organizer or the Microsoft Outlook software and replaced with new data when you perform a data sync. There are two options at the top of the window: To select a specific sync date, click the drop-down arrow in the Last Sync on: field and select the date from the calendar. Click the Restore Defaults button to restore system defaults for all PracticeToGo settings. To set your PracticeToGo user preferences: Select System > PracticeToGo > User Preferences . The PracticeToGo User Preferences window is displayed. Click the Intervals tab. The PracticeToGo User Preferences Intervals tab is displayed. Complete the following fields: Sync Ref Doctor and Pharmacy records every _ days: Set a syncing interval that specifies how often referring doctor and pharmacy records are updated. Referring doctors and pharmacies do not typically need to be updated very often. The default is seven days, or once a week. Sync Patient records every _ days: Set a syncing interval that specifies how often the patient list is updated. Patient lists do not typically need to be updated very often. The default is 14 days, or every two weeks. Only include patients seen in the last _ days: This setting enables you to limit the patients included in the sync to only the patients you have seen recently. The default is 365 days, or one year. RX from previous _ days: This setting specifies a number of days in the past. A list of prescriptions for the set number of days will be transferred. The default is 10 days. Entering a 1 will load data for today plus the set number of past calendar days. For example: If it is Monday, and you enter 1, you will load data for Monday and Sunday. Schedule and Goals for next _ days: This setting specifies a number of days in the future. Appointments and Goal information for the set number of days will be transferred. The default is one day. Entering a 1 will load data through the next business day, while entering any number higher than one will load that many calender days. For example: If it is Friday, and you enter 1 , you will get data for Friday through Monday (including Saturday and Sunday), but if you enter 2 , you will get data for only Saturday and Sunday. Receivables for last _ days: This setting specifies a number of days in the past. Actual productions and collections for today (if you have run a daysheet) and the set number of days in the past will be transferred. The default is seven days. Entering a 1 will load data for today plus the set number of past calendar days. For example: If it is Monday, and you enter 1, you will load data for Monday and Sunday. Memos for next _ days: This setting specifies a number of days in the future. Memos for the set number of days will be transferred. The default is one day. Entering a 1 will load data thro

topics.title: Running the Transactions for a Period Report
topics.body: Running the Transactions for a Period Report Handling Reports > Accounting Reports Running the Transactions for a Period Report This report details all transactions posted during a specific period and summarizes transactions by provider. You also can find out how many adjustments were made in a month, year, and so on. You can select individual patient details/summaries and totals. To run the Transactions for a Period report: Select Reports > Accounting > Trans for a Period . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output type and click OK . The Transactions for a Period Report window is displayed. In the Dates section, select the start and end date for the report. In the Format section, type a format for the report. 1. List Each Transaction Separately�Lists every transaction posted during the specified date range. 2. Summarize Patient Transactions�Lists the patient only once for the day and groups the dollar amounts by production, charges, and so on. 3. Only List Totals�Lists only the totals for the specified date range, and groups the totals by production, charges, and so on. Select the Include Indirect Sources for Doctor (if Applicable) option to have the report show all of the production dollars for the specified doctor(s). More This means that the indirect production will be rolled over into the doctor�s production. If you do not select the option, only the production done solely by the doctor(s) is shown. Select the Include Inactive Accounts/Patients option to include inactive accounts or patients in the report. In the Code Types section, set the type of transactions to include in the report. In the Accounts With User Codes section, type the user codes for the accounts you want to include or exclude. In the Patients With User Codes section, type the user codes for the patients you want to include or exclude. In the Codes section, type the transaction codes you want to include in the report. In the Doctors section, click the drop-down arrow to select the doctors for the report, or leave the default of 999 to report on all doctors. Deselect the Include Color/Shading checkbox if you do not want shading to display on the report. Click OK . Additional Details: The first section of the report lists transactions within the date range by their production amounts, charges, production adjustments, and payment adjustments. It also indicates whether payment was cash, check, or credit. Report Details: Date�Date of transaction. ID and Name�Patient or account ID and name. Transactions posted to the account will have the account's first name. D$�The collecting provider. Dr�The service provider. Code�The posted transactions code. Prod�Dollar amount of production codes posted (codes 100.00+). Charges�Dollar amount of charges posted (codes 61.00-95.89, 97.00-97.89, 99.00-99.89). Prod Adj�Dollar amount of production adjustments posted (codes 40.00-60.00). Cash�Dollar amount of cash payments (codes 1.00-1.89). Check�Dollar amount of check payments (codes 2.00-10.89). Credit�Dollar amount of credit card payments (codes 11.00-20.89). Pay Adj�Dollar amount of collection adjustments posted (codes 21.00-39.89, 96.00-96.89, 98.00-98.89). The provider summary portion of the report lists all specified providers with transactions for the date range, which are classified as productions, charges, production adjustments, tax (if applicable), collections, and collections adjustments. Report Details: Dr�The doctor who collected/produced today's charges. P

topics.title: Transaction Audit Trail Report Codes
topics.body: Transaction Audit Trail Report Codes System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Transaction Audit Trail Reports Transaction Audit Trail Report Codes The following is a list of codes used to describe the transaction and can be found in the Type field on the Transaction Audit Trail reports: A = Actual (Completed work) B = Budget Plan (Account Budget Plan transaction) E = Pre-Treatment Estimate (Type = Estimate on treatment plan transactions) H = History Transaction (Initial inside work or transaction from patient's transaction history) L = Insurance Plan Transaction (Capitation/DMO plans) O = Outside Work (Outside work on the chart, will post with no Dr$ and Dr on the Audit trail) P = Primary Treatment Plan (Type = "Primary" on treatment plan transactions) S = Secondary Treatment Plan (Type = "Secondary" on treatment plan transactions) T = Tertiary Treatment Plan (Type = "Tertiary" on treatment plan transactions) Transaction Audit Trail types Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!
