topics.body: ADA, Transaction, and Diagnosis Code Reports Handling Reports ADA, Transaction, and Diagnosis Code Reports You can access the following reports from the Reports > ADA/Trans/Diag Codes menu: Report Name Description Frequency Single Fee Schedule Select this report to generate a list of transaction codes and fees associated with a specified fee schedule. For more information, see Running the Single Fee Schedule Report . As Needed All Fee Schedules Select this report to generate a list of transaction codes associated with all fee schedules. For more information, see Running the All Fee Schedules Report . As Needed Diagnosis Codes Select this report to generate a list of diagnosis codes. For more information, see Running the Diagnosis Codes Report . As Needed Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Treatment Plans
topics.body: Treatment Plans What's New in Version 14.0 > Charting Features Treatment Plans Treatment plans have received enhancements in the following areas: New Treatment Plan Comment Window A Treatment Plan Comments window containing predefined treatment plan comments can now be accessed from the Change System Settings menu ( System > Change System Settings > Treatment Plan Comments ). This window is similar to the Treatment Plan Financial Arrangement Notes window. See Saving Treatment Plan Comments for more information. Adding Pre-Defined Comments After Printing a Treatment Plan The Additional Comments section of the Treatment Plan Additional Setup window�displayed when you print a treatment plan using the form named 1# SOFTDENT Laser TP w/Comments & Financial Box (available from System > Form Selection )�now includes a drop-down list that enables you to select pre-defined comments. The window also features a checkbox which enables you to indicate that a current comment you just entered should be saved for future use. The Treatment Plan Additional Comments Setup window�displayed when you print a treatment plan using the form named 2# SOFTDENT Plain Paper TP w/Comments Box (available from System > Form Selection )�now includes a drop-down list that enables you to select pre-defined comments. The window also features a checkbox which enables you to indicate that a current comment you just entered should be saved for future use. See Printing Treatment Plans for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Trojan as an Insurance Estimation Reference
topics.body: Using Trojan as an Insurance Estimation Reference Trojan Using Trojan as an Insurance Estimation Reference Trojan information is not used for insurance estimation in the software. However, you can use the Trojan record as a reference to update default insurance calculations. To use the Trojan record: Select List > Patient . Click Transaction on the toolbar. The patient�s Transaction window is displayed. Click Add on the toolbar. If you are adding a transaction to a patient whose insurance plan is linked to a Trojan insurance plan, click Trojan to display the Trojan Insurance Plan record. The Trojan record is displayed as a reference only. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tracking Bluebook Changes
topics.body: Tracking Bluebook Changes Setting Up Insurance > Using the Insurance Bluebook Tracking Bluebook Changes Tags are displayed in the Bluebook entries for List window when you: Change estimation information in the Insurance Plan window. Change your fees. Enter faulty data when updating a bluebook amount. These tags enable you to diagnose bluebook entry problems. When you post an insurance payment that does not match the bluebook entry, the: Update Bluebook entry window is displayed. CHG column is enabled. Select the column, make changes, and click OK . To ensure your insurance payment information is updated in the bluebook, update the bluebook entry with the most recent payment information. The tags displayed in the Bluebook Entries for List window are: $ : The code�s fee schedule 0 has been updated since you last updated the entry. The new fee is displayed, and a new payment amount is calculated using the new fee information. After you update the bluebook entry and post an insurance payment to verify the payment amount, the tag is no longer displayed. T : Trojan fees are used to update bluebook entries. CHG : An insurance payment that does not match the entry was posted. 1 : Displayed when the bluebook entry was updated by posting an insurance payment to a patient who is not charged the fee schedule 0 amount. 2 : Displayed when you attempt to update a bluebook entry that has been changed since the associated procedure was posted. 3 : Displayed when you attempt to update a bluebook entry and the % field has been changed since the associated procedure was posted. 4 : Displayed when you attempt to update a bluebook entry and the fee has changed since the associated procedure was posted. 5 : Displayed when you attempt to update a bluebook entry and the fee submitted to the insurance company is not the same as the fee schedule 0 amount. 6 : Displayed when you attempt to update a bluebook entry and the deductible is calculated into the insurance payment amount. 7 : Displayed when you attempt to update a bluebook entry and the bluebook fee is different from the transaction fee. If a plan is created as a flat fee plan with bluebook entries and then the plan is changed to a percentage plan, the bluebook entries are marked with a percent (%) tag. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tracking Goals
topics.body: Tracking Goals Entering Providers, Employers, and Schools > Handling Provider Information Tracking Goals You can view the daily production goals, actual scheduled amounts, and the percentage of the daily goal met information for each provider. To view daily production and goal information: Select List > View Daily RCVTOT Totals > Goals/Prod > Provider Goals . The Select Provider Goals window is displayed. Select the provider and click OK . The Rcvtot List window is displayed. Estimated write-off amounts for PPO procedures are not included in production amounts. Example : If a PPO procedure�s fee is $54, the allowed amount is $44, the estimated insurance payment is $42, the copay is $2, and the write-off is $10; the production amount is $44, which is the estimated copay plus the estimated insurance payment. To compare the production and scheduling goals to the actual productions and collections for a specific date, double-click the goal information in the list. The Rcvtot window is displayed. The information displayed in this window cannot be modified. Click Close . Tip Use the Create Practice Goal Utility to combine individual provider goals into a single cumulative goal. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tracking HIPAA Privacy Compliance and Understanding HIPAA Security
topics.body: Tracking HIPAA Privacy Compliance and Understanding HIPAA Security System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features Tracking HIPAA Privacy Compliance and Understanding HIPAA Security You can track which patients have received notification of your HIPAA Privacy compliance efforts. You can automatically display a HIPAA Privacy compliance notification when patients, who have not been informed of your regulatory compliance, are checked in using the following windows: InOffice Daily Appointments Book At A Glance After informing the patient, you can add the following items: HIPAA acknowledgement Consent Authorization Disclosure Complaint contacts You can enable the HIPAA Privacy feature on computers that are used in public areas to safeguard your patients� privacy. When this feature is enabled on a workstation: Abbreviations are displayed instead of patient names Patient phone numbers are not displayed in the following windows: InOffice Daily Appointments Book At A Glance History Appointments Patient phone numbers are not printed on the Daily Operatory report You can use HIPAA Security features to help you comply with HIPAA security requirements and ensure that the data maintained in the database is protected. HIPAA security settings also enable you to: Enforce user name and password usage Track the activity of each user Automatically log out of the software when each workstation is inactive for a specified time Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tracking a Lab Case Status
topics.body: Tracking a Lab Case Status Managing Pharmacies, Prescriptions, Labs, and Lab Cases > Handling Lab Cases Tracking a Lab Case Status Lab cases are divided into one of the following categories: OPEN/EXPECTED : Indicates the lab case is currently at the lab. These cases are displayed in green text in the Lab case List window. OPEN/RETURNED : Indicates the lab case has been returned by the lab. These cases are displayed in blue text in the Lab case List window. COMPLETE : Indicates the lab case has been returned from the lab and all clinical procedures associated with the case have been performed. These cases are displayed in red text in the Lab case List window. Related Topics Viewing a Lab Case Status Marking Cases as Returned or Completed Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tracking Missed, Broken, and Late Appointments
topics.body: Tracking Missed, Broken, and Late Appointments Scheduling Appointments Tracking Missed, Broken, and Late Appointments The software tracks information about a patient's missed, broken, and late appointments in the Tracked Appointments window. You can view this information, add a note concerning an appointment, or run the Appointments Tracking List report in this window. To view missed, broken, and late appointments for a specific patient: Select an option: In the Patient List window, select a patient and click Other Management > Missed/Broken/Late Appointments . In the Patient window, click Other Management > Missed/Broken/Late Appointments . The Tracked Appointments window is displayed. To add a note to an appointment entry, select the appointment and click Edit , or double-click the appointment. The Tracked Appointments note window is displayed. In the Notes field, type a note concerning the appointment. Click OK . To run the Appointments Tracking List report for the patient, click Print in the Tracked Appointments window. Related Topics Running the Appointments Tracking List Report Setting Scheduler Defaults Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tracking Patients in the Office
topics.body: Tracking Patients in the Office Tracking Patients in the Office You can track patients from arrival to check out. Using the InOffice feature, you can: View a list of patient appointments scheduled for the day Track patient arrival times Track patient locations Track the amount of time each patient spends in the operatory View patient and account information Post transactions Submit claims Access the charting module Related Topics Using InOffice Window Options Understanding the Electronic Router Window Understanding HIPAA and the InOffice Feature Handling InOffice Transactions Generating Patient Visit Statistics Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tracking Patient Referrals
topics.body: Tracking Patient Referrals Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Patient Referrals Tracking Patient Referrals You can track patients referred to your practice by an existing patient, associate the referred and referring patient records, and view a list of patients referred by a specific patient. The number of patients referred by each patient is d isplayed in the Ref�d column in the Patient List window. This column is updated each time a new referral is recorded. To view the patients referred by a patient, select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the appropriate patient and select Other Patient Management > Other Patients Referred from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Other Patient Management > Other Patients Referred from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Referred Patients List window is displayed. The patients referred to your practice by the patient are displayed in the list. From the Referred Patients List window, you can view the following for any patient in the list: Appointments Treatment plans Medical histories Images Prescriptions HIPAA acknowledgements Patient pictures Account records Documents Budget plans Contacts For any referred patient, you can also: Call referred patients Print letters for Make appointments for View the claim history Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tracking Outgoing Referrals to an Outside Doctor
topics.body: Tracking Outgoing Referrals to an Outside Doctor Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals Tracking Outgoing Referrals to an Outside Doctor To track an outgoing referral to an outside doctor: Select an option: Select List > Referring Dr . In any window, press Ctrl + F. Click List Referring Providers on the Power Bar. The REFERRING DR List window is displayed. Select the doctor and select Outgoing Referrals from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Tracking Incoming Referrals from an Outside Doctor
topics.body: Tracking Incoming Referrals from an Outside Doctor Patient and Doctor Referrals > Handling Doctor Referrals Tracking Incoming Referrals from an Outside Doctor To view referrals from an outside doctor: Select an option: Select List > Referring Dr . In any window, press Ctrl + F. Click List Referring Providers on the Power Bar. The REFERRING DR List window is displayed. Select the doctor and select Incoming Referrals from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Transmitting Claims Automatically
topics.body: Transmitting Claims Automatically eServices > Using the eClaims Service Transmitting Claims Automatically The Auto option automates the electronic submission process by automatically loading, validating, and transmitting claims. To become familiar with the claim submission process, transmit claims manually before using the Auto option. To use the Auto option, select an option: In the eServices window : Click Auto . From the main menu : Select Submit Data > Auto . The Claim Submission List window is displayed as the claims are being validated. This function stops if a validation error or warning occurs during the transmission. Validated claims are automatically transmitted. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Transmitting Claims
topics.body: Transmitting Claims eServices > Using the eClaims Service > Sending Claims Transmitting Claims To transmit validated claims: Select an option: In the eServices window : Click Transmit . From the main menu : Select Submit Data > Transmit . If you are using Internet File Transfer : You can transmit claims from any computer with an Internet connection. During transmission, the PWCOMM Web Enabled Transfer window displays the connection status messages. If you are using a modem : You can transmit claims only from the computer with the modem, and the telephone line must be clear. The PWCOMM window displays the modem connection and status messages. If a message is displayed, click OK . Any downloaded reports are displayed in the Report List window. These reports can be previewed or printed and contain important status and bulletin information. When you are finished reviewing the reports, click Exit . You can submit claims as often as you like. Claims received after 7:30 p.m. Eastern Standard Time (approximately) are processed on the next business day. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Transmitting Automated Statements
topics.body: Transmitting Automated Statements eServices > Using the Automated Statements Service > Using Automated Statements Transmitting Automated Statements To transmit Automated Statements: Select an option: From the SoftDent main menu : Select Insurance > eServices . On the SoftDent Power bar : Click Run eClaims/Automated Statements . The eServices window is displayed. Select an option: In the eServices window : Click Send Statements . From the main menu : Select Submit Data > Statement options > Transmit stmts only . The Transmission window is displayed. After the file is transmitted, the Reports List window is displayed. Click PRINT ALL . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Trojan
topics.body: Trojan Trojan You can access Trojan, an insurance plan database, to receive the most current and accurate insurance information available. As a Trojan subscriber, you: Receive monthly update disks Can download Trojan data directly into the database Related Topics Installing Trojan Updating the Trojan Database Handling Trojan Traditional Plan Managed Care Plans Linking Software and Trojan Information Estimating Insurance Coverage Information Using Trojan as an Insurance Estimation Reference Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Trojan Plan Reports
topics.body: Trojan Plan Reports Handling Reports Trojan Plan Reports You can access the following reports from the Reports > Trojan Plan Reports menu: Report Name Description Frequency Alphabetical Traditional Plans Select this report to generate an alphabetical list of traditional Trojan plans. As Needed Traditional Plans By ID Select this report to generate a list of traditional Trojan plans by identification number. As Needed Alphabetical Managed Care Plans Select this report to generate a list of managed care Trojan plans in alphabetical order. As Needed Managed Care Plans By ID Select this report to generate a list of managed care Trojan plans by identification number. As Needed Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Treatment Plan Transactions Report
topics.body: Running the Treatment Plan Transactions Report Treatment Planning > Running Treatment Plan Reports Running the Treatment Plan Transactions Report The Treatment Plan Transactions report g enerates a list of posted treatment plan procedures. The percentage of practice treatment plan transactions is also included in the report. To generate the Treatment Plan Transactions report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Treatment Plan Reports > Transactions . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Transactions Posted From Treatment Plans window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending date range from a calendar. Select an option: To restrict the report to a specific provider : Select the provider identification number from the Provider drop-down list. To restrict the report to a specific procedural code range : Select the codes from the fields in the Codes section. Note: To specify a single code, type it in the left Codes column and type 0 in the right field. Click OK . Additional Treatment Plan Transactions Report Information: Treatment plan transactions are recorded as posted only when they are posted from the Treatment Plan window or by auto-posting the transactions. The report contains the following information: Date�Date of posted transaction. ID, Name�Patient ID and name. D$�The collecting provider. Dr�The service provider. Code�Transaction code, including extension. Prod�Dollar amount of posted production transactions (codes 100.00+). Charges�Dollar amount of posted charges (codes 61.00-91.99). Prod Adj�Dollar amount of posted production adjustments (codes 40.00-60.99). Totals�Total dollar amounts for production, charges, and production adjustments. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Testing Communications
topics.body: Testing Communications eServices > Setting Up Communications Testing Communications To test communications: Select an option: In the eServices window : Click Receive Only . From the main menu at the top of the window : Select Submit Data > Receive Only . eServices connects with the eServices server through the Internet or modem, and reports are downloaded to your computer. The Report List window is displayed, and you can preview and print the reports that are listed. If you must press additional buttons on your telephone to access a dial tone, you might need to switch the modem to a line with direct access to a dial tone. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up TeamTalk Databases and Events
topics.body: Setting Up TeamTalk Databases and Events Using TeamTalk > Setting Up TeamTalk Notes Setting Up TeamTalk Databases and Events You can specify the events that display an account, patient, employer, school, or insurance plan without adding or appending the TeamTalk note. To set up TeamTalk database events: Select TeamTalk > Change Events Selection . The Select Events window is displayed. By default, Account is displayed in the Event List for drop-down list, and account events are selected in the list. To change the database, select the database from the Event List for drop-down list. For a list of defined databases and events, see Understanding TeamTalk Events . Select the events for the database from the SoftDent will look for TeamTalk note when section. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Account Database TeamTalk Conditions
topics.body: Account Database TeamTalk Conditions Using TeamTalk > Understanding TeamTalk Events > Understanding TeamTalk Conditions Account Database TeamTalk Conditions The account record conditions that can restrict the event that displays a TeamTalk note are: Guarantor 1 Employer ID : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more guarantor 1 employer identification numbers. Guarantor 1 Dental Plan : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more guarantor 1 dental plans. Guarantor 1 Medical Plan : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more guarantor 1 medical plans. Guarantor 1 YTD Deductible : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more guarantor 1 year-to-date deductible amounts. Guarantor 1 Last Rollover Date : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more guarantor 1 rollover dates. Guarantor 2 Employer ID : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more guarantor 2 employer identification numbers. Guarantor 2 Dental Plan : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more guarantor 2 dental plans. Guarantor 2 Medical Plan : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more guarantor 2 medical plans. Guarantor 2 YTD Deductible : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more guarantor 2 year-to-date deductible amounts. Guarantor 2 Last Rollover Date : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more guarantor 2 rollover dates. Balance : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account balance amounts. Insurance Balance : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account insurance balance amounts. Provider ID : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account provider identification numbers. Insurance Calculation Method : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account insurance calculation methods. Category Code 1 : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account category code number ones. Category Code 2 : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account category code number twos. Category Code 3 : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account category code number threes. Category Code 4 : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account category code number fours. Category Code 5 : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account category code number fives. Minimum Payment : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account minimum payment amounts. Last Statement Sent Date : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account last statement sent dates Budget Plan Balance : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account budget plan balance amounts. Last BP Statement Sent Date : Restricts the TeamTalk note to be displayed for one or more account budget plan last statement sent dates. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding TeamTalk Conditions
topics.body: Understanding TeamTalk Conditions Using TeamTalk > Understanding TeamTalk Events Understanding TeamTalk Conditions You can add conditions to TeamTalk notes that further restrict the event that displays the note. You can add up to three conditions for each note. Related Topics Account Database TeamTalk Conditions Employer/School Database TeamTalk Conditions Insurance Plan Database TeamTalk Conditions Patient Database TeamTalk Conditions Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding TeamTalk Events
topics.body: Understanding TeamTalk Events Using TeamTalk Understanding TeamTalk Events Using TeamTalk, you can specify the databases and events that display a note. You can set up TeamTalk to be displayed before a specific task or event is saved to the following databases: Account Patient Employer School Insurance plan Related Topics Account Database Events Patient Database Events Employer/School Database Events Insurance Plan Database Events Understanding TeamTalk Conditions Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: TeamTalk Menu Commands
topics.body: TeamTalk Menu Commands Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Main Menu Bar TeamTalk Menu Commands Us e the TeamTalk menu to view and set up TeamTalk information. The TeamTalk menu commands are: Change Events Selection : Select or deselect events that trigger TeamTalk notes. TeamTalk Setup : Enter multiple events, add conditions to a note, and attach an addendum to a note. General TeamTalk Lists : Add, edit, or delete notes and view conditions. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating TeamTalk Notes for Contacts
topics.body: Creating TeamTalk Notes for Contacts Using Experts > Using the Contact Expert Creating TeamTalk Notes for Contacts You can set up TeamTalk notes to display for a specific event, for a specific patient or account, and for a specific time range. To add a TeamTalk note to a patient or account contact: In the Contact Expert window, select a record from the list in the left pane. Select Create TeamTalk Note from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The TeamTalk Setup window is displayed. Select the events to trigger the note from the When list located in the Show Note section. Select an option: To display the note the next time the event is performed : Select At Next Event . To display the note for a specific date or date range : Select In Date Range . Select the from and to dates from the calendars. To display the note every time the event is performed : Select Always . Type a note description in the Description field. Type the note text in the Text field. To play a sound when the note is displayed, select Play sound when note appears . Click Save . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Viewing TeamTalk Note Conditions
topics.body: Viewing TeamTalk Note Conditions Using TeamTalk > Using TeamTalk Options Viewing TeamTalk Note Conditions You can view a summary of any conditions associated with a TeamTalk note. To view the conditions associated with a note: Select TeamTalk > General TeamTalk Lists . The TeamTalk - Notes window is displayed. Select the database from the List Notes for drop-down list. Select the note and select View Conditions from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The conditions are displayed in the TeamTalk Conditions Summary window. After you have viewed the conditions, click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up TeamTalk Notes from the TeamTalk Window
topics.body: Setting Up TeamTalk Notes from the TeamTalk Window Using TeamTalk > Setting Up TeamTalk Notes Setting Up TeamTalk Notes from the TeamTalk Window To set up TeamTalk notes from the TeamTalk window: Select TeamTalk > TeamTalk Setup . The TeamTalk window is displayed. Select the database for the note from the drop-down list. For a complete list of databases and events, see Understanding TeamTalk Events . Select an option: To display the TeamTalk note each time the event is performed for each record contained in the database : Select the Every____Record drop-down list. To select several records, press Ctrl, select the records and double-click the last record. Click Next . To display a TeamTalk note each time the event is performed for a specific set of records contained in the database : Select One/Few____Records and click Next . Select the event to display a note from the Show Note section. Select an option: To display the TeamTalk note only the next time the event is performed : Select At Next Event . To display the TeamTalk note for a specific date or date range : Select In Date Range and select the dates from the From and To drop-down calendars. To display the TeamTalk note every time the event is performed : Select Always . To add a condition and further restrict the event that displays the note, click Extra Settings . Select the field to which to apply the condition from the drop-down list : Click Next . Select the restrictions from the drop-down list and click Next . A message asks you if you want to add another condition. To add another condition, click Yes and repeat this step. For a list of database conditions, see Understanding TeamTalk Conditions . When you are finished adding conditions : Click No . The TeamTalk Setup Wizard - Conditions window is displayed. Review your conditions, and click Finish . The TeamTalk window is displayed. Type the note subject in the Description field, and type the note text in the Note field. The spell checker function checks the note for misspelled words. If you do not enter text into the Description field, the first 40 characters of the note text are automatically populated in the field. To play a sound each time the note is displayed, select Play sound when note appears , and select the sound from the list. Click OK . If a note for the same event already exists, a message asks if you want to append the new note to the existing one. To append the new note, click Yes . If you click No , the note is displayed at the same time as the existing note. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up TeamTalk Notes Using the TeamTalk Wizard
topics.body: Setting Up TeamTalk Notes Using the TeamTalk Wizard Using TeamTalk > Setting Up TeamTalk Notes Setting Up TeamTalk Notes Using the TeamTalk Wizard The TeamTalk Wizard guides you through the process of creating a TeamTalk note. To set up TeamTalk notes using the TeamTalk Wizard: Select Wizards > TeamTalk Wizard . Read the introductory paragraph, and click Next . Select the database for the note from the drop-down list, and click Next . For a complete list of databases and events, see Understanding TeamTalk Events . The events are specific to the selected database. Select the event to display a note, and click Next . The option text is specific to the database. Select an option: To display the TeamTalk note each time the event is performed to each record in the database : Select Every____Record and click Next . To select several records, press Ctrl, select the records, and double-click the last record. Click Next . To display a TeamTalk note each time the event is performed to a specific set of records in the database : Select One/Few____Records and click Next . Select an option: To display the TeamTalk note only the next time the event is performed : Select At Next Event and click Next . To display the TeamTalk note for a specific date or date range : Select In Date Range , select the dates from the From and To drop-down calendars, and click Next . To display the TeamTalk note every time the event is performed : Select Always and click Next . Select an option: To add a condition and further restrict the event that displays the note : Select Extra Settings and click Next . Select the field to which to apply the condition from the drop-down list and click Next . Select the restrictions from the drop-down list and click Next . A message asks you if you want to add another condition. To add another condition, click Yes and repeat this step. When you are finished adding conditions, click No . For a list of database conditions, see Understanding TeamTalk Conditions . To add no conditions that further restrict the event that displays the note : Deselect Extra Settings and click Next . To play a sound each time the note is displayed, select Play sound when note appears , select the sound from the list, and click Next . Type the note subject in the Description field, type the note text in the Note field, and click Next . The spell checker function checks the note for misspelled words. If you do not enter text into the Description field, the first 40 characters of the note text are automatically populated in the field. Ensure that your TeamTalk note event, condition, and text are correct and click Finish . Click OK . If a note for the same event already exists, a message asks if you want to append the new note to the existing one. To append the new note, click Yes . If you click No , the note is displayed at the same time as the existing note. A message asks if you want to create a TeamTalk note for the same database. Select an option: To add another TeamTalk note for the same database : Click Yes . Repeat steps 4 through 11. To close the wizard : Click No . A message asks if you want to create a TeamTalk note for another database. Select an option: To add another TeamTalk note to another database : Click Yes . Repeat steps 3 through 11. To close the wizard : Click No . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the SoftDent Practice Management Software Tutorial
topics.body: Using the SoftDent Practice Management Software Tutorial Finding Answers Using the SoftDent Practice Management Software Tutorial The software comes with demonstration software that has data loaded. This tutorial enables you to familiarize yourself with the software without harming your production database. You can add, delete, and modify data, and when you are finished, you can reset the tutorial data to its original state. To access the tutorial: Click Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > Tutorial > SoftDent Tutorial . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Press Enter in the Name and Password fields, and click OK . The SoftDent Practice Management Software tutorial is displayed. To reset the data in the tutorial: Make sure that the tutorial is closed. Click Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > Reset Tutorial Data . The demo database is reset, and any data you entered is lost. Use the tutorial to complete the exercises in the SoftDent Practice Management Software Initial Training Workbook and the SoftDent Charting Module Training Workbook . Access these workbooks by selecting Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > Online Documentation. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Placing Treatment Plan Appointments in the Tickler File
topics.body: Placing Treatment Plan Appointments in the Tickler File Scheduling Appointments > Using the Tickler Placing Treatment Plan Appointments in the Tickler File You can place treatment plan procedures or groups in the Tickler or Short Call Tickler to enable you to schedule the appointments later. For more information, see Treatment Planning . To place a treatment group or procedure in the Tickler: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select a patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options menu or the Options section of the Express bar. In the Patient window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options menu or the Options section of the Express bar. In the patient Transactions window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options menu or the Options section of the Express bar. In the InOffice window : Select a patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options menu or the Options section of the Express bar. The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Select a procedure to place in the Tickler, and select Schedule Appointment from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Treatment Plan Scheduling window is displayed. Click OK . Select Tickler from the Options section of the Express bar or the top of the window. A submenu is displayed. Select an option: To place the treatment group in the Tickler : Select Place In Tickler . A message that informs you that the information is placed in the Tickler is displayed. Click OK . If the patient is willing to take an appointment on short notice : Select Place in Short Call Tickler . A message that informs you the information is placed in the Shortcall Tickler is displayed. Click OK . The Treatment Plan Scheduling window is displayed. Planned Treatment is displayed in the Tickler Reason field in the Treatment Entry window. Click Close twice . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Collection Doctor Report
topics.body: Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Collection Doctor Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Transaction Audit Trail Reports Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Collection Doctor Report This report contains a record of every deletion or modification (including back-dating) to transactions, patients, and accounts. It includes the date and time of the change, and the user name of the person making the change (if system security is enabled). To generate a list of audit entries by collection doctors: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Transaction Audit Trail > By Collection Doctor . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Transaction Audit By Collection Doctor window is displayed. Select the starting and ending collection doctor identification numbers from the drop-down lists. S elect an option: To exclude added transactions: Select Exclude added transactions . To exclude deleted patient or account transactions: Select Exclude deleted patients/accounts . Click OK . Additional Transaction Audit Trail by Collection Doctor Report Information: The following is a list of codes used to describe the transaction and can be found in the Type field on the report. A = Actual (Completed work) B = Budget Plan (Account Budget Plan transactions) E = Pre-Treatment Estimate (Type = Estimate on treatment plan transactions) H = History Transaction (Initial inside work or transaction from patient's transaction history) L = Insurance Plan Transaction (Capitation/DMO plans) O = Outside Work (Outside work on the chart, will post with no Dr$ and Dr on the Audit trail) P = Primary Treatment Plan (Type = "Primary" on treatment plan transactions) S = Secondary Treatment Plan (Type = "Secondary" on treatment plan transactions) T = Tertiary Treatment Plan (Type = "Tertiary" on treatment plan transactions) For transactions that are deleted or modified, this report lists the account or patient ID involved, the code number, provider ID, transaction type, the transaction amount, the date and time the change was made, the original transaction date and amount, and the date and amount of the transaction as it exists now. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Date Report
topics.body: Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Date Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Transaction Audit Trail Reports Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Date Report This report contains a record of every deletion or modification (including back-dating) to transactions, patients, and accounts. It includes the date and time of the change, and the user name of the person making the change (if system security is enabled). To generate a list of audit entries by the date on which they were created: Select Reports > Audit Trail > Transaction Audit Trail > By Date . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Transaction Audit Trail By Date window is displayed. Select the starting and ending audit entry creation dates from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To exclude added transactions: Select Exclude added transactions . To exclude deleted patient or account transactions: Select Exclude deleted patients/accounts . Click OK . Additional Transaction Audit Trail by Date Report Information: The following is a list of codes used to describe the transaction and can be found in the Type field on the report. A = Actual (Completed work) B = Budget Plan (Account Budget Plan transaction) E = Pre-Treatment Estimate (Type = Estimate on treatment plan transactions) H = History Transaction (Initial inside work or transaction from patient's transaction history) L = Insurance Plan Transaction (Capitation/DMO plans) O = Outside Work (Outside work on the chart, will post with no Dr$ and Dr on the Audit trail) P = Primary Treatment Plan (Type = "Primary" on treatment plan transactions) S = Secondary Treatment Plan (Type = "Secondary" on treatment plan transactions) T = Tertiary Treatment Plan (Type = "Tertiary" on treatment plan transactions) For transactions that are deleted or modified, this report lists the account or patient ID involved, the code number, provider ID, transaction type, the transaction amount, the date and time the change was made, the original transaction date and amount, and the date and amount of the transaction as it exists now. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Patient Name Report
topics.body: Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Patient Name Report System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Using Audit Trails > Running Transaction Audit Trail Reports Running the Transaction Audit Trail By Patient Name Report This report contains a record of every deletion or modification (including back-dating) to transactions, patients, and accounts. It includes the date and time of the change, and the user name of the person making the change (if system security is enabled). The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Transaction Audit Trail By Patient Name window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patients from the drop-down lists. S elect an option: To exclude added transactions: Select Exclude added transactions . To exclude deleted patient or account transactions: Select Exclude deleted patients/accounts . Click OK . Additional Transaction Audit Trail By Patient Name Report Information: The following is a list of codes used to describe the transaction and can be found in the Type field on the report. A = Actual (Completed work) B = Budget Plan (Account Budget Plan transaction) E = Pre-Treatment Estimate (Type = Estimate on treatment plan transactions) H = History Transaction (Initial inside work or transaction from patient's transaction history) L = Insurance Plan Transaction (Capitation/DMO plans) O = Outside Work (Outside work on the chart, will post with no Dr$ and Dr on the Audit trail) P = Primary Treatment Plan (Type = "Primary" on treatment plan transactions) S = Secondary Treatment Plan (Type = "Secondary" on treatment plan transactions) T = Tertiary Treatment Plan (Type = "Tertiary" on treatment plan transactions) For transactions that are deleted or modified, this report lists the account or patient ID involved, the code number, provider ID, transaction type, the transaction amount, the date and time the change was made, the original transaction date and amount, and the date and amount of the transaction as it exists now. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

S elect an option: To exclude added transactions: Select Exclude added transactions . To exclude deleted patient or account transactions: Select Exclude deleted patients/accounts . Click OK . Additional Transaction Audit Trail By User Report Information: The following is a list of codes used to describe the transaction and can be found in the Type field on the report. A = Actual (Completed work) B = Budget Plan (Account Budget Plan transactions) E = Pre-Treatment Estimate (Type = Estimate on treatment plan transactions) H = History Transaction (Initial inside work or transaction from patient's transaction history) L = Insurance Plan Transaction (Capitation/DMO plans) O = Outside Work (Outside work on the chart, will post with no Dr$ and Dr on the Audit trail) P = Primary Treatment Plan (Type = "Primary" on treatment plan transactions) S = Secondary Treatment Plan (Type = "Secondary" on treatment plan transactions) T = Tertiary Treatment Plan (Type = "Tertiary" on treatment plan transactions) For transactions that are deleted or modified, this report lists the account or patient ID involved, the code number, provider ID, transaction type, the transaction amount, the date and time the change was made, the original transaction date and amount, and the date and amount of the transaction as it exists now.
