topics.title: Understanding Transaction and Procedure Code Ranges
topics.body: Understanding Transaction and Procedure Code Ranges Handling Transactions and Codes Understanding Transaction and Procedure Code Ranges Transaction codes specify transaction types. Standard ADA procedure codes include standard dental procedures. Medical procedures are indicated by CPT codes. Explosion codes group a series of procedures that are often performed together. Diagnosis codes are not maintained in the same database as transaction and procedure codes. These ranges and any codes marked as Reserved cannot be changed. The ten code ranges are: Code Type Code Range Payment Codes 1.00 - 20.99 Payment Adjustments 21.00 - 39.99 Charge Adjustments 40.00 - 60.99 Charge Codes 61.00 - 91.99 Reserved Codes 92.00 - 99.99 ADA Procedure Codes 100.00 - 9999.99 CPT Procedure Codes 10000.00 - 99999.99 Explosion Codes 100000.00 - 100999.99 Statement Text or Notes 101000.00 - 101999.99 Fee Schedule Titles 102000.00 - 102026.99 Detailed description of each code range and how it affects the software: Code Type Code Range Description Effect Payment Code 1.00 Reserved Cash The account balance is decreased. The account collection amount is increased. Payment Code 1.01 - 1.89 Other Cash The account balance is decreased. The account collection amount is increased. Payment Code 2.00 Reserved Insurance check The account balance is decreased. The account collection amount is increased. Payment Code 2.01 - 2.89 Other insurance checks The account balance is decreased. The account collection amount is increased. Payment Code 2.90 Reserved Electronic funds transfer The account balance is decreased. The account collection amount is increased. Payment Code 3.00-10.89 Checks The account balance is decreased. The account collection amount is increased. Payment Code 11.00 - 20.89 Credit cards The account balance is decreased. The account collection amount is increased. Payment Adjustment 21.00 - 29.89 Collection Adjustments The account balance is decreased. The account collection amount is increased. Payment Adjustment 29.00 Reserved Initial credit balance The account balance is decreased. The account collection amount is increased. Payment Adjustment 29.01 Reserved Compression starting credit balance The account balance is decreased. The account collection amount is increased. Payment Adjustment 30.00 - 39.89 Collection debit adjustments The account balance is increased. The account collection amount is decreased. Charge Adjustment 40.00 - 49.89 Production debit adjustments The production amount is increased. The account balance is increased. Charge Adjustment 40.90 Reserved DMO per-visit charge The production amount is increased. The account balance is increased. Charge Adjustment 48.00 Reserved Monthly budget plan charge The production amount is increased. The account balance is increased. Charge Adjustment 48.01 Reserved Budget plan production debit adjustment The production amount is increased. The account balance is increased. Charge Adjustment 48.90 Reserved Monthly ortho budget plan charge The production amount is increased. The account balance is increased. Charge Adjustment 49.00 Reserved Initial debit balance The production amount is increased. The account balance is increased. Charge Adjustment 49.01 Reserved Compression starting debit balance The production amount is increased. The account balance is increased. Charge Adjustment 50.00 - 60.89 Production credit adjustments The production amount is decreased. The account balance is decreased. Charge Adjust

topics.title: Transaction Code Reports
topics.body: Transaction Code Reports Handling Transactions and Codes Transaction Code Reports Select Reports > ADA/Trans/Diag codes to run the following reports: Single Fee Schedule : Lists transaction codes and fees for a fee schedule. The code, office code, description, and fee for the selected codes in the fee schedule are included in the list. All Fee Schedules : Lists transaction codes, descriptions, and fees for all fee schedules. Diagnosis Codes : Lists all diagnosis codes and descriptions. Tip To generate a list of transactions posted within a specific date range, select Reports > Accounting to run the Transactions for a Period report. Related Topics Running the Single Fee Schedule Report Running the All Fee Schedules Report Running the Diagnosis Codes Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Print Archived Transaction History Utility
topics.body: Running the Print Archived Transaction History Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities > Running the Transaction History Maintenance Utilities Running the Print Archived Transaction History Utility Run this utility t o view or print archived transactions. To print archived transactions: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance >Transaction History Maintenance > Print Archived Transaction History . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . Restart the software. Select System > View Audit Trails > Archived Transactions Audit Trail to view archived transactions. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Transaction History Maintenance Utilities
topics.body: Running the Transaction History Maintenance Utilities Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the Transaction History Maintenance Utilities Run the Transaction History Maintenance utilities in the following order: Move Selected Transactions Archive & Delete Print Transaction History Print Archived Transaction History Related Topics Running the Move Selected Transactions Utility Running the Archive and Delete Utility Running the Print Current Transaction History Utility Running the Print Archived Transaction History Utility Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Transaction List Report
topics.body: Running the Transaction List Report Budget Planning > Running Budget Plan Reports > Running Account Budget Plan Reports Running the Transaction List Report The Transaction List report g enerates a list of all transactions that have been posted to the account budget plan within a specified date range. The report also includes account aging information. To run the Transaction List report: In the Account Budget Plan window, select Print Transactions from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Transaction List Report Setup window is displayed. Select the start and end dates to include in the report from the drop-down lists. To include account aging information, do not select a date range. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown Report
topics.body: Running the Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown Report Treatment Planning > Running Treatment Plan Reports Running the Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown Report The Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown report g enerates a breakdown of treatment plans. To generate a breakdown of treatment plan information: Select Reports > Practice Management > Treatment Plan Reports > Detailed Breakdown . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Treatment Plan Detailed Report Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending date range from a calendar. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. To include inactive patients in the report: Click the Include Inactive Pats checkbox. To restrict the report to a specific provider : Select the provider identification number from the Doctors section. Leave 999 to report on all providers. To include information for a specific plan in the report results : Type the information in the Options section. To restrict the report to a specific procedural code range : Select the codes from the fields in the Codes section. Note: To specify a single code, type it in the left Codes column and type 0 in the right field. Type the number that corresponds to the appropriate report format in the Report Format section. Click OK . Additional Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown Report Information: There are five formats for this report: List All Patient Treatment Plans�Lists all patient treatment plans. Under the patient name, the treatment plan items are sorted/listed by group number. For each patient, the total dollar value is listed for each treatment plan group, as well as total dollar value for the group�s estimated insurance. This format shows if the group is accepted/unaccepted, scheduled/not scheduled, and the appointment date and time if scheduled. Unaccepted Treatment Plans Only�Lists only the patients with unaccepted treatment plans. Accepted But Not Scheduled Treatment Plans�Lists only the patients with accepted treatment plans who have not been scheduled. All Not Scheduled And Not In Tickler�Lists only the patients with treatment plans who have not been scheduled or placed in the tickler file. All Scheduled Treatment Plans�Lists only the patients with scheduled treatment plans. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Treatment Plan Modes
topics.body: Using the Treatment Plan Modes Restorative Charting > Understanding Restorative Tab Modes Using the Treatment Plan Modes When the Restorative tab is displayed in a treatment plan mode , y ou can chart primary, secondary, or tertiary treatment plan procedures. Inside and outside work and conditions are displayed in this mode. By default, primary treatment plan items are displayed in red on the chart. Secondary treatment plan items are displayed in magenta, and tertiary treatment plan items are displayed in aqua. The Header bar�the area containing the patient name and patient number�is displayed in the color of the selected treatment plan mode. To view the chart in primary treatment plan mode, select an option: Select Mode > Treatment Plan Primary from the Mode menu. Click the Chart Treatment Plan button on the Chart Mode toolbar and select Treatment Plan 1 . Press F2 . To view the chart in secondary treatment plan mode, select an option: Select Mode > Treatment Plan Secondary from the Mode menu. Click the Chart Treatment Plan button on the Chart Mode toolbar and select Treatment Plan 2 . Press Ctrl + F2 . To view the chart in tertiary treatment plan mode, select an option: Select Mode > Treatment Plan Tertiary from the Mode menu. Click the Chart Treatment Plan button on the Chart Mode toolbar and select Treatment Plan 3 . Press Ctrl + F3 . When viewing an extracted tooth in a treatment plan mode, the tooth is hidden and an X is displayed to indicate that the tooth is no longer in the patient�s mouth. The X is displayed in blue to indicate inside work and green to indicate outside work. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Saving Treatment Plan Financial Arrangement Notes
topics.body: Saving Treatment Plan Financial Arrangement Notes Treatment Planning Saving Treatment Plan Financial Arrangement Notes You can use the software to save financial arrangement notes and print them on treatment plans. You can save, edit, and delete financial arrangement notes from either the Treatment Plan Financial Arrangements Setup window or while printing a treatment plan in Treatment Plan Financial Note List windows. To save treatment plan financial arrangement notes while printing a treatment plan, see Printing Treatment Plans . To save, edit, or delete a treatment plan financial arrangement note: Select System > Change System Settings > Treatment Plan Financial Arrangement Notes . The Treatment Plan Financial Note List window is displayed. Select an option: To add a new financial arrangement note : Click Add . The Treatment Plan Financial Note window is displayed. Type the note title and text in the appropriate fields, and click OK . Click Close . To edit a financial arrangement note : Select the note and click Edit . The note information is displayed in the Treatment Plan Financial Note window. Type the changes in the appropriate fields, and click OK . Click Close . To delete a financial arrangement note : Select the note and click Delete . A message asking if you want to delete the selected note is displayed. Click Yes . Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Treatment Planning
topics.body: Treatment Planning Treatment Planning To make pre-authorization and scheduling easier, treatment plans can be: Created Scheduled Organized into different treatment groups They do not affect: Patient Account Insurance balances For insured patients, the plan information includes the: Expected insurance amount Patient copay amount To chart treatment plans, see Charting Treatment Plans . Related Topics Understanding Treatment Plan List Window Options Setting Up Treatment Plans Submitting Treatment Plans for Pre-Authorization Printing Treatment Plans Saving Treatment Plan Financial Arrangement Notes Saving Treatment Plan Comments Changing Treatment Plan Acceptance States Deleting Treatment Plan Procedures Scheduling Treatment Plans Posting Treatment Plan Procedures Reconciling Treatment Plans Updating Treatment Plan Fee Schedules Viewing Archived Treatment Plans Placing Treatment Plans in the Tickler Recalculating Benefits Running Treatment Plan Reports Using the Case Presentation Module Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Treatment Plan List Window Options
topics.body: Understanding Treatment Plan List Window Options Treatment Planning Understanding Treatment Plan List Window Options You can: Schedule an appointment Change acceptance Print the treatment plan Re-estimate insurance Access the SoftDent case presentation module In this window, you can view: Electronic insurance eligibility Patient guarantor Claim status information The commands available from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu when using Enhanced Workspace are: Submit Ins : Displays the Submit Pre-Auths window. Use this window to start the insurance submission process. Enter the information to submit an insurance claim. Outstanding Ins : Displays the Claim List window. A list of all open claims for the patient is displayed. Claim Status : Hides the Charges, Ins Amt, and Patient columns in the Treatment Plan List window. The Amount, Est Ins, and Claim columns are displayed in the window. Pre-authorizations are indicated by an Au next to the claim number. You can view claim status information when these columns are displayed. To hide these columns and display the Charges, Ins Amt, and Patient columns, select the command. Eligibility : This command is enabled only if you use eVerifications insurance eligibility. eClaim Status : This command is enabled only if you use eClaims . Change State : Changes the treatment plan acceptance state. See Changing Treatment Plan Acceptance States . Print Treatment Plan : Displays the Patient Treatment Plan Report window. Use this command to print treatment plan group information. See Printing Treatment Plans . Guarantors : Displays the Patient Guarantor window. Use this command to view and update patient guarantor information. View Claim for Transaction : Displays the Claim window. Use this command to view the claim information for the treatment plan item. View Archived TP: Displays all declined and completed treatment plan procedures for the patient. Schedule Appointment : Displays the Treatment Plan Scheduling window. Use this command to schedule an appointment to perform the treatment plan procedure or the procedural group. See Scheduling Treatment Plans . Appt Book : Displays the Daily Appointments window. Use this command to view, schedule, and cancel patient appointments. Re-estimate Insurance : Recalculates the insurance estimates and patient benefits based on the used and unused benefits. Use this command if treatment plan items were originally calculated after the patient exhausted yearly plan benefits, and the benefits become available. Case Presentation Module : Displays the SoftDent Case Presentation Module List window. Post: Posts the selected treatment plan procedure. Post Group: Posts all of the procedures for the treatment plan group number you indicate. DITTO : Displays the Treatment Plan window. When the window is displayed, the information for the last entered transaction is displayed. MedVisor: Starts the MedVisor software. This command is enabled only if you have installed the MedVisor software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running Treatment Plan Reports
topics.body: Running Treatment Plan Reports Treatment Planning Running Treatment Plan Reports You can run reports that generate treatment plan information. Treatment plan-related reports are available from the Reports menu. You can run user-selected reports based on criteria that you define. Related Topics Running the Treatment Plan Summary Report Running the Treatment Plan Detailed Breakdown Report Running the Treatment Plan Transactions Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Treatment Plan Summary Report
topics.body: Running the Treatment Plan Summary Report Treatment Planning > Running Treatment Plan Reports Running the Treatment Plan Summary Report The Treatment Plan Summary report s ummarizes treatment plan information. The report tells you how many treatment plans are in your database, their cash value, the number and percentage of those plans that have been accepted and their cash value, the number and percentage of treatment plans scheduled, and how many plans are insurable or need pre-authorization. To generate the treatment plan summary: Select Reports > Practice Management > Treatment Plan Reports > Summary . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output and click OK . The Treatment Plan Summary Report Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending date range from a calendar. Select an option: To restrict the report to a specific provider : Select the provider identification number from the Doctors section. To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. To include inactive patients in the report: Click the Include Inactive Pats checkbox. To restrict the report to a specific procedural code range : Select the codes from the appropriate fields in the Codes section. Note: To specify a single code, type it in the left Codes column and type 0 in the right field. Click OK . Additional Treatment Plan Summary Report Information: The report contains the following information: Number of Planned Treatments�Number of treatment plan transactions posted. Value of Planned Treatments�Total dollar amount of all treatment plan transactions posted. Number Accepted by Patients�Number of treatment plan transactions marked as accepted. Value Accepted by Patients�Total dollar amount of all treatment plan transactions marked as accepted. Value NOT Yet Accepted�Total dollar amount of all treatment plan transactions not marked as accepted. Percent Accepted�Patient accepted = Number accepted by patients divided by Number of Planned Treatments. Number Currently Scheduled�Number of treatment plan transactions that have been scheduled. Percent of Accepted Scheduled�Percent of Accepted Scheduled = Number currently scheduled divided by Number accepted by patients. Number Insurable�Number of treatment plan transactions posted to insured patients. Number Pre-Authorized�Number of insurable treatment plan transactions that have a payment posted against the claim. Percent Pre-Authorized�Percent Pre-Authorized = Number preauthorized divided by Number insurable. Sample Report Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Treatment Plan Mode Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Treatment Plan Mode Toolbar Charting Treatment Plans > Using Treatment Planning Tab Toolbars Using the Treatment Plan Mode Toolbar The Treatment Plan Mode toolbar commands e nable you to toggle between the presentation manager and treatment planning views. To add a button to the toolbar, see Customizing Toolbars . Click the icon for the description of the Treatment Plan Mode toolbar buttons. Use the presentation manager to present treatment plan information to the patient . View the treatment plan list. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Treatment Plan Item Options
topics.body: Using Treatment Plan Item Options Using the SoftDent Charting Module > Using the Clinical History Tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container > Handling Treatment Plan Items Using Treatment Plan Item Options To use treatment plan item options: On the Restorative or Treatment Planning tabs in the Chart for window, click the Clinical History tab of the Clinical Detail Tab Container. Select the treatment plan item, right-click, and select an option: To display the item details : Select View . To delete the treatment plan item : Select Delete . A message asks you to confirm the deletion. Click Yes . To move the treatment plan item to the primary, secondary, or tertiary treatment plan : Select Switch selected items to TP1, TP2, or TP3 . A message informs you the selected item will be moved. Click Yes . To change the group number for the treatment plan item : Select Change Group Number . The Modify Group Number window is displayed. Select the treatment plan group, and click OK . To indicate that work for the treatment plan item is complete : Select Mark as Completed . To indicate the patient has either accepted or declined treatment : Select Accept/Reject . To view the chart details for the date on which the treatment plan was planned : Select View Chart as of . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Treatment Plan Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Treatment Plan Toolbar Charting Treatment Plans > Using Treatment Planning Tab Toolbars Using the Treatment Plan Toolbar The Treatment Plan toolbar commands enable you to: Delete Accept Reject Switch treatment groups Complete treatment plan items Build a case presentation module presentation Schedule an appointment Print the treatment plan Re-estimate insurance View online eligibility for the patient To add a button to the toolbar, see Customizing Toolbars . Click the icon for the description of the Treatment Plan toolbar buttons. Select a treatment plan item and click this button to switch the item from one treatment plan to the other. Select a treatment plan item and click this button to change the assigned treatment plan group. Select a treatment plan item and click this button to delete it. Select a treatment plan item and click this button to create a case presentation module presentation for it. Select a treatment plan item and click this button to change the treatment plan state. Click this button to indicate that all primary treatment plan items are complete. You are prompted to post the items. Click this button to indicate that all secondary treatment plan items are complete. You are prompted to post the items. Click this button to indicate that all tertiary treatment plan items are complete. You are prompted to post the items. Select a treatment plan item and click this button to indicate the item is complete. Select a treatment plan item and click this button to display the Treatment Planning Scheduling window. From this window, you can schedule an appointment for the procedure. Select a treatment plan item and click this button to re-estimate insurance for the treatment plan . Click this button to check the insurance eligibility for the patient. Click t his button to review guarantor information for the patient. Click this button to review the claim submitted for a treatment plan procedure. Click this button to submit a pre-authorization for a treatment plan group. Click this button to review any open patient claim. Click this button to review the status of an electronic claim. Click this button to review an archived treatment plan transaction. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Placing Treatment Plans In The Tickler
topics.body: Placing Treatment Plans In The Tickler Treatment Planning Placing Treatment Plans in the Tickler Use the Tickler t o store appointment information until it is convenient for you to reschedule it. You can place treatment plan procedures or groups in the Tickler or Short Call Tickler to enable you to schedule the appointments later. For more information, see Using the Tickler . To place a treatment group or procedure in the Tickler: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the patient Transactions window : Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the InOffice window : Select the patient and select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Select a procedure to place in the Tickler, and select Schedule Appointment from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Treatment Plan Scheduling window is displayed. Click OK . Select Tickler from the Options section of the Express bar or the top of the window. A submenu is displayed. Select an option: To place the treatment group in the Tickler : Select Place In Tickler . A message that informs you that the information is placed in the Tickler is displayed. Click OK . If the patient is willing to take an appointment on short notice : Select Place in Short Call Tickler . A message that informs you that the information is placed in the Short Call Tickler is displayed. Click OK . Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Treatment Planning Tab Toolbars
topics.body: Using Treatment Planning Tab Toolbars Charting Treatment Plans Using Treatment Planning Tab Toolbars The following toolbars can be displayed in the Toolbars section on the Treatment Planning tab: Common Treatment Plan Mode Treatment Plan You can customize each toolbar to display the commands that you most often use. To use the Common toolbar, see Using the Common Toolbar . Related Topics Using the Treatment Plan Mode Toolbar Using the Treatment Plan Toolbar Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Types of Backup Media
topics.body: Types of Backup Media Backing Up Data Types of Backup Media Depending on your system, there are many types of backup media available, including tapes, CD-ROMs, DVDs, and Internet-based services. It is recommended that you work with your hardware vendor to determine the best media type for your practice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Unblocking Slots
topics.body: Unblocking Slots Setting Up the Scheduler > Using Blocked Time Slots Unblocking Slots To unblock a blocked time slot, select an option: In the Daily Appointments (Daybook) or Book At A Glance windows : Select a time slot and press B. The slot is unblocked. In the Daily Appointments (Daybook) or Book At A Glance windows : Select a time slot and select Block/Unblock Slot from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The slot is unblocked. In the Daily Appointments (Daybook) or Book At A Glance windows : Select a time slot and schedule an appointment. A message asking you whether to override the blocked slot is displayed. Click Yes , the slot is unblocked, and the appointment is scheduled. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Flat Fee Plans
topics.body: Understanding Flat Fee Plans Setting Up Insurance > Handling Insurance Plan Information Understanding Flat Fee Plans Flat fee plans pay according to a flat rate. To estimate insurance payments for flat fee plans, set up accurate payment values in the bluebook for all procedures. When insurance payments for flat fee plans are estimated, the bluebook entry amount is used. If there is no bluebook entry, the percentages sp e cified on the Coverage tab of the Insurance plan record are used. Specify whether a deductible applies to each procedural class when entering dental flat fee plans. For more information, see Estimating Insurance for Flat Fee Plans . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding Medical History Notifications
topics.body: Understanding Medical History Notifications Handling Patient Medical History Understanding Medical History Notifications When a medical history is associated with a patient, the following is true: A red cross is displayed in the Alerts column of the Patient List window. A red cross is displayed at the bottom of a patient appointment in the Daily Appointments window. Medical alert information is displayed in the patient banner, in the patient chart. Medical alert information is displayed in the Med Alerts field in the Confirm Appt for window. Medical alert information is displayed on Page 1 of the Assistant. The Med Hist. field on the Dates tab of the patient record is updated each time you enter medical history information. This date is also updated when you print a Medical History quick letter. The date of the last medical history entry is displayed in the Last History column in the InOffice window. Patient history information is printed on the Daily Operatory schedule, routing slips, and the Patient Information report. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding NEA Status Colors
topics.body: Understanding NEA Status Colors Understanding NEA Status Colors To help you differentiate between the various NEA status types, claims are displayed in several colors. The colors include: Fuchsia: Used for Required, Not Sent, Rejected, and Ignored statuses. Black: Used for regular claims and for the Pending status. Blue: Used for pre-authorizations. Orange: Used for pre-authorizations with Required, Not Sent, Rejected, and Ignored statuses. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding NEA Status Types
topics.body: Understanding NEA Status Types Understanding NEA Status Types The following table lists the NEA statuses available. Status Definition Blank The status field is blank for existing claims created prior to installing SoftDent v11.0.11 or v12.0.1. Blank If the insurance company for a claim does not have NEA payer information, the status field is blank. Required This status is displayed for claims with procedure codes that require attachments to be sent, but which have not yet been created. The claims have not been submitted electronically. When the primary claim is marked Final, the NEA status of the secondary claim is changed to Required, if the secondary claim is ECS and NEA enabled and at least one of the procedures requires an attachment. Claims with the NEA status of Required that are loaded into the Electronic Services program can be submitted electronically. If such a claim is sent without an attachment, a warning is displayed on the Validation Error report, and the NEA status changes to Ignored. Pending This status is displayed for claims whose insurance companies do not want attachments until requested. Claims with the NEA status of Pending that are loaded into the Electronic Services program can be submitted electronically. A warning is displayed on the Validation Error report. Not Sent This status is displayed for claims with attachments that were created but have not been sent to NEA. Claims with the NEA status of Not Sent that are loaded into the Electronic Services program are not submitted electronically. An error is displayed on the Validation Error report. Ignored This status is displayed for claims that originally required the attachment to be sent before the claim, but were submitted electronically before the attachment was sent. Rejected This status is displayed for claims with attachments that were rejected by NEA. NEA#xxxxxxxx This status is displayed for claims with attachments that were successfully transmitted to NEA. This numeric ID returned from NEA is also displayed in the Notes field for the claim. Claims with the NEA status of NEA#xxxxxxxx that are loaded into the Electronic Services program can be submitted electronically and will not appear on the Validation Error report. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Understanding the Documents for Capture Window Toolbar
topics.body: Understanding the Documents for Capture Window Toolbar Using the Document Manager > Managing Documents Understanding the Documents for Capture Window Toolbar The buttons on the Documents for capture window toolbar include: Close: Closes the Documents for capture window. File: Enables you to import images, select a TWAIN source, and TWAIN acquire. View: Enables you to display buttons on the toolbar. Save Capture: Saves the image and adds it to the patient document folder. Previous: Moves to the previously scanned image in the document. Next: Moves to the next scanned image in the document. Re-Scan Document: Deletes the document and begins the scanning process. Re-Scan Page: Deletes the current page and begins the scanning process. Append Page(s): Adds a single or multi-page scan to the end of a document. Add to Review Queue: Adds the document to the Document Review window, where it can be assigned to another user to review. Exits the video program. Selects a TWAIN source from which to import images. Imports an image from the selected TWAIN source. Imports an image from an existing folder. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Uninsured Accounts Report
topics.body: Running the Uninsured Accounts Report Setting Up Accounts > Running Account Reports Running the Uninsured Accounts Report The Uninsured Accounts report generates a list of uninsured accounts, including the account guarantor's home phone number and current balance. Sample Report To run the Uninsured Accounts report: Select Reports > Account > Not Insured . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output and click OK . The Account List Report window is displayed. In the Account Names section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending names you want in the report. Select Include Inactive Accounts to include inactive accounts in the report. In the Account User Codes section, type the user codes you want to include or exclude from the report. If you selected the Merge Letter Expert output option, the Letter Expert Template ID field is active. Click the drop-down arrow to select a template from the list. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Uninsured Patients Report
topics.body: Running the Uninsured Patients Report Setting Up Patients > Running Patient Reports Running the Uninsured Patients Report The Uninsured Patient report generates an alphabetical list of uninsured patients. Sample Report To generate a list of uninsured patients: Select Reports > Patient > Uninsured . The Output Options window is displayed. Select an output option and click OK . The Patient List Report window is displayed. Select the starting and ending patient names from the drop-down lists. Select an option: To include information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Include field. To exclude information for patients with a specific user code : Type the code in the Exclude field. If you are exporting the report to Excel, the following options are selected: Print Raw Data: Each piece of data in the report is placed in a separate column on the Raw tab. Print Formatted Data: The report information is displayed in the same manner as if it were sent to the printer on the Formatted tab. Deselect an option if you do not want that type of data to display. If you leave both options selected, the report information is displayed on two tabs in the Excel spreadsheet. Deselect Include Color/Shading if you do not want shading on the report. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Accessing Unlisted Websites
topics.body: Accessing Unlisted Websites Using E-mail and WebLink > Accessing the Internet Accessing Unlisted Websites To visit a website that is not added to the database: Select Internet > Other Web Sites . The Web Sites List window is displayed. Click Add . The Web Sites window is displayed. Click Use Current Site . Your browser is displayed. Locate the appropriate site. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Posting An Unscheduled Treatment Plan Procedure
topics.body: Posting An Unscheduled Treatment Plan Procedure Treatment Planning > Posting Treatment Plan Procedures Posting an Unscheduled Treatment Plan Procedure After you have added a treatment plan procedure in the software, you can post it without scheduling an appointment. To post an unscheduled treatment plan procedure: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Transactions from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Transactions from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The patient Transactions List window is displayed. Select Treatment Plan from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. To post a new procedure, click Add . The Treatment Plan window is displayed. After you enter the procedure, click OK . The Treatment Plan List window is displayed. Select an option: To post the procedure : Select Post from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. To post the treatment procedural group : Select Post Group from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the View Unsettled VisaNet Transactions Utility
topics.body: Running the View Unsettled VisaNet Transactions Utility Utilities > Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running the View Unsettled VisaNet Transactions Utility Run this utility to view unsettled VisaNet transactions. To view unsettled VisaNet transactions: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Maintenance > View Unsettled VisaNet Transactions . The View Unsettled VisaNet Transactions window is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Unsubmitted Claims Report
topics.body: Running the Unsubmitted Claims Report Submitting and Tracking Claims > Running Claim Reports Running the Unsubmitted Claims Report The Unsubmitted Claims report g enerates a list of insurable transactions that have not been submitted to an insurance company for payment. The report includes a list of all unsubmitted charges for each patient by service date. You should run this report weekly. Sample Report To generate a list of unsubmitted claims: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Unsubmitted Claims . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Unsubmitted Insurance Report Setup window is displayed. Select the starting and ending dates from the drop-down lists. Type the format in which to print the report in the Report Format field. To exclude treatment plan transactions from the report, deselect Include Treatment Plan Transactions . Click OK . Additional Unsubmitted Claims Report Information: Information on this report is sorted by patient ID and includes: Patient name and ID Date ADA code Charge description Procedure fee Estimated insurance payment Procedure type (P = Pre-Auth, A = Actual, O = Ortho, and S = Secondary). Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Account Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Credit Card Information
topics.body: Updating Account Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Credit Card Information Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans > Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Account Budget Plans Updating Account Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Credit Card Information To use a different credit card for account budget plan recurring credit card payments: In the Account List window, select an account and select Acct Budget Plan . The Account Budget Plan List window is displayed. Select Terms . The FIXED LOAN TERMS window is displayed. Click the CC Payment tab. The tab is the active window. Select the day on which the credit card payment is submitted from the Day to Authorize drop-down list, and click Reset Credit Card Info . Do one of the following: Type the credit card information in the Credit Card Info section of the window. Swipe the credit card to enter credit card information. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Credit Card Payment Plan Credit Card Information
topics.body: Updating Credit Card Payment Plan Credit Card Information Budget Planning > Creating Credit Card Payment Plans Updating Credit Card Payment Plan Credit Card Information To use a different credit card for credit card payments: Do one of the following: To update account credit card payment plan information, in the Account List window, select an account and select Credit Card Payment Plan . To update patient credit card payment plan information, in the Patient List window, select a patient and select Credit Card Payment Plan . All recurring credit card payments will be billed to the credit card. Click the CC Payment tab. The tab is the active window. Select the day on which the credit card payment is submitted from the Day to Authorize drop-down list, and click Reset Credit Card Info . Do one of the following: Type the credit card information in the Credit Card section of the window. Swipe the credit card to enter credit card information. Click OK . Need more information? topics.title: Updating Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Information
topics.body: Updating Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Information Budget Planning > Handling Patient Budget Plans > Setting Up Recurring Credit Card Payments for Patient Budget Plans Updating Patient Budget Plan Recurring Credit Card Payment Information To use a different credit card for patient budget plan recurring credit card payments: In the Patient List window, select a patient and select Budget Plan . The Patient Budget Plan window is displayed. Click the CC Payment tab. The tab is the active window. Select the day on which the credit card payment is submitted from the Day to Authorize drop-down list, and click Reset Credit Card Info . Do one of the following: Type the credit card information in the Credit Card Info section of the window. Swipe the credit card to enter credit card information. Click OK . Need more information? topics.title: Updating SoftDent
topics.body: Updating SoftDent Getting Started > Using the Software Update Service (SUS) Updating SoftDent All workstations are notified about an update, but you must first install the update on the server using the procedure below. Once you update the server, you will be prompted to update the workstations when you start SoftDent. Tip: You can manually check for updates at any time. Right-click the triangle logo and select Check for Updates . You will be required to restart your computer after an update. You might be prompted to shut down your computer before the update can take place. To update SoftDent: Do one of the following: Click the Carestream Software Updates message. Right-click the triangle logo in the system tray, and select View Updates . Click the click here link when you open SoftDent and an update is available. The Software Update Service � Carestream Software Updates window is displayed. Select the update you want to install, click Install , and follow the prompts. After the update has been installed on the server, repeat the steps above for each workstation. OR Click Schedule . The Software Update Service � Schedule Update Installation window is displayed. Select a day and time for the update installation, and click OK . When you schedule an update, the SUS will attempt to update all machines that are running. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Bluebook Fees
topics.body: Updating Bluebook Fees Handling Transactions and Codes > Using Fee Schedules Updating Bluebook Fees To update bluebook fees: Select System > Fees Maintenance > Update Bluebook Fees . The Update Bluebook Fee window is displayed. Click OK . All bluebooks are updated with the current fee schedule 0 from the ADA/Transaction Codes window. Related Topics Using Fee Schedules Copying a Fee Schedule Changing a Fee Schedule Updating a Fee Schedule Copying a Bluebook Between Plans Copying an Allowed Amount from Plan to Plan Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Update Benefits to Patient Utility
topics.body: Using Update Benefits to Patient Utility eServices > Using Additional eServices Options Using Update Benefits to Patient Utility The Update Benefits to Patient utility e nables you to change the designation of benefits on an insurance plan. To run this utility: In the eServices window, select Setup > Benefits to patient Utility . The Update Benefits To Patient window is displayed. Select the plans you want to update and click Update . Plans that are not selected assign benefits to the provider. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating a Fee Schedule
topics.body: Updating a Fee Schedule Handling Transactions and Codes > Using Fee Schedules Updating a Fee Schedule The process of updating a fee schedule changes the fees for existing appointments or treatment plans. To update a fee schedule: Select System > Fees Maintenance > Update Fee Schedule . The Update Fee Schedule window is displayed. Select an option: To update a single fee: Type the procedure code in the AdaCode field. To update all fees: Leave the AdaCode field set to 0. Select an option: To apply the new fee schedule to appointments already scheduled: Select Update Appointment Fee . To apply the new fee schedule to existing treatment plans: Select Update Treatment Plan Fee . To update only those treatment plans of a particular age, type the number of days or a date in the appropriate field. Click OK . Related Topics Using Fee Schedules Copying a Fee Schedule Changing a Fee Schedule Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Fees�End of Year
topics.body: Updating Fees�End of Year End of Year Procedures Updating Fees�End of Year At the end of the year, you might decide to increase the fees for the procedures your office performs. If you only have a few procedure fees to change, you update the fees individually. If you want to increase all of the fees your office charges (for example, a 5% increase), you can update all of the fees at one time. Select from the following options: Updating Individual Fees Updating All Fees Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Medical Histories
topics.body: Updating Medical Histories Handling Patient Medical History Updating Medical Histories You can edit medical history information only on the same day it is entered. After that, you can only update the medical history and create a history with a new date. You cannot delete medical history information. Only users with the proper security settings can enter a medical history. For more information, see Setting Medical History Security . When you edit information in the Medical History window, the associated patient record is locked. You can view the Medical History window without locking the patient record. Related Topics Editing Medical Histories Entered on the Current Date Updating Previously Entered Medical Histories Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating All Fees�End of Year
topics.body: Updating All Fees�End of Year End of Year Procedures > Updating Fees -- End of Year Updating All Fees�End of Year You must have security rights to edit or delete codes. To update all of the fees in a fee schedule: Select System > Fees Maintenance > Change Fee Schedule . The Change Fee Schedule window is displayed. In the Fee Schedule # to change field, type the number of the fee schedule you want to change. Select the Increase or Decrease option button. Select the percentage by which to increase or decrease the fee schedule. If you don't want to use the available percentages, click the Other option button and type the percentage you want. Click OK . A window is displayed, asking you to confirm the change. Click Yes . The changes might take several minutes, during which time status messages are displayed. The Update Fee Schedule window is displayed. Select an option: To update the fee for existing appointments: Select Update Appointment Fee. T o update the fee for existing treatment plans: Select Update Treatment Plan Fee. To update only those treatment plans of a particular age, type the number of days or a date in the appropriate field. Click OK . If you click Cancel , no updates are made, but the fee schedule is changed. Related Topics Updating Fees�End of Year Updating Individual Fees�End of Year Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Individual Fees�End of Year
topics.body: Updating Individual Fees�End of Year End of Year Procedures > Updating Fees -- End of Year Updating Individual Fees�End of Year You must have security rights to edit or delete codes. To edit an existing procedure fee: Select List > ADA Codes . The ADA/Transaction Code List window is displayed. Select the code and click Edit . The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window is displayed. Select the Scheduler/Fees tab, and type the new fee in the appropriate Fee Schedule field(s). Click OK . The Update Fees Wizard window is displayed. Select an option: To update fees for existing appointments and treatment plans at a later time: Click Update Later . To update fees for existing appointments and treatment plans now: Click Update Now . The Update Fee Scheduler window is displayed. Select Update Appointment Fee to update the fee for existing appointments. Select Update Treatment Plan Fee to update the fee for existing treatment plans. To update only those treatment plans of a particular age, type the number of days or a date in the appropriate field. Click OK twice. Click Close twice. Tip In any window, press Ctrl + C to access the ADA/CPT/Transaction Code List window. Related Topics Updating Fees�End of Year Updating All Fees�End of Year Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Update New Plans
topics.body: Using Update New Plans eServices > Using Additional eServices Options Using Update New Plans The Update New Plans option u pdates insurance plans that are capable of transmitting claims electronically, but have not been enabled. To update insurance plans: From the main menu, select Setup > Update new plans . The Update New Insurance Plans window is displayed. Select an option: To update all insurance plans : Click Update . All insurance plans listed are enabled to transmit claims electronically. To update selected insurance plans : Deselect the plans you do not want to update and click Update . You are prompted to run this option every 30 days. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Presentation Dates
topics.body: Updating Presentation Dates Treatment Planning > Using the Case Presentation Module > Handling Case Presentations Updating Presentation Dates To update when the case presentation was shown to the patient to the current date: Select an option: In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Documents from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. The Documents for window is displayed. The document tree is separated into sections that contain the clinical images, documents, case presentations, and portrait images associated with the patient. Select the presentation from the Case Presentation Module Presentations section of the tree, right-click, and select Update Date Presented . A message that asks you to confirm updating the presentation date to the current date is displayed. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating the Trojan Database
topics.body: Updating the Trojan Database Trojan Updating the Trojan Database If you subscribe to Trojan, you will receive monthly update disks for both traditional and managed care insurance plans. To keep your insurance current and accurate, install these disks as soon as you get them. Depending on the amount of data, the update disks can be installed in a matter of minutes. To update the Trojan database: Select File > Trojan > Monthly Update . The Trojan Disk Load Options window is displayed. Select an option: To update traditional plan information : Select Load Traditional Plan Disks . To update managed care plan information : Select Load Managed Care Plan Disk . Select the letter that corresponds to the drive into which the Trojan disks are inserted in the Enter Disk Drive to Load from drop-down list. Click OK . A message informs you to insert the disk into the drive. Insert the disk and press Enter. After the information is copied, a message informs you to insert another disk. Insert the next disk and click Yes . When you have installed all of the disks, click No . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Updating Treatment Plan Fee Schedules
topics.body: Updating Treatment Plan Fee Schedules Treatment Planning Updating Treatment Plan Fee Schedules Use fee schedules t o attach several charges to a code. You can: Assign a different fee for each doctor or incorporate unique requirements, such as a senior citizen discount, into a fee schedule. Enter up to 27 different fee schedules for each code. To assign a fee schedule, see Adding ADA, CPT, and Transaction Codes and Using Fee Schedules . If you need to change a fee schedule amount for a production code, you can update all treatment plan transactions for the code at the same time. To update a production code or treatment plan fee schedule: Select an option: Select ADA Codes from the List menu. From any window, press Ctrl + C. The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code List window is displayed. Select the code that requires modification, and click Edit . The ADA/CPT/Transaction Code window is displayed. Click the Scheduler/Fees tab. Type the fee in the Fee Schedules section of the window, and click OK . The Update Fees Wizard is displayed. Select an option: To update all future treatment plan transactions for the code : Click Update Now . The Update Fee Scheduler window is displayed. Select Update Treatment Fee , select the effective date from the Treatment Plans Before _ will be updated field, and click OK . A message that informs you that the fee schedules have been updated is displayed. Click OK . If a patient has previously scheduled or accepted a treatment plan containing an updated code, the fee is not updated. If the patient has DMO or Medicaid and the code in the Appointment Card window is a Bluebook code, the appointment card is not updated with the new fee. To update treatment plan transactions at a later date : Click Update Later . The ADA/Transaction Code window is displayed. Click Close twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Contacts
topics.body: Using Contacts Contacts and Memos Using Contacts The contact management feature enables you to track all communications to and from your practice. Logging contacts creates a history of your office's interaction with: Patients Insurance plans Account guarantors Referring doctors Providers Employers Insurance companies Schools Laboratories Pharmacies A contact is automatically logged each time you: Add a patient to a merge file Generate a patient quick letter You can also manually log a contact; for example, to remind yourself of a fax or letter you have received. When you use the Auto Dialer to call an individual or company or when confirming an appointment, a message asks you to log a contact. Related Topics Setting Up Contact Notes Adding Contacts Editing Contacts Deleting Contacts Printing Contacts Viewing HIPAA-Specific Contacts Adding Memos to a Contact Compressing Contacts Running Contact Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Ditto
topics.body: Using Ditto Treatment Planning > Setting Up Treatment Plans Using Ditto The software enables you to use the DITTO feature to enter multiple iterations of a treatment plan procedure. Example : If the treatment plan you suggest requires sealants on five teeth, add the first sealant, and use the feature to populate the standard sealant information automatically. You can select the appropriate tooth, but do not have to retype information in other sections of the window. To use the DITTO feature: After saving the treatment plan item in the Treatment Plan window, select DITTO from the Options section of the Express bar. The information for the previous transaction is displayed. For more information, see Setting Up Treatment Plans . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Document Manager
topics.body: Using the Document Manager Using the Document Manager Using the document manager, you can: Capture documents and attach them to records Assign account, patient, referring doctor, claim, insurance company, insurance plan, and lab case documents to another user to review Any x-rays or intraoral pictures captured in the charting module: Are automatically attached to the patient Can be attached to correspondence or claims Using the document manager with clinical images, you can: E-mail Capture new Set capture preferences Annotate Set properties Edit Delete You can also access the dental imaging software. Related Topics Viewing Documents Reviewing Documents Organizing Documents in the Document Tree Managing Documents Viewing Image Comments Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Experts
topics.body: Using Experts Using Experts The office expert, letter expert, and contact expert features enable you to: Automatically run reports Merge patient information to a letter Track contact information between you and your patients Use the office expert to select: Office communication items Run times After these items run, the results display in the Office Expert window. Use the letter expert to: Generate letters from a patient�s record to be printed later Generate a group of letters to be printed later Batch letters to be printed later Use the contact expert to record details of any: Attempted contacts Successful contacts The following information is displayed for each contact and can be used to quickly view details: Patient Transaction Account Guarantors Appointment Treatment plan Claim Related Topics Using the Office Expert Using the Contact Expert Using the Letter Expert Working with Letter Expert Templates Setting Letter Expert Preferences Merging Letter Expert Documents Executing the Word Processor Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the File Menu
topics.body: Using the File Menu Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the File Menu The File menu e nables you to close the charting module, print a patient chart, set printer preferences, e-mail the current chart, and exit the software. The commands available from the File menu are: Close Charting Module : Select this command to close the charting module and return to the software. Print : Select this command to print the current chart. Print Setup : Select this command to set up your printer preference. E-Mail Current Chart : Select this command to e-mail the current chart. Exit : Select this command to close the charting module and the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Help
topics.body: Using Help Getting Started Using Help Online Help topics: Can be accessed from anywhere in the application by pressing F1 or selecting Help > Help Topics from the menu bar. Correspond to manual chapter and feature headings. Include general information about each window, feature, menu, and function. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

Include general information about each window, feature, menu, and function. Can be bookmarked using the Favorites tab on each workstation. topics.title: Using the KIOSK Check-In Module
topics.body: Using the KIOSK Check-In Module Using the KIOSK Check-In Module You can use the KIOSK check-in module to set up a KIOSK station for your waiting room that your patients can use to sign themselves in when they arrive for appointments. Welcome Window The KIOSK displays a Welcome window you can customize for your practice. This window has instructions for proceeding to the next window for signing in. Data Entry Window From the Welcome window, the patient is presented with a series of windows where he is asked to enter the following information: Last name: The patient is prompted to enter his last name. If only one appointment is found for a patient with that last name, a message is displayed, telling the patient that the office is aware of his arrival. If more than one appointment for that last name is found, the patient is asked to enter his date of birth. Date of birth: Since there is more than one appointment with the same last name, the patient is prompted to enter his date of birth. If only one appointment is found for a patient with the same name and date of birth, a message is displayed, telling the patient that the office is aware of his arrival. If more than one appointment is found for a patient with the same name and the same date of birth, the patient is prompted to enter his home phone number. Home phone number: Since there is more than one appointment for the same name and date of birth, the patient is prompted to enter his home phone number. If only one appointment is found for a patient with the same name, date of birth, and phone number, a message is displayed, telling the patient that the office is aware of his arrival. If more than one appointment is found for a patient with the same name, date of birth, and phone number, the patient is asked to see the receptionist. Goodbye Window When the patient has successfully signed in, a message is displayed, telling the patient that the office is aware of his arrival. If the sign-in was not successful, a message is displayed, telling the patient to see the receptionist. You can customize the messages used in the Goodbye window to suit the needs of your practice. Related Topics Installing the KIOSK Check-In Module on a Workstation Activating and Deactivating the KIOSK Check-In Module Opening and Closing the KIOSK Check-In Module Configuring KIOSK Check-In Module Settings Monitoring Checked-In Patients Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Memos
topics.body: Using Memos Contacts and Memos Using Memos You can enter reminders about pending contacts with: Patients Insurance plans Account guarantors Referring doctors Providers Employers Insurance companies Schools Laboratories Pharmacies You can also use reminder memos for: Interoffice communication Personal reminders Memos are displayed in the Today's Memos window on the appropriate day. A message asks you to create a reminder memo when you add a lab case or when you contact someone using the auto dialer. A memo is created automatically to remind you when it is time to send a continuous care form. Related Topics Adding Reminder Memos Editing Reminder Memos Deleting Reminder Memos Deleting Memos by Date Range Accessing the Auto Dialer While Viewing Memos Handling Today's Memos Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Practice Central
topics.body: Using Practice Central Getting Started Using Practice Central The Practice Central window contains all your important daily information, provides a focal point for your daily activities, and enables you to view and access key information. Because Practice Central content is customized for each user, office managers and individual users can specify the content contained in the window to reflect each user's responsibilities. Practice Central content is displayed as static data or linked data. Static data is a view-only list of data. Linked data is a hypertext list of data. Clicking linked data accesses the corresponding function. You must run Microsoft Internet Explorer software, version 8 or higher, to display the Practice Central window. See Practice Central Items for a description of items that can be displayed in the Practice Central window. The current date and time is displayed in the upper-right corner of the Practice Central window. Clicking Refresh in the upper-right corner refreshes the Practice Central data. The following links are displayed in the Resource Center section: My Preferences : Displays the Practice Central Preferences window. Logout/Switch User : Exits the software or switches users. Exit SoftDent : Exits the software. The following links are displayed in the Links section: SoftDent Home Page : Accesses the SoftDent practice management software web page. Product Education : Accesses the web page containing training and education opportunities for the software. Online Resource Center: Accesses the Online Resource Center, a technical support database you can search for information on troubleshooting technical issues. The following information is displayed in the Production Goals section: Today : Displays the goals, scheduled and actual, for the current date in the Rcvtot window. Tomorrow : Displays the scheduled goal and actual goal for tomorrow in the Rcvtot window. Week : Displays the scheduled goal and actual goal for the current week in the Week At A Glance window. Month : Displays the scheduled goal and scheduled amount for the current month in the Month At A Glance window. The following data is displayed in the FYI section located in the lower-left side of the Practice Central window: Patients Remaining Today : Displays the number of remaining patients scheduled for an appointment on the current date. Insured Patients : Displays the number of remaining insured patients scheduled for an appointment on the current date. Remaining New Patients : Displays the number of remaining new patients scheduled for an appointment on the current date. Non-HIPAA Acknowledgement Patients : Displays the number of remaining patients who have not received a HIPAA Acknowledgement scheduled for an appointment on the current date. Last Scheduled Appointment : Displays the time of the last scheduled appointment for the current date. T he left side of the Practice Central window contains the features selected in the Practice Central Preferences window. The information in this section of the window can be customized to ensure information reflects the office responsibilities of the user. Tip You can access Practice Central by selecting View > Practice Central . Related Topics A ccessing Information from Practice Central Setting Practice Central Preferences Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Perio Menu
topics.body: Using the Perio Menu Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the Perio Menu The commands available from the Perio menu are: Options : This command is enabled when the Perio tab is the active window. Select this command to display the Perio Properties window. In this window, you can set perio charting options and properties. See Setting Perio Chart Options and Properties . Voice Activation Module > SoftDent Voice Activation Module Options : Select this command to display the SoftDent Voice Activation Module Options window. In this window, you can set voice activation module options. See Setting Voice Activation Module Options . Voice Activation Module > Show 'What Can I Say' Dialog : Select this command to display the Show 'What Can I Say' window. In this window, you can view a list of voice activation module commands. See Using Voice Recognition Commands . Voice Activation Module > Pause Voice Activation Module : Select this command to pause the voice activation module. See Using Auto Advance for Perio Charting . New Perio Chart : This command is enabled when the Perio tab is the active window. Select this command to create a new perio chart. Perio Mode : This command is enabled when the Perio tab is the active window. Select this command to display the Perio tab in the Chart for window. A checkmark is displayed next to the command to indicate the chart is displayed in perio mode. Display Numeric Chart : This command is enabled when the Perio tab is the active window. Select this command to display the Perio tab in perio numeric mode. View Perio History : This command is enabled when the Perio tab is the active window. Select this command to display the charted periodontal history for the chart. Tooth Detail : Select a tooth and select this command to display the Posted Work Performed for Tooth window. In this window, you can view the posted work associated with the tooth. Provider List : Select this command to display the Provider List window. In this window, you can view a list of practice providers. You can select a provider from this window. Select/Compare Perio Exams : This command is enabled when the Perio tab is the active window. Select this command to display the View Perio Exams window. Patient Education : Click this command to display the Patient Education Setup window. In this window, you can run a patient education program within the charting module. The first time you run the program, you must enter the drive, directory, and path where the education program is located. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Perio Mode Chart
topics.body: Using the Perio Mode Chart Periodontal Charting > Understanding Perio Tab Modes Using the Perio Mode Chart When the perio mode chart is selected: The most up-to-date charting information is displayed You can chart all periodontal information Periodontal values are displayed To view the perio mode, select Perio Mode on the Perio Views toolbar . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Perio Mobility Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Perio Mobility Toolbar Periodontal Charting > Using Perio Tab Toolbars Using the Perio Mobility Toolbar The Perio Mobility toolbar commands enable you to chart periodontal mobility. Click the icon for the description of the Perio Mobility toolbar buttons. Click this button and select a tooth to assign a mobility value of 0 . Click this button and select a tooth to assign a mobility value of 1 . Click this button and select a tooth to assign a mobility value of 2 . Click this button and select a tooth to assign a mobility value of 3 . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Presentation Manager
topics.body: Using the Presentation Manager Charting Treatment Plans Using the Presentation Manager The presentation manager e nables you to use an anatomical tooth display when showing treatment plan information to the patient. To display charted treatment plan information in the presentation manager: After charting the treatment plan items, click the Treatment Planning tab. Select Switch to Presentation manager on the Treatment Plan Mode toolbar . Completed procedures are displayed in blue. Completed procedures that were performed by another doctor are displayed in green. Tooth conditions are displayed in red. Posted primary treatment plan items are displayed in red. Posted secondary treatment plan items are displayed in magenta. Posted tertiary treatment plan items are displayed in dark green. To view the patient images or x-rays associated with the patient, click the Clinical Images tab in the Clinical Detail Tab container. To view the treatment plan items, select Switch to Treatment Planning . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Perio Views Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Perio Views Toolbar Periodontal Charting > Using Perio Tab Toolbars Using the Perio Views Toolbar The Perio Views toolbar commands e nable you to display a new perio chart, the periodontal numeric chart, or view the periodontal history associated with the selected chart. Click the icon for the description of the Perio Views toolbar buttons. Click this button to display a new perio chart . Click this button to display the tab in perio mode. This button is selected when you open a new patient chart . Click this button to display the tab in perio numeric mode . Click this button to display the charted periodontal history of the chart . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Electronic Router
topics.body: Using the Electronic Router Tracking Patients in the Office > Handling InOffice Transactions Using the Electronic Router The Electronic Router feature is accessible from the InOffice window, the Daily Appointments window, and the patient chart. The Electronic Router contains patient personal and financial information, which is typically printed on routing slips. From the ELECTRONIC ROUTER window, you can: Set up or schedule treatment plan procedures Schedule up to three follow-up appointments View existing appointments Create history files From this window, you can also view : Contacts, guarantors, insurance information, financial information, and any patients that are referred by the selected patient. To use the Electronic Router: In the InOffice window, select Electronic Router from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The ELECTRONIC ROUTER window is displayed. Select an option: To edit the appointment scheduled for the current day : Select Edit Appointment from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Appointment Card window is displayed. Make the changes and click OK . To display the guarantor name, chart number, exam history, and the names and ages of other patients on the account : Click the Add'l Info tab. To schedule a patient for a follow-up appointment : Select Follow Up from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Electronic Router: Follow up 1 window is displayed. Enter the information and click OK . You can enter up to three follow-up appointments for a patient. To enter another appointment, select Follow Up from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu, and enter the information. The appointment information is saved and the Appointment Card window is displayed Appointments set up in the ELECTRONIC ROUTER window must be scheduled at the front desk during check out. For more information, see Checking Out Patients . After all scheduled procedures are performed, select Complete from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. When Complete is selected : Complete is displayed in red text at the bottom right of the Appointment Card window and in the Code column of the InOffice window to ensure that the front office staff knows that all procedures on the patient�s appointment card were performed. You cannot edit any appointment card procedures after the status is changed to Complete. The Complete command is a toggle switch. Select the command again to edit procedures in the Appointment Card window. Click Close . Related Topics Displaying the Electronic Router in the Daily Appointments Window Setting Call Back Reminders in the Electronic Router Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!
