topics.title: Using the Toolbar
topics.body: Using the Toolbar Getting Started > Using the Workspace Using the Toolbar The toolbar is displayed at the top of the active window in the client area of the Enhanced Workspace. Toolbar buttons that are disabled and greyed out indicate that you do not have the appropriate security access rights or that the operation is not available from the current mode. Example : Find is disabled while you view the patient record details. To select a toolbar button, click the button. If the window is displayed in SCAN mode, press the key corresponding to the underlined letter on the button. Example : Press D to delete a record. If the window is displayed in ADD or EDIT mode, press Alt + the underlined letter on the button keys. Example : If you are editing a record, press the Alt key + C to cancel the edits. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Toolbars
topics.body: Using Toolbars Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using Toolbars Each Chart for window tab contains toolbars that enable you to access specific features. A description of the current function is displayed in the Description field, the date of the chart is displayed in the Date of Chart drop-down list, and the tab-specific toolbars are displayed beneath this information. The following toolbars can be displayed in the Toolbars section of the Restorative tab: Common Procedure Codes Chart Work Conditions Mobility The following toolbars can be displayed in the Toolbars section of the Perio tab: Perio Views Probe Keypad Perio Mobility Plaque Calculus Conditions The following toolbars can be displayed in the Toolbars section of the Exams tab: Common Exam Exam Options The following toolbars can be displayed in the Toolbars section of the Clinical Imaging tab: Common Clinical Imaging Image Editor Image Annotate Image Capture Image Layout The following toolbars can be displayed in the Toolbars section of the Treatment Planning tab: Common Treatment Plan Mode Treatment Plan Related Topics Showing and Hiding Toolbars Customizing Toolbars Showing and Hiding Toolbar Titles Using the Common Toolbar Need more information? topics.title: Using the Tickler
topics.body: Using the Tickler Scheduling Appointments Using the Tickler The Tickler is a rescheduling system that can be accessed from all of the scheduler windows. When you cancel an appointment, you can save the information to the following files until you are ready to reschedule the appointment: Tickler Short Call You can also: Enter treatment plan appointments Send all broken and missed appointment to Tickler file When you attempt to make an appointment for a patient who has an entry in the Tickler file : A message that asks whether to display the patient�s Tickler entry is displayed. You can retrieve a previously canceled appointment from the Tickler file. You can add information to the short call Tickler : To indicate that a patient is willing to be rescheduled on short notice. Related Topics Placing Canceled Appointments in the Tickler File Placing Missed Appointments in the Tickler File Placing Treatment Plan Appointments in the Tickler File Adding Appointment Information to the Tickler File Scheduling Tickler Appointments Deleting Tickler File Entries Need more information? topics.title: Using TeamTalk
topics.body: Using TeamTalk Using TeamTalk The TeamTalk feature enables you to set up notes to be displayed for specific tasks, records, and events at specific times. For example, if you create a note to be displayed when you edit an account, the note is displayed whenever the account record is opened in EDIT mode. TeamTalk notes are specific to the databases in which they are saved. For example, account database events can include adding a patient, posting a payment, or printing a billing statement. Patient database events can include activating a new patient or making an appointment. Insurance plan database events can include editing an insurance plan, updating from Trojan data, or calling a plan administrator. TeamTalk notes that you have set up for the future or that have expired are not shown in this window. Related Topics Understanding TeamTalk Events Setting Up TeamTalk Notes Using TeamTalk Options Viewing Notes for a Specific Record Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the View Menu
topics.body: Using the View Menu Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the View Menu The commands available from the View menu are: Main Tool Bar : This command is enabled if you use Classic Workspace to view your windows. Select this command to either show or hide the main toolbar. A checkmark next to the command indicates that the main toolbar is displayed. Status Bar : Select this command to either show or hide the Status bar. A checkmark indicates that the bar is displayed at the bottom of the window. Watch Summary : Select this command to display the Watch Summary window. In this window, you can view watch information for a chart. Review Assistant's Info : Select this command to display the Assistant's Info window. See Viewing the Assistant's Info Window . 'What Can I Say' Dialog : This command is enabled when the Perio tab is the active window and voice activation is active. Select this command to display the Show 'What I Can Say' window. In this window, you can view a list of voice activation module commands. See Using the Voice Activation Module Commands . Quadrant 1 : Select this command to display the first quadrant. Quadrant 2 : Select this command to display the second quadrant. Quadrant 3 : Select this command to display the third quadrant. Quadrant 4 : Select this command to display the fourth quadrant. Upper Arch : This command is enabled when the Perio tab is the active window. Select this command to display the upper arch. Lower Arch : This command is enabled when the Perio tab is the active window. Select this command to display the lower arch. Full Mouth : Select this command to display the full mouth. Charting Module Toolbars : Select this command to display a list of charting module toolbars. Select the appropriate toolbar to either show or hide it. A checkmark next to the toolbar indicates that it is displayed. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using a Backup Checklist
topics.body: Using a Backup Checklist Backing Up Data Using a Backup Checklist It is your responsibility to keep your data backups accurate, current, and protected. Many backup systems provide limited protection, unless they are part of a comprehensive backup program. To maintain effective protection for your records, you must facilitate an effective backup strategy that actively involves staff participation in your daily operation. It is recommended that you work with your hardware technician to create your own checklist. A comprehensive backup checklist includes the following: Establishing a backup routine. Testing your backup system. Determining what files need to be backed up. Understanding the different types of backup media. Providing safe storage for your backup media. Maintaining backup devices. Replacing worn backup media. Understanding the restore process. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Amazing Charts
topics.body: Using Amazing Charts Using Amazing Charts SoftDent software now has an integration with Amazing Charts. The Amazing Charts Electronic Health Record (EHR) is an EHR solution that has been certified for meaningful use and includes the Meaningful Use Wizard, which simplifies report creation to complete the attestation process. You must install the Amazing Charts software on your server and enable the Carestream Dental interface before you can enable the integration with SoftDent. Contact Amazing Charts for information on purchasing the software and enabling the Carestream Dental interface. A mazing Charts is not currently available in SoftDent Cloud. Related Topics Enabling Amazing Charts How Data Changes in SoftDent Affect Amazing Charts Viewing Amazing Charts Errors Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using CareCredit Lookup Windows
topics.body: Using CareCredit Lookup Windows eServices > Using CareCredit Using CareCredit Lookup Windows To verify whether or not a patient is a CareCredit account holder or check the amount of available credit, use the CareCredit lookup windows. You can also use the lookup windows to obtain account information when making a payment or refund and the patient does not have his CareCredit card with him. Note: A CareCredit icon displayed in color in the upper-right of the Patient window indicates that the patient is a CareCredit customer; an icon displayed in B&W indicates that the patient is not a CareCredit customer. Lookup by SSN and Zip To perform a lookup by Social Security Number and zip code when the patient's CareCredit card is not available: Select the patient or account from the list, click CareCredit Integration on the Express Bar, and select Lookup by SSN and Zip . The CareCredit Account Lookup window is displayed. Select the provider, and click Lookup . The Lookup CareCredit Account window is displayed. Do one of the following: If the Social Security Number and zip code fields are populated: Click OK . If the patient has an active account, the CareCredit Account Lookup window is displayed, with the account number and the amount available fields populated. If the Social Security Number and zip code fields are not populated: Ask for the Social Security Number and zip code of the person who owns the CareCredit account, and type the information in the fields. Click OK . If the cardholder has an active account, the CareCredit Account Lookup window displays the account number and the amount available. Click Cancel . Lookup by Account No. To perform a lookup by CareCredit account number: Select the patient or account from the list, click CareCredit Integration on the Express Bar, and select Lookup by Account No . The CareCredit Account Lookup window is displayed. Select the provider, and click Lookup . The Lookup CareCredit Account window is displayed. Type the account number in the field, and click Lookup . The CareCredit Account Lookup window is displayed, with the account number and amount available fields populated. Click Cancel . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using CDT Codes
topics.body: Using CDT Codes Setting Up Insurance Using CDT Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2022) codes is included with SoftDent. When the CDT-2022 codes are installed: Sixteen new codes are added to the database. Thirteen codes have revised nomenclature. Nine codes have editorial changes. Six codes are deleted. Eight codes tied to COVID-19 (adopted in spring 2021) are added to the database. The CDT version assigned to your insurance plans is not reset automatically. Related Topics Manually Installing CDT-2022 Codes Enabling CDT-2022 Code Formatting Resetting_Insurance_Plan_CDT_Versions Viewing_ADA_Code_Descriptions Viewing_CDT_Copyright_Information Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Clinical Conditions
topics.body: Using Clinical Conditions Restorative Charting Using Clinical Conditions A list of clinical conditions is delivered with the software, but you can add new conditions as needed. You can search for a specific record and sort the condition records by name or identification number. You can also display or hide inactive records. To view the list, select List > Clinical Conditions . You cannot delete clinical conditions. Mark unused conditions inactive. Related Topics Setting Clinical Condition Security and Overrides Charting Clinical Conditions Charting Supernumerary Teeth Charting Impacted Teeth Adding a Clinical Condition Editing a Clinical Condition Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the eForms Module
topics.body: Using the eForms Module eServices Using the eForms Module The eForms module uses electronic forms that eliminate the need for paper copies of documents used in your office every day. Using the eForms module, you can move closer to the goal of becoming a paperless office, as it eliminates the need for filling out paper forms and then entering the data into the patient's record via keyboard or scanner. Patients fill out and update forms electronically, and patients' signatures are captured electronically. This information is then added to the database with minimal effort on the part of the staff. In many cases, the patient will need to complete or sign his forms in the office. You can use the workstation and signature pad at the front desk for this purpose, or you can use the eForms-Enabled Kiosk. The kiosk enables the patient to use a dedicated workstation and signature pad; or an iPad to complete the forms. Once you have enrolled in eForms, you can access a patient's eForms from several areas of the software: Scheduler Express Bar Appointment submenu eForms icon on appointment in the daybook Patient List Express Bar InOffice Express Bar If you are not using an iPad, you must use the Topaz signature pad to have patients sign forms. Follow the installation instructions with the pad, and select the Use Topaz Electronic Signature Pad checkbox in the Startup Preferences window before using the pad. To enroll in eForms, contact your Aftermarket Sales representative. Related Topics Using eForms on the Web Configuring the eForms Module Configuring eForms E-Mail Settings Configuring Patient eForms Settings Setting Up eForms Templates Sending and Receiving eForms Resending eForms Reviewing eForms Adding or Removing eForms Viewing Archived eForms Using the eForms-Enabled Kiosk Monitoring Checked-In Patients eForms Security Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using eForms on the Web
topics.body: Using eForms on the Web eServices > Using the eForms Module Using eForms on the Web The procedures for using eForms on the Web are: Your office automatically sends an e-mail to a patient a predefined number of days before his appointment, telling him that he has forms that need to be completed or updated. The patient clicks a link in the e-mail and is taken to a security window, where the patient provides authentication information. Next, the patient is taken to the patient home page, where he clicks on a form, and the form is displayed on the screen. The patient completes the forms and clicks Submit, and the forms are marked as completed and sent to the Carestream database. The forms are sent to your office the next time the eForms module checks for forms. During the review process, a staff member opens a completed form on a computer on which eForms has been installed. If the patient made changes, the changes are displayed in red. The staff member checks the information that has changed and might decide to type a note regarding the change. If a form requires the patient's signature, the patient will electronically sign the form�at the front desk, at the kiosk, or with an iPad�when he arrives for his appointment. The staff member clicks Accept, the new information is added to the database, and an electronic copy of the form is created and archived. The staff member continues with the remaining forms. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using eServices Reports
topics.body: Using eServices Reports eServices Using eServices Reports There are several eServices reports that are automatically sent to your office after you have loaded, validated, and transmitted your claims to the eServices clearinghouse. These reports include: Account Balance Summary Claim Error and Rejection Reports Claim and Statement Transmission Reports Claim Status Reports You can generate several eServices reports when necessary. These reports include: Available and Previous eServices Reports Summary Reports Electronically Capable Payer Report Real-Time Payer Report Pending Statements Report Related Topics Account Balance Summary Claim Error and Rejection Reports Claim and Statement Transmission Reports Claim Status Reports Printing eServices Reports Running the Summary Report Running the Electronically Capable Payer Report Running the Real Time Payer Report Running the Pending Statements Report Need more information? topics.title: Using Medication Information in the Medical History Window
topics.body: Using Medication Information in the Medical History Window Handling Patient Medical History Using Medication Information in the Medical History Window You can track up to 50 medications currently used by a patient. To include a medication in a patient's medical history, you must first enter the medication in the Drug window. See Handling Prescriptions for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Online Statement Service
topics.body: Using the Online Statement Service eServices > Using the Automated Statements Service Using the Online Statement Service The Online Statement Service sends a patient an e-mail containing practice information, a summary of statement amounts, and a link to a secure site where the patient can view the full statement. Once logged in, the patient sees all of the information he typically sees on his paper statement. The patient can print the online statement so he can send a payment to your office, or if your office uses ePayments, he can make a credit card or ACH payment online. After payments are made, a report is sent to your office the following day, listing which payments can be posted. To enroll, contact an eServices representative. Related Topics Enabling Online Statements Running the Set Up Account Automated Statements Service Options Utility Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Quick Letters
topics.body: Using Quick Letters Setting Up Patients > Patient List and Patient Window Options Using Quick Letters Quick Letters are templates for common letters, notes, and forms you might send or give to a patient or on behalf of a patient. The Quick Letter options available include: Welcome Letter: Prints a patient welcome letter. See Printing a Welcome Letter for more information. Thanks to Referring Patient: Prints a thank you letter for a patient who has referred a patient to your office. See Printing a Thanks to Referring Patient Letter for more information. Thanks to Referring Doctor: Prints a thank you letter to a doctor who has referred a patient to your office. See Printing a Thanks to Referring Doctor Letter for more information. Complete Refer Work: Prints a letter informing a referring doctor that your office has completed the specified work. See Printing a Complete Refer Work Letter for more information. Referring Doctor Inquiry: Prints a letter to a doctor to whom you have referred a patient. See Printing a Referring Doctor Inquiry Letter for more information. Referral Intro Note: Prints an introductory letter to a doctor to whom you have referred the patient. See Printing a Referral Intro Note for more information. Excused Absence Note: Prints a patient excused absence letter. See Printing an Excused Absence Note for more information. Insurance Inquiry: Prints a patient insurance claim inquiry. See Printing an Insurance Inquiry Letter for more information. New Patient Info Form: Prints the New Patient Information form. See Printing a New Patient Info Form for more information. Update Patient Info Form: Prints the Current Patient Update form. See Printing An Update Patient Info Form for more information. Insurance Retreatment: Prints a patient Insurance Retreatment letter. See Printing an Insurance Retreatment Letter for more information. Medical History: Prints the Confidential Medical - Dental History form. See Printing a Medical History Form for more information. Delinquency Notice: Prints a statement of delinquency for the patient. See Printing a Delinquency Notice for more information. To open a Quick Letter for an existing patient: Select an option: From the List menu : Select Patient . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the Power Bar : Select Patient List Window . The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the Account List or Account window : Select Patients from the Options menu of the Express bar or the Options menu. A Patient List window containing all of the patients associated with the selected account is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the InOffice window : Select Patient from the Options menu of the Express bar. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. In the patient chart: Hover over the patient name on the patient banner and click the link. In any window : Press Ctrl + O. The Patient List window is displayed. Double-click the patient. The patient is displayed in the Patient window. Select Letters and Labels > Quick Letters from the Options section of the Express bar or the Options menu. A list of quick letters is displayed. Click on the quick letter, select the output option, and click OK , or complete the information. Related Topics Printing a Welcome Letter Printing a Thanks to Referring Patient Letter Printing a Thanks to Referring Doctor Letter Printing a Complete Refer Work Letter Printing a Referring Doctor Inquiry Letter Prin

topics.title: Using the CareCredit Online Application
topics.body: Using the CareCredit Online Application eServices > Using CareCredit Using the CareCredit Online Application When the patient is ready to apply for CareCredit, you can have the patient complete an application on the CareCredit web site. In many cases, the patient can receive an instant credit decision. Upon credit approval, you can schedule the procedure immediately. To access the CareCredit web site and online application: From the Patient or Account window, click CareCredit Integration on the Express bar, and select Online Application . The Online CareCredit Application window is displayed. Select the provider, and click OK . The CareCredit web site opens, and the Online Application Processing message is displayed. Follow the instructions on the window, complete the online application, and click Submit . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the CareCredit Payment Calculator
topics.body: Using the CareCredit Payment Calculator eServices > Using CareCredit Using the CareCredit Payment Calculator You can go to the CareCredit web site and use the payment calculator tool to explain the financing options available through CareCredit and enable the patient to select which financing option works best for him. To access the CareCredit web site and online application: From the Patient or Account window, click CareCredit Integration on the Express bar, and select Payment Calculator . The CareCredit Payment Calculator window is displayed. Follow the instructions on the window to present the options to the patient. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the eForms-Enabled Kiosk
topics.body: Using the eForms-Enabled Kiosk eServices > Using the eForms Module Using the eForms-Enabled Kiosk Usin g the eForms-Enabled Kiosk, your patients can check themselves in and fill out eForms in your office. The kiosk enables patients to fill out forms from start to finish in your waiting room, and it also enables patients to complete and sign forms they have filled out on the Web at home. If you are not using an iPad, you must use the Topaz signature pad to have patients sign forms. Follow the installation instructions with the pad, and select the Use Topaz Electronic Signature Pad checkbox in the Startup Preferences window before using the pad. You can run the kiosk in either of two modes: Kiosk �Displays a sign-in window, which enables patients to access their Welcome page and complete forms. This option is typically used when the kiosk software is running on a desktop computer. Patients can check in and access their forms up to an hour before and an hour after their appointment time. Reception Sign In �Requires a staff member to sign in the patient. This option is typically used when the kiosk software is running on an iPad, and a staff member will hand the iPad to the patient. The kiosk can be used in several workflows: Workflow 1: Reception Sign In Mode: The patient fills out forms online at home (though he might opt to wait until he is in the office). The patient arrives, and a staff member selects the patient's name on the iPad. The iPad sends the practice management software a request to check in the patient. If the patient has eForms to complete, a web browser opens, and the patient's welcome page is displayed. The staff member hands the iPad to the patient to complete the forms. The patient completes the forms. If a form requires the patient's signature, he electronically signs the form using the stylus and returns the iPad to the front desk. Workflow 2: Kiosk Mode: The patient fills out forms online at home (though he might opt to wait until he is in the office). The patient arrives, completes the fields in the Sign In window, and clicks Sign in. The kiosk sends the practice management software a request to check in the patient. If the patient has eForms to complete, a web browser opens, and the patient's welcome page is displayed. Note: If the patient does not have eForms to complete, a message is displayed, and the patient can return to the waiting area. The patient completes the forms. If a form requires the patient's signature, he electronically signs the form at the kiosk using the signature pad. The patient clicks Sign Out and takes a seat in the waiting room. Workflow 3: Concierge: The patient fills out forms online at home (though he might opt to wait until he is in the office). The patient arrives, and a staff member checks the patient in and fills out the forms while speaking with the patient, using either the kiosk mode or the reception sign-in mode. The front desk staff completes the kiosk check-in process using the Monitor KIOSK Check-In window. See Monitoring Checked-In Patients for more information. Related Topics Monitoring Checked-In Patients Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Electronically Capable Payer Report
topics.body: Running the Electronically Capable Payer Report eServices > Using eServices Reports Running the Electronically Capable Payer Report The Electronically Capable Payer Report option provides a list of insurance companies that can accept claims electronically. To print the report: From the SoftDent main menu, select Insurance > Electronic Services . The Electronic Services window is displayed. Select Report Options > Electronic Payer Report . The Electronically Capable Payer Report window is displayed. Select an option: To view the report: Click Print Preview . To print the report: Click Print . Need more information? If you are not currently an ePrescriptions service subscriber, contact your Carestream eServices representative for more information. topics.body: Running the Real Time Payer Report eServices > Using eServices Reports Running the Real Time Payer Report The Real-Time Payer Report option provides a list of insurance companies that can instantaneously receive and adjudicate claims. To print the report: From the SoftDent main menu, select Insurance > Electronic Services . The Electronic Services window is displayed. Select Report Options > Real-Time Payer Report . The Real Time Payer Report window is displayed. Select an option: To view the report: Click Print Preview . To print the report: Click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Report Manager
topics.body: Using the Report Manager Handling Reports > Generating User-Selected Reports Using the Report Manager Both patient and account user-selected reports can be included in report groups in the Report Manager. When you add reports to a group in the Report Manager, select a predefined report from the User-Selected Patients or User-Selected Accounts list window. See Generating Reports Using Report Manager . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Selection Criteria Window
topics.body: Using the Selection Criteria Window Handling Reports > Generating User-Selected Reports Using the Selection Criteria Window The Selection Criteria window enables you to establish the criteria for a patient or account report by selecting general information you want to include, as well as transaction and category information you want to include or exclude. An Info tab lists general criteria that you can select. A Trans/Category tab lists category code and transaction criteria you can select. After you make a selection on either tab, you further define that selection by using an operator, or selection code, and assigning a value to it. For example, to create a report for patients of a certain age group, select Age on the Info tab and then further define that selection by specifying an age range with the operators. The following table defines each user-selected operator and uses patient report criteria. Operator Meaning Example GT greater than To select all records with a chart number greater than 100, double-click Chart No on the Info tab. Type GT in the first Op field and 100 in the Value 1 field. Chart number 100 will not be included. LT less than To select all records with patient IDs less than 5999, double-click Patient ID on the Info tab. Type LT in the first Op field and 5999 in the Value 1 field. Patient ID #5999 will not be included. EQ equal to To select records for female patients only, double-click Sex on the Info tab. Type EQ in the Op field and F in the Value 1 field. NE not equal to To select all patient records not on fee schedule 0, double-click Fee Schedule on the Info tab. Type NE in the first Op field and 0 in the Value 1 field. LE less than or equal to To select all patients whose last names begin with A, double-click Last Name on the Info tab. Type LE in the Op field and Az in the Value 1 field. GE greater than or equal to To select all patients between the ages of 18 and 30, double-click Age on the Info tab. Type GE and 18 in the first Op and Value 1 fields, and LE and 30 in the second Op and Value 2 fields. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Software Update Service (SUS)
topics.body: Using the Software Update Service (SUS) Getting Started Using the Software Update Service (SUS) The Software Update Service (SUS) is installed automatically when you install the latest version of SoftDent. After the update service is configured, it automatically checks for updates and enables you to install the updates when convenient. The SUS is not used with SoftDent Cloud. SUS Benefits The SUS provides the following benefits: Updates give you the latest features. Updates are fast and efficient. Updates are delivered automatically to your desktop. Updates are securely downloaded and verified. You can schedule updates during non-working hours. SUS Features Features of the SUS include: The service provides additional information about each update; you decide if and when to update. The service works behind the scenes and does not disrupt your workflow. The service uses the same technology as Microsoft to deliver updates. The service alerts you if there are any issues. The service utilizes a simple registration procedure that uses the same account details as other Carestream Dental services. The service collects only the information necessary to operate the service (email address, account number, Carestream Dental software versions installed, and a number assigned to your site and each PC). You receive no additional or intrusive marketing. For more information, see the SoftDent Practice Management Software Installation Guide . Access this guide from your Windows task bar by selecting Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > Online Documentation > Installation Guide . Related Topics Configuring the SUS Authenticating an Existing SSO Account and Configuring the SUS Adding and Removing SUS Accounts Updating SoftDent Receiving and Viewing Alerts Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Summary Report
topics.body: Running the Summary Report eServices > Using eServices Reports Running the Summary Report Use the Summary Report option to print lists of the following: Electronically capable providers Electronically capable insurance companies Electronically capable insurance plans Prosthesis/crown/inlay procedure codes All of the above To print a report: From the SoftDent main menu, select Insurance > Electronic Services . The Electronic Services window is displayed. Select Report Options > Summary Report > Report Type . Select an option: To view the report: Click Print Preview . To print the report: Click Print . To print all summary information: From the SoftDent main menu, select Insurance > Electronic Services . The Electronic Services window is displayed. Select Report Options > Summary Report > All Summary Info . Click Print . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the eReminders Service
topics.body: Using the eReminders Service eServices Using the eReminders Service The eReminders Service is an appointment reminder service that automatically contacts patients due for an appointment using the patient's preferred contact type�phone call, e-mail, or text message (SMS). After the appointment/reminder contact is made, the appointment information is automatically updated, enabling you to spend more time helping patients in the office. Once you have enrolled in the service, you can access the eReminders settings for a patient or responsible party by clicking eReminders Settings in the Options section of the Express Bar in the following areas of the software: Account List window Account window Patient List window Patient window Patient Appointments window Daybook Book Quick Glance To enroll in the service, contact your eServices representative. Related Topics Enabling eReminders Modifying eReminders Settings Running eReminders Reports Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using Profiles
topics.body: Using Profiles Getting Started Using Profiles The initial color and appearance of list windows is set by default. Creating a profile enables you to view list windows in colors and styles that you select. For example, you might prefer to use a light-pink highlight band�to indicate that an item is selected�and a list constructed with grid lines. A fellow employee might prefer to use a blue highlighting border and no grid lines. If your office does not require you to use a system profile, you can each set up an individual profile and view the list windows in the way you prefer when you are logged in to the software. The office administrator can create a system profile that can function as the default for the office; employees have the option of using the system profile or creating an individual profile. If the office administrator prefers that list windows have the same look and feel on all workstations, he or she can apply the system profile to all users, overriding any user preferences. Related Topics Creating an Individual Profile Creating a System Profile Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Insurance Bluebook
topics.body: Using the Insurance Bluebook Setting Up Insurance Using the Insurance Bluebook Use the following information to estimate insurance payments and track how much each insurance plan pays for charges submitted by your practice: Insurance plan Plan bluebook entry When you post a transaction, the default amount is the insurance payment amount entered in the plan�s bluebook entry and the procedural code. The patient portion is then calculated as the difference between the insurance payment and your fee or the plan�s allowed amount. Set up your insurance and enter insurance company and insurance plan information before setting up any bluebook entries. For more information, see Setting Insurance Defaults . If you know a plan�s bluebook amounts, enter them in the bluebook database. You can also automatically update bluebook entry files every time you post an insurance payment. Bluebook entries: Are used only for patients using fee schedule 0. Displayed in the Bluebook Entries for List and Bluebook Entries for windows are specific to an estimation type. All bluebook entries include: Procedural codes and descriptions Insurance payment amounts Fees Date the insurance payment was last updated Related Topics Updating the Bluebook After an Insurance Payment Understanding the Bluebook Entries for List Window Adding Bluebook Entries Editing Bluebook Entries Tracking Bluebook Changes Purging Bluebook Entries Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Letter Expert
topics.body: Using the Letter Expert Using Experts Using the Letter Expert You can use the letter expert feature t o create templates for writing letters. These templates can be used to print multiple form letters that contain different patient names, addresses, and so on. The letter expert requires you to use Microsoft Word software, versions 2000 or XP. Using the letter expert, you can do the following: Set letter expert startup preferences Create Microsoft Word software templates Set letter expert preferences Merge letters Start Microsoft Word software Related Topics Setting Letter Expert Startup Preferences Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the ePostings Service
topics.body: Using the ePostings Service eServices Using the ePostings Service The ePostings Service enables you to electronically receive insurance payment information from participating insurance companies. The information received through ePostings functions as an electronic Explanation Of Benefits. To enroll in ePostings, contact your eServices representative. The procedures for using the ePostings Service are: Your office submits claims through eClaims. The insurance company sends the eClaims clearinghouse information about the payment. The eClaims clearinghouse sends the ePostings data to your office, where it is processed by SoftDent. The software notifies you (through Practice Central or Office Expert) that ePostings records are ready for review. You open the ePostings Payment Viewer window and review the ePostings payment information. You ensure that all of the claims for the payment match up to the existing SoftDent claims. When all of the claims match, you can post the payment. If the ePostings records are for a payment by check, versus an electronic funds transfer, you might have to wait until you receive the check before posting. Related Topics Enabling ePostings Specific ADA Codes for Use with ePostings Accessing ePostings Understanding the ePostings Payment Viewer Window Understanding the ePostings Payment Detail Window Understanding the ePostings Claim Detail Window Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Window Understanding the Completed ePostings Payment Detail Window Using the ePostings Adjustments Window Viewing the Original ePostings Data Viewing ePostings Documents in the Document Manager Viewing ePostings Information in Reports Using ePostings Data in Utilities Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Perio Numeric Chart
topics.body: Using the Perio Numeric Chart Periodontal Charting > Understanding Perio Tab Modes Using the Perio Numeric Chart When the perio numeric chart mode is selected: The most up-to-date charting information is displayed. You can view all periodontal information. The periodontal, FGM, and MGJ values are used to calculate the degree of attachment loss. You can view blood, suppuration, and furcation grade. To view the perio numeric mode, select Perio Numeric Chart on the Perio Views toolbar . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Unlocking User Access
topics.body: Unlocking User Access System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Handling HIPAA Privacy and Security Features > Setting HIPAA Security and Privacy Options Unlocking User Access When the Strong Password Protection feature is selected in the HIPAA Options window , a user cannot access the software for ten minutes after unsuccessfully attempting to log in five times. When this occurs, and the user requires immediate access to the software, User ID 1 can unlock the user name. To unlock a user record: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. The text for locked records is displayed in red, and a lock icon is displayed in the Status column. Select the locked user record and click UnLock . You can also unlock a user record from the Rights By User window. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating User-Defined Charting Paths
topics.body: Creating User-Defined Charting Paths Periodontal Charting Creating User-Defined Charting Paths You can create up to three customized charting paths. Paths are workstation specific. To create a user-defined charting path: In the Chart for window, select Perio > Options . The Perio Properties window is displayed. Click the Path tab. The settings that enable you to control which pockets are charted and the charting path are displayed at the bottom of the window. If you create and select a custom path, it is the Auto Advance default. Select the pockets from the Pockets To Measure section of the window. Example : To chart pockets 2, 3, and 5, deselect pockets 1,4, and 6. Select the path in the Tooth Paths section of the window, type the path, and select the pocket. When you type the path, you must indicate the path surface and side information. You can use the following information: FL : The path starts on the facial surface and moves toward the left side of the mouth. FR : The path starts on the facial surface and moves toward the right side of the mouth. LL : The path starts on the lingual surface and moves toward the left side of the mouth. LR : The path starts on the lingual surface and moves toward the right side of the mouth. Click OK . The tooth path selection is workstation-specific, not user-specific. If users share a workstation and have different tooth path preferences, each user will need to select his or her preferred path before perio charting. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Up the User-Defined Recall System
topics.body: Setting Up the User-Defined Recall System Recalling Patients > Setting Up Recalls Setting Up the User-Defined Recall System To set up your User-Defined Recall system: In the Recall Settings window, type the name of your User-Defined Recall system in the Codes that set the User-Defined Recall System section. The default name of the User-Defined Recall system is Perio. Type the number of months between User-Defined recalls in the appropriate field. Select the User-Defined Recall codes from the drop-down lists. You can select up to five codes that trigger a patient recall. To print the recall period and the last exam date on recall postcards or labels, select Show Recall information on Postcards/Labels . Click OK . Click Close . Any patient records added to the software before you set up your recall system have no recall information. Run the Recall Setup/Utility Wizard to update all of your patients with the new codes. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Define User Quick Letters Utility
topics.body: Running the Define User Quick Letters Utility Utilities > Running Customize Menu Utilities Running the Define User Quick Letters Utility Run t his utility to create up to 10 user-defined quick letters. These letters are stored in the \CUSTOM directory under the names USER1.LTR, USER2.LTR, USER3.LTR, and so on. To define user quick letters: Instruct all users to exit the software. Select Start > All Programs > SoftDent Software > SoftDent Utilities . The SoftDent Login window is displayed. Type your user name and password and click OK . The SoftDent Utilities window is displayed. Select Customize > Define User Quick Letters . The User Defined Quick Letters window is displayed. Type the quick letter name in the appropriate field. This title is displayed in the Quick Letters window accessed from the Patient and Patient List windows. Type up to 20 lines of text in the body of the letter. Click OK . Restart the software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Rights by User List Window
topics.body: Using the Rights by User List Window System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Using the Rights by User List Window In the Rights by User List window, you can: View a list of users and passwords View, add, or edit a user login record In the Find field, you can: Type a user login and search for the corresponding record Sort results by user name The following information for each user is displayed in the list: Identification number Name Password Status To access the Rights by User List window, select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Related Topics Rights by User Window Users By Rights Window Tasks with Limited Access Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Users By Rights Window
topics.body: Using the Users By Rights Window System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Using the Users By Rights Window In the Users By Rights window, you can: Select a restricted task and view a list of users who have access to perform the task Enable a user to or prevent a user from performing a task To access the Users By Rights window, select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Users by Rights . The Users By Rights window is displayed. Related Topics Tasks with Limited Access Rights by User List Window Rights by User Window Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: User-Selected Reports
topics.body: User-Selected Reports What's New in Version 14.0 > Reports User-Selected Reports User-Selected reports have received enhancements in the following areas: User-Selected Reports Export to Excel When running a user-selected report, you can now select Excel as an output option, enabling you to further manipulate the data using Excel. Order of Report Criteria Changed Report criteria has been reorganized to group similar entries together. Previously, new entries were added to the bottom of the list. Last Update Report Option A Last Update option is now available when running either account or patient user-selected reports. This option enables you to produce a report for accounts or patients whose records have been updated within a specified date range. When you double-click the Last Update option (or select the option and click Edit Values ), you can enter comparison operations and date ranges to which you can apply the comparisons. Cell Phone and E-Mail Options Cell Phone and E-mail options are now available when running either account or patient user-selected reports. These options enable you to produce a report for accounts or patients with cell phones and e-mail addresses. The Account Selection Criteria window includes the following new options: G #1 Cell Phone, G #2 Cell Phone; and G #1 E-Mail, G #2 E-Mail, G #3 E-Mail, and G #4 E-Mail. The Patient Selection Criteria window includes the following new options: Cell Phone, and E-Mail. When you double-click Cell Phone or E-Mail (or select one of these options and click Edit Values ), you can enter comparison operations and text ranges to which you can apply the comparisons. See Generating User-Selected Reports for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Creating and Modifying User Templates
topics.body: Creating and Modifying User Templates System Security and HIPAA Compliance > Setting Up Security Creating and Modifying User Templates User templates help you create a set of permissions for a specific job description and then apply the permissions to each user who performs that job within your practice. When you install SoftDent, several templates are included. To create or modify a user template: Select System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User . The Rights By User List window is displayed. Press Enter and click Add . The Rights By User window is displayed. Type the appropriate information in the User Information section. Click Select from Template . The User Template List window is displayed. Select an option: To add a new template : Click Add . The User Template window is displayed. Type a template description in the User Description field. To edit a template : Select the template and click Edit . The User Template window is displayed. Select an option: To dictate the template access : Select the appropriate category from the left side of the window and select the checkbox next to each task users assigned to this template can perform in the right side of the window. For a complete list of tasks by category, see Tasks with Limited Access . To give the users assigned to this template rights to perform all the tasks : Click Select All . Click OK . Click Close . Related Topics Default Template Permissions Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Video Menu
topics.body: Using the Video Menu Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the Video Menu The commands available from the Video menu are: Options : Select this command to display the Video Capture/Display Properties window. In this window, you can set video capture options. See Setting Video Capture Options . Live Video : Select this command to capture images from a live video source. View Images : Select this command to make the Clinical Imaging tab the active window. PW DICOM Imaging : This command is enabled if you use PW DICOM Imaging software. Select this command to access the software. Xray Vision : This command is enabled if you use Xray Vision software. Select this command to access the software. Vipersoft : Select this command to access a list of Vipersoft software commands. TigerView : Select this command to access the TigerView software. Tekscan - T-Scan II : Select this command to access the Tekscan - T-Scan II software. DenTelVision : Select this command to access the DenTelVision software. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Utilities
topics.body: Utilities Utilities It is not recommended that you run SoftDent Utilities on a weekly, monthly, or yearly basis. You typically run utilities in case of database corruption, most commonly expressed as SDWin DBM Error, File No. ### Error = # . There might be other instances where utilities are used to resolve or troubleshoot an issue. You should run utilities only when you are instructed to do so by a SoftDent technical support representative. SoftDent utilities are used to: Correct data problems Automate tasks Update information in databases Make other global modifications to your system Many of these utilities alter data and system settings; therefore, use them with extreme caution. When you run any utility, all other users must exit the application. Related Topics How Often Should I Run Utilities? Using File Menu Utilities Using Maintenance Menu Utilities Running Customize Menu Utilities Using Report Menu Utilities Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Utilities
topics.body: Utilities What's New in Version 14.0 Utilities The following enhancement has been made to the utilities functions: Viewing a Log File for Claims Deleted After Running the Resubmit or Delete by Age Utility You can now view and print a log file reflecting the claims that are deleted as a result of running the Resubmit/Delete by Age utility. The log file is called DelResubClaims.log and is located in the SoftDent data directory. See Deleting Claims By Age for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the Voice Activation Module
topics.body: Using the Voice Activation Module Periodontal Charting Using the Voice Activation Module The voice activation module enables you to perform perio charting by using voice commands to control keyboard or mouse actions. For voice activation training, the individual user needs to have his or her own Microsoft Windows login for the workstation on which training occurs. This login is distinct from the SoftDent login. Before you can use this feature, you must have the following compatible items installed: Sound card Microphone Enable the voice activation module on each computer that performs perio charting using voice commands. Related Topics Activating the Voice Activation Module Enabling the Voice Activation Module Training the Voice Activation Module Using the Voice Activation Module Commands Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Validating ADA Codes
topics.body: Validating ADA Codes Handling Transactions and Codes Validating ADA Codes New codes are validated to ensure only authorized codes are used when handling the following: Appointments Treatment plans Transactions Bluebook entries Clinical notes Account payments Recall appointments Fee schedules Account payments Insurance payments The following validation rules are true: Previously existing invalid codes associated with account or insurance payments, scheduled appointments, treatment plans, bluebook entries, clinical notes, recalls, budget plans, discounts, transactions, fee schedules, default exams, or electronic router information are not validated. Therefore, your records are not adversely affected by the validation process. Previously existing invalid codes can be reassociated with or assigned to an account or insurance payment, scheduled appointment, treatment plan, bluebook entry, clinical note, recall, budget plan, discount, transaction, fee schedule, default exam, or electronic router records. Example : Invalid code 7635 was associated with a patient transaction. You can associate the transaction with a valid code or reassociate code 7635 with the patient transaction. However, you cannot associate a new invalid code with the patient transaction. You cannot associate a previously existing invalid code with account or insurance payments, scheduled appointments, treatment plans, bluebook entries, clinical notes, recalls, budget plans, discounts, transactions, fee schedules, default exams, or electronic router information to which they were not previously associated. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Validating Claims
topics.body: Validating Claims eServices > Using the eClaims Service > Sending Claims Validating Claims The validation process checks all electronic claims for errors. To validate claims: Select an option: In the eServices window : Click Validate . From the main menu : Select Submit Data > Validate . In the Claim Submission List window : Click Validate . If warning messages are displayed, read them and click OK . After validation, the Validation Report Options window is displayed. The Claim Submission List window shows the status of each claim and the number of claims that passed validation. Claims that did not pass validation are displayed in red. A claim with a warning message is transmitted, but the insurance company may request additional information before processing the claim. In the Validation Report Options window, select an option: To show the validation warnings in the report : Select Print warnings in the report . To preview the report : Select Print Preview . To print the report : Select Print . Click OK . The Validation Error report explains the data that is missing or incorrect on each claim. If a corrected claim still displays an error, click Load and then Validate to revalidate the claim. For explanations of validation errors and warnings, see the eServices online Help. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Verifying an NEA Transmission in the Claim
topics.body: Verifying an NEA Transmission in the Claim Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services Verifying an NEA Transmission in the Claim After you send attachments to NEA, the NEA reference number is displayed in the Notes field of the claim. To ensure the insurance company receives the claim with the NEA reference number: Do not send the claim before transmitting the attachment to NEA. If a claim is sent before transmitting the attachments to NEA, the reference number is not sent to the insurance company. To view a claim, see Using the Claim Record . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

The following information is available: Appointments Financials Rx Information Pharmacies Call Backs Referring Drs Patients Memos Related Topics Viewing Appointments and Financials in Microsoft Outlook Viewing Rx, Pharmacies, Call Backs, Referring Drs, and Patients in Microsoft Outlook Viewing Memos in Microsoft Outlook Need more information?
