topics.title: What's New in Version 14.0.1
topics.body: What's New in Version 14.0.1 What's New in Version 14.0.1 SoftDent, version 14.0.1, includes enhancements in the following areas: Late Appointment Tracking Scheduler Settings In version 14.0, a feature was added that enables you to track late appointments. When a patient is late for his appointment, the software displays a prompt, asking if you want to track the appointment as a late appointment in the Tracked Appointments window. In version 14.0.1, two settings have been added to the Scheduler Settings window ( System > Change System Settings > Scheduler ) and the Scheduler Setup Wizard ( Wizards > Scheduler Wizard ) that enable you to control late appointment tracking: Track late appointments: This option is turned on by default for new and existing users. If you deselect this option: The software will not check for late appointments. No warning messages are displayed. No Late appointment tracking record is added to the database. Show warning message for late arrivals: This option is turned on by default for new and existing users. This option is unchecked and disabled if you deselect the Track late appointments checkbox. A Late appointment tracking record is still added to the database if you opt to only disable the warning message. See Setting Scheduler Defaults and Tracking Missed, Broken, and Late Appointments for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: What's New in Version 14.0.3
topics.body: What's New in Version 14.0.3 What's New in Version 14.0.3 SoftDent, version 14.0.3, includes enhancements in the following areas: CDT-2011 Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2011) codes is included with this version of SoftDent. When the CDT-2011 codes are installed, the following things occur: Eight new codes are added to the database. Nineteen codes are modified. The CDT-2011 code set has been replaced by the CDT-2022 code set. See Using CDT Codes for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: What's New in Version 14.2.1
topics.body: What's New in Version 14.2.1 What's New in Version 14.2.1 SoftDent, version 14.2.1, includes enhancements in the following areas: CDT-2013 Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2013) codes is included with this version of SoftDent. When the CDT-2013 codes are installed, the following things occur: Thirty-five new codes or categories are added to the database. Thirty-seven modifications to the nomenclature or descriptor of existing codes or categories are made. Twelve codes are removed. Also, a Display Additional Info option has been added to the Express bar in the ADA/Transaction Codes window ( List > ADA Codes > double-click a code > click OK ). The ADA has provided additional information for some codes, and if you are viewing a code in the ADA/Transaction Codes window and the Display Additional Info option is enabled, you can click the option to view the additional code information. When you open the software for the first time, a CDT 2013 End User License Agreement window is displayed. Read the license agreement carefully. If you agree to the terms, click I Agree to load the new codes. The CDT-2013 code set has been replaced by the CDT-2022 code set. See Using CDT Codes and Viewing ADA Code Descriptions for more information. 2012 ADA Claim Form The ADA Dental Claim Form has been revised to incorporate key changes to the HIPAA standard electronic dental claim transaction. The form enables you to include a diagnosis code (ICD-9-CM) when a specific diagnosis may have an impact on the adjudication of a claim; for example, when a procedure might minimize the risk associated with a patient�s oral health and a systemic health condition. Select 200 # STANDARD ADA FORM 2012 Laser Form from the Insurance Forms Selection window to use the new form. See Selecting Insurance Forms for more information. Printing Discounts on Claim Forms Some insurance companies are requesting that discounts posted to the patient's ledger be printed on the claim form. For example: A patient comes in for a given procedure and is charged a fee but is also given a discount to waive the patient copay. In this case, the claim form would need to include the procedure being submitted as well as the line item for the discount that was provided. A Print discount transactions on claim option has been added to the Claim Filing tab of the Insurance Plan window. If this option is selected, and the discount transaction is listed in the Discount Schedule window ( System > Discount Schedule ) and the date of service falls within the claim submission dates, the discount is printed on the claim. See Adding Insurance Plans and Printing Discounts on a Claim Form for more information. New York Medicare Claim Form Requires the Word "Same" in Certain Circumstances New York Medicare requires the word "SAME" in box 7 of the New York Medicare Claim form if the patient and insured are the same person, and in box 4 if the patient is the insured person and Medicare is the secondary insurance. The New York Medicare form�24 # NY Medicare CMS-1500 2006 Preprinted Laser Form�has been changed to meet this requirement. See Selecting Insurance Forms for more information. Show Ins Company Option Added to Patient Windows and Scheduler Windows A Show Ins Company option has been added to the list of options in the Patient list and Patient windows, and the Daily Appointments and Book At A Glance windows. The option enables you to access the insurance company information for the se

topics.title: What's New in Version 14.2.4
topics.body: What's New in Version 14.2.4 What's New in Version 14.2.4 SoftDent, version 14.2.4, includes enhancements in the following areas: Date Filter Added to Hygiene Analysis Report An Exclude patients who had their last visit before: option has been added to the Hygiene Analysis report. If your office has overdue patients who have not been marked inactive, using this filter will keep the number of patients due from being inflated in the report. See Running the Hygiene Analysis Report for more information. E-mail Address Added to Routing Slip An E-mail Address field has been added to the Patient and Account sections of the routing slip. Printing Lengthy Contact Notes and Exporting Contact Notes to Excel In past versions of SoftDent, you could not print the entire content of a contact note if the note exceeded 325 characters. A Print one contact note per page option has been added to the Contact List Report Setup window which enables you to print the entire note. You can now also output contact notes to Excel. See Printing Contacts for more information. Two Signature Fields on Laser Prescription Form A new prescription form containing two signature lines (Substitution Permitted and Dispense as written) has been added to the list of available forms. Select 11# Laser Prescription Form, Two Signature Lines to use this form. See Selecting Prescription Forms for more information. Capture New Clinical Document Option in Document Manager If you need to scan clinical images but do not have dental imaging software, you can scan the images using Document Manager. A Capture New Clinical Document button has been added to the Document Manager to enable you to scan or import a clinical image into the patient document tree. See Adding Clinical Documents for more information. New ePayments Windows Several changes have been made to ePayments windows. Credit Card Authorization Window In past versions of SoftDent, when you selected a credit card payment type, the Payment window contained transaction information and credit card information: Now this information is split into two windows. In version 14.2 and newer, when you select a credit card payment type, the following window is displayed: After you type the amount and click OK , the Carestream ePayments Charge Authorization window is displayed: For additional information, see Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Using a Card Swiper and Adding Payments to Patient Transactions Manually . Settlement Window In past versions of SoftDent, when you selected Reports > Accounting > Credit Card Settlement , the Recurring Credit Card Settlement window was displayed: Clicking Send settled any authorized cards. In SoftDent 14.2 and newer, selecting Reports > Accounting > Credit Card Settlement displays the Carestream ePayments Pending Transactions window: See Printing Settlement Reports for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: What's New in Version 14.2.6
topics.body: What's New in Version 14.2.6 What's New in Version 14.2.6 SoftDent, version 14.2.6, includes enhancements in the following areas: CDT-2014 Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2014) codes is included with this version of SoftDent. When the CDT-2014 codes are installed, the following things occur: Twenty-nine new codes are added to the database. Eighteen codes are revised. Four codes are removed. When you open the software for the first time, a CDT 2014 End User License Agreement window is displayed. Read the license agreement carefully. If you agree to the terms, click Accept to load the new codes. The CDT-2014 code set has been replaced by the CDT-2022 code set. See Using CDT Codes and Viewing ADA Code Descriptions for more information. Posting a Payment or Refund to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers An office that is using ePayments and has its credit card processing set to Enabled for Providers can now post one credit card payment or refund per billing provider with a single card swipe. In past versions, an office using the Enabled for Providers credit card processing setting would make one credit card payment or refund for the first doctor in the payment distribution list, and all funds would be applied to the first doctor's account. After posting, you are prompted to print a receipt listing each charge and the provider name under which each charge was made. See Posting a Payment to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers and Posting a Refund to Multiple Credit-Card-Enabled Providers for more information. CS Solutions Options Added to Charting Two buttons have been added to the main charting toolbar that enable you to access CS Solutions technology: Capture an Intraoral Image Using CS 3500 Scan a 3D Object Using Carestream Dental Imaging Software These options have also been added to the X-ray menu. See Using the Main Charting Toolbar and Using the X-ray Menu for more information. SoftDent Software Now Windows Server 2012 Compatible You can now use SoftDent, version 14.2.6, with Windows Server 2012. The fingerprint scanner is currently not supported in Windows Server 2012. If your office is using the fingerprint scanner, do not install Windows Server 2012. SoftDent Software Now Windows 8 Compatible You can now use SoftDent, version 14.2.6, with Windows 8. The fingerprint scanner is currently not supported in Windows 8. If your office is using the fingerprint scanner, do not install Windows 8. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: What's New in Version 14.2.7
topics.body: What's New in Version 14.2.7 What's New in Version 14.2.7 SoftDent, version 14.2.7, includes enhancements in the following areas: ADA Sales Tax Code Now Printed on ADA Claim Form The standard ADA claim form has been updated to include the new ADA code for sales tax (9985). The updated form contains the following information: The ADA code 9985 is printed in box 29. A Sales Tax description is printed in box 30. The tax amount is printed in box 31. Select 202 # STANDARD ADA FORM 2012 Laser Form w/new sales tax from the Insurance Forms Selection window to use the updated form. See Selecting Insurance Forms for more information. Viewing Patient Information in the Electronic Router A View Patient option has been added to the list of options in the Electronic Router. Clicking this option opens the Patient Information window, where you can access patient demographic, treatment, and payment information. See Understanding the Electronic Router Window for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: What's New in Version 16.1.1
topics.body: What's New in Version 16.1.1 What's New in Version 16.1.1 SoftDent, version 16.1.1, includes enhancements in the following areas: ePrescriptions ePrescriptions enables you to automatically send electronic patient prescriptions directly to a preferred pharmacy. After enrolling in the service and configuring the software, you can access ePrescriptions directly from the Prescriptions window by clicking the Prescribe Drug button. See Using the ePrescriptions Module for more information. ICD-10 Diagnosis Codes The dental codes from the ICD-10 diagnosis code set are installed with this version of SoftDent. By default, all insurance companies are set to use the ICD-9 codes; however, if an insurance company requests that you use ICD-10 codes, you can set that option for that specific company. As more insurance companies start requesting the use of diagnosis codes, a Set Up Plans to Use Diagnosis Codes button has been added to the Insurance Company window. Clicking this button selects the Use Diagnosis Codes option (on the Claim Filing tab of the Insurance Plan window) for all of the plans administered by that insurance company. This option enables the Diagnosis Codes fields on the Transactions window. Several sections have been added to the ADA Dental Claim Form to accommodate ICD-10 codes: Box 29a. Diag Pointer: Indicates the most appropriate of the four ICD diagnostic codes listed in Box 34a. The entry in 29a is either A, B, C, or D. Box 34 Diagnosis Code List Qualifier: Indicates which set of ICD codes was used. A diagnosis code followed by the letter B indicates it is an ICD-9 code. A diagnosis code followed by the letters AB indicates it is an ICD-10 code. Box 34a. Diagnosis Codes: The primary diagnosis code is displayed in field "A," and any subsequent or follow-up diagnosis codes display in the other fields. Several ICD-10 code fields have also been added to the CMS 1500 Health Insurance Claim Form. See Adding an Insurance Company for more information. Personal Notes Added to Assistant's Info Window in Patient Chart Personal notes (for example, 'John is a big baseball fan.') from the Notes tab of the Patient window are now displayed on the first page of the Assistant's Info window in the patient chart, enabling you to make personal contact with the patient. See Adding New Patients from the Patient Windows and Viewing the Assistant's Info Window for more information. Patient and Account Preferred Contact When adding a new patient or account, or when editing existing demographic information, you can now indicate the preferred method of contact -- Cell, Home, Email, etc. When you select the contact using the new Preferred Contact drop-down list, the preferred contact type is displayed in the following areas: In the Patient and Account Information window, the preferred contact is highlighted in yellow. In the Daily Appointments window, on the patient appointment slot and in the scheduling Assistant window (accessed by clicking Zoom), the preferred contact is displayed in bold italics. If Email is the preferred method, due to space constraints on the appointment slot, a "Zoom for eMail" note is displayed. Click the Zoom icon to view the email address in the scheduling Assistant window. In reports, the preferred contact is displayed in bold italics. The preferred contact for an account can be different from that of an associated patient. See Adding New Patients from the Patient Windows and Adding New Accounts for more information. New Payments

topics.title: What's New in Version 16.2.1
topics.body: What's New in Version 16.2.1 What's New in Version 16.2.1 SoftDent, version 16.2.1, includes enhancements in the following areas: Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report A Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation report has been added to the Practice Management Insurance Reports menu ( Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation ) . This report lists Medicaid services provided during a given time period, as required by some states' Medicaid offices. See Running the Medicaid Wrap Reconciliation Report for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: What's New in Version 16.2.3
topics.body: What's New in Version 16.2.3 What's New in Version 16.2.3 SoftDent, version 16.2.3, includes enhancements in the following areas: Third-Party Imaging Software Integration A new imaging integration enables SoftDent Cloud users to launch their third-party imaging software, as well as Florida Probe, directly from the patient chart and gives on-premises users access to additional third-party imaging software. You can now access the following imaging software from the patient chart: Bold text indicates the imaging software that SoftDent Cloud and on-premises users can select. Regular text indicates the imaging software that only on-premises users can select. Asterisks indicate new imaging software options. Dentsply/New Image - Xamine-It Dentsply/Gendex - VixWin Digora Schick Trophy - ui Dexis Cygnus Imaging PlanMeca Dimaxis CliniView Dolphin* TigerView PW Dicom Patient Gallery* ImageFXSycan* Medicor MiCAPS* Florida Probe* If your office has previously been accessing Dentsply/New Image - Xamine-It, Trophy - ui, Cygnus Imaging, PlanMeca Dimaxis, or CliniView from the patient chart, your settings are retained, and you do not need to take any action. If your office uses any of the imaging software highlighted in bold, if you have never used SoftDent or imaging before, or if your office uses SoftDent Cloud, you must configure each machine that will be running imaging software. To configure your third-party imaging software: Select Patient Chart > Chart > Options > X-ray . The Chart Properties X-ray tab is displayed. Select the imaging software name from the drop-down list and select an option: If the X-ray EXE Path: field is enabled: Click Browse , navigate to the imaging software .exe file, and select it. Click OK . Click OK . OR If the X-ray EXE Path: field is greyed out: Click OK . The first time you launch the imaging software (by selecting X-ray > Start ' software name' ), a message asking you to configure the imaging software is displayed. Click OK . The Imaging Launcher Preferences window is displayed. Click the ... (Browse) button, navigate to the imaging software .exe file, and select it. Click Open . Click Save and Launch . In addition, imaging options for Dolphin, Patient Gallery, ImageFXSycan, Medicor MiCAPS, and Florida Probe have been added to the list of imaging software on the Other tab ( Patient Chart > Chart > Options > Other ). See Setting Charting Options and Properties for more information. CDT-2016 Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2016) codes is included with this version of SoftDent. When the CDT-2016 codes are installed, the following things occur: Nineteen new codes are added to the database. Twelve codes are revised. Eight codes are removed. When you open the software for the first time, a CDT 2016 End User License Agreement window is displayed. Read the license agreement carefully. If you agree to the terms, click Accept to load the new codes. The CDT-2016 code set has been replaced by the CDT-2022 code set. See Using CDT Codes and Viewing ADA Code Descriptions for more information. SoftDent Software Now Windows 10 Compatible You can now use SoftDent, version 16.2.3, with Windows 10. The fingerprint scanner is currently not supported in Windows 10. If your office is using the fingerprint scanner, do not install Windows 10. Trophy Review and Annotate (R&A) Software Development Kit (SDK) The first time you open SoftDent after installing version 16.2.3, you might be prompted to run the Trophy

topics.title: What's New in Version 16.2.5
topics.body: What's New in Version 16.2.5 What's New in Version 16.2.5 SoftDent, version 16.2.5, includes enhancements in the following areas: CDT-2017 Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2017) codes is included with this version of SoftDent. When the CDT-2017 codes are installed, the following things occur: Eleven new codes are added to the database. Thirty-seven codes are revised. One code is deleted. When you open the software for the first time, a CDT 2017 End User License Agreement window is displayed. Read the license agreement carefully. If you agree to the terms, click Accept to load the new codes. The CDT-2017 code set has been replaced by the CDT-2022 code set. See Using CDT Codes and Viewing ADA Code Descriptions for more information. New CDT Code D6085 (Provisional Implant Crown) Represented by Implant Icon One of the new CDT codes, D6085 (Provisional Implant Crown), is represented with the implant icon when charted. See Charting ADA Procedures for more information. Mobility Settings for SoftDent Cloud Users If you are a SoftDent Cloud user and will be using Mobility to access practice data from a mobile device and the web, you must first enable the Mobility option for the practice. Then you enable the option for each user and enter each user's email address. Make these changes: In the General System Settings window ( System > Change System Settings > General System ), select Enable Mobility . In the Rights by User window ( System > Change System Settings > System Security > Rights By User > double-click user name ), type the user's Carestream single-sign-on (SSO) account email address in the field. If the user doesn't have an SSO account, type the user's work email address in the field. Select Enable Mobility . You must make these changes for each user who will be using Mobility. See General System Settings and Adding Users for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: What's New in Version 16.2
topics.body: What's New in Version 16.2 What's New in Version 16.2 SoftDent, version 16.2, includes enhancements in the following areas: CareCredit Integration The software for CareCredit, the healthcare credit card, is now integrated with SoftDent, enabling you to accept a patient's CareCredit payment in the same manner as any other credit card payment. Now you can offer patients the opportunity to apply for a CareCredit account, present financing options to the patient, take CareCredit payments, or make refunds from inside SoftDent windows you use every day. Changes to Reports Two reports--Transactions for a Period and the Treatment Plan Detailed Report--now have customized CareCredit versions. In addition, the Daysheet now includes a separate page for CareCredit transactions, and the deposit slip also has a separate CareCredit page. See Using CareCredit for more information. One-Time-Use Credit Card Insurance Payments You can now apply an insurance payment to a claim using a one-time-use credit card, which some insurance companies are providing for payments. New Credit Card Insurance Payment Codes The following codes have been added to the ADA/Transaction Code list for new offices (or offices who have not renamed codes): 11.93 MasterCard Insurance Payment 12.93 VISA Insurance Payment 13.93 Discover Insurance Payment 14.93 American Express Insurance Payment These codes have also been added to the Default ADA tab on the Practice Information window. For offices that are using renamed credit card codes, the first available extension, starting with .93, will be used. For example: If your office uses code 17 for VISA, and you are not using code 17.93, the system will assign code 17.93 as the VISA Insurance Payment code for your practice. If code 17.93 is already in use, the next available extension (.94) is used, and so on. If your office has used all of the reserved extensions for a particular credit card type, a message is displayed, stating that you must manually set up the code � first in the ADA/Transaction Code List window and then in the Practice Information window. Only codes entered in the Insurance Payment by section on the Default ADA tab of the Practice Information window are available for credit card processing and claim attachment. The bulk insurance payment process does not currently support credit card insurance payments. Changes to Insurance and Accounting Reports Insurance credit card payment amounts are now included in the following reports: Contractual Plan Analysis Report: Insurance payments by credit card are listed as separate entries with an amount in the Credit column. The Contractual Plan Analysis Summary page lists the new "Insurance Payments" amounts in the Collections section. Capitation Collections Summary Report: Amounts posted for insurance payments by credit card are added to the Supplemental Insurance Payments line. Insurance Check Distribution Report (now renamed the Insurance Payment Distribution Report): Insurance payments by credit card are listed as separate entries. Deposit Slip: Insurance payments by credit card are displayed on the Credit Card Slips to Deposit page. Daysheet: Insurance payments by credit card are displayed on the first page, with an amount in the Credit column. Insurance payments by credit card are also displayed on the Credit Card Deposit Slips page. Account Billing Statement: Insurance payments by credit card are displayed as separate entries and include the claim number, if a claim has been

topics.title: What's New in Version 17.0
topics.body: What's New in Version 17.0 What's New in Version 17.0 SoftDent, version 17.0, includes enhancements in the following areas: User Interface Update The overall look and feel of SoftDent, version 17.0, has been updated with a more modern, streamlined design to enable you to focus on your patient information. Changes to SoftDent windows include: Removal of "3-D look" Rounded corners on text fields and buttons New color palette and background colors Charting Module Updates Several enhancements have been made to the charting module, including: New Overview tab � displays key information about a patient's clinical history in one window. See Overview Tab for more information. Enlargement of existing Restorative, Perio, Exams, Clinical Imaging, and Treatment Planning tabs. New Notes tab � gives you convenient access to the Patient Clinical Notes window. New Labs tab � gives you convenient access to the Lab Case window. New Documents tab � gives you convenient access to the Documents for window. Record Locking Improvements You can now access and update a record (a patient record, patient chart, or an account record) from multiple computers at the same time. Previously, if you attempted to open a record while another user had it open, a message was displayed, stating that the record was being modified by another user, and you could only look at the record in View mode. Now you can immediately work with any record with no delays. CDT-2018 Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2018) codes is included with this version of SoftDent. When the CDT-2018 codes are installed: Eighteen new codes are added to the database. Sixteen codes are revised. Three codes are deleted. If you have not already downloaded the CDT-2018 codes , when you open the software for the first time, a CDT 2018 End User License Agreement window is displayed. Read the license agreement carefully. If you agree to the terms, click Accept to load the new codes. The CDT-2018 code set has been replaced by the CDT-2022 code set. See Using CDT Codes and Viewing ADA Code Descriptions for more information. Frequently Asked Questions A "Frequently Asked Questions" topic, based on questions posted on The Exchange (Carestream Dental's user community), has been added to the online help. This topic contains some of the most-asked or most-viewed questions we have received on The Exchange. See Frequently Asked Questions for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: What's New in Version 17.1
topics.body: What's New in Version 17.1 What's New in Version 17.1 SoftDent, version 17.1, includes enhancements in the following areas: Charting Module Patient Banner (Header Bar) Updated The patient banner--the area at the top of the patient chart, also called the header bar--has been updated to enable you to see specific patient information regardless of which charting tab you are using. Enhancements to the patient banner include: Patient Information--Displays the patient's portrait or avatar, along with a link to the Patient Portrait window, where you can capture a portrait, if necessary. Also displays the patient ID, name, and nickname, along with a link to the Patient Information window. Medical Alerts--Displays any patient medical alerts, along with a link to the Conditions tab of the Medical History window. Allergies--Displays any patient allergies, along with a link to the General tab of the Medical History window. Meds--Displays the number of medications the patient is currently taking, along with a link to the General tab of the Medical History window. Prescr--Displays the number of prescriptions the patient has been prescribed by the office, along with a link to the Prescriptions for List window. Provider--Displays the patient's assigned provider, along with a link to the Provider List window. In Acct--Displays the number of patients in the same account as the selected patient, along with a link to a list of patients in the account. The chart mode and quadrant view are also displayed in the patient banner. See Using the Header Bar for more information. Cell Phone/Cell Fields Now Available A Cell Phone or Cell field has been added to several windows in SoftDent, including: New Patient Appointment window Patient List window Account List window Patient Appointment window Appointment Card window Phone Center window Tooth Surface Added to Clinical Notes List A Surf (Surface) column has been added to the Clinical Notes List window, enabling you to see the surface of the tooth for which a note applies. Preferred Contact Indicator Added to Confirmation Window A client's preferred method of contact�if one has been set on the Patient Information window�is now displayed in bold italics in the Contact Info section of the Appointment Confirmation window ( right-click appt. > Confirm Appt. ). See Confirming Appointments for more information. Electronic Fund Transfer (EFT) Payment Information Added to Reports The daysheet, register reports, and the Insurance Payment Distribution report have been changed to include EFT payment information. Daysheet A new page-- Deposit Slip for EFT Payments --has been added to the Daysheet report that lists only EFT payments. The new page has the same layout as the existing Check Deposit Slip that prints with the Daysheet. The Deposit Slip for EFT Payments page will also print to Excel when the Daysheet is printed to Excel. See Running the Daysheet Report for more information. Register Reports An EFT field has been added to the Daily, Monthly, Yearly, and Period register reports. The reports have changed in the following ways: Each register report will have an EFT field added to the Collections section, under the Credit Card field. The calculated EFT value, based on selection criteria, will be added to the report to the right of the EFT field. The calculated EFT value will be added to the Net Collections total. The calculated EFT value will be dispersed in the Insurance Plan Collection and Regular Collection totals, as

topics.title: What's New in Version 18.0
topics.body: What's New in Version 18.0 What's New in Version 18.0 SoftDent, version 18.0, includes enhancements in the following areas: ePayments Secure ePayments Secure gives your practice and patients additional credit card payment security. Advantages of ePayments Secure include: No credit card numbers stored on your local computers Automatic settlement (no more manual settlement) New credit card chip reader/swiper/NFC machine Same workflow as existing ePayments You must purchase a new credit card chip reader/swiper/NFC device in order to use ePayments Secure. Contact your Carestream Dental representative for more details. New ePayments customers must use ePayments Secure. Existing ePayments users can continue to use version 1 and do not need to upgrade. See ePayments Secure for more information. Workflows and Roles A "Workflows and Roles" section has been added to the online help. This section shows the general workflow within SoftDent for each role (for example, Front Desk Administrator, Dental Hygienist, and so on) in the practice and lists the tasks those team members perform. See Workflows and Roles for more information. "Name" and "Date Created" Fields Added to TeamTalk -- Notes Window To help you organize and manage your TeamTalk messages, two fields� Name and Date Created �have been added to the TeamTalk -- Notes window ( TeamTalk > General TeamTalk Lists ). Use the slider to view all of the fields in the window. See Using TeamTalk Options for more information. Additional "Print Routing Slip" Options You can now print the routing slip from several additional areas in the software. A Print Routing Slip option has been added to the following areas: At the bottom of the Options section on the Express Bar in the Daybook window (the option is enabled when you select an appointment) At the top of the Appointment Card window On the Appointment Submenu (displayed when you right-click on an appointment in the schedule) See Understanding Daybook Options , Editing Appointment Card Information , and Appointment Submenu Commands for more information. "Reviewed By Dr." Checkbox and "Rev. By Dr." Column Added to Patient Clinical Notes Windows A Reviewed by Dr. checkbox has been added to the Patient Clinical Notes window ( Patient Chart > Clinical Notes > View Clinical Notes > Add ), which enables the doctor to indicate that he has checked the clinical notes for the patient. The Work Performed window ( Patient Chart > Clinical Notes > Post End of Day Notes ) and Patient Clinical Notes List window ( Patient List > Clinical Management > View Clinical Notes ) now contain a Rev. by Dr. column. A Yes is displayed in this column when the doctor has selected the Reviewed by Dr. option in the Patient Clinical Notes window. See Adding Clinical Notes at the End of the Day , Editing Patient Clinical Notes , and Amending Patient Clinical Notes for more information. "Modify Reviewed by Dr. Checkbox" Permission Added to Clinical Security Settings List A Modify Reviewed by Dr. Checkbox security permission has been added to the Clinical Security Settings list. A user with this permission can check and uncheck the Rev. by Dr. checkbox on the Patient Clinical Notes window. A Rev. by Dr -- Checked or Rev. by Dr -- Unchecked entry is made in the Transaction Audit Trail report each time the checkbox is selected or deselected. See Clinical Tasks with Limited Access for more information. Additional Options Added to Place of Service Drop-Down List Nineteen options have been ad

topics.title: What's New in Version 19.0.1
topics.body: What's New in Version 19.0.1 What's New in Version 19.0.1 SoftDent, version 19.0.1, includes enhancements in the following areas: CDT-2021 Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2021) codes is included with this version of SoftDent. When the CDT-2021 codes are installed: Twenty-eight new codes are added to the database. Seven codes have revised nomenclature. Twenty-two codes have editorial changes. Four codes are deleted. If you have not already downloaded the CDT-2021 codes , when you open the software for the first time, a CDT 2021 End User License Agreement window is displayed. Read the license agreement carefully. If you agree to the terms, click Accept to load the new codes. The CDT-2021 code set has been replaced by the CDT-2022 code set. See Using CDT Codes and Viewing ADA Code Descriptions for more information. Importing Multiple Documents You are now able to import multiple document files at the same time when importing/capturing new documents. During the file select process, you can select multiple files, and they will be imported into the document tree for the selected patient. Print Button for Clinical Notes A Print button is now available from the Clinical Notes List window and from the Chart > Clinical Notes menu, with the exception of the End of Day Notes. The Print button has been added to the following locations: Express Bar > Clinical Management > View Clinical Notes List > Clinical Notes > Generic Clinical Notes Chart > Clinical Notes on the chart's menu bar > View Clinical Notes Chart > Notes tab Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: What's New in Version 19.0.3
topics.body: What's New in Version 19.0.3 What's New in Version 19.0.3 SoftDent, version 19.0.3, includes enhancements in the following areas: CDT-2022 Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2022) codes is included with this version of SoftDent. When the CDT-2022 codes are installed: Sixteen new codes are added to the database. Thirteen codes have revised nomenclature. Nine codes have editorial changes. Six codes are deleted. Eight codes tied to COVID-19 (adopted in spring 2021) are added to the database. If you have not already downloaded the CDT-2022 codes , when you open the software for the first time, a CDT 2022 End User License Agreement window is displayed. Read the license agreement carefully. If you agree to the terms, click Accept to load the new codes. See Using CDT Codes and Viewing ADA Code Descriptions for more information. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: What's New in Version 19.0
topics.body: What's New in Version 19.0 What's New in Version 19.0 SoftDent, version 19.0, includes enhancements in the following areas: CS Imaging 8 Integration This version of SoftDent includes the integration for Carestream Dental's newest imaging software, CS Imaging 8. Updates to the imaging platform provide one-stop access to all of your 2D images, 3D images, and CAD/CAM data. Changes include: Powerful dashboard image gallery Faster image review Integration of 3D imaging and CAD/CAM Enhanced integration and installation Modern, user-friendly design CDT-2020 Codes The latest edition of the Current Dental Terminology (CDT-2020) codes is included with this version of SoftDent. When the CDT-2020 codes are installed: Thirty-seven new codes are added to the database. Five codes have revised nomenclature. Periodontal category descriptors are revised. Six codes are deleted. If you have not already downloaded the CDT-2020 codes , when you open the software for the first time, a CDT 2020 End User License Agreement window is displayed. Read the license agreement carefully. If you agree to the terms, click Accept to load the new codes. The CDT-2020 code set has been replaced by the CDT-2022 code set. See Using CDT Codes and Viewing ADA Code Descriptions for more information. Updated Insurance Forms The following insurance forms have been added to the Insurance Forms Selection window drop-down list ( Insurance > Select Insurance Forms ): Indiana Medicaid Claim INS286.M INS286.FRM CMS 1500 BCBS Rhode Island Preprinted Form INS304.FRM INS308.FRM 2012 BCBS Claim Form INS270.M INS270.FRM Ambetter CMS 1500 Form INS303.FRM See Changing the Insurance Form Used by an Insurance Plan for more information. 2019 ADA Claim Form The 2019 ADA Dental Claim Form is included in this version of SoftDent. The differences between the 2012 form and the 2019 form include: Boxes 7, 14, and 22 now include a "U" checkbox for an "unspecified" gender. Box 32 no longer supports the "A" qualifier. Box 34 no longer supports ICD 9 codes. Select 210 # STANDARD ADA FORM 2019 Laser Form from the Insurance Forms Selection window to use the new form. See Selecting Insurance Forms for more information. Security Updates To protect your patients' data and privacy, several security changes have been made in this version of the software. Password Required at Login If an employee did not previously use a password, he or she is now required to enter a user name and an 8- to 15-character password when logging in. A prompt asking for this information is displayed when the employee attempts to log in to version 19.0 for the first time. The prompt is also displayed if a user has a password that is less than 8 characters or if the password contains special characters. See Changing User Passwords and Changing Logins for more information. Patient Information Audit Trail A new audit trail -- Patient Information Audit Trail -- has been added to the System menu ( System > View Audit Trails > Patient Information Audit Trail ). When an employee changes patient demographic data (changes to clinical data are not included), the audit trail records the date, time, user name, and a description of the change. See Viewing Audit Trails for more information. Patient Information Audit Trail Reports Two Patient Information Audit Trail reports -- Patient Information Audit Trail By Date and Patient Information Audit Trail By User -- have been added to the Reports menu ( Reports > Audit Trail > Patient Information Audit Trai

topics.title: What's New in Version 14.2
topics.body: What's New in Version 14.2 What's New in Version 14.2 SoftDent, version 14.2, includes enhancements in the following areas: Submitting Real-Time Claims for Immediate Payor Response A Submit Real-Time for immediate payor response option has been added to the Submit Primary and Submit Secondary windows that are displayed when you submit an electronic claim. If you use the Real Time Claim Submission service, select this option, and click OK . The eServices window is immediately opened, and the Real Time claim submission process begins. When the process is complete, a Claim Status window is displayed, showing whether the claim has been paid and the amount that was paid. The report that is returned from the insurance company during Real Time Claim processing is added to the patient's document tree in the Real Time Claim Responses folder. A Real Time Claims Capable checkbox has been added to the Information tab of the Insurance Company window. This checkbox is disabled and unchecked if your office does not use the Real Time Claim Submission service or if the insurance company does not support Real Time Claims. See Submitting Claims for more information. Enhanced ePayments Credit Card Security The Carestream ePayments service is in full compliance with the Payment Card Industry (PCI) Data Security Standard (PCI DSS). PCI has developed security standards for handling cardholder information, and the ePayments service adheres to the PCI DSS requirements for security management, policies, procedures, network architecture, software design, and other critical protective measures. When editing credit card payments, you must now click the View Credit Card Info button to review a patient's credit card information. The patient's credit card number is masked, with only the last 4 digits exposed, enabling you to see what card a patient has on file. Dr Signature on File Option Several Dr Signature on File options have been added to insurance windows: An Accept Dr Signature on File option has been added to the Claim Filing tab of the Insurance Plan window ( List > Insurance Plan > Claim Filing ). This setting enables you to specify whether a plan accepts Signature On File for the Billing and Treating Drs signature areas on a claim form. A Dr Signature on File option has been added to the Status tab of the Claims window ( List > Claim > Status ). If the current open claim is a primary claim, this option uses the primary plan's Dr Signature on File setting. If the current open claim is a secondary claim, this option uses the secondary plan's Dr Signature on File setting. In either case, you can manually change the setting before submitting the claim. A Dr Signature on File option has been added to the Status tab of the Completed Claims window ( List > Completed Claim > Status ). This option reflects the state of the claim's Dr Signature on File setting when the claim was submitted. All SoftDent forms that are based on the standard 2006 ADA Dental Claim Form are updated to print Signature on File in box 53 (Treating Dr Signature). All SoftDent forms that are based on the standard CMS 1500 Medical Claim Form are updated to print Signature on File in box 31 (Signature of Physician). See Adding Insurance Plans and Checking Claim Status Information for more information. Multi-Page Scanning You can now scan multiple pages into SoftDent without having to scan one page at a time or respond to multiple prompts. After you feed in all of the pages to your multi-page sca

topics.title: Running the Who's Due Back Referrals Report
topics.body: Running the Who's Due Back Referrals Report Patient and Doctor Referrals > Running Referral Reports Running the Who's Due Back Referrals Report The Who's Due Back Referrals report lists all patients who your practice has referred to other providers. Information on this report includes the patient�s name and ID, referral date, expected return date, referral reason, and last visit date. Patients on this report have the Due Back field in their patient record set to a date that falls within the specified date range. Sample Report To run the Who's Due Back Referrals report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Referral Reports > Who�s Due Back . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output option and click OK . The Outstanding Referrals List Setup window is displayed. In the Dates section, click the drop-down arrows to select the starting and ending expected return dates from a calendar. To include patients with future appointments, click the Include Patients with a future appointment checkbox. To include color and shading on the report, click the Include/Color Shading checkbox. Click OK . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Setting Workspace Preferences
topics.body: Setting Workspace Preferences Getting Started > Using the Workspace Setting Workspace Preferences You can dictate if the software windows are displayed using the Enhanced Workspace or Classic Workspace . You can also set Express bar docking preferences and view options. To set your workspace preferences: Select View > Select Workspace Preferences . The Workspace Preferences window is displayed. Select one of the following: To use the Classic Workspace, select Use Classic Workspace . To use the Enhanced Workspace, select Use Enhanced Workspace and select an option: Dock Left : Docks the Express bar to the left of the client area. Dock Right : Docks the Express bar to the right of the client area. Full Screens (where available) : Uses the entire client area to display windows when possible. Click OK . A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Workflows and Roles
topics.body: Workflows and Roles Workflows and Roles Workflows are instruction guides on how to perform tasks in the SoftDent software. Roles, also known as personas, refer to jobs as defined by the tasks usually performed by that individual. Depending upon the size of the practice, one person may perform multiple roles. Select one of the following options to view workflows for a specific role: Role: Front Desk Administrator Role: Treatment Coordinator Role: Dental Hygienist Role: Office Manager Role: Dental Assistant Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Working with Medvisor Dental Software
topics.body: Working with Medvisor Dental Software Using the SoftDent Charting Module Working with Medvisor Dental Software SoftDent enables you to integrate with the MedVisor dental software. Images can be shared between MedVisor and SoftDent, retaining the patient information with the images. MedVisor enables you to export images to SoftDent. To open MedVisor in a patient�s chart, click Start MedVisor Software . You can also access MedVisor in other areas of SoftDent: In the Patient List window, select MedVisor from the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Book At A Glance or Daily Appointments window, select MedVisor from the Express bar or the Options menu. Right-click an image in any thumbnail viewer and select MedVisor . In the InOffice window, select MedVisor from the Express bar or the Options menu. In the Treatment Plan window, select MedVisor from the Express bar or the Options menu. Open a patient�s Documents for window. Right-click an image in the document tree and select MedVisor . You can open any clinical or portrait image in MedVisor. To open an image in MedVisor, right-click the image in the thumbnail viewer and select MedVisor . Images from MedVisor are displayed in the thumbnail viewer with a light green border. If the image is selected, the border is red. Tip Images exported from MedVisor into SoftDent are displayed in the document tree with a MedVisor icon. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Working with Additional File Types Working with Additional File Types To view a file that is not an image in the document manager: Select the document in the tree. A placeholder is displayed in the preview window. Double-click the placeholder, or right-click and select Open . To access the document manager menus: Right-click on a document in the tree to display the context menu. Append Page(s) Edit Document Description Attach/Detach to Other Rec(s) E-Mail Print Document Delete Review Change Category Close Right-click on a placeholder in the preview window to display the thumbnail menu. Open Properties Delete Copy to Clipboard Export Print Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Running the Write-Off Totals Report
topics.body: Running the Write-Off Totals Report Setting Up Insurance > Running Insurance Reports > Running Insurance Practice Management Reports Running the Write-Off Totals Report This report generates a list of codes that have had a portion of their fee written off. It includes the total write-offs posted in the specified date range, including those that may have been compressed, and a total of the actual write-offs. Additional Details: This report only calculates write-offs posted as code 50.90. Any other codes do not display. The total for Actual Write-Offs Posted might differ from the total of Write-Offs due to posted write-off transaction modifications. The Actual Write-Offs Posted field gives a total of what is actually posted in your system. The Risk and Development Write-Offs Posted field displays the percentage of the expected insurance payment that has to be written off, calculated using the total of all codes 50.91 posted. If the Summary format is selected, only totals listed for Total and Writeoffs display. Sample Report To run the Write-Off Totals report: Select Reports > Practice Management > Insurance Reports > Writeoff Totals . The Output Options window is displayed. Select the output type and click OK . The Transaction Writeoff Report Setup window is displayed. Use the drop-down arrows to select a specific date range. In the Summary Format section, select the checkbox if you want to summarize codes by ranges. In the Insurance Co ID and Plan ID sections, use the drop-down arrows to select a specific insurance company or plan, or leave as 0 for all companies and plans. In the Doctors section, use the drop-down arrows to select specific providers, or leave as 999 to report on all providers. In the Patient User Codes section, type the patient user codes to include or exclude in the report. In the Codes section, use the drop-down arrows to select up to five code ranges. To specify a single code, type the code in the second Codes field and leave the first field blank. Click OK . Additional Write-Off Totals Report Information: Actual Write-Offs (List each code format) Code�Procedure codes posted against the insurance plan for the specified date ranges. Total�Total dollar amount charged for that code. (This can be different from the fee schedule 0 amount.) Allowed�The current allowed amount in the plan�s bluebook. Write-Offs�The actual total of all codes 50.90 posted for the specified date range. In order to get the actual write-off per code, payments must be posted from the Insurance Payment screen. Expected Write-Offs (List each code format) Code�Procedure codes posted against the insurance plan for the specified date range. Total�Total dollar amount charged for that code. (This can be different from the fee schedule 0 amount.) Allowed�The current allowed amount in the plan�s bluebook. Write-Offs�The expected total of all write-offs based on the write-off amount stored with the transaction for the specified date range. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.body: Wizards Menu Commands Getting Started > Using the Workspace > Using the Main Menu Bar Wizards Menu Commands U se the Wizards menu to configure the Scheduler, Insurance, Billing Statement, Questionnaire, and TeamTalk wizards. The Wizards menu commands are: Scheduler Wizard : Select this command to customize the Scheduler setup. Insurance Wizard : Select this command to customize the Insurance setup. Billing Statements Wizard : Select this command to customize the Billing Statement setup. Questionnaire Creation Wizard : Select this command to customize a patient questionnaire. TeamTalk Wizard : Select this command to customize the TeamTalk entry setup. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Transferring Account Balances to a Fixed Loan Budget Plan
topics.body: Transferring Account Balances to a Fixed Loan Budget Plan Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans Transferring Account Balances to a Fixed Loan Budget Plan You can set up a fixed loan budget plan for a fixed amount. The loan amount is to be repaid at a specified interest rate over a specified number of payments. To transfer an account balance to a fixed loan budget plan: Select an option: Select List > Account . Select Account List Window in the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. From any window, press Ctrl + A. The Account list window is displayed in FIND mode. Click anywhere in the list area or press Enter to switch to SCAN mode. Select the account and select Transfer to BP (Z) from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Account Payments window is displayed in Budget Plan Transfer mode. The outstanding balance for each patient assigned to the account is displayed. If the selected account is associated with Insurance Calculation Method 1 (ICM1) or Insurance Calculation Method 2 (ICM2) : The patient portion of any outstanding claim is not included in the amount that can be distributed until the final insurance claim payment is posted. The patient portion of the balance minus any insurable amounts is the default value in the PAYMENT and ADJ BAL columns. Select an option: To transfer the balance (the amount displayed in the PAYMENT column) for all patients associated with the account to an account budget plan : Select Post from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. To transfer a specific amount of the balance to the budget plan : Select Edit Distributions from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu, type the appropriate amount in the PAYMENT column, click OK , and select Post from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The distribution amount cannot exceed the adjusted balance amount. You cannot change the ICM for the account when there is an outstanding budget plan balance. The Account Budget Plan window for fixed loans is displayed. If the transferred amount exceeds the default credit limit of $500 : A warning is displayed informing you that the credit limit is exceeded. To extend the credit limit, click Yes . If Enable REVOLVING CREDIT is selected in the General System Settings window, a message that asks you whether to open a revolving account budget plan is displayed. Click No to apply a fixed account budget plan to the account. To apply a revolving credit budget plan to the account, see Setting Up Account Balances for a Revolving Credit Budget Plan . The Account Budget Plan List window is displayed. The account information, the loan amount, and the payoff amount are displayed at the top of the window. A code 48 line item detailing the budget plan amount is displayed. Select Terms from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The FIXED LOAN TERMS window is displayed. The transferred payment amount is displayed in the Credit Line, Loan Amount, and Loan Payoff fields. The default value for the Interest Starts on or after field is the first day of the next calendar month. The loan amount divided by twelve months is the default value of the Payment Due and Regular Monthly fields. Type the annual interest rate in the Interest Rate field. Select an option: Type the number of loan payments in the No. of Payments field. Click the drop-down button next to the No. of Payments field : The Compute Number of Payments window is disp

topics.title: Transferring an Account Balance to a Revolving Credit Budget Plan
topics.body: Transferring an Account Balance to a Revolving Credit Budget Plan Budget Planning > Handling Account Budget Plans > Setting Up Account Balances for a Revolving Credit Budget Plan Transferring an Account Balance to a Revolving Credit Budget Plan To transfer an account balance to a revolving credit budget plan: Select an option: Select List > Account . Select Account List Window in the Power Bar. See Using the Power Bar for more information. From any window, press Ctrl + A. The Account list window is displayed in FIND mode. Click anywhere in the list area or press Enter to switch to SCAN mode. Select the account and select Z Transfer to BP from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Account Payments window is displayed in Budget Plan Transfer Mode. The outstanding balance for each patient assigned to the account is displayed. Select an option: To transfer the balance (the amount displayed in the PAYMENT column) for all patients associated with the account to an account budget plan : Select Post from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. To transfer a specific amount of the balance to the budget plan : Select Edit Distributions from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu, type the amount in the PAYMENT column, click OK , and select Post from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The distribution amount cannot exceed the adjusted balance amount. If the transferred amount exceeds the default credit line limit of $500 : A warning is displayed informing you that the credit limit is exceeded. To extend the credit line amount, click Yes . A message that asks you whether to open a revolving account budget plan is displayed. Click Yes to apply a revolving credit budget plan to the account. The Account Budget Plan List window is displayed. Select Terms from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The REVOLVING LOAN TERMS window is displayed. The transferred payment amount is displayed in the Credit Line, Loan Amount, and Loan Payoff fields. The default value in the Defer interest charges until field is the first day of the next calendar month. The loan amount divided by twelve months is the default value of the Payment Due and Regular Monthly fields. The default value in the Interest Rate field is 0. If you are an existing user, default values may be displayed in the Credit Line and Interest Rate fields. The credit line and interest rate information for any loans that were previously attached to the account are displayed as the default values even if the loan has been deleted. Type the annual interest rate in the Interest Rate _ % APR field. Type the number of loan payments in the Payment Due = 1/ field. For example, type 12 in the field to bill the account guarantor one-twelfth of the balance each month. The loan will be paid off in 12 months or a year. To send a statement for the remaining balance when the balance is less than a specific amount, type the amount in the Bill full balance when below field. To defer any interest charges on the unpaid balance until a specific date, select the date from the Defer interest charges until calendar. Click OK . The current payment due and monthly payment amount is calculated and displayed in the Payment Due and Regular Monthly fields located at the bottom of the Account Budget Plan List window. The amount required to pay off the budget plan is displayed in the Loan Payoff field. The loan payoff amount increases when int

topics.title: Transferring Claim Balances to the Patient
topics.body: Transferring Claim Balances to the Patient Submitting and Tracking Claims Transferring Claim Balances to the Patient To help increase cash flow and reduce your receivables, you can transfer outstanding insurance balances back to the patient. Any claims that can be submitted to a secondary insurance plan are sent to the insurance claim batch file. Any claim balances covered only by the primary insurance become the patient�s responsibility. If you plan to transfer any outstanding claim balances over 90 days, run the Outstanding Claims by Patient report to generate a list of all patients whose claim balances are overdue by 90 days or more. To transfer a claim balance: Select Insurance > Transfer Balance of Old Claims to Patient . The Transfer Old Insurance Claims To Patients window is displayed. To delete claims that are older than a specific number of days, type the number in the Transfer claims older than� field and click OK . The number of days defaults to 60. A message is displayed that asks you to confirm the balance transfer. Click Yes . When all transfers are complete, the Transfer Claims Totals window is displayed. A message is displayed that asks whether to print the transfer log file. Click Yes . A message is displayed that asks you to print or delete the file. Select the appropriate option. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Transferring Credits
topics.body: Transferring Credits Posting Payments > Posting Account Payments Transferring Credits You can automatically transfer credits from one doctor or patient to another. Example : If someone pays $20 on a $100 procedure, and the practice receives an insurance check for $90, the patient has a $10 credit. If another patient in the account has a balance, the remainder may be applied to the balance of the other patient. To transfer a credit balance: Select an option: In the Account List window : Select the account and select Payments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Account window : Select Payments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient List window : Select the patient and select Transactions from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Patient window : Select Transactions from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The patient Transactions window is displayed. Select Payments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The Payments window is displayed. Select Transfer Credits in the Select Operation section. A confirmation message is displayed. Click Yes . The Payments - Transfer Credits window is displayed. Automatic is displayed in the Code drop-down list. The total for transactions displayed in the window is displayed in the Amount field. Select an option: To transfer the credit amount to transactions by the transaction creation date : Click By Age . The credit is applied based on the age of the transactions. To transfer the credit amount evenly to all transactions : Click Evenly . A percentage of the credit is transferred to all transactions based on the transaction�s percentage of the outstanding balance. To add a note, select the Notes column for the transaction, and type the information. Click Post . Code 98 is posted to the patient receiving the debit and code 96 to the patient receiving the credit. Click Close . Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Transmitting Attachments to NEA
topics.body: Transmitting Attachments to NEA Submitting and Tracking Claims > Using National Electronic Attachments, Inc. (NEA) Services Transmitting Attachments to NEA You can automatically send attachments after you create them, or you can send the attachments at another time. To send attachments to NEA at another time: Select an option: In the Claims List window : Select the claim and select Send NEA Attachments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. In the Claims window : Select Send NEA Attachments from the Options section of the Express bar or Options menu. The FastAttach Main Screen window is displayed. Add any required information, and click Send . The NEAComm1.7 window is displayed. Click Send Attachments . If you have configured the software to print the report and display the Reports tab automatically, the Reports tab is displayed. Click the Reports tab, and click Print Report . Click OK twice. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!

topics.title: Using the X-ray Menu
topics.body: Using the X-ray Menu Using the SoftDent Charting Module Using the X-ray Menu The commands available from the X-ray menu are: PW DICOM Imaging : This command is enabled if you use PW DICOM Imaging software. Select this command to access the software. Start Carestream Imaging Software : This command is enabled if you use CS Imaging software. Select this command to access the software. View or Acquire Local Images: Accesses CS Imaging to acquire patient images. This command is enabled only if you are using SoftDent Cloud. Capture Pano Image : This command is enabled if you use CS Imaging software. Select this command to access the software to capture a panoramic image. Capture 3D Image: This command is enabled if you use CS Imaging software, version 6.8, and have installed the 9000 3D extraoral imaging system. Select this command to access the 3D Acquisition window to capture a 3D image. Capture Ceph Image : This command is enabled if you use CS Imaging software. Select this command to access the software to capture a cephalometric image. Capture RVG Image : This command is enabled if you use CS Imaging software. Select this command to access the software to capture an RVG image. Capture Intraoral Image: This command is enabled if you use CS Imaging software, version 6, and have installed the 1000 intraoral video camera. Select this command to access the software to capture an intraoral image. Capture CR Image: This command is enabled if you use CS Imaging software and have a CR 7400 installed. Select this command to access the software to capture an image. Capture Intraoral Scan: This command is enabled if you use CS Imaging software. Select this command to access the software to capture an intraoral 3D model using the CS 3500, CS 3600, or CS 3700. Capture CS 3DIO Image: This command is enabled if you use CS Imaging software. Select this command to capture an intraoral image using the CS 9000 or CS 9300. Need more information? Connect with us on The Exchange!
